name: श्रीमाता
wordByWord: श्री / श्रिया - Lakshmi, wealth, auspiciousness, splendour, learning.
श्रियं माति - She measures, limits, classifies, distributes, or contains Śrī.
विषं माति - She holds poison in the throat; the feminine counterpart of Śrīkaṇṭha.
summary: (i) The supreme mother.

When we are in deep trouble, the first word uttered by us involuntarily is Ma. Human beings are afflicted by three kinds of Tapas (difficulties), which cannot be solved by the mother in her human form. The possession of the power to resolve these difficulties makes Her the “Superior Mother”. Worshipping Her, who has given birth to the whole world, will prevent birth again in this world from the mother’s womb. (353rd name can be compared). The best of love is mother’s affection. Hence to contemplate and worship Her as the mother will give us proximity to Her.
(ii) Sree means Lakshmee (goddess of wealth). Hence Sreemata means Lakshmee’s mother. Sree also means Saraswathi (goddess of learning). She is mother of both Lakshmee and Saraswathi.

(iii) Ma means “to measure”. Sreemata therefore means that She is capable of measuring wealth. One that measures is superior to the one that is measured. Ma also be interpreted to mean one who “distributes” or “classifies”. She is the one who classified the Vedas. From this angle, Sreedevee can also be indicated as Brahma and/or Vyasa. “Richo Yajumshi Samani Sahi Srihi Amrta Sadam” - when interpreted in this manner this name is masculine.

(iv) Sree means venom. One who held the venom in her throat (Mathi). Lord Parameshwara is called Srikanta because he retained the Halahala venom in his throat. The feminine form which is part of Him is also called by this same name.

(v) The prefix Sree is used to refer respectable men or objects - for instance Sree Chakra, Sree Vidya, Sree Sailam, etc. Thus She is aptly called as Sreemata, as she is the mother of the whole world. Moreover, the word Sree is itself auspicious. Is it not, hence appropriate to commence this series of thousand names with Sree?

(vi) This hymn of thousand names was first recited by the eight Vagdevata’s led by Vasini. They appeared from the word power of Sreedevee herself. They were ordained to perform this holy task of reciting this hymn at her behest. They, in turn, selected and dedicated these thousand names out of millions of powerful names, and, by way of reminiscing the mother’s boundless love and compassion bestowed on them, start this series with the name Sreemata (Mother).

(vii) Merging the three letters of Bala Mantra to the six consonants ha, sa, ka, la, ra and ta, we derive a three-part Mantra, which is indicated as Matha. This is contained in this name. This secret Mantra has to be learnt through an appropriate teacher, in a proper way.

(viii) There is a poem (Sloka) called “Mata Kundalini Kriya Madhumathee” in the book “Laghustuthi” written by a great Devee Upasakar, “Laghu Bhattarakar”. The word Mata mentioned here also has a special cryptic meaning. This name is an indication of that special meaning.

(ix) Mother here also indicates that She has given birth to the entire universe. It indicates one of the three main functions of the Supreme Being viz. creation. Vedas also reflect the same sense in Yatho Vaimani Bhoothani Jayanthee.

(ix) mother here also indicates that she has given birth to the entire universe. it indicates one of the three main functions of the supreme being viz. creation. vedas also reflect the same sense in yatho vaimani bhoothani jayanthee. अर्थ (meaning): she who is "endowed with श्री". therefore, she is the माता who is eternally accompanied by divine splendor and auspiciousness. अर्थ (meaning): the mother who is accompanied by or identified with श्री. अर्थ (meaning): श्रियं माति इति श्रीमाता. she who measures, limits, or controls material लक्ष्मी (wealth). by putting a limit on material existence, she bestows the limitless, boundless state of मोक्ष (liberation). अर्थ (meaning): she who contains the श्री (poison) in her throat. this derivation identifies the divine mother directly with the cosmic action of containing the poison during the churning of the ocean, establishing non-difference from नीलकण्ठ (shiva).
esoteric: अर्थ (Meaning): She who is "endowed with श्री". Therefore, she is the माता who is eternally accompanied by divine splendor and auspiciousness.
अर्थ (Meaning): The Mother who is accompanied by or identified with श्री.

अर्थ (Meaning): श्रियं माति इति श्रीमाता. She who measures, limits, or controls material लक्ष्मी (wealth). By putting a limit on material existence, she bestows the limitless, boundless state of मोक्ष (liberation).

अर्थ (Meaning): She who contains the श्री (poison) in her throat. This derivation identifies the Divine Mother directly with the cosmic action of containing the poison during the churning of the ocean, establishing non-difference from नीलकण्ठ (Shiva).

name: श्रीमहाराज्ञी
samasa: महती च असौ राज्ञी च महाराज्ञी। श्रीयुता महाराज्ञी श्रीमहाराज्ञी।
wordByWord: श्रीयुता महाराज्ञी - the great empress endowed with Śrī.
summary: (i) The great empress (that is the protector of the whole world); the second of the three functions of the Supreme Being viz. Protection is indicated in this place. The Vedas state the same thought as Yena Jatanee Jeevantee.
(ii) Three secret syllables of Sree Vidya are hidden in this name. They are to be learnt through an appropriate teacher, in a proper way.

(iii) Any king’s wife is called queen. A queen is called as Rajnee in Sanskrit. The wife of an emperor is Empress - Sreemaharajnee. It is not that Sreedevee is honoured because Her consort is an emperor. She on her own carries out the function of governance and hence She is Maharajnee.
searchText: 2 श्रीमहाराज्ञी महती च असौ राज्ञी च महाराज्ञी। श्रीयुता महाराज्ञी श्रीमहाराज्ञी। श्री - auspiciousness, splendour, divine wealth. महती - great. राज्ञी - queen, ruler. महाराज्ञी - great empress. श्रीयुता महाराज्ञी - the great empress endowed with śrī. (i) the great empress (that is the protector of the whole world); the second of the three functions of the supreme being viz. protection is indicated in this place. the vedas state the same thought as yena jatanee jeevantee. (ii) three secret syllables of sree vidya are hidden in this name. they are to be learnt through an appropriate teacher, in a proper way. (iii) any king’s wife is called queen. a queen is called as rajnee in sanskrit. the wife of an emperor is empress - sreemaharajnee. it is not that sreedevee is honoured because her consort is an emperor. she on her own carries out the function of governance and hence she is maharajnee. when bhāskararāya decodes महाराज्ञी, he moves past the conventional meaning of a royal monarch ruling an empire and elevates it to cosmic and mantric planes: i. the sovereign of manifestation and dissolution the title राज्ञी comes from the verbal root राज् (rāj), meaning "to shine" or "to illuminate." she is the महाराज्ञी because she shines supreme as the independent, self-luminous consciousness (स्वप्रकाश चिद्रूप) that illuminates the entire universe. unlike planetary rulers or cosmic deities (like indra or brahmā) whose domains are temporary and bounded by time, her sovereignty spans across all three cosmic states: creation (sṛṣṭi), preservation (sthiti), and dissolution (saṃhāra). ii. the ruler of the nine enclosures (śrī cakra) in the esoteric geography of the śrī cakra, the three outermost lines are called the bhūpura. within the entire citadel (śrī nagara), she sits at the absolute center at the बिन्दु (bindu). esoteric mapping: as the महाराज्ञी, she is the absolute commanding consciousness of the entire cosmic diagram. all the peripheral or enclosure deities (āvaraṇa devatās) act merely as her structural expansions or rays of light. iii. resolution into non-duality (advaita) bhāskararāya asserts that true sovereignty lies in the absence of a second entity. if there is a rival or a separate object, dominion is limited. because she is the non-dual supreme reality (ekam-eva-advitīyam), there is nothing outside of her. her rule is absolute because the universe is her own self-projection (vimarśa). therefore, श्रीमहाराज्ञी signifies the supreme, unconditioned state of absolute autonomy (स्वातन्त्र्य शक्ति).
esoteric: When Bhāskararāya decodes महाराज्ञी, he moves past the conventional meaning of a royal monarch ruling an empire and elevates it to cosmic and mantric planes:
I. The Sovereign of Manifestation and Dissolution
The title राज्ञी comes from the verbal root राज् (rāj), meaning "to shine" or "to illuminate."
She is the महाराज्ञी because She shines supreme as the independent, self-luminous consciousness (स्वप्रकाश चिद्रूप) that illuminates the entire universe.

Unlike planetary rulers or cosmic deities (like Indra or Brahmā) whose domains are temporary and bounded by time, Her sovereignty spans across all three cosmic states: creation (Sṛṣṭi), preservation (Sthiti), and dissolution (Saṃhāra).
II. The Ruler of the Nine Enclosures (Śrī Cakra)
In the esoteric geography of the Śrī Cakra, the three outermost lines are called the Bhūpura. Within the entire citadel (Śrī Nagara), She sits at the absolute center at the बिन्दु (Bindu).

Esoteric Mapping: As the महाराज्ञी, She is the absolute commanding consciousness of the entire cosmic diagram. All the peripheral or enclosure deities (Āvaraṇa Devatās) act merely as Her structural expansions or rays of light.
III. Resolution into Non-Duality (Advaita)
Bhāskararāya asserts that true sovereignty lies in the absence of a second entity. If there is a rival or a separate object, dominion is limited.

Because She is the non-dual supreme reality (Ekam-eva-advitīyam), there is nothing outside of Her. Her rule is absolute because the universe is Her own self-projection (Vimarśa). Therefore, श्रीमहाराज्ञी signifies the supreme, unconditioned state of absolute autonomy (स्वातन्त्र्य शक्ति).

name: श्रीमत्सिंहासनेश्वरी
samasa: आसनेषु सिंहः (श्रेष्ठम्)। श्रीमच्च तत्सिंहासनं च श्रीमत्सिंहासनम्। तस्य ईश्वरी।
सिंहः आसनं यस्याः सा सिंहासना। सिंहासना च असौ ईश्वरी च।
wordByWord: श्रीमत् - splendid, auspicious, endowed with wealth.
सिंह - lion; also connected with power and majesty.
श्रीमत्सिंहासनस्य ईश्वरी - the sovereign of the auspicious lion-throne.
summary: (i) She controls and rules the world seated on a great grand throne.

(ii) The seat of a king is a throne - Simhasana. The seat of kings is designed as if the ruler is seated on a lion, because the lion is the king of animals. By virtue of Her position as a controller and ruler of World Empire, She is the lord of Simhasan, symbolises the wealth (Lakshmee) of the empire of the Universe and seated in this throne.

(iii) She herself rides on a lion and uses it as a seat. It also means that She is Simhavahinee - one who rides on a lion. According to Devee Puranam, the demon Mahishan was killed by Sreedevee riding on a lion. She thus acquired the names of Mahishagni (killer of Mahishan) and Simhasaneshwari.

(iv) The word Simha is derived from the verb Himsa. Simha is the corrupted form of Himsa. Thus Himsasaneshwari means, one who has the power to destroy the whole world - the third function of the Supreme Being, viz. to destroy, is indicated here. The Vedas refer to this as Yath Prayantyapi Samvisanthi.
(v) The previous name was Sreemaharajnee - the empress; it is therefore appropriate that she is seated on a Simhasana and is called Sreematsimhasaneshwaree.

(vi) At the end of the Devee Navavarga Pooja, a throne (Simhasana) with five steps is contemplated on the Bindu. Five forms of Sreedevee are imagined on each of these five steps in the four directions north-west, north-east, south-west, and south-east and in the centre. This worship is called Pancha Panchika. In the first step are seated the five Lakshmees viz. Sree Vidya Lakshmee, Lakshmee-Lakshmee, Mahalakshmee-Lakshmee, Trisakthi Lakshmee, and Sarvasamrajya Lakshmee. Since the throne on which Sreedevee is seated has these five Lakshmees, She is referred to as Sreematsimhasaneshwari.

(vii) There are also Mantras which carry the name Simhasana. These are to be contemplated on the four sides and in the centre of the Bindu as described below: Three in the East viz. Bala, Chaitanya, Bhairavi and Sampathprada Bhairavi. Five in the South viz. Kameshwari, Rakthanetra, Shatkuta Bhairavi, Aghora Bhairavi and Sampathprada Bhairavi. Seven in the West viz. Sanjeevani, Mrithyunjaya, Amruta Sanjeevani Bhairavi, Vaireshwari, Tripura Bhairavi, Bhayahari, and Sampathprada Bhairavi. Four in the North viz. DamarEshwari, Bhayadvamsini Bhairavi, Aghora Bhairavi and Sampathprada Bhairavi. Five in the Centre viz. Prathama Sundaree, Dwitheeya Sundaree, Tritheeya Sundaree, Chaturtha Sundaree and Panchama Sundaree.
The Mantras of these 24 Devatas are called Simhasana Mantras. Sreedevee is the lord (Eshwari) of these Mantras.

Thus, by the first three names, the three functions of creation, protection and destruction are indicated. Sreedevee has the name Panchakrityaparayana (274th name) - one who carries out five functions. The remaining two functions viz. Thirodhana and Anugraha will be dealt with later.
searchText: 3 श्रीमत्सिंहासनेश्वरी सिंहचिह्नितासनं सिंहासनम्। सिंहासनस्य ईश्वरी। आसनेषु सिंहः (श्रेष्ठम्)। श्रीमच्च तत्सिंहासनं च श्रीमत्सिंहासनम्। तस्य ईश्वरी। सिंहः आसनं यस्याः सा सिंहासना। सिंहासना च असौ ईश्वरी च। सिंहेन (हिंसया) असनं (क्षेपणं) सिंहासनम्। तत्र ईश्वरी (समर्था)। श्रीमती च असौ सिंहासनेश्वरी च। श्रीमत् - splendid, auspicious, endowed with wealth. सिंह - lion; also connected with power and majesty. आसन - seat, throne. सिंहासन - lion-throne, the royal throne. ईश्वरी - sovereign lady, ruler, controller. श्रीमत्सिंहासनस्य ईश्वरी - the sovereign of the auspicious lion-throne. (i) she controls and rules the world seated on a great grand throne. (ii) the seat of a king is a throne - simhasana. the seat of kings is designed as if the ruler is seated on a lion, because the lion is the king of animals. by virtue of her position as a controller and ruler of world empire, she is the lord of simhasan, symbolises the wealth (lakshmee) of the empire of the universe and seated in this throne. (iii) she herself rides on a lion and uses it as a seat. it also means that she is simhavahinee - one who rides on a lion. according to devee puranam, the demon mahishan was killed by sreedevee riding on a lion. she thus acquired the names of mahishagni (killer of mahishan) and simhasaneshwari. (iv) the word simha is derived from the verb himsa. simha is the corrupted form of himsa. thus himsasaneshwari means, one who has the power to destroy the whole world - the third function of the supreme being, viz. to destroy, is indicated here. the vedas refer to this as yath prayantyapi samvisanthi. (v) the previous name was sreemaharajnee - the empress; it is therefore appropriate that she is seated on a simhasana and is called sreematsimhasaneshwaree. (vi) at the end of the devee navavarga pooja, a throne (simhasana) with five steps is contemplated on the bindu. five forms of sreedevee are imagined on each of these five steps in the four directions north-west, north-east, south-west, and south-east and in the centre. this worship is called pancha panchika. in the first step are seated the five lakshmees viz. sree vidya lakshmee, lakshmee-lakshmee, mahalakshmee-lakshmee, trisakthi lakshmee, and sarvasamrajya lakshmee. since the throne on which sreedevee is seated has these five lakshmees, she is referred to as sreematsimhasaneshwari. (vii) there are also mantras which carry the name simhasana. these are to be contemplated on the four sides and in the centre of the bindu as described below: three in the east viz. bala, chaitanya, bhairavi and sampathprada bhairavi. five in the south viz. kameshwari, rakthanetra, shatkuta bhairavi, aghora bhairavi and sampathprada bhairavi. seven in the west viz. sanjeevani, mrithyunjaya, amruta sanjeevani bhairavi, vaireshwari, tripura bhairavi, bhayahari, and sampathprada bhairavi. four in the north viz. damareshwari, bhayadvamsini bhairavi, aghora bhairavi and sampathprada bhairavi. five in the centre viz. prathama sundaree, dwitheeya sundaree, tritheeya sundaree, chaturtha sundaree and panchama sundaree. the mantras of these 24 devatas are called simhasana mantras. sreedevee is the lord (eshwari) of these mantras. thus, by the first three names, the three functions of creation, protection and destruction are indicated. sreedevee has the name panchakrityaparayana (274th name) - one who carries out five functions. the remaining two functions viz. thirodhana and anugraha will be dealt with later. she is the supreme ruler and residing deity of the royal throne of consciousness. the lion denotes absolute dominion over the created universe. bhāskararāya aligns this with the śrī cakra, where her seat is composed of the highest cosmic principles (the sadāśiva-mahāpreta-padmāsana). it stands supreme above all other divine seats or states of yogic absorption. this directly maps her identity to the cosmic power that rides upon the lion (the vehicle of durgā / jagadambā), representing the absolute control and direction of the untamed, kinetic energy of the cosmos (rajo-guṇa). this addresses the saṃhāra-krama (the dissolution phase of consciousness). she is the sovereign power that completely eliminates samsāra, throwing away the limitations of names and forms to reveal the undifferentiated self. it confirms that her absolute rule over the throne of the universe is not dry or purely destructive, but is eternally infused with श्रीमद्—the nectarine, auspicious, and blissful light of pure awareness (cit-vilāsa).
esoteric: She is the supreme ruler and residing deity of the royal throne of consciousness. The lion denotes absolute dominion over the created universe.

Bhāskararāya aligns this with the Śrī Cakra, where Her seat is composed of the highest cosmic principles (the Sadāśiva-mahāpreta-padmāsana). It stands supreme above all other divine seats or states of yogic absorption.

This directly maps Her identity to the cosmic power that rides upon the lion (the vehicle of Durgā / Jagadambā), representing the absolute control and direction of the untamed, kinetic energy of the cosmos (Rajo-guṇa).

This addresses the Saṃhāra-krama (the dissolution phase of consciousness). She is the sovereign power that completely eliminates Samsāra, throwing away the limitations of names and forms to reveal the undifferentiated Self.

It confirms that Her absolute rule over the throne of the universe is not dry or purely destructive, but is eternally infused with श्रीमद्—the nectarine, auspicious, and blissful light of pure awareness (Cit-vilāsa).

name: चिदग्निकुण्डसम्भूता
samasa: अग्नेः कुण्डम् अग्निकुण्डम्। चिद् एव अग्निकुण्डं वा चिदरूपं अग्निकुण्डं वा चिदग्निकुण्डम्।
तस्मात् सम्भूता चिदाग्निकुण्डसम्भूता। अथवा तत्र सम्यक् (अभेदेन) भूता (स्थिता)।
wordByWord: चिदग्निकुण्ड - the sacrificial fire-pit of consciousness or knowledge.
सम्भूता - born, arisen, emerged, fully manifested.
चिदग्निकुण्डसम्भूता - She who emerged from the fire-pit of consciousness.
summary: (i) One, who emerged from the sacrificial fire of knowledge.

(ii) The word Chit means flawless Brahmam. It is called Chidagni as it is the enemy of the darkness of ignorance. Sreedevee emerged from the fire of Chit that is knowledge. In other words, Sreedevee’s real form emerges from the sacrificial fire of knowledge for the purpose of annihilating ignorance.
(iii) That knowledge as fire is mentioned in several places in Vedic and other spiritual literatures. For instance Gnanagni: Sarvakarmani Bhasmasat kurute tatha (Sreemad Bhagavat Geeta IV-37).

(iv) The legend of Bhandasura occurs in the same Brahmanda Purana where this Sahasranamam is also recited. Indra and other Devas performed a sacrifice to propitiate Sreedevee in order to destroy Bhandasura, who was harassing them. The sacrificial fire raised by them was called Chidagnikunda. Once the Devas started offering organs of their own bodies in the sacrificial fire, Sreedevee emerged from that pit (Kunda). The illusory “I” related to the physical and causal bodies, which are the root cause of self-esteem and arrogance (the false notions of “I”) was thrown into the fire by the Devas and Sreedevee, who is the real “I” emerged.

(v) There is one method of worship of Sreedevee called Rahooyaga. The fire of knowledge, which burns on its own without any fuel, is enhanced by adding the fuel of Vasanas. Attributes arising out of previous thoughts and actions and when the fire is aglow, all the good deeds, sins, thoughts, actions and duties are offered therein and the soul assumes the form of pure light, which is Sreedevee. (Refer 381st name - Rahooyagakramaradya). Antar nirantara nirindana medamanee moohandhakara paribandhini samvidagnow - this is the Mantra chant and the special argya is poured in the Samayika pooja after Tatvasodanam. This is the meaning of this name.
searchText: 4 चिदग्निकुण्डसम्भूता अग्नेः कुण्डम् अग्निकुण्डम्। चिद् एव अग्निकुण्डं वा चिदरूपं अग्निकुण्डं वा चिदग्निकुण्डम्। तस्मात् सम्भूता चिदाग्निकुण्डसम्भूता। अथवा तत्र सम्यक् (अभेदेन) भूता (स्थिता)। चित् - consciousness, knowledge, brahman. अग्नि - fire. कुण्ड - fire-pit, sacrificial pit. चिदग्निकुण्ड - the sacrificial fire-pit of consciousness or knowledge. सम्भूता - born, arisen, emerged, fully manifested. चिदग्निकुण्डसम्भूता - she who emerged from the fire-pit of consciousness. (i) one, who emerged from the sacrificial fire of knowledge. (ii) the word chit means flawless brahmam. it is called chidagni as it is the enemy of the darkness of ignorance. sreedevee emerged from the fire of chit that is knowledge. in other words, sreedevee’s real form emerges from the sacrificial fire of knowledge for the purpose of annihilating ignorance. (iii) that knowledge as fire is mentioned in several places in vedic and other spiritual literatures. for instance gnanagni: sarvakarmani bhasmasat kurute tatha (sreemad bhagavat geeta iv-37). (iv) the legend of bhandasura occurs in the same brahmanda purana where this sahasranamam is also recited. indra and other devas performed a sacrifice to propitiate sreedevee in order to destroy bhandasura, who was harassing them. the sacrificial fire raised by them was called chidagnikunda. once the devas started offering organs of their own bodies in the sacrificial fire, sreedevee emerged from that pit (kunda). the illusory “i” related to the physical and causal bodies, which are the root cause of self-esteem and arrogance (the false notions of “i”) was thrown into the fire by the devas and sreedevee, who is the real “i” emerged. esoteric: When Bhāskararāya decodes this pivotal name—which marks the literal manifestation narrative of Lalitā Tripura-sundarī out of the Mahā-yāga (the great cosmic sacrifice)—he bypasses the external mythological imagery to unveil a dynamic internal transformation:
I. The Alchemical Dissolution of Duality

In external rituals, objects are offered into a physical fire-pit to be consumed. Esoterically, the चिदग्निकुण्ड is the internal fire of pure awareness (Jñānāgni). The Sādhaka pours the entire manifest universe, along with their individual ego (Jīva-bhāva), into this fire as an oblation (Āhuti). When all sense of separation is completely burned away, what remains and emerges out of that absolute void of consciousness is the supreme deity Herself—चिदग्निकुण्डसम्भूता.
II. The Awakening of the Absolute Ego (Ahaṃtā)

Bhāskararāya emphasizes that Her emergence from the fire-pit signifies that She is not created; rather, She manifests when the limitations of the objective mind are dissolved. As the phrase "तत्र सम्यक् अभेदेन स्थिता" implies, She does not leave the fire; She is the very stability of that non-dual consciousness. She represents the pure, unconditioned cosmic I-consciousness (विमर्श / पूर्णांहता) that dawns when the dry intellect is consumed by spiritual fire.

name: देवकार्यसमुद्यता
wordByWord: देवकार्य - the task or purpose of the Devas.
समुद्यता - risen, come forward, prepared, engaged.
देवकार्यसमुद्यता - She who has come forward for the purpose of the Devas.
summary: (i) One, who came forward to service Devas.

(ii) Why should Sreedevee, who is of eternal existence incarnate or take a particular form? The purpose is to help the Devas achieve their objectives. Devanam Karyasidhyartham Avirbhavati Sa Yada Utpanneetitada Lookee Sa Nithyapyabhidheeyathee (Saptashatee). The objective of the Devas is to annihilate the demons like Bhandasura, Mahishasura, etc. Although She is eternal, She takes an incarnation every time to remove the grievances of the Devas.

(iii) Demons like Bhandasura are monsters bound by ignorance. Actions arising out of ignorance with selfish goals, take the form of Devils. Sreedevee takes different incarnations to release the Devas from the bondage of ignorance so that they can carry out their respective divine functions. The 4th and 5th names describe Sreedevee’s Chit (Prakasa) form. The description of the Vimarsa form follows.
searchText: 5 देवकार्यसमुद्यता देवानां कार्याणि देवकार्याणि। देवकार्याणां समुद्यता (शेषषष्ठ्या तदर्थं समुद्यता)। देव - devas, divine beings. कार्य - work, task, purpose. देवकार्य - the task or purpose of the devas. समुद्यता - risen, come forward, prepared, engaged. देवकार्यसमुद्यता - she who has come forward for the purpose of the devas. (i) one, who came forward to service devas. (iii) demons like bhandasura are monsters bound by ignorance. actions arising out of ignorance with selfish goals, take the form of devils. sreedevee takes different incarnations to release the devas from the bondage of ignorance so that they can carry out their respective divine functions. the 4th and 5th names describe sreedevee’s chit (prakasa) form. the description of the vimarsa form follows. when bhāskararāya parses देवकार्यसमुद्यता, he transitions the text from a mythological battle blueprint (the request of the gods to slay bhandāsura) into an active metaphysical and structural process within consciousness: i. the true identity of the "devas" in the esoteric vocabulary of the agamas and upaniṣads, the word देव (deva) is derived from the root दिव् (div - to shine). bhāskararāya establishes that the true devas are the internal sensory organs and faculties of perception (इन्द्रियाणि), which illuminate the external and internal worlds. the devakārya (task of the gods) is the liberation of these faculties from the tyrannical grip of भण्डासुर (bhaṇḍāsura, who represents cosmic limitation, ignorance, and the ego). she is samudyatā because her entire manifest nature is geared toward freeing the senses from bondage. ii. the dynamic architecture of grace (anugraha) the term समुद्यता signifies absolute readiness and immediate, active expression. bhāskararāya observes that while pure shiva remains in an unmoving, transcendent state (prakāśa), shakti is inherently samudyatā—creatively upraised. she represents the kinetic projection of divine grace (anugraha-śakti) that actively intervenes in the cosmos to restore equilibrium whenever the individual soul cries out for liberation. iii. the resolution of śeṣa-ṣaṣṭhī by utilizing śeṣa-ṣaṣṭhī ("the remaining genitive relations"), the grammar implies that her relationship to the divine work is not an external chore or an accidental duty. the work is not separate from her essence; rather, the protection, evolution, and ultimate dissolution of the universe back into the absolute is her natural, spontaneous play (līlā).
esoteric: When Bhāskararāya parses देवकार्यसमुद्यता, he transitions the text from a mythological battle blueprint (the request of the gods to slay Bhandāsura) into an active metaphysical and structural process within consciousness:
I. The True Identity of the "Devas"

In the esoteric vocabulary of the Agamas and Upaniṣads, the word देव (Deva) is derived from the root दिव् (div - to shine). Bhāskararāya establishes that the true Devas are the internal sensory organs and faculties of perception (इन्द्रियाणि), which illuminate the external and internal worlds. The Devakārya (task of the gods) is the liberation of these faculties from the tyrannical grip of भण्डासुर (Bhaṇḍāsura, who represents cosmic limitation, ignorance, and the ego). She is Samudyatā because Her entire manifest nature is geared toward freeing the senses from bondage.
II. The Dynamic Architecture of Grace (Anugraha)

The term समुद्यता signifies absolute readiness and immediate, active expression. Bhāskararāya observes that while pure Shiva remains in an unmoving, transcendent state (Prakāśa), Shakti is inherently Samudyatā—creatively upraised. She represents the kinetic projection of divine grace (Anugraha-śakti) that actively intervenes in the cosmos to restore equilibrium whenever the individual soul cries out for liberation.
III. The Resolution of Śeṣa-ṣaṣṭhī

By utilizing Śeṣa-ṣaṣṭhī ("the remaining genitive relations"), the grammar implies that Her relationship to the divine work is not an external chore or an accidental duty. The work is not separate from Her essence; rather, the protection, evolution, and ultimate dissolution of the universe back into the absolute is Her natural, spontaneous play (Līlā).

name: उद्यद्भानुसहस्राभा
samasa: उद्यतां भानूनां सहस्रम् उद्यद्भानुसहस्रम्। तस्य आभया तुल्या आभा यस्याः सा।
wordByWord: सहस्र - thousand; also infinite or countless.
उद्यद्भानुसहस्राभा - She whose lustre is like that of thousands of rising suns.
summary: (i) Her lustre is equal to thousands of rising Suns. The word Sahasram also means infinite.
(ii) Her lustre is equal to Infinite (thousands) rays of rising Suns.
(iii) The word Sahasram can be attributed to “Sun” or its “rays”. In that case Her splendor can be equated to countless (thousands) number of rising Suns or countless number of rays of a rising Sun.

(iv) The word Abha can be interpreted as “lustre” or “colour”. Sreedevee is famous both for her lustre as well as her Crimson colour. In the morning, the rising Sun graciously reduces his intensity of the heat emanating from his light, just for us to see his cherry coloured rays. Similarly Sreedevee is also of Crimson colour. This colour itself is called Aruna colour, one of the names of the Sun himself.
(v) Every mood or character has a colour. The colour of kindness or graciousness is Crimson.

(vi) In most of the places the Supreme Being is referred to as a column of light in scriptures: Na Tatra Suryo Bhati Na Chandra Tarakam; Jyotirmayam (Sakthi Mahimna Stooram); Jyotirmayee (Laghustuti); Many a place in Bhagavat Geeta; Vidyutdhamasamaprabham (Dyana Sloka of Sapta Sathi).

The Prakasa and Vimarsa forms Sreedevee have been described so far. The forthcoming names seek to detail three more forms viz. the Stula, Sukshma and Param forms. The Stula form is the one with human physical organs like hands, feet, etc. The Sukshma is in the form of Mantras. The Param is in the form of Vasanas. The Stula form is taken up first.

many a place in bhagavat geeta; vidyutdhamasamaprabham (dyana sloka of sapta sathi). the prakasa and vimarsa forms sreedevee have been described so far. the forthcoming names seek to detail three more forms viz. the stula, sukshma and param forms. the stula form is the one with human physical organs like hands, feet, etc. the sukshma is in the form of mantras. the param is in the form of vasanas. the stula form is taken up first. when bhāskararāya evaluates this precise structural form, he focuses heavily on the dynamic tension implied by उद्यताम् (actively rising) and सहस्रम् (infinite multiplication): i. the dynamic spanda of consciousness unlike a static sun sitting at its zenith, उद्यताम् signifies the precise moment of dawning—the transition from darkness to absolute illumination. in mantra-śāstra, this represents स्पन्द (spanda), the initial throb or kinetic leap of consciousness as it moves from the unmanifest (prakāśa) to manifest awareness (vimarśa). she is not merely light; esoteric: When Bhāskararāya evaluates this precise structural form, he focuses heavily on the dynamic tension implied by उद्यताम् (actively rising) and सहस्रम् (infinite multiplication):
I. The Dynamic Spanda of Consciousness

Unlike a static sun sitting at its zenith, उद्यताम् signifies the precise moment of dawning—the transition from darkness to absolute illumination. In Mantra-Śāstra, this represents स्पन्द (Spanda), the initial throb or kinetic leap of consciousness as it moves from the unmanifest (Prakāśa) to manifest awareness (Vimarśa). She is not merely light; She is the dynamic, active dawning of absolute reality within the practitioner's consciousness.
II. The Dissolution of Mental Obscurity

A single sun removes the darkness of the external world, but it leaves shadows. The imagery of a thousand rising suns shining simultaneously means there is no possibility of a shadow anywhere in the cosmos. Bhāskararāya notes that this represents the total, instantaneous destruction of मूलाविद्या (mūlāvidyā, root cosmic ignorance). When Her light dawns in the Dahara-ākāśa (the inner space of the heart), the illusion of division is instantly incinerated by the uniform red glow of non-dual awareness.
III. The Radiance of the Kāmakalā

The intense red luster of the rising suns specifically encrypts the red seed-potency of the Śrī Vidyā triad. Bhāskararāya frequently references that this specific shade of red (Aruṇa) represents unconditional compassion (Karuṇā) and absolute autonomy (Svātantrya). The infinite rays are the letters of the alphabet (Mātṛkās) spreading outward to construct the universe, all anchored in Her singular, self-luminous essence.

name: चतुर्बाहुसमन्विता
wordByWord: चतुर्बाहुसमन्विता - She who is endowed with four arms.
summary: (i) One with four hands.

(ii) Sreedevee has four hands - two from each shoulder in front and back. The four hands only have been indicated in the forms usually contemplated during meditation. Hands come first and foremost whenever we try to concentrate on the form mentioned in the Dhyana Sloka (meditation verse).
(iii) The four Devees who are the commandants of Sreedevee’s army viz. Ashwaruda, Sampatkaree, Mantreenee and Varahee, may be deemed to be the four hands of Sreedevee.
(iv) The Mantras pertaining to the above four Devees are indicated by this name.

(v) The Dhyana verse of this Sahasranamam mentions only two hands for Sreedevee. Preceding part may be referred for the meaning of the Dhyana Sloka (meditation verse). Mention was made about the four hands and followed by the weapons carried in these four hands.

(iv) the mantras pertaining to the above four devees are indicated by this name. they are the natural, spontaneous structural expansions of her non-dual essence as she projects outward from the unmanifest point into cosmic play (līlā).
esoteric: When Bhāskararāya parses चतुर्बाहुसमन्विता, he moves beyond the anthropomorphic visual representation to reveal the underlying cosmic architecture and internal psychological framework:
I. The Mastery Over the Antaḥkaraṇa (Internal Organs)
Bhāskararāya establishes that the four arms are the physical symbols of the four divisions of the internal psyche (अन्तःकरणचतुष्टय).
The Four Faculties: मनस् (Manas - the deliberating mind), बुद्धि (Buddhi - the decisive intellect), चित्त (Citta - the memory/retentive consciousness), and अहङ्कार (Ahaṅkāra - the ego-sense).

By being perfectly integrated with these four arms, She signifies that the individual human psyche is merely a limited reflection of Her four cosmic vectors of awareness. She holds absolute command over the psychological mechanism of every living being.
II. The Structural Anchors of the Śrī Cakra
In the ritual mapping of the Śrī Cakra, the four arms correspond directly to the weapons She wields, which occupy specific spatial directions in the central chamber (Bindu-cakra).
The arms represent the deployment of Her primordial cosmic energies: इच्छा (Icchā - Will), ज्ञान (Jñāna - Knowledge), and क्रिया (Kriyā - Action).

The four arms represent the four standard paths of Vedic revelation (the four Vedas) and the four absolute states of consciousness (Jāgrat, Svapna, Suṣupti, and Turīya), showing that She encompasses both the transactional states of experience and the transcendent reality.
III. The Resolution of Samanvitā

The prefix सम् (sam) implies perfection and absolute equilibrium, while अन्विता (anvitā) implies an inseparable, intrinsic connection (anvaya). Bhāskararāya notes that these four arms are not accidental appendages attached to Her form; they are the natural, spontaneous structural expansions of Her non-dual essence as She projects outward from the unmanifest point into cosmic play (Līlā).

name: रागस्वरूपपाशाढ्या
samasa: राग एव स्वं रूपः यस्य पाशस्य सः रागस्वरूपपाशः। तेन आढ्या।
wordByWord: आढ्या - endowed with, rich in, furnished with.
रागस्वरूपपाशाढ्या - She who is furnished with the noose whose very form is desire.
summary: (i) One who carries the weapon of desire in the form of a noose.
(ii) A noose is a rope thrown to tie and drag something. Sreedevee is holding both the ends of the noose in one hand. It is in the Stula form in her rear left arm. The presiding deity is Ashwaruda.

(iii) Raga and Anuraga are the two features of mind - wish and desire. This is the Vasana form of the weapon noose. This form has the twin opposite characters viz. love and hatred. Vasana is the effect of the deeds of previous births of Jeeva. In the hands of Sreedevee these take the form of Iccha Sakti and Gnana Sakti.

(iv) The noose helps us to round up and drag a distant object to oneself. We will be able to do this only if both the ends of the noose are under our control. Sreedevee holds both the ends of the noose in her hand. This indicates that “our wishes will get fulfilled automatically by Her grace.”

(ii) a noose is a rope thrown to tie and drag something. sreedevee is holding both the ends of the noose in one hand. it is in the stula form in her rear left arm. the presiding deity is ashwaruda. (iii) raga and anuraga are the two features of mind - wish and desire. this is the vasana form of the weapon noose. this form has the twin opposite characters viz. love and hatred. vasana is the effect of the deeds of previous births of jeeva. in the hands of sreedevee these take the form of iccha sakti and gnana sakti. (iv) the noose helps us to round up and drag a distant object to oneself. we will be able to do this only if both the ends of the noose are under our control. sreedevee holds both the ends of the noose in her hand. this indicates that “our wishes will get fulfilled automatically by her grace.” in the saubhāgyabhāskara, bhāskararāya completely deconstructs the conventional concept of a weapon (pāśa) used to bind an enemy, transmuting it into a profound non-dual instrument of liberation: i. the transmutation of rāga (desire) for ordinary bound souls (jīvas), rāga is an afflictive mental modification (kleśa) that binds them tightly to the objective wheel of births and deaths. however, bhāskararāya explains that when this same power belongs to the divine mother, it is इच्छाशक्ति (icchā-śakti—the primordial will of the absolute). it is the pure, cosmic desire to manifest, play, and ultimately reclaim all projected beings. by holding this noose, she demonstrates that she controls the very fabric of cosmic attraction. ii. the noose as the gravity of grace linguistically, a noose catches, pulls, and restrains an object that is moving away. bhāskararāya asserts that her noose represents the inward-pulling gravity of her divine grace (anugraha). when the practitioner's mind wanders outward into the multi-colored illusions of the world, her pāśa catches the outward-bound consciousness and pulls it firmly back toward the silent center—the bindu. iii. the structural placement in worshipping the śrī cakra bhāskararāya notes that this weapon is held in her lower-left hand and is structurally related to the element of space and sound. in the ritual meditation (dhyāna), the sādhaka visualizes this noose not as a rope of bondage, but as a radiant, loop of absolute crimson light that represents the ultimate beauty and captivating power of the self (ātma-rati).
esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara, Bhāskararāya completely deconstructs the conventional concept of a weapon (pāśa) used to bind an enemy, transmuting it into a profound non-dual instrument of liberation:
I. The Transmutation of Rāga (Desire)

For ordinary bound souls (jīvas), rāga is an afflictive mental modification (kleśa) that binds them tightly to the objective wheel of births and deaths. However, Bhāskararāya explains that when this same power belongs to the Divine Mother, it is इच्छाशक्ति (Icchā-śakti—the primordial Will of the Absolute). It is the pure, cosmic desire to manifest, play, and ultimately reclaim all projected beings. By holding this noose, She demonstrates that She controls the very fabric of cosmic attraction.
II. The Noose as the Gravity of Grace

Linguistically, a noose catches, pulls, and restrains an object that is moving away. Bhāskararāya asserts that Her noose represents the inward-pulling gravity of Her divine grace (anugraha). When the practitioner's mind wanders outward into the multi-colored illusions of the world, Her pāśa catches the outward-bound consciousness and pulls it firmly back toward the silent center—the Bindu.
III. The Structural Placement in Worshipping the Śrī Cakra

Bhāskararāya notes that this weapon is held in Her lower-left hand and is structurally related to the element of space and sound. In the ritual meditation (dhyāna), the Sādhaka visualizes this noose not as a rope of bondage, but as a radiant, loop of absolute crimson light that represents the ultimate beauty and captivating power of the Self (Ātma-rati).

name: क्रोधाकाराङ्कुशोज्ज्वला
samasa: क्रोधस्य आकार इव आकारः यस्य अङ्कुशस्य सः क्रोधाकाराङ्कुशः। तेन उज्ज्वला।
wordByWord: आकार - form, shape; also knowledge as the form taken by the mind.
क्रोधाकाराङ्कुशोज्ज्वला - She who shines with the goad whose form is anger.
summary: (i) Her lustre is enhanced by holding the goad symbolising anger.

(ii) Goad is a pointed weapon used by a mahout to control an elephant. It has a sharp edge. Elephant is one of the biggest and strongest among all animals. It is extremely difficult to control a rogue elephant. Goad is the only weapon which can control it. In the hands of Sreedevee it is in a Stula form.

(iii) Its Vasana form is anger. If the mind is not able to get what it wants or does not derive the satisfaction expected out of it the mental reaction is anger. It is symbolised by the lustrous goad worn by Sreedevee.

(iv) Krodhakarankusham can be interpreted in two ways - (1) Goad in the form of anger or (2) Anger and Akara (knowledge). How is Akara interpreted as knowledge? When we see an object through our eyes, our mind recognises and takes the “form” (photo print) of that object - that is Akara. If we see a book, our mind recognises the form of the book and becomes that form (Akara) itself. Then only we understand that what we see is a book or have the knowledge that it is a book. Recognition of all the objects by the mind taking the form (Akara) of that object. Hence the word Akara gives the meaning as knowledge.
(v) Anger is a state of mind arising out of the knowledge of an object. Sreedevee adorns the goad that controls anger. Knowledge about an object is destroyed in order to annihilate anger.

(vi) A weapon will act as intended by the person who takes aim. Here these weapons will act as per the wish of Sreedevee. For worshippers of Sreedevee, She will make the “desire” and “hatred” act according to the wishes of the devotees and ultimately raise them to Her level.

(v) anger is a state of mind arising out of the knowledge of an object. sreedevee adorns the goad that controls anger. knowledge about an object is destroyed in order to annihilate anger. (vi) a weapon will act as intended by the person who takes aim. here these weapons will act as per the wish of sreedevee. for worshippers of sreedevee, she will make the “desire” and “hatred” act according to the wishes of the devotees and ultimately raise them to her level. in the *saubhāgyabhāskara*, bhāskararāya decodes the goad (*aṅkuśa*) as **क्रोध** (aversion or anger)[cite: 1]. * **the power of restraint:** while anger in ordinary mortals is a binding mental modification, in her hands, the goad represents the power of **ज्ञानशक्ति** (*jñāna-śakti* — the power of pure discernment). * **cosmic correction:** just as a goad keeps an elephant on the right track, her goad reins in the outward-bound senses, stopping the mental fluctuations and redirecting the mind toward inner absorption (*samādhi*).
esoteric: In the *Saubhāgyabhāskara*, Bhāskararāya decodes the goad (*aṅkuśa*) as **क्रोध** (aversion or anger)[cite: 1].

* **The Power of Restraint:** While anger in ordinary mortals is a binding mental modification, in Her hands, the goad represents the power of **ज्ञानशक्ति** (*Jñāna-śakti* — the power of pure discernment).

* **Cosmic Correction:** Just as a goad keeps an elephant on the right track, Her goad reins in the outward-bound senses, stopping the mental fluctuations and redirecting the mind toward inner absorption (*Samādhi*).

name: मनोरूपेक्षुकोदण्डा
samasa: मन एव रूपं यस्य इक्षोः सः मनोरूपेक्षुः। इक्षुमयं कोदण्डं यस्याः सा।
wordByWord: मनोरूपेक्षु - sugarcane whose form is mind.
मनोरूपेक्षुकोदण्डा - She who bears the sugarcane bow whose form is mind.
summary: (i) She dons a bow made of sugarcane - the physical form of “mind”.
(ii) Sreedevee has in her left forearm a bow made of sugarcane. Its symbolic form is the mind. Its presiding deity is Mantrini.

(iii) The action of the mind executed through the five organs is to take the form of the object seen and realise that “I see the object”, “This can give me pleasure”, “I desire to get this object”, “This gives me sorrow and hence I should avoid it” and the like. This is called Sankalpa. At the same time the thought “Is this object suitable for me or not”, “Should I try to get it or not”, and the like are called Vikalpa. The action of the mind is of these two types. This mind takes the form of sugarcane bow in the hands of Sreedevee.

(iv) In general a bow is made up of completely dry bamboo slats and is capable of being bent and unbent. Sugarcane is full of moisture; Sugarcane bow is a weapon, though it is sweet; again it is sweet though it is a weapon. It shows that Sreedevee’s mind is full of benignity. Is it not Her mind full of sweetness and graciousness?

(iii) the action of the mind executed through the five organs is to take the form of the object seen and realise that “i see the object”, “this can give me pleasure”, “i desire to get this object”, “this gives me sorrow and hence i should avoid it” and the like. this is called sankalpa. at the same time the thought “is this object suitable for me or not”, “should i try to get it or not”, and the like are called vikalpa. the action of the mind is of these two types. this mind takes the form of sugarcane bow in the hands of sreedevee. (iv) in general a bow is made up of completely dry bamboo slats and is capable of being bent and unbent. sugarcane is full of moisture; sugarcane bow is a weapon, though it is sweet; again it is sweet though it is a weapon. it shows that sreedevee’s mind is full of benignity. is it not her mind full of sweetness and graciousness? bhāskararāya notes that the sugarcane bow represents **मनस्** (*manas*), the choosing and doubting mind. * **the sweetness of the mind:** sugarcane must be crushed and processed to yield its sweet juice; similarly, the mind must be systematically refined through spiritual discipline to experience its inherent, nectarine bliss (*svātmānanda*). * **the seat of kriyā-śakti:** because she holds the mind as an instrument (*kodaṇḍa*), she proves that the cosmic mind is entirely under her command as **क्रियाशक्ति** (*kriyā-śakti* — the power of action).
esoteric: Bhāskararāya notes that the sugarcane bow represents **मनस्** (*Manas*), the choosing and doubting mind.

* **The Sweetness of the Mind:** Sugarcane must be crushed and processed to yield its sweet juice; similarly, the mind must be systematically refined through spiritual discipline to experience its inherent, nectarine bliss (*Svātmānanda*).

* **The Seat of Kriyā-śakti:** Because She holds the mind as an instrument (*kodaṇḍa*), She proves that the cosmic mind is entirely under Her command as **क्रियाशक्ति** (*Kriyā-śakti* — the power of action).

name: पञ्चतन्मात्रसायका
samasa: पञ्च तन्मात्राणि एव सायकाः यस्याः सा।
wordByWord: तन्मात्राणि - subtle sense-elements; sound, touch, form/colour, taste, smell.
पञ्चतन्मात्रसायका - She whose arrows are the five tanmatras.
summary: (i) One who has five characteristics as arrows.

(ii) In her physical form, Sreedevee has five flowers as arrows in her right forearm viz. Lotus, Red Lotus, Red Lily, Red Jasmine and Mango flower. The characteristics of these arrows are the effect they make on the objects attacked by them viz. to induce happiness, to give taste, to enthrall, to dry out and to kill. The presiding deity of these arrows is Varahi also known as Dandanatha.

(iii) The secret form of these arrows is the five self-characteristics (Tanmatras). These characteristics are the unique features of the five elements, which constitute the physical earth. Elements (Bhutas) and Self-Characteristics (Tanmatras): Space (Akasa) - Sound (Shabda); Wind / Air (Vayu) - Sense of touch (Sparsa); Fire (Agni) - Form / Colour (Rupa); Water (Jala) - Taste (Rasa); Earth (Prithvi) - Odour (Gandha). These characteristics stand alone only in the very intricate and atomic state of the elements. The Universe, we perceive is the composite form of these elements and hence each element cannot be perceived separately. Before the evolution of the universe, from the Absolute (Mulaprakriti), i.e. when matter and energy are in a state of indistinguishable union, the elements could be identified by their individual characteristics - space by sound, air by sense of touch and so on. In this state these are called Tanmatras.

(iv) The arrows hit their targets and give their desired effect, as intended by the person who aims them. A sincere devotee targeted by Sreedevee’s arrow will experience the good and pleasurable effects of these characteristics. If the devotee is insincere (bad in intention), the arrows also will give only undesirable effects such as agitation or disturbance, maddening or intoxication, etc.

(v) These arrows have three forms in three different states - in the physical (Stula) state, they take the form of flowers; in the secret (Sukshma) state, they are Mantras; and in the Vasana state they take the form of Para, a form of sound.

(vi) Sreedevee also has weapons - Sugarcane as bow and flowers as arrow, like Cupid (Manmatha). Hence She is called as Lalita or Kameshwari. As the emotion of love (Sringara rasa) is important to Her, She is endowed with beautiful things as weapons.

(vii) The presiding deity of sugarcane bow is Mantrini representing the power of intellect (Buddhi Sakti) and the presiding deity of arrows is Dandanatha - commander-in-chief representing the physical power. The Vag Devees symbolically emphasise that intellect and power should act in unison.
(viii) Mantras of the weapons described are encompassed in these names 8 to 11. These are so secret that they should be learnt through a proper teacher.

When She shoots these floral arrows into the practitioner's heart, they pierce the outer sensory layer and dissolve objective experience, returning the senses to their pure, uncompounded state within consciousness.

name: निजारुणप्रभापूरमज्जद्ब्रह्माण्डमण्डला
wordByWord: निजारुणप्रभापूरमज्जद्ब्रह्माण्डमण्डला - She in whose own crimson lustre the spheres of universes are immersed.
summary: (i) One who immerses the zones of galaxy by the flow of her lustre.

(ii) Our Earth, space and the stellar zone constitute one galaxy (Brahmandam). Universe (Brahmandamandalam) consists of millions of such galaxies. Sreedevee sinks this entire universe in her red lustre. Again the red lustre alone shines immersing them all.
(iii) The physical form of Sreedevee’s red lustre is described in the ensuing names. This description starts from Her hair and ends in Her feet.
searchText: 12 निजारुणप्रभापूरमज्जद्ब्रह्माण्डमण्डला निजः (स्वकीयः) यः अरुणप्रभायाः पूरः निजारुणप्रभापूरः। तस्मिन् मज्जन्तीति निजारुणप्रभापूरमज्जानि। तेषामेव ब्रह्माण्डानां मण्डलानि यस्याः सा। निज - own, natural. अरुण - red, crimson. प्रभा - lustre, light. पूर - flood, fullness, flow. मज्जत् - sinking, immersed. ब्रह्माण्ड - universe, cosmic egg, galaxy. मण्डल - sphere, zone, circle. निजारुणप्रभापूरमज्जद्ब्रह्माण्डमण्डला - she in whose own crimson lustre the spheres of universes are immersed. (i) one who immerses the zones of galaxy by the flow of her lustre. (ii) our earth, space and the stellar zone constitute one galaxy (brahmandam). universe (brahmandamandalam) consists of millions of such galaxies. sreedevee sinks this entire universe in her red lustre. again the red lustre alone shines immersing them all. (iii) the physical form of sreedevee’s red lustre is described in the ensuing names. this description starts from her hair and ends in her feet. bhāskararāya uses this name to prove *advaita-vāda* (non-duality) and the primacy of *shakti*: * **the red flood of vimarśa:** the red glow represents **विमर्श** (*vimarśa* — self-reflective consciousness). the universe is not a dry, external entity sitting apart from her. it is entirely suspended, sustained, and dissolved (*majjat*) inside her infinite, self-luminous red light of awareness.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya uses this name to prove *Advaita-vāda* (non-duality) and the primacy of *Shakti*:

* **The Red Flood of Vimarśa:** The red glow represents **विमर्श** (*Vimarśa* — self-reflective consciousness). The universe is not a dry, external entity sitting apart from Her. It is entirely suspended, sustained, and dissolved (*majjat*) inside Her infinite, self-luminous red light of awareness.

name: चम्पकाशोकपुन्नागसौगन्धिकलसत्कचा
wordByWord: सौगन्धिक - fragrant, red lotus / fragrant flower.
चम्पकाशोकपुन्नागसौगन्धिकलसत्कचा - She whose hair shines adorned with fragrant flowers such as Champaka, Ashoka, Punnaga and Red Lotus.
summary: (i) One who has beautiful hair studded with Magnolia, Asoka, Rhizophora mucronata (Punnaga) and Red Lotus flowers.
(ii) It is Sreedevee’s hair bedecked with flowers, which emerges first out of the sacrificial fire; hence the description of the Sreedevee’s physical form commences with the hair.
(iii) The first part of Her mantra is equated with Her face. Hence the description starts with the hair. (for instance 85th name - Sreemad Vagbhava Kootikaswarupa Mukapankaja).
searchText: 13 चम्पकाशोकपुन्नागसौगन्धिकलसत्कचा चम्पकानि च अशोकाश्च पुन्नागाश्च चम्पकाशोकपुन्नागाः। तैः लसन्तः कचाः यस्याः सा। चम्पक - champaka / magnolia flower. अशोक - ashoka flower. पुन्नाग - punnaga flower. सौगन्धिक - fragrant, red lotus / fragrant flower. लसत् - shining, bedecked, adorned. कचा - hair. चम्पकाशोकपुन्नागसौगन्धिकलसत्कचा - she whose hair shines adorned with fragrant flowers such as champaka, ashoka, punnaga and red lotus. (i) one who has beautiful hair studded with magnolia, asoka, rhizophora mucronata (punnaga) and red lotus flowers. (ii) it is sreedevee’s hair bedecked with flowers, which emerges first out of the sacrificial fire; hence the description of the sreedevee’s physical form commences with the hair. (iii) the first part of her mantra is equated with her face. hence the description starts with the hair. (for instance 85th name - sreemad vagbhava kootikaswarupa mukapankaja). this name initiates the *keśādi-pāda-varṇana* (head-to-toe description) of the devi. * **esoteric hair as chidākāśa:** the dark, dense tresses (*kaca*) represent the unmanifest primordial cosmic space (*chidākāśa*). the shining, fragrant flowers represent the active, manifest seeds of spiritual wisdom and virtue blossoming within the crown chakra (**सहस्रार** — *sahasrāra*).
esoteric: This name initiates the *Keśādi-pāda-varṇana* (head-to-toe description) of the Devi.

* **Esoteric Hair as Chidākāśa:** The dark, dense tresses (*kaca*) represent the unmanifest primordial cosmic space (*Chidākāśa*). The shining, fragrant flowers represent the active, manifest seeds of spiritual wisdom and virtue blossoming within the crown chakra (**सहस्रार** — *Sahasrāra*).

name: कुरुविन्दमणिश्रेणीकनत्कोटीरमण्डिता
wordByWord: कुरुविन्दमणिश्रेणीकनत्कोटीरमण्डिता - She who is adorned with a crown glittering with rows of ruby stones.
summary: (i) Kuruvindamani is the precious stone Ruby. Sreedevee’s crown is studded with a string of Rubies.
(ii) Adorning of rubies will result in fulfillment of desires. Attachment will be created to good things; desire, affection, welfare and devotion to Sree Vishnu also.

(iii) Sreedevee’s crown is of Koteera model. This is a particular design, which does not hide beauty of the hair as well as the flowers adorned. The crowns worn by men will hide the hair and is called Makuta, Mukuta or Kireeta.
(iv) The red rubies match the red lustre of Sreedevee’s body.
(v) Meditation of Sreedevee’s face with the crown will increase the devotion.
searchText: 14 कुरुविन्दमणिश्रेणीकनत्कोटीरमण्डिता कुरुविन्दमणीनां श्रेणी कुरुविन्दमणिश्रेणी। तया कनत् (दीप्यमानः) कोटीरः (मुकुटः) कुरुविन्दमणिश्रेणीकनत्कोटीरः। तेन मण्डिता। कुरुविन्दमणि - ruby gemstone. श्रेणी - row, string, series. कनत् - shining, glittering. कोटीर - crown. मण्डिता - adorned. कुरुविन्दमणिश्रेणीकनत्कोटीरमण्डिता - she who is adorned with a crown glittering with rows of ruby stones. (i) kuruvindamani is the precious stone ruby. sreedevee’s crown is studded with a string of rubies. (ii) adorning of rubies will result in fulfillment of desires. attachment will be created to good things; desire, affection, welfare and devotion to sree vishnu also. (iii) sreedevee’s crown is of koteera model. this is a particular design, which does not hide beauty of the hair as well as the flowers adorned. the crowns worn by men will hide the hair and is called makuta, mukuta or kireeta. (iv) the red rubies match the red lustre of sreedevee’s body. (v) meditation of sreedevee’s face with the crown will increase the devotion. * **sovereignty of the unmanī:** *kuruvinda* denotes a rare class of crimson ruby. the crown (*koṭīra*) sits above the head, symbolizing the transcendent state above the sahasrāra—known as **उन्मनी** (*unmanī*) or the state of pure, non-dual sovereignty over all creation.
esoteric: * **Sovereignty of the Unmanī:** *Kuruvinda* denotes a rare class of crimson ruby. The crown (*koṭīra*) sits above the head, symbolizing the transcendent state above the Sahasrāra—known as **उन्मनी** (*unmanī*) or the state of pure, non-dual sovereignty over all creation.

name: अष्टमीचन्द्रविभ्राजदलिकस्थलशोभिता
wordByWord: अष्टमीचन्द्रविभ्राजदलिकस्थलशोभिता - She whose forehead shines like the moon of the eighth lunar day.
summary: (i) One whose forehead shines like the Moon on the eighth day of lunar half month.

(ii) Of the fifteen phases of the moon, on the eighth day of bright lunar half month the eighth phase will be seen. Or reduced upto eight phases on the eighth day of the lunar dark half month and moon will be visible as a semicircle. In the emerging form of Sreedevee from the sacrificial fire, the crown, hair and flowers come out one after the other and the forehead emerges as an inverted semicircle resembling the eighth day moon, with flawless cool light.
(iii) This can be compared to 46th verse of Soundaryalaharee starting with Lalatam Lavanyadvithi Vimalamabhati.
searchText: 15 अष्टमीचन्द्रविभ्राजदलिकस्थलशोभिता अष्टम्यां चन्द्रः अष्टमीचन्द्रः। अलिकस्य (ललाटस्य) स्थलम् अलिकस्थलम्। अष्टमीचन्द्रेण इव विभ्राजता अलिकस्थलेन शोभिता। अष्टमी - eighth lunar day. चन्द्र - moon. विभ्राजत् - shining brilliantly. अलिकस्थल - forehead. शोभिता - beautified, made splendid. अष्टमीचन्द्रविभ्राजदलिकस्थलशोभिता - she whose forehead shines like the moon of the eighth lunar day. (i) one whose forehead shines like the moon on the eighth day of lunar half month. (ii) of the fifteen phases of the moon, on the eighth day of bright lunar half month the eighth phase will be seen. or reduced upto eight phases on the eighth day of the lunar dark half month and moon will be visible as a semicircle. in the emerging form of sreedevee from the sacrificial fire, the crown, hair and flowers come out one after the other and the forehead emerges as an inverted semicircle resembling the eighth day moon, with flawless cool light. (iii) this can be compared to 46th verse of soundaryalaharee starting with lalatam lavanyadvithi vimalamabhati. * **the light of the ājñā:** the eighth-day moon is a perfect, balanced semi-circle. the forehead represents the seat of the **आज्ञा चक्र** (*ājñā cakra*), the gateway of intuitive knowledge. it represents the *kāmakaḷā* structure, wherein the crescent moon represents the upper dynamic arc holding the equilibrium of shiva and shakti.
esoteric: * **The Light of the Ājñā:** The eighth-day moon is a perfect, balanced semi-circle. The forehead represents the seat of the **आज्ञा चक्र** (*Ājñā Cakra*), the gateway of intuitive knowledge. It represents the *Kāmakaḷā* structure, wherein the crescent moon represents the upper dynamic arc holding the equilibrium of Shiva and Shakti.

name: मुखचन्द्रकलङ्काभमृगनाभिविशेषका
samasa: मुखम् एव चन्द्र इति रूपकं तत्कलङ्कत्वेन तुल्यः मृगनाभेः (कस्तूर्याः) विशेषकः (तिलकः) यस्याः सा।
wordByWord: मुखचन्द्रकलङ्काभमृगनाभिविशेषका - She whose musk tilaka resembles the dark mark on the moon-like face.
summary: (i) The bindi (musk dot) on the forehead of Sreedevee resembles the black spot on the surface of the Moon and it further beautifies the face.

(ii) It is a literary tradition to consider the black spot on the moon as a stain on the life of the Moon god. The Moon is sometimes described as Mruganka, as the stain looks like a deer and as Sasanka as the stain also looks like a rabbit. When Sreedevee’s forehead is described as a semicircular Moon (crescent), there is no stain, but the musk spot beautifies the face.
searchText: 16 मुखचन्द्रकलङ्काभमृगनाभिविशेषका मुखम् एव चन्द्र इति रूपकं तत्कलङ्कत्वेन तुल्यः मृगनाभेः (कस्तूर्याः) विशेषकः (तिलकः) यस्याः सा। मुख - face. चन्द्र - moon. कलङ्क - dark mark, spot. आभ - resembling. मृगनाभि - musk. विशेषक - tilaka, decorative mark. मुखचन्द्रकलङ्काभमृगनाभिविशेषका - she whose musk tilaka resembles the dark mark on the moon-like face. (i) the bindi (musk dot) on the forehead of sreedevee resembles the black spot on the surface of the moon and it further beautifies the face. (ii) it is a literary tradition to consider the black spot on the moon as a stain on the life of the moon god. the moon is sometimes described as mruganka, as the stain looks like a deer and as sasanka as the stain also looks like a rabbit. when sreedevee’s forehead is described as a semicircular moon (crescent), there is no stain, but the musk spot beautifies the face. bhāskararāya offers a profound interpretation of the "blemish" (*kalaṅka*): * **māyā as an ornament:** the dark spot on the moon is traditionally a flaw, but on the face of consciousness (the devi), the black musk mark (*tilaka*) increases her beauty. this represents **माया** (*māyā*). to the bound soul, *māyā* is a blemish of ignorance; but to the devi, it is a mere decorative ornament of her creative play (*līlā*), entirely under her sovereignty.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya offers a profound interpretation of the "blemish" (*kalaṅka*):

* **Māyā as an Ornament:** The dark spot on the moon is traditionally a flaw, but on the face of consciousness (the Devi), the black musk mark (*tilaka*) increases Her beauty. This represents **माया** (*Māyā*). To the bound soul, *Māyā* is a blemish of ignorance; but to the Devi, it is a mere decorative ornament of Her creative play (*Līlā*), entirely under Her sovereignty.

name: वदनस्मरमाङ्गल्यगृहतोरणचिल्लिका
samasa: वदनम् एव स्मरस्य माङ्गल्यगृहं तस्य तोरण एव चिल्लिका (भ्रूलता) यस्याः सा।
wordByWord: वदनस्मरमाङ्गल्यगृहतोरणचिल्लिका - She whose eyebrows are the festoons at the auspicious house of Cupid, which is Her face.
summary: (i) One whose eyebrows look like festoons in front of Cupid’s house which is Sreedevee’s face.

(ii) The roof at the entrance of the houses will be constructed in a raised manner so that the house is not affected by rain or Sunshine. This is called festoon. Sreedevee’s face is so beautiful as to infatuate Sree Kameshwara. Hence Cupid uses this face as an office for his auspicious activities. The eyebrows look like beautiful festoons in front of this house (office).
searchText: 17 वदनस्मरमाङ्गल्यगृहतोरणचिल्लिका वदनम् एव स्मरस्य माङ्गल्यगृहं तस्य तोरण एव चिल्लिका (भ्रूलता) यस्याः सा। वदन - face. स्मर - cupid, manmatha. माङ्गल्यगृह - auspicious house. तोरण - festoon, arch at entrance. चिल्लिका - eyebrow-creeper. वदनस्मरमाङ्गल्यगृहतोरणचिल्लिका - she whose eyebrows are the festoons at the auspicious house of cupid, which is her face. (i) one whose eyebrows look like festoons in front of cupid’s house which is sreedevee’s face. (ii) the roof at the entrance of the houses will be constructed in a raised manner so that the house is not affected by rain or sunshine. this is called festoon. sreedevee’s face is so beautiful as to infatuate sree kameshwara. hence cupid uses this face as an office for his auspicious activities. the eyebrows look like beautiful festoons in front of this house (office). * **the primal throb (spanda):** *smara* (cupid) is the cosmic desire for manifestation. her face is the palace where this auspicious creation begins. the curve of her eyebrows (*cillikā*) represents the creative tension or vibration (**स्पन्द** — *spanda*) that triggers the projection of space and time.
esoteric: * **The Primal Throb (Spanda):** *Smara* (Cupid) is the cosmic desire for manifestation. Her face is the palace where this auspicious creation begins. The curve of Her eyebrows (*cillikā*) represents the creative tension or vibration (**स्पन्द** — *Spanda*) that triggers the projection of space and time.

name: वक्त्रलक्ष्मीपरीवाहचलन्मीनाभलोचना
samasa: वक्त्रलक्ष्म्याः मुखकान्तेः परीवाहे चलद्भ्यां मीनाभ्यां तुल्ये लोचने यस्याः सा।
wordByWord: वक्त्रलक्ष्मीपरीवाहचलन्मीनाभलोचना - She whose eyes resemble fish moving in the stream of the beauty of Her face.
summary: (i) One whose eyes look like fish swimming in the stream of beauty of Sreedevee’s face.

(ii) The beauty of Sreedevee’s face undergoes momentary changes and this continuous change looks like a steady and slow stream. The motion of Sreedevee’s two eyes is compared to the fast movement of fish in this stream, which increases its beauty.

(iii) Fish are not mammals but lay eggs. It is believed that fish nurse their offspring just by affectionate looks. Similarly, Sreedevee’s eyes, which are compared to fish swimming in the stream of beauty uplifts Her devotees just by a gracious look. She is therefore called Meenakshi, here referred to as Meenabhalochana.
searchText: 18 वक्त्रलक्ष्मीपरीवाहचलन्मीनाभलोचना वक्त्रलक्ष्म्याः मुखकान्तेः परीवाहे चलद्भ्यां मीनाभ्यां तुल्ये लोचने यस्याः सा। वक्त्र - face. लक्ष्मी - beauty, splendour. परीवाह - stream, flow. चलत् - moving. मीन - fish. आभ - resembling. लोचना - eyes. वक्त्रलक्ष्मीपरीवाहचलन्मीनाभलोचना - she whose eyes resemble fish moving in the stream of the beauty of her face. (i) one whose eyes look like fish swimming in the stream of beauty of sreedevee’s face. (ii) the beauty of sreedevee’s face undergoes momentary changes and this continuous change looks like a steady and slow stream. the motion of sreedevee’s two eyes is compared to the fast movement of fish in this stream, which increases its beauty. (iii) fish are not mammals but lay eggs. it is believed that fish nurse their offspring just by affectionate looks. similarly, sreedevee’s eyes, which are compared to fish swimming in the stream of beauty uplifts her devotees just by a gracious look. she is therefore called meenakshi, here referred to as meenabhalochana. * **solar and lunar currents:** fish animate the water in which they swim. esoterically, the two eyes represent the cosmic tracks of the sun and the moon, or the twin subtle channels **इडा** (*iḍā*) and **पिङ्गला** (*piṅgalā*). their movement (*calat*) governs the rhythmic cycles of cosmic time.
esoteric: * **Solar and Lunar Currents:** Fish animate the water in which they swim. Esoterically, the two eyes represent the cosmic tracks of the Sun and the Moon, or the twin subtle channels **इडा** (*Iḍā*) and **पिङ्गला** (*Piṅgalā*). Their movement (*calat*) governs the rhythmic cycles of cosmic time.

name: नवचम्पकपुष्पाभनासादण्डविराजिता
samasa: नवचम्पकस्य पुष्पं नवचम्पकपुष्पम्। तेन तुल्यः नासादण्डेन विराजिता।
wordByWord: नासादण्ड - bridge or shaft of the nose.
नवचम्पकपुष्पाभनासादण्डविराजिता - She whose nose shines like a fresh Champaka flower.
summary: (i) One whose nose is like a fresh Champaka flower.
(ii) Nava can be interpreted as “new” or “partly blossomed”. A partly blossomed Champaka bud generates a pleasant aroma.
searchText: 19 नवचम्पकपुष्पाभनासादण्डविराजिता नवचम्पकस्य पुष्पं नवचम्पकपुष्पम्। तेन तुल्यः नासादण्डेन विराजिता। नव - new, fresh, partly blossomed. चम्पकपुष्प - champaka flower. आभ - like, resembling. नासादण्ड - bridge or shaft of the nose. विराजिता - shining, adorned. नवचम्पकपुष्पाभनासादण्डविराजिता - she whose nose shines like a fresh champaka flower. (i) one whose nose is like a fresh champaka flower. (ii) nava can be interpreted as “new” or “partly blossomed”. a partly blossomed champaka bud generates a pleasant aroma. * **the path of suṣumṇā:** the campaka bud is perfectly linear and flawless. in *kuṇdoṣṭi-yoga*, the straightness of the nose bridge is the physical external indicator of the straight alignment of the **सुषुम्णा नाडी** (*suṣumṇā nādī*). its linear beauty represents the straight path of the ascending lifeforce (*prāṇa*).
esoteric: * **The Path of Suṣumṇā:** The Campaka bud is perfectly linear and flawless. In *Kuṇdoṣṭi-yoga*, the straightness of the nose bridge is the physical external indicator of the straight alignment of the **सुषुम्णा नाडी** (*Suṣumṇā Nādī*). Its linear beauty represents the straight path of the ascending lifeforce (*Prāṇa*).

name: ताराकान्तितिरस्कारिनासाभरणभासुरा
samasa: तारायाः कान्तिं तिरस्करोतीति ताराकान्तितिरस्कारि। तदेव नासाभरणम्। तेन भासुरा (शोभमाना)।
wordByWord: तारा - star; also name of a deity.
ताराकान्तितिरस्कारिनासाभरणभासुरा - She who shines with a nose ornament that surpasses the brilliance of the stars.
summary: (i) One whose nose ring challenges the lustre of the stars.
(ii) Tara can also be interpreted to mean the two Devees - Mangala and Sukla or the Devata - Taraka.
(iii) Sreedevee’s nose rings are made of Ruby and Pearl.
searchText: 20 ताराकान्तितिरस्कारिनासाभरणभासुरा तारायाः कान्तिं तिरस्करोतीति ताराकान्तितिरस्कारि। तदेव नासाभरणम्। तेन भासुरा (शोभमाना)। तारा - star; also name of a deity. कान्ति - lustre, brilliance. तिरस्कारि - surpassing, putting to shame. नासाभरण - nose ornament, nose ring. भासुरा - shining, brilliant. ताराकान्तितिरस्कारिनासाभरणभासुरा - she who shines with a nose ornament that surpasses the brilliance of the stars. (i) one whose nose ring challenges the lustre of the stars. (ii) tara can also be interpreted to mean the two devees - mangala and sukla or the devata - taraka. (iii) sreedevee’s nose rings are made of ruby and pearl. * **the luminous point of self:** stars represent distant, objective, finite points of light in the external sky. her nose-pearl, which rests closest to the breath of life (*prāṇa*), represents the singular, immediate point of pure subjective light (*bindu*). it outshines the stars because inner self-awareness far eclipses any external cosmic object.
esoteric: * **The Luminous Point of Self:** Stars represent distant, objective, finite points of light in the external sky. Her nose-pearl, which rests closest to the breath of life (*Prāṇa*), represents the singular, immediate point of pure subjective light (*Bindu*). It outshines the stars because inner self-awareness far eclipses any external cosmic object.

name: कदम्बमञ्जरीकॢप्तकर्णपूरमनोहरा
samasa: कदम्बमञ्जर्या क्लृप्तः (कल्पितः) कर्णपूरः कदम्बमञ्जरीक्लृप्तः। तेन मनोहरा (रमणीया)।
wordByWord: कर्णपूर - ear ornament or floral filling for the ear.
मनोहरा - captivating, charming, stealing the mind.
कदम्बमञ्जरीकॢप्तकर्णपूरमनोहरा - She who enchants by wearing Kadamba flower-bunches as ear ornaments.
summary: (i) One who wears a bunch of Kadamba flowers in Her ears and steals the heart with Her beauty.
(ii) Wearing a bunch of flowers in the earlobes increases the beauty. Kadamba flower is suitable for such adornment.
(iii) A garden of Kadamba trees is situated outside the Chintamani Graha, the abode of Sreedevee, in Sreepura. Sreedevee wears the flowers plucked from those trees to adorn Her ear.
searchText: 21 कदम्बमञ्जरीकॢप्तकर्णपूरमनोहरा कदम्बमञ्जर्या क्लृप्तः (कल्पितः) कर्णपूरः कदम्बमञ्जरीक्लृप्तः। तेन मनोहरा (रमणीया)। कदम्बमञ्जरी - bunch of kadamba flowers. कॢप्त / क्लृप्त - arranged, made, fashioned. कर्णपूर - ear ornament or floral filling for the ear. मनोहरा - captivating, charming, stealing the mind. कदम्बमञ्जरीकॢप्तकर्णपूरमनोहरा - she who enchants by wearing kadamba flower-bunches as ear ornaments. (i) one who wears a bunch of kadamba flowers in her ears and steals the heart with her beauty. (ii) wearing a bunch of flowers in the earlobes increases the beauty. kadamba flower is suitable for such adornment. (iii) a garden of kadamba trees is situated outside the chintamani graha, the abode of sreedevee, in sreepura. sreedevee wears the flowers plucked from those trees to adorn her ear. the kadamba tree represents the dense forest of manifest existence. the ears are the receptors of sound (*śabda*), the primary quality of space (*ākāśa*) and the source of speech. * **the ornaments of śruti:** by wearing kadamba blossoms as ear ornaments, she shows that all sound, all vedic revelation (*śruti*), and all manifest languages are merely beautiful ornaments hanging upon her listening consciousness.
esoteric: The Kadamba tree represents the dense forest of manifest existence. The ears are the receptors of sound (*śabda*), the primary quality of space (*ākāśa*) and the source of speech.

* **The Ornaments of Śruti:** By wearing Kadamba blossoms as ear ornaments, She shows that all sound, all Vedic revelation (*Śruti*), and all manifest languages are merely beautiful ornaments hanging upon Her listening consciousness.

name: ताटङ्कयुगलीभूततपनोडुपमण्डला
samasa: तथा तपनस्य (सूर्यस्य) उडपस्य (चन्द्रस्य) च मण्डले यस्याः सा।
wordByWord: ताटङ्क - large ear ornament, palm-leaf type ornament.
ताटङ्कयुगलीभूततपनोडुपमण्डला - She whose ear-ornaments are the paired discs of the Sun and Moon.
summary: (i) One who wears the Sun and Moon as two large earrings.
(ii) One type of ornament worn by ladies in their ears is called Tatanka (palm-leaf). The Sun and the Moon take the form of these Tatankas to beautify Sreedevee’s face.
(iii) The Sun and the Moon have the fortune to become Sreedevee’s eyes, earrings and Her breasts.

(iv) Tatanka is considered to be an auspicious ornament for Sumangalis (ladies living with their husbands). Sree Adi Sankara, in his Soundaryalaharee, describes with awe that Sree Parameshwara could survive, even after consuming the Halahala (poison), only by the power of Sreedevee’s Tatankas (28th verse of Soundaryalaharee).
searchText: 22 ताटङ्कयुगलीभूततपनोडुपमण्डला उडनि पातीति उडपः। अभूतं ताटङ्कयुगलं भूतम् इति ताटङ्कयुगलीभूतम्। तथा तपनस्य (सूर्यस्य) उडपस्य (चन्द्रस्य) च मण्डले यस्याः सा। ताटङ्क - large ear ornament, palm-leaf type ornament. युगलीभूत - becoming a pair. तपन - sun. उडुप - moon. मण्डल - disc, orb. ताटङ्कयुगलीभूततपनोडुपमण्डला - she whose ear-ornaments are the paired discs of the sun and moon. (i) one who wears the sun and moon as two large earrings. (ii) one type of ornament worn by ladies in their ears is called tatanka (palm-leaf). the sun and the moon take the form of these tatankas to beautify sreedevee’s face. (iii) the sun and the moon have the fortune to become sreedevee’s eyes, earrings and her breasts. (iv) tatanka is considered to be an auspicious ornament for sumangalis (ladies living with their husbands). sree adi sankara, in his soundaryalaharee, describes with awe that sree parameshwara could survive, even after consuming the halahala (poison), only by the power of sreedevee’s tatankas (28th verse of soundaryalaharee). the *tāṭaṅka* (ear-ring) is the symbol of eternal auspiciousness (*saumaṅgalya*), as she never undergoes widowhood. * **wielding time:** the sun and moon are the twin cosmic measurers of time. by wearing them as mere ear-rings, she wears time itself as an accessory. this also represents that the solar and lunar breaths (**इडा** and **पिङ्गला**) are anchored in her cosmic hearing.
esoteric: The *Tāṭaṅka* (ear-ring) is the symbol of eternal auspiciousness (*Saumaṅgalya*), as She never undergoes widowhood.

* **Wielding Time:** The Sun and Moon are the twin cosmic measurers of time. By wearing them as mere ear-rings, She wears Time itself as an accessory. This also represents that the solar and lunar breaths (**इडा** and **पिङ्गला**) are anchored in Her cosmic hearing.

name: पद्मरागशिलादर्शपरिभाविकपोलभूः
wordByWord: पद्मरागशिलादर्शपरिभाविकपोलभूः - She whose cheeks surpass a mirror made of Padmaraga stone.
summary: (i) One whose cheeks are most wonderful than a glass made of Padmaraga (Sapphire).
(ii) Sreedevee’s cheeks are smooth and reflect like a mirror. Since it also seems to be red on account of Her complexion, the cheeks seem to be superior to a glass made of Sapphire.

(i) one whose cheeks are most wonderful than a glass made of padmaraga (sapphire). (ii) sreedevee’s cheeks are smooth and reflect like a mirror. since it also seems to be red on account of her complexion, the cheeks seem to be superior to a glass made of sapphire. * **cheeks of reflection:** a mirror reflects whatever stands before it but has no color of its own. her cheeks, however, are like ruby mirrors—they are red with her own internal bliss (*vimarśa*) and reflect the entire universe back to itself. bhāskararāya implies that the entire cosmos is merely a reflection appearing upon the clean, luminous cheek-mirrors of the mother's consciousness.

Her cheeks, however, are like ruby mirrors—they are red with Her own internal bliss (*Vimarśa*) and reflect the entire universe back to itself. Bhāskararāya implies that the entire cosmos is merely a reflection appearing upon the clean, luminous cheek-mirrors of the Mother's consciousness.

name: नवविद्रुमबिम्बश्रीन्यक्कारिरदनच्छदा
samasa: नवानां विद्रुमबिम्बानां श्रियं न्यक्कुरुतः (अधःकुरुतः) रदनच्छदौ यस्याः सा।
wordByWord: रदनच्छद - lips, covering of the teeth.
नवविद्रुमबिम्बश्रीन्यक्कारिरदनच्छदा - She whose lips surpass the beauty of fresh coral and bimba fruit.
summary: (i) One whose (gorgeous) lips challenge the lustre of fresh Corals and the Bimba fruit (a fruit of a creeper, which when ripe is Red).
(ii) Sreedevee’s lips are Red in colour, by nature. Coral and the Bimba fruit are the two usually referred to for comparing lustrous Red objects. Sreedevee’s Rosy lips surpass these two objects.
(iii) Radanacchada is another version and the meaning is the same.
