(i) one whose (gorgeous) lips challenge the lustre of fresh corals and the bimba fruit (a fruit of a creeper, which when ripe is red). (ii) sreedevee’s lips are red in colour, by nature. coral and the bimba fruit are the two usually referred to for comparing lustrous red objects. sreedevee’s rosy lips surpass these two objects. (iii) radanacchada is another version and the meaning is the same. * **the portal of grace:** lips enclose speech. their deep red color signifies her overflowing compassion (*karuṇā*). before her speech transforms into spoken words, it sits behind the red barrier of her lips as pure, unmanifest grace. the lips mock coral because coral is dead mineral material, whereas her lips glow with the pulsing, vibrant life of supreme consciousness.
esoteric: * **The Portal of Grace:** Lips enclose speech. Their deep red color signifies Her overflowing compassion (*karuṇā*). Before Her speech transforms into spoken words, it sits behind the red barrier of Her lips as pure, unmanifest grace. The lips mock coral because coral is dead mineral material, whereas Her lips glow with the pulsing, vibrant life of supreme consciousness.

name: शुद्धविद्याङ्कुराकारद्विजपङ्क्तिद्वयोज्ज्वला
samasa: शुद्धविद्यायाः अङ्कुराणाम् इव आकारः यस्य द्विजपङ्क्तिद्वयस्य, तेन द्विजपङ्क्तिद्वयेन उज्ज्वला।
wordByWord: शुद्धविद्या - Shuddha Vidya, superior Shodashakshari Mantra / Sree Vidya.
शुद्धविद्याङ्कुराकारद्विजपङ्क्तिद्वयोज्ज्वला - She who shines with two rows of teeth resembling sprouts of Shuddha Vidya.
summary: (i) One whose rows of teeth shine like sprouts of Shuddha Vidya.

(ii) The word Dvija means twice born. This term is used to describe three different things: a. Brahmin - First birth from the mother and the second when the Upanayanam is performed (when the sacred thread is first worn). b. Birds - First birth as egg and the second when the chick comes out of the egg. c. Tooth - First as milk teeth and second when the tooth sprouts in place of fallen teeth.

(iii) Shuddha Vidya refers to the superior Shodasakshari Mantra. It consists of 16 letters or beejas. Beejas also mean seeds. The teeth look like the two sprouts from the seeds - the upper and lower rows.
(iv) Dattatreya Samhita and other texts state that Shuddha Vidya is the same as Sree Vidya. Hence the 16 letters of Shodasi Vidya are hidden in this name.
(v) Brahmins learn and propagate Vedas. Hence they are called Vidyankuras that is sprout of Vidya. Sreedevee’s two rows of teeth are compared to two rows of Brahmins, the sprout of Vidya.

(vi) In the Tantra text 32 types of initiations are mentioned - starting from Shuddha Vidya, Bala, Dwadasantha, Matangin and ending with Anutthara. These 32 types form the two rows of teeth of Sreedevee - another interpretation of this name.
(vii) It is indicated only the Dvijas (Brahmins) purified by these 32 initiations can reach Sreedevee’s feet.

(viii) Sreedevee’s Triakshari Mantra is also called as Shuddha Vidya. One becomes eligible for the 32 initiations (Deekshas) only after he is inducted into Triakshari. Shuddha Vidya is the base for the two rows of teeth which sprout from Shuddha Vidya.

(ix) In the Paribhasha Sloakas in the Nama Vibhaga part of this book, it is stated that the 1000 names begin only with 32 letters out of 51 letters in the Sanskrit alphabet, omitting 19 letters. It is also indicated that entire Sahasranama Stoora is structured on these 32 letters.

name: कर्पूरवीटिकामोदसमाकर्षिदिगन्तरा
samasa: • कर्पूरेण (करुपूरादिभिः) युक्ता वीटिका (ताम्बूलः)। तस्याः आमोदं याः समाकर्षन्ति। ताः एव दिशः अन्तं यस्याः सा।
wordByWord: दिगन्तरा — the quarters and the intervening spaces
कर्पूरवीटिकामोदसमाकर्षिदिगन्तरा — She who attracts the quarters by the aroma of the camphor-scented betel from Her mouth
summary: i. One who attracts the four directions towards Her by the pleasant aroma of the betel (tamboola) emanating from Her lotus-like mouth, or one who spreads that aroma in all directions. Veetika is a small pack (folded inside a betel leaf) with proper mixture of aromatic spices such as camphor, cardamom, cloves, musk, saffron, nutmeg, betelnut, lime, etc. The aroma emanating from Sreedevee’s mouth when She chews the Veetika spreads in all directions. It is symbolically described as if the directions compete with each other in surrounding Sreedevee to have a scent of this aroma. Dik refers to the four directions and Digambara is the intervening space between the four directions such as Southeast, Southwest, etc.
ii. In the latter part of this Stotra, there is another name Tamboolapooritamukhi (559th name), which describes Sreedevee chewing betel leaves.
iii. The name Samakarshadigantara is also mentioned as Samakarshidigantara in some editions. There is no difference in meaning between these two versions.

iv. The 14th name in Sree Lalita Trishati, namely, Karpooraveetisaurabhya Kallolitakaguptata, also states the same idea. Sree Adi Sankara in his commentary has stated that the whole world is drenched with this aroma emanating from Sreedevee’s mouth and Sreedevee is thus Maharajabhogavathi (one who enjoys the comforts of an empress).

v. It is told that there were legendary poets, who were Upasakas of Sreedevee, viz. Kalidasa and Mooka Kavi, got their excellent capacity to write poems by consuming the juice of Sreedevee’s tamboola.
vi. It is implied that the Dikpalas — the regents of the eight cardinal points — encircle Sreedevee to offer their prayers.

name: निजसल्लापमाधुर्यविनिर्भर्त्सितकच्छपी
samasa: • नीजस्य (स्वीयस्य) सल्लापस्य माधुर्येण विनिर्भर्त्सिता कच्छपी यया सा।
wordByWord: निजसल्लापमाधुर्यविनिर्भर्त्सितकच्छपी — She whose own sweet speech humbles the Kacchapi Veena
summary: i. One whose sweet voice humbles the Veena (a string instrument) called Kacchapi in the hands of Sree Saraswathi (goddess of learning).

ii. Kacchapi is the name of Sree Saraswathi’s Veena. Normally, in any string instrument like Veena, only the musical notes will be heard and not the lyrics of a song. But since Sree Saraswathi is the embodiment of sound and music, the musical notes as well as the words in the lyrics are heard from her Veena. However, the words would not be explicit, but resemble the utterances of parrot and infants. Sreedevee’s voice is so sweet, that it wins over the sweetness of Sree Saraswathi’s Veena.

iii. There is one verse in Soundaryalaharee (66th verse beginning with Vipanchya Gayanthi). It is stated that when Sree Saraswathi played her Veena in the presence of Sreedevee, She admired its sweetness and uttered the word “awesome”. On hearing this Sree Saraswathi was ashamed by the sweetness of Sreedevee’s voice which surpassed the sound of her Veena and kept it inside its cover.

The copy (instrumental music) is naturally shamed and humbled before the sweet, original source (Her voice).

name: मन्दस्मितप्रभापूरमज्जत्कामेशमानसा
samasa: • मन्दस्य स्मितस्य (ईशद्धासस्य) प्रभापूरे मज्जतीति मन्दस्मितप्रभापूरमज्जत्। तदेव कामस्य ईशस्य मानसं यस्याः सा।
wordByWord: मन्दस्मितप्रभापूरमज्जत्कामेशमानसा — She whose soft smile floods and immerses the mind of Kameshwara
summary: i. One who overwhelms Sree Kameshwara’s mind in the inundate of Her smile.
ii. Smita means smile — without the teeth being visible. Especially Mandasmita means a very soft smile.

iii. Majjat means sinking. Sree Kameshwara does not want to come out of the flood of light that is Sreedevee’s smile. At the same time He does not want to miss the opportunity of enjoying the beauty of the other parts; hence the use of the expression “sinking”.
iv. In Kamakala, Kama means bindu. Kala means Sun, Moon and fire. Bindu denotes ahankara. Kama and kala denote Kamakala. Kameshwara means both Rajarajeshwara and the embodiment of Kamakala.

v. When Sreedevee emerged out of the fire of knowledge (Chidagnikunda), before crowning Her as empress, the question arose as who can be made as Her consort. The only eligible candidate was Sree Kameshwara. But the Devas were perplexed as His physical appearance — with knotted locks, covered with an elephant skin or wearing nothing at all, with snakes as jewels and with ash smeared all over the body — was in no way a match to Her bewitching beauty. Immediately Sree Parameshwara took a beautiful form of Kameshwara. Sreedevee swung a garland declaring that he on whose neck the garland falls would be Her consort; it fell on Sree Kameshwara’s neck and brought forth a smile in Sreedevee’s lips. Sree Kameshwara was drowned in the beauty of Her smile. This is narrated in Sree Lalithopakhyana.

name: अनाकलितसादृश्यचिबुकश्रीविराजिता
samasa: • अनाकलितं (न कल्पितं) यत् सादृश्यं यस्याः चिबुकश्रियाः तादृश्या चिबुकश्रिया विराजिता।
wordByWord: अनाकलितसादृश्यचिबुकश्रीविराजिता — She who shines by the incomparable beauty of Her chin
summary: i. One who shines by virtue of the incomparable beauty of Her chin.
ii. Even the Vagdevatas (the goddess of words) could not find similes to describe the beauty of Her chin.
iii. Sree Adi Sankara also opines the same, in his Soundaryalaharee, as Kathankaram Bhruma; tavasubukam oupamya rahitam (67th verse).
iv. Chubuka or Chibuka mean the same.

v. The 85th name Sreemadvagbhava Kootika Swrupa Mukha Pankaja, states the part of Sreedevee’s body above the neck denotes the first part of Sree Vidya Panchadasakshareemantra viz. Vagbhavakoota. The description of this part of the body starts from the 13th name, Champakashokapunnaga-sougandhikalasatkacha and ends here.

Because She is the non-dual absolute, any comparison (*sādṛśya*) with worldly objects is fundamentally impossible (*anākalita*).

name: कामेशबद्धमाङ्गल्यसूत्रशोभितकन्धरा
samasa: • कामेशेन (परमशिवेन) बद्धं यत् माङ्गल्यसूत्रं तेन शोभिता कन्धरा यस्याः सा।
wordByWord: कामेशबद्धमाङ्गल्यसूत्रशोभितकन्धरा — She whose neck is beautified by the auspicious string tied by Kamesha
summary: i. One whose neck is so beautiful by the auspicious string tied by Sree Kameshwara at the time of wedding.

ii. Sreedevee is a Nitya Sumangali, who is always a Sumangali, wearing the mangala Sutra. Although She emerged out of Chidagni Kunda, already wearing the mangala Sutra, the Devas wanted to see the wedding ceremony of the Holy couple and made Him tie the string again on Sreedevee.

iii. Sree Parameshwara acquired the name Kamari after he reduced Cupid to ashes. Yet on seeing Sreedevee, it appears that He gave life to Cupid and subjected Himself as the aim for the arrows by Cupid so that love would arise in Him upon Sreedevee, that too through the grace of Sreedevee. (84th name — Haranetragni Sandagdha Kama sanjeevanoushadhi may be compared). Hence He becomes the lord of Cupid — Kameesan.
iv. Induced by His desire to marry Sreedevee, Eshwara made friendship with Cupid and was worshipped by him as “Thou art my Lord”.

v. This mangala Sutra tied by Kamesha showered blessings to the entire world. The first such beneficiary of this shower was Rathi-Manmatha (Cupid) couple and that too for the upliftment of the whole world.

name: कनकाङ्गदकेयूरकमनीयभुजान्विता
samasa: • अङ्गं ददातीति अङ्गदम्। कनकम् एव अङ्गदं येषां केयूराणां तैः कमनीया ये भुजाः तैः अन्विता।
wordByWord: कनकाङ्गदकेयूरकमनीयभुजान्विता — She whose beautiful arms are adorned with golden armlets and Keyuras
summary: i. One whose shoulders are adorned by golden shoulder bangles.

ii. Angada and Keyura are the two ornaments worn on the hands. Keyura is for beautification whereas Angada acts as a shield for shoulders and arms of warriors. As Sreedevee is a queen, She wears both the ornaments.

name: रत्नग्रैवेयचिन्ताकलोलमुक्ताफलान्विता
samasa: • रत्नखचितेन ग्रैवेयेण चिन्ताकेन च लोलैः मुक्ताफलैः च अन्विता।
wordByWord: ग्रैवेय — neck pendant or necklace ornament
रत्नग्रैवेयचिन्ताकलोलमुक्ताफलान्विता — She who is adorned with a gem-studded neck ornament and swinging pearls
summary: i. One who wears a necklace with a pendant studded with gemstones and garland of Pearls.
ii. Griva means neck. Graiveya means the pendant in the necklace which adorns the neck and the string of Pearls aligning the necklace.

iii. The necklace, the pendant and the string of pearls represent the three types of devotees. Some will be chanting the names of Sreedevee (doing Japa), but their minds will be wandering somewhere else. Their thoughts of Sreedevee stop with the neck. They are Graiveya Chintakas, limiting their thought of Sreedevee above the neck. They hang and swing from the necklace of Sreedevee, hence they are called Loolas. For some others, the thoughts and actions will be oscillating between Sreedevee and other extraneous thoughts, like a pendant hanging from the necklace. The third type is the Muktas — the detached. They have got rid of their extraneous thoughts and action and think of Sreedevee only. Thus, this name classifies the devotees into three types.

iv. The fruits of actions of these three types of devotees are also different. For the Graiveya Chintaka and Loola the benefits are few — A-phala. For the Muktas A-phalam — full benefits. Two meanings are indicated for A-phala — few and full. For those who just utter the names (of Sreedevee) without concentration of mind, the benefits are few. For those whose minds oscillate between material things (Loolas) the benefits are still less. Those whose mind, word and action act in unison derive full benefits. All the three types of devotees are blessed with the gems like benefits they deserve.

name: कामेश्वरप्रेमरत्नमणिप्रतिपणस्तनी
samasa: • कामेश्वरस्य प्रेम एव रत्नमणिः। रत्नमणौ प्रतिपणभूतौ स्तनौ यस्याः सा।
wordByWord: कामेश्वरप्रेमरत्नमणिप्रतिपणस्तनी — She who offers Her breasts as the price for the gem of Kameshwara’s love
summary: i. One who offers Her breasts as price (or in exchange) for the gem of love of Sree Kameshwara.
ii. Sreedevee has purchased Sree Kameshwara’s invaluable love for Her, with her breasts.
iii. Sreedevee has offered twice the price or price like (two jewels of breasts) for a single jewel of Sree Kameshwara’s love. (Refer 4th verse of Soundaryalaharee — Phalamapicha Vanchasamadhikam).

name: नाभ्यालवालरोमालिलताफलकुचद्वयी
samasa: • नाभिः एव आलवालः यस्याः रोमालवल्याः लतायाः तस्याः फलभूता कुचद्वयी यस्याः सा।
wordByWord: आलवाल — trench; basin at the root
नाभ्यालवालरोमालिलताफलकुचद्वयी — She whose two breasts are fruits of the creeper-like hair-line rising from the navel-trench
summary: i. Sreedevee’s two breasts are like fruits in a creeper (line of hair) climbing from the trench (the naval).
ii. The deep naval is like a trench. The narrow line of hair grown upwards from the naval is like a creeper. The two breasts appear as if they are hanging from the creeper (an excellent metaphor).
iii. 78th verse of Soundaryalaharee may be referred; Stanamukula Romavali lata.

name: लक्ष्यरोमलताधारतासमुन्नेयमध्यमा
samasa: • लक्ष्यितुं योग्या या रोमलताधारता तया सम्यगुन्नेयं मध्यमं यस्याः सा।
wordByWord: लक्ष्य — to be inferred; to be noticed
आधारता — state of being the support/base
लक्ष्यरोमलताधारतासमुन्नेयमध्यमा — She whose waist is inferred as the base of the creeper-like hair-line
summary: i. One for whom, the existence of a waist base for the climbing creeper of line of hair is to be presumed.
ii. The creeper of hair line mentioned in the previous name is visible. But the base where the root of the creeper is located is not to be seen. The existence of a limb as waist is to be presumed.
iii. As per Samudrika Sastra, the waist of upper classes of women will be so small as to be presumed as non-existent.

name: स्तनभारदलन्मध्यपट्टबन्धवलित्रया
samasa: • स्तनयोः भारेण दलत इव मध्यस्य पट्टबन्धः एव वलित्रयं यस्याः सा।
wordByWord: स्तनभारदलन्मध्यपट्टबन्धवलित्रया — She whose three abdominal folds appear as bandages around a waist breaking under the weight of Her breasts
summary: i. One whose three folds in the abdominal area appear to be three bandages worn on the waist to prevent it from breaking due to the weight of the breasts.
ii. Samudrika Sastra specifies that higher class men and women will have three lines (folds) on their forehead, neck and abdomen. They are signs of extremely good fortune.
iii. Refer 80th verse of Soundaryalaharee — Thridhanaddham Deveetrivalee lavaleevaleebhiriva.
searchText: 36 स्तनभारदलन्मध्यपट्टबन्धवलित्रया • स्तनयोः भारेण दलत इव मध्यस्य पट्टबन्धः एव वलित्रयं यस्याः सा। स्तनभार — weight of the breasts दलत् — breaking; cracking as if मध्य — middle; waist पट्टबन्ध — bandage; belt-like binding वलित्रय — three folds स्तनभारदलन्मध्यपट्टबन्धवलित्रया — she whose three abdominal folds appear as bandages around a waist breaking under the weight of her breasts i. one whose three folds in the abdominal area appear to be three bandages worn on the waist to prevent it from breaking due to the weight of the breasts. ii. samudrika sastra specifies that higher class men and women will have three lines (folds) on their forehead, neck and abdomen. they are signs of extremely good fortune. iii. refer 80th verse of soundaryalaharee — thridhanaddham deveetrivalee lavaleevaleebhiriva. * **the three folds of space-time:** the three folds (*vali-traya*) represent the three cosmic boundaries: the three *guṇas* (sattva, rajas, tamas), the three worlds, and the three divisions of time (past, present, future). these boundaries act as the "protective binding strap" (*paṭṭabandha*) that stabilizes and holds the weight of manifest creation, preventing the absolute unmanifest consciousness from collapsing.
esoteric: * **The Three Folds of Space-Time:** The three folds (*vali-traya*) represent the three cosmic boundaries: the three *Guṇas* (Sattva, Rajas, Tamas), the three worlds, and the three divisions of time (past, present, future). These boundaries act as the "protective binding strap" (*paṭṭabandha*) that stabilizes and holds the weight of manifest creation, preventing the absolute unmanifest consciousness from collapsing.

name: अरुणारुणकौसुम्भवस्त्रभास्वत्कटीतटी
samasa: • अरुणारुणेन (अतिशयेन अरुणेन अरुणवद् अरुणं वा) कौसुम्भं यद् वस्त्रं तेन भास्वती कट्याः तटी यस्याः सा।
wordByWord: कौसुम्भ — red silk dyed with Kusumbha
अरुणारुणकौसुम्भवस्त्रभास्वत्कटीतटी — She whose waist shines with crimson-red silk cloth
summary: i. One whose waist shines with the crimson silk worn on it.
ii. The dual expression Aruna-Aruna means dark red or Crimson.
iii. The Red here is like that of Aruna, the charioteer of the Sun God, who rises before Sun rises.
iv. Kausumbha Vastra means Red silk cloth. The juice of Kausumbha herb is used to dye cloths in Red; hence Kausumbha Vastra.
v. Sreedevee has worn Kausumbha Vastra on Her waist.
vi. It appears as though Aruna Vagdevata’s name is indirectly stated here.
searchText: 37 अरुणारुणकौसुम्भवस्त्रभास्वत्कटीतटी • अरुणारुणेन (अतिशयेन अरुणेन अरुणवद् अरुणं वा) कौसुम्भं यद् वस्त्रं तेन भास्वती कट्याः तटी यस्याः सा। अरुणारुण — deep red; crimson कौसुम्भ — red silk dyed with kusumbha वस्त्र — cloth भास्वत् — shining कटीतटी — slope/region of the waist अरुणारुणकौसुम्भवस्त्रभास्वत्कटीतटी — she whose waist shines with crimson-red silk cloth i. one whose waist shines with the crimson silk worn on it. ii. the dual expression aruna-aruna means dark red or crimson. iii. the red here is like that of aruna, the charioteer of the sun god, who rises before sun rises. iv. kausumbha vastra means red silk cloth. the juice of kausumbha herb is used to dye cloths in red; hence kausumbha vastra. v. sreedevee has worn kausumbha vastra on her waist. vi. it appears as though aruna vagdevata’s name is indirectly stated here. * **the garment of pure desire:** safflower (*kusumbha*) yields a vibrant, glowing red dye. the hip region corresponds to the **स्वाधिष्ठान** (*svādhiṣṭhāna*) and **मूलाधार** (*mūlādhāra*) cakras, which govern primal energy and stability. her intensely red garment represents the covering of **इच्छाशक्ति** (*icchā-śakti* — pure will and creative desire) that wraps around the root of cosmic manifestation.
esoteric: * **The Garment of Pure Desire:** Safflower (*kusumbha*) yields a vibrant, glowing red dye. The hip region corresponds to the **स्वाधिष्ठान** (*Svādhiṣṭhāna*) and **मूलाधार** (*Mūlādhāra*) cakras, which govern primal energy and stability. Her intensely red garment represents the covering of **इच्छाशक्ति** (*Icchā-śakti* — pure will and creative desire) that wraps around the root of cosmic manifestation.

name: रत्नकिङ्किणिकारम्यरशनादामभूषिता
samasa: • रत्नैः (रत्नमयीभिः) किङ्किणिकाभिः रम्येण रशनया एव दाम्या भूषिता।
wordByWord: रत्नकिङ्किणिकारम्यरशनादामभूषिता — She adorned with a beautiful girdle-string lined with gem-bells
summary: i. One who wears a girdle string (Meekala Sutra) lined with tinkling gems.
ii. The golden girdle string is lined with small tinkle bells. The small gems inside the bells make a soft pleasant sound.

iii. The part of Sreedevee’s body below the neck and above the thigh represents the central portion of Pancha Dasaksharee Mantra consisting of six letters. These are indirectly stated from 30th till 38th names.
searchText: 38 रत्नकिङ्किणिकारम्यरशनादामभूषिता • रत्नैः (रत्नमयीभिः) किङ्किणिकाभिः रम्येण रशनया एव दाम्या भूषिता। रत्न — gem किङ्किणिका — small tinkling bell रम्य — beautiful रशना — girdle; waist-string दाम — garland/string भूषिता — adorned रत्नकिङ्किणिकारम्यरशनादामभूषिता — she adorned with a beautiful girdle-string lined with gem-bells i. one who wears a girdle string (meekala sutra) lined with tinkling gems. ii. the golden girdle string is lined with small tinkle bells. the small gems inside the bells make a soft pleasant sound. iii. the part of sreedevee’s body below the neck and above the thigh represents the central portion of pancha dasaksharee mantra consisting of six letters. these are indirectly stated from 30th till 38th names. * **the rhythms of the cosmos:** the belt is tied around the waist, stabilizing the center of gravity. the jingling of the tiny bells (*kiṅkiṇikā*) represents the subtle, rhythmic vibrations of the unmanifest sound (*aṇu-nāda* or *nāda-bindu*) that continuously hums at the base of creation, driving the cycles of nature.
esoteric: * **The Rhythms of the Cosmos:** The belt is tied around the waist, stabilizing the center of gravity. The jingling of the tiny bells (*kiṅkiṇikā*) represents the subtle, rhythmic vibrations of the unmanifest sound (*Aṇu-nāda* or *Nāda-bindu*) that continuously hums at the base of creation, driving the cycles of nature.

name: कामेशज्ञातसौभाग्यमार्दवोरुद्वयान्विता
samasa: • कामेशेन एव ज्ञाते सौभाग्यमार्दवे ययोः ऊर्वोः तयोः द्वयेन अन्विता।
wordByWord: कामेशज्ञातसौभाग्यमार्दवोरुद्वयान्विता — She endowed with two thighs whose beauty and softness are known only to Kameshwara
summary: i. One who has two beautiful and soft thighs known only to Sree Kameshwara.
ii. The softness and the smoothness of Her thighs are known only to Sree Kameshwara.
iii. Soubhagya here means exquisiteness or beauty.
searchText: 39 कामेशज्ञातसौभाग्यमार्दवोरुद्वयान्विता • कामेशेन एव ज्ञाते सौभाग्यमार्दवे ययोः ऊर्वोः तयोः द्वयेन अन्विता। कामेश — sree kameshwara ज्ञात — known सौभाग्य — exquisiteness; beauty मार्दव — softness ऊरुद्वय — two thighs अन्विता — endowed with कामेशज्ञातसौभाग्यमार्दवोरुद्वयान्विता — she endowed with two thighs whose beauty and softness are known only to kameshwara i. one who has two beautiful and soft thighs known only to sree kameshwara. ii. the softness and the smoothness of her thighs are known only to sree kameshwara. iii. soubhagya here means exquisiteness or beauty. * **the unknowable essence of bliss:** the thighs are kept covered, symbolizing that which is secret and deeply private. bhāskararāya explains that her deepest bliss (*saubhāgya*) and tenderness (*mārdava*) are completely inaccessible to the external, dualistic mind. only when the individual consciousness completely dissolves into kāmeśvara (pure, non-dual subjective awareness) can this supreme, intimate state of divine bliss be realized.
esoteric: * **The Unknowable Essence of Bliss:** The thighs are kept covered, symbolizing that which is secret and deeply private. Bhāskararāya explains that Her deepest bliss (*saubhāgya*) and tenderness (*mārdava*) are completely inaccessible to the external, dualistic mind. Only when the individual consciousness completely dissolves into Kāmeśvara (pure, non-dual subjective awareness) can this supreme, intimate state of divine bliss be realized.

name: माणिक्यमकुटाकारजानुद्वयविराजिता
samasa: • माणिक्येन निर्मितस्य मकुटस्य इव आकारः ययोः जानुद्वययोः तयोः विराजिता।
wordByWord: माणिक्यमकुटाकारजानुद्वयविराजिता — She whose two knees resemble ruby crowns
summary: i. One whose knees resemble hard, Red caps made of rubies.
ii. The knees are so Red and seem to be like a hard cap.
iii. The softness of the thighs mentioned in the previous name is contrasted with the hardness of the knees.
iv. The reason for hardness of Sreedevee’s knees is stated in the 82nd verse of Soundaryalaharee.
v. Mukuta and Makuta — both have the same meaning.
searchText: 40 माणिक्यमकुटाकारजानुद्वयविराजिता • माणिक्येन निर्मितस्य मकुटस्य इव आकारः ययोः जानुद्वययोः तयोः विराजिता। माणिक्य — ruby मकुट — crown; hard cap आकार — form; shape जानुद्वय — two knees विराजिता — shining माणिक्यमकुटाकारजानुद्वयविराजिता — she whose two knees resemble ruby crowns i. one whose knees resemble hard, red caps made of rubies. ii. the knees are so red and seem to be like a hard cap. iii. the softness of the thighs mentioned in the previous name is contrasted with the hardness of the knees. iv. the reason for hardness of sreedevee’s knees is stated in the 82nd verse of soundaryalaharee. v. mukuta and makuta — both have the same meaning. * **the kneeling of cosmic powers:** the knees provide the structural pivot for bowing and kneeling. by comparing her knees to ruby crowns (*makuṭa*), bhāskararāya hints at a profound cosmic inversion: the highest crowns of all the cosmic deities (indra, brahmā, viṣṇu) bow down and rest in absolute surrender at her feet, reflecting their crowns upon her knees.
esoteric: * **The Kneeling of Cosmic Powers:** The knees provide the structural pivot for bowing and kneeling. By comparing Her knees to ruby crowns (*makuṭa*), Bhāskararāya hints at a profound cosmic inversion: the highest crowns of all the cosmic deities (Indra, Brahmā, Viṣṇu) bow down and rest in absolute surrender at Her feet, reflecting their crowns upon Her knees.

name: इंद्रगोपपरिक्षिप्तस्मरतूणाभजंघिका
samasa: • इंद्रगोपैः परितः क्षिप्तौ यौ स्मरस्य तूणौ तयोः तुल्ये जङ्घिके यस्याः सा।
wordByWord: इंद्रगोपपरिक्षिप्तस्मरतूणाभजंघिका — She whose shanks resemble Cupid’s quiver surrounded by fireflies
summary: i. One whose shanks resemble Cupid’s quiver surrounded by fireflies.
ii. A quiver is a case for carrying arrows, carried by the warriors, with a broad mouth and tapered at the bottom.

iii. The two shanks of Sreedevee are firm and reddish. This resembles Cupid’s quiver surrounded by insects sparkling at night. The brightness of the ankles is enhanced by the sparkling insects. The reddish legs are glowing naturally, but as a metaphor this is compared to the fireflies shining in the night.

iv. Sree Adi Sankara says in 83rd verse of Soundaryalaharee, that Cupid, who was burnt down by Lord Shiva was waiting for an opportunity to seek revenge on Him, and when He was attracted by Sreedevee’s beauty, Cupid made Sreedevee’s shanks his quiver and Her toes his arrows; Parajethum RudhramadhvigunaCharagarbhau Girisute Nishangou Janghete Vishama Vichikho Badhamakrta.
searchText: 41 इंद्रगोपपरिक्षिप्तस्मरतूणाभजंघिका • इंद्रगोपैः परितः क्षिप्तौ यौ स्मरस्य तूणौ तयोः तुल्ये जङ्घिके यस्याः सा। इन्द्रगोप — firefly/red insect परिक्षिप्त — surrounded स्मर — cupid तूण — quiver जंघिका — shanks इंद्रगोपपरिक्षिप्तस्मरतूणाभजंघिका — she whose shanks resemble cupid’s quiver surrounded by fireflies i. one whose shanks resemble cupid’s quiver surrounded by fireflies. ii. a quiver is a case for carrying arrows, carried by the warriors, with a broad mouth and tapered at the bottom. iii. the two shanks of sreedevee are firm and reddish. this resembles cupid’s quiver surrounded by insects sparkling at night. the brightness of the ankles is enhanced by the sparkling insects. the reddish legs are glowing naturally, but as a metaphor this is compared to the fireflies shining in the night. iv. sree adi sankara says in 83rd verse of soundaryalaharee, that cupid, who was burnt down by lord shiva was waiting for an opportunity to seek revenge on him, and when he was attracted by sreedevee’s beauty, cupid made sreedevee’s shanks his quiver and her toes his arrows; parajethum rudhramadhvigunacharagarbhau girisute nishangou janghete vishama vichikho badhamakrta. * **the quivers of creative force:** the quivers of cupid hold the arrows of desire. the *indragopa* insect is famous in sanskrit literature for its deep, velvety crimson color. this indicates that her lower limbs hold the concentrated, velvet-red potential of all creative forces, ready to be deployed to guide and capture the minds of seekers, turning them away from worldly pursuits.
esoteric: * **The Quivers of Creative Force:** The quivers of Cupid hold the arrows of desire. The *Indragopa* insect is famous in Sanskrit literature for its deep, velvety crimson color. This indicates that Her lower limbs hold the concentrated, velvet-red potential of all creative forces, ready to be deployed to guide and capture the minds of seekers, turning them away from worldly pursuits.

name: गूढगुल्फा
wordByWord: गूढगुल्फा — She whose ankles are covered/hidden by firm muscles
summary: i. One whose shanks are covered by firm muscles.
ii. Gulpha refers to the calf muscles above the ankles; it means firm and beautiful ankles.
searchText: 42 गूढगुल्फा • गूढौ गुल्फौ यस्याः सा। गूढ — hidden; covered गुल्फ — ankle गूढगुल्फा — she whose ankles are covered/hidden by firm muscles i. one whose shanks are covered by firm muscles. ii. gulpha refers to the calf muscles above the ankles; it means firm and beautiful ankles. * **the veiled path of yoga:** *gūḍha* means hidden or secret. in *aṣṭāṅga-yoga*, the ankles represent the physical structural transition to the feet (contact with the earth). her hidden ankles represent that the path of transitioning from material existence (*samsāra*) to spiritual elevation is deeply hidden and must be approached through secret, internal contemplation rather than ostentatious external display.
esoteric: * **The Veiled Path of Yoga:** *Gūḍha* means hidden or secret. In *Aṣṭāṅga-yoga*, the ankles represent the physical structural transition to the feet (contact with the earth). Her hidden ankles represent that the path of transitioning from material existence (*Samsāra*) to spiritual elevation is deeply hidden and must be approached through secret, internal contemplation rather than ostentatious external display.

name: कूर्मपृष्ठजयिष्णुप्रपदान्विता
samasa: • कूर्मयोः पृष्ठे जयिष्णुनी (जयतः इति) ये प्रपदे (पादाग्रे) ताभ्याम् अन्विता।
wordByWord: प्रपद — forepart of foot; foot front
कूर्मपृष्ठजयिष्णुप्रपदान्विता — She whose feet surpass the back of a turtle
summary: i. One whose foot resembles a turtle’s back.
ii. The back of the feet of higher class women would be pyramid like. In the absence of any other suitable comparison, turtle’s back (though of an inferior class) is used as a simile.

iii. The 88th verse of Soundaryalaharee also explicitly states that this simile is not the most appropriate one; Padam Tekeertteenam Prapadam Apadam Devee Vipadam Ktham Neetam Sadbhih Katina Kamateekarprara Tulam.
searchText: 43 कूर्मपृष्ठजयिष्णुप्रपदान्विता • कूर्मयोः पृष्ठे जयिष्णुनी (जयतः इति) ये प्रपदे (पादाग्रे) ताभ्याम् अन्विता। कूर्म — turtle पृष्ठ — back जयिष्णु — excelling; surpassing प्रपद — forepart of foot; foot front अन्विता — endowed with कूर्मपृष्ठजयिष्णुप्रपदान्विता — she whose feet surpass the back of a turtle i. one whose foot resembles a turtle’s back. ii. the back of the feet of higher class women would be pyramid like. in the absence of any other suitable comparison, turtle’s back (though of an inferior class) is used as a simile. iii. the 88th verse of soundaryalaharee also explicitly states that this simile is not the most appropriate one; padam tekeertteenam prapadam apadam devee vipadam ktham neetam sadbhih katina kamateekarprara tulam. * **the base of cosmic stability:** the tortoise (*kūrma*) is the classic symbol of ultimate stability and withdrawal of the senses. by having feet that conquer the tortoise shell, she is identified as the supreme foundation of the entire universe, holding the pristine, unshakeable stability (**कूर्म-नाडी** — the channel of complete physical and mental stillness).
esoteric: * **The Base of Cosmic Stability:** The tortoise (*Kūrma*) is the classic symbol of ultimate stability and withdrawal of the senses. By having feet that conquer the tortoise shell, She is identified as the supreme foundation of the entire universe, holding the pristine, unshakeable stability (**कूर्म-नाडी** — the channel of complete physical and mental stillness).

name: नखदीधितिसंछन्ननमज्जनतमोगुणा
samasa: • नखानां दीधितिभिः (किरणैः) सम्यक् छन्नो नमतां जनानां तमोगुणः यस्याः सा।
wordByWord: नखदीधितिसंछन्ननमज्जनतमोगुणा — She whose toenail rays cover and remove the darkness of those who bow to Her
summary: i. One who removes the darkness of ignorance of those who prostrate (Tamo Guna) before Her by the lustre of Her toe nails.

ii. When Brahma, Vishnu and other Devas prostrate before Sreedevee’s feet, the lustre of their crown face the lustre of Sreedevee’s toe nails, and unable to stand, their brightness go back inside them and remove the darkness of ignorance.
iii. Or, just by contact, the lustre of the toe nails removes the darkness of ignorance.
iv. Not only for the Devas, but also for all those who worship Sreedevee, the same benefit i.e. the removal of ignorance — is obtained.
searchText: 44 नखदीधितिसंछन्ननमज्जनतमोगुणा • नखानां दीधितिभिः (किरणैः) सम्यक् छन्नो नमतां जनानां तमोगुणः यस्याः सा। नख — nail दीधिति — ray; lustre संछन्न — covered; enveloped नमज्जन — people who bow/prostrate तमोगुण — darkness; ignorance नखदीधितिसंछन्ननमज्जनतमोगुणा — she whose toenail rays cover and remove the darkness of those who bow to her i. one who removes the darkness of ignorance of those who prostrate (tamo guna) before her by the lustre of her toe nails. ii. when brahma, vishnu and other devas prostrate before sreedevee’s feet, the lustre of their crown face the lustre of sreedevee’s toe nails, and unable to stand, their brightness go back inside them and remove the darkness of ignorance. iii. or, just by contact, the lustre of the toe nails removes the darkness of ignorance. iv. not only for the devas, but also for all those who worship sreedevee, the same benefit i.e. the removal of ignorance — is obtained. * **the dawn of inner light:** *tamas* is the dark quality of inertia, sleep, and spiritual blindness. when a devotee bows their head to her feet, their crown (*sahasrāra*) comes into contact with the light rays emanating from her toe-nails. these ten toe-nails act like ten miniature suns, instantly dissolving the deep-seated darkness of spiritual ignorance (*avidyā*) and replacing it with the light of self-realization.
esoteric: * **The Dawn of Inner Light:** *Tamas* is the dark quality of inertia, sleep, and spiritual blindness. When a devotee bows their head to Her feet, their crown (*Sahasrāra*) comes into contact with the light rays emanating from Her toe-nails. These ten toe-nails act like ten miniature suns, instantly dissolving the deep-seated darkness of spiritual ignorance (*Avidyā*) and replacing it with the light of self-realization.

name: पदद्वयप्रभाजालपराकृतसरोरुहा
samasa: • पदद्वयस्य प्रभाजालेन पराकृते सरोरुहे यस्याः सा।
wordByWord: पदद्वयप्रभाजालपराकृतसरोरुहा — She whose two feet surpass the lotus by their lustre
summary: i. One who won over the lotus flower by the lustre of Her two feet.

ii. Generally, it is a literary tradition to take the lotus flower for comparison with the face, feet and eyes of beautiful men and women. Lustre, softness, beautiful structure and a lovely appearance are the special features of a lotus. In the case of Sreedevee’s feet, there is one more special characteristic. Lotus will blossom and shrivel on the same day, whereas Sreedevee’s feet remain permanently lustrous, and thus they win over lotus.
searchText: 45 पदद्वयप्रभाजालपराकृतसरोरुहा • पदद्वयस्य प्रभाजालेन पराकृते सरोरुहे यस्याः सा। पदद्वय — two feet प्रभाजाल — net/mass of lustre पराकृत — surpassed; defeated सरोरुह — lotus पदद्वयप्रभाजालपराकृतसरोरुहा — she whose two feet surpass the lotus by their lustre i. one who won over the lotus flower by the lustre of her two feet. ii. generally, it is a literary tradition to take the lotus flower for comparison with the face, feet and eyes of beautiful men and women. lustre, softness, beautiful structure and a lovely appearance are the special features of a lotus. in the case of sreedevee’s feet, there is one more special characteristic. lotus will blossom and shrivel on the same day, whereas sreedevee’s feet remain permanently lustrous, and thus they win over lotus. * **the transcendent lotus:** the red lotus (*saroruha*) is the standard symbol of beauty and purity in the material world. however, the lotus grows out of mud and water. her feet, being pure consciousness, excel the lotus because they are entirely self-luminous (*svaprakāśa*) and free from any worldly origin. they represent the ultimate sanctuary of absolute purity.
esoteric: * **The Transcendent Lotus:** The red lotus (*saroruha*) is the standard symbol of beauty and purity in the material world. However, the lotus grows out of mud and water. Her feet, being pure consciousness, excel the lotus because they are entirely self-luminous (*Svaprakāśa*) and free from any worldly origin. They represent the ultimate sanctuary of absolute purity.

name: शिञ्जानमणिमञ्जीरमण्डितश्रीपदाम्बुजा
samasa: • सिञ्जानाः मणयः ययोः मञ्जीरयोः ताभ्यां मण्डिता श्रीः ययोः पदाम्बुजयोः तादृशे पदाम्बुजे यस्याः सा।
wordByWord: शिञ्जानमणिमञ्जीरमण्डितश्रीपदाम्बुजा — She whose lotus feet are decorated with tinkling gem anklets
summary: i. One whose Lotus feet are decorated with anklets with tinkling bells.

ii. The anklets worn on Sreedevee’s feet have bells fixed with gems. They make a sweet and soft jingle while oscillating. They add Sreeta Her lotus feet. (Sree is one word which cannot be translated into any other language, to bring forth its entire meaning). Broadly it means “all that is good”.
iii. Chinjana is another expression in some texts. The meaning is the same.
searchText: 46 शिञ्जानमणिमञ्जीरमण्डितश्रीपदाम्बुजा • सिञ्जानाः मणयः ययोः मञ्जीरयोः ताभ्यां मण्डिता श्रीः ययोः पदाम्बुजयोः तादृशे पदाम्बुजे यस्याः सा। शिञ्जान — tinkling मणि — gem मञ्जीर — anklet मण्डित — decorated श्री — auspicious beauty पदाम्बुज — lotus feet शिञ्जानमणिमञ्जीरमण्डितश्रीपदाम्बुजा — she whose lotus feet are decorated with tinkling gem anklets i. one whose lotus feet are decorated with anklets with tinkling bells. ii. the anklets worn on sreedevee’s feet have bells fixed with gems. they make a sweet and soft jingle while oscillating. they add sreeta her lotus feet. (sree is one word which cannot be translated into any other language, to bring forth its entire meaning). broadly it means “all that is good”. iii. chinjana is another expression in some texts. the meaning is the same. * **the melodies of creation:** the sweet tinkling (*siñjāna*) of her anklets represents the primordial cosmic sound (**नाद** — *nāda*). as she walks (manifests and directs cosmic processes), the movement of her feet unleashes the primary vibrations that organize chaos into ordered space and time. it is the music of the spheres playing at her feet.
esoteric: * **The Melodies of Creation:** The sweet tinkling (*siñjāna*) of Her anklets represents the primordial cosmic sound (**नाद** — *Nāda*). As She walks (manifests and directs cosmic processes), the movement of Her feet unleashes the primary vibrations that organize chaos into ordered space and time. It is the music of the spheres playing at Her feet.

name: मरालीमन्दगमना
wordByWord: मरालीमन्दगमना — She whose gentle gait is like that of a female swan
summary: i. One whose gait is slow and beautiful like a female swan.
ii. The gait of a swan is always soft and beautiful. The gait of a female swan is all the more graceful. Hence the female swan is compared.
iii. Everyone stands spellbound by the beauty of Sreedevee coming out of the sacrificial fire.

iv. The same idea is expressed in Soundaryalaharee and Mooka Panchasati. The swans in Sreedevee’s palace try to learn and copy the gait of Sreedevee. The soft sound of Sreedevee’s anklets seem to teach lessons to the swans. Refer: Padanyasa Krida Parichaya Mivarabhdhu Manasa: Skhalantaste Khelam Bhavanakalohamsa Na Jahati Athastheesham Shikshaam Subhagamanji Manjira Ranjita-Chaladachakshanam Charanakamalam Charucharitare (91st verse of Soundaryalaharee). Maralinam Yamabhyasanakalana Moolagurave (Mookapanchasati — Padaravinda Satakam - 3).
v. That part of Sreedevee’s body below the waist represents the Shaktikuta of Sreedevee’s Mantra consisting of four syllables.
searchText: 47 मरालीमन्दगमना • मराली इव मन्दगमनं यस्याः सा। मराली — female swan मन्द — slow; gentle गमन — gait; movement मरालीमन्दगमना — she whose gentle gait is like that of a female swan i. one whose gait is slow and beautiful like a female swan. ii. the gait of a swan is always soft and beautiful. the gait of a female swan is all the more graceful. hence the female swan is compared. iii. everyone stands spellbound by the beauty of sreedevee coming out of the sacrificial fire. iv. the same idea is expressed in soundaryalaharee and mooka panchasati. the swans in sreedevee’s palace try to learn and copy the gait of sreedevee. the soft sound of sreedevee’s anklets seem to teach lessons to the swans. refer: padanyasa krida parichaya mivarabhdhu manasa: skhalantaste khelam bhavanakalohamsa na jahati athastheesham shikshaam subhagamanji manjira ranjita-chaladachakshanam charanakamalam charucharitare (91st verse of soundaryalaharee). maralinam yamabhyasanakalana moolagurave (mookapanchasati — padaravinda satakam - 3). v. that part of sreedevee’s body below the waist represents the shaktikuta of sreedevee’s mantra consisting of four syllables. * **the gait of ultimate discrimination:** the swan (*marālī* or *haṃsa*) is the ultimate symbol of spiritual discrimination (*viveka*), possessing the legendary ability to separate milk from water. her slow, majestic gait (*manda-gamana*) represents the steady, deliberate process of divine discrimination that separates the eternal reality (*nitya*) from the transient illusion (*anitya*) in the cosmos.
esoteric: * **The Gait of Ultimate Discrimination:** The swan (*marālī* or *haṃsa*) is the ultimate symbol of spiritual discrimination (*Viveka*), possessing the legendary ability to separate milk from water. Her slow, majestic gait (*manda-gamana*) represents the steady, deliberate process of divine discrimination that separates the eternal reality (*Nitya*) from the transient illusion (*Anitya*) in the cosmos.

name: महालावण्यशेवधिः
wordByWord: महालावण्यशेवधिः — She who is the great repository of all beauty
summary: i. She is a repository or container of all beauty.

ii. Sreedevee’s divine beauty is unparalleled. Her face is described as a container gathering all the beauty. The below 12th verse of Soundaryalaharee may also be referred. Tvadiyam Soundaryam Tuhinagirikanye Tulayitum Kaveendrah Kalpante Kathamapi Virinchi-Prabhrutayah.
searchText: 48 महालावण्यशेवधिः • महतः लावण्यस्य शेवधिः। महा — great लावण्य — beauty; charm शेवधि — treasure; repository महालावण्यशेवधिः — she who is the great repository of all beauty i. she is a repository or container of all beauty. ii. sreedevee’s divine beauty is unparalleled. her face is described as a container gathering all the beauty. the below 12th verse of soundaryalaharee may also be referred. tvadiyam soundaryam tuhinagirikanye tulayitum kaveendrah kalpante kathamapi virinchi-prabhrutayah. * **the inexhaustible source of aesthetic grace:** *lāvaṇya* is not mere physical symmetry; it is defined in aesthetics as the glowing, fluid luster that shines through a form, like saltiness in food or light through a pearl. as the *śevadhi* (treasure-house), she is the singular source from which all beauty, art, harmony, and aesthetic joy in the universe are drawn.
esoteric: * **The Inexhaustible Source of Aesthetic Grace:** *Lāvaṇya* is not mere physical symmetry; it is defined in aesthetics as the glowing, fluid luster that shines through a form, like saltiness in food or light through a pearl. As the *śevadhi* (treasure-house), She is the singular source from which all beauty, art, harmony, and aesthetic joy in the universe are drawn.

name: सर्वारुणा
wordByWord: सर्वारुणा — She who is entirely red/crimson
summary: i. Her body, garments, jewels, flower and lustre are all Red.
ii. It was not just because She emerged from the sacrificial fire, but it was natural to Her.
iii. Lauhityametasya Sarvasya Vimarsah — Pavanopanishad (29) may also be referred.
iv. This name occurs as the 138th name in Lalita Trishati. In his commentary, Sree Adi Sankara quotes the Vedic text Asau Yastamroo Arunaha.
searchText: 49 सर्वारुणा • सर्वम् एव अरुणं यस्याः सा। सर्व — all; entire अरुण — red; crimson सर्वारुणा — she who is entirely red/crimson i. her body, garments, jewels, flower and lustre are all red. ii. it was not just because she emerged from the sacrificial fire, but it was natural to her. iii. lauhityametasya sarvasya vimarsah — pavanopanishad (29) may also be referred. iv. this name occurs as the 138th name in lalita trishati. in his commentary, sree adi sankara quotes the vedic text asau yastamroo arunaha. * **the uniform glow of self-reflection:** red (*aruṇa*) is the color of *vimarśa* (self-awareness and compassion). by being *sarvāruṇā*, there is no patch of darkness or other color within her. this signifies the absolute homogeneity (*ekarasatva*) of her non-dual nature. she is pure, unadulterated, uniform consciousness, radiating unconditional love and grace throughout the cosmos.
esoteric: * **The Uniform Glow of Self-Reflection:** Red (*Aruṇa*) is the color of *Vimarśa* (Self-awareness and compassion). By being *Sarvāruṇā*, there is no patch of darkness or other color within Her. This signifies the absolute homogeneity (*Ekarasatva*) of Her non-dual nature. She is pure, unadulterated, uniform consciousness, radiating unconditional love and grace throughout the cosmos.

name: अनवद्याङ्गी
samasa: • अनवद्याङ्गी। न अवद्यानि अनवद्यानि। तानि अङ्गानि यस्याः सा।
wordByWord: अनवद्याङ्गी — She whose limbs are flawless
summary: i. One who has a flawless body structure.
ii. Her flawless body looked at from foot to head or from head to foot bestows bliss on those who are blessed to have a vision.
searchText: 50 अनवद्याङ्गी • अनवद्याङ्गी। न अवद्यानि अनवद्यानि। तानि अङ्गानि यस्याः सा। अनवद्य — faultless; without blemish अङ्ग — limb अनवद्याङ्गी — she whose limbs are flawless i. one who has a flawless body structure. ii. her flawless body looked at from foot to head or from head to foot bestows bliss on those who are blessed to have a vision. * **the flawless state of absolute reality:** *avadya* means that which can be criticized, limited, or found faulty. every created object in the relative world has some inherent defect or limitation. because she is the uncreated, primordial source of all being, her form (*aṅgī*) is completely flawless (*anavadya*), representing the pristine, unconditioned state of absolute perfection.
esoteric: * **The Flawless State of Absolute Reality:** *Avadya* means that which can be criticized, limited, or found faulty. Every created object in the relative world has some inherent defect or limitation. Because She is the uncreated, primordial source of all being, Her form (*aṅgī*) is completely flawless (*anavadya*), representing the pristine, unconditioned state of absolute perfection.

name: सर्वाभरणभूषिता
summary: i. One who is adorned with all ornaments.

ii. The jewels worn by Sreedevee (from the Choodamani worn on the head to the toe ring) are described in the Kalpa Sutra, Kalika Purana, etc. For one who has a flawless body structure as stated in the Previous name, all the jewels try to add to Her beauty, but fail in their attempts. On the other hand, the elegance of the jewels is enhanced by their presence on Sreedevee's body. (The list of jewels. is given under the 235" name Chatushshashti Upacharadya).
iii. Completely decorated with all the ornaments. It can also be interpreted to mean that Sreedevee is the one who endows the status of ornaments on the jewels worn By Her.
iv. The same name occurs as the 140" one in Lalita Trishati as Sarvabharanabhooshita or Sarvabhooshonabhooshita, (v) After describing the physical form of Sreedevee from head to foot,
searchText: 51 सर्वाभरणभूषिता • सर्वाभरणभूषिता। सर्वैः आभरणैः भूषिता। सर्वैः — all भूषिता। — adorned i. one who is adorned with all ornaments. ii. the jewels worn by sreedevee (from the choodamani worn on the head to the toe ring) are described in the kalpa sutra, kalika purana, etc. for one who has a flawless body structure as stated in the previous name, all the jewels try to add to her beauty, but fail in their attempts. on the other hand, the elegance of the jewels is enhanced by their presence on sreedevee's body. (the list of jewels. is given under the 235" name chatushshashti upacharadya). iii. completely decorated with all the ornaments. it can also be interpreted to mean that sreedevee is the one who endows the status of ornaments on the jewels worn by her. iv. the same name occurs as the 140" one in lalita trishati as sarvabharanabhooshita or sarvabhooshonabhooshita, (v) after describing the physical form of sreedevee from head to foot, the cosmos as her ornament: bhāskararāya explains that she does not require external gems to gain splendor; rather, the ornaments themselves gain beauty by touching her form. esoterically, the "all ornaments" (sarvābharaṇa) represent the entire manifest universe. the stars, planets, elements, and all levels of cosmic principles (tattvas) are the decorative jewels resting upon her infinite body of consciousness.
esoteric: The Cosmos as Her Ornament: Bhāskararāya explains that She does not require external gems to gain splendor; rather, the ornaments themselves gain beauty by touching Her form. Esoterically, the "all ornaments" (sarvābharaṇa) represent the entire manifest universe. The stars, planets, elements, and all levels of cosmic principles (tattvas) are the decorative jewels resting upon Her infinite body of consciousness.

name: शिवकामेश्वराङ्कस्था
samasa: • शिवश्च असौ कामश्च असौ ईश्वरश्चेति शिवकामेश्वरः। तस्य अङ्के तिष्ठतीति।
wordByWord: Word-by-word meaning could not be generated reliably from the available samāsa line.
summary: i. One who sits on the left lap of Her consort Sree Shivakameeshwara.
ii. Kama means a beautiful person. Shiva means the auspicious one. ISward Is the one who controls (rules over) the actions of others according to his wish. Hence Shivakameeshwara.

iii. Kameshwara is one who is capable of taking any form of his choice. It also means that he is more handsome than Kama (the God of love) — Cupid, hence Kameshwara, {iv) According to Sruti (Vedas), Pragnana is Kameshwara,
v. Brihadéranya Uponishad (1-4-17) states that the Ishward who wished to create the world was called Kama. (Atmaivedamagra Asiteekaeeva Sookimayata)

vi. The term Shivakdémeeshward has not been used in the sense Sadérana Rudra or Manmatha. What is referred to here is the Icchashaktiof the Supreme Being. (vil/Sreedevee is Shivakémeeshwari, as She sits on the left lap of Shivakémeeshwara. All the explanations given above are equally applicable to Her,

(atmaivedamagra asiteekaeeva sookimayata) vi. the term shivakdémeeshward has not been used in the sense sadérana rudra or manmatha. what is referred to here is the icchashaktiof the supreme being. (vil/sreedevee is shivakémeeshwari, as she sits on the left lap of shivakémeeshwara. all the explanations given above are equally applicable to her, the union of stillness and kinship: aṅka means the lap, but it also represents the central point or locus. shiva-kāmeśvara represents prakāśa (unmoving, transcendent consciousness). she (as vimarśa or active self-awareness) resides permanently at the very core of his being. this sits at the center of the śrī cakra(bindu), symbolizing that creation only occurs when the transcendent father and dynamic mother exist in absolute, inseparable embrace.
esoteric: The Union of Stillness and Kinship: Aṅka means the lap, but it also represents the central point or locus. Shiva-Kāmeśvara represents Prakāśa (unmoving, transcendent consciousness). She (as Vimarśa or active self-awareness) resides permanently at the very core of His being. This sits at the center of the Śrī Cakra(Bindu), symbolizing that creation only occurs when the transcendent father and dynamic mother exist in absolute, inseparable embrace.

name: शिवा
summary: i. Shiva”s consort, and hence Shiva — inseparable from Shiva. (ii) Embodiment of auspiciousness, bestower of auspiciousness — The Icchashaktiof the Supreme Being -Liberation personified.
iii. Everything ultimately rests with Sreedevee. Hence Shiva.
iv. Parameshwara has eight forms, viz. Earth, Water, Fire, Wind, “Space, Sun, Moon and Jeeva. Shiva in the form wind is called iséna. His consort is Shiva.
v. The statement in the Vedas — Yathee Rudra Shivaéthanoohmay be referred.
searchText: 53 शिवा • शिवं करोतीति। • शिवाभेदात् शिवा। • शेते अस्मिन् सर्वमिति। शिवा — auspicious; śiva शिवं — auspicious; śiva शिवा। — auspicious; śiva i. shiva”s consort, and hence shiva — inseparable from shiva. (ii) embodiment of auspiciousness, bestower of auspiciousness — the icchashaktiof the supreme being -liberation personified. iii. everything ultimately rests with sreedevee. hence shiva. iv. parameshwara has eight forms, viz. earth, water, fire, wind, “space, sun, moon and jeeva. shiva in the form wind is called iséna. his consort is shiva. v. the statement in the vedas — yathee rudra shivaéthanoohmay be referred. the non-dual auspiciousness: bhāskararāya highlights the absolute identity of shiva and śivā. just as fire and its burning power are inseparable, shiva and his power of consciousness (śaktī) are one. she is śivā because she is the active delivery of his peaceful, auspicious nature to the universe. furthermore, as the ultimate resting place (śete asmin sarvam), she is the great cosmic womb into which all creation sleeps during the great dissolution (mahāpralaya).
esoteric: The Non-Dual Auspiciousness: Bhāskararāya highlights the absolute identity of Shiva and Śivā. Just as fire and its burning power are inseparable, Shiva and His power of consciousness (Śaktī) are one. She is Śivā because She is the active delivery of His peaceful, auspicious nature to the universe. Furthermore, as the ultimate resting place (śete asmin sarvam), She is the great cosmic womb into which all creation sleeps during the great dissolution (Mahāpralaya).

name: स्वाधीनवल्लभा
summary: i. One who has a loving and amenable consort. Shiva's shakti (energy) is Devee. Energy is a characteristic feature, which cannot exist on its own, but always exists with matter. Thus it means that Sokth/ lives with Shiva and is obedient to Her. But in fact Shiva is controlled by Shakti and always seeks Her help in all his actions.
iii. The 1*verse of Soundaryalaharee — Shivasakthya Yukta Yadibhavati Saktahprabhavitum Nacheedevam Devo Na Kahlu Kusataspanditu “mapi may be referred.
iv. In practice, if anybody is idle he is referred as Shivonee.
v. One by whose grace women got their husbands. Sukanya wed Chyavana, Sachi Devee wed Indra and Rukmani wed Krishna by worshipping Sreedevee and by her blessings,
vi. Although She is independent and not under the control of Parameshwara, She is his beloved. Swadhina — independent; Vallabha — beloved.
vii. When Vag Deveesstarted to explain the dwelling of Sreedevee, they first talk about the lap of Shivaand then continue to describe other dwellings.
searchText: 54 स्वाधीनवल्लभा • स्वस्याः अधीनः वल्लभः यस्याः/यया सा। वल्लभः — beloved consort i. one who has a loving and amenable consort. shiva's shakti (energy) is devee. energy is a characteristic feature, which cannot exist on its own, but always exists with matter. thus it means that sokth/ lives with shiva and is obedient to her. but in fact shiva is controlled by shakti and always seeks her help in all his actions. iii. the 1*verse of soundaryalaharee — shivasakthya yukta yadibhavati saktahprabhavitum nacheedevam devo na kahlu kusataspanditu “mapi may be referred. iv. in practice, if anybody is idle he is referred as shivonee. v. one by whose grace women got their husbands. sukanya wed chyavana, sachi devee wed indra and rukmani wed krishna by worshipping sreedevee and by her blessings, vi. although she is independent and not under the control of parameshwara, she is his beloved. swadhina — independent; vallabha — beloved. vii. when vag deveesstarted to explain the dwelling of sreedevee, they first talk about the lap of shivaand then continue to describe other dwellings. the sovereignty of will: in external aesthetics, this describes a woman whose husband is completely devoted to her. esoterically, the vallabha (beloved) is shiva, who represents pure, unmoving existence. he remains passive and inactive until she activates him. because he yields his absolute power to her dynamic direction, she holds complete sway over him. it represents that the active expression of the divine is entirely the domain of her supreme will (स्वातन्त्र्यशक्ति — svātantrya-śakti).
esoteric: The Sovereignty of Will: In external aesthetics, this describes a woman whose husband is completely devoted to Her. Esoterically, the Vallabha (beloved) is Shiva, who represents pure, unmoving existence. He remains passive and inactive until She activates Him. Because He yields His absolute power to Her dynamic direction, She holds complete sway over Him. It represents that the active expression of the divine is entirely the domain of Her supreme Will (स्वातन्त्र्यशक्ति — Svātantrya-śakti).

name: सुमेरुमध्यशृङ्गस्था
wordByWord: Word-by-word meaning could not be generated reliably from the available samāsa line.
summary: 55, Sumerumadhyasrungasthd -Qerayyen
i. One who dwells in the central peak of mount Meru.

i. Sumeru is a golden mountain which supports the Earth as its base. There are three peaks located triangularly in the centre of the mountain, and there is another fourth and higher peak at the centre of the triangle. The Sreenagaram where Sreedevee dwells is in this fourth peak. These details are beautifully described in Lolitéstavaraja written by sage Durvasa.
searchText: 55 सुमेरुमध्यशृङ्गस्था • सुमेरुमध्यशृङ्गस्था। word-by-word meaning could not be generated reliably from the available samāsa line. 55, sumerumadhyasrungasthd -qerayyen i. one who dwells in the central peak of mount meru. i. sumeru is a golden mountain which supports the earth as its base. there are three peaks located triangularly in the centre of the mountain, and there is another fourth and higher peak at the centre of the triangle. the sreenagaram where sreedevee dwells is in this fourth peak. these details are beautifully described in lolitéstavaraja written by sage durvasa. the axis of the subtle body: mount sumeru is the cosmic golden mountain at the center of the universe. in the esoteric geography of the subtle body (piṇḍāṇḍa), sumeru represents the spinal column (merudaṇḍa). the "middle peak" (madhya-śṛṅga) is the topmost point of this axis—the sahasrāra at the crown of the head. therefore, she is sumerumadhyaśṛṅgasthā because her ultimate, supreme station of rest is the crown center of non-dual union.
esoteric: The Axis of the Subtle Body: Mount Sumeru is the cosmic golden mountain at the center of the universe. In the esoteric geography of the subtle body (Piṇḍāṇḍa), Sumeru represents the spinal column (Merudaṇḍa). The "middle peak" (madhya-śṛṅga) is the topmost point of this axis—the Sahasrāra at the crown of the head. Therefore, She is Sumerumadhyaśṛṅgasthā because Her ultimate, supreme station of rest is the crown center of non-dual union.

name: श्रीमन्नगरनायिका
summary: 56. Sreemannagaranayika — Strar TeaaT
i. The head of (nayika) Sreenagaram.
ii. Sreenagaram is always known as Sreemad Lakshmee Vidya “Nagaram and Sreedevee Is its head of state.
iii. This city (nagara) is said to be in two locations; one is as stated in Lalitastavaraja. The other is the centre of Sudhasamudra as told in Rudry Gmala.
iv. Sreemannagaram may also be understood to mean Sree Chakra and hence Sree Chakra nayika. This has also been stated in Goudapa Sutras.

esoteric: The Citadel of Consciousness: Śrī-Nagara is the mystical, multi-walled city described in Śākta cosmology. Esoterically, this city is the Śrī Cakra itself, which maps the entire journey of consciousness from the outer gates of the physical world to the central point of the Self. She is the Nāyikā (the commanding directress) because She rules over every gateway, enclosure, and sub-deity (Āvaraṇa Devatā) within this cosmic diagram.

name: चिन्तामणिगृहान्तस्था
samasa: • चिन्तामणिभिः रचितं गृहं चिन्तामणिगृहम्। तस्य अन्तः तिष्ठतीति।
summary: 57. Chintamanigruhantastha — RearatrperreT
i. One who dwells in a house constructed of Chintamani.

ii. Chintamaniis a gem stone which bestows all the desires of the mind. Chintamanigraha isa palace built with Chintamanigems. She dwells in it The syllables in Sreedevee”s mantra are also capable of bestowing what the mind desires and hence referred to as Chintamani, Sreedevee dwells in those syllables (Bijaksharas).

name: पञ्चब्रह्मासनस्थिता
samasa: • पञ्चभिः ब्रह्मभिः निर्मितं आसनं पञ्चब्रह्मासनम्। तस्मिन् स्थिता।
summary: i. One who sits on a seat made of five Brahmams.

ii. Sreedevee sits, besides Kameshwara, on a seat (cot) whose four legs are Brahma, Vishnu, Rudra&Isana, and the sitting plank is “Sadasiva, When Parabrahmam desires to evolve this world, these five Brahmams help to materialise this desire by carrying out the five functions of creation (Srishti), sustenance (Sthithi), destruction (Samhéra), screaming from the impacts of material pleasure & displeasure (Tirodhdna) and blessings (Anugraha) to bring about the changes. These Brahmams respectively are the legs on Southeast, Southwest, Northwest and Northeast directions and the sitting plank. {ili) Since all the five are constantly in meditation, with their eyes closed, contemplating on Sreedevee, they are called Panchabrahmams. Since all these five emerge from and ultimately converge in the same Parabrahmam, they are also referred to as Brahmams. Sometimes Sreedeveeabsorbs in herself the energies of these five Brahmams like creation, etc. in those situations these five Brahmams are called Panchapretds. Consequently,Sreedeveeis referred to as Panchapretdsandseend (249" name) and Panchapretamanjadhiséyini (947" name). {iv) The reason for these Brahmams being the legs of the cot is their desire to be of faithful service to Sreedevee in close proximity.
searchText: 58 पञ्चब्रह्मासनस्थिता • पञ्चभिः ब्रह्मभिः निर्मितं आसनं पञ्चब्रह्मासनम्। तस्मिन् स्थिता। पञ्चभिः — with five ब्रह्मभिः — brahmā/brahman आसनं — seat स्थिता। — situated i. one who sits on a seat made of five brahmams. ii. sreedevee sits, besides kameshwara, on a seat (cot) whose four legs are brahma, vishnu, rudra&isana, and the sitting plank is “sadasiva, when parabrahmam desires to evolve this world, these five brahmams help to materialise this desire by carrying out the five functions of creation (srishti), sustenance (sthithi), destruction (samhéra), screaming from the impacts of material pleasure & displeasure (tirodhdna) and blessings (anugraha) to bring about the changes. these brahmams respectively are the legs on southeast, southwest, northwest and northeast directions and the sitting plank. {ili) since all the five are constantly in meditation, with their eyes closed, contemplating on sreedevee, they are called panchabrahmams. since all these five emerge from and ultimately converge in the same parabrahmam, they are also referred to as brahmams. sometimes sreedeveeabsorbs in herself the energies of these five brahmams like creation, etc. in those situations these five brahmams are called panchapretds. consequently,sreedeveeis referred to as panchapretdsandseend (249" name) and panchapretamanjadhiséyini (947" name). {iv) the reason for these brahmams being the legs of the cot is their desire to be of faithful service to sreedevee in close proximity. the sovereign of five cosmic functions: the "five brahmās" are the five deities responsible for the cosmic cycles: brahmā (creation), viṣṇu(preservation), rudra (dissolution), īśvara (concealment), and sadāśiva (grace). these five act merely as the physical legs and plank of her royal seat. this shows that the highest cosmic powers are entirely subordinate to her, serving as instruments for her supreme, play-filled commands.
esoteric: The Sovereign of Five Cosmic Functions: The "five Brahmās" are the five deities responsible for the cosmic cycles: Brahmā (Creation), Viṣṇu(Preservation), Rudra (Dissolution), Īśvara (Concealment), and Sadāśiva (Grace). These five act merely as the physical legs and plank of Her royal seat. This shows that the highest cosmic powers are entirely subordinate to Her, serving as instruments for Her supreme, play-filled commands.

name: महापद्माटवीसंस्था
samasa: • महान्ति पद्मानि यस्याम् अटव्यां सम्यक् तिष्ठतीति।
summary: 59. Mahdpadmdtaveesamstha — werraeat ae
i. One who lives in a big Lotus jungle. This jungle is in the centre of Chintamanigraha. (ji) One who lives in the Brahmarandhra,-below the skull, in a Lotus with thousand (countless) petals.

iii. Mahapadma is a superior species of elephants. The forest, inhabited by such elephants is Gajdranyd, also known as Tiruvanaika (a temple near Tiruchirapalli, in Tamilnadu). The presiding deity of this temple is Sree Akiléndeswar)).

(ji) one who lives in the brahmarandhra,-below the skull, in a lotus with thousand (countless) petals. iii. mahapadma is a superior species of elephants. the forest, inhabited by such elephants is gajdranyd, also known as tiruvanaika (a temple near tiruchirapalli, in tamilnadu). the presiding deity of this temple is sree akiléndeswar)). the forest of spiritual centers: in the subtle anatomy of yoga, each energy center (cakra) is represented as a lotus. the "great lotus forest" (mahā-padmāṭavī) represents the entire system of these interconnected spiritual centers within the subtle body. she is saṃsthā (fully established) throughout this forest, indicating that she is the singular, underlying lifeforce running through every single node of the energy network.

name: कदम्बवनवासिनी

i. One who lives in Kadamba woods.
ii. Chintamanigraha is a palace, of gems surrounded by a jungle of Kadamba trees. Sreedevee dwells in this jungle.

iii. Sreepura is surrounded by 25 walls made of metals and gems representing 25 concepts (Tatvas). The Kadamba jungle is located between the gold and silver walls. {iv) Madurai (2 holy city in Tamilnadu) is also known as Kadambavana. is the presiding deity here with the name Meenakshee-
searchText: 60 कदम्बवनवासिनी • कदम्बानां वने वसतीति। वने — forest 60. kadambavanavasinee —aaaaratert i. one who lives in kadamba woods. ii. chintamanigraha is a palace, of gems surrounded by a jungle of kadamba trees. sreedevee dwells in this jungle. iii. sreepura is surrounded by 25 walls made of metals and gems representing 25 concepts (tatvas). the kadamba jungle is located between the gold and silver walls. {iv) madurai (2 holy city in tamilnadu) is also known as kadambavana. is the presiding deity here with the name meenakshee- the realm of sweet manifestation: the kadamba forest represents the thick, lush variety of the manifest world. the kadamba blossom is noted for its spherical, golden shape and sweet fragrance. by dwelling in this forest, she shows that her presence is not confined to ascetic withdrawal; she is actively present and can be experienced within the rich, sweet, and fragrant variety of life itself.
esoteric: The Realm of Sweet Manifestation: The Kadamba forest represents the thick, lush variety of the manifest world. The Kadamba blossom is noted for its spherical, golden shape and sweet fragrance. By dwelling in this forest, She shows that Her presence is not confined to ascetic withdrawal; She is actively present and can be experienced within the rich, sweet, and fragrant variety of life itself.

name: सुधासागरमध्यस्था
summary: 61. Sudh@sdgaramadhyasthé— Uran TereaeAT
i. One who lives in the centre of the ocean of nectar.

i. The location of this ocean of nectar is given differently in various sources, According to Rudrydmalé, the Chintdmanigraha is located in this ocean. {ili) The Vedas say Yoovaithim Brahmanoovedam Amrithendvrotam Purim (City surrounded by nectar) — Aruna Pragné — 27.116. {iv) The Bindusthdnd in the Chandramandala, in the Lotus with thousand petals, below our skull is referred to as Sudhdsindhu (Ocean of nectar).

v. According to Chandogya Upanishad, this ocean is located in the city “of Apardjita, which can be reached by worshipping a god with attributes (Sagunopasand). Aparajita for Sreedevee worshippers is like Kailas for Shiva worshippers and Sree Vaikugtam for Vishgu”s worshippers. There are two tanks in this city called Aram and Nyam, which are referred to as Sudhd Sagara.

vi. In the commentary on the Brahma Sdtra,Andvettih Sabd&h (IV — 42), these oceans of nectar are referred to. (vii)The 8"verse of Soundaryaloharee, starting with Sudhasindhoora madhy@, also talks of Sudha Sagara.

She sits at the very center (madhyasthā) of this reservoir, showing that She is the ultimate source and keeper of that supreme, non-dual joy.

name: कामाक्षी

summary: i. One with beautiful eyes.
ii. One who has (beloved) Kameshwara as her eyes.
iii. One who bestows all desires of Her devotees by Her eyes.

(lv) One who has Saraswathi (KG) and Lokshmee (Ma) as Her eyes. {v) Kancheepuram is one of the 51 Shakti Pitams, where Sreedevee's waist (the place where the waist band, Kanchi, is worn) fell. Brahma performed a sacrifice in this place seeking the powers of creation. Sreedevee was called Kamakshee as she fulfilled Brahma”s desires.
vi. Kama (Cupid) was destroyed by Paramashiva's third eye and restored to life by Sreedevee's vision. Hence Kamakshee. 63. Kémadayinee— ())_ One who fulfills Her devotees desires.
ii. Dayini — one who gives to Her devotees, Kama — Kameshwora, that is, one who leads Her devotees to Kameshwara and become one with Him, sftafrar RENAME Sree Lolita Sohasrandmom

iii. Kamada means Shiva — that is one who destroyed Cupid. Ayini means auspiciously united. Kamadayini means, one who is auspiously united with the destroyer of Kama. {iv) Hereditary property is called Dayam. Sreedevee has Kama as Dayam. Her association with Kama is timeless. {v) 989"name-Vanchith Grthapradiyiniand Trishati_ 43""name — Ipsitharthapradiyinimay be referred.

name: कामदायिनी
summary: i. Meaning text requires manual entry from the Ramamurthy page image.
searchText: 63 कामदायिनी • कामं ददातीति। कामं — desire; kāma i. meaning text requires manual entry from the ramamurthy page image. the giver of the ultimate goal: while she fulfills the mundane, preliminary desires (puruṣārthas of dharma, artha, and kāma) of her devotees to build their trust, her ultimate gift is the supreme desire: मोक्ष (mokṣa — liberation). she is kāmadāyinī because she destroys the desire for limited, worldly objects and replaces it with the singular, consuming desire for self-realization.
esoteric: The Giver of the Ultimate Goal: While She fulfills the mundane, preliminary desires (Puruṣārthas of Dharma, Artha, and Kāma) of Her devotees to build their trust, Her ultimate gift is the supreme desire: मोक्ष (Mokṣa — liberation). She is Kāmadāyinī because She destroys the desire for limited, worldly objects and replaces it with the singular, consuming desire for self-realization.

name: देवर्षिगणसङ्घातस्तूयमानात्मवैभवा
samasa: • देवानां गणश्च ऋषीनां गणश्च यः तयोः सङ्घातेन स्तूयमानः आत्मा (स्वरूपं) यस्य तद्वत् वैभवं यस्याः सा।
summary: i. One whom groups of devas and rishis (gods and sages) pray to, and celebrate.
ii. Vibhu means omnipresent. Vaibhava is omnipresence. Sreedevee is omnipresent so that She can bestow Her devotees” desires every: where and immediately.
iii. Songhatasthooyamana means, one whom groups of devos and sagespray to. Sanghéta also means variously. Devas and sages pray to Her in various forms.

iv. Sanghéta also means a kind of hell. Devas and sages pray to Sreedevee to get released from this hell. (W) If interpreted as Samyak-ghata means totally annihilated. The “annihilation of Bhangdsura to be referred to in the next name is indicated here. {vi) One to whom Devas (Brahma and others), sages (Vasishta and others), Devarishis (Narada and others) and Gagas (Aditya and others) pray.

vii. Atma is the dynamic form of Sreedevee — omnipresent, omnipotent, love in all forms (Sat, chit and ananda — that is existence, knowledge and bliss). Its in this form to whom groups of devas and sages praV” This Atma is the undivided form of Sreedevee. Refer: Sadanand2 Poorna: Svatmaiva Paradevata Lalita (Bhavanopanishad — 28). name: भण्डासुरवधोद्युक्तशक्तिसेनासमन्विता
samasa: • भण्डनामकोऽसुरः भण्डासुरः। तस्य वधे उद्युक्तानां शक्तीनां सेनाभिः सम्यक् अन्विता।
summary: 65. Bhanddsuravadhodyuktashaktisenasamanvité—
i. One who is leading an army of Shakt is intent on destroying the demon called Bhanga.

ii. The objective of devas referred to earlier (in name 5) — that is destruction of Bhangasura, 's indicated here. She stands prepared to achieve that objective with an army of Shaktis in different forms created by Her.
ii. One with whom the several form of Shakti is an integral part. {iv) One who has the power or is capable of destroying ego of which Bhandasura is embodied.

v. When Manmatha (Cupid) was reduced to ashes by the fire of Shiva's third eye, Lord Ganapathy collected the ashes and made a male figure out of them. It came alive, did penance and obtained many boons from Shiva. Brahma acclaimed this achievement saying. Bhand, Bhand (good, good) and hence he got the name Bhandasura. His cruel (demonist) character was the result of his emergence from the ashes arising out of the fire of anger of Shiva. Sreedevee came with the army of Shaktis to destroy him.

vi. Bhanga was bound by his-own ignorance, and lived with the thought that sensual pleasures are supreme (Asushu Mamthee Asurah). He was ignorant of his Atmaswardpa (soul), It was necessary to destroy his cruel (demonistic) character. What was needed was, effort (Udyama}; when effort was mobilised, the power réquired to destroy this cruel character would be generated to help him. The power of soul (atma), hidden in a state of ignorance, when emerges by a process of knowledge, evolves as various forms of Shakti. The real concept (tatvartha) is that, the power of soul, hitherto hidden from Jeevé by ignorance, comes out as powerful Shaktis when effort is made to achieve self realisation.
vii. Gougapada Sutra(16) states Bhangasura Honandrtham Eeka Eevo Aneeka.

name: चक्रराजरथारूढसर्वायुधपरिष्कृता
samasa: • चक्रराजाख्यं रथम् आरूढैः सर्वैः आयुधैः परिष्कृता।
• श्रीचक्रमेव (श्रीविद्या) रथम्। तम् आरूढानि यानि सर्वायुधानि (आत्मज्ञानसाधनानि) तैः परिष्कृता।
summary: i. One who is mounted on the Sree Chakra chariot surrounded by all kinds of weapons. | ii) Sree Chakra is considered as the king of all Chakras (Yantras of gods) and hence is called Sree Chakraraja, Sreedevee is always present in this, and this is also the chariot of Sreedevee. The nine dvarands of Sree Chakra are the nine tiers of the chariot. The area of Sree Chakra is 4 yojands and the height 10 yojanas.
iii. The Sarvanandamaya Chakra in the centre of the Sree Chakro shines as the flag on the top of the chariot. itis called Anandadwoja (flag of bliss).

iv. The four weapons of Sreedevee, namely, Pasa (Noose), “Ankusa(goad), sugarcane bow and flower arrows mount the chariot before Sreedevee enters and take their positions around Her seat, assuming the form of Shaktis. Similarly all the other weapons also mounted the chariot assuming their respective forms of Shaktis. Normally a soldier mounts the chariot carrying all the weapons, but here the weapons assume the forms of Shaktis and mount the chariot on their own (that is the meaning of the word aroogha).. {v) These weapons are the means for achieving self realisation,

vi. The chakrardja chariot has the six wheels as the base, or the means for controlling or breaking through the six chakras take the form of the weapons (meaning — suddha Vidya — Sreedevee is adorned by these weapons.
