name: गेयचक्ररथारूढमन्त्रिणीपरिसेविता
samasa: • गेयं चक्रं यस्य तादृशः रथः यस्य (सर्यमण्डलस्य) तस्मिन् आरूढभिः मन्त्रिणीभिः परिसेविता।
summary: 69. Geyachakrarathdroodhamantrineeparisevité
i. One who is worshipped by Mantrini Deve who rides the Geya Chakra,

i. Shyamala is a Shakti created by Sreedevee out of her sugarcane bow “as an embodiment of mind energy. Her complexion is that of raincloud, and her knowledge of music is profound, She has a Veena in “one hand and a parrot in another. She is capable of bestowing on her devotees, sweet voice, knowledge of music and power to attract the mind. She has taken charge of administrative functions of Sreedevee's kingdom. [Refer 786" name — Mantrineeyastha Rajyadhooh}. {ili) In Sreedevee”s army, Mantrinee occupies a position next to Her. In statecraft, the king/ queen may have to have consultations on many matters without other's knowledge. This process is known “mantralochana. Just as mantras are said to be secret, matters in statecraft are also referred to by the same name. One who renders such advise is called Mantree/ Mantrinee. {iv) The name of her chariot is Geyachakra. Geya means famous. It has seven tiers. When it moves, the sound it makes is like musical notes, and that is another reason, why it is called Geya (musical) chariot Yoginis occupy the seven tiers of this chariot. (W) Gey — important; Chakraratha — Devee mounted on a circular chariot; aroogha — one who contemplates on; Mantrini— one who is worshiped by those in itiated into the mantra.
vi. The concept, of contemplating Sreedevee and self as one and the same, is explained in the tantrardja. This has to be learnt only through a proper teacher:

vii. Mantrinee was once born as the daughter of sage Matanga and was thus known as Matangee. Typical of women living in jungles, she wears earrings made of palm leaf, and headgear made of wild flowers. The very sound of the Veena in her hands killed her enemies. The parrot in her hand was Veda personified. Dhanurveda, which is a part of the Vedas, was made into bow (called Chitrajeeva) and arrows and given to Sreedevee. The Veena and the parrot in the hands of Shyamala became the Shaktis known as Yantrini and Tantrinee.

(vill) If themantras are contemplated with controlled mind and clear intellect, they convert the mind into Sreedevee”s form. This is “mantrasiddhi (fruition of the power of mantra). One who attains that status is Mantree. 70. _ Kirichakraratharoodhadandanathapuraskruta —
i. One who is piloted by Dandanatha riding the chariot called Kirichakra.

ii. Kiri (kiti) means 2 Vardha (Boar). Dandandtha”s chariot is in the shape of a Boar. {iii) Dandanatha is also known as Vardhi. She was created by Sreedevee from her five arrows. She is the commander-in-chief of Sreedevee”s “army. Head of Dandanatha army — knight. She carries a weapon Called Dandayutha in her hand. She has a very strong physique like @ wild boar and hence Varahi.
iv. She is shrewd enough to act as per Sreedevee's will and hence known as Sankeeta.
v. Agnachakreshwari — one who is responsible for carrying our Sreedevee's orders.

vi. One who resides in the Agnachakrain the human body. he Kirichakra has five tiers, which are occupied by Yoginis. (vilf) Kiri also means the cycle of creation, sustenance and destruction; Dandandtha is mounted on the chariot symbolising this cycle. Even then Dandanatha — Apuraskrita — one who does not come under the control of Yama (Lord of death). The purport of this is that Yama will not approach those who concentrate on Sreedevee in their mind,

summary: i. One who dwells in the centre of the fort of fire built by Jwalamalini.

ii. In the Sree Chakra, there are fifteen Nitya Devees seated, five each “on the three sides of the triangle around the Bindhu. They are immortal and symbolising the time. Jwélamalini is worshipped on the fourteenth day of bright half and on the second day of the dark half.

iii. According to Lolithopakyand, in the course of the battle with Bhangasurd, in order to protect the army, Sreedevee instructed Jwalémélini to build a fort of fire with 100 yojana area and 30 yojana height, in the centre of which She staged with Her army.

iv. Jwalamalini represents the five triangles of Shakti and Vahniprakara represents the four triangles of Shiva (in the form of fire); Akshiptaintegral, that is all the nine triangles are integrated in the centre of which (Bindusthana) is Sreedevee. (v) As mentioned in the previous name, although She is the cause of three functions viz. creation, sustenance and destruction, She is not affected or does not undergo any change on account these acts. summary: i. One who is pleased with the valour of the Shaktis, engaged in destroying Bhanga”s army.
ii. Sreedevee created an army of Shaktis out of her own self to destroy Bhanda”s army and She was pleased when Her army achieved that objective.

ii. Bhangasura did not realise his self due to his ignorance. He had created his army out of his own mental aberrations, which was the result of his mental differentiation. To counter this Sreedevee herself took various forms of Shaktis and destroyed that ignorance. She was pleased with the gradual destruction of ignorance and “emergence of knowledge.

name: मन्त्रिण्यम्बाविरचितविषङ्गवधतोषिता
summary: 75. — Mantrinyambdvirachitavishangavadhatoshité —
i. One who was pleased by the killing of the demon Vishangan by Mantrinee Devee (Shyamal6).

ii. The demon Bhandan did periance to Lord Rudra and got the power to create demons as per his will. Accordingly he created the demon called Visukran from his right shoulder and the demon called Vishangan from his left shoulder. He treated both of them as his brothers. He appointed Vishangan as his minister and Visukran as his commander-in-chief. Shyamola, the Mantrini Devee, killed Vishangan. Sreedevee was pleased by seeing this.

iii. Shangam means interést. Vi means. wrongful. Hence Vishangan means wrongful attraction. That is, indulging into wrongful acts by “the organs. Mantrini in the form Mantra Shakti of Sreedevee destroyed this wrongful attraction.

esoteric: Overcoming Spiritual Apathy: Viṣaṅga literally means "hostile attachment" or "crooked path." i. One who created Sree Ganeshwara just by looking at the face of Sree Kameshwara.

ii. By seeing the destruction of his army, Bhandan ordered Visukran to set the Vighnayantram. Made by illusion of demons, this yantram was kept in the midst of the army of Sreedevee. On account of this, the army of Sreedevee was affected by the following eight feelings — laziness, disinterest in fighting, an inferiority complex that we cannot do it, fatigue, feeling sleepy, unconsciousness, lack of moti vation like cowards and loss of self respect. Montrinee complained to Sreedevee about this. She just made a glimpse at SreeKémeshwara, who was seated by Her side, The two sights merged with smile and affection. esoteric: The Birth of Wisdom from Pure Seeing: This names the birth of Ganesha. Esoterically, Kāmeśvara is the supreme father (Prakāśa), and Lalitā is the mother (Vimarśa). Her gazing upon His face is the reflective look of Self-awareness (विमर्श रूप). Out of this mutual, ecstatic gazing—without any physical contact—arises Gaṇeśvara (the lord of the inner categories of mind). Ganesha represents the birth of pure spiritual wisdom and obstacle-removing discrimination, born directly from the non-dual union of stillness and reflection.

When this wisdom dawns, it completely breaks the complex machinery of subconscious limitations, bringing deep joy (praharṣitā) to the inner self.

She does all these through Her ten finger nails without any effort.

name: कामेश्वरास्त्रनिर्दग्धसभंडासुरशून्यका
samasa: • कामेश्वरस्य यद् अस्त्रं तस्य अग्निना निर्दग्धं भंडासुरेण सहितं शून्यकाख्यं (नगरं) यया सा।
summary: 82. Kameshwardstranirdagdhasabhandasurashoonyaké— ants
i. One who burnt Bhangasura and his city Sdnyaka using the flames from the missile Kameshwara.

ii. Bhandasura built Sonitha and Sdnyaka exclusively for his capital cities. The missile Maha Kameshworais still powerful than Mahépasupatham. This missile completely cleared Bhandasura and his two cities. Soonyam means “Nil”, Typical of the name, the city has become “Nil”.

iii. Sonitha means blood. The blodd is the one which strengthens arrogance in human mind. Because it was as red as blood, it was called Sonitha. Sunyaka — zero. It is an illusion that there is something available. In this battle the remainders were Bhanga and his two cities. They were also made to ashes without even a symptom by Kameshastra.
iv. The names so far explained how the Jeeva matures to liberation to reach liberation (moksha). This name explains that nothing remains — only the sinya remains.

v. Kameshwara is the form of Atma — self. Atmana kamaya sarvam priyam bhavathi — that means — likes everything and liked by every“thing and hence the Chit Atma is Kameshwara. Bhandasura has mental differentiation and hence has Dwaito in his mind. This mental aberration has been burnt in the chit blaze of Kameshwara. Hence Jeevé has merged with chitatma — self. The balance is only a “null” —The form of Chinmaya.

esoteric: Dissolving the Illusion of the Void: Śūnyaka literally means "the void" or "nothingness." It is the citadel of Bhaṇḍāsura. While standard practices can dismantle individual ego-thoughts, the root impression of ignorance (Mūlāvidyā) often retreats into a blank void of deep sleep or passive emptiness (Śūnya). The Kāmeśvarāstra is the weapon of supreme, self-luminous non-dual realization. It burns down not just the active ego, but the very "void" or blank canvas of ignorance, leaving only the pristine light of pure consciousness.

name: हरनेत्राग्निसन्दग्धकामसञ्जीवनौषधिः
samasa: • हरस्य नेत्रेण अग्निना सम्यक् दग्धस्य कामस्य सञ्जीवनौषधिः।
summary: 84. Haranetragnisandagdhakémasanjeevanoushadhi: 1m of emer-
i. One who gave the herb — Sanjeevi that revived Cupid's life after he was burnt by flame from the eyes of Haran.

i. Sanjeevini is a herb. it has the capacity to revive a dead person. Jeevathu is another name for this. (ii) It is stated in the story that after killing of Bhangasura, Sreedevee revived the life of Cupid as per the requests of all the Devas starting from Brahma.
iv. A child punished by the father goes to the mother for support. In the same fashion, Cupid, who was burnt by the father Paramashivo, got back his life from mother Sreedevee,

v. As per the saying Harourushtegurusthratha Gurouruchenakacchano = When Haranis angry the teacher will support, but when the teacher himself is angry there is no one to support. The word Guru instead of teacher, can mean Sreedevee. (Refer 603" name Guru Moorthy).

vi. The word Hara means plucking of self embodiment — guiding to do this — netha —i,e. AVidya or cause as ignorance — Since it is pervaded in all places like a fire, it is called as Haranethragni. By this ignorance, Samdakta — completely burnt out, Kama — by the feeling of the soul in form of Kameshwara Som — utterly: i.e, by completely destroying all the root cause, ignorance Jeeva — a herb to reach the reality of God “She is the herb to make the ignorant reach the reality of God bY ty destroying all the root causes. it is mentioned that by praying to Sreedevee in the form of Vidhya, the ignorance is removed, one can reach the actual and real self and the liberation.

vii. Haran, who does the job of.destruction is Virakthar— does not have interest or feeling in any of the worldly affairs. Hence he burnt Cupid (attachment). Sreedevee attracted the same Haron towards Her and made Him, Eshwaran for Cupid also — Kameshworan. The eyes of Haran were filled with anger. When Kameshwaran”s sight merged with that of Komeshwari, it became the herb to give rebirth to Cupid.

viii. The Brahmam, which got the power from Haran, destroyed the whole world. Eshwaran made this as raw material and merged again with Brahmam. Again, Sreedevee, by the sight of Kameshwaran resolved to create the world. She became a B8rahmam called Sadéshivan and started to create the world. She is the one who desired to recreate the world (names 264 to 274 may be referred).

ix. Going ahead the subtle form of Sreedevee is described. That is in three forms viz., subtle, subtler and subtlest: Panchadasee Vidyaswaroopa — subtlest; Kémokalakshararoopa — subtler: kungalineeroopa — subtle. Out of these, first, subtle is explained in “the next 3 names.

She acts as the Sañjīvana-auṣadhi (the life-giving medicine) to resurrect Him. This represents the transmutation of desire. She does not resurrect carnal desire; rather, She revives desire in its highest, most auspicious form—as भक्ति(Bhakti — love for the divine) and इच्छा (Icchā — the creative will to manifest good in the world).

name: मूलकूटत्रयकलेवरा

i. One who has Her body (in the subtle form) identical to the moolomantra itself with all its three groups or combination of letters.

iv. Kamakald has one Bindhu at the top, two in the centre and a triangle at the bottom. Similar is Her body profile.

summary: i. One who revelsonly in kulamrutham — the nectar flowing from the

ii. So far Sreedevee'”s gross and subtle forms were described. Now the subtle form of energy called Kundalinee in every human being is being discussed. The sushumnd nddi runs from the bottom of the butt, till inner part of the skull through the centre of the body along the back bone. In this, the Kungalinee energy sleeps at the bottom tip, in the form of a snake woven itself in three and half rounds, keeping its head in the centre downwards. If this Kundalinee energy can be awakened by practicing Yoga, it passes through sushumnd Nadi and reaches the inner part of the skull. When this energy bangs sited there, melts like butter, seeps, slips down and flows through“out the body as nectar. The nectar thus flows, is called kulémrutha. Sreedevee whoops it up in this juice.

name: कुलसङ्केतपालिनी
summary: i. One who guards the esoteric doctrine of the Koulas.

i. The energy called Koula is kept in a secret place in the body. That energy is sleeping. To awaken the same; raise it upwards and make the Kulémrutm flowalong the ndgis of the body, is beyond our knowledge. Why is this energy steeping without being useful? How to awaken it? How does this awakened Kundalinee gives various powers (siddis)? All these questions have been answered in a cryptic “manner in Tontrasastrés. It is explained in a ciphered way so that “whoever has the capability can only understand this. The knowledge about Koula (kundalinee) is “also called Koula. Its secret language is “cryptic. Kulasanketapdlinee protects it so that the ineligible cannot understand it. (iti) The cryptic language detailing about kundalineeis only known to Her and Her devotees. Hénce Kulasanketapdlinee.

iv. Sastras mention Achdram also as kulam, These Achdrams are cryptic — secrets. Sreedevee protects then”. (v) There are three cryptic ways in the pooja (updsand) of Sreedevee + cryptic chakras, cryptic mantras and cryptic pooja.

name: समयाचारतत्परा
summary: i. One who is interested in Samayachara.

ii. The samaya method is an internal worshipping format. Once in itiated, with the blessings of the teacher, through Shatvidaikya nusanthanam and Chaturvidaikya Anusanthanam get Pavang Tartyam on a Mahanavami day and get the Mahaveda sacrament (Somskéram) done. After all these, the aspirant worships Sreedevee in the following method. Sreedevee, who is sleeping alone in the Moolédhdram, awakes up, dressés Herself in Her secluded place of Swadishtanam, She comes out of Manipoorakam, as a gorgeous lady to beseen by everybody. There the aspirant Her,47 types of upachdrams starting from Pédyam and ending with Bhooshana Dhdranam. She accepts them all and sits in a chair, made of gems, alongwith her group (parivaram). From there She moves on to An Ghatam and accepts the prayers upto Tamboola Dhéranam (chewing of betel leaves). After accepting Arati at Vishuddi, She accepts Neerdjanam and other upacharams at Ajno. happily alongwith Shiva behind a screen, She leaves Shiva and returns to Moolédhdra to.hide again. In this way through imagination, enjoying the game of the grace (Chitshakti) is called Somayacharam. By alchemy copper becomes gold. In a similar fashion the aspirant becomes Sreedevee Herself by continuous perception.

ii. Without any of the tools used in Bahya Pooja, the aspirant imagines the Sree Chakra in mind, going forward experience the union of Shiva and Shakti and through this he also experiences the union of teacher, mantra, Sreedevee, Sree Chakra and self. The chakras imagined as the Avaranams. The Kungalineeis imagined as Sree Devee with the three groups “of Panchadashee, respectively from Her face till bottom. name: मूलाधारैकनिलया
summary: i. One whose main residence is Mooladharam.

ii. The backbone of the body is called Meru Dhandam. The navi called “Sushumna runs inside this. The bottom most part of this is the butt: In the midst of this, the Lingo stays in a place called Moolédharam. Next the chakra called Swadhishtanam, which resides in the root location of the Linga. Third Manipoorakam is at lower abdomen, Andhatam at the chest area, Vishuddhi in the neck and Ajna, the sixth one, in between two eye brows. Above all these six is the (Padmam). Out of these six, respectively a pair of chakras is called as Kondam. These are called Agni (fire), Soorya (Sun) and Chandra (Moon) Kangas. Above and below each of the Kandas there are two knots (Granthis).

iii. Out of these, Mooladhdra Lotus has four petals. Kundalinee always sleeps, keeping it head downwards, in the bindhu called Kulakundam, in a karnika in this chakra. Hence it is said as: Sreedevee, in the form of Kundalinee, has Moolédhéra as Her main dwelling.
iv. It is called Moolddhara, since it is the base for Kundalinee and also the root of Sushumna.

summary: 100. Brahmagranthivibhedinee -weru Purasaiett
i. One who pierces through the above said Brahma Granthi.

i. As explained in the previous name description, the Kandam for the first two chakras is called Agni (fire) Kandam. This has Srushti (creation) chakras as the dwelling of Brahma whose role is the creation. {iii) In the religion of Samaya, these six chakras are supposed as Sree Chakra. The Srushtichakra has three Avaronas as Chaturasram, Shogasadalam and Ashtadalam.

iv. The knot or Granthi is a small hole like between two chakras. The Kungalinee, starts from Moolddhéra to enter Swddhishtana and “again from there to enter Manipooraka, has to pierce and travel “through these holes like knots only. The first knot is called Brahma Granthi in the place of Brahma. (The verb Granth means shortening. a long one. A book is called a Grantham since a long subject is shortened and given in it. searchText: 109 महासक्तिः • महे आसक्तिः यस्या सा। • महती आ समन्तात् सक्तिः यस्याः सा। महे — in the great; in festival/splendour आसक्तिः — attachment; fondness महती — great सक्तिः — attachment; joinedness महासक्तिः — she of great attachment or great power i. the word mahé has two meaning - festival or splendour asakti means immense joy ii. one who is fond of festival (the union of shiva and shakti). this has been clearly explained in the 9th verse of soundaryalaharee: 'maheem mooladhare kamopi manipoore hutavaham sthitam svadhishtane hrudhi marutamakdshamuparh 'manopi 8hrumadye sokolamapi bhitva kulapatham iii. the same description is made by sree adi sankara in his chintamani 'stavam; varane pumsé sankamya sankamya rasam prasoothe iv. one who is very much interested in celebrating offering of prayers asa festival v. the word mahat means - so huge expanding to all the directions. her greatness is spread in all directions vi. her unison is spread in all the places and is visible even in an atom the gravity of cosmic love: bhāskararāya unpacks the dual layers of sakti(attachment). on the transcendent plane, she holds an unbreakable attachment (maha-āsakti) to shiva, ensuring their eternal union. on the manifest plane, her attachment takes the form of infinite, all-encompassing compassion (karuṇā) that acts like a magnetic pull, drawing every individual soul back toward the source of liberation.
esoteric: The Gravity of Cosmic Love: Bhāskararāya unpacks the dual layers of Sakti(attachment). On the transcendent plane, She holds an unbreakable attachment (Maha-āsakti) to Shiva, ensuring their eternal union. On the manifest plane, Her attachment takes the form of infinite, all-encompassing compassion (Karuṇā) that acts like a magnetic pull, drawing every individual soul back toward the source of liberation.

esoteric: The Coiled Power of Evolution: This is the direct, structural title of the evolutionary force within the human body. As the Kuṇḍalinī, She rests at the base center, coiled three and a half times like a snake. This coiled state represents potential energy. When awakened through systematic yoga and discrimination, this potential energy unfolds, straightening its path through the central channel to guide the practitioner toward non-dual liberation.

esoteric: The Infinite Subtlety of Awareness: Bhāskararāya notes that the fiber inside a lotus stalk (bisa-tantu) is nearly invisible to the naked eye, yet it runs unbroken through the entire length of the stem. This represents the ultimate subtlety (sūkṣmatva) of the awakened Kuṇḍalinī as She enters the central channel. Consciousness is not a gross physical object; it is the finest, most microscopic thread of awareness that permeates the entire physical structure, completely uncatchable by the gross senses yet holding the entire network together.

name: भवानी

summary: i. Warf

ii. The word Bhava has three meanings - Lord Mahadeva, the human life cycle and Cupid, She gives life (Anayathee) to all these three and hence Bhavanee. (li) Since LordParamashiva creates all the living things his name is Bhava. His consort is Bhavanee

iii. l) Out of the eight forms of LordParamashiva, the form of water is called Bhava. His consort is Bhavanee. One who gives life to the living form of water is Bhavénee. (Tayonmruga Jeevase - Sree Rudra verse has to be reminded), (iv) The name of Sreedevee in Sthaneshwara Shetra is Bhavanee

iv. In the 22"verse of Soundaryalaharee, assuming two usages for the word Bhavanee the grace ofSreedevee is explained. The word Bhavanee is used as an exclamation and as a predicate. A devotee thinks to address Sreedevee as Bhavaneeland ask Her - why Be raierar BEAT Sree Lait Sohasrandmam can't you pass your graceful sight on the slave like me? In this regard the devotee just starts with the two words "Bhavanee You" - Instantly Sreedevee takes the request as "I have to become you". She provides the salvation,which is rare even for the Devas including Brahma also. 'thus goes the verse: bhavani tvam dase mayi vitara drushtim sakeruném iti sthothum vanchan kathayati bhavani tvamiti ya: tadaiva tvam tasmai dishasi nijasdsyujya padaveem 'mukunda brahmendrasputa makuta neerdjitapadém | v. mahashodanyésam (prapanchanyasam) says that she is in the form destruction of the universe the quickener of existence: bhava means existence or the manifest universe. śiva in his absolute state remains unmoving, like a silent corpse (śava). she is bhavānī because she infuses life, movement, and reality into him, transforming him into bhava (the living lord of existence). as the root statement of the śāktaagamas goes: "without her, he cannot even stir." she is the grand animator who brings the world into active being[cite: 1].
esoteric: The Quickener of Existence: Bhava means existence or the manifest universe. Śiva in His absolute state remains unmoving, like a silent corpse (Śava). She is Bhavānī because She infuses life, movement, and reality into Him, transforming Him into Bhava (the living lord of existence). As the root statement of the ŚāktaAgamas goes: "Without Her, He cannot even stir." She is the grand animator who brings the world into active being[cite: 1].

name: भावनागम्या
wordByWord: भावनागम्या — She who is reached by contemplation
summary: i. HATTA (One who can be realised through devoted meditaion
ii. Thus the five forms viz. physical, gross, subtle, subtler and subtlest, of Sreedeveewere described and now the ideas to reach them are being mentioned
iii. l) The method of adoration through meditation is explained in Pavanopanishad. SreeBhaskaraRdya himself has written @ method to use this
iv. The 870" name - Antharmukhasamaradhya can be referred
v. As per the saying Yadbhavam Tadbhavati - we become that whichever was incessantly thought of. A worm becoming a grub is being taken as an instance in this regard

vi. Meditation is of two types -One is about the word and the other is about the meaning. Relating to the word - is the one - To worship the words i.e. sounds, (it has to be taken as holl religious words or sounds) as taught by the teacher and as per his wish. In his book Meemamsa Vada Koutoohalam, Sree Baskara Rayar writes that he has described it only by the wish of Lord Eshwara. (vii) Sree Bhaskararya says that he has described (in the above said 'Meemamsa Vada Koutoohalam) that the meditation relating to meaning (meditation tending towards action Pravrttiroopa)and the sensuous form are co-related
vii. il) Two opposite meanings can be given - She is unreachable by all these by splitting Bhava + Agamyé or She Is reachable by splitting as Bhavan + Agamya

viii. It can also be interpreted as meditation with word Is Saguga Upasand (meditation imagining some form). In due course when it becomes meditation with meaning, it becomes Wirgupg Updsana(meditation on the formless and attribute-less)

ix. Inaddition, by interpreting the word Bhavand as attaining salvation through religious rites (karma mérga), the meaning is conveyed as before by splitting as AgomydorAgamya. Another meaning is that She is not attainable through religious rites (karma mérga); One other meaning is She Is attainable by untainted mind got through religious rites (karma marga)

x. Or - (Bhavana) Meditationis of three types. It is mentioned in the KoormaPurdna as; Brahmi, Maheshworee Chaiva Tatalvakshara Bhavandl Tisrastu Bhdvand Rudra Vortante Satatam Dvjé:ll Le. Brahmi, Mahesvarl and Aksara type of meditations are always with Lord Rudra

'Second - Meditating the chit energy of Lord Parameshweara, Third - Saguga Upasano - meditation imagining some form or other

name: भवारण्यकुठारिका
samasa: • भवः एव आरण्यः। तस्य कुठारेव कुठारिका।
wordByWord: भवारण्यकुठारिका — She who is the axe to the forest of worldly existence
summary: i. waroaperttent
ii. One who is like an axe for clearing the jungle of transmigratory existence (the ocean of Samséra)

iii. The forest is dense with trees which grow again and again. Similarly this life cycle (Samséra Ocean) also, makes repeated birth again and again on account of cravings on various external objects in this world. The forest will be destroyed only when all the trees are cut. In the same way only if the desires on the worldly matters are destructed the Samsara forest will be shattered. She is mentioned to.be the axe to cut the trees,
iv. tt has to be understood that once we get the knowledge of Sreedevee the bonding to this world will be cut
searchText: 114 भवारण्यकुठारिका • भवः एव आरण्यः। तस्य कुठारेव कुठारिका। भवः — worldly existence आरण्यः — forest कुठारिका — small axe भवारण्यकुठारिका — she who is the axe to the forest of worldly existence i. waroaperttent ii. one who is like an axe for clearing the jungle of transmigratory existence (the ocean of samséra) iii. the forest is dense with trees which grow again and again. similarly this life cycle (samséra ocean) also, makes repeated birth again and again on account of cravings on various external objects in this world. the forest will be destroyed only when all the trees are cut. in the same way only if the desires on the worldly matters are destructed the samsara forest will be shattered. she is mentioned to.be the axe to cut the trees, iv. tt has to be understood that once we get the knowledge of sreedevee the bonding to this world will be cut clearing the forest of bondage: the world of endless births and deaths (saṃsāra) is compared to a wild, tangled forest (bhava-āraṇya) because it is dark, full of hidden dangers (desires, delusions), and highly difficult to escape[cite: 1]. she is the kuṭhārikā (the axe) because the dawn of her grace provides the exact intellectual sharpness needed to completely cut down the root of this ignorance, clearing the path to instant liberation.
esoteric: Clearing the Forest of Bondage: The world of endless births and deaths (Saṃsāra) is compared to a wild, tangled forest (bhava-āraṇya) because it is dark, full of hidden dangers (desires, delusions), and highly difficult to escape[cite: 1]. She is the kuṭhārikā (the axe) because the dawn of Her grace provides the exact intellectual sharpness needed to completely cut down the root of this ignorance, clearing the path to instant liberation.

searchText: 115 भद्रप्रिया • भद्रं प्रियं यस्याः सा। भद्रं — auspiciousness; good प्रियं — dear भद्रप्रिया — she who loves auspiciousness i. gitar ii. one who is fond of everything auspicious iii. l) sheis too fond of offering all the auspicious things to her devotees iv. a kind of species in elephants is called bhadram. she has affection to that species of elephants the affection for true welfare: bhadram in the vedas refers to the highest spiritual welfare and goodness. she is bhadrapriyā because she takes delight in the genuine well-being, virtue, and non-dual evolution of her devotees. she actively guides seekers away from harmful attachments, replacing them with the ultimate auspiciousness of a peaceful mind.
esoteric: The Affection for True Welfare: Bhadram in the Vedas refers to the highest spiritual welfare and goodness. She is Bhadrapriyā because She takes delight in the genuine well-being, virtue, and non-dual evolution of Her devotees. She actively guides seekers away from harmful attachments, replacing them with the ultimate auspiciousness of a peaceful mind.

wordByWord: भक्तसौभाग्यदायिनी — She who gives good fortune to devotees
summary: i. waetaraartett
ii. One who grants all round advancement to Her devotees

iii. in the Agni Purdna the word Bhaga has been given various meanings like - wealth, desire, praising stories, vigor, preparation, illumination, pride, etc. Since She holds all these, She has a name Subhagé (name 761). Her nature is prosperity (Soubhdgyam)
iv. Also exemplary and fortune are other meanings. The one,who con. templates all these, is Soubhagyam. One who grants prosperity (Soubhdgyam) to Her devotees

v. Again, as per Agni Purdnam, since these eight things are used in auspicious occasions - Sugar Cane, Peepul tree, sprouted cumin, coriander, cow's milk (including its variations as curd, butter, ghee, 'etc,), things that are in yellow colour, flowers and salt, these are called Soubhagyashtokam (eight auspicious things). It can also be understood as She offers all these to Her devotees. 'the 559th name Bhagavan, he quotes the verse with the meaning below and says that one who has all these is called Bhagavan: Bhaga means complete wealth, charity, glory, richness, relinguishment, and liberation from all the bondage. It is appropriate to mean that She provides all these to the devotees. name: भक्तिगम्या
wordByWord: भक्तिगम्या — She who is reached through devotion
summary: i. ). Three interpretations can be made for this name. By true devotion Shewill be present, can be attained and can be recognised

ii. Inthe Veda: Parénchikhani Vyatrunat Swayambhoo: Tasmat Pardnpashyati Nantardtman! Kaschiddheera: Pratyagatmanamaikshadavruita Chakshu: Amrutatvamichchant The self-existent (Swayambhoo:) constraints the senses. They turn to external objects; therefore man sees the external objects, not the internal self; but'the wise, with eyeaverted, and desirous of immor-tality, behold the inner self. (lil) The Veda also says, the Yogins see the eternal Lord. in the Yoga Sutra, Rdja_Marthénda interprets Pranidhéna-_(in Eshwaropranidhéndtva) as devotion. (iv) In the Brahma Satra also (\ll-2-24) Api Sampradhane Pratyakshénumdnabhydm (in the state of the entire universe being receded and the soul is in the form, which cannot ordinarily be perceived, can be comprehended by the Yogis through devotion/ meditation. To be seen like this, to focus on the soul ete., are evidenced through Vedas (Sree Adi Sankara's commentaries). Even in the unperceivable nature, the Brahmam can be comprehended through devotion

iii. In Sreemad Bhagavat Gita also it has been stressed that it can be reached only through devotion (11-54): 'Bhaktya Tvananyaya Shakya Ahamevam Vidhorjuna | Gyatum Drashtum Cha Tatvena Praveshtum Cha Parantopa Il 'The word Praveshtum here means liberation in the form of Brahmam. Hence in this name we can mean Gamya as "She can be reached."

iv. In Chanthogya Upanishad (ll 23.2) - Brahmasamosttho Amrutatvameti- One who stays in'the Brahmam is immortal. In this context the word 'stays' means devotion. (vil)it is the apt meaning for the word 'stay', since it has been mentioned in Sandilya Sdtra as Tatsamsttasyém Rutatvopadesat (I - 4) 'and in Brahma Satra as Tannishtasya Brahmopadesat (|- 1-7)

v. In NityaTantra also, after describing the nature of devotion, it is mentioned that all the worldly substances can be obtained through this. There itself it is mentioned that the people who are without devotion would suffer both in this world and the celestial worlds,

vi. Or - devotion also a meaning of characteristic features. (Sree Bhaskara Rayar quotes this as used in Jaimini Satra). In this regard, it can be interpreted as, even though She is actually invisible, Her form can be recognised through Hercharacteristic features. The 'words truth and knowledge mean divine bliss. But they explain only the characteristic features and not the actual form. (Yato Vache Nivarthanthe - cannot be explained). Hence the 72" name in Trishati -Lakshanagamyo. (xd For the 192" name in Trishati - Labdhabhaktisulobha - Sree Adi Sankarahas given two interpretations: a, She can be easily identified by those knowledgeable people who have realised Brahmam through devotion
vii. b. She will be present to those who have ardent devotion on Her without any thought of anything else

(vil)it is the apt meaning for the word 'stay', since it has been mentioned in sandilya sdtra as tatsamsttasyém rutatvopadesat (i - 4) 'and in brahma satra as tannishtasya brahmopadesat (|- 1-7) v. in nityatantra also, after describing the nature of devotion, it is mentioned that all the worldly substances can be obtained through this. there itself it is mentioned that the people who are without devotion would suffer both in this world and the celestial worlds, vi. or - devotion also a meaning of characteristic features. (sree bhaskara rayar quotes this as used in jaimini satra). in this regard, it can be interpreted as, even though she is actually invisible, her form can be recognised through hercharacteristic features. the 'words truth and knowledge mean divine bliss. but they explain only the characteristic features and not the actual form. (yato vache nivarthanthe - cannot be explained). hence the 72" name in trishati -lakshanagamyo. (xd for the 192" name in trishati - labdhabhaktisulobha - sree adi sankarahas given two interpretations: a, she can be easily identified by those knowledgeable people who have realised brahmam through devotion vii. b. she will be present to those who have ardent devotion on her without any thought of anything else the portal of devotion: intellectual analysis can map out the structural parameters of the mind, but it cannot touch the living essence of the absolute. she is bhaktigamyā because bhakti acts as the direct shortcut that bypasses the doubts and limits of the logical intellect, opening a clear, intuitive path straight into the heart of reality.
esoteric: The Portal of Devotion: Intellectual analysis can map out the structural parameters of the mind, but it cannot touch the living essence of the Absolute. She is Bhaktigamyā because Bhakti acts as the direct shortcut that bypasses the doubts and limits of the logical intellect, opening a clear, intuitive path straight into the heart of reality.

esoteric: The Complete Captivity by Love: This name contains a stunning theological declaration: the supreme, omnipotent ruler of infinite universes, who is completely independent (Svātantrya-śakti), willingly surrenders Her autonomy and becomes parādhīnā (dependent) before the unselfish love of a true bhakta[cite: 1]. The intensity of devotion dissolves the distance between the worshiper and the worshiped, making Her completely one with the seeker's inner voice.

esoteric: The Destruction of Dualistic Fear: As the Upaniṣads explicitly declare: "Verily, fear arises only when there is a second entity" (Dvitīyād vai bhayaṃ bhavati). Fear is a natural consequence of duality—the fear of loss, death, or judgment by another. She is Bhayāpahā because She dissolves the very illusion of a "second entity." By revealing that the entire universe is Her own self-projection, She establishes the practitioner in the fearless, non-dual state of absolute safety.

name: शांभवी
wordByWord: शाम्भवानां — of the followers/children of Shambhu
शांभवी — She who belongs to Shambhu
searchText: 122 शांभवी • शम्भोः इयं (पत्नी)। • शाम्भवानां इयं (माता)। शम्भोः — of shambhu; shiva पत्नी — wife शाम्भवानां — of the followers/children of shambhu शांभवी — she who belongs to shambhu the mudrā of silent awareness: beyond being the consort of śiva[cite: 1], śāmbhavī denotes the highest state of internal yoga—the शाम्भवी मुद्रा (śāmbhavī mudrā). in this state, the eyes are physically open, gazing outward, but the entire attention is fixed permanently inward on the non-dual self, completely unmoved by external modifications. she embodies this mudrā, representing the peak of quiet, stable baseline awareness.
esoteric: The Mudrā of Silent Awareness: Beyond being the consort of Śiva[cite: 1], Śāmbhavī denotes the highest state of internal yoga—the शाम्भवी मुद्रा (Śāmbhavī Mudrā). In this state, the eyes are physically open, gazing outward, but the entire attention is fixed permanently inward on the non-dual Self, completely unmoved by external modifications. She embodies this mudrā, representing the peak of quiet, stable baseline awareness.

name: शारदाराध्या
wordByWord: शारदाराध्या — She who is worshipped by Sharada or in the autumn season
searchText: 123 शारदाराध्या • शारदया आराध्या। • शारदे (ऋतौ) आराध्या। • शारदा च असौ आराध्या च। शारदया — by sharada आराध्या — worshipped शारदे — in autumn ऋतौ — season शारदाराध्या — she who is worshipped by sharada or in the autumn season the source of all learning: sāradā (sarasvatī) is the patron of arts, sciences, and classical learning[cite: 1]. by being worshiped by her, it shows that all worldly knowledge bows down to the supreme spiritual knowledge (parā-vidyā) that lalitā represents. the autumn season (śarad) represents clarity, where the muddy waters of the monsoons settle and clear up[cite: 1]. this maps onto the calm, clear intellect (śuddha-buddhi) where the divine presence is perfectly reflected and adored.
esoteric: The Source of All Learning: Sāradā (Sarasvatī) is the patron of arts, sciences, and classical learning[cite: 1]. By being worshiped by Her, it shows that all worldly knowledge bows down to the supreme spiritual knowledge (Parā-vidyā) that Lalitā represents. The autumn season (Śarad) represents clarity, where the muddy waters of the monsoons settle and clear up[cite: 1]. This maps onto the calm, clear intellect (śuddha-buddhi) where the divine presence is perfectly reflected and adored.

name: शर्वाणी
wordByWord: शर्वाणी — She who is the consort of Sharva
summary: i. Consort of Lord Paramashiva,

ii. One of the eight forms of Lord Paraniashiva is earth. His consort is Sukesee. Their son is Angdraké (Mars). It can be seen: In Linga Purénd 'y Characharandm Bhooténém Dhaté Visvambhardtmaka:! Sarva Ityucyate Deva: Sarva Sastrantha Paragai:| Visvambharatmanstasya Sarvasya Parameshtina:| Sukesee Kathyate Patnee tanujongéraka: Smruta:\! vii. Sabhagava: Kasmin Pratishtita Iti Sve Mahimna Iti Hovaca (Chandokya Upanishad Vil-24-1). It is replied for the question "Where is that Brahmam stationed? as; it is conspicuous without any support other than self. There are two kinds of worshipping Sreedevee as external (BGhyam) and internal (Antaram). And again the external has two divisions, Vedic and Tantric. The internal method is also of two kinds with support (Sédhdra) and without support (Niréahara). The Sddhdra worship is to worship mental Images in the form of mantras as taught by a teacher. TheNirddhdraworship is by pure intellect - focusing the mind on Sreedevee's image obtained by knowledge. in reality, pure intellect means nothing else, but the supreme Sreedevee. Hence for the destruc: tion of somsdra, one should worship the Sreedevee, the very self, the witness, free from the glamour of the manifold universe. By one's own direct experience of Maheshwaree as the self, She should be worshipped with much reverence, this is the worship which leads to salvation. name: निर्विकारा
summary: i. Pifefaire
ii. One who is not subject to any change,

iii. The soul and nature are,the two originless truths accepted in 'SankhyaSiddantha, Out of these the soul is intellect, It Is different from body, organs and mind. It lives as witness to the changes in the world and but does not change itself. Nature is the root for the world. This is originless, eternal matter and ever changing. This has the three characters goodness (satva), passion (rajas) and ignorance (tamas). The world is created by the unison of the soul and the earth. Buddhi (intellect) is the next direct evolute from mahat (intelligence) followed byahankara (ego). This is followed by sense organs, organs for actions, mind, five basic elements and five primary elements. As per the Sankhyo Philosophy (enumeration or analysis of the Universe) there are 24 principles of creation, starting from nature. The soul is the 25"one. These philosophies are divided into four parts - that are: 1. Only causes. 2. Only effects. 3. Causes for some and effects for some other. 4, Neither as cause nor as an effect. 'Out of these the basic entity (Moola Prakruti), also the foremost one, is always a cause. These are described in Sankhya Tatva Karikai and its commentarySankhya Tatva Koumudee. in Sankhya Tatva Karikai, it isvery clear that the basic entity is a cause. Moolaprakrutiravikrutir Mahadadya: Prakrutivikruto; Shodashakastu Vikoro Naprakrutirnavikruti: Purusha'll Sree Adi Sapkaro, in his commentaries for the Brahma Siitra also men tions about this. The seven - intelligence, ego and 5 basic elements are both cause and: Soptal effects. Five sense organs, 5 organs of action, mind and 5 primary elements are effects. The soul Is with neither cause nor effect,
iv. She has been described in this name as not subject to 23 types of changes

v. As per SreeVidyasastrd, SreedeveeHerselt is the universal absolute. Hence the Sonkhyaphilosophers do not accept that nature (either primary or unmanifest) is matter. However, they accept these 25 principles, with a littleDeviation in the meaning of soul. In Sree Vidya 11 more principles are added totaling to 36 as in Saiva philoscophy of Kashmer

name: नित्यशुद्धा

searchText: 148 नित्यशुद्धा • नित्यं शुद्धा। शुद्धा — pure नित्यशुद्धा — she who is eternally pure i. one who is eternally pure, ii. she is beyond any impurity in all the three times (tenses); hence this name, iii. l) veda says; asparshascha mahén shuci: - she does not touch anything, elder to all and always pure, iv. another verse of veda says; atyanta malino deho dehee chatyanta nirmala: - the body is untidy, but the person inside is clean, the inherent purity: worldly purity is conditional and can be lost through defilement. she is nityaśuddhā because her purity is an absolute, non-negotiable aspect of her being. no matter how dense the material world becomes, her core light of awareness remains unsullied, working as an active clearing agent for anyone who contemplates her form.

iii. She is the head of everything. Hence there is no superior to Her
iv. l) Meemamsa and Sankhy@ are two philosophies. Sankhyd has further sub philosophies as with-Eshwar and without. The former is nondual (Advaitam) and the later is atheism. She belongs to both

name: नीरागा

ii. From this name onwards till 187" name Niratyaya, it proceeds to eject the theory that, the differences in the four types of mind viz. mind, intellect, volition and ego belong to the self (soul). Further to teach that renunciation is the means of overcoming the six impediments; desire, wrath, covetousness, bewilderment, pride and envy

iii. One, who (by mistake) believes that these enemies are the characters of the soul, travels in the way shown by them and get destroyed. But, they never go along with the soul. One, who understands that these are the actions of the mind, controls them or to tum aside or to be unrelated with them, gets pride. (v) Because She has obtained all her desires, She is Neerdga

name: रागमथनी
searchText: 157 रागमथनी • रागं मथ्नातीति। रागं — attachment; passion रागमथनी — she who destroys attachment the destroyer of bondage: churning (mathana) requires applied force to break down a substance. she acts as rāgamathanī because her grace forcefully breaks down the sticky, binding attachments within the seeker's mind, freeing them from emotional dependence on worldly objects.
esoteric: The Destroyer of Bondage: Churning (mathana) requires applied force to break down a substance. She acts as Rāgamathanī because Her grace forcefully breaks down the sticky, binding attachments within the seeker's mind, freeing them from emotional dependence on worldly objects.

name: निर्मदा
wordByWord: निर्मदा — She who is free from pride
summary: i. Frfar
ii. One who is without pride
iii. l) Pride is - to think that one has something which is not with others 'or has succeeded in doing something which others could not and propagate the same
iv. ll) When Sreedevee is not seen by others the question of pride does not arise

v. 508" name is Atigarvita. When contemplating the Brahmam, Veda mentions contradicting characters. Smaller than the smallest, bigger than the biggest - Anoraneeyan Mahato Maheeyn. Hence there is no controversy between this name - without pride and 508" name with high pride. 159. Madandshinee Wemfart
vi. One who destroys all pride

vii. By splitting as; Madanam (the flower of thorn apple) + Ashnati (one who eats it) - it is interpreted as - One who is interested in eating. the flower of thorn apple.) The flower of thorn apple is very much liked by LordParameshwara, It is said that he wears it in his head. Also, it is said that jt is used to make gold (alchemy). dl

viii. tt is apt that the flower of thorn apple is liked by Sreedevee also since it is liked by Parameshwara. (It has to be considered that there is no difference between them and they are one and the same). () It also implies that - like an ordinary metal is being converted to gold, an ordinary devotee is taken to a great position

ix. Maheshastvam Dhatte Shirasi Rasardjasya Jayinee/ Vishitdhis Tvat Sangat Kanakmayam Ett Tribhuvanam. (ii)it is also interpreted as - Madana means acute anxiety; She removes aftefrar Hea Sree Lots Sohasrondmam it (eats it out)

(it has to be considered that there is no difference between them and they are one and the same). (ii)it is also interpreted as - madana means acute anxiety; she removes aftefrar hea sree lots sohasrondmam it (eats it out) the humility of the whole: pride (mada) arises when an individual compares themselves to others and feels superior. because she is the non-dual whole, there is no separate entity alongside her to compare herself against. she stands free from pride, resting in absolute, natural equilibrium.
esoteric: The Humility of the Whole: Pride (mada) arises when an individual compares themselves to others and feels superior. Because She is the non-dual whole, there is no separate entity alongside Her to compare Herself against. She stands free from pride, resting in absolute, natural equilibrium.

name: मदनाशिनी
searchText: 159 मदनाशिनी • मदं नाशयतीति। मदं — pride मदनाशिनी — she who destroys pride the leveler of the ego: arrogance blocks spiritual evolution by freezing the intellect. she is madanāśinī because her presence acts as a powerful leveler that systematically dissolves the seeker's ego-driven pride, restoring the mental fluidity and humility needed to receive higher wisdom.
esoteric: The Leveler of the Ego: Arrogance blocks spiritual evolution by freezing the intellect. She is Madanāśinī because Her presence acts as a powerful leveler that systematically dissolves the seeker's ego-driven pride, restoring the mental fluidity and humility needed to receive higher wisdom.

name: निश्चिन्ता
wordByWord: निश्चिन्ता — meaning follows from the samasa: निर्गता चिन्ता यस्याः सा।
summary: i. One who is free from all anxieties.
ii. In general - Chintd means recollection of earlier thoughts; in particular recollection ofpainful topics is Chinté or anxiety.

iii. Chinté Chitésam Gnoyé Chinté Vai BindunadhikG Chit Dahoti Nirjeevam Chinté Dahati Jeevitam - Anxiety should be known as (resembling) afuneral pile, the only difference is that additional ‘n’ ‘sound (additional dot when written in Samskrit) inchintd, but as the pile (chitd) burns the corpse, so anxiety burhs theliving man. {iv) According to the dictionary called Visva, chinta means get cheated, Shedoes not have any delusion and hence Nishcinta,
searchText: 160 निश्चिन्ता • निर्गता चिन्ता यस्याः सा। निर्गता — gone away; absent चिन्ता — anxiety; worry यस्याः — of whom; whose सा — she निश्चिन्ता — meaning follows from the samasa: निर्गता चिन्ता यस्याः सा। i. one who is free from all anxieties. ii. in general - chintd means recollection of earlier thoughts; in particular recollection ofpainful topics is chinté or anxiety. iii. chinté chitésam gnoyé chinté vai bindunadhikg chit dahoti nirjeevam chinté dahati jeevitam - anxiety should be known as (resembling) afuneral pile, the only difference is that additional ‘n’ ‘sound (additional dot when written in samskrit) inchintd, but as the pile (chitd) burns the corpse, so anxiety burhs theliving man. {iv) according to the dictionary called visva, chinta means get cheated, shedoes not have any delusion and hence nishcinta, the serene confidence of truth: worry (cintā) is a product of uncertainty and fear of the future. because she holds complete command over past, present, and future, there is no unpredictable event that could cause her anxiety. she remains perfectly relaxed in the quiet certainty of her absolute rule.
esoteric: The Serene Confidence of Truth: Worry (cintā) is a product of uncertainty and fear of the future. Because She holds complete command over past, present, and future, there is no unpredictable event that could cause Her anxiety. She remains perfectly relaxed in the quiet certainty of Her absolute rule.

name: निरहङ्कारा
wordByWord: निरहङ्कारा — meaning follows from the samasa: निर्गतः अहङ्कारः यस्याः सा।
summary: i. One who is without any ego.
ii. Ego is of three kinds; Vaikhareeka, Taijasa and Bhutddi, according to sattva, rajas, and tamas characters, respectively. 397” name Moolaprakruti can be referred.

ii. Earlier in 139” name - Nirgugd, it was mentioned that She is beyond the three characters viz. sattva, rajas, and tamas. Accordingly She does not have the ego relating to those characters also. Hence Nirahankéra.
searchText: 161 निरहङ्कारा • निर्गतः अहङ्कारः यस्याः सा। निर्गतः — gone away; absent अहङ्कारः — ego; self-sense यस्याः — of whom; whose सा — she निरहङ्कारा — meaning follows from the samasa: निर्गतः अहङ्कारः यस्याः सा। i. one who is without any ego. ii. ego is of three kinds; vaikhareeka, taijasa and bhutddi, according to sattva, rajas, and tamas characters, respectively. 397” name moolaprakruti can be referred. ii. earlier in 139” name - nirgugd, it was mentioned that she is beyond the three characters viz. sattva, rajas, and tamas. accordingly she does not have the ego relating to those characters also. hence nirahankéra. the universal i-consciousness: ahaṅkāra is the psychological faculty that draws a boundary line saying "i am this limited body, and not that." she is nirahaṅkārā because she has no limited boundary line. her i-identity is पूर्णाहन्ता(pūrṇāhaṃtā — the infinite, universal i-consciousness) that encompasses all existence without exclusion.
esoteric: The Universal I-Consciousness: Ahaṅkāra is the psychological faculty that draws a boundary line saying "I am this limited body, and not that." She is Nirahaṅkārā because She has no limited boundary line. Her I-identity is पूर्णाहन्ता(Pūrṇāhaṃtā — the infinite, universal I-consciousness) that encompasses all existence without exclusion.

name: निर्मोहा
wordByWord: निर्मोहा — meaning follows from the samasa: निर्गताः मोहाः यस्याः।
summary: i. One who is free from bewilderment (fascination or perplexity).
ii. Bewilderment is an indication of confusion of thought or distraction of the mind. It infers the wavering of mind or mistaking one for the other. She is beyond all these.
searchText: 162 निर्मोहा • निर्गताः मोहाः यस्याः। निर्गताः — gone away; absent यस्याः — of whom; whose निर्मोहा — meaning follows from the samasa: निर्गताः मोहाः यस्याः। i. one who is free from bewilderment (fascination or perplexity). ii. bewilderment is an indication of confusion of thought or distraction of the mind. it infers the wavering of mind or mistaking one for the other. she is beyond all these. the flawless clarity: delusion (moha) occurs when the mind mistakes the transient temporary world for the permanent reality. being the foundational truth itself, she is permanently free from confusion, radiating flawless clarity that cuts through the illusions of the world.
esoteric: The Flawless Clarity: Delusion (moha) occurs when the mind mistakes the transient temporary world for the permanent reality. Being the foundational truth itself, She is permanently free from confusion, radiating flawless clarity that cuts through the illusions of the world.

name: मोहनाशिनी
wordByWord: मोहनाशिनी — meaning follows from the samasa: मोहं नाशयतीति।
summary: i. One who dispels all illusions.
ii. By imparting the idea of unity to her devotees She destroys bewilderment.
ii. The Veda - Ishavasya Upanishad 7” verse; Tatra Ko Moha: Ekatvamanu Pashyata:-"to him, who perceives unity,what bewilderment and what sorrow can there be?”
searchText: 163 मोहनाशिनी • मोहं नाशयतीति। नाशयतीति — destroys मोहनाशिनी — meaning follows from the samasa: मोहं नाशयतीति। i. one who dispels all illusions. ii. by imparting the idea of unity to her devotees she destroys bewilderment. ii. the veda - ishavasya upanishad 7” verse; tatra ko moha: ekatvamanu pashyata:-"to him, who perceives unity,what bewilderment and what sorrow can there be?” the dispeller of illusion: she acts as mohanāśinī because her grace acts like a bright light brought into a dark room. she instantly shatters the deep-seated delusions that keep the seeker bound to material attachments, illuminating the true nature of the eternal self.
esoteric: The Dispeller of Illusion: She acts as Mohanāśinī because Her grace acts like a bright light brought into a dark room. She instantly shatters the deep-seated delusions that keep the seeker bound to material attachments, illuminating the true nature of the eternal Self.

name: निर्ममा
wordByWord: निर्ममा — meaning follows from the samasa: निर्गतः ममत्वं यस्याः सा।
summary: i. One who has no self conceitt. li) Self interest necessarily implies separating things as "this is mine”. Since She is everything there is no room for such a thought and hence no question of conceitt.
searchText: 164 निर्ममा • निर्गतः ममत्वं यस्याः सा। निर्गतः — gone away; absent ममत्वं — sense of mine-ness यस्याः — of whom; whose सा — she निर्ममा — meaning follows from the samasa: निर्गतः ममत्वं यस्याः सा। i. one who has no self conceitt. li) self interest necessarily implies separating things as "this is mine”. since she is everything there is no room for such a thought and hence no question of conceitt. the freedom from ownership: possessiveness (mamatā) is the secondary branch of the ego that seeks to grab and control objects. because the entire universe belongs to her as her own expansion, she has no need to possess things in a limited way. she stands free from ownership, giving her grace unconditionally to all.
esoteric: The Freedom from Ownership: Possessiveness (mamatā) is the secondary branch of the ego that seeks to grab and control objects. Because the entire universe belongs to Her as Her own expansion, She has no need to possess things in a limited way. She stands free from ownership, giving Her grace unconditionally to all.

name: ममताहन्त्री
wordByWord: ममताहन्त्री — meaning follows from the samasa: ममतायाः हन्त्री।
summary: i. One who destroys the above said self conceit

ii. We could relate this to the request of Vaisya in the 13" chapter of Durga Saptashatee. It has to be noted that Vaisyd has a special adjective here as Prégya: - Sopi Vasyos Tato Gnanam Vovre Nivinaménasa: Mametyahamiti Pragya: Sanka Vishnuti Karakam.
searchText: 165 ममताहन्त्री • ममतायाः हन्त्री। हन्त्री — destroyer ममताहन्त्री — meaning follows from the samasa: ममतायाः हन्त्री। i. one who destroys the above said self conceit ii. we could relate this to the request of vaisya in the 13" chapter of durga saptashatee. it has to be noted that vaisyd has a special adjective here as prégya: - sopi vasyos tato gnanam vovre nivinaménasa: mametyahamiti pragya: sanka vishnuti karakam. severing the ties of ownership: the sense of "mine" creates intense mental bondage and fear of loss. she is mamatāhantrī because she systematically cuts the tight cords of possessiveness in the seeker's heart, teaching them to see all things as passing expressions of the divine rather than personal property.
esoteric: Severing the Ties of Ownership: The sense of "mine" creates intense mental bondage and fear of loss. She is Mamatāhantrī because She systematically cuts the tight cords of possessiveness in the seeker's heart, teaching them to see all things as passing expressions of the divine rather than personal property.

name: निष्पापा
wordByWord: निष्पापा — meaning follows from the samasa: निर्गताः पापाः यस्याः सा।
summary: i. One who is without any sin.

ii. Any sin or virtue is created only by deeds, primarily due to desires and actions. Earlier it was explained in the 156th name Neeraga that She is without any desires. No actions for Her. Resultantly no sin or virtue also.
iii. In Srimad Bhagavad Gita (IV-14) we see: Na Mam Karmani Limpanti Na Me Karmaphale Spruha.
searchText: 166 निष्पापा • निर्गताः पापाः यस्याः सा। निर्गताः — gone away; absent पापाः — sins यस्याः — of whom; whose सा — she निष्पापा — meaning follows from the samasa: निर्गताः पापाः यस्याः सा। i. one who is without any sin. ii. any sin or virtue is created only by deeds, primarily due to desires and actions. earlier it was explained in the 156th name neeraga that she is without any desires. no actions for her. resultantly no sin or virtue also. iii. in srimad bhagavad gita (iv-14) we see: na mam karmani limpanti na me karmaphale spruha. the field of absolute good: pāpa (sin) is an action born from ignorance and selfishness that generates negative mental impressions. because her nature is pure, non-dual light and unconditional love, there is no room for selfish distortion within her. she is pure goodness personified.
esoteric: The Field of Absolute Good: Pāpa (sin) is an action born from ignorance and selfishness that generates negative mental impressions. Because Her nature is pure, non-dual light and unconditional love, there is no room for selfish distortion within Her. She is pure goodness personified.

name: पापनाशिनी
wordByWord: पापनाशिनी — meaning follows from the samasa: पापान् नाशयतीति।
summary: i. One who destroys the sins of devotees.
ii. By the repetition of Her mantra, etc., She destroys the sins of devotees.
iii. In Veda (Chandogya Upanishad IV-24-3) it is mentioned that, like the point of a reed in the fire, all the sins are burnt up.

iv. The Vashista Smruti, Padma Purana, Devi Bhagavatam, Brahmanda Purana, the Phalasruti of this Sahasranama, and commentaries on Vishnu Sahasranama are cited to show the destruction of sins through mantra, worship, meditation, and remembrance.
searchText: 167 पापनाशिनी • पापान् नाशयतीति। नाशयतीति — destroys पापनाशिनी — meaning follows from the samasa: पापान् नाशयतीति। i. one who destroys the sins of devotees. ii. by the repetition of her mantra, etc., she destroys the sins of devotees. iii. in veda (chandogya upanishad iv-24-3) it is mentioned that, like the point of a reed in the fire, all the sins are burnt up. iv. the vashista smruti, padma purana, devi bhagavatam, brahmanda purana, the phalasruti of this sahasranama, and commentaries on vishnu sahasranama are cited to show the destruction of sins through mantra, worship, meditation, and remembrance. the fire of purification: past sins create deep karmic impressions (vāsanās) that weigh down the mind. she acts as pāpanāśinī because her grace acts like a roaring fire that instantly burns away the heaviest accumulations of past demerits, lightened the seeker's mental path.
esoteric: The Fire of Purification: Past sins create deep karmic impressions (vāsanās) that weigh down the mind. She acts as Pāpanāśinī because Her grace acts like a roaring fire that instantly burns away the heaviest accumulations of past demerits, lightened the seeker's mental path.

name: निष्क्रोधा

summary: i., One who is without anger.
ii. She has no hatred on anyone;She does not get angry also.
ii. in Srimad Bhagavad Gita (\X-23) we read; Na Me Dveshyosti Nopriya:\l

name: क्रोधशमनी
wordByWord: क्रोधशमनी — meaning follows from the samasa: क्रोधं शमयतीति।
summary: i. One who destroys the tendency to get angry.

i. Every human has six internal enemies - lust, anger, stinginess, libidinous recklessness and amusement. She suppresses the anger of the devotees and destroys the lust in them. ) Sage Apastambdexplains the cruelty of the anger as; Krodha Yukto Yedyojati Yajjuhoti Yadarsatit Sa Tasya Harate Sarvamamkumbho Yathodakamlt He, who sacrifices, offer obligations, or worships with anger, is deprived of all benefits therefrom like water in a vessel of unbaked clay.

iv. name: निर्लोभा

summary: () One who is free from any greed.
ii. Because She is exceedingly liberal.
searchText: 170 निर्लोभा • निर्गताः लोभाः यस्याः सा। निर्गताः — gone away; absent लोभाः — greed यस्याः — of whom; whose सा — she निर्लोभा — meaning follows from the samasa: निर्गताः लोभाः यस्याः सा। () one who is free from any greed. ii. because she is exceedingly liberal. the satisfied source: greed (lobha) is the constant urge to accumulate more, driven by a deep feeling of lack. being the inexhaustible source of all abundance, she can never experience lack. she remains perfectly resting in the satisfaction of her own fullness.
esoteric: The Satisfied Source: Greed (lobha) is the constant urge to accumulate more, driven by a deep feeling of lack. Being the inexhaustible source of all abundance, She can never experience lack. She remains perfectly resting in the satisfaction of Her own fullness.

name: लोभनाशिनी
wordByWord: लोभनाशिनी — meaning follows from the samasa: लोभं नाशयतीति।
summary: i. One who destroys greed.
ii. Since, greed destroys all other good qualities, it is important that it has to be destroyed first. Sreedevee destroys the greed in the minds of the devotees.
searchText: 171 लोभनाशिनी • लोभं नाशयतीति। नाशयतीति — destroys लोभनाशिनी — meaning follows from the samasa: लोभं नाशयतीति। i. one who destroys greed. ii. since, greed destroys all other good qualities, it is important that it has to be destroyed first. sreedevee destroys the greed in the minds of the devotees. breaking the grip of avarice: greed keeps the mind running outward after material possessions. she is lobhanāśinī because her grace breaks this outward grip, helping the seeker realize that true wealth lies within the peace of their own consciousness.
esoteric: Breaking the Grip of Avarice: Greed keeps the mind running outward after material possessions. She is Lobhanāśinī because Her grace breaks this outward grip, helping the seeker realize that true wealth lies within the peace of their own consciousness.

name: निःसंशया

summary: i. One who is without doubt.
ii. Doubt is a state of mind which wavers between two alternatives. Since She is knowledge itself, such doubt is absent in Her.

esoteric: The Absolute Certainty: Doubt (saṃśaya) arises when the intellect cannot choose between conflicting ideas. Because She is the supreme, all-knowing clarity, She rests in absolute certainty, guiding seekers with an unyielding compass of truth.

name: निर्विकल्पा
wordByWord: निर्विकल्पा — meaning follows from the samasa: निर्गताः विकल्पाः यस्याः सा।
summary: i. One who is without the notion of covering by mere words without the corresponding objects

i. The Yogasutré [1.9] says; Shabda Motrdnupatee Vastu Vastu Shoonyo Vikalpa:.Vikolpa(fancy) is a notionconveyed by mere words, but of which there is no object corresponding to reality. For instance: horns of rabbit, horns of horse, etc. That, which is not real, ‘which is created in the mind. This name advises that - Sreedevee is in the form of unconditioned eternal knowledge.
iii. Or, Nirvikalpd, in the last mental modification, (last stage ‘ofmeditation), there are no specific distinctions (vikalpa).

iv. Or vi - opposed; kalpa - theory. There is no theory opposed to Sreedeveebecause everything is inseparable from her, i.e. Sreedevee is devoid of all separateness. The Brahma Sutra(\\I-2-14) says, Aroopavat Eva Hi Tatpradh Gnatvat - For Brahman is merely devoid ‘of form, on account of this beingthe main purport of Scripture”.
v. The very meaning of Veda is to explain that the Brahman has no characters lke form, etc.
vi. Everything is integrated with Sreedevee and hence there is no object separate from Her.

searchText: 177 निराबाधा • निर्गतः बाधः यस्याः सा। निर्गतः — gone away; absent बाधः — obstruction; affliction यस्याः — of whom; whose सा — she निराबाधा — meaning follows from the samasa: निर्गतः बाधः यस्याः सा। i. one who has undisturbed mind, ii. the mind is disturbed by (mis) seeing the shell as silver. this disturbance is removed once the illusion vanishes. there is no object ‘other than sreedevee. hence she does not have any such disturbance in mind, the absolute reality: in philosophy, a statement is bādhita if it is contradicted and proven false by a higher truth. she is nirābādhā because she is the ultimate, supreme truth that can never be negated, contradicted, or eclipsed by any higher reality.
