summary: i. One who puts an end to evil customs. il) Evil customs are contrary to the scriptures. She quickly destroys all the sins of mencémmitted by neglecting the daily rites and observing the prohibitedones.
searchText: 194 दुराचारशमनी • दुराचारं शमयतीति। शमयतीति — pacifies; ends दुराचारशमनी — meaning follows from the samasa: दुराचारं शमयतीति। i. one who puts an end to evil customs. il) evil customs are contrary to the scriptures. she quickly destroys all the sins of mencémmitted by neglecting the daily rites and observing the prohibitedones. the restorer of righteousness: unrighteous conduct (durācāra) creates discord and pulls energy downward. she acts as durācāraśamanī because her grace acts like a corrective current, pacifying wild behaviors and guiding human attention back toward harmony and right living (sadācāra).
esoteric: The Restorer of Righteousness: Unrighteous conduct (durācāra) creates discord and pulls energy downward. She acts as Durācāraśamanī because Her grace acts like a corrective current, pacifying wild behaviors and guiding human attention back toward harmony and right living (Sadācāra).

name: दोषवर्जिता
wordByWord: दोषवर्जिता — meaning follows from the samasa: दोषैः वर्जिता।
summary: i. One who is free from all faults.
ii. A fault is something which spoils the mind, talking and acting in a way, which reflects the feelings of longings and aversion. Since She is free from longings.and aversion, She is faultless.
searchText: 195 दोषवर्जिता • दोषैः वर्जिता। दोषैः — by faults वर्जिता — free from; devoid of दोषवर्जिता — meaning follows from the samasa: दोषैः वर्जिता। i. one who is free from all faults. ii. a fault is something which spoils the mind, talking and acting in a way, which reflects the feelings of longings and aversion. since she is free from longings.and aversion, she is faultless. the flawless substrate: in philosophy, everything in nature contains variations of flaws (doṣa). being the uncreated primordial cause, she sits entirely above these structural limitations, remaining a perfect, unblemished field of absolute purity.
esoteric: The Flawless Substrate: In philosophy, everything in nature contains variations of flaws (doṣa). Being the uncreated primordial cause, She sits entirely above these structural limitations, remaining a perfect, unblemished field of absolute purity.

name: सर्वज्ञा
wordByWord: सर्वज्ञा — meaning follows from the samasa: सर्वं जानातीति।
summary: {) One who is omniscient.
ii. Mundaka Upanishad (|-1-9) says; Ya: Sarvajna; Sarva Vid - one who perceives all and knows all.
iii. In Devee Puranam it is said as; Sarvajna Sarva Vetrutvét - As she knows everything she is called omniscient.
searchText: 196 सर्वज्ञा • सर्वं जानातीति। जानातीति — knows सर्वज्ञा — meaning follows from the samasa: सर्वं जानातीति। {) one who is omniscient. ii. mundaka upanishad (|-1-9) says; ya: sarvajna; sarva vid - one who perceives all and knows all. iii. in devee puranam it is said as; sarvajna sarva vetrutvét - as she knows everything she is called omniscient. the eye of cosmic intellect: her omniscience (sarvajñā) is not a database of external facts; it is the natural consequence of her being the very substrate of all existence. because everything is an expansion of her own self, she knows all things instantly from within as their inner witness.
esoteric: The Eye of Cosmic Intellect: Her omniscience (Sarvajñā) is not a database of external facts; it is the natural consequence of Her being the very substrate of all existence. Because everything is an expansion of Her own self, She knows all things instantly from within as their inner witness.

name: सान्द्रकरुणा
wordByWord: सान्द्रकरुणा — meaning follows from the samasa: सान्द्रा करुणा यस्याः सा।
summary: i. One who shows intense compassion.
searchText: 197 सान्द्रकरुणा • सान्द्रा करुणा यस्याः सा। सान्द्रा — dense; intense करुणा — compassion यस्याः — of whom; whose सा — she सान्द्रकरुणा — meaning follows from the samasa: सान्द्रा करुणा यस्याः सा। i. one who shows intense compassion. the solid wave of grace: sāndra means solid or intensely packed, like cream. her compassion is not a weak, intermittent emotion; it is a dense, solid wave of protective grace that fills every corner of space, instantly holding up any devotee who calls upon her.
esoteric: The Solid Wave of Grace: Sāndra means solid or intensely packed, like cream. Her compassion is not a weak, intermittent emotion; it is a dense, solid wave of protective grace that fills every corner of space, instantly holding up any devotee who calls upon Her.

name: समानाधिकवर्जिता
wordByWord: समानाधिकवर्जिता — meaning follows from the samasa: समानाधिकाभ्यां वर्जिता।
summary: i. One who neither has equal nor a superior.
i. We read in Svetasvatara Upanishad(VI-8); Na Tat Samas Capyadhikas Ca Drushyate - No one is seen equal or superior tohim.
searchText: 198 समानाधिकवर्जिता • समानाधिकाभ्यां वर्जिता। समानाधिकाभ्यां — from equal and superior वर्जिता — free from; devoid of समानाधिकवर्जिता — meaning follows from the samasa: समानाधिकाभ्यां वर्जिता। i. one who neither has equal nor a superior. i. we read in svetasvatara upanishad(vi-8); na tat samas capyadhikas ca drushyate - no one is seen equal or superior tohim. the absolute peak: if an entity has an equal, its power is limited; if it has a superior, it is subordinate. she stands as samānādhikavarjitā because she sits at the absolute peak of the cosmic pyramid—having no rival to challenge her and no master to command her.
esoteric: The Absolute Peak: If an entity has an equal, its power is limited; if it has a superior, it is subordinate. She stands as Samānādhikavarjitā because She sits at the absolute peak of the cosmic pyramid—having no rival to challenge Her and no master to command Her.

name: सर्वशक्तिमयी
wordByWord: सर्वशक्तिमयी — meaning follows from the samasa: सर्वाः शक्तयः अस्याः सन्तीति सर्वशक्तिमयी।
summary: i. ) One who has all the divine powers (omnipotent).

ii. By the saying; Ulagam Brahmanayam, it can be perceived as there is no difference between the world and the Brahman. The article Maya indicates in-differentiation. Similarly this name indicates Er Sreedevee is not different from the shaktis called Paloi and Pokalai, In Bohuvrushopanishad also it is mentioned that Sreedevee is called by various names as in the below verse; Saisha Shodaseesreevidya Poncathashaksharee Sree Mahétripurasundaree Baldmbiketi Bagaleti Va ‘Matangeeti Svayamvarokalydneeti Bhuvaneshwareet Camundati Condeti Varéheeti Tiraskarineeti Rajamatongeeti Vasukashyamaleti Va Laghu Syamaleti V6 Ashvéroogheti Va Pratyankira Dhoomavatee Savitree Gayatree Sarasvatee Brohmananda Kaleti {iv) OF, She is in the form of energy Samooha. The energies of all the Devas are felicities of Sreedevee only and only with Her blessings Devas have acquired the energies.
v. Sarva may mean infinite,
searchText: 199 सर्वशक्तिमयी • सर्वाः शक्तयः अस्याः सन्तीति सर्वशक्तिमयी। सर्वाः — all शक्तयः — powers सन्तीति — exist सर्वशक्तिमयी — meaning follows from the samasa: सर्वाः शक्तयः अस्याः सन्तीति सर्वशक्तिमयी। i. ) one who has all the divine powers (omnipotent). ii. by the saying; ulagam brahmanayam, it can be perceived as there is no difference between the world and the brahman. the article maya indicates in-differentiation. similarly this name indicates er sreedevee is not different from the shaktis called paloi and pokalai, in bohuvrushopanishad also it is mentioned that sreedevee is called by various names as in the below verse; saisha shodaseesreevidya poncathashaksharee sree mahétripurasundaree baldmbiketi bagaleti va ‘matangeeti svayamvarokalydneeti bhuvaneshwareet camundati condeti varéheeti tiraskarineeti rajamatongeeti vasukashyamaleti va laghu syamaleti v6 ashvéroogheti va pratyankira dhoomavatee savitree gayatree sarasvatee brohmananda kaleti {iv) of, she is in the form of energy samooha. the energies of all the devas are felicities of sreedevee only and only with her blessings devas have acquired the energies. v. sarva may mean infinite, the powerhouse of the universe: every type of force in the universe—whether physical gravity, mental willpower, or spiritual light—is merely a small expression of her grand matrix. she is the ultimate powerhouse, holding all cosmic energies within her unified being.
esoteric: The Powerhouse of the Universe: Every type of force in the universe—whether physical gravity, mental willpower, or spiritual light—is merely a small expression of Her grand matrix. She is the ultimate powerhouse, holding all cosmic energies within Her unified being.

name: सर्वमङ्गला
wordByWord: सर्वमङ्गला — meaning follows from the samasa: सर्वाणि मङ्गलानि यस्याः सा।
summary: i. One who is the source of all that is auspicious. She is the root of all auspicious things.
ii. Devi Purana states that She grants all auspicious and desired things, removes the distress of devotees, and is therefore Sarvamangala.
iii. Or, Sarvamangala is a Tithi Nitya Devi; hence this name may indicate the Pranava sound in the mantra of Sarvamangala.

iv. The 224th name of Lalita Trishati, Sarvamangala, states that Sreedevee is the Chit energy not different from Brahman and gives all good fortune to those who satisfy Her through meditation, praise, songs, and worship.
v. This may be compared with Mangalanam Mangalam in Vishnu Sahasranama.
searchText: 200 सर्वमङ्गला • सर्वाणि मङ्गलानि यस्याः सा। मङ्गलानि — auspicious things यस्याः — of whom; whose सा — she सर्वमङ्गला — meaning follows from the samasa: सर्वाणि मङ्गलानि यस्याः सा। i. one who is the source of all that is auspicious. she is the root of all auspicious things. ii. devi purana states that she grants all auspicious and desired things, removes the distress of devotees, and is therefore sarvamangala. iii. or, sarvamangala is a tithi nitya devi; hence this name may indicate the pranava sound in the mantra of sarvamangala. iv. the 224th name of lalita trishati, sarvamangala, states that sreedevee is the chit energy not different from brahman and gives all good fortune to those who satisfy her through meditation, praise, songs, and worship. v. this may be compared with mangalanam mangalam in vishnu sahasranama. the source of good fortune: she is the definition of good fortune. invoking her energy converts any negative or discordant space into a field of complete harmony, ensuring that all actions undertaken in her name resolve into auspiciousness.
esoteric: The Source of Good Fortune: She is the definition of good fortune. Invoking Her energy converts any negative or discordant space into a field of complete harmony, ensuring that all actions undertaken in Her name resolve into auspiciousness.

name: सद्गतिप्रदा
wordByWord: सद्गतिप्रदा — meaning follows from the samasa: सतां गतिं प्रकर्षेण ददातीति।
summary: i. One who leads along the path of salvation
ii. The word sadgatimeans all stages from heaven to moksha as per cour wish, She who gives various stages of liberation as per eligibi
iii. Gati - goal. Heaven and moksa are attainable goals by two ways. Sreedevee guides to reach the goals easily
iv. Gati- wisdom; Sreedevee gives good knowledge,
v. Gati-way to reach; Sreedevee enlightens good path.
vi. Sat- eternal absolute. Sreedevee provides the path to reach it (path to knowledge).

vii. Satgati means the path followed by good people. Sreedevee enables us to reach the good place. (vill) Sat - She is the Sat. Gati - the goal to reach is also Herself. 1.e. She is the path and the goal.
(ix) Sree Adi Sankara, in his commentary for Lalita Trishatee 229" name Sadgatidéyinee, says - She destroys the ignorance and gives self illuminating bliss.

(x) Sree Adi Sankara, in his commentary for Vishnu Sahasranamam, She who can be reached by those who understands that the Brahman is the truth. b. She has elegant, best Gat/- knowledge. If we take the previous meaning it can be taken as She gives herself (Brahman itself).
searchText: 201 सद्गतिप्रदा • सतां गतिं प्रकर्षेण ददातीति। सतां — of good people गतिं — path; goal प्रकर्षेण — excellently; intensely ददातीति — gives सद्गतिप्रदा — meaning follows from the samasa: सतां गतिं प्रकर्षेण ददातीति। i. one who leads along the path of salvation ii. the word sadgatimeans all stages from heaven to moksha as per cour wish, she who gives various stages of liberation as per eligibi iii. gati - goal. heaven and moksa are attainable goals by two ways. sreedevee guides to reach the goals easily iv. gati- wisdom; sreedevee gives good knowledge, v. gati-way to reach; sreedevee enlightens good path. vi. sat- eternal absolute. sreedevee provides the path to reach it (path to knowledge). vii. satgati means the path followed by good people. sreedevee enables us to reach the good place. (vill) sat - she is the sat. gati - the goal to reach is also herself. 1.e. she is the path and the goal. (ix) sree adi sankara, in his commentary for lalita trishatee 229" name sadgatidéyinee, says - she destroys the ignorance and gives self illuminating bliss. (x) sree adi sankara, in his commentary for vishnu sahasranamam, she who can be reached by those who understands that the brahman is the truth. b. she has elegant, best gat/- knowledge. if we take the previous meaning it can be taken as she gives herself (brahman itself). the guide to liberation: sadgati is the high trajectory that leads a soul away from rebirth and straight into liberation. she acts as sadgatipradā because she stands as the protective guide who ensures that the seeker's energy travels smoothly up the central channel to its true destination.
esoteric: The Guide to Liberation: Sadgati is the high trajectory that leads a soul away from rebirth and straight into liberation. She acts as Sadgatipradā because She stands as the protective guide who ensures that the seeker's energy travels smoothly up the central channel to its true destination.

name: सर्वेश्वरी
wordByWord: सर्वेश्वरी — meaning follows from the samasa: सर्वेषाम् ईश्वरी।
summary: i. One who is the mistress of the whole universe.
ii. Because Sheleads all into the right path, She is Sorveshvaree. (ili) There is no place where the control of Sreedevee is obstructed. Hence She is the Eshvari of all
iv. One among the eight Vagdevees.
searchText: 202 सर्वेश्वरी • सर्वेषाम् ईश्वरी। ईश्वरी — ruler; mistress सर्वेश्वरी — meaning follows from the samasa: सर्वेषाम् ईश्वरी। i. one who is the mistress of the whole universe. ii. because sheleads all into the right path, she is sorveshvaree. (ili) there is no place where the control of sreedevee is obstructed. hence she is the eshvari of all iv. one among the eight vagdevees. the royal command: this names her absolute dominion. all planetary movements, cosmic laws, and living beings run under her central command post, establishing her as the sovereign ruler of the entire manifest universe.
esoteric: The Royal Command: This names Her absolute dominion. All planetary movements, cosmic laws, and living beings run under Her central command post, establishing Her as the sovereign ruler of the entire manifest universe.

name: सर्वमयी
wordByWord: सर्वमयी — meaning follows from the samasa: सर्वम् अस्याम् अस्तीति।
summary: i. ) One who is omnipresent and omnipotent.
i. She is in the form of all things.

iii. This universe has within it, thirty-six tatvas - the universal absolute WS ‘and the incarnations ‘a. This universe that is 24 self totvas (Athmo tatvas) or statictatvas (jada tatvas); i. Five basic elements, ii, Five self-characteristics (Tanmatrés) - subtle mediums of the sensations, |. _ Five sense organs, iv. Five organs for actions, v. Ego, mind, intellect, nature b. Seven vidya(or maya}tatvas - the soul, the system that controls it to make it a slave for Shiva, fate, smell, art, eduction and norance. Five Shivotatvas viz. 1, The knowledge of Suddha Vidya which clearly explains that Parabrahmam and the world are one and the same. ii, Eshwar disintegrated from Brahman and showing himself as this world. iii. Sadasiva who got the knowledge that this world and its root cause is self. iv, Shakti(which seems to be disintegrated from Brahman to ‘create this world). v. Shiva, who has that Shakt/ inside himself. Worshipping Sreedevee in the form of above 36 tatvds is called tatvatva. This is one of the six methods to reach Her.
searchText: 203 सर्वमयी • सर्वम् अस्याम् अस्तीति। सर्वम् — all अस्याम् — in her सर्वमयी — meaning follows from the samasa: सर्वम् अस्याम् अस्तीति। i. ) one who is omnipresent and omnipotent. i. she is in the form of all things. iii. this universe has within it, thirty-six tatvas - the universal absolute ws ‘and the incarnations ‘a. this universe that is 24 self totvas (athmo tatvas) or statictatvas (jada tatvas); i. five basic elements, ii, five self-characteristics (tanmatrés) - subtle mediums of the sensations, |. _ five sense organs, iv. five organs for actions, v. ego, mind, intellect, nature b. seven vidya(or maya}tatvas - the soul, the system that controls it to make it a slave for shiva, fate, smell, art, eduction and norance. five shivotatvas viz. 1, the knowledge of suddha vidya which clearly explains that parabrahmam and the world are one and the same. ii, eshwar disintegrated from brahman and showing himself as this world. iii. sadasiva who got the knowledge that this world and its root cause is self. iv, shakti(which seems to be disintegrated from brahman to ‘create this world). v. shiva, who has that shakt/ inside himself. worshipping sreedevee in the form of above 36 tatvds is called tatvatva. this is one of the six methods to reach her. the non-dual matrix: this names her absolute immanence. she is not separate from the world; the world is her own physical body. every atom, tree, star, and living being is a vibrant wave appearing upon the singular ocean of her consciousness.
esoteric: The Non-Dual Matrix: This names Her absolute immanence. She is not separate from the world; the world is Her own physical body. Every atom, tree, star, and living being is a vibrant wave appearing upon the singular ocean of Her consciousness.

name: सर्वमन्त्रस्वरूपिणी
wordByWord: सर्वमन्त्रस्वरूपिणी — meaning follows from the samasa: सर्वे मन्त्राः स्वरूपम् अस्याः इति।
summary: {i One who is in the form (very essence) of all mantras.
i. ) it is said that there are seven crores (70 millions) of mantras. She is in the form of all these mantras. Or Her form itself is these mantras.
ii. She is in the form of mantras in the method of worshipping called “Mantratva, one of the six Atvas.
searchText: 204 सर्वमन्त्रस्वरूपिणी • सर्वे मन्त्राः स्वरूपम् अस्याः इति। मन्त्राः — mantras स्वरूपम् — own form; essential nature सर्वमन्त्रस्वरूपिणी — meaning follows from the samasa: सर्वे मन्त्राः स्वरूपम् अस्याः इति। {i one who is in the form (very essence) of all mantras. i. ) it is said that there are seven crores (70 millions) of mantras. she is in the form of all these mantras. or her form itself is these mantras. ii. she is in the form of mantras in the method of worshipping called “mantratva, one of the six atvas. the queen of sound: every mantra in existence draws its sonic potency from her primary vibration. she is the underlying energy of all sacred sound-formulas, rendering them effective when chanted with clear intent.
esoteric: The Queen of Sound: Every mantra in existence draws its sonic potency from Her primary vibration. She is the underlying energy of all sacred sound-formulas, rendering them effective when chanted with clear intent.

name: सर्वयन्त्रात्मिका
wordByWord: सर्वयन्त्रात्मिका — meaning follows from the samasa: सर्वेषां यन्त्राणाम् आत्मिका।
summary: {)) One who is in the form ofall the yantras, Wi) Yantras are in the form lines which have in themselves, the concerned presiding deities.
searchText: 205 सर्वयन्त्रात्मिका • सर्वेषां यन्त्राणाम् आत्मिका। यन्त्राणाम् — of yantras आत्मिका — embodiment; inner self सर्वयन्त्रात्मिका — meaning follows from the samasa: सर्वेषां यन्त्राणाम् आत्मिका। {)) one who is in the form ofall the yantras, wi) yantras are in the form lines which have in themselves, the concerned presiding deities. the soul of sacred geometry: a yantra is a geometric layout that maps energy tracks. she is the inner life of these diagrams, transforming them from dry drawings into active portals of spiritual power when aligned with meditation.
esoteric: The Soul of Sacred Geometry: A yantra is a geometric layout that maps energy tracks. She is the inner life of these diagrams, transforming them from dry drawings into active portals of spiritual power when aligned with meditation.

name: सर्वतन्त्ररूपा
samasa: • सर्वाणि तन्त्राणि एव रूपं (शरीरं) यस्याः सा।
wordByWord: सर्वतन्त्ररूपा — meaning follows from the samasa: सर्वाणि तन्त्राणि एव रूपं (शरीरं) यस्याः सा।
summary: (One who is the spirit of all Tontras in her form.

ii. Since all the Tantras indicate Sreedevee Herself and prove Her, She is in the form of all Tantras. Tuayyeva Nipatantyete Srotasvinya Ivdrnavell ) The literature Kdmikd describes each of Tantras to the organs of Her body: 1 Kamika - the lotus feet 2 Yogoja - ankles 5 Ajit - the knees 6 Deepta - the thighs 7 Amsumat - back 8 ‘Suprabheda - navel 9 Vijay - stomach nu Svdyambhuva - the bosom 12 Anala - the three eyes B Virdgama - throat 4 Ruru - ears 15 Makuto - crown, 16 Vipula (Vial?) - ‘arms 7 Candrajndna - chest 18 Bimba - face 19 Prodgitd - tongue 20 Lolité - cheeks 2 Samténa - earrings 23 Kirana - jewels 24 Vétula = garments ‘Thus She has all the agamas or Tantras in her body. (There are some differences between what is said above and what is told in Shiva Agamés. Four of the Saiva Agamds are not mentioned here; Sarvokta, Parameshwara, Chintya and Sookshma. Again the Agama ‘alled Prasrut as mentioned above does find a place in Shiva Agamd as Sohasra).
searchText: 206 सर्वतन्त्ररूपा • सर्वाणि तन्त्राणि एव रूपं (शरीरं) यस्याः सा। तन्त्राणि — tantras रूपं — form यस्याः — of whom; whose सा — she सर्वतन्त्ररूपा — meaning follows from the samasa: सर्वाणि तन्त्राणि एव रूपं (शरीरं) यस्याः सा। (one who is the spirit of all tontras in her form. ii. since all the tantras indicate sreedevee herself and prove her, she is in the form of all tantras. tuayyeva nipatantyete srotasvinya ivdrnavell ) the literature kdmikd describes each of tantras to the organs of her body: 1 kamika - the lotus feet 2 yogoja - ankles 5 ajit - the knees 6 deepta - the thighs 7 amsumat - back 8 ‘suprabheda - navel 9 vijay - stomach nu svdyambhuva - the bosom 12 anala - the three eyes b virdgama - throat 4 ruru - ears 15 makuto - crown, 16 vipula (vial?) - ‘arms 7 candrajndna - chest 18 bimba - face 19 prodgitd - tongue 20 lolité - cheeks 2 samténa - earrings 23 kirana - jewels 24 vétula = garments ‘thus she has all the agamas or tantras in her body. (there are some differences between what is said above and what is told in shiva agamés. four of the saiva agamds are not mentioned here; sarvokta, parameshwara, chintya and sookshma. again the agama ‘alled prasrut as mentioned above does find a place in shiva agamd as sohasra). the method personified: tantra represents the practical system of spiritual application. she is the very soul of these methods, showing that every genuine system of spiritual practice is merely a pathway designed to explore her non-dual nature.
esoteric: The Method Personified: Tantra represents the practical system of spiritual application. She is the very soul of these methods, showing that every genuine system of spiritual practice is merely a pathway designed to explore Her non-dual nature.

name: मनोन्मनी
wordByWord: मनोन्मनी — meaning follows from the samasa: मनोन्मनाख्यशिवस्य शक्तिः।
summary: i. One who is in the form of Manonmani- transcendent consciousness,

ii. There are nine places from Agndchokra (in the centre between the eyebrows), till the Brahmarandhra;Bindu, Rothinee, Natha, a Nathonto, Shakti, Vyépinee,Samand, Unmanee or Manonmanee and ‘Mahdbindu. This Mehdbindu, also called as Indu or half moon, Is the minute energy. This is called Manonmanee. The characteristic of ‘Manonmanee is; Yashokti: Kéranatvena Tatoortvam Chonmaneesmrtal Notrokalotalamanam Natatvam Nachdevatall Sunirvinam Parom Suddam Rudravattram Tatvashyatel ‘Shivashaktiitikyata Nivikalpé Niranjandlt

ii. it is said that in the Hreemkard, above the Bindu there are eight divisions - one is subtler than the other. The beeja ‘Mis in the form of eight small elipses. (299 name Natharoopd can be referred). Sreedevee is in the form of that Unmanee.

iv. As per the Veda saying Vamadevaya Namo, the forms of Shiva are; Vamadevor, Jyeshtar, Sreshtar, Rudrar, Kalor, Kalovikaranar, Balar, Balavikeranar, Balapramatanar, Sarvabhootadamanar and ‘Manonmanar, Out of these, the consort of Manonmanar is ‘Manonmanee.

v. As per Yoga Sastra, when the mind is in a controlled state, free from attachment to objects, fixed on the mind it attains the state of ‘Unmagi. Mana - mind, Unmano - in a state of longing. Again Manonmanee is a kind of Mudrd, its characteristics aredescribed thus; By this process the eyes neither close nor open, bywhich breath is neither inhaled nor exhaled and the mind is @ blankneither speculating nor doubting, meditating the form of Sreedevee. ‘Netre Yayonmeshanimeshamukte Voyuryaya Varjita 7 sn eames por Sannidhatearit

iii. In the process of meditation of Sreedevee, meditator and the object we of meditation are entirely destroyed, then Unmanee state arises; consequently he enjoys the ambrosia of wisdom.

searchText: 211 मृडप्रिया • मृडस्य प्रिया। मृडस्य — of mrida; shiva प्रिया — beloved मृडप्रिया — meaning follows from the samasa: मृडस्य प्रिया। i. one who is dear to mrida, namely lord shiva. the comfort of the consort: lord mṛḍa is the compassionate face of shiva that forgives all mistakes. she is her beloved, showing that her dynamic grace is perfectly aligned with this wave of divine mercy.

name: महारूपा
wordByWord: महारूपा — meaning follows from the samasa: महद्रूपं यस्याः सा।
summary: i. One who is in great magnificent form. {li) Supreme Brahman hasfour forms; Purusha, the manifested (vyaktafive gross elements) and unmanifested (avyakta - Pradhdna) and lastly time. These four are the root cause for creation, protection, destruction and core values (moolatatva). She is the root cause for all these and hence greater than these.

iii. A part of the supreme Brahman that emerged in the beginning of all creations is called unmanifestation. That is, it is in an unrecognizable form. It is in a state to be formed as this world. Again at the ‘end of this cosmos, the universe in the individual state turns into the root matter (moola porul) and reaches the form of unmanisfestation. When the unidentified form of manifestation is turned into unmanifested form it is called Mahat. She is in the form of this Mohat and hence Mahéroopa.

i. Brahma, Vishou and Shiva, who do the three main tasks of creation, protection and destruction, are the great gods (mahan). Since She does these three tasks by taking those forms She is called Mahéroopa. Similarly She is also in the form of other yogis and siddas and hence Mah Groopa,

ii. Greater than the great people and hence Mahatee. Since She is worshipped by Brahma, Vishnu and Shiva, She is Poojya. Hence as Mahatee and Poojyé, She is Mahdpoojya.

iv. Ithas been mentioned in Padma Purana and Devee Bhdgavatam as; each of the devas worship Sreedevee in different forms and/ or yantras - out of these Agni, Sukra and Soorya worship Sreedevee in an idol form made of ruby; Shiva Brahma Vishou Kubert Vishvedeva Vayu Vasu Varuna, ‘Agni Sukra Soma Soorya Graha Rokshasa Pisacho Matru Ganati Pedhena Tottatpoojaneeya Devee Moortti Bedho Mantre Chailendra Neela Svarna Roubhya Pittala Komasya Spatika Monikya Muktapala Pravala Vaidoorye Trpuseesa Vajya Loha.
v. The Brahmanga Purdga says that the expiation for all the sins, knowingly or unknowingly done, is just remembering the lotus feet of Sreedevee. Hence Mahdpoojya.

name: महासत्वा
wordByWord: महासत्वा — the full name, understood through the samasa above
summary: (i) One who is the supreme reality —full of Satva character.
(ii) Satva means - character, strength and state of existence. Sreedevee has great character or great strength or great existence.

(ii) each letter in the word bhairava has a significance; bha - creation (bharana ~ to fill, ro - protection (ramana) and va = destruction (vomana}; indicates all the three tasks. hence this indicates lord paramashiva, the accomplisher of creation, preservation and destruction (of the universe) and hence mahdbhairava.padma purana says that he worshiped sreedevee, ‘shambhu: poojayatedeveem mantrashaktimayeem shubhém| akshamélém kredhrutva nyasenaiva bhavotbhava:ll mohabhairava, by a great sacrifice (by mahdyagdkrama) caused levee to become manifest from alter of the fire of consciousness stata rew sree lolite sohosrandmam (chidagnikunda). hence mahabhairavopoojita. {iv) the word bhairava means ~ bheeroondm samooha: - a group of panicky ladies. she was worshipped by them. the absolute reciprocity: mahābhairava represents the most terrifyingly grand, absolute aspect of transcendent consciousness, who consumes time, space, and all dualities into himself. for him to worship her (pūjitā) shows that dynamic energy and silent witness-awareness are completely equal and mutually adore each other, maintaining the ultimate cosmic balance.

esoteric: The Authority of Will: A mahiṣī is not merely a wife, but the officially crowned queen who holds co-equal administrative and sovereign powers with the king. name: महात्रिपुरसुन्दरी
samasa: • त्रयाणां पुरं तदात्मिका च सा महती च सा सुन्दरी च।
wordByWord: तदात्मिका — having that as her nature
महात्रिपुरसुन्दरी — the full name, understood through the samasa above
summary: (i) One who is the great Tripurasundaree - the di that name.

(ii) She takes all the three forms as ~ one who knows, the intellect which knows and the knowing object. Depending on the person and the object the intellect can be differentiated as small and large. However, the divine knowledge, which is the base and the root cause of all the knowledge (Mahatripurd). She is in the form of ‘Mahétripurdand does not undergo any changes/ transformation by becoming the knower or the known (Mathru or Mey). In addition She is also the divine beauty. Hence Mohdtripurasundaree.
(iii) Her beauty is so great that it cannot be seen in any of the three worlds.

(ii) she takes all the three forms as ~ one who knows, the intellect which knows and the knowing object. depending on the person and the object the intellect can be differentiated as small and large. however, the divine knowledge, which is the base and the root cause of all the knowledge (mahatripurd). she is in the form of ‘mahétripurdand does not undergo any changes/ transformation by becoming the knower or the known (mathru or mey). in addition she is also the divine beauty. hence mohdtripurasundaree. (iii) her beauty is so great that it cannot be seen in any of the three worlds. the sovereign of the three states: tripura refers to the three basic dimensions: the three states of consciousness (waking, dreaming, deep sleep), the three elemental worlds, and the three primary steps of the mantra. she is sundarī(beauty personified) because she balances and runs these three dimensions flawlessly, serving as the transcendent fourth state (turīya) that ties them together.
esoteric: The Sovereign of the Three States: Tripura refers to the three basic dimensions: the three states of consciousness (waking, dreaming, deep sleep), the three elemental worlds, and the three primary steps of the mantra. She is Sundarī(beauty personified) because She balances and runs these three dimensions flawlessly, serving as the transcendent fourth state (Turīya) that ties them together.

esoteric: The Sacredness of Art and Action: The sixty-four kalās include all human arts, sciences, logic, music, and crafts. This name shows that Her worship is not limited to narrow religious items; rather, every single expression of human creativity, skill, and refined beauty is a direct offering that adores Her cosmic presence.

name: महाचतुष्षष्टिकोटियोगिनीगणसेविता
samasa: • चतुष्षष्टिकोटिसङ्ख्यकाः योगिनीनां गणाः। तैः महद्भिः सेविता।
wordByWord: महाचतुष्षष्टिकोटियोगिनीगणसेविता — the full name, understood through the samasa above
summary: (ii) There are 8 Prakatayoginees as Matruk@Devees, in the middle layer of Chatusrd (Noopurd) of Sree Chakra. They are; Brahmee, Maheshwaree, Kouméree, Vaishnavee, Vardhee, Mahendree, ‘Chamunda and Mahdlakshme. Each ofthese has 8 yoginees as allied Devees. Each of these 8 yoginees has one crore as retinue. Hence 8 x8 = 64 crores yoginees. Hence Sreedevee is called as being served by these yoginees. Mahdcatushshashtikotiyogineeganasevita.

(ii) The word Maha means nine. Mahdcatushshashtikotimeans 9 x 64 = 576 crores ofPevees. in every Avaragachakra of Sree Chakra thereare 64 crores of yoginees. Hence in all the 9 Avarnas there are 576 yoginees. Sreedevee is being worshipped by all these yoginees.
searchText: 237 महाचतुष्षष्टिकोटियोगिनीगणसेविता • चतुष्षष्टिकोटिसङ्ख्यकाः योगिनीनां गणाः। तैः महद्भिः सेविता। चतुष्षष्टिकोटिसङ्ख्यकाः — numbering sixty-four crores योगिनीनां — of yoginis गणाः। — hosts; groups महद्भिः — by great सेविता। — served; worshipped महाचतुष्षष्टिकोटियोगिनीगणसेविता — the full name, understood through the samasa above (ii) there are 8 prakatayoginees as matruk@devees, in the middle layer of chatusrd (noopurd) of sree chakra. they are; brahmee, maheshwaree, kouméree, vaishnavee, vardhee, mahendree, ‘chamunda and mahdlakshme. each ofthese has 8 yoginees as allied devees. each of these 8 yoginees has one crore as retinue. hence 8 x8 = 64 crores yoginees. hence sreedevee is called as being served by these yoginees. mahdcatushshashtikotiyogineeganasevita. (ii) the word maha means nine. mahdcatushshashtikotimeans 9 x 64 = 576 crores ofpevees. in every avaragachakra of sree chakra thereare 64 crores of yoginees. hence in all the 9 avarnas there are 576 yoginees. sreedevee is being worshipped by all these yoginees. the grid of cosmic forces: the sixty-four crores of yoginis represent the infinite sub-currents of kinetic energy that manage the atomic and cosmic processes of nature. she sits at the dead center of this massive network as its commanding director, completely served and obeyed by every single stream of power.
esoteric: The Grid of Cosmic Forces: The sixty-four crores of yoginis represent the infinite sub-currents of kinetic energy that manage the atomic and cosmic processes of nature. She sits at the dead center of this massive network as its commanding director, completely served and obeyed by every single stream of power.

name: मनुविद्या
wordByWord: मनुविद्या — the full name, understood through the samasa above
summary: (i) One who is in the form Vidyé adored by Manu.

(ii) It is being told that earlier Sree Vidya was adored by twelve devotees; Manu, Chandra (Moon), Kubera, Lopémudra, Agasthyé, Monmatha (Cupid), Agni (fire), Soorya (Sun), Indra, Skanda, Shiva and Doorvasa. Each of them, by the skills of penance, felt a type of ‘mantra and worshipped. Manu is the top most among them. Sreedevee is in the form of Sree VidyaMantra formed by him.
(iii) Sree Vidya worshipped by these 12 devotees has been mentioned in 13 names - 239, 297, 305, 359, 375, 406, 558, 586, 647, 715, 720, 733 and 785.
searchText: 238 मनुविद्या • मनुविद्या। मनुविद्या। — the vidya of manu मनुविद्या — the full name, understood through the samasa above (i) one who is in the form vidyé adored by manu. (ii) it is being told that earlier sree vidya was adored by twelve devotees; manu, chandra (moon), kubera, lopémudra, agasthyé, monmatha (cupid), agni (fire), soorya (sun), indra, skanda, shiva and doorvasa. each of them, by the skills of penance, felt a type of ‘mantra and worshipped. manu is the top most among them. sreedevee is in the form of sree vidyamantra formed by him. (iii) sree vidya worshipped by these 12 devotees has been mentioned in 13 names - 239, 297, 305, 359, 375, 406, 558, 586, 647, 715, 720, 733 and 785. the lineage of the primordial lawgiver: manu is the primordial lawgiver of cosmic order. the manu-vidyā is a specific twelve-syllabled variation of the śrī vidyā mantra. this identifies her as the source of that fundamental wisdom that sets up and maintains the moral and structural balance (dharma) of human eras.
esoteric: The Lineage of the Primordial Lawgiver: Manu is the primordial lawgiver of cosmic order. The Manu-vidyā is a specific twelve-syllabled variation of the Śrī Vidyā mantra. This identifies Her as the source of that fundamental wisdom that sets up and maintains the moral and structural balance (Dharma) of human eras.

searchText: 239 चन्द्रविद्या • चन्द्रविद्या। चन्द्रविद्या। — the vidya of chandra चन्द्रविद्या — the full name, understood through the samasa above (i) chandra is the second in the 12 people mentioned in the earlier name. that vidyd is being mentioned here. the path of lunar bliss: the candra-vidyā is the specific mantric methodology practiced by the moon, focusing on the dynamic expansion of the lunar currents (amṛta) within the crown center. it represents the path of cool, soothing, internal bliss that balances the solar fires of logic.
esoteric: The Path of Lunar Bliss: The Candra-vidyā is the specific mantric methodology practiced by the Moon, focusing on the dynamic expansion of the lunar currents (amṛta) within the crown center. It represents the path of cool, soothing, internal bliss that balances the solar fires of logic.

searchText: 241 चारुरूपा • चारु रूपं यस्याः सा। चारु — beautiful; charming रूपं — form यस्याः — of whom; whose सा। — she चारुरूपा — the full name, understood through the samasa above (i) she has such a beautiful form as mentioned in the names tripurasundaree, lolita and sundaree. the harmony of reality: cāru implies a beauty that satisfies the mind and brings immediate peace. her form is cārurūpā because it embodies the ultimate, geometric harmony of truth, acting as a natural clearing agent that balances the chaotic thoughts of the observer.

name: पञ्चप्रेतासनासीना

summary: (i) One who has her seat formed of five corpses.

(ii) The five forms of Shiva as Brahma, Vishnu, Rudra, Esand and Saddshiva appeared from Brahmam. They, as per the order of Sreedevee, do the S tasks respectively of creation, protection, destruction, thirodhéné and anugroha. During the destruction of the universe (mahdpralayé) they are relieved from their respective duties and merge with Sreedevee. That is, Sadéshiva becomes the sitting plank and the four others become the four legs of the seat. Since they deprived of their power, they are called as corpses. Pra + Ita — the one that has started from the place of dwelling to other place.

(iii) The above five have respective Shaktis such asVamé, Gyeshtd, Roudhree, etc. Since they are deprived of their Shaktis, they are individually incapable of action and hence corpses. (Wv) Paramashiva, who is the sitting plank, is the 25"tatva above all. When Shakti unites with him it becomes the 26" Sadakyatatva. This is the Samarasya form of Shakti and Paramoshiva. They are an inteBrated form without any difference. (“) This can be compared with 947" name PanchapretamanchadhiSsdyinee.

(iii) the above five have respective shaktis such asvamé, gyeshtd, roudhree, etc. since they are deprived of their shaktis, they are individually incapable of action and hence corpses. (wv) paramashiva, who is the sitting plank, is the 25"tatva above all. when shakti unites with him it becomes the 26" sadakyatatva. this is the samarasya form of shakti and paramoshiva. they are an intebrated form without any difference. (“) this can be compared with 947" name panchapretamanchadhissdyinee. the lord of inactive principles: the five pretas are brahmā, viṣṇu, rudra, īśvara, and sadāśiva when they are separated from their active energetic matrix. without her dynamic energy, these highest cosmic functional principles become completely inert, like corpses. she sits upon them, showing that she is the singular, master life force that awakens and directs them.

summary: (ii) Dharma indicates a sort of limit or restrains. That is, the controls for the caste, religion and other dshramds respectively are called dharma. if these are broken, it is adharma. In this context also, She is not bound by these distinctions. Hence Sreedevee is beyond dharma and adharma,

(iv) Dharma is a quality or character. For instance to be hot is 2 character, hence dharma. One wha has a character is called dharmee. \f the fire loses its character of heat then it is adharma (In Ramayana, Sita requests {not orders} fire to be cool with Hanuman ~ sheeto bhova hanumata: - hence it was cool.). If one loses its character it is adharma. Sreedevee is not bound by any character and hence She does not have dharma or adharma. (V) Dharma also means bondage. The soul is bound by various bondages like husband, wife, son, friend, etc. Each relationship is craming the soul with different bondages. Being without these bondages or getting relieved of them is called liberation, hence it is ‘dharma. Sreedevee is beyond bondages or relief from them.

(vi) Dharma is a quality or character. A dharmee is with this. Hence She can be called also as adharmee. Sreedevee is without any quality or attachment to it. (vil) Dharma indicates that letter of Shakti, and adharma that of Shiva. In the lettersofPanchadosakshareemantra the three ‘K's and two 'HA's indicate Shiva letters. searchText: 255 धर्माधर्मविवर्जिता • धर्मश्चाधर्मश्च ताभ्यां विशेषेण वर्जिता। धर्मश्चाधर्मश्च — dharma and adharma ताभ्यां — from/by those two विशेषेण — especially; completely वर्जिता। — devoid of धर्माधर्मविवर्जिता — the full name, understood through the samasa above (ii) dharma indicates a sort of limit or restrains. that is, the controls for the caste, religion and other dshramds respectively are called dharma. if these are broken, it is adharma. in this context also, she is not bound by these distinctions. hence sreedevee is beyond dharma and adharma, (iv) dharma is a quality or character. for instance to be hot is 2 character, hence dharma. one wha has a character is called dharmee. \f the fire loses its character of heat then it is adharma (in ramayana, sita requests {not orders} fire to be cool with hanuman ~ sheeto bhova hanumata: - hence it was cool.). if one loses its character it is adharma. sreedevee is not bound by any character and hence she does not have dharma or adharma. (vi) dharma is a quality or character. a dharmee is with this. hence she can be called also as adharmee. sreedevee is without any quality or attachment to it. (vil) dharma indicates that letter of shakti, and adharma that of shiva. in the lettersofpanchadosakshareemantra the three ‘k's and two 'ha's indicate shiva letters. the letters ‘a’, ‘e and three ‘u's indicateshakti letters. the three ‘hreem’s are that of united shaktr and shiva. this is indicated in the results part of lalitatrishatee. the same has also been mentioned by sreebhdskararéyar in his shrarar heaatam sree lolita sohosrandmam vorivesyérahasyam. the word vivarjitd means to grow. the inherent meaning is that because of the letters in the form shivaand shakti the panchadasdksharee is completely grown. the beyond-moral absolute: dharma and adharma are rules that govern individual action in the dualistic world to maintain structural balance. because she is the supreme absolute whole, relative rules of merit and demerit cannot condition her. she sits as the transcendent background that supports the rules without being bound by them.

name: विश्वरूपा
samasa: • श्वा इव वृत्तिः विगतं यस्याः सा।
wordByWord: विश्वरूपा — the full name, understood through the samasa above
summary: i) In the order of creation, in the formation of all the tatvds from ature (prakrutee) till universe (prithivee), it has been mentioned that all the subtle tatvds one above the other merges with nature. ‘The creation, from the Brahmam the first manifestation is darkness {tamas), the next is that of intellect called mahat, then that of the soul threefold egotism (chamkéré), followed by the five subtle ‘elements beginning with sound; among these are the five energies ‘of knowledge and the five energies of action, in these energies the formerfive individually generate the five senses and collectively the ‘antahkkarana, The latter five generate individually the five organs of action and collectively the prdnds. The sound and the others four subtle forces generate the five gross elements. This is an established doctrine. There the chaitanyamanifestedthrough the gross, subtle, and causal vehicles respectively termed, Vishva, Taljasd, and PragnG; these manifested ones (three groups of vehicles) respectively are turned Vaisvénara, Hiranyagarbha and Isvara. Among this Vishvd is in awaken (JGgrath) state, Taijasdin the dream (Swapna) state and Prdgnd in the deep sleep state (Sushupti). searchText: 257 जागरिणी • जागर अस्याः अस्तीति। जागर — waking state अस्याः — of her अस्तीति। — exists; is present जागरिणी — the full name, understood through the samasa above (i) one who is in waking state of the soul. name: तैजसात्मिका
wordByWord: तैजसात्मिका — the full name, understood through the samasa above
summary: (i) Sreedevee is in the state of dream wherein the name of the soul is Taijasan.
(ii) Taijas6 Is called Hiranygarbha in the collective form of the souls She is in that form.
searchText: 259 तैजसात्मिका • तैजसाः आत्मा यस्याः सा। तैजसाः — taijasa; dream-state self आत्मा — self यस्याः — of whom; whose सा। — she तैजसात्मिका — the full name, understood through the samasa above (i) sreedevee is in the state of dream wherein the name of the soul is taijasan. (ii) taijas6 is called hiranygarbha in the collective form of the souls she is in that form. in the saubhāgyabhāskara, bhāskararāya illuminates this name through the lens of vedānta and the states of consciousness (avasthā-traya). the subtle state: in cosmic manifestation, तैजस (taijasa) is the presiding deity of the subtle universe, associated with the dreaming state (svapna-avasthā), where the mind experiences objects fashioned out of pure impressions (vāsanā) and light. she is the internal witness and the very essence of this subtle, luminous layer of reality. the principle of splendor: it also signifies her identity with pure energy or brilliance (tejas). she is the collective effulgence of all cosmic forces, the foundational light that allows the subtle organs (mind, intellect, senses) to perceive and function.
esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara, Bhāskararāya illuminates this name through the lens of Vedānta and the states of consciousness (avasthā-traya).

The Subtle State: In cosmic manifestation, तैजस (Taijasa) is the presiding deity of the subtle universe, associated with the dreaming state (svapna-avasthā), where the mind experiences objects fashioned out of pure impressions (vāsanā) and light. She is the internal witness and the very essence of this subtle, luminous layer of reality.

The Principle of Splendor: It also signifies Her identity with pure energy or brilliance (tejas). She is the collective effulgence of all cosmic forces, the foundational light that allows the subtle organs (mind, intellect, senses) to perceive and function.

name: सुप्ता
wordByWord: सुप्ता — the full name, understood through the samasa above
summary: (i) Sreedevee is in the state of sleep of the soul
searchText: 260 सुप्ता • सुप्ता। सुप्ता — the full name, understood through the samasa above (i) sreedevee is in the state of sleep of the soul in the saubhāgyabhāskara, bhāskararāya explains सुप्ता through both micro-cosmic yoga and macro-cosmic states of consciousness: the dormant power: in the subtle body of living beings, she resides as कुण्डलिनी in the मूलाधार chakra, remaining in a dormant or sleeping state until awakened by spiritual practice. the state of deep sleep: she represents the state of deep, dreamless sleep (सुषुप्ति), which is one of the states of consciousness. in this state, the individual soul temporarily dissolves all mental modifications, names, and forms, resting in a state of unmanifested causal bliss. cosmic dissolution: on a cosmic level, सुप्ता refers to the state of प्रलय (cosmic dissolution), where the entire universe rests asleep within her, waiting for the next cycle of creation.
esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara, Bhāskararāya explains सुप्ता through both micro-cosmic yoga and macro-cosmic states of consciousness:
The Dormant Power: In the subtle body of living beings, She resides as कुण्डलिनी in the मूलाधार chakra, remaining in a dormant or sleeping state until awakened by spiritual practice.

The State of Deep Sleep: She represents the state of deep, dreamless sleep (सुषुप्ति), which is one of the states of consciousness. In this state, the individual soul temporarily dissolves all mental modifications, names, and forms, resting in a state of unmanifested causal bliss.
Cosmic Dissolution: On a cosmic level, सुप्ता refers to the state of प्रलय (cosmic dissolution), where the entire universe rests asleep within Her, waiting for the next cycle of creation.

name: प्राज्ञात्मिका
wordByWord: प्राज्ञात्मिका — the full name, understood through the samasa above
summary: (i) The soul fond of the body, when in deep sleep state is called Prdgyan. She is in that form.

(ii) In the deep sleep state, there are three different experiences. Knowing nothing (Agndnam - ignorant), Ahamtd — the thought that | am different and happy (Sukhd) - To say after getting up - | had a ‘g00d sleep, | do not know anything, In this state he gets the name Pragyan. This form is also Sreedevee. The same in a collective form ‘of all the souls is called Eshwara, She is in that form also.
searchText: 261 प्राज्ञात्मिका • प्राज्ञस्य आत्मिका। प्राज्ञस्य — of prajna; deep-sleep self आत्मिका। — whose nature is प्राज्ञात्मिका — the full name, understood through the samasa above (i) the soul fond of the body, when in deep sleep state is called prdgyan. she is in that form. (ii) in the deep sleep state, there are three different experiences. knowing nothing (agndnam - ignorant), ahamtd — the thought that | am different and happy (sukhd) - to say after getting up - | had a ‘g00d sleep, | do not know anything, in this state he gets the name pragyan. this form is also sreedevee. the same in a collective form ‘of all the souls is called eshwara, she is in that form also. following the structural progression of the states of consciousness (avasthā-traya) in the saubhāgyabhāskara, bhāskararāya links this directly to the preceding names (तैजसात्मिका and सुप्ता): the causal state: in vedāntic cosmology, while taijasa rules the dream state, प्राज्ञ (prājña) is the lord of the deep sleep state (suṣupti). in this state, all individual distinctions, mental ripples, and dualities dissolve into a unified mass of baseline consciousness (prajñāna-ghana), full of bliss. she is that underlying causal sheet (kāraṇa-śarīra). supreme knowledge: the name also breaks down as प्रकृष्टः ज्ञः (prakṛṣṭhaḥ jñaḥ — the highest knower). she is the absolute, omniscient intelligence that remains awake as the ultimate witness even when the individual mind completely shuts down in deep sleep.
esoteric: Following the structural progression of the states of consciousness (avasthā-traya) in the Saubhāgyabhāskara, Bhāskararāya links this directly to the preceding names (तैजसात्मिका and सुप्ता):

The Causal State: In Vedāntic cosmology, while Taijasa rules the dream state, प्राज्ञ (Prājña) is the lord of the deep sleep state (suṣupti). In this state, all individual distinctions, mental ripples, and dualities dissolve into a unified mass of baseline consciousness (prajñāna-ghana), full of bliss. She is that underlying causal sheet (kāraṇa-śarīra).

Supreme Knowledge: The name also breaks down as प्रकृष्टः ज्ञः (prakṛṣṭhaḥ jñaḥ — the highest knower). She is the absolute, omniscient intelligence that remains awake as the ultimate witness even when the individual mind completely shuts down in deep sleep.

Tureeyananda Nathasya Prasannatvadh Varénane\ Turyeti Nama Vikyatam Tasya Devyénirantaram!| (wi) It is mentioned in Soundaryalaharee as; as the Saddshiva form is above Brahma, Vishnu and Rudra ~ the fourth form Mahdmaya is above the three forms Saraswathee, Lakshmee and Parvathee.
searchText: 262 तुर्या • तुर्या। तुर्या। — the fourth state तुर्या — the full name, understood through the samasa above (i) one who is the soul in the fourth state (state of ecstasy). (ii) shuddhavidya philosophy is to clearly understand the three states awaken,dream and deep sleep and distinguish the differences between the souls in each of these statesvishva, taijasd, and prajnasuch an understanding state is called thefourth state or tureeyo. hencesreedeveealsoacquires that name, {ii the biiss obtained in the fourth state makes the enjoyer feels that this is the true state and other three are not. the soul which has reached this state still has the other states, based on the worldly trends. but, still he would not be affected by those. (iv) the state mentioned in vedas -shivamatvaitam chaturttom manyanteis called tureeya. (v) the tripura siddhdnto says, as she presented herself to turlyanandanétha (a certain teacher) sreedevee is known by the name turiya. tureeyananda nathasya prasannatvadh varénane\ turyeti nama vikyatam tasya devyénirantaram!| (wi) it is mentioned in soundaryalaharee as; as the saddshiva form is above brahma, vishnu and rudra ~ the fourth form mahdmaya is above the three forms saraswathee, lakshmee and parvathee. bhāskararāya interprets this as the ultimate culmination of the preceding states of consciousness: transcendence of triads: तुर्या (turyā) is the transcendental state of consciousness that underlies, permeates, and transcends the other three relative states—waking (jāgrat), dreaming (svapna), and deep sleep (suṣupti). she is not a separate state, but the very substratum of pure, non-dual awareness (cit) upon which the other three states appear and disappear. the witness identity: while the first three states are bound by time, space, and causality, the fourth is unconditioned, self-luminous, and eternal. by realizing her as turyā, the practitioner breaks free from cosmic delusion (māyā) and recognizes their own identity as pure brahman.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya interprets this as the ultimate culmination of the preceding states of consciousness:

Transcendence of Triads: तुर्या (Turyā) is the transcendental state of consciousness that underlies, permeates, and transcends the other three relative states—waking (jāgrat), dreaming (svapna), and deep sleep (suṣupti). She is not a separate state, but the very substratum of pure, non-dual awareness (cit) upon which the other three states appear and disappear.

The Witness Identity: While the first three states are bound by time, space, and causality, the Fourth is unconditioned, self-luminous, and eternal. By realizing Her as Turyā, the practitioner breaks free from cosmic delusion (māyā) and recognizes their own identity as pure Brahman.

name: सर्वावस्थाविवर्जिता

Since by crossing all the four states the soul merges with the Supreme being, Sreedevee, there is no state for it. Further in this state there is nothing like individual or collective forms.

(ii) This state ~ even if this is not correct ~ because this is not a state — this is not the fifth one ~ this is unexplainable. Beyond comprehension by the mind and speech.
