searchText: 263 सर्वावस्थाविवर्जिता • सर्वाभिः अवस्थाभिः विशेषेण वर्जिता। सर्वाभिः — by all अवस्थाभिः — states विशेषेण — especially; completely वर्जिता। — devoid of सर्वावस्थाविवर्जिता — the full name, understood through the samasa above (i)  one who is devoid of all states. {i it was mentioned that in addition to the three states, the fourth state is called swoon(thureeyam). beyond this state is transcendental(thureeyatheetham). it is not called as a state. this is transcendental ~ beyond the fourth state. since by crossing all the four states the soul merges with the supreme being, sreedevee, there is no state for it. further in this state there is nothing like individual or collective forms. (ii) this state ~ even if this is not correct ~ because this is not a state — this is not the fifth one ~ this is unexplainable. beyond comprehension by the mind and speech. to be understood only by experience. bhdskarordya's varivasyarahasyam states; anandaikaghanastvamya dvachdmapil nagocharon roondm tuyadheedhavesthall bhāskararāya provides the definitive philosophical conclusion to this entire sequence of names: beyond the states: though she manifests as the waking state (jāgariṇī), the dreaming state (svapantī/taijasātmikā), the deep sleep state (suptā/prājñātmikā), and even the fourth state (turyā), she is ultimately तूर्यातीत (turyātīta)—completely untouched by any modifications or conditions. absolute immutability: states belong to the changing world of matter (prakṛti) and the finite mind. as the immutable, supreme reality (nirguṇa-brahman), she undergoes no transitions. she is the eternal screen upon which the cosmic movie of changing states is projected, remaining forever unblemished and free.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya provides the definitive philosophical conclusion to this entire sequence of names:

Beyond the States: Though She manifests as the waking state (jāgariṇī), the dreaming state (svapantī/taijasātmikā), the deep sleep state (suptā/prājñātmikā), and even the fourth state (turyā), She is ultimately तूर्यातीत (turyātīta)—completely untouched by any modifications or conditions.

Absolute Immutability: States belong to the changing world of matter (prakṛti) and the finite mind. As the immutable, supreme reality (nirguṇa-brahman), She undergoes no transitions. She is the eternal screen upon which the cosmic movie of changing states is projected, remaining forever unblemished and free.

name: सृष्टिकर्त्री
wordByWord: सृष्टिकर्त्री — the full name, understood through the samasa above
summary: (i) One who does the task of creation. {li) Creation is the function of Esvard when the rajas quality predominates. To distinguish the forms like Brahma from supreme-being is an illusion (Maya).
searchText: 264 सृष्टिकर्त्री • सृष्टेः कर्त्री। सृष्टेः — of creation कर्त्री। — doer; creator सृष्टिकर्त्री — the full name, understood through the samasa above (i) one who does the task of creation. {li) creation is the function of esvard when the rajas quality predominates. to distinguish the forms like brahma from supreme-being is an illusion (maya). in the saubhāgyabhāskara, bhāskararāya explains that while ordinary systems attribute creation to brahmā, the ultimate agency belongs solely to her. the imparting of agency: she is the fundamental kinetic power (śakti) that activates brahmā. without her presence, the masculine creative principle remains inert and incapable of projecting the universe. identity with brahmā: bhāskararāya notes that she herself assumes the form of brahmā (brahmarūpā) during the cosmic dawn to manifest the name-and-form universe out of her own being.
esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara, Bhāskararāya explains that while ordinary systems attribute creation to Brahmā, the ultimate agency belongs solely to Her.

The Imparting of Agency: She is the fundamental kinetic power (Śakti) that activates Brahmā. Without Her presence, the masculine creative principle remains inert and incapable of projecting the universe.
Identity with Brahmā: Bhāskararāya notes that She herself assumes the form of Brahmā (Brahmarūpā) during the cosmic dawn to manifest the name-and-form universe out of Her own being.

name: ब्रह्मरूपा
wordByWord: ब्रह्मरूपा — the full name, understood through the samasa above
summary: (i) One who is in the form of Brahma.
(ii) Brahma, who takes care of the creation task, is the distinguished form of Sreedevee created by illusion (Maya).
searchText: 265 ब्रह्मरूपा • ब्रह्मब्रह्मा रूपं यस्याः सा। ब्रह्मब्रह्मा — brahman/brahma रूपं — form यस्याः — of whom; whose सा। — she ब्रह्मरूपा — the full name, understood through the samasa above (i) one who is in the form of brahma. (ii) brahma, who takes care of the creation task, is the distinguished form of sreedevee created by illusion (maya). bhāskararāya provides a dual semantic interpretation here to bridge mythological function with non-dual philosophy: the personal creator: she manifests as the cosmic architect brahmā, charged with the rajo-guṇa necessary to project the material worlds. the impersonal absolute: beyond the deity brahmā, the name refers to her nature as परब्रह्म (parabrahman). she is the infinite, unconditioned reality itself, upon which the illusion of the universe is superimposed.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya provides a dual semantic interpretation here to bridge mythological function with non-dual philosophy:
The Personal Creator: She manifests as the cosmic architect Brahmā, charged with the rajo-guṇa necessary to project the material worlds.

The Impersonal Absolute: Beyond the deity Brahmā, the name refers to Her nature as परब्रह्म (Parabrahman). She is the infinite, unconditioned reality itself, upon which the illusion of the universe is superimposed.

name: गोप्त्री
wordByWord: गोप्त्री — the full name, understood through the samasa above
summary: (i) One who does the task of protector. ~ \ii) Sreedevee takes up the protection function to sustain the created universe ~ when Hersatva quality predominates.
searchText: 266 गोप्त्री • गोप्त्री। गोप्त्री। — protector गोप्त्री — the full name, understood through the samasa above (i) one who does the task of protector. ~ \ii) sreedevee takes up the protection function to sustain the created universe ~ when hersatva quality predominates. bhāskararāya defines her protective nature across both the macrocosm and the individual path of the practitioner: sustenance of the cosmos: as the inner sustaining force, she preserves the cosmic order during the period of manifestation, keeping the laws of nature and karma in perfect equilibrium. protection of secret knowledge: bhāskararāya emphasizes a specific esoteric meaning of the root gup—to conceal. she is the guardian of the śrīvidyā tradition, keeping this sacred knowledge hidden (gopaniya) from the uninitiated and revealing it only to those with pure devotion.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya defines Her protective nature across both the macrocosm and the individual path of the practitioner:
Sustenance of the Cosmos: As the inner sustaining force, She preserves the cosmic order during the period of manifestation, keeping the laws of nature and karma in perfect equilibrium.

Protection of Secret Knowledge: Bhāskararāya emphasizes a specific esoteric meaning of the root gup—to conceal. She is the guardian of the Śrīvidyā tradition, keeping this sacred knowledge hidden (gopaniya) from the uninitiated and revealing it only to those with pure devotion.

name: गोविन्दरूपिणी
wordByWord: गोविन्दरूपिणी — the full name, understood through the samasa above
summary: (i) One who is in the form of Govinda, that is, Vishnu.
(ii) The protector of the universe, Vishnu, who predominantly has the Satva character, is also Sreedevee only.

(iii) Go means speech. Govinda means one who can be understood by speech. The Supreme Being is beyond the reach of speech. Since Govinda has taken the protection task, once he hears the crying of the soul, he immediately appears to him.

(iv) The name Govinda is explained in several ways: since he bore and takes out the earth that is being destructed, he is Govinda; head of cows; one who makes speech reach him; one who can be reached through languages; and one who understands the souls through the statements of Vedanta.
searchText: 267 गोविन्दरूपिणी • गोविन्दस्य रूपम् अस्याः अस्तीति। गोविन्दस्य — of govinda अस्याः — of her अस्तीति। — exists; is present गोविन्दरूपिणी — the full name, understood through the samasa above (i) one who is in the form of govinda, that is, vishnu. (ii) the protector of the universe, vishnu, who predominantly has the satva character, is also sreedevee only. (iii) go means speech. govinda means one who can be understood by speech. the supreme being is beyond the reach of speech. since govinda has taken the protection task, once he hears the crying of the soul, he immediately appears to him. (iv) the name govinda is explained in several ways: since he bore and takes out the earth that is being destructed, he is govinda; head of cows; one who makes speech reach him; one who can be reached through languages; and one who understands the souls through the statements of vedanta. bhāskararāya details her role during the maintenance phase of the universe: cosmic preservation: to sustain the universe created by brahmā, she adopts the sattva-guṇa and manifests as govinda (viṣṇu), the all-pervading protector. linguistic derivation of govinda: he highlights the structural roots of गोविन्द—go can mean the vedas, the earth, or the senses, and vinda means the knower or finder. she is the ultimate entity found through the vedas, the protector of the earth, and the inner ruler of the senses.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya details Her role during the maintenance phase of the universe:
Cosmic Preservation: To sustain the universe created by Brahmā, She adopts the sattva-guṇa and manifests as Govinda (Viṣṇu), the all-pervading protector.

Linguistic Derivation of Govinda: He highlights the structural roots of गोविन्द—go can mean the Vedas, the earth, or the senses, and vinda means the knower or finder. She is the ultimate entity found through the Vedas, the protector of the earth, and the inner ruler of the senses.

name: संहारिणी
wordByWord: संहारिणी — the full name, understood through the samasa above
summary: (i) One who is does the task of destruction (Samhérd),
(i) Destruction of the universe by reducing into atoms is the third task. This is the function of Sreedevee in the form Esvara when the tamas quality predominates. Hence She is Samhdrinee.
searchText: 268 संहारिणी • संहारिणी। संहारं करोतीति। संहारं — dissolution; destruction करोतीति। — does; performs संहारिणी — the full name, understood through the samasa above (i) one who is does the task of destruction (samhérd), (i) destruction of the universe by reducing into atoms is the third task. this is the function of sreedevee in the form esvara when the tamas quality predominates. hence she is samhdrinee. in the saubhāgyabhāskara, bhāskararāya glosses this name as the withdrawal or gathering back of the projected universe into its primal causal state. the act of reabsorption: she is the ultimate consciousness that reabsorbs all names, forms, and elements back into herself at the end of a cosmic cycle (kalpa). this dissolution is not a destructive act but a return to the source. the resolution of duality: on an internal level, she is the power that dissolves the practitioner's sense of duality, drawing the outward-bound mind back into the heart center.
esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara, Bhāskararāya glosses this name as the withdrawal or gathering back of the projected universe into its primal causal state.

The Act of Reabsorption: She is the ultimate consciousness that reabsorbs all names, forms, and elements back into Herself at the end of a cosmic cycle (kalpa). This dissolution is not a destructive act but a return to the source.
The Resolution of Duality: On an internal level, She is the power that dissolves the practitioner's sense of duality, drawing the outward-bound mind back into the heart center.

name: रुद्ररूपा
wordByWord: रुद्ररूपा — the full name, understood through the samasa above
summary: (i) One who js the in the form of Rudra.

(ii) The form of Sreedevee when the tamas quality dominates is called Rudra. This destroys the world. Rudra, who drives away sorrow; because of the samsdrd cycle. Rujom — sorrow. Drdvayati removes, Rotayati means to make other cry. The separation on account of destruction makes others cry. Hence Rudran. Sun is also called as Rudra. During great dissolution Sun rises and burns the entire world. On account of this torridly rains and world submerges in the waters.
(iv) To start with he originated from the forehead of Brahma. He went loudly and fearfully; because he wept, he is called the weeper(Rudro).
searchText: 269 रुद्ररूपा • रुद्रः रूपं यस्याः सा। रुद्रः — rudra रूपं — form यस्याः — of whom; whose सा। — she रुद्ररूपा — the full name, understood through the samasa above (i) one who js the in the form of rudra. (ii) the form of sreedevee when the tamas quality dominates is called rudra. this destroys the world. rudra, who drives away sorrow; because of the samsdrd cycle. rujom — sorrow. drdvayati removes, rotayati means to make other cry. the separation on account of destruction makes others cry. hence rudran. sun is also called as rudra. during great dissolution sun rises and burns the entire world. on account of this torridly rains and world submerges in the waters. (iv) to start with he originated from the forehead of brahma. he went loudly and fearfully; because he wept, he is called the weeper(rudro). bhāskararāya clarifies her direct alignment with the third aspect of the cosmic trinity: the principle of dissolution: to execute the act of saṁhāra (dissolution), she assumes the tamo-guṇa and manifests as rudra. etymological roots: bhāskararāya highlights that रुद्र denotes the one who melts or drives away (drāvayati) the sorrow (rut) of saṁsāra. therefore, her form as rudra is ultimately an expression of compassion that dissolves the bondages of finite existence.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya clarifies Her direct alignment with the third aspect of the cosmic trinity:
The Principle of Dissolution: To execute the act of saṁhāra (dissolution), She assumes the tamo-guṇa and manifests as Rudra.

Etymological Roots: Bhāskararāya highlights that रुद्र denotes the one who melts or drives away (drāvayati) the sorrow (rut) of saṁsāra. Therefore, Her form as Rudra is ultimately an expression of compassion that dissolves the bondages of finite existence.

name: तिरोधानकरी
wordByWord: तिरोधानकरी — meaning follows from the samasa: तिरोधानं करोतीति।
summary: i. One who is the cause of the disappearance of all.

ii. Tirodhana is complete destruction, that is, the absorption of even the atoms into finer atoms, integrating ego, intelligence and intellect with nature, in the order in which the universe originated. Sreedevee does this in this form.
iii. Tiraskarini is the name of one of the associates of Sreedevee. She is in that form. Tiraskarini means one who hides Her form from those who do not have devotion.
searchText: 270 तिरोधानकरी • तिरोधानं करोतीति। तिरोधानं — concealment; disappearance करोतीति — makes; manifests तिरोधानकरी — meaning follows from the samasa: तिरोधानं करोतीति। i. one who is the cause of the disappearance of all. ii. tirodhana is complete destruction, that is, the absorption of even the atoms into finer atoms, integrating ego, intelligence and intellect with nature, in the order in which the universe originated. sreedevee does this in this form. iii. tiraskarini is the name of one of the associates of sreedevee. she is in that form. tiraskarini means one who hides her form from those who do not have devotion. bhāskararāya explains this through the foundational five-fold cosmic acts (pañcakṛtya) of the śāktāgamas: the power of māyā: तिरोधान (tirodhāna) is the fourth cosmic function, where the absolute consciousness conceals its true, infinite nature from individual souls. she creates the limitation of ego, time, and space, allowing the play of the relative world to occur. without this veiling power, the experience of a differentiated universe would be impossible.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya explains this through the foundational five-fold cosmic acts (pañcakṛtya) of the Śāktāgamas:

The Power of Māyā: तिरोधान (tirodhāna) is the fourth cosmic function, where the absolute consciousness conceals its true, infinite nature from individual souls. She creates the limitation of ego, time, and space, allowing the play of the relative world to occur. Without this veiling power, the experience of a differentiated universe would be impossible.

name: ईश्वरी
wordByWord: ईश्वरी — meaning follows from the samasa: ईश्वरी।
summary: i. One who is in the form of Ishvara.
ii. One who does the fourth task called Tirodhana is Ishvara, completely in the form of sattva quality. Sreedevee is in this form.
iii. The quality of Ishvara is lordship, activity, independence, consciousness, etc. Since Sreedevee is with all these qualities, She is called Ishvari.
iv. Sree Adi Sankara, in his commentary on Vishnu Sahasranamam for the 36th name Ishvara, mentions: one who has all the wealth without being affected by any type of sorrows.
searchText: 271 ईश्वरी • ईश्वरी। ईश्वरी — ruler; mistress ईश्वरी — meaning follows from the samasa: ईश्वरी। i. one who is in the form of ishvara. ii. one who does the fourth task called tirodhana is ishvara, completely in the form of sattva quality. sreedevee is in this form. iii. the quality of ishvara is lordship, activity, independence, consciousness, etc. since sreedevee is with all these qualities, she is called ishvari. iv. sree adi sankara, in his commentary on vishnu sahasranamam for the 36th name ishvara, mentions: one who has all the wealth without being affected by any type of sorrows. in the saubhāgyabhāskara, bhāskararāya elevates this from a simple title of lordship to a specific functional state: the lord of concealment: in the hierarchy of the five cosmic principles, she is the sovereign lord associated with the act of concealment (tirodhāna). absolute independence: the name denotes her absolute, unobstructed freedom of will (svātantrya-śakti). she is the supreme director of the universe, subject to no laws other than her own divine intent.
esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara, Bhāskararāya elevates this from a simple title of lordship to a specific functional state:
The Lord of Concealment: In the hierarchy of the five cosmic principles, She is the sovereign lord associated with the act of concealment (tirodhāna).

Absolute Independence: The name denotes Her absolute, unobstructed freedom of will (svātantrya-śakti). She is the supreme director of the universe, subject to no laws other than Her own divine intent.

name: सदाशिवा
wordByWord: सदाशिवा — meaning follows from the samasa: सदा शिवा।
summary: i. One who is in the form of Sadashiva.
ii. Sad means always. Shiva means auspicious form.
iii. Sreedevee, taking the form of Sadashiva, does the fifth task called Anugraha.
searchText: 272 सदाशिवा • सदा शिवा। सदा — always शिवा — auspicious; shiva-form सदाशिवा — meaning follows from the samasa: सदा शिवा। i. one who is in the form of sadashiva. ii. sad means always. shiva means auspicious form. iii. sreedevee, taking the form of sadashiva, does the fifth task called anugraha. bhāskararāya explains this name as the bridge to the final cosmic act: the presiding deity of grace: she manifests as the fifth cosmic principle, sadāśiva, whose fundamental nature is pure sattva untouched by the lower modifications. eternal auspiciousness: while other manifestations adjust according to cosmic cycles, her nature as sadāśiva is immutable baseline benevolence—the ultimate source from which liberation flows.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya explains this name as the bridge to the final cosmic act:
The Presiding Deity of Grace: She manifests as the fifth cosmic principle, Sadāśiva, whose fundamental nature is pure sattva untouched by the lower modifications.
Eternal Auspiciousness: While other manifestations adjust according to cosmic cycles, Her nature as Sadāśiva is immutable baseline benevolence—the ultimate source from which liberation flows.

name: अनुग्रहदा
wordByWord: अनुग्रहदा — meaning follows from the samasa: अनुग्रहं ददातीति।
summary: i. One who confers blessings.

ii. As described earlier, to recreate the Tirodhana world after complete annihilation, the universe again assumes the form of the primordial atoms, and the process is called Anugraha. This is the function of Sreedevee in the form of Paramashiva.

iii. Tirodhana and Anugraha mean respectively bondage and release. In this meaning Ishvara and Paramashiva, who are respectively the external and internal manifestations, are the causes of bondage and release. Sreedevee in those forms gets two names.
iv. The state of Ishvara, who does Tirodhana, has powers to function within prescribed laws. Ishvara does the functions which are externally seen by all - Bahirunmesha-lakshana.

v. Sadashiva shows so much compassion on the souls that they do not suffer a lot. His quality is not known to others, but he has much empathy. Hence his function is not known to others - Antarunmesha-lakshana. Both these are forms of Sreedevee only.
searchText: 273 अनुग्रहदा • अनुग्रहं ददातीति। अनुग्रहं — grace; favour ददातीति — gives अनुग्रहदा — meaning follows from the samasa: अनुग्रहं ददातीति। i. one who confers blessings. ii. as described earlier, to recreate the tirodhana world after complete annihilation, the universe again assumes the form of the primordial atoms, and the process is called anugraha. this is the function of sreedevee in the form of paramashiva. iii. tirodhana and anugraha mean respectively bondage and release. in this meaning ishvara and paramashiva, who are respectively the external and internal manifestations, are the causes of bondage and release. sreedevee in those forms gets two names. iv. the state of ishvara, who does tirodhana, has powers to function within prescribed laws. ishvara does the functions which are externally seen by all - bahirunmesha-lakshana. v. sadashiva shows so much compassion on the souls that they do not suffer a lot. his quality is not known to others, but he has much empathy. hence his function is not known to others - antarunmesha-lakshana. both these are forms of sreedevee only. bhāskararāya details the ultimate capstone of the five cosmic acts: the bestowal of liberation: अनुग्रह (anugraha) is the final act where she shatters the veil of concealment (tirodhāna) that she previously cast. it is the descent of divine grace (śaktipāta) that awakens the individual soul, dissolving spiritual ignorance and granting immediate liberation (mokṣa).
esoteric: Bhāskararāya details the ultimate capstone of the five cosmic acts:

The Bestowal of Liberation: अनुग्रह (anugraha) is the final act where She shatters the veil of concealment (tirodhāna) that She previously cast. It is the descent of divine grace (śaktipāta) that awakens the individual soul, dissolving spiritual ignorance and granting immediate liberation (mokṣa).

name: पञ्चकृत्यपरायणा
samasa: • पञ्चासु कृत्येषु परम् अयनं यस्याः सा।
wordByWord: पञ्चकृत्यपरायणा — meaning follows from the samasa: पञ्चासु कृत्येषु परम् अयनं यस्याः सा।
summary: i.. One who is devoted to the five functions mentioned earlier.
ii. She has much interest and involvement in ail the five tasks.
ii. tt can also be interpreted that all the five functions depend on Her.

iv. The five functions viz. creation, protection, destruction, annihilation, and causing thereappearance of the universe are that of Brahma, Vishnu, Rudra, Ishvaraa and Paramashiva. These five are different forms or Shaktis of that of Sreedevee only.

v. The officials of these five functions carry out their tasks with the permission of Sreedevee only. Its Her order to make them do these functions. Soundaryalahari says that She issues those orders just by twinkling Her eye-brows for a micro second; Jagatsoote Dhata Hari Ravati Rudra: Kshopayati 'Sad@poorva: Sarvam Tadidam Anugrun Haticha Shiva: Tavagyam Alambaya Kshanacholitayo: Bhroolatikayo:\!
searchText: 274 पञ्चकृत्यपरायणा • पञ्चासु कृत्येषु परम् अयनं यस्याः सा। पञ्चासु — in five कृत्येषु — functions; acts परम् — supreme अयनं — abode; course यस्याः — of whom; whose सा — she पञ्चकृत्यपरायणा — meaning follows from the samasa: पञ्चासु कृत्येषु परम् अयनं यस्याः सा। i.. one who is devoted to the five functions mentioned earlier. ii. she has much interest and involvement in ail the five tasks. ii. tt can also be interpreted that all the five functions depend on her. iv. the five functions viz. creation, protection, destruction, annihilation, and causing thereappearance of the universe are that of brahma, vishnu, rudra, ishvaraa and paramashiva. these five are different forms or shaktis of that of sreedevee only. v. the officials of these five functions carry out their tasks with the permission of sreedevee only. its her order to make them do these functions. soundaryalahari says that she issues those orders just by twinkling her eye-brows for a micro second; jagatsoote dhata hari ravati rudra: kshopayati 'sad@poorva: sarvam tadidam anugrun haticha shiva: tavagyam alambaya kshanacholitayo: bhroolatikayo:\! bhāskararāya brings the entire preceding sequence (from nāma 264 to 273) to a grand synthesis: the harmonizer of the five acts: the five operations are सृष्टि (creation), स्थिति (preservation), संहार (dissolution), तिरोधान (concealment), and अनुग्रह (grace). bhāskararāya highlights that she is the singular, non-dual power running through all five deities (brahmā, viṣṇu, rudra, īśvara, and sadāśiva). she remains perpetually absorbed (parāyaṇā) in maintaining this cosmic rhythm.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya brings the entire preceding sequence (from Nāma 264 to 273) to a grand synthesis:

The Harmonizer of the Five Acts: The five operations are सृष्टि (creation), स्थिति (preservation), संहार (dissolution), तिरोधान (concealment), and अनुग्रह (grace). Bhāskararāya highlights that She is the singular, non-dual power running through all five deities (Brahmā, Viṣṇu, Rudra, Īśvara, and Sadāśiva). She remains perpetually absorbed (parāyaṇā) in maintaining this cosmic rhythm.

name: भानुमण्डलमध्यस्था
samasa: • भानोः यत् मण्डलं तस्य मध्ये तिष्ठतीति।
wordByWord: भानुमण्डलमध्यस्था — meaning follows from the samasa: भानोः यत् मण्डलं तस्य मध्ये तिष्ठतीति।
summary: i. One who is in the midst of the solar orbit.
ii. It is a method of worshipping Sreedevee by meditating upon Her in the Sun's disc.
iii. It is said that the golden man seen within the Sun is the Supreme Lord Parameshvara. This name indicates that Sreedevee is in that form.
iv. It is also described that Bhanumandala is the Anahata chakra. Sreedevee is in the form of Kundalini there.
searchText: 275 भानुमण्डलमध्यस्था • भानोः यत् मण्डलं तस्य मध्ये तिष्ठतीति। भानोः — of the sun मण्डलं — disc; orbit भानुमण्डलमध्यस्था — meaning follows from the samasa: भानोः यत् मण्डलं तस्य मध्ये तिष्ठतीति। i. one who is in the midst of the solar orbit. ii. it is a method of worshipping sreedevee by meditating upon her in the sun's disc. iii. it is said that the golden man seen within the sun is the supreme lord parameshvara. this name indicates that sreedevee is in that form. iv. it is also described that bhanumandala is the anahata chakra. sreedevee is in the form of kundalini there. bhāskararāya interprets this across external, internal, and mantric domains: the external solar path: she resides as the innermost luminous deity within the physical sun, matching upanishadic statements about the golden puruṣa seen within the solar orb. the subtle body layer: in kuṇḍalinī yoga, the भानुमण्डल represents the anāhata (heart) center or the region of the internal fire. she is realized as the glowing witness residing in the center of this internal light. the gāyatrī identity: it establishes her non-differentiation from the supreme light invoked in the gāyatrī mantra.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya interprets this across external, internal, and mantric domains:
The External Solar Path: She resides as the innermost luminous deity within the physical sun, matching Upanishadic statements about the golden Puruṣa seen within the solar orb.

The Subtle Body Layer: In Kuṇḍalinī Yoga, the भानुमण्डल represents the Anāhata (heart) center or the region of the internal fire. She is realized as the glowing witness residing in the center of this internal light.
The Gāyatrī Identity: It establishes Her non-differentiation from the supreme light invoked in the Gāyatrī Mantra.

name: भैरवी
wordByWord: भैरवी — meaning follows from the samasa: भैरवस्य पत्नी।
summary: i. The consort of Bhairava.
ii. Since Bhairava does the three functions, he is in terrific form. She is his energy.
iii. It is a natural quality of ladies to get scared. Bhiru means woman. All women taken collectively are called Bhairavam. Since She is in that form, She is Bhairavi.
iv. In the eighth Avarana Devata Tripuramba Chakreshvari mantra, in the middle division, when R is eliminated, it is known as Bhairavi mantra. Sreedevee is in that form.
v. A girl of twelve years old is worshipped in the name of Bhairavi.
searchText: 276 भैरवी • भैरवस्य पत्नी। • भीरूणां (स्त्रीणां) संहतिः। भैरवस्य — of bhairava पत्नी — wife; consort भैरवी — meaning follows from the samasa: भैरवस्य पत्नी। i. the consort of bhairava. ii. since bhairava does the three functions, he is in terrific form. she is his energy. iii. it is a natural quality of ladies to get scared. bhiru means woman. all women taken collectively are called bhairavam. since she is in that form, she is bhairavi. iv. in the eighth avarana devata tripuramba chakreshvari mantra, in the middle division, when r is eliminated, it is known as bhairavi mantra. sreedevee is in that form. v. a girl of twelve years old is worshipped in the name of bhairavi. bhāskararāya unpacks the esoteric roots of the word भैरव: the triad of functions: the syllable भै stands for bharaṇa (sustenance), र stands for ramaṇa (dissolution), and व stands for vamana (creation). thus, bhairava is the absolute controller of the cosmic cycles, and she is his non-dual dynamic power (bhairavī). the terror of transience: to those bound by ego, she appears terrifying because she destroys the illusion of time and mortality. to the wise, she is the protective mother who removes the ultimate fear of death.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya unpacks the esoteric roots of the word भैरव:

The Triad of Functions: The syllable भै stands for bharaṇa (sustenance), र stands for ramaṇa (dissolution), and व stands for vamana (creation). Thus, Bhairava is the absolute controller of the cosmic cycles, and She is His non-dual dynamic power (Bhairavī).

The Terror of Transience: To those bound by ego, She appears terrifying because She destroys the illusion of time and mortality. To the wise, She is the protective mother who removes the ultimate fear of death.

name: भगमालिनी
wordByWord: मलतीति — wears like a garland; possesses
भगमालिनी — meaning follows from the samasa: भगं मलतीति।
summary: {i) One who is in the form of Bhagamalinee. ment, discrimination, dispassion, effort, excellence, fame, glory, liberation, lordliness, magnanimity, power, prosperity, omniscience, righteousness, supremacy, Sun, trying, wisdom,womb, worldly concerns, etc. Sreedevee is possessor of all these qualities and hence Bhagamdlinee.
ii. Conventionally it is the practice, to call those who have the male emblem (Lingd) are the manifestation of Shiva; and those having the female emblem (Bhagd) are the manifestation of Sreedevee.
iv. The thithi Nityé Deveepertaining to the second day of bright lunar half month and the 14"day of dark lunar half month is Bhagamalini.
v. One among the three in the eighth Avarana Devatés.
searchText: 277 भगमालिनी • भगं मलतीति। भगं — bhaga; fortune; splendour; power मलतीति — wears like a garland; possesses भगमालिनी — meaning follows from the samasa: भगं मलतीति। {i) one who is in the form of bhagamalinee. ment, discrimination, dispassion, effort, excellence, fame, glory, liberation, lordliness, magnanimity, power, prosperity, omniscience, righteousness, supremacy, sun, trying, wisdom,womb, worldly concerns, etc. sreedevee is possessor of all these qualities and hence bhagamdlinee. ii. conventionally it is the practice, to call those who have the male emblem (lingd) are the manifestation of shiva; and those having the female emblem (bhagd) are the manifestation of sreedevee. iv. the thithi nityé deveepertaining to the second day of bright lunar half month and the 14"day of dark lunar half month is bhagamalini. v. one among the three in the eighth avarana devatés. bhāskararāya decodes this through classical dictionary definitions (amarakośa) and śrīvidyā ritual architecture: the six divine attributes: भग refers to the six-fold absolute splendors: lordship (aiśvarya), righteousness (dharma), fame (yaśas), wealth (śrī), wisdom (jñāna), and dispassion (vairāgya). she wears these qualities effortlessly like a garland. the nityā deity: in the structural geometry of the śrī cakra, she is the second nityā deity, representing the infinite rays of creative energy emanating from the central bindu.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya decodes this through classical dictionary definitions (Amarakośa) and Śrīvidyā ritual architecture:

The Six Divine Attributes: भग refers to the six-fold absolute splendors: lordship (aiśvarya), righteousness (dharma), fame (yaśas), wealth (śrī), wisdom (jñāna), and dispassion (vairāgya). She wears these qualities effortlessly like a garland.
The Nityā Deity: In the structural geometry of the Śrī Cakra, She is the second Nityā deity, representing the infinite rays of creative energy emanating from the central bindu.

name: पद्मासना
wordByWord: पद्मासना — meaning follows from the samasa: पद्मम् एव आसनं यस्याः सा।
summary: i. One who has lotus flower as Her seat.
ii. Brahma, Sarasvati and Lakshmi have the lotus flower as their seats. Sreedevee is in all these three forms and hence Padmasana.
iii. The lotus flower has nature as leaves, categories as filaments and knowledge as the stalk. Sreedevee has such a flower as Her seat.
iv. Padmasana is a sitting posture as per yoga shastra. She is in that form.
v. She distributes wealth, Padma or Lakshmi, to Her devotees.
vi. Padma may also indicate the demon Surapadma, the slayer of that demon.
vii. Padma may also mean the bindu. She is seated on this.
searchText: 278 पद्मासना • पद्मम् एव आसनं यस्याः सा। • पद्मां (लक्ष्मीं) सनति। • पद्मम् अस्यति। पद्मम् — lotus आसनं — seat यस्याः — of whom; whose सा — she पद्मां — lakshmi; lotus-like prosperity पद्मासना — meaning follows from the samasa: पद्मम् एव आसनं यस्याः सा। i. one who has lotus flower as her seat. ii. brahma, sarasvati and lakshmi have the lotus flower as their seats. sreedevee is in all these three forms and hence padmasana. iii. the lotus flower has nature as leaves, categories as filaments and knowledge as the stalk. sreedevee has such a flower as her seat. iv. padmasana is a sitting posture as per yoga shastra. she is in that form. v. she distributes wealth, padma or lakshmi, to her devotees. vi. padma may also indicate the demon surapadma, the slayer of that demon. vii. padma may also mean the bindu. she is seated on this. bhāskararāya moves seamlessly across these three meanings: yogic absorption: as padmāsanā, she sits in perfect meditative stillness within the lotuses of the subtle body, particularly the heart and the crown. the source of abundance: she is the supreme power that dispenses all forms of worldly and spiritual wealth, controlling the dispensing of lakṣmī-tattva. transcendence of matter: by surpassing the lotus, her unblemished purity remains entirely unaffected by the mud of cosmic manifestation.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya moves seamlessly across these three meanings:
Yogic Absorption: As Padmāsanā, She sits in perfect meditative stillness within the lotuses of the subtle body, particularly the heart and the crown.
The Source of Abundance: She is the supreme power that dispenses all forms of worldly and spiritual wealth, controlling the dispensing of Lakṣmī-tattva.
Transcendence of Matter: By surpassing the lotus, Her unblemished purity remains entirely unaffected by the mud of cosmic manifestation.

name: भगवती
wordByWord: भगवती — meaning follows from the samasa: भगाः अस्यां सन्तीति।
summary: i. One who is with prosperity, that is, the power of Ishvara.
ii. The meanings of the word Bhaga were detailed in the previous names. Sreedevee has all those qualities without any shortage.
iii. As She knows the origin and dissolution, the going and coming of beings, knowledge and ignorance, She is called Bhagavati.

iv. Sree Adi Sankara, in his commentary on Vishnu Sahasranamam for the 558th name Bhagavan, explains Bhaga as complete wealth, charity, fame, liberation, etc.; or one who knows everything about living beings such as birth, death, destruction, movement, knowledge and ignorance.
searchText: 279 भगवती • भगाः अस्यां सन्तीति। भगाः — divine excellences अस्यां — in her सन्तीति — exist भगवती — meaning follows from the samasa: भगाः अस्यां सन्तीति। i. one who is with prosperity, that is, the power of ishvara. ii. the meanings of the word bhaga were detailed in the previous names. sreedevee has all those qualities without any shortage. iii. as she knows the origin and dissolution, the going and coming of beings, knowledge and ignorance, she is called bhagavati. iv. sree adi sankara, in his commentary on vishnu sahasranamam for the 558th name bhagavan, explains bhaga as complete wealth, charity, fame, liberation, etc.; or one who knows everything about living beings such as birth, death, destruction, movement, knowledge and ignorance. bhāskararāya quotes the classical definition from the viṣṇu purāṇa to establish the absolute status of this name: the knower of cosmos: the title bhagavān/bhagavatī belongs strictly to the one who knows the origin and dissolution of beings, the comings and goings of creatures, and the nature of true knowledge and ignorance. she is the repository of these infinite potencies, functioning as the personal, worshipful aspect of the absolute brahman.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya quotes the classical definition from the Viṣṇu Purāṇa to establish the absolute status of this name:

The Knower of Cosmos: The title Bhagavān/Bhagavatī belongs strictly to the one who knows the origin and dissolution of beings, the comings and goings of creatures, and the nature of true knowledge and ignorance. She is the repository of these infinite potencies, functioning as the personal, worshipful aspect of the absolute Brahman.

name: पद्मनाभसहोदरी
wordByWord: पद्मनाभसहोदरी — meaning follows from the samasa: पद्मनाभस्य सहोदरी। '
summary: i. One who is a sister of Vishnu, Padmanabha.

ii. Vishnu created the lotus from his navel and hence is Padmanabha. As per Markandeya Purana, Sreedevee created three pairs of twins as per Her desire: Brahma and Lakshmi in gold colour, Vishnu and Sreedevee in blue colour, and Shiva and Sarasvati in white colour. Their colours indicate the three gunas. As per this mythology, Vishnu and Sreedevee are siblings.
iii. Similarly Brahma Purana says that Parvati, born from the womb of Menaka in a former birth, in the next birth is born as Subhadra, sister of Sreekrishna in the womb of Devaki.
iv. Again we read: Katyayaniti Ya Jata Kamsa Shatroh Sahodari.

v. In another place, Sreedevee along with Her brother Sree Vishnu appeared to Brahma at Kanchi. Sree Bhaskararaya explains in many places that each letter of this Sahasranama is a root letter of a mantra; according to this special interpretation, the three names Padma, Nabha and Sahodari indicate the sacred syllables hidden in this name.
searchText: 280 पद्मनाभसहोदरी • पद्मनाभस्य सहोदरी। ' पद्मनाभस्य — of padmanabha; vishnu सहोदरी — sister पद्मनाभसहोदरी — meaning follows from the samasa: पद्मनाभस्य सहोदरी। ' i. one who is a sister of vishnu, padmanabha. ii. vishnu created the lotus from his navel and hence is padmanabha. as per markandeya purana, sreedevee created three pairs of twins as per her desire: brahma and lakshmi in gold colour, vishnu and sreedevee in blue colour, and shiva and sarasvati in white colour. their colours indicate the three gunas. as per this mythology, vishnu and sreedevee are siblings. iii. similarly brahma purana says that parvati, born from the womb of menaka in a former birth, in the next birth is born as subhadra, sister of sreekrishna in the womb of devaki. iv. again we read: katyayaniti ya jata kamsa shatroh sahodari. v. in another place, sreedevee along with her brother sree vishnu appeared to brahma at kanchi. sree bhaskararaya explains in many places that each letter of this sahasranama is a root letter of a mantra; according to this special interpretation, the three names padma, nabha and sahodari indicate the sacred syllables hidden in this name. bhāskararāya explores this relationship to illustrate cosmic non-duality and shared function: the shared womb of nature: mythologically, she is narāyaṇī, the sister of kṛṣṇa/viṣṇu. esoterically, bhāskararāya points out that they emerge from the exact same supreme matrix of unmanifested causal nature (mūlaprakṛti). equivalence of form and power: viṣṇu represents the pervasive, protective consciousness, while she is the active energy (śakti) that makes that protection effective. they share identical sattva-guṇa properties during the sustenance phase of the universe, demonstrating that the supreme dynamic power and the supreme pervasive consciousness are fundamentally one.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya explores this relationship to illustrate cosmic non-duality and shared function:

The Shared Womb of Nature: Mythologically, She is Narāyaṇī, the sister of Kṛṣṇa/Viṣṇu. Esoterically, Bhāskararāya points out that they emerge from the exact same supreme matrix of unmanifested causal nature (mūlaprakṛti).

Equivalence of Form and Power: Viṣṇu represents the pervasive, protective consciousness, while She is the active energy (śakti) that makes that protection effective. They share identical sattva-guṇa properties during the sustenance phase of the universe, demonstrating that the supreme dynamic power and the supreme pervasive consciousness are fundamentally one.

name: उन्मेषनिमिषोत्पन्नविपन्नभुवनावली
samasa: • उन्मेषनिमिषाभ्यामेव उत्पन्ना विपन्नाश्च भुवनानाम् आवल्यः यस्याः सा।
wordByWord: उन्मेषनिमिषोत्पन्नविपन्नभुवनावली — meaning follows from the samasa: उन्मेषनिमिषाभ्यामेव उत्पन्ना विपन्नाश्च भुवनानाम् आवल्यः यस्याः सा।
summary: i. One in whose opening and closing of eyes the series of worlds come into being and disappear.
ii. Unmesha means opening the eyes and nimesha means closing the eyes. The creation and dissolution of worlds are indicated by these movements.
iii. This also shows that creation and dissolution take place effortlessly by Her mere will.
searchText: 281 उन्मेषनिमिषोत्पन्नविपन्नभुवनावली • उन्मेषनिमिषाभ्यामेव उत्पन्ना विपन्नाश्च भुवनानाम् आवल्यः यस्याः सा। भुवनानाम् — of worlds आवल्यः — series; rows यस्याः — of whom; whose सा — she उन्मेषनिमिषोत्पन्नविपन्नभुवनावली — meaning follows from the samasa: उन्मेषनिमिषाभ्यामेव उत्पन्ना विपन्नाश्च भुवनानाम् आवल्यः यस्याः सा। i. one in whose opening and closing of eyes the series of worlds come into being and disappear. ii. unmesha means opening the eyes and nimesha means closing the eyes. the creation and dissolution of worlds are indicated by these movements. iii. this also shows that creation and dissolution take place effortlessly by her mere will. in the saubhāgyabhāskara, bhāskararāya highlights the absolute ease and sovereignty of her cosmic operations: effortless cosmic cycles: the cosmic projection (sṛṣṭi) and reabsorption (layā) require no labor; they correspond simply to her उन्मेष (opening of eyes/outward manifestation) and निमिष (closing of eyes/inward withdrawal). the scale of time: while these terms imply a split-second blinking to mortals, on a cosmic scale, a single blink spans the entire lifespan of multiple brahmās and cosmic systems, showing her complete transcendence over time.
esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara, Bhāskararāya highlights the absolute ease and sovereignty of Her cosmic operations:

Effortless Cosmic Cycles: The cosmic projection (sṛṣṭi) and reabsorption (layā) require no labor; they correspond simply to Her उन्मेष (opening of eyes/outward manifestation) and निमिष (closing of eyes/inward withdrawal).

The Scale of Time: While these terms imply a split-second blinking to mortals, on a cosmic scale, a single blink spans the entire lifespan of multiple Brahmās and cosmic systems, showing Her complete transcendence over time.

name: सहस्रशीर्षवदना
samasa: • सहस्राणि शीर्षाणि च वदनानि च यस्याः सा।
wordByWord: सहस्रशीर्षवदना — meaning follows from the samasa: सहस्राणि शीर्षाणि च वदनानि च यस्याः सा।
summary: i. One who has thousands of heads and faces.
ii. This indicates Her cosmic form, as described in the Vedic Purusha Suktam.
searchText: 282 सहस्रशीर्षवदना • सहस्राणि शीर्षाणि च वदनानि च यस्याः सा। सहस्राणि — thousands शीर्षाणि — heads वदनानि — faces यस्याः — of whom; whose सा — she सहस्रशीर्षवदना — meaning follows from the samasa: सहस्राणि शीर्षाणि च वदनानि च यस्याः सा। i. one who has thousands of heads and faces. ii. this indicates her cosmic form, as described in the vedic purusha suktam. bhāskararāya reveals her identity with the macrocosmic personage described in classical vedic revelation: the cosmic puruṣa: this directly evokes the puruṣa sūkta ("सहस्रशीर्षा पुरुषः"). bhāskararāya interprets "thousand" not as a mathematical limit, but as an expression of infinity (ananta). since she is the inner self of all living beings, every head that exists belongs to her macrocosmic form (viráṭ-rūpa).
esoteric: Bhāskararāya reveals Her identity with the macrocosmic personage described in classical Vedic revelation:

The Cosmic Puruṣa: This directly evokes the Puruṣa Sūkta ("सहस्रशीर्षा पुरुषः"). Bhāskararāya interprets "thousand" not as a mathematical limit, but as an expression of infinity (ananta). Since She is the inner self of all living beings, every head that exists belongs to Her macrocosmic form (Viráṭ-rūpa).

name: सहस्राक्षी
wordByWord: सहस्राक्षी — meaning follows from the samasa: सहस्राणि अक्षीणि यस्याः सा।
summary: i. One who has thousands of eyes.
ii. This also describes Her cosmic form and all-pervading vision.
searchText: 283 सहस्राक्षी • सहस्राणि अक्षीणि यस्याः सा। सहस्राणि — thousands अक्षीणि — eyes यस्याः — of whom; whose सा — she सहस्राक्षी — meaning follows from the samasa: सहस्राणि अक्षीणि यस्याः सा। i. one who has thousands of eyes. ii. this also describes her cosmic form and all-pervading vision. bhāskararāya expands this past the physical interpretation into the domain of omniscience: the infinite witness: as the ultimate seer (draṣṭā), she witnesses all actions, thoughts, and modifications simultaneously across all planes of existence. every single eye in the universe functions only because it is animated by a fraction of her consciousness.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya expands this past the physical interpretation into the domain of omniscience:

The Infinite Witness: As the ultimate seer (draṣṭā), She witnesses all actions, thoughts, and modifications simultaneously across all planes of existence. Every single eye in the universe functions only because it is animated by a fraction of Her consciousness.

name: सहस्रपात्
wordByWord: सहस्रपात् — meaning follows from the samasa: सहस्राः पादाः यस्याः सा।
summary: i. One who has thousands of feet.
ii. This means She pervades all beings and all worlds.
searchText: 284 सहस्रपात् • सहस्राः पादाः यस्याः सा। पादाः — feet यस्याः — of whom; whose सा — she सहस्रपात् — meaning follows from the samasa: सहस्राः पादाः यस्याः सा। i. one who has thousands of feet. ii. this means she pervades all beings and all worlds. in the saubhāgyabhāskara, this completes the puruṣa sūkta triad of omnipresence: all-pervading motion: because she is the dynamic kinetic energy (kriyā-śakti) that moves the entire universe, all locomotive power stems from her. she walks through all feet, existing everywhere simultaneously, leaving no space unoccupied by her presence.
esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara, this completes the Puruṣa Sūkta triad of omnipresence:

All-Pervading Motion: Because She is the dynamic kinetic energy (Kriyā-śakti) that moves the entire universe, all locomotive power stems from Her. She walks through all feet, existing everywhere simultaneously, leaving no space unoccupied by Her presence.

name: आब्रह्मकीटजननी
wordByWord: आब्रह्मकीटजननी — meaning follows from the samasa: आ ब्रह्मणः कीटपर्यन्तानां जननी।
summary: i. One who is the mot Her of all from Brahmo onwards till a worm.
searchText: 285 आब्रह्मकीटजननी • आ ब्रह्मणः कीटपर्यन्तानां जननी। ब्रह्मणः — from brahma जननी — mother आब्रह्मकीटजननी — meaning follows from the samasa: आ ब्रह्मणः कीटपर्यन्तानां जननी। i. one who is the mot her of all from brahmo onwards till a worm. bhāskararāya highlights her non-discriminating nature as the ultimate matrix: equal source of being: whether it is the exalted consciousness of brahmā or the primitive spark of a microscopic worm, both are born from the same causal womb (mūlaprakṛti). she treats all components of manifestation with the same maternal equilibrium, as they are all her children.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya highlights Her non-discriminating nature as the ultimate matrix:

Equal Source of Being: Whether it is the exalted consciousness of Brahmā or the primitive spark of a microscopic worm, both are born from the same causal womb (mūlaprakṛti). She treats all components of manifestation with the same maternal equilibrium, as they are all Her children.

searchText: 286 वर्णाश्रमविधायिनी • वर्णाश्च आश्रमाश्च तेषां विधायिनी। विधायिनी — ordainer वर्णाश्रमविधायिनी — meaning follows from the samasa: वर्णाश्च आश्रमाश्च तेषां विधायिनी। i. one who established the social divisions and castes. {ii) after creating all from brohma to worms,she created the vedas, which are her own commands, in order to lead the created beings into the right path, the vedas are divided into two parts, the karma kando and gnaenokénda; as according to therule, the devos and lower animals have no share in the karmakgnda, the (divine) mot her established righteousness (dharma) through karmakénda after dividing human beings into four castes and four orders. universe having heard his words, remembering her husband (shivo) answered her fat her with a smile. oh lord of mountains, hear from me t his doctrine, which is supreme, secret, known to eshvara only, practiced by brahmavadins alone... describing the means of attaining... by meditation, korma yoga, devotion, and wisdom, oh lord of 'ot her actions (karmds); thus concluding again she says in order to describe karma yoga..karmé is ordained by srutis and smrtis according to castes and orders; always perform t his actionassociated with the knowledge of the self, for the sake of liberation. devotion arises from righteousness (dharma), by devotion, the supreme is attained, 'srutismrutyuktam samyak karma varndsra matmakom\ adyatma gana sahitam muktaye sotatam kurull darmétsanjayate bhakti: bhaktydsangydyate param\ srutismeutibhyam utitodharmo yégyadiko mata:|i nanyatogyayatedharma: vedatdharmohi nippapoul tasman mumukshurdharmérttam matroopam vedamashrayetl! esoteric: Bhāskararāya explains Her role as the foundational lawmaker of cosmic and social order:

The Basis of Dharma: The structure of society and spiritual growth is not an accidental human invention. She establishes these systems to ensure the gradual evolution of individual souls (jīvas) by aligning their duties with their inherent qualities (guṇa and karma).

name: निजाज्ञारूपनिगमा
samasa: • याः निजां (स्वीयाम्) आज्ञां रूपयन्ति तादृशाः निगमा यस्याः सा।
wordByWord: निजाज्ञारूपनिगमा — meaning follows from the samasa: याः निजां (स्वीयाम्) आज्ञां रूपयन्ति तादृशाः निगमा यस्याः सा।

One who has the Vedas as the expressions of Her commands. j =) icin: ae {iy Veda statements command human beings with the do's and don'ts in t His life what set forth the object to be attained, the means and the practical directions. These commands limit the social divisions and castes. The Commands of the Empress: The Vedas (Nigama) are not mere suggestions; they represent the absolute royal decree (ājñā) of the supreme sovereign. To follow Vedic injunctions is to align with Her divine will, while to stray from them is to step outside Her cosmic harmony.

name: पुण्यापुण्यफलप्रदा
samasa: • पुण्यस्य अपुण्यस्य च फले प्रकरषेण ददातीति।
wordByWord: पुण्यापुण्यफलप्रदा — meaning follows from the samasa: पुण्यस्य अपुण्यस्य च फले प्रकरषेण ददातीति।
summary: i. One who is the dispenser of fruits of righteous as well as evil actions (sins).

ii. As mentioned in the previous name, Sreedevee decides the fruits of the good and bad results arising from ordained and prohibited actions respectively. (ili) It is mentioned as Punya (righteous) and Apunya (unrighteous) and not as Punya (righteous) and Papa (sins). Unrighteous is something looking like righteous. It is quiet natural to do the unrighteous deeds thinking as righteous on account of ignorance or ego. Sreedevee directly confers the results of righteous and unrighteous actions.
searchText: 288 पुण्यापुण्यफलप्रदा • पुण्यस्य अपुण्यस्य च फले प्रकरषेण ददातीति। पुण्यस्य — of merit अपुण्यस्य — of demerit फले — fruits ददातीति — gives पुण्यापुण्यफलप्रदा — meaning follows from the samasa: पुण्यस्य अपुण्यस्य च फले प्रकरषेण ददातीति। i. one who is the dispenser of fruits of righteous as well as evil actions (sins). ii. as mentioned in the previous name, sreedevee decides the fruits of the good and bad results arising from ordained and prohibited actions respectively. (ili) it is mentioned as punya (righteous) and apunya (unrighteous) and not as punya (righteous) and papa (sins). unrighteous is something looking like righteous. it is quiet natural to do the unrighteous deeds thinking as righteous on account of ignorance or ego. sreedevee directly confers the results of righteous and unrighteous actions. in the saubhāgyabhāskara, bhāskararāya removes the concept of an arbitrary, punishing deity: the law of karma: she operates as the neutral cosmic intelligence that returns the exact fruits of actions to the doer. she bestows joy for puṇya and corrective suffering for apuṇya, ensuring the perfect equilibrium of the cosmic moral law.
esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara, Bhāskararāya removes the concept of an arbitrary, punishing deity:

The Law of Karma: She operates as the neutral cosmic intelligence that returns the exact fruits of actions to the doer. She bestows joy for puṇya and corrective suffering for apuṇya, ensuring the perfect equilibrium of the cosmic moral law.

name: श्रुतिसीमन्तसिन्धूरीकृतपादाब्जधूलिका

श्रुतिसीमन्तसिन्धूरीकृतपादाब्जधूलिका — meaning follows from the samasa: श्रुतीनां सीमन्ताः सिन्धूरवत् (तदन्तर्गतरहस्यार्थवत्) कृता पादाब्जयोः धूलिः यया सा।
summary: i. One whose dust of the lotus feet is the vermilion on the parting line of the Vedas.
ii. The Vedas are compared to women who wear the dust of Her lotus feet as sindura on their heads. This indicates that the highest secret meaning of the Vedas culminates at Her feet.
searchText: 289 श्रुतिसीमन्तसिन्धूरीकृतपादाब्जधूलिका • श्रुतीनां सीमन्ताः सिन्धूरवत् (तदन्तर्गतरहस्यार्थवत्) कृता पादाब्जयोः धूलिः यया सा। सीमन्ताः — parting of the hair; crest-line सिन्धूरवत् — like vermilion पादाब्जयोः — of the lotus feet धूलिः — dust सा — she श्रुतिसीमन्तसिन्धूरीकृतपादाब्जधूलिका — meaning follows from the samasa: श्रुतीनां सीमन्ताः सिन्धूरवत् (तदन्तर्गतरहस्यार्थवत्) कृता पादाब्जयोः धूलिः यया सा। i. one whose dust of the lotus feet is the vermilion on the parting line of the vedas. ii. the vedas are compared to women who wear the dust of her lotus feet as sindura on their heads. this indicates that the highest secret meaning of the vedas culminates at her feet. bhāskararāya illuminates the profound status of her feet relative to vedic revelation: the crown of knowledge: the śrutis (vedas) are personified as divine women. the highest part of the head is the parting of the hair (sīmanta), representing the upaniṣads (vedānta). the dust of her feet rests upon this peak, meaning that the highest heights of vedic knowledge are merely dust particles underneath her feet.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya illuminates the profound status of Her feet relative to Vedic revelation:

The Crown of Knowledge: The Śrutis (Vedas) are personified as divine women. The highest part of the head is the parting of the hair (sīmanta), representing the Upaniṣads (Vedānta). The dust of Her feet rests upon this peak, meaning that the highest heights of Vedic knowledge are merely dust particles underneath Her feet.

name: सकलागमसन्दोहशुक्तिसम्पुटमौक्तिका
samasa: • सकलाश्च ते आगमाश्च तेषां सन्दोहाः एव शुक्तयः ताभिः सम्पुटं मौक्तिकं यस्याः सा।
wordByWord: सकलागमसन्दोहशुक्तिसम्पुटमौक्तिका — meaning follows from the samasa: सकलाश्च ते आगमाश्च तेषां सन्दोहाः एव शुक्तयः ताभिः सम्पुटं मौक्तिकं यस्याः सा।
summary: i. One who is the pearl enclosed in the shell-caskets formed by the collections of all Agamas.
ii. All Agamas are compared to shells; their inner pearl is Sreedevee.
searchText: 290 सकलागमसन्दोहशुक्तिसम्पुटमौक्तिका • सकलाश्च ते आगमाश्च तेषां सन्दोहाः एव शुक्तयः ताभिः सम्पुटं मौक्तिकं यस्याः सा। सन्दोहाः — collections शुक्तयः — shells यस्याः — of whom; whose सा — she सकलागमसन्दोहशुक्तिसम्पुटमौक्तिका — meaning follows from the samasa: सकलाश्च ते आगमाश्च तेषां सन्दोहाः एव शुक्तयः ताभिः सम्पुटं मौक्तिकं यस्याः सा। i. one who is the pearl enclosed in the shell-caskets formed by the collections of all agamas. ii. all agamas are compared to shells; their inner pearl is sreedevee. bhāskararāya highlights her position within the agamic and tantric traditions: the hidden essence: just as a pearl is hidden away, safe inside a closed oyster shell, the true, esoteric nature of the devi is hidden inside the complex rituals and structures of the śāktāgamas. the texts are merely the shell container; she is the priceless pearl found within them.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya highlights Her position within the Agamic and Tantric traditions:

The Hidden Essence: Just as a pearl is hidden away, safe inside a closed oyster shell, the true, esoteric nature of the Devi is hidden inside the complex rituals and structures of the Śāktāgamas. The texts are merely the shell container; She is the priceless pearl found within them.

name: पुरुषार्थप्रदा
wordByWord: पुरुषार्थप्रदा — meaning follows from the samasa: पुरुषार्थान् प्रकर्षेण ददातीति।
summary: () One who bestows fourfold values of human desire, {i) The four objects of human desire are religious virtue, wealth, bliss and liberation, These are called Purush Graths, A human being can get all these four only by the blessings of Sreedevee.
ii. The worship of the unconditioned Brahman and the results thereof is with the blessings of Sreedevee's alone.
searchText: 291 पुरुषार्थप्रदा • पुरुषार्थान् प्रकर्षेण ददातीति। पुरुषार्थान् — human aims प्रकर्षेण — excellently; completely ददातीति — gives पुरुषार्थप्रदा — meaning follows from the samasa: पुरुषार्थान् प्रकर्षेण ददातीति। () one who bestows fourfold values of human desire, {i) the four objects of human desire are religious virtue, wealth, bliss and liberation, these are called purush graths, a human being can get all these four only by the blessings of sreedevee. ii. the worship of the unconditioned brahman and the results thereof is with the blessings of sreedevee's alone. bhāskararāya explains her capacity to fulfill every layer of human aspiration: the giver of the four aims: she fulfills all four human endeavors: धर्म (righteousness), अर्थ (wealth), काम (desire), and मोक्ष (liberation). she does not simply grant spiritual goals while leaving the practitioner poor, nor does she trap them in material wealth; she provides a balanced distribution of all four.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya explains Her capacity to fulfill every layer of human aspiration:

The Giver of the Four Aims: She fulfills all four human endeavors: धर्म (righteousness), अर्थ (wealth), काम (desire), and मोक्ष (liberation). She does not simply grant spiritual goals while leaving the practitioner poor, nor does She trap them in material wealth; She provides a balanced distribution of all four.

name: पूर्णा
wordByWord: पूर्णा — meaning follows from the samasa: पूर्णा।
summary: i. One who is the all encompassing whole,.
ii. She is free from the limitation of time, place and circumstance. Hence Sreedevee, in the form of Brahman, is fully omnipresent in everything and everywhere.
ii. Poorgé means the fifth, tenth and fifteenth Nitya deities. Thefourteenth bright night is also called Poorna. {iv) Poorga is also the name of a sacred river.
searchText: 292 पूर्णा • पूर्णा। पूर्णा — full; complete पूर्णा — meaning follows from the samasa: पूर्णा। i. one who is the all encompassing whole,. ii. she is free from the limitation of time, place and circumstance. hence sreedevee, in the form of brahman, is fully omnipresent in everything and everywhere. ii. poorgé means the fifth, tenth and fifteenth nitya deities. thefourteenth bright night is also called poorna. {iv) poorga is also the name of a sacred river. bhāskararāya connects this directly to the famous bṛhadāraṇyaka peace invocation ("पूर्णमदः पूर्णमिदम्"): the indivisible absolute: she lacks nothing, contains everything, and is completely self-contained. because she is pūrṇā, no addition can increase her and no subtraction can diminish her. she remains the absolute, non-dual reality behind all changing phenomena.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya connects this directly to the famous Bṛhadāraṇyaka peace invocation ("पूर्णमदः पूर्णमिदम्"):

The Indivisible Absolute: She lacks nothing, contains everything, and is completely self-contained. Because She is Pūrṇā, no addition can increase Her and no subtraction can diminish Her. She remains the absolute, non-dual reality behind all changing phenomena.

name: भोगिनी

summary: i. One who is the enjoyer.
i. Bhoga means bliss. She enjoys it. She is the bliss and the enjoyer as well. Veda says Annom Cha Brahma Aham Cha Brahma.
iii. The word Bhogé means the body of a snake. Sreedevee is in the form Kundalini in energy in the human body as a female snake.
iv. Bhoginee is the name of a female serpent (Nagakanya).
searchText: 293 भोगिनी • भोगः अस्याः अस्तीति। भोगः — enjoyment भोगिनी — meaning follows from the samasa: भोगः अस्याः अस्तीति। i. one who is the enjoyer. i. bhoga means bliss. she enjoys it. she is the bliss and the enjoyer as well. veda says annom cha brahma aham cha brahma. iii. the word bhogé means the body of a snake. sreedevee is in the form kundalini in energy in the human body as a female snake. iv. bhoginee is the name of a female serpent (nagakanya). bhāskararāya highlights a unique element of śrīvidyā doctrine: the cosmic enjoyer: unlike systems that reject the world as evil, here the devi is bhoginī—she who experiences the joy of her own creation. she is the collection of all pleasures across all planes, experiencing them through the senses of all living beings, while remaining internally unattached.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya highlights a unique element of Śrīvidyā doctrine:

The Cosmic Enjoyer: Unlike systems that reject the world as evil, here the Devi is Bhoginī—She who experiences the joy of Her own creation. She is the collection of all pleasures across all planes, experiencing them through the senses of all living beings, while remaining internally unattached.

(ili) the word bhuvana has many meanings - earth, heaven, water, etc. she is the head of all these. fiv) the mayébeeja, hreem is called bhuvoneshvaree beeja. she is the presiding deity of this. the letter 'ha' indicates the sky, 'r' indicates fire and 'e' indicates water. since she only created all these and originated t his world, she is called bhvaneshoree. v. the word hreem, has many meanings like hrullekd beejo, lajjd beeja, chintémani beeja, etc. vi. there are three divisions viz., divya, sidda and manvaugha in the lineage of teachers. inthis, in the ménvaugha division there was a teac her by name bhuvondnandandtha. as per tripurasiddhénta, since she appeared before and blessed him, she got the name bhuvoneshvaree. bhuvan gnando nathasya prasannatvat maheshvoreel bhuvaneshvoreetivikhyata shambhavee bhuvaneshvareel| bhāskararāya interprets this name through both cosmic lordship and structural mantric seed forms: sovereignty over the fourteen worlds: she is the supreme queen governing all fourteen realms of manifestation. the bīja identity: in the śrīvidyā tradition, she is the physical personification of the sacred ह्रीं (hrīṁ) bīja, known as the bhuvaneśvarī mantra, which sustains the structural matrix of space.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya interprets this name through both cosmic lordship and structural mantric seed forms:
Sovereignty over the Fourteen Worlds: She is the supreme queen governing all fourteen realms of manifestation.
The Bīja Identity: In the Śrīvidyā tradition, She is the physical personification of the sacred ह्रीं (Hrīṁ) bīja, known as the Bhuvaneśvarī Mantra, which sustains the structural matrix of space.

name: अम्बिका

summary: (2) One who is a great mother.
ii. Amma, Ambha and Ambikd are the words used to call a mot Her with affection. Since Sreedevee is the mot Her of t His entire universe, She is Ambika.

iii. The word Ambika means Jaganmdtha (mot Her of the world), Bh Gratee (the goddess of speech), and earth; also, Ambiké - form of Rudrani - the aggregate of three shaktis - iccha(desire), gnéna (knowledge) and kriyd (action). Sreedevee is in these forms.

iv. Ambiké means night or sleep. Sreedevee in the form of night and sleep, as a mot Her hugs with affection and gives compulsory rest and peace. Only because of t His rest and sleep, Vishnu took the incarnation as HayagreevG and through him we got t His Sahasranéma, \t can be compared to Devee Mahdtmyd verse - Ya Devee Sarva Bhooteshu Nidra Roopena Samsthita.
searchText: 295 अम्बिका • अम्बा एव अम्बिका। अम्बा — mother अम्बिका — meaning follows from the samasa: अम्बा एव अम्बिका। (2) one who is a great mother. ii. amma, ambha and ambikd are the words used to call a mot her with affection. since sreedevee is the mot her of t his entire universe, she is ambika. iii. the word ambika means jaganmdtha (mot her of the world), bh gratee (the goddess of speech), and earth; also, ambiké - form of rudrani - the aggregate of three shaktis - iccha(desire), gnéna (knowledge) and kriyd (action). sreedevee is in these forms. iv. ambiké means night or sleep. sreedevee in the form of night and sleep, as a mot her hugs with affection and gives compulsory rest and peace. only because of t his rest and sleep, vishnu took the incarnation as hayagreevg and through him we got t his sahasranéma, \t can be compared to devee mahdtmyd verse - ya devee sarva bhooteshu nidra roopena samsthita. bhāskararāya explains that this name emphasizes her accessible, compassionate aspect: the ultimate source of nourishment: beyond her roles as an infinite ruler or an absolute cosmic pearl, she is fundamentally the loving mother of every soul. bhāskararāya notes that ambikā also represents the integration of the three foundational powers: icchā (will), jñāna (knowledge), and kriyā (action) working to protect creation.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya explains that this name emphasizes Her accessible, compassionate aspect:

The Ultimate Source of Nourishment: Beyond Her roles as an infinite ruler or an absolute cosmic pearl, She is fundamentally the loving mother of every soul. Bhāskararāya notes that Ambikā also represents the integration of the three foundational powers: Icchā (will), Jñāna (knowledge), and Kriyā (action) working to protect creation.

name: अनादिनिधना
samasa: • आदिः निधनं च न विद्यते यस्याः सा।
wordByWord: अनादिनिधना — meaning follows from the samasa: आदिः निधनं च न विद्यते यस्याः सा।
summary: i. One who exists without a beginning or an end.

ii. The Supreme Being has neit Her a beginning nor an end. On the 'ot Her hand, the human being begins in the middle and ends in the middle. THis name indicates a difference between humans and Sreedevee. THis difference' itself helps to deduce Her. | am with a aa _EE ee beginning and end,Sreedevee should be without these two. (is) The word Nidhané indicates destruction. Anything that obstructs worshipping Sreedevee is called Nidhana. According to the systems of Vararuchi's Katapayédi method-of number notation, the word Adi means eighty, (do = 8, and na or a = 0); hence she whose worship delivers 2 man from the eighty causes of death, These eighty are divided into two as 52 Pasa and 28 Vadha, {iv) The above Vadha, which is of three kinds; ashakti, Tushti and siddhi is the first obstacle to perfection. Ashakti is inability of the senses to receive their respective objects, defects caused by blindness, deafness, etc.; they are eleven because there are eleven senses - five sense organs, five organs for actions and mind.

v. Tushti (elation) is @ misleading form of happiness. An illusion that a man thinks -1 am liberated by the absorption of mind, when he is far off from it (liberation is the final destination of worship) attached to this, it is impossible to come out. THis is called Prakruti elation. 2. The second is - when a man thinks that he has succeeded" 'on merely wearing the robes of an ascetic, etc. THis is called updddna elation. 3. The third Is when one says, what is the use of painful actions like meditation? Success will come with time; t His is called kala elation. 4, The fourth one is when one says, success will come by the intervention of Sreedevee; t His is called bhdgya (luck elation). 5-9. Trying to reach Her thorough the five sense organs and a kind of elation arises when after observing the disadvantages attached to the gratification of objectives senses, he ceases their pursuit. THis is called Indreeya Thushti (organic elation). Thus are the nine elations.

li)In the same waythere are eight kinds of siddhis. In the beginning of Worship, the devotee gets some powers automatically; 1. Ooha (intuition) means understanding the meanings without being taught, by intuition. 2, Shabda (hearing), understanding the meaning by accidentally hearing the words. 3. Adhyayana (study), under Standing the real meaning from the teaching of a teac Her and thinking over it. 4.8y having friendship with those who has under Stood Sreedevee's philosophy. THis is Suhruda. 5. Sushrusha is to get an opportunity to serve the teacher, ascetics and the learned. 6 to ee Esty 8. The ot Her three sidd His are the reverse of the three kinds of misery, concerninghimself, concerning Devas, and concerning the elements (adhyatma, adhibhoota and ddhidaivata). By gaining good friends, he attainsarthasiddhi. Thus there are twenty-eight kinds of 'obstructions for the adoration of Sreedevee. Since Sheensures that the devotees are not affected by these, She is Anddinidhana, {vili) A pasa is the one which creates obstruction in thought process and make the yoga impossible to get. name: हरिब्रह्मेन्द्रसेविता

wordByWord: हरिब्रह्मेन्द्रसेविता — meaning follows from the samasa: हरिश्च ब्रह्म च इन्द्रश्च तैः सेविता।
summary: ())_ One who is adored by Hari, Brahmaandindro.

ii. In the Sreecakra (nagara), the Chintdmanigraha is the dwelling place of Sreedevee. Around this, between the seventeenth and the eighteenth walls, is the seat of Vishnu; between the sixteenth and the seventeenth, is of Brahma; between the fourteenth and the fifteenth, is of Indra and ot Her digodlas. They all always meditate upon Sreedevee.

ii. There is an opinion that though they are Hari, Brahma, Indr& and others, since they adore Sreedevee, Her devotees need not worship them. name: नादरूपा
wordByWord: नादरूपा — meaning follows from the samasa: नाद एव रूपं यस्याः सा।
summary: i. One who is in the form of sound.

ii. There are four stages in the formation of sound viz., 1. Para which finds manifestation only in Prana, 2. Pashyanti which finds manifestation in the mind, 3. Madhyama which finds manifestation in the organs, and 4. Vaikharee which finds manifestation in articulate expression. All these four are found as names of Sreedevee in t His Sahasranama. a ee The last stage Vaikharee is the form which we hear as sound. There are nine notes (nadds) between the third stage Madhyomo and the fourth Voikharee; Avikrutam, Sconyam, Sparsam, Natham, Dvani, Bindu, Shakti, Beejam and Aksharam. These are Samskrit letters A,Ka, Cha, To, Tha, Pa, ya, Sha and La. As a cause ndtham is spread in all these, That natham is Sreedevee herself. {ili)In t His sound, in the beeja like Hreem, the last half letter 'M" sounds: in an extended way. There are eight notes (varnas) above the bindu of the syllable hreem,etc., such as ardhacandra, rodhini, nétha, nadénta, shokti, vy Gpiké,samand and unmagi. Each of these will have different light forms and colours. These are clearly explained in Vorivasyé Rahasya by Sree Bhdskara Rayar. THis name says that Sreedevee is in the form of third one Natha in these eight. Natham is in the form of suture with the luminosity like the red precious stone Zircon (Padparadscha - Pushporaga).
