esoteric: Bhāskararāya traces this name through the esoteric layers of phonic manifestation (Vāk):

Primal Vibration: Before the universe manifests as physical matter, it exists as a subtle, undifferentiated cosmic resonance known as अनाहत नाद (Anāhata Nāda). She is this primary vibration, the source of all mantras, syllables, and languages, moving from the deepest Parā state into audible speech.

name: नामरूपविवर्जिता
wordByWord: नामरूपविवर्जिता — meaning follows from the samasa: नामरूपाभ्यां विवर्जिता।
summary: i. One who has neither name nor form.
ii. Everything in this world is identified only by name and form. She is without both. She cannot be indicated by name or form.

iii. Everything created in this universe has identification in five forms: existence, knowledge, bliss or affection, name and form. The first three indicate the relationship of Chit with the world and are called Chit-granthi. The latter two, name and form, are illusory and are called Achit-granthi.

iv. Asti is existence; the essence of Brahman reflects in the feeling "I exist". Pati is knowledge; objects are understood because they reflect in consciousness. Priyam is bliss or affection; all activity happens because of love or affection. Nama is name and Rupa is form.
v. Only Brahman is beyond name and form. Hence Sreedevee is beyond name and form.
searchText: 300 नामरूपविवर्जिता • नामरूपाभ्यां विवर्जिता। विवर्जिता — free from; devoid of नामरूपविवर्जिता — meaning follows from the samasa: नामरूपाभ्यां विवर्जिता। i. one who has neither name nor form. ii. everything in this world is identified only by name and form. she is without both. she cannot be indicated by name or form. iii. everything created in this universe has identification in five forms: existence, knowledge, bliss or affection, name and form. the first three indicate the relationship of chit with the world and are called chit-granthi. the latter two, name and form, are illusory and are called achit-granthi. iv. asti is existence; the essence of brahman reflects in the feeling "i exist". pati is knowledge; objects are understood because they reflect in consciousness. priyam is bliss or affection; all activity happens because of love or affection. nama is name and rupa is form. v. only brahman is beyond name and form. hence sreedevee is beyond name and form. bhāskararāya highlights the profound, non-dual paradox constructed by placing nāma 299 and 300 right next to each other: the transcendent absolute: immediately after stating that she is sound (nādarūpā), the text states she is nāmarūpavivarjitā. bhāskararāya explains that while she assumes all names and forms for the sake of the devotee's worship, her true, essential nature (svarūpa) is the attribute-less, unconditioned brahman (nirguṇa), completely untouched by any mental definitions or physical shapes.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya highlights the profound, non-dual paradox constructed by placing Nāma 299 and 300 right next to each other:

The Transcendent Absolute: Immediately after stating that She is sound (Nādarūpā), the text states She is Nāmarūpavivarjitā. Bhāskararāya explains that while She assumes all names and forms for the sake of the devotee's worship, Her true, essential nature (Svarūpa) is the attribute-less, unconditioned Brahman (Nirguṇa), completely untouched by any mental definitions or physical shapes.

name: ह्रींकारी
wordByWord: ह्रींकारी — meaning follows from the samasa: ह्रीं करोतीति।
summary: i. One who is in the form of Hrim bija letters.
ii. Hri means modesty, shyness and bashfulness. One who does this is Hrimkari. In Saptashati it is mentioned that we bow to Sreedevee, who is in the form of bashfulness in all souls.
iii. In Swatantra Tantra it is mentioned that since Sreedevee takes care of creation, protection and destruction tasks, She is called Hrimkari.

iv. Hrim itself is one of the names of Sreedevee, the 99th name in Lalita Trishati also. Sree Adi Sankara mentions that all energies are attributes of Sreedevee; by disconnecting them, we get Her liberated form.
v. This is the maya-bija, also called Bhuvaneshvari bija.
vi. This bija is in the Panchadashi mantra at the end of every division. The letters Ka and Ha correspond to Shiva, and the letters A, E, La and Sa correspond to Shakti.
vii. Om in general is called Pranavam. This is also called Shakta Pranavam.

viii. Lalita Trishati has three hundred names, twenty for each letter of the Panchadashi mantra; sixty names correspond to Hrim. The detailed meanings can be seen in the commentary of Sree Adi Sankara.
searchText: 301 ह्रींकारी • ह्रीं करोतीति। ह्रीं — hrim seed-syllable करोतीति — makes; manifests ह्रींकारी — meaning follows from the samasa: ह्रीं करोतीति। i. one who is in the form of hrim bija letters. ii. hri means modesty, shyness and bashfulness. one who does this is hrimkari. in saptashati it is mentioned that we bow to sreedevee, who is in the form of bashfulness in all souls. iii. in swatantra tantra it is mentioned that since sreedevee takes care of creation, protection and destruction tasks, she is called hrimkari. iv. hrim itself is one of the names of sreedevee, the 99th name in lalita trishati also. sree adi sankara mentions that all energies are attributes of sreedevee; by disconnecting them, we get her liberated form. v. this is the maya-bija, also called bhuvaneshvari bija. vi. this bija is in the panchadashi mantra at the end of every division. the letters ka and ha correspond to shiva, and the letters a, e, la and sa correspond to shakti. vii. om in general is called pranavam. this is also called shakta pranavam. viii. lalita trishati has three hundred names, twenty for each letter of the panchadashi mantra; sixty names correspond to hrim. the detailed meanings can be seen in the commentary of sree adi sankara. in the saubhāgyabhāskara, bhāskararāya unpacks the immense ontological weight of this bīja within śrīvidyā theology: the cosmic sound form: ह्रीं is the supreme lajjā-bīja (syllable of cosmic modesty/containment). bhāskararāya details that she is both the source and the ultimate reality indicated by this syllable. it represents the unmanifested consciousness containing the seeds of the entire universe, ready to project outward. the power of manifestation: as hrīṁkārī, she animates the primal vibration that marks the boundary line between the absolute unmanifested brahman (nirguṇa) and the manifested, qualified universe (saguṇa).
esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara, Bhāskararāya unpacks the immense ontological weight of this bīja within Śrīvidyā theology:

The Cosmic Sound Form: ह्रीं is the supreme Lajjā-bīja (syllable of cosmic modesty/containment). Bhāskararāya details that She is both the source and the ultimate reality indicated by this syllable. It represents the unmanifested consciousness containing the seeds of the entire universe, ready to project outward.

The Power of Manifestation: As Hrīṁkārī, She animates the primal vibration that marks the boundary line between the absolute unmanifested Brahman (Nirguṇa) and the manifested, qualified universe (Saguṇa).

name: हृद्या
wordByWord: हृद्या — meaning follows from the samasa: हृदि भवा।
summary: {)) One who abides in the heart.
i. She lives in the heart of ascetics/ devotees. {ii) t can also be interpreted that She is delightful or pleasant to the mind.
searchText: 303 हृद्या • हृदि भवा। हृदि — in the heart भवा — dwelling; being हृद्या — meaning follows from the samasa: हृदि भवा। {)) one who abides in the heart. i. she lives in the heart of ascetics/ devotees. {ii) t can also be interpreted that she is delightful or pleasant to the mind. bhāskararāya aligns this directly with upanishadic meditation models: the heart substratum: the true spiritual heart (hṛdaya) is not the physical organ, but the deep cavernous space of pure consciousness (daharākāśa). she is the eternal light residing at the center of this cave, accessible through internal meditation (antarmukha-kalanā).
esoteric: Bhāskararāya aligns this directly with Upanishadic meditation models:

The Heart Substratum: The true spiritual heart (Hṛdaya) is not the physical organ, but the deep cavernous space of pure consciousness (Daharākāśa). She is the eternal light residing at the center of this cave, accessible through internal meditation (Antarmukha-kalanā).

name: हेयोपादेयवर्जिता
samasa: • हातुम् उपादातुं च योग्ये हेयोपादेये। ताभ्यां वर्जिता।
wordByWord: हेयोपादेये — what is to be rejected and accepted
हेयोपादेयवर्जिता — meaning follows from the samasa: हातुम् उपादातुं च योग्ये हेयोपादेये। ताभ्यां वर्जिता।
summary: {i) One who has nothing to regret or accept.

i. Because the scriptures, which describe injunction and prohibition are confined to the ignorant ones. Since Sreedevee Is in a form of knowledge, She has no relationship to these. Ui) Geyam - to abstain since people do not have interest. Upadeyam - to accept since people are interested, Sreedevee has no interest or disinterest or aversion, Hence She does not have anything to accept or reject or regret, Hence Heyopddeyavarjita. (One who was adored by Rajaraja.
i. Rajaraja indicates 12 - presiding deities of various mantras, starting from Manu, Moon, etc. THis name is due to worshipping of Sreedevee by them.

ii. 1t can also be'interpreted that She is in the form of different Sree,. Vidvé types taught by them. \W) tts described in Lalité Stavaratnam by sage Sree Durvasar - The ae ee 3a Chintémanigraho, in the Sree Chakra (nagara - city), is the dwelling place of Sreedevee. Here in between 14" and 15" walls, Kuberd is seated with His associates like Manibhadré and adore Her. Thus She 'was worshipped by Kubero.
searchText: 304 हेयोपादेयवर्जिता • हातुम् उपादातुं च योग्ये हेयोपादेये। ताभ्यां वर्जिता। हेयोपादेये — what is to be rejected and accepted वर्जिता — free from हेयोपादेयवर्जिता — meaning follows from the samasa: हातुम् उपादातुं च योग्ये हेयोपादेये। ताभ्यां वर्जिता। {i) one who has nothing to regret or accept. i. because the scriptures, which describe injunction and prohibition are confined to the ignorant ones. since sreedevee is in a form of knowledge, she has no relationship to these. ui) geyam - to abstain since people do not have interest. upadeyam - to accept since people are interested, sreedevee has no interest or disinterest or aversion, hence she does not have anything to accept or reject or regret, hence heyopddeyavarjita. (one who was adored by rajaraja. i. rajaraja indicates 12 - presiding deities of various mantras, starting from manu, moon, etc. this name is due to worshipping of sreedevee by them. ii. 1t can also be'interpreted that she is in the form of different sree,. vidvé types taught by them. \w) tts described in lalité stavaratnam by sage sree durvasar - the ae ee 3a chintémanigraho, in the sree chakra (nagara - city), is the dwelling place of sreedevee. here in between 14" and 15" walls, kuberd is seated with his associates like manibhadré and adore her. thus she 'was worshipped by kubero. in the saubhāgyabhāskara, bhāskararāya uses this to declare her absolute non-dual status: beyond dual attraction and repulsion: rejection (heya) and acceptance (upādeya) exist only where there is a second object distinct from the self. because she is the all-pervading, non-dual absolute reality (advaita-brahman), there is nothing outside of her to acquire and nothing within her to cast away. she remains eternally content in her own fullness (pūrṇatva).
esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara, Bhāskararāya uses this to declare Her absolute non-dual status:

Beyond Dual Attraction and Repulsion: Rejection (heya) and acceptance (upādeya) exist only where there is a second object distinct from the self. Because She is the all-pervading, non-dual absolute reality (Advaita-Brahman), there is nothing outside of Her to acquire and nothing within Her to cast away. She remains eternally content in Her own fullness (Pūrṇatva).

name: राजराजार्चिता
wordByWord: राजराजार्चिता — meaning follows from the samasa: राज्ञां राज्ञा अर्चिता।
summary: i. One who was adored by Rajaraja.
ii. Rajaraja indicates twelve presiding deities of various mantras, starting from Manu, Moon, etc. This name is due to the worship of Sreedevee by them.
iii. It can also be interpreted that She is in the form of different Sree Vidya types taught by them.

iv. It is described in Lalita Stavaratnam by sage Sree Durvasa that in the Chintamani-griha, in the Sree Chakra city, Kubera is seated with his associates like Manibhadra and adores Her. Thus She was worshipped by Kubera.
searchText: 305 राजराजार्चिता • राज्ञां राज्ञा अर्चिता। राज्ञां — of kings राज्ञा — by the king अर्चिता — worshipped राजराजार्चिता — meaning follows from the samasa: राज्ञां राज्ञा अर्चिता। i. one who was adored by rajaraja. ii. rajaraja indicates twelve presiding deities of various mantras, starting from manu, moon, etc. this name is due to the worship of sreedevee by them. iii. it can also be interpreted that she is in the form of different sree vidya types taught by them. iv. it is described in lalita stavaratnam by sage sree durvasa that in the chintamani-griha, in the sree chakra city, kubera is seated with his associates like manibhadra and adores her. thus she was worshipped by kubera. bhāskararāya interprets this name through both mythological status and mantric lineage: the worship of kubera: kubera, the lord of wealth and the king of kings, is one of the foundational seers of the śrīvidyā tradition (the marakata-mata or kubera-vidyā). his intense adoration of the devi grants him his unyielding prosperity. the imperial path: it demonstrates that even those who have achieved the absolute peak of material sovereignty (sāmrājya) must bow before her to sustain their power.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya interprets this name through both mythological status and Mantric lineage:

The Worship of Kubera: Kubera, the lord of wealth and the king of kings, is one of the foundational seers of the Śrīvidyā tradition (the Marakata-mata or Kubera-vidyā). His intense adoration of the Devi grants him his unyielding prosperity.
The Imperial Path: It demonstrates that even those who have achieved the absolute peak of material sovereignty (sāmrājya) must bow before Her to sustain their power.

name: राज्ञी
wordByWord: राज्ञी — meaning follows from the samasa: राज्ञी।
summary: i. One who is a queen.
ii. Sreedevee is the empress of Rajarajeshvara (Mahdkameshvara). Hence She is Ragjee. (0 One who the most beautiful.
searchText: 306 राज्ञी • राज्ञी। राज्ञी — queen राज्ञी — meaning follows from the samasa: राज्ञी। i. one who is a queen. ii. sreedevee is the empress of rajarajeshvara (mahdkameshvara). hence she is ragjee. (0 one who the most beautiful. bhāskararāya explains her sovereign status over all cosmic planes: the supreme sovereign: she is ललिता महात्रिपुरसुन्दरी (lalitā mahātripurasundarī), the absolute empress who dictates the governance of the cosmos from her seat on the cintāmaṇi-gṛha. all cosmic laws, elements, and deities operate strictly under her imperial commands.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya explains Her sovereign status over all cosmic planes:

The Supreme Sovereign: She is ललिता महात्रिपुरसुन्दरी (Lalitā Mahātripurasundarī), the absolute empress who dictates the governance of the cosmos from Her seat on the Cintāmaṇi-gṛha. All cosmic laws, elements, and deities operate strictly under Her imperial commands.

name: रम्या
wordByWord: रम्या — meaning follows from the samasa: रम्या। (सौन्दर्यवती)।
summary: i. One who is the most beautiful.
searchText: 307 रम्या • रम्या। (सौन्दर्यवती)। रम्या — beautiful; pleasing रम्या — meaning follows from the samasa: रम्या। (सौन्दर्यवती)। i. one who is the most beautiful. in the saubhāgyabhāskara, bhāskararāya connects her beauty to the experience of pure consciousness: the source of delight: she is the ultimate embodiment of absolute aesthetic and spiritual bliss (ānanda). any object in the universe that appears beautiful or delightful to the mind reflects merely an infinitesimal drop of her inherent splendor (svarūpa-saundarya).
esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara, Bhāskararāya connects Her beauty to the experience of pure consciousness:

The Source of Delight: She is the ultimate embodiment of absolute aesthetic and spiritual bliss (Ānanda). Any object in the universe that appears beautiful or delightful to the mind reflects merely an infinitesimal drop of Her inherent splendor (Svarūpa-saundarya).

name: राजीवलोचना
wordByWord: राजीवलोचना — meaning follows from the samasa: राजीव इव लोचने यस्याः सा।
summary: i. One whose eyes are like deer, fish and red lotus flower.

ii. The word Rajeeva means deer, fish and red lotus flower. Her eyes 'are compared to all these. 561" name Mrugakshee can be referred. {iii) The word Rajeeva also means one who is dependent on king. Since She sights, with affection and blessings, the devotees of Her consort Réjardjesvara, She is Réjeevalochana.
i. One who delights Her devotees.

ii. She delights Her devotees by providing bliss in t His world and liberation in the ot Her world. {iil) Ranjayatee means to make red. tt has been mentioned in more than one place that the colour of Sreedevee is red. For instance, the dhyana verse may be referred - by Her presence, the rosy-tinted Deve colours the pure Paramashiva as the red flower colours the crystal. Sree Adi Sankara, in his Soundaryalahari, 92" verse, describes the same sense; Gataste Manchatvam Drhina Hari Rudresvara Bhruta: Shiva: Svachcha Chchaya Ghatita Kopata Pranchadopota:| Tvateeyandm Bhdsdm Pratiphalana Ragdrunatay6 z 'Shareeree Srungaro Rasa Iva Drushém Dogdhi Kutukamlt
iv. She is mentioned as the consort of Prajépati, in Mahdshoda Nyasam (Moorthynyasom).
searchText: 308 राजीवलोचना • राजीव इव लोचने यस्याः सा। राजीव — lotus; deer; fish लोचने — eyes यस्याः — of whom; whose सा — she राजीवलोचना — meaning follows from the samasa: राजीव इव लोचने यस्याः सा। i. one whose eyes are like deer, fish and red lotus flower. ii. the word rajeeva means deer, fish and red lotus flower. her eyes 'are compared to all these. 561" name mrugakshee can be referred. {iii) the word rajeeva also means one who is dependent on king. since she sights, with affection and blessings, the devotees of her consort réjardjesvara, she is réjeevalochana. i. one who delights her devotees. ii. she delights her devotees by providing bliss in t his world and liberation in the ot her world. {iil) ranjayatee means to make red. tt has been mentioned in more than one place that the colour of sreedevee is red. for instance, the dhyana verse may be referred - by her presence, the rosy-tinted deve colours the pure paramashiva as the red flower colours the crystal. sree adi sankara, in his soundaryalahari, 92" verse, describes the same sense; gataste manchatvam drhina hari rudresvara bhruta: shiva: svachcha chchaya ghatita kopata pranchadopota:| tvateeyandm bhdsdm pratiphalana ragdrunatay6 z 'shareeree srungaro rasa iva drushém dogdhi kutukamlt iv. she is mentioned as the consort of prajépati, in mahdshoda nyasam (moorthynyasom). bhāskararāya expands this common poetic description into the domain of spiritual sight: the lotus sight: her lotus-like eyes shower pure compassion (karuṇā) and immediate peace upon those who look toward her. it also implies that her gaze is perfectly open, wide, and untainted by the mud of relative worldly existence, just like a lotus leaf remaining dry in water.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya expands this common poetic description into the domain of spiritual sight:

The Lotus Sight: Her lotus-like eyes shower pure compassion (karuṇā) and immediate peace upon those who look toward Her. It also implies that Her gaze is perfectly open, wide, and untainted by the mud of relative worldly existence, just like a lotus leaf remaining dry in water.

name: रञ्जनी
wordByWord: रञ्जनी — meaning follows from the samasa: भक्तान् रञ्जयतीति।
summary: ii. Brahma, was the first one to be created by Sreedevee in the form of of spider's web - a micro worm; She gave birth to all these beings. (ili) It is indicated as, though there is a huge difference between a worm and Brahma, as a mot Her She has equal affection with all.
searchText: 309 रञ्जनी • भक्तान् रञ्जयतीति। भक्तान् — devotees रञ्जयतीति — delights; colours रञ्जनी — meaning follows from the samasa: भक्तान् रञ्जयतीति। ii. brahma, was the first one to be created by sreedevee in the form of of spider's web - a micro worm; she gave birth to all these beings. (ili) it is indicated as, though there is a huge difference between a worm and brahma, as a mot her she has equal affection with all. bhāskararāya illuminates the internal psychological process of devotion (bhakti): coloring the mind: to "color" the mind means to saturate the devotee's thoughts completely with divine consciousness. she removes the dull, colorless state of worldly indifference and fills the practitioner's heart with the vibrant red dye of pure devotion and bliss.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya illuminates the internal psychological process of devotion (bhakti):

Coloring the Mind: To "color" the mind means to saturate the devotee's thoughts completely with divine consciousness. She removes the dull, colorless state of worldly indifference and fills the practitioner's heart with the vibrant red dye of pure devotion and bliss.

name: रमणी
wordByWord: रमणी — meaning follows from the samasa: भक्तान् रमयतीति।
summary: ee ed {iy On account of Her greatness and pride, the devotees with fear, 'amazement and admiration, may not come close to Her. By making herself simple, She moves closely with the devotees.

ii. In Sreemad Bhagavad Gita (X|- 14) also, Krishna described Arjuna, who was seeing = the_--cosmic_- form, -- as; Vismaydvishto Hrushtaroma: Arjuna describes himself as; Bhayena Cha Pravyathitam Mano Me (Xi - 45). Thus Sreedevee makes the devotees move closely without any fear. {iv) The Veda (Chandogya Upanishad VIll-12-3) says, laughing, playing and rejoicing.
searchText: 310 रमणी • भक्तान् रमयतीति। भक्तान् — devotees रमयतीति — causes to rejoice रमणी — meaning follows from the samasa: भक्तान् रमयतीति। ee ed {iy on account of her greatness and pride, the devotees with fear, 'amazement and admiration, may not come close to her. by making herself simple, she moves closely with the devotees. ii. in sreemad bhagavad gita (x|- 14) also, krishna described arjuna, who was seeing = the_--cosmic_- form, -- as; vismaydvishto hrushtaroma: arjuna describes himself as; bhayena cha pravyathitam mano me (xi - 45). thus sreedevee makes the devotees move closely without any fear. {iv) the veda (chandogya upanishad vill-12-3) says, laughing, playing and rejoicing. bhāskararāya defines her role as the ultimate granter of resting bliss: the rest within consciousness: she brings absolute delight (ramaṇa) to the hearts of practitioners by terminating their restless wandering through saṁsāra. in her presence, the mind finds its ultimate rest, experiencing the natural joy of the self.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya defines Her role as the ultimate granter of resting bliss:

The Rest Within Consciousness: She brings absolute delight (ramaṇa) to the hearts of practitioners by terminating their restless wandering through saṁsāra. In Her presence, the mind finds its ultimate rest, experiencing the natural joy of the Self.

name: रस्या
wordByWord: रस्या — meaning follows from the samasa: रसयितुं योग्या।
summary: {i) One who Is to be tasted of the essence (of bliss of realization of self) {i) The feeling that follows the continuous experience that the mind gets is called essence (rasa). If it is tasteful it is rasa else it is virasa.

ii. The universal absolute is called rasa by the Vedas - (Taittiriya Uponishad 11.6.1) - Raso Vai Sa: - He is alone the essence. Thus She is to be experienced as essence. We squeeze the fruits, take out the juice and enjoy it. Likewise from the gross physical, the subtle is taken out as essence and enjoyed. In the same way She is to be enjoyed.
searchText: 311 रस्या • रसयितुं योग्या। रसयितुं — to taste; to relish योग्या — fit; worthy रस्या — meaning follows from the samasa: रसयितुं योग्या। {i) one who is to be tasted of the essence (of bliss of realization of self) {i) the feeling that follows the continuous experience that the mind gets is called essence (rasa). if it is tasteful it is rasa else it is virasa. ii. the universal absolute is called rasa by the vedas - (taittiriya uponishad 11.6.1) - raso vai sa: - he is alone the essence. thus she is to be experienced as essence. we squeeze the fruits, take out the juice and enjoy it. likewise from the gross physical, the subtle is taken out as essence and enjoyed. in the same way she is to be enjoyed. bhāskararāya anchors this name directly in the famous taittirīya upaniṣad declaration ("रसो वै सः" — he is indeed rasa): the ultimate experience: she is the essential juice, sweetness, and emotional flavor behind all experiences. she is the object of ultimate tasting for real mystics, who drink the nectar of non-dual bliss during deep absorption.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya anchors this name directly in the famous Taittirīya Upaniṣad declaration ("रसो वै सः" — He is indeed Rasa):

The Ultimate Experience: She is the essential juice, sweetness, and emotional flavor behind all experiences. She is the object of ultimate tasting for real mystics, who drink the nectar of non-dual bliss during deep absorption.

name: रणत्किङ्किणिमेखला
samasa: • रणन्त्यः किङ्किण्यः यस्यां तादृशी मेखला यस्याः सा।
wordByWord: रणत्किङ्किणिमेखला — meaning follows from the samasa: रणन्त्यः किङ्किण्यः यस्यां तादृशी मेखला यस्याः सा।
summary: i. One who wears a girdle with tinkling bells.
ii. Sree Adi Sankara, in his Soundaryalahari(7™ verse), describes the same sense; Kvanatkdncheedémo.
searchText: 312 रणत्किङ्किणिमेखला • रणन्त्यः किङ्किण्यः यस्यां तादृशी मेखला यस्याः सा। रणन्त्यः — tinkling; sounding किङ्किण्यः — small bells मेखला — girdle यस्याः — of whom; whose सा — she रणत्किङ्किणिमेखला — meaning follows from the samasa: रणन्त्यः किङ्किण्यः यस्यां तादृशी मेखला यस्याः सा। i. one who wears a girdle with tinkling bells. ii. sree adi sankara, in his soundaryalahari(7™ verse), describes the same sense; kvanatkdncheedémo. bhāskararāya moves from the physical adornment to the mantric acoustics of the subtle body: the sound of the spheres: the tinkling bells (kiṅkiṇi) on her girdle represent the subtle sound frequencies emanating from the rotation of the internal chakras. as she moves through the subtle channels of the practitioner, her presence generates the baseline cosmic music (nāda) that awakens the internal energy centers.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya moves from the physical adornment to the mantric acoustics of the subtle body:

The Sound of the Spheres: The tinkling bells (kiṅkiṇi) on Her girdle represent the subtle sound frequencies emanating from the rotation of the internal chakras. As She moves through the subtle channels of the practitioner, Her presence generates the baseline cosmic music (Nāda) that awakens the internal energy centers.

name: रमा
wordByWord: रमा — meaning follows from the samasa: रमा।
summary: i. One who is in the form of Lakshmi.
ii. The Suta Samhita says She appears like a dancer in the form of Lakshmi, Sarasvati, etc.
iii. In Saptashati also She is worshipped as Ya Sreeh Svayam Sukritinam Bhavaneshu.
iv. The fourth vowel "e" of Sanskrit letters is indicated by this name. This is the third letter in Panchadashimantra. The related name Ratirupa may be referred to.
v. In Mahashoda Nyasa, She is indicated as in the form of a river.
searchText: 313 रमा • रमा। रमा — lakshmi; delight रमा — meaning follows from the samasa: रमा। i. one who is in the form of lakshmi. ii. the suta samhita says she appears like a dancer in the form of lakshmi, sarasvati, etc. iii. in saptashati also she is worshipped as ya sreeh svayam sukritinam bhavaneshu. iv. the fourth vowel "e" of sanskrit letters is indicated by this name. this is the third letter in panchadashimantra. the related name ratirupa may be referred to. v. in mahashoda nyasa, she is indicated as in the form of a river. in the saubhāgyabhāskara, bhāskararāya defines her identity with the principle of cosmic prosperity: the principle of lakṣmī: she is not separate from lakṣmī (ramā). she embodies all forms of wealth, beauty, and sovereignty that manifest throughout the universe, confirming that the goddess of prosperity is an aspect of her own expansive power.
esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara, Bhāskararāya defines Her identity with the principle of cosmic prosperity:

The Principle of Lakṣmī: She is not separate from Lakṣmī (Ramā). She embodies all forms of wealth, beauty, and sovereignty that manifest throughout the universe, confirming that the goddess of prosperity is an aspect of Her own expansive power.

name: राकेन्दुवदना
wordByWord: राकेन्दुवदना — meaning follows from the samasa: राकेन्दुवत् वदनं यस्याः सा।
summary: i. One who has face like a full moon.
ii. We get happiness to see again and again an ocean, an elephant and the moon. We never get tired. She has such a face.
iii. By referring as moon, it is described as circle and crescent.
searchText: 314 राकेन्दुवदना • राकेन्दुवत् वदनं यस्याः सा। राकेन्दुवत् — like the full moon वदनं — face यस्याः — of whom; whose सा — she राकेन्दुवदना — meaning follows from the samasa: राकेन्दुवत् वदनं यस्याः सा। i. one who has face like a full moon. ii. we get happiness to see again and again an ocean, an elephant and the moon. we never get tired. she has such a face. iii. by referring as moon, it is described as circle and crescent. bhāskararāya decodes this through both physical illumination and śrīvidyā geometry: the coolness of grace: the full moon of the rākā night radiates a light that cools the heat of the day. similarly, her face radiates waves of nectar (amṛta) that instantly extinguish the burning planetary sorrows (tāpatraya) of the devotee. the crown light: it also references the glowing white radiance of the sahasrāra moon center at the crown of the head.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya decodes this through both physical illumination and Śrīvidyā geometry:

The Coolness of Grace: The full moon of the Rākā night radiates a light that cools the heat of the day. Similarly, Her face radiates waves of nectar (amṛta) that instantly extinguish the burning planetary sorrows (tāpatraya) of the devotee.
The Crown Light: It also references the glowing white radiance of the Sahasrāra moon center at the crown of the head.

name: रतिरूपा
wordByWord: रतिरूपा — meaning follows from the samasa: रतिः एव रूपं यस्याः सा।
summary: i. One who is in the form of Rati (Cupid's consort).

ii. Rati is the external interest and involvement on anything. Unless it is achieved people do not get peace. Rati is the consort of Cupid. Cupid's goal is the person who has wish. Rati helps Cupid by creating love and interest on things. Sreedevee's energy only comes out in the form Rati and hence Rotiroopa.

iii. Or. In the previous two names the letter 'E* (bindu and anusvéram) were cryptically indicated. When these two integrate the Kamakala letter 'Em' is created. It is a known fact that Cupid creates the erotic feeling and hence His consort is identified as Kamakald and hence t His meaning. +

summary: {l) One who is most liked (by the learned). {ii) Those who desire liberation desire, to obtain Her by knowledge. Hence Kamya. {iil Kamya is the deity of the twelfth night of the dark fortnight of the lunar month, Toitreeyo Bréhmagam - Katakam says; As per Sree Vidya practice, the 12" day of the dark fortnight and 4" day of bright fortnight (of the lunar month) have same presiding deity. THis deity is called Bherunda. But as per Veda the presiding deity of 4" night of bright fortnight is Vishvaroopd and the presiding deity of en RS 12" night of dark of fortnight is Kamya. 256" name Vishvaroopé, referred. 'The names of the presiding deities, for the days and nights of bright and dark fortnights are differently mentioned in Vedas and Sree Vidya practice, The relationship between these is described by Lakshmeedarar in His commentary for the 32 verse of Soundaryalahari.
searchText: 321 काम्या • [ज्ञानेन प्राप्तव्यत्वेन मुमुक्षुभिः] काम्या। काम्या — desired; worthy of being sought काम्या — meaning follows from the samasa: [ज्ञानेन प्राप्तव्यत्वेन मुमुक्षुभिः] काम्या। {l) one who is most liked (by the learned). {ii) those who desire liberation desire, to obtain her by knowledge. hence kamya. {iil kamya is the deity of the twelfth night of the dark fortnight of the lunar month, toitreeyo bréhmagam - katakam says; as per sree vidya practice, the 12" day of the dark fortnight and 4" day of bright fortnight (of the lunar month) have same presiding deity. this deity is called bherunda. but as per veda the presiding deity of 4" night of bright fortnight is vishvaroopd and the presiding deity of en rs 12" night of dark of fortnight is kamya. 256" name vishvaroopé, referred. She is Kāmyā because She represents the ultimate fulfillment that terminates all other lesser cravings.

name: कदम्बकुसुमप्रिया
wordByWord: कदम्बकुसुमप्रिया — meaning follows from the samasa: कदम्बकुसुमानि प्रियानि यस्याः सा।
summary: i. One who is fond of kadambaflowers. '
i. We earlier read in 60" name as Kadambovanavasinee, Only since She is fond of kadamba trees, a kadamba jungle has been set uP 'ouiside Her Chintémanigraha house.

ii. THis indicates the goddess Meenakshee, who is blessing the devoemma tees at Madurai. The 11" name in Trishati is Kadambakusumapriya. The note given by Sree Sir Subramagia lyer while translating. Sree Adi Sankara's, commentary of t His nameis; Kodamba tree is 'one of the Kalpaka trees (deva tree). Inner-self is a group of Satva quality of five elements. Similarly Kalpaka tree is a common name for five types of trees viz, Santdnom, Herisantanam, Mantarom, Porijétam and Kadambam. The five parts of inner self is mind, intellect, volition, ego and heart. Mind is the satva part of wind. Intellect is the satva part of fire. Similarly, volition - water, ego - earth and heart or combined inner-self satva part of ether. Hence kadamba is mind; one who resides in the forest of mind is called Kadambavanavasé (10 name in -'Trishati) or Kadambavanavasinee (60 name). Kadambokusumd is the expansion of the mind. Knowledge and expansion are inseparable forms. There is no knowledge without expansion and vice versa. Hence for Sreedevee, who is in the integrated form of knowledge on subjects and tatvas, is very much of fond of kadamba flower in the form of expansion of mind.

The Kadamba Grove: Mythologically, She resides in a forest of Kadamba trees (Kadamba-vana). Esoterically, the Kadamba tree represents the blossoming expansion of joy within the Sahasrāra chakra. Offering these round, golden flowers signifies the surrender of one's fully blossomed heart-intellect at Her feet.

name: कल्याणी
wordByWord: कल्याणी — meaning follows from the samasa: [मङ्गलस्वरूपत्वात्] कल्याणी।
summary: i. One who is in an auspicious form.
ii. Sreedevee is complete auspiciousness.
iii. Kalya means good words, and ana means to utter. Kalyam + anati means one who always utters good words.
iv. According to the Padma Purana, in the Malaya Mountain, She is worshipped as Kalyani.

v. The second name in Sree Lalita Trishati is Kalyani. Sree Adi Sankara comments that auspiciousness is happiness. This happiness in worldly activities, as per the Veda, is health and youth, from ordinary human happiness up to the happiness of Brahma; all are atom-sized drops from complete bliss. Sreedevee is complete bliss. As the Veda says, Vijnanam Anandam Brahma - She is complete bliss in the form of Brahman, without blemish.
searchText: 324 कल्याणी • [मङ्गलस्वरूपत्वात्] कल्याणी। मङ्गलस्वरूपत्वात् — because of auspicious nature कल्याणी — auspicious one कल्याणी — meaning follows from the samasa: [मङ्गलस्वरूपत्वात्] कल्याणी। i. one who is in an auspicious form. ii. sreedevee is complete auspiciousness. iii. kalya means good words, and ana means to utter. kalyam + anati means one who always utters good words. iv. according to the padma purana, in the malaya mountain, she is worshipped as kalyani. v. the second name in sree lalita trishati is kalyani. sree adi sankara comments that auspiciousness is happiness. this happiness in worldly activities, as per the veda, is health and youth, from ordinary human happiness up to the happiness of brahma; all are atom-sized drops from complete bliss. sreedevee is complete bliss. as the veda says, vijnanam anandam brahma - she is complete bliss in the form of brahman, without blemish. in the saubhāgyabhāskara, this name defines her absolute identity with welfare: the essence of well-being: she does not merely perform good actions; her very form is goodness itself (maṅgala-svarūpa). by simply remembering her name, the darkness of bad portents, negative thoughts, and karmic obstructions is instantly dissolved, replaced by spiritual clarity.
esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara, this name defines Her absolute identity with welfare:

The Essence of Well-being: She does not merely perform good actions; Her very form is goodness itself (Maṅgala-svarūpa). By simply remembering Her name, the darkness of bad portents, negative thoughts, and karmic obstructions is instantly dissolved, replaced by spiritual clarity.

name: जगतीकन्दा
wordByWord: जगत्याः — of the world; of the universe
कन्दं — root; bulb; source of support
जगतीकन्दा — she whose root or source is the universe, or who is the root-cause of the universe
summary: i. One who is the root cause of this universe.

ii. The word Kanda has several meanings, such as root of plants, cloud, and that which fills. The root of a plant supports the entire plant and provides the required strength. In the same way She supports and gives strength to this whole world.
iii. This may be compared with the verse XV-1 of the Bhagavad Gita, which speaks of the imperishable Asvattha tree with roots above and branches below.
searchText: 325 जगतीकन्दा • जगत्याः कन्दं यस्याः सा। जगत्याः — of the world; of the universe कन्दं — root; bulb; source of support यस्याः — of whom; whose सा — she जगतीकन्दा — she whose root or source is the universe, or who is the root-cause of the universe i. one who is the root cause of this universe. ii. the word kanda has several meanings, such as root of plants, cloud, and that which fills. the root of a plant supports the entire plant and provides the required strength. in the same way she supports and gives strength to this whole world. iii. this may be compared with the verse xv-1 of the bhagavad gita, which speaks of the imperishable asvattha tree with roots above and branches below. bhāskararāya uses a botanical analogy to explain her position as the source: the cosmic bulb: just as a massive plant emerges, draws nourishment, and grows from a single underlying root bulb (kanda), the entire multi-dimensional universe shoots forth from her unmanifested causal nature. she is the foundational anchor holding the tree of creation upright.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya uses a botanical analogy to explain Her position as the source:

The Cosmic Bulb: Just as a massive plant emerges, draws nourishment, and grows from a single underlying root bulb (kanda), the entire multi-dimensional universe shoots forth from Her unmanifested causal nature. She is the foundational anchor holding the tree of creation upright.

name: करुणारससागरा
samasa: • करुणा एव रसः येषु सागरेषु तादृशाः सागराः यस्याः सा।
wordByWord: करुणारससागरा — she whose oceans have compassion as their essence
summary: i. One who is the ocean of compassion.

ii. Sreedevee is like an ocean whose water is compassion; her compassion is greater than an ocean. The later name Avyaja-karuna-murtih explains that her compassion for devotees is without deceit, partiality, gambling, dispute, or expectation.
iii. The meditation verse of this Sahasranamam also describes her eyes as waves of compassion.

iv. In Trishati also the compassion of Sreedevee is described in names such as Karunamrita-sagara, Kataksha-syandi-karuna, and Karunya-vigraha. These explain that her compassion is like the ocean of nectar of liberation, that it flows through her benign glance, and that compassion itself is her body.
searchText: 326 करुणारससागरा • करुणा एव रसः येषु सागरेषु तादृशाः सागराः यस्याः सा। करुणा — compassion रसः — essence; fluid; rasa सागरा — oceans यस्याः — of whom; whose सा — she करुणारससागरा — she whose oceans have compassion as their essence i. one who is the ocean of compassion. ii. sreedevee is like an ocean whose water is compassion; her compassion is greater than an ocean. the later name avyaja-karuna-murtih explains that her compassion for devotees is without deceit, partiality, gambling, dispute, or expectation. iii. the meditation verse of this sahasranamam also describes her eyes as waves of compassion. iv. in trishati also the compassion of sreedevee is described in names such as karunamrita-sagara, kataksha-syandi-karuna, and karunya-vigraha. these explain that her compassion is like the ocean of nectar of liberation, that it flows through her benign glance, and that compassion itself is her body. bhāskararāya emphasizes her absolute accessibility to suffering souls: the ocean of mercy: the ocean is vast, deep, and inexhaustible. her compassion is not a passing emotional reaction but an infinite, shoreless sea. no matter how deep a soul has fallen into the darkness of ignorance, her waves of mercy are capable of washing away that limitation instantly.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya emphasizes Her absolute accessibility to suffering souls:

The Ocean of Mercy: The ocean is vast, deep, and inexhaustible. Her compassion is not a passing emotional reaction but an infinite, shoreless sea. No matter how deep a soul has fallen into the darkness of ignorance, Her waves of mercy are capable of washing away that limitation instantly.

name: कलावती
wordByWord: कलावती — she who possesses the arts
summary: i. One who is the embodiment of the sixty-four arts.
ii. The sixty-four arts have been described in the name Chatushshashti-kalamayi.
searchText: 327 कलावती • (चतुःषष्टिसङ्ख्याः) कला अस्यां सन्तीति। चतुःषष्टिसङ्ख्याः — sixty-four in number कलाः — arts; kalas अस्याम् — in her सन्ति — exist कलावती — she who possesses the arts i. one who is the embodiment of the sixty-four arts. ii. the sixty-four arts have been described in the name chatushshashti-kalamayi. bhāskararāya interprets kalā through multiple systematic frameworks: the sixty-four arts: she is the master and source of all 64 classical arts (catur-ṣaṣṭhi kalā) that govern human culture and intelligence. the lunar digits: in śrīvidyā lunar calendars, she embodies the changing digits of the moon (kalā), representing the passage of time while remaining fixed as the background light.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya interprets Kalā through multiple systematic frameworks:
The Sixty-Four Arts: She is the master and source of all 64 classical arts (catur-ṣaṣṭhi kalā) that govern human culture and intelligence.
The Lunar Digits: In Śrīvidyā lunar calendars, She embodies the changing digits of the moon (kalā), representing the passage of time while remaining fixed as the background light.

name: कलालापा
samasa: • कं लालावत् आपः यस्याः सकाशात् सा।
wordByWord: कलालापा — she whose speech itself is art
summary: i. One who has arts as her conversation.
ii. Everything Sreedevee talks is an art.
iii. According to the Amarakosha, kala means sweet-sounding and alapa means speech. Hence Kalalapa means one who has sweet speech.

iv. Kam means Brahman, lala means saliva, and apa means attainment. Just as saliva flows naturally by thinking of tasty food, the attainment of Brahman becomes natural through her grace and leads along the path of salvation.
searchText: 328 कलालापा • कल एव आलापः यस्याः सा। • कं लालावत् आपः यस्याः सकाशात् सा। कला — art; also sweet sound आलापः — speech; conversation यस्याः — of whom; whose कलालापा — she whose speech itself is art कम् — brahman लाला — saliva; natural flow आपः — attainment i. one who has arts as her conversation. ii. everything sreedevee talks is an art. iii. according to the amarakosha, kala means sweet-sounding and alapa means speech. hence kalalapa means one who has sweet speech. iv. kam means brahman, lala means saliva, and apa means attainment. just as saliva flows naturally by thinking of tasty food, the attainment of brahman becomes natural through her grace and leads along the path of salvation. bhāskararāya addresses both the auditory and subtle yogic meanings: the sweetness of truth: her voice represents the ultimate, uncorrupted sweet harmony of cosmic truth. the downpour of nectar: esoterically, the speech refers to the vibration that releases the flow of moon-nectar (sudhā-dhārā) from the upper palate during advanced internal practices.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya addresses both the auditory and subtle yogic meanings:
The Sweetness of Truth: Her voice represents the ultimate, uncorrupted sweet harmony of cosmic truth.
The Downpour of Nectar: Esoterically, the speech refers to the vibration that releases the flow of moon-nectar (sudhā-dhārā) from the upper palate during advanced internal practices.

name: कान्ता
summary: i. One who is beautiful.

ii. Since she is effulgent, she appears as ultimate beauty. Lustre is the beauty and brightness of the body. The names Kanti and Kantimati may be referred to. The later name Akanta may also be referred to, where it is understood as destroyer of sorrow or sin.

iii. Kam means Brahman and anta means final. Unconditioned Brahman is her final form. This indicates the final philosophy of Sreedevee's form without quality; she makes the saguna and nirguna forms perceptible.
iv. The presiding deity of the eleventh night of the dark fortnight of the lunar month is called Kanta. She is in that form and in the form of all presiding deities of days and nights.
searchText: 329 कान्ता • कान्ता [कमनीयत्वात्]। • कम् एव अन्तः यस्याः सा। कान्ता — beautiful; beloved कमनीयत्वात् — because of loveliness कम् — brahman अन्तः — end; final state यस्याः — of whom; whose i. one who is beautiful. ii. since she is effulgent, she appears as ultimate beauty. lustre is the beauty and brightness of the body. the names kanti and kantimati may be referred to. the later name akanta may also be referred to, where it is understood as destroyer of sorrow or sin. iii. kam means brahman and anta means final. unconditioned brahman is her final form. this indicates the final philosophy of sreedevee's form without quality; she makes the saguna and nirguna forms perceptible. iv. the presiding deity of the eleventh night of the dark fortnight of the lunar month is called kanta. she is in that form and in the form of all presiding deities of days and nights. bhāskararāya decodes this name to show her dual nature: the supreme beauty: she is the ultimate beloved, drawing all hearts naturally toward herself through her sheer aesthetic perfection. the resolution in bliss: by breaking the word down as कम् + अन्तः, bhāskararāya explains that she is the ultimate terminal point (anta) where all search for happiness (kam) ends in absolute finality.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya decodes this name to show Her dual nature:
The Supreme Beauty: She is the ultimate beloved, drawing all hearts naturally toward Herself through Her sheer aesthetic perfection.
The Resolution in Bliss: By breaking the word down as कम् + अन्तः, Bhāskararāya explains that She is the ultimate terminal point (anta) where all search for happiness (kam) ends in absolute finality.

name: कादम्बरीप्रिया
wordByWord: कादम्बरीप्रिया — she to whom kadambari is dear
summary: i. One who is fond of mead.

ii. In Navavarana Puja there are five articles beginning with the letter Ma: madhu, mamsa, mangai, meen, and mudra. Mead is one of these, and they are consumed as the consecrated offering of Sreedevee after being offered to her. Tripura Upanishad mentions offering mead, fish, flesh, and cooked cereals in the triangle representing the great Devata.

iii. Those who are not restricted from consuming such articles may use them; Brahmins should use only substitutes and only with the corresponding tattvas in mind. The commentary of Sreemad Appayya Dikshita on Tripura Upanishad may be referred to; he advises that such passages are for understanding and not for ordinary practice.

iv. Sreedevee is fond of high-quality mead. Related names include Samayachara-tatpara, Varuni-mada-vihvala, Kaula-marga-tatpara-sevita, Madhu-prita, Madhvi-panalasa, Madhumati, Savyapasavya-margastha, and Dakshina-dakshinaradhya. In Saptashati also it is said that Sreedevee consumed mead in order to bring rajas to the forefront and show no mercy to the demons.
searchText: 330 कादम्बरीप्रिया • कादम्बरी (उत्तमा मदिरा) प्रिया यस्याः सा। कादम्बरी — mead; high-quality liquor प्रिया — dear; beloved यस्याः — of whom; whose कादम्बरीप्रिया — she to whom kadambari is dear i. one who is fond of mead. ii. in navavarana puja there are five articles beginning with the letter ma: madhu, mamsa, mangai, meen, and mudra. mead is one of these, and they are consumed as the consecrated offering of sreedevee after being offered to her. tripura upanishad mentions offering mead, fish, flesh, and cooked cereals in the triangle representing the great devata. iii. those who are not restricted from consuming such articles may use them; brahmins should use only substitutes and only with the corresponding tattvas in mind. the commentary of sreemad appayya dikshita on tripura upanishad may be referred to; he advises that such passages are for understanding and not for ordinary practice. iv. sreedevee is fond of high-quality mead. related names include samayachara-tatpara, varuni-mada-vihvala, kaula-marga-tatpara-sevita, madhu-prita, madhvi-panalasa, madhumati, savyapasavya-margastha, and dakshina-dakshinaradhya. in saptashati also it is said that sreedevee consumed mead in order to bring rajas to the forefront and show no mercy to the demons. bhāskararāya strips away any crude, literal interpretations of intoxicants: the wine of divine intoxication: in tantric literature, कादम्बरी signifies the supreme nectar of self-realization that drops from the sahasrāra chakra. this nectar intoxicates the yogi, dissolving their rational ego into absolute ecstasy. she loves this specific internal intoxication of devotion.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya strips away any crude, literal interpretations of intoxicants:

The Wine of Divine Intoxication: In Tantric literature, कादम्बरी signifies the supreme nectar of self-realization that drops from the Sahasrāra chakra. This nectar intoxicates the yogi, dissolving their rational ego into absolute ecstasy. She loves this specific internal intoxication of devotion.

name: वरदा
summary: i. One who bestows boons.
ii. She grants boons to Brahma, Vishnu, and other devotees. Sree Bhaskararaya explains that her uplifted hand ever confers boons and fulfils the desires of the Devas, Daityas, and ascetics.
iii. Sree Adi Sankara, in the commentary on Soundaryalahari verse 4, says that even without showing the gesture of boon-giving, Sreedevee grants devotees more than they desire.

iv. Devee Purana says that she fulfils the desires of the Devas who seek boons. From the root vri, to choose, she is called Varada, the conferrer of what is chosen. Varaha Purana also says that she becomes the giver of boons to all worlds when worshipped with concentration on the ninth lunar day.
searchText: 331 वरदा • वरान् ददातीति। वरान् — boons ददाति — gives वरदा — giver of boons i. one who bestows boons. ii. she grants boons to brahma, vishnu, and other devotees. sree bhaskararaya explains that her uplifted hand ever confers boons and fulfils the desires of the devas, daityas, and ascetics. iii. sree adi sankara, in the commentary on soundaryalahari verse 4, says that even without showing the gesture of boon-giving, sreedevee grants devotees more than they desire. iv. devee purana says that she fulfils the desires of the devas who seek boons. from the root vri, to choose, she is called varada, the conferrer of what is chosen. varaha purana also says that she becomes the giver of boons to all worlds when worshipped with concentration on the ninth lunar day. bhāskararāya distinguishes her boon-giving capacity from ordinary gods: the ultimate gift: while other deities grant specific relative boons (like long life, wealth, or power), she grants the ultimate boon—the gift of her own self (atma-dāna), which brings immediate liberation from the cycle of birth and death.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya distinguishes Her boon-giving capacity from ordinary gods:

The Ultimate Gift: While other deities grant specific relative boons (like long life, wealth, or power), She grants the ultimate boon—the gift of Her own self (Atma-dāna), which brings immediate liberation from the cycle of birth and death.

name: वामनयना
wordByWord: वामं — the left path; also fruit of action
वामनयना — she with beautiful eyes; she who leads by the left path
summary: i. One who has beautiful or graceful eyes.
ii. Vamam means beauty, and nayanam means eyes or proof. The name may therefore mean one who has beautiful eyes, beautiful proofs, or a beautiful means of understanding.
iii. Vama also means left-handed; she leads devotees who follow the left-handed path.
iv. In Chandogya Upanishad, vama is explained as the fruits of actions. In this sense she makes devotees reap the fruits of their actions; the name Punyapunya-phalaprada may be compared.
v. As per Matrika Nyasa, the bija for the left eye is Em. Since this is a Kamakala bija, the name may also indicate Kamakala.
searchText: 332 वामनयना • वामानि नयनानि यस्याः सा। • वामं नयतीति। वामानि — beautiful; graceful; also left नयनानि — eyes; means of knowledge यस्याः — of whom; whose वामं — the left path; also fruit of action नयति — leads वामनयना — she with beautiful eyes; she who leads by the left path i. one who has beautiful or graceful eyes. ii. vamam means beauty, and nayanam means eyes or proof. the name may therefore mean one who has beautiful eyes, beautiful proofs, or a beautiful means of understanding. iii. vama also means left-handed; she leads devotees who follow the left-handed path. iv. in chandogya upanishad, vama is explained as the fruits of actions. in this sense she makes devotees reap the fruits of their actions; the name punyapunya-phalaprada may be compared. v. as per matrika nyasa, the bija for the left eye is em. since this is a kamakala bija, the name may also indicate kamakala. bhāskararāya unpacks the esoteric direction built into this name: the guide to liberation: beyond her beautiful eyes, the root nī means to lead. she is vāmanayanā because she actively leads (nayati) the practitioner toward the highest spiritual beautification (vāma), which is the state of final liberation.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya unpacks the esoteric direction built into this name:

The Guide to Liberation: Beyond Her beautiful eyes, the root nī means to lead. She is Vāmanayanā because She actively leads (nayati) the practitioner toward the highest spiritual beautification (vāma), which is the state of final liberation.

name: वारुणीमदविह्वला
samasa: • वरुणस्य इयं वारुणीमान् तस्य इव अविह्वला।
wordByWord: वारुण्याः — of Varuni liquor; of Varuna-related power
summary: i. One who is perturbed by the intoxicating liquor, the wine of spiritual bliss.

ii. Varuni is a kind of liquor obtained from the date fruit and is so called because the god Varuna is fond of it. The expression indicates forgetting external objects and simply enjoying; the name Svatmarama may be compared.
iii. According to Taittiriya Upanishad, the Brahma Vidya taught by Varuna to Bhrigu is called Varuni.
iv. The name may also be split as Varunimat and Avihvala: by her grace Adishesha, connected with the region of Varuna, is not fatigued in supporting the universe.
v. Varuni is also a nadi connected with Vayu in the body. One who has conquered it through yoga is called Varuniman. Sreedevee removes weariness and exhaustion from devotees.
searchText: 333 वारुणीमदविह्वला • वारुण्याः मदेन विह्वला। • वरुणस्य इयं वारुणीमान् तस्य इव अविह्वला। वारुण्याः — of varuni liquor; of varuna-related power मदेन — by intoxication; by bliss विह्वला — overwhelmed; perturbed अविह्वला — not fatigued or exhausted i. one who is perturbed by the intoxicating liquor, the wine of spiritual bliss. ii. varuni is a kind of liquor obtained from the date fruit and is so called because the god varuna is fond of it. the expression indicates forgetting external objects and simply enjoying; the name svatmarama may be compared. iii. according to taittiriya upanishad, the brahma vidya taught by varuna to bhrigu is called varuni. iv. the name may also be split as varunimat and avihvala: by her grace adishesha, connected with the region of varuna, is not fatigued in supporting the universe. v. varuni is also a nadi connected with vayu in the body. one who has conquered it through yoga is called varuniman. sreedevee removes weariness and exhaustion from devotees. bhāskararāya elevates the concept of vāruṇī: the solar nectar: वारुणी refers to the purifying internal solar energy that flows through the suṣumṇā channel during high-level yoga. the "intoxication" (mada) is the state of spiritual ecstasy where the outer world is forgotten, and the mind dissolves into pure light, a state that she governs and embodies.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya elevates the concept of Vāruṇī:

The Solar Nectar: वारुणी refers to the purifying internal solar energy that flows through the Suṣumṇā channel during high-level yoga. The "intoxication" (mada) is the state of spiritual ecstasy where the outer world is forgotten, and the mind dissolves into pure light, a state that She governs and embodies.

name: विश्वाधिका
wordByWord: विश्वाधिका — she who transcends the universe
summary: i. One who transcends the whole world.

ii. She is beyond the thirty-six tattvas from earth up to Shiva. The Veda speaks of the Rudra who is beyond the universe. Since she is the united form of Shiva and Shakti and is in the form of Sadakhya-tattva, she is Vishvadhika.
iii. Sreedevee is beyond waking, dream, and deep sleep states, and even beyond the turiya state. Names from Vishvarupa to Sarvavastha-vivarjita may be referred to.
searchText: 334 विश्वाधिका • विश्वाधिका। विश्वस्मात् अधिका। विश्वस्मात् — than the universe अधिका — greater; beyond विश्वाधिका — she who transcends the universe i. one who transcends the whole world. ii. she is beyond the thirty-six tattvas from earth up to shiva. the veda speaks of the rudra who is beyond the universe. since she is the united form of shiva and shakti and is in the form of sadakhya-tattva, she is vishvadhika. iii. sreedevee is beyond waking, dream, and deep sleep states, and even beyond the turiya state. names from vishvarupa to sarvavastha-vivarjita may be referred to. in the saubhāgyabhāskara, bhāskararāya clarifies her concurrent immanence and transcendence: surpassing the manifested: though the universe is her own physical projection, she is not limited by it. she is viśvādhikā because her essential nature remains completely untouched by cosmic mutations, existing infinitely beyond the boundaries of cosmic space and matter.
esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara, Bhāskararāya clarifies Her concurrent immanence and transcendence:

Surpassing the Manifested: Though the universe is Her own physical projection, She is not limited by it. She is Viśvādhikā because Her essential nature remains completely untouched by cosmic mutations, existing infinitely beyond the boundaries of cosmic space and matter.

name: वेदवेद्या
wordByWord: वेदवेद्या — she who is known through the Vedas
summary: i. One who can be known through the Vedas.
ii. She can be realised through the Rig, Yajur, Sama, and Atharva Vedas. In the Bhagavad Gita, Lord Krishna says: by all the Vedas I alone am to be known.
iii. In Sree Nagara, the dwelling place of Sreedevee, the four Vedas are the four entrances. Only by passing through these entrances can one reach Sreedevee.

iv. In the centre of the Sree Chakra, in the Bindu, the Amnaya deities are worshipped. They are goddesses connected with the four Vedas, and since she is recognised by these Vidyas, Sreedevee is Vedavedya.
searchText: 335 वेदवेद्या • वेदैः वेद्या। वेदैः — by the vedas वेद्या — to be known; knowable वेदवेद्या — she who is known through the vedas i. one who can be known through the vedas. ii. she can be realised through the rig, yajur, sama, and atharva vedas. in the bhagavad gita, lord krishna says: by all the vedas i alone am to be known. iii. in sree nagara, the dwelling place of sreedevee, the four vedas are the four entrances. only by passing through these entrances can one reach sreedevee. iv. in the centre of the sree chakra, in the bindu, the amnaya deities are worshipped. they are goddesses connected with the four vedas, and since she is recognised by these vidyas, sreedevee is vedavedya. bhāskararāya connects this directly to the brahma sūtra ("शास्त्रयोनित्वात्"): the ultimate subject of revelation: the four vedas, along with all their secret upaniṣads, have only one ultimate subject matter. though they describe various rituals and lesser deities, their final corporate intent is to reveal her—the supreme, non-dual consciousness.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya connects this directly to the Brahma Sūtra ("शास्त्रयोनित्वात्"):

The Ultimate Subject of Revelation: The four Vedas, along with all their secret Upaniṣads, have only one ultimate subject matter. Though they describe various rituals and lesser deities, their final corporate intent is to reveal Her—the supreme, non-dual consciousness.

name: विन्ध्याचलनिवासिनी
wordByWord: विन्ध्याचलनिवासिनी — she who dwells in the Vindhya mountain
summary: i. One who resides in the Vindhya mountains.

ii. In Saptashati and Pancharatra Lakshmi Tantra it is said that Sreedevee incarnated during Vaivasvata Manvantara in the house of Nandagopa, from the womb of Yashoda, to destroy the demons Shumbha and Nishumbha, and then dwelt in the Vindhya mountains.
searchText: 336 विन्ध्याचलनिवासिनी • विन्ध्याख्ये अचले निवासतीति। विन्ध्याख्ये — called vindhya अचले — mountain निवसति — dwells विन्ध्याचलनिवासिनी — she who dwells in the vindhya mountain i. one who resides in the vindhya mountains. ii. in saptashati and pancharatra lakshmi tantra it is said that sreedevee incarnated during vaivasvata manvantara in the house of nandagopa, from the womb of yashoda, to destroy the demons shumbha and nishumbha, and then dwelt in the vindhya mountains. bhāskararāya interprets this location across geographical and internal landscapes: the historical abode: mythologically, she resides in the thick forests of the vindhya mountain range as the fierce, protective deity. the unshakable base: esoterically, acala means that which does not move. the vindhya range represents the firm, unmoving, grounded station of the mūladhāra chakra at the base of the spine, where she sits anchored before her ascent.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya interprets this location across geographical and internal landscapes:
The Historical Abode: Mythologically, She resides in the thick forests of the Vindhya mountain range as the fierce, protective deity.

The Unshakable Base: Esoterically, Acala means that which does not move. The Vindhya range represents the firm, unmoving, grounded station of the Mūladhāra chakra at the base of the spine, where She sits anchored before Her ascent.

name: विधात्री
wordByWord: विधात्री — supporter; ordainer; consort of Brahma
summary: i. One who supports this universe.
ii. Since she supports and nourishes the universe, she is Vidhatri. The later name Jagaddhatri conveys the same sense.
iii. Vidhata means Brahma; his consort is Vidhatri.
iv. The word may also be connected with dhatri, myrobalan, because she is fond of myrobalans.

v. Dhatri also means mother, who bears the child in her womb and releases it at the proper time. A great mother is Vidhatri; names such as Sreemata, Mata, Janani, Prasavitri, and Amba may be referred to.
searchText: 337 विधात्री • विदधातीति। • विधातुः पत्नी वा। विदधाति — supports; arranges; ordains विधातुः — of vidhata, brahma पत्नी — wife; consort विधात्री — supporter; ordainer; consort of brahma i. one who supports this universe. ii. since she supports and nourishes the universe, she is vidhatri. the later name jagaddhatri conveys the same sense. iii. vidhata means brahma; his consort is vidhatri. iv. the word may also be connected with dhatri, myrobalan, because she is fond of myrobalans. v. dhatri also means mother, who bears the child in her womb and releases it at the proper time. a great mother is vidhatri; names such as sreemata, mata, janani, prasavitri, and amba may be referred to. bhāskararāya focuses on her absolute structural governance: the ordainer of fate: she is the supreme manager who designs, coordinates, and dispenses the individual lines of destiny (karma) for every single living being in the cosmos, performing this function with absolute justice.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya focuses on Her absolute structural governance:

The Ordainer of Fate: She is the supreme manager who designs, coordinates, and dispenses the individual lines of destiny (karma) for every single living being in the cosmos, performing this function with absolute justice.

name: वेदजननी
summary: i. One who is the mother of the Vedas.

ii. Since the Vedas themselves arise from her and reveal her, she is the mother of the Vedas. As the source of sacred knowledge and as the one who is known through the Vedas, she is called Vedajanani.
searchText: 338 वेदजननी • वेदानां जननी। वेदानाम् — of the vedas जननी — mother; source वेदजननी — mother of the vedas i. one who is the mother of the vedas. ii. since the vedas themselves arise from her and reveal her, she is the mother of the vedas. as the source of sacred knowledge and as the one who is known through the vedas, she is called vedajanani. bhāskararāya traces the origin of revelation back to her breath: the source of revelation: the vedas are eternal (apauruṣeya), meaning they are not authored by human minds. bhāskararāya notes that they emanate effortlessly as her natural breath at the beginning of each cosmic dawn, proving that she is the parent matrix of all sacred text and sound.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya traces the origin of revelation back to Her breath:

The Source of Revelation: The Vedas are eternal (Apauruṣeya), meaning they are not authored by human minds. Bhāskararāya notes that they emanate effortlessly as Her natural breath at the beginning of each cosmic dawn, proving that She is the parent matrix of all sacred text and sound.

name: विष्णुमाया
wordByWord: माया — power of manifestation; divine power
summary: i. One who is the Maya of Vishnu.

ii. Vishnu is the all-pervading principle, and Maya is the power through which names, forms, and the universe are projected. Sreedevee, as the power of Vishnu, is called Vishnumaya. This also indicates that the sustaining function of the universe operates through her power.
searchText: 339 विष्णुमाया • विष्णोः माया। विष्णोः — of vishnu माया — power of manifestation; divine power विष्णुमाया — the maya of vishnu i. one who is the maya of vishnu. ii. vishnu is the all-pervading principle, and maya is the power through which names, forms, and the universe are projected. sreedevee, as the power of vishnu, is called vishnumaya. this also indicates that the sustaining function of the universe operates through her power. bhāskararāya clarifies her role as the grand cosmic veil: the projective illusion: she is that supreme, mysterious power of viṣṇu mentioned in the devī māhātmya ("या देवी सर्वभूतेषु विष्णुमायेति शब्दिता"). she casts the blanket of name and form over the absolute consciousness, making the non-dual appear as dual, while simultaneously holding the key to removing that very illusion.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya clarifies Her role as the grand cosmic veil:

The Projective Illusion: She is that supreme, mysterious power of Viṣṇu mentioned in the Devī Māhātmya ("या देवी सर्वभूतेषु विष्णुमायेति शब्दिता"). She casts the blanket of name and form over the absolute consciousness, making the non-dual appear as dual, while simultaneously holding the key to removing that very illusion.

name: विलासिनी
wordByWord: विलासः — sport; graceful play; shining expression
विलासिनी — she who possesses divine sport or graceful play
summary: i. One who is sportive and graceful.

ii. Vilasa denotes play, graceful movement, and manifestation. The universe is her divine play, and her beauty, actions, and manifestations are expressions of that play. She is therefore called Vilasini.
searchText: 340 विलासिनी • विलासः अस्याः अस्तीति। विलासः — sport; graceful play; shining expression अस्याः — of her अस्ति — exists विलासिनी — she who possesses divine sport or graceful play i. one who is sportive and graceful. ii. vilasa denotes play, graceful movement, and manifestation. the universe is her divine play, and her beauty, actions, and manifestations are expressions of that play. she is therefore called vilasini. in the saubhāgyabhāskara, this name defines her relationship with the world process: the universe as play: the entire projection, maintenance, and collapse of the infinite worlds is not an arduous task for her; it is her effortless, joyful play (vilāsa). she is the ultimate actress enjoying the performance of her own cosmic theater.
esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara, this name defines Her relationship with the world process:

The Universe as Play: The entire projection, maintenance, and collapse of the infinite worlds is not an arduous task for Her; it is Her effortless, joyful play (vilāsa). She is the ultimate actress enjoying the performance of Her own cosmic theater.

name: क्षेत्रस्वरूपा
wordByWord: क्षेत्रस्वरूपा — she whose own form is the field
summary: i. One whose body is matter, the body of all beings.

ii. Kshetram is the field ready for cultivation through removal of weeds, ploughing, and watering. Similarly, a place made ready through mantra, tantra, and yantra for attaining godliness is called kshetra.
iii. The thirty-six categories, from earth to Shiva, form her body. That is why Parameshvara is called Kshetrajna. The Bhagavad Gita describes the relation between kshetra and kshetrajna.
searchText: 341 क्षेत्रस्वरूपा • क्षेत्रं स्वं रूपं यस्याः सा। क्षेत्रं — field; body; matter स्वं रूपं — own form यस्याः — of whom; whose क्षेत्रस्वरूपा — she whose own form is the field i. one whose body is matter, the body of all beings. ii. kshetram is the field ready for cultivation through removal of weeds, ploughing, and watering. similarly, a place made ready through mantra, tantra, and yantra for attaining godliness is called kshetra. iii. the thirty-six categories, from earth to shiva, form her body. that is why parameshvara is called kshetrajna. the bhagavad gita describes the relation between kshetra and kshetrajna. bhāskararāya interprets this directly through the 13th chapter of the bhagavad gītā: the material field: क्षेत्र represents the entire mutable universe, the physical body, and the field of matter (prakṛti). she clarifies that the physical universe is not a separate, dirty element to be discarded; it is her own visible, tangible form.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya interprets this directly through the 13th chapter of the Bhagavad Gītā:

The Material Field: क्षेत्र represents the entire mutable universe, the physical body, and the field of matter (Prakṛti). She clarifies that the physical universe is not a separate, dirty element to be discarded; it is Her own visible, tangible form.

name: क्षेत्रेशी
wordByWord: क्षेत्रस्य — of the field; of matter
तस्य इयम् — she who belongs to him; his power or consort
क्षेत्रेशी — ruler of the field; consort of Kshetesha
summary: i. One who is the ruler of matter, or the consort of Kshetesha, Shiva.
ii. There is absolutely no difference between Sreedevee and Parameshvara. The Tripura Upanishad is remembered in this context as saying that Shakti and Bhagavan are one in essence.
searchText: 342 क्षेत्रेशी • क्षेत्रस्य यः ईशः तस्य इयम्। क्षेत्रस्य — of the field; of matter ईशः — lord; ruler तस्य इयम् — she who belongs to him; his power or consort क्षेत्रेशी — ruler of the field; consort of kshetesha i. one who is the ruler of matter, or the consort of kshetesha, shiva. ii. there is absolutely no difference between sreedevee and parameshvara. the tripura upanishad is remembered in this context as saying that shakti and bhagavan are one in essence. bhāskararāya establishes her governance over matter: the owner of the field: while she manifests as the physical field (kṣetra), she simultaneously functions as its absolute ruler (īśvarī). she commands the laws of nature, biology, and chemistry, ensuring that the field operates in perfect synchronization.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya establishes Her governance over matter:

The Owner of the Field: While She manifests as the physical field (Kṣetra), She simultaneously functions as its absolute ruler (Īśvarī). She commands the laws of nature, biology, and chemistry, ensuring that the field operates in perfect synchronization.

name: क्षेत्रक्षेत्रज्ञपालिनी
wordByWord: क्षेत्रक्षेत्रज्ञपालिनी — she who protects both matter and the knower of matter
summary: i. One who is the protector of matter and the knower of matter.
ii. Kshetram is the body and Kshetrajna is the soul. She protects both; the Bhagavad Gita may be referred to.
iii. Linga Purana says that the twenty-four tattvas are kshetras and the purusha who enjoys them is the kshetrajna. Sreedevee alone takes the form of all these kshetras and kshetrajnas.
searchText: 343 क्षेत्रक्षेत्रज्ञपालिनी • क्षेत्रं क्षेत्रज्ञं च पालयतीति। क्षेत्रं — field; body; matter क्षेत्रज्ञं — knower of the field च — and पालयति — protects क्षेत्रक्षेत्रज्ञपालिनी — she who protects both matter and the knower of matter i. one who is the protector of matter and the knower of matter. ii. kshetram is the body and kshetrajna is the soul. she protects both; the bhagavad gita may be referred to. iii. linga purana says that the twenty-four tattvas are kshetras and the purusha who enjoys them is the kshetrajna. sreedevee alone takes the form of all these kshetras and kshetrajnas. bhāskararāya details her dual protective custody: sustaining matter and soul: she sustains the objective physical world (kṣetra) so that experiences can occur, and she simultaneously guards the subjective individual soul (kṣetrajña) on its long journey through saṁsāra, ensuring its eventual awakening to its own true nature.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya details Her dual protective custody:

Sustaining Matter and Soul: She sustains the objective physical world (kṣetra) so that experiences can occur, and She simultaneously guards the subjective individual soul (kṣetrajña) on its long journey through saṁsāra, ensuring its eventual awakening to its own true nature.

name: क्षयवृद्धिविनिर्मुक्ता
wordByWord: क्षयवृद्धिविनिर्मुक्ता — she who is free from growth and decay
summary: i. One who is free from decay and growth.

ii. Growth and decay belong to kshetra. Though she is related to kshetra and is the ruler and protector of kshetra and kshetrajna, she is free from growth and decay. The Bhagavad Gita says that weapons do not cut the soul, fire does not burn it, water does not wet it, and wind does not dry it.

iii. She is free from those actions which are the result of actions, and she is not connected with actions. Brihadaranyaka Upanishad says that this is the eternal glory of the knower of Brahman: he neither increases by actions nor decreases by bad actions.
searchText: 344 क्षयवृद्धिविनिर्मुक्ता • क्षयवृद्धिभ्यां विनिर्मुक्ता। क्षय — decay; reduction वृद्धि — growth; increase विनिर्मुक्ता — completely free from क्षयवृद्धिविनिर्मुक्ता — she who is free from growth and decay i. one who is free from decay and growth. ii. growth and decay belong to kshetra. though she is related to kshetra and is the ruler and protector of kshetra and kshetrajna, she is free from growth and decay. the bhagavad gita says that weapons do not cut the soul, fire does not burn it, water does not wet it, and wind does not dry it. iii. she is free from those actions which are the result of actions, and she is not connected with actions. brihadaranyaka upanishad says that this is the eternal glory of the knower of brahman: he neither increases by actions nor decreases by bad actions. in the saubhāgyabhāskara, bhāskararāya highlights her immutability (nirvikāra): beyond material mutations: decay (kṣaya) and growth (vṛddhi) are two of the six structural mutations (ṣaḍ-bhāva-vikāra) that apply strictly to material objects and physical bodies. as the eternal, changeless consciousness background, she undergoes no modifications, remaining constant across all time.
esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara, Bhāskararāya highlights Her immutability (Nirvikāra):

Beyond Material Mutations: Decay (kṣaya) and growth (vṛddhi) are two of the six structural mutations (ṣaḍ-bhāva-vikāra) that apply strictly to material objects and physical bodies. As the eternal, changeless consciousness background, She undergoes no modifications, remaining constant across all time.

name: क्षेत्रपालसमर्चिता
wordByWord: क्षेत्रपालसमर्चिता — she who is well worshipped by Kshetrapala
summary: i. One who is adored by Kshetrapala.

ii. As per Linga and other Puranas, Kali was created by Shiva to slay Darukasura. After killing the demon her wrath did not subside, and Shiva assumed the form of a crying infant. She suckled the child, who drank the fire of her anger with milk. This child is called Kshetrapala, an incarnation of Shiva, and Sreedevee was prayed to by him.

iii. In the results part of this Sahasranamam it is said that Kshetrapala kills anyone who snatches the wealth of those who recite this Sahasranamam. In this way Kshetrapala protects the devotees. She was worshipped by that Kshetrapala.
iv. Literally, kshetra is the precinct of the sacrificial ceremony and pala is the protector. Kshetrapala protects sacrificial ceremonies, and she was worshipped by that Kshetrapala.
v. Kshetra is also the body. The protectors in this body are fire, air, sun, and so on. She is worshipped by them.
searchText: 345 क्षेत्रपालसमर्चिता • क्षेत्रं पालयति तेन समयग् अर्चिता। क्षेत्रं — field; sacred precinct; body पालः — protector सम्यक् अर्चिता — well worshipped क्षेत्रपालसमर्चिता — she who is well worshipped by kshetrapala i. one who is adored by kshetrapala. ii. as per linga and other puranas, kali was created by shiva to slay darukasura. after killing the demon her wrath did not subside, and shiva assumed the form of a crying infant. she suckled the child, who drank the fire of her anger with milk. this child is called kshetrapala, an incarnation of shiva, and sreedevee was prayed to by him. iii. in the results part of this sahasranamam it is said that kshetrapala kills anyone who snatches the wealth of those who recite this sahasranamam. in this way kshetrapala protects the devotees. she was worshipped by that kshetrapala. iv. literally, kshetra is the precinct of the sacrificial ceremony and pala is the protector. kshetrapala protects sacrificial ceremonies, and she was worshipped by that kshetrapala. v. kshetra is also the body. the protectors in this body are fire, air, sun, and so on. she is worshipped by them. bhāskararāya decodes this through both ritual tradition and subtle yoga: the guardian's adoration: mythologically, kṣetrapāla is the terrifying bhairava form assigned to guard the boundaries of her temples and sacred zones. the guardian of the gate: esoterically, he represents the structural boundary intelligence guarding the entry to the higher spiritual channels. even this supreme guardian must worship her to maintain his office.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya decodes this through both ritual tradition and subtle yoga:
