The Guardian's Adoration: Mythologically, Kṣetrapāla is the terrifying Bhairava form assigned to guard the boundaries of Her temples and sacred zones.

The Guardian of the Gate: Esoterically, he represents the structural boundary intelligence guarding the entry to the higher spiritual channels. Even this supreme guardian must worship Her to maintain his office.

name: विजया
wordByWord: विजया — she whose victory is special; victorious one
summary: i. One who is victorious.

ii. Vijaya means special victory. She is victorious over all obstacles, demons, ignorance, and the forces opposed to dharma. The name also indicates the victory obtained by devotees through her grace.
searchText: 346 विजया • विशिष्टः जयः यस्याः सा। विशिष्टः — special; excellent जयः — victory यस्याः — of whom; whose विजया — she whose victory is special; victorious one i. one who is victorious. ii. vijaya means special victory. she is victorious over all obstacles, demons, ignorance, and the forces opposed to dharma. the name also indicates the victory obtained by devotees through her grace. bhāskararāya interprets her victorious nature as absolute and unconditioned: the triumphant power: her victory is not a temporary triumph over a rival; it is her fundamental state of being. as the supreme reality, she remains forever victorious over the dark forces of ignorance (avidyā), delusion, and death, guaranteeing victory to those who align with her.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya interprets Her victorious nature as absolute and unconditioned:

The Triumphant Power: Her victory is not a temporary triumph over a rival; it is Her fundamental state of being. As the supreme reality, She remains forever victorious over the dark forces of ignorance (avidyā), delusion, and death, guaranteeing victory to those who align with Her.

name: विमला
wordByWord: विमला — she who is free from impurity
summary: i. One who is without impurity.
ii. Mala means impurity. Since no impurity belongs to her, she is Vimala. She is pure consciousness, untouched by the impurities that bind the soul.
searchText: 347 विमला • विगतः मलः यस्याः सा। विगतः — gone; removed मलः — impurity; blemish यस्याः — of whom; whose विमला — she who is free from impurity i. one who is without impurity. ii. mala means impurity. since no impurity belongs to her, she is vimala. she is pure consciousness, untouched by the impurities that bind the soul. bhāskararāya defines mala according to the three structural impurities of the agamas: free from the three impurities: she is completely untouched by the three fundamental taints: आणवमल (āṇava-mala — the stain of limitation/ego), मायीयमल (māyīya-mala — the stain of illusion/differentiated perception), and कार्ममल (kārma-mala — the stain of binding action). she remains pure, unblemished awareness.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya defines Mala according to the three structural impurities of the Agamas:

Free from the Three Impurities: She is completely untouched by the three fundamental taints: आणवमल (āṇava-mala — the stain of limitation/ego), मायीयमल (māyīya-mala — the stain of illusion/differentiated perception), and कार्ममल (kārma-mala — the stain of binding action). She remains pure, unblemished awareness.

name: वन्द्या
wordByWord: वन्दितुम् — to be saluted; to be worshipped
summary: i. One who is worthy of worship.
ii. She is to be worshipped by all, because she is the source, support, and goal of the universe. Devas, sages, and devotees bow to her.
searchText: 348 वन्द्या • वन्दितुं योग्या। वन्दितुम् — to be saluted; to be worshipped योग्या — worthy वन्द्या — worthy of worship i. one who is worthy of worship. ii. she is to be worshipped by all, because she is the source, support, and goal of the universe. devas, sages, and devotees bow to her. bhāskararāya notes the absolute necessity of bowing before her: the object of universal salutation: she is the baseline source of all life and light. therefore, she is the natural, ultimate point of adoration for all living beings, from the simple seeker to the highest cosmic deities. bowing to her is the method to dissolve the rigid individual ego.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya notes the absolute necessity of bowing before Her:

The Object of Universal Salutation: She is the baseline source of all life and light. Therefore, She is the natural, ultimate point of adoration for all living beings, from the simple seeker to the highest cosmic deities. Bowing to Her is the method to dissolve the rigid individual ego.

name: वन्दारुजनवत्सला
wordByWord: वन्दारून् — those who bow or worship
वन्दारुजनवत्सला — she who loves and protects worshipping people like her children
summary: i. One who is affectionate to those who bow to her.
ii. Vatsala is one who shows love like a mother toward her child. Sreedevee shows such tenderness to worshippers and protects those who bow down to her.
searchText: 349 वन्दारुजनवत्सला • वन्दारून् जनान् वत्सवत् लातीति। वन्दारून् — those who bow or worship जनान् — people वत्सवत् — like calves; with affection लाति — protects; nourishes वन्दारुजनवत्सला — she who loves and protects worshipping people like her children i. one who is affectionate to those who bow to her. ii. vatsala is one who shows love like a mother toward her child. sreedevee shows such tenderness to worshippers and protects those who bow down to her. in the saubhāgyabhāskara, bhāskararāya highlights her overwhelming maternal instinct: the maternal reflex: वत्सला (vatsalā) describes the pure, instinctive love a cow has for her newborn calf, rushing to protect it and feeding it unconditionally. she displays this exact spontaneous, fiercely protective grace toward any devotee who drops their pride and bows before her.
esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara, Bhāskararāya highlights Her overwhelming maternal instinct:

The Maternal Reflex: वत्सला (vatsalā) describes the pure, instinctive love a cow has for her newborn calf, rushing to protect it and feeding it unconditionally. She displays this exact spontaneous, fiercely protective grace toward any devotee who drops their pride and bows before Her.

name: वाग्वादिनी
wordByWord: वादयति — causes to speak; makes speech function
वाग्वादिनी — she who speaks, and who causes speech to arise
summary: i. One who speaks, or who causes speech.
ii. She is the power behind speech and the one who enables speech to function. As Vak, she is the source of words, meaning, and expression.
searchText: 350 वाग्वादिनी • वाचं वदतीति। • वाचं वादयतीति। वाचम् — speech; word वदति — speaks वादयति — causes to speak; makes speech function वाग्वादिनी — she who speaks, and who causes speech to arise i. one who speaks, or who causes speech. ii. she is the power behind speech and the one who enables speech to function. as vak, she is the source of words, meaning, and expression. bhāskararāya decodes her identity as the animator of intelligence: the power of articulation: she is the underlying force that causes speech and language to manifest. bhāskararāya notes that she does not just speak; she sits as the inner voice that inspires and translates raw thought into articulate speech inside the intellects of poets, scholars, and seers.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya decodes Her identity as the animator of intelligence:

The Power of Articulation: She is the underlying force that causes speech and language to manifest. Bhāskararāya notes that She does not just speak; She sits as the inner voice that inspires and translates raw thought into articulate speech inside the intellects of poets, scholars, and seers.

name: वामकेशी
samasa: • वामाः एव वामकाः ये तेषाम् ईशस्य पत्नी।
wordByWord: ईशस्य पत्नी — consort of the Lord
वामकेशी — she with beautiful hair; consort of the Lord of the Vamaka group
summary: i. One who has beautiful hair.
ii. The name also admits another interpretation connected with Vamaka and the Lord. In the simple sense, her hair is beautiful and auspicious.
searchText: 351 वामकेशी • वामाः केशाः यस्याः सा। • वामाः एव वामकाः ये तेषाम् ईशस्य पत्नी। वामाः — beautiful; also left-sided केशाः — hair यस्याः — of whom; whose ईशस्य पत्नी — consort of the lord वामकेशी — she with beautiful hair; consort of the lord of the vamaka group i. one who has beautiful hair. ii. the name also admits another interpretation connected with vamaka and the lord. in the simple sense, her hair is beautiful and auspicious. bhāskararāya illuminates her position within the agamic divisions: the consort of vāmakeśvara: in the tantric corporate architecture, she is the primary deity of the vāmakeśvara tantra, one of the most core source texts of the śrīvidyā tradition. her "hair" represents the infinite, uncoiled rays of cosmic energy that stream out to form the manifest worlds.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya illuminates Her position within the Agamic divisions:

The Consort of Vāmakeśvara: In the Tantric corporate architecture, She is the primary deity of the Vāmakeśvara Tantra, one of the most core source texts of the Śrīvidyā tradition. Her "hair" represents the infinite, uncoiled rays of cosmic energy that stream out to form the manifest worlds.

name: वह्निमण्डलवासिनी
wordByWord: वह्निमण्डलवासिनी — she who dwells in the circle of fire
summary: i. One who dwells in the circle of fire.
ii. Fire is a sacred and purifying principle. She is contemplated as dwelling in the fire-orb, and the worship connected with fire and inner radiance is indicated by this name.
searchText: 352 वह्निमण्डलवासिनी • वह्नेः यत् मण्डलं तत्र वसतीति। वह्नेः — of fire मण्डलं — circle; orb तत्र — there वसति — dwells वह्निमण्डलवासिनी — she who dwells in the circle of fire i. one who dwells in the circle of fire. ii. fire is a sacred and purifying principle. she is contemplated as dwelling in the fire-orb, and the worship connected with fire and inner radiance is indicated by this name. bhāskararāya interprets this across structural yogic levels: the fire center: in the internal layout of kuṇḍalinī yoga, the वह्निमण्डल represents the mūladhāra and maṇipūra regions where the primal digestive and spiritual fire resides. she stays hidden inside this internal fire, functioning as the spark that purifies the practitioner's gross karmic elements during her ascent.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya interprets this across structural yogic levels:

The Fire Center: In the internal layout of Kuṇḍalinī Yoga, the वह्निमण्डल represents the Mūladhāra and Maṇipūra regions where the primal digestive and spiritual fire resides. She stays hidden inside this internal fire, functioning as the spark that purifies the practitioner's gross karmic elements during Her ascent.

name: भक्तिमत्कल्पलतिका
wordByWord: भक्तिमताम् — of those who possess devotion
भक्तिमत्कल्पलतिका — she who is like a wish-fulfilling creeper for devotees
summary: i. One who is like a wish-fulfilling creeper to the devotees.

ii. Kalpalatika means a creeper that grants what is desired. She fulfils the true aspirations of devotees. Latika also suggests a fragrant, mind-soothing creeper; Sreedevee is like such a soothing creeper to her devotees.
searchText: 353 भक्तिमत्कल्पलतिका • भक्तिमतां कल्पलतिका इव। भक्तिमताम् — of those who possess devotion कल्पलतिका — wish-fulfilling creeper इव — like भक्तिमत्कल्पलतिका — she who is like a wish-fulfilling creeper for devotees i. one who is like a wish-fulfilling creeper to the devotees. ii. kalpalatika means a creeper that grants what is desired. she fulfils the true aspirations of devotees. latika also suggests a fragrant, mind-soothing creeper; sreedevee is like such a soothing creeper to her devotees. bhāskararāya uses a celestial botanical metaphor to explain her responsiveness: the wishing creeper: the mythological kalpa-latā yields any fruit immediately upon being asked. similarly, she wraps around the consciousness of those who possess genuine devotion (bhaktimat), effortlessly dropping whatever fruits they require—whether material security or the ultimate fruit of liberation.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya uses a celestial botanical metaphor to explain Her responsiveness:

The Wishing Creeper: The mythological Kalpa-latā yields any fruit immediately upon being asked. Similarly, She wraps around the consciousness of those who possess genuine devotion (bhaktimat), effortlessly dropping whatever fruits they require—whether material security or the ultimate fruit of liberation.

name: पशुपाशविमोचनी
samasa: • पशुपस्य आशा येषां ते पशुपाशाः। तान् विशेषण मोचयतीति।
wordByWord: पशोः — of the pashu, the bound soul or ignorant being
पशुपाशविमोचनी — she who releases bound souls from their bonds
summary: i. One who releases the ignorant from bondage.

ii. Pashus are those who are devoid of the conviction of non-separation. Brihadaranyaka Upanishad says that one who worships another deity thinking that the deity is different and he himself is different does not know; he is like a pashu.

iii. Pa means thirst and asa means hunger. Pasha therefore indicates hunger and thirst. These arise from five afflictions, and these afflictions themselves are bindings. On the whole there are seven bondages. Sreedevee releases ignorant people from these bondages. They are called pashus because they are concerned only with eating and drinking.

iv. The five afflictions are: avidya, the absence of discrimination between the Self and not-Self; asmita, egoism; raga, desire for worldly things; dvesha, anger or hatred when desire is obstructed; and abhinivesha, deep attachment even to what one knows is not beneficial. These are explained in Yoga Sutra and also in Devi Bhagavatam and Linga Purana under different names.

v. Kularnava Tantra mentions eight afflictions such as cruelty, doubt, fear, shame, disgust, race, fame, and caste. Bhaskararaya notes that the divisions become very many and does not expand all sub-divisions further.

vi. According to the Katapayadi method, pasha indicates the number fifty-one, though it may also be taken as fifty-two in some explanations. The point is that she releases the devotees from all these afflictions, whether counted as fifty-one, fifty-two, or otherwise.

vii. The afflictions are also considered as three types: atomic impurity, difference, and action. Those bound by them are classified in different ways in Pratyabhijna Shastra, Shiva Sutra, Nityahridaya, and Svachchanda Tantra. Liberation from these bonds is possible only through the liberating energy of Sreedevee; without that energy Shiva does not act. Thus she is Pashupasha-vimochani.
searchText: 354 पशुपाशविमोचनी • पशोः पाशं विमोचयतीति। • पशूनां पाशे (पिपासाशनाये) विशिष्य मोचयतीति। • पशुपस्य आशा येषां ते पशुपाशाः। तान् विशेषण मोचयतीति। पशोः — of the pashu, the bound soul or ignorant being पाशं — bondage; fetter विमोचयति — releases completely पशूनाम् — of ignorant beings पिपासा-अशनाये — thirst and hunger विशिष्य — especially; completely पशुपाशविमोचनी — she who releases bound souls from their bonds i. one who releases the ignorant from bondage. ii. pashus are those who are devoid of the conviction of non-separation. brihadaranyaka upanishad says that one who worships another deity thinking that the deity is different and he himself is different does not know; he is like a pashu. iii. pa means thirst and asa means hunger. pasha therefore indicates hunger and thirst. these arise from five afflictions, and these afflictions themselves are bindings. on the whole there are seven bondages. sreedevee releases ignorant people from these bondages. they are called pashus because they are concerned only with eating and drinking. iv. the five afflictions are: avidya, the absence of discrimination between the self and not-self; asmita, egoism; raga, desire for worldly things; dvesha, anger or hatred when desire is obstructed; and abhinivesha, deep attachment even to what one knows is not beneficial. these are explained in yoga sutra and also in devi bhagavatam and linga purana under different names. v. kularnava tantra mentions eight afflictions such as cruelty, doubt, fear, shame, disgust, race, fame, and caste. bhaskararaya notes that the divisions become very many and does not expand all sub-divisions further. vi. according to the katapayadi method, pasha indicates the number fifty-one, though it may also be taken as fifty-two in some explanations. the point is that she releases the devotees from all these afflictions, whether counted as fifty-one, fifty-two, or otherwise. vii. the afflictions are also considered as three types: atomic impurity, difference, and action. those bound by them are classified in different ways in pratyabhijna shastra, shiva sutra, nityahridaya, and svachchanda tantra. liberation from these bonds is possible only through the liberating energy of sreedevee; without that energy shiva does not act. thus she is pashupasha-vimochani. bhāskararāya provides an extensive breakdown using classical śaiva/śākta agamic terminology: the soul and the tether: पशु (paśu) refers to the individual soul, bound because it behaves like an animal driven by instinct. पाश (pāśa) is the rope or tether consisting of ignorance, ego, and karma that keeps the soul tied to the post of saṁsāra. the act of liberation: as paśupāśavimocanī, she cuts these bindings cleanly, transforming the bound creature (paśu) into the absolute lord (pati), granting immediate spiritual freedom.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya provides an extensive breakdown using classical Śaiva/Śākta Agamic terminology:

The Soul and the Tether: पशु (Paśu) refers to the individual soul, bound because it behaves like an animal driven by instinct. पाश (Pāśa) is the rope or tether consisting of ignorance, ego, and karma that keeps the soul tied to the post of saṁsāra.
The Act of Liberation: As Paśupāśavimocanī, She cuts these bindings cleanly, transforming the bound creature (Paśu) into the absolute lord (Pati), granting immediate spiritual freedom.

name: संहृताशेषपाषण्डा
wordByWord: पाषण्डाः — heretics; deniers of sacred dharma
संहृताशेषपाषण्डा — she by whom all heresy is destroyed
summary: i. One who is the destroyer of all heretics, those who are averse to spiritual values.

ii. Linga Purana describes heretics as those who observe vows not enjoined in the Vedas and those excluded from ceremonies enjoined by Shruti and Smriti. Such persons are not to mingle with or be spoken to by the twice-born.

iii. Brahma Vaivarta Purana also says that Puranas, Nyaya, Mimamsa, Dharma Shastras, the six Vedangas, and the four Vedas are the seats of righteousness. Whatever is imagined by people beyond these is heresy; she destroys all such pashandas.
iv. In some schools the word is written as Pakhanda, with the same meaning: pa, the Vedas, and khanda, cutting off. She destroys such pakhanda also.
searchText: 355 संहृताशेषपाषण्डा • संहृताः अशेषाः पाषण्डाः यया सा। संहृताः — destroyed; removed अशेषाः — without remainder; all पाषण्डाः — heretics; deniers of sacred dharma यया — by whom संहृताशेषपाषण्डा — she by whom all heresy is destroyed i. one who is the destroyer of all heretics, those who are averse to spiritual values. ii. linga purana describes heretics as those who observe vows not enjoined in the vedas and those excluded from ceremonies enjoined by shruti and smriti. such persons are not to mingle with or be spoken to by the twice-born. iii. brahma vaivarta purana also says that puranas, nyaya, mimamsa, dharma shastras, the six vedangas, and the four vedas are the seats of righteousness. whatever is imagined by people beyond these is heresy; she destroys all such pashandas. iv. in some schools the word is written as pakhanda, with the same meaning: pa, the vedas, and khanda, cutting off. she destroys such pakhanda also. bhāskararāya defines pāṣaṇḍa through the lens of scriptural integrity: the destruction of delusion: pāṣaṇḍa refers to systems that distort the truth, promote nihilism, or lead souls away from the realization of the true self. she does not destroy the people, but she completely dismantles their false views, replacing intellectual blindness with the clean, non-dual light of real knowledge.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya defines Pāṣaṇḍa through the lens of scriptural integrity:

The Destruction of Delusion: Pāṣaṇḍa refers to systems that distort the truth, promote nihilism, or lead souls away from the realization of the true self. She does not destroy the people, but She completely dismantles their false views, replacing intellectual blindness with the clean, non-dual light of real knowledge.

name: सदाचारप्रवर्तिका
wordByWord: प्रवर्तिका — promoter; one who sets in motion
सदाचारप्रवर्तिका — she who promotes right conduct
summary: i. One who inspires right action.
ii. Because she destroys the wicked, she incites people to right action.
iii. Sat means great or auspicious, and achara means conduct. She directs people toward great and auspicious conduct.

iv. She gives the inclination to observe the precepts enjoined in the Karma Kanda of the Vedas and the Advaita Brahman taught in the Jnana Kanda, through the means laid down in the Puranas. In Kurma Purana, Devi says that the eighteen Puranas were promulgated by Vyasa by the command of Brahman and that righteousness is established in them. Thus the supreme dharma coming from Brahma, Manu, Vyasa, and others remains established until dissolution.
searchText: 356 सदाचारप्रवर्तिका • सदाचारस्य प्रवर्तिका। सत् — good; noble; auspicious आचारस्य — of conduct; practice प्रवर्तिका — promoter; one who sets in motion सदाचारप्रवर्तिका — she who promotes right conduct i. one who inspires right action. ii. because she destroys the wicked, she incites people to right action. iii. sat means great or auspicious, and achara means conduct. she directs people toward great and auspicious conduct. iv. she gives the inclination to observe the precepts enjoined in the karma kanda of the vedas and the advaita brahman taught in the jnana kanda, through the means laid down in the puranas. in kurma purana, devi says that the eighteen puranas were promulgated by vyasa by the command of brahman and that righteousness is established in them. thus the supreme dharma coming from brahma, manu, vyasa, and others remains established until dissolution. in the saubhāgyabhāskara, bhāskararāya explains her role as the guardian of practice: the instiller of right conduct: true spiritual practice requires a clean baseline of ethical and ritual discipline (sadācāra). she operates as the inner conscience that inspires seekers to perform their daily duties, clean their minds, and maintain the external and internal purity necessary for higher meditation.
esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara, Bhāskararāya explains Her role as the guardian of practice:

The Instiller of Right Conduct: True spiritual practice requires a clean baseline of ethical and ritual discipline (Sadācāra). She operates as the inner conscience that inspires seekers to perform their daily duties, clean their minds, and maintain the external and internal purity necessary for higher meditation.

name: तापत्रयाग्निसंतप्तसमाह्लादनचन्द्रिका
samasa: • तापानां त्रयं यस्य तेन अग्निना सम्यक् तप्तानां सम्यग् आह्लादनेन चन्द्रिका इव।
wordByWord: तापत्रयाग्निसंतप्तसमाह्लादनचन्द्रिका — she who soothes like moonlight those scorched by the fire of the three afflictions
summary: i. One who gives, like the moon, soothing relief to those who suffer from the fire of the three kinds of distress.
ii. The sufferings are of three types: adhyatmikam, adhidaivikam, and adhibhautikam.
Adhyatmikam means disturbances stemming from the self and relating to the body, the five sense organs, the five organs of action, mind, intellect, ego, and volition.
Adhibhautikam means disturbances that come from the world and the five basic elements relating to the above organs.
Adhidaivikam means mental disturbances that come from divine or unseen causes beyond human control, such as flood and similar events.
These three are characteristics of the cycle of life. This name indicates that she makes the soul suffering from these disturbances happy by removing suffering through her moon-like compassion.
searchText: 357 तापत्रयाग्निसंतप्तसमाह्लादनचन्द्रिका • तापानां त्रयं यस्य तेन अग्निना सम्यक् तप्तानां सम्यग् आह्लादनेन चन्द्रिका इव। तापत्रय — the three sufferings अग्नि — fire संतप्त — scorched; afflicted समाह्लादन — complete soothing; delighting चन्द्रिका — moonlight तापत्रयाग्निसंतप्तसमाह्लादनचन्द्रिका — she who soothes like moonlight those scorched by the fire of the three afflictions i. one who gives, like the moon, soothing relief to those who suffer from the fire of the three kinds of distress. ii. the sufferings are of three types: adhyatmikam, adhidaivikam, and adhibhautikam. adhyatmikam means disturbances stemming from the self and relating to the body, the five sense organs, the five organs of action, mind, intellect, ego, and volition. adhibhautikam means disturbances that come from the world and the five basic elements relating to the above organs. adhidaivikam means mental disturbances that come from divine or unseen causes beyond human control, such as flood and similar events. these three are characteristics of the cycle of life. this name indicates that she makes the soul suffering from these disturbances happy by removing suffering through her moon-like compassion. bhāskararāya identifies the three specific fires that afflict human existence: the three afflictions: the fires are आध्यात्मिक (ādhyātmika — miseries arising from one's own body and mind), आधिभौतिक (ādhibhautika — miseries caused by other living creatures), and आधिदैविक (ādhidaivika — miseries caused by cosmic or supernatural forces). the cooling moonlight: the heat of these three fires makes life a constant burning struggle. her presence acts as pure, soothing moonlight (candrikā), which instantly cools the burning distress of the mind, bringing absolute stillness.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya identifies the three specific fires that afflict human existence:

The Three Afflictions: The fires are आध्यात्मिक (ādhyātmika — miseries arising from one's own body and mind), आधिभौतिक (ādhibhautika — miseries caused by other living creatures), and आधिदैविक (ādhidaivika — miseries caused by cosmic or supernatural forces).

The Cooling Moonlight: The heat of these three fires makes life a constant burning struggle. Her presence acts as pure, soothing moonlight (Candrikā), which instantly cools the burning distress of the mind, bringing absolute stillness.

name: तरुणी
summary: i. One who is ever young.
ii. Sreedevee does not have growth or reduction. The name Kshaya-vriddhi-vinirmukta may be referred to.
iii. Sreedevee does not have a beginning or an end. If there is a beginning there will be growth; if there is an end there will be reduction. Sreedevee does not have these.
iv. She is eternal, present in all three times. The names Nitya and Purna convey the same sense, and the name Nitya-yauvana may also be referred to.
v. The sense conveyed by the Brihadaranyaka Upanishad - without ageing and immortal - is reflected here.
searchText: 358 तरुणी • नित्यतारुणवत्वात् तरुणी। नित्य — eternal; always तारुणवत्वात् — because of youthfulness तरुणी — ever young i. one who is ever young. ii. sreedevee does not have growth or reduction. the name kshaya-vriddhi-vinirmukta may be referred to. iii. sreedevee does not have a beginning or an end. if there is a beginning there will be growth; if there is an end there will be reduction. sreedevee does not have these. iv. she is eternal, present in all three times. the names nitya and purna convey the same sense, and the name nitya-yauvana may also be referred to. v. the sense conveyed by the brihadaranyaka upanishad - without ageing and immortal - is reflected here. bhāskararāya clarifies that her youth is completely free from the decay of linear time: eternally youthful: she is nitya-tāruṇya-vatī—permanently anchored in a state of fresh, vibrant youthfulness. unlike material bodies that age, decay, and wrinkle, her consciousness remains eternally fresh, dynamic, and vital, never suffering the decrepitude of time.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya clarifies that Her youth is completely free from the decay of linear time:

Eternally Youthful: She is Nitya-tāruṇya-vatī—permanently anchored in a state of fresh, vibrant youthfulness. Unlike material bodies that age, decay, and wrinkle, Her consciousness remains eternally fresh, dynamic, and vital, never suffering the decrepitude of time.

name: तापसाराध्या
wordByWord: तापसैः — by ascetics; by those practising austerity
तापसाराध्या — she who is worshipped by ascetics
summary: i. One who is worshipped by ascetics.

ii. Or, tapa means samsara, the source of misery; sara means essence or depth; and adhya means meditation. Sreedevee, who is the root cause of this samsara life-cycle, is the essential object of meditation.
searchText: 359 तापसाराध्या • तापसैः आराध्या। तापसैः — by ascetics; by those practising austerity आराध्या — worshipped; to be adored तापसाराध्या — she who is worshipped by ascetics i. one who is worshipped by ascetics. ii. or, tapa means samsara, the source of misery; sara means essence or depth; and adhya means meditation. sreedevee, who is the root cause of this samsara life-cycle, is the essential object of meditation. bhāskararāya describes the level of qualifications required to truly grasp her nature: the object of deep penance: she is not easily reached by scattered, superficial minds. she is tāpasārādhyā because the true method to approach her is through tapas—the intense, concentrated burning away of external desires and sensory distractions. she is the ultimate goal sought after by those who have dedicated their entire lives to quiet internal absorption.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya describes the level of qualifications required to truly grasp Her nature:

The Object of Deep Penance: She is not easily reached by scattered, superficial minds. She is Tāpasārādhyā because the true method to approach Her is through Tapas—the intense, concentrated burning away of external desires and sensory distractions. She is the ultimate goal sought after by those who have dedicated their entire lives to quiet internal absorption.

name: तनुमध्या
wordByWord: तनुमध्या — she whose waist is slender
summary: i. One who has a slender waist.

ii. Earlier, in the thirty-fifth name Lakshya-romalata-dharata-samunneya-madhyama, the middle portion of Sreedevee was described. This name again indicates the slenderness and beauty of that middle portion.

iii. Tanumadhya is also the deity worshipped in the country of Kanchi. A verse prays that Sreedevee Tanumadhya, the consort of Bilvesvara and dwelling on the bank of the river Niva, may protect the devotee.

iv. Tanumadhya is also the name of a metre. With six letters in each foot, it has twenty-four letters. The Gayatri metre also has twenty-four letters, and the Bhagavad Gita statement Gayatri chandasam aham may be remembered.
searchText: 360 तनुमध्या • तनुः मध्यः यस्याः सा। तनुः — slender; thin मध्यः — waist; middle यस्याः — of whom; whose सा — she तनुमध्या — she whose waist is slender i. one who has a slender waist. ii. earlier, in the thirty-fifth name lakshya-romalata-dharata-samunneya-madhyama, the middle portion of sreedevee was described. this name again indicates the slenderness and beauty of that middle portion. iii. tanumadhya is also the deity worshipped in the country of kanchi. a verse prays that sreedevee tanumadhya, the consort of bilvesvara and dwelling on the bank of the river niva, may protect the devotee. iv. tanumadhya is also the name of a metre. with six letters in each foot, it has twenty-four letters. the gayatri metre also has twenty-four letters, and the bhagavad gita statement gayatri chandasam aham may be remembered. in the saubhāgyabhāskara, bhāskararāya moves past the physical description to structural cosmology and yoga: the cosmic juncture: madhya represents the unmanifest state between creation and dissolution. her waist is tanu (infinitesimally small or non-existent), meaning the boundary line between the manifest universe and the unmanifest absolute is extraordinarily subtle, almost imperceptible. the suṣumṇā channel: in the subtle body, she represents the core energy path, where the central junction (madhya)—the suṣumṇā channel—is incredibly fine and subtle (tanu).
esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara, Bhāskararāya moves past the physical description to structural cosmology and yoga:

The Cosmic Juncture: Madhya represents the unmanifest state between creation and dissolution. Her waist is tanu (infinitesimally small or non-existent), meaning the boundary line between the manifest universe and the unmanifest absolute is extraordinarily subtle, almost imperceptible.
The Suṣumṇā Channel: In the subtle body, She represents the core energy path, where the central junction (madhya)—the Suṣumṇā channel—is incredibly fine and subtle (tanu).

name: तमोपहा
summary: i. One who removes darkness.
ii. She removes from devotees the darkness called ignorance.
iii. The Ishavasya Upanishad says that those who worship ignorance enter into blind darkness. The same idea is recalled in Lalita Trishati, where the Shruti condemns those who worship ignorance.
searchText: 361 तमोपहा • तमः अपहन्तीति। तमः — darkness; ignorance अपहन्ति — removes; destroys तमोपहा — remover of darkness i. one who removes darkness. ii. she removes from devotees the darkness called ignorance. iii. the ishavasya upanishad says that those who worship ignorance enter into blind darkness. the same idea is recalled in lalita trishati, where the shruti condemns those who worship ignorance. bhāskararāya targets the ultimate cause of spiritual bondage: the destruction of ignorance: तमस् (tamas) refers to both physical darkness and the spiritual ignorance (avidyā) that veils the soul's true nature. as tamopahā, she acts as the spiritual sun that burns away the structural darkness of egoic illusion, instantly granting the clarity of self-realization.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya targets the ultimate cause of spiritual bondage:

The Destruction of Ignorance: तमस् (tamas) refers to both physical darkness and the spiritual ignorance (avidyā) that veils the soul's true nature. As Tamopahā, She acts as the spiritual sun that burns away the structural darkness of egoic illusion, instantly granting the clarity of self-realization.

name: चितिः
summary: i. One who is in the form of knowledge.
ii. In Pratyabhijna Hridaya it is said that Chit is independent and is the cause of the establishment of the universe.
iii. In Mahavasishtha, Sreedevee is called Chit because she is the life of those who desire life.
iv. In Saptashati, Chiti is mentioned as the conscious form that pervades the whole universe.
searchText: 362 चितिः • चितिः। चितिः — consciousness; knowledge; awareness i. one who is in the form of knowledge. ii. in pratyabhijna hridaya it is said that chit is independent and is the cause of the establishment of the universe. iii. in mahavasishtha, sreedevee is called chit because she is the life of those who desire life. iv. in saptashati, chiti is mentioned as the conscious form that pervades the whole universe. bhāskararāya defines this name as her absolute, unconditioned foundation: pure awareness: she is not merely an entity that possesses consciousness; she is consciousness itself (citi). this is the static, eternal background light that illuminates all mental operations, thoughts, and physical movements without undergoing any change itself.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya defines this name as Her absolute, unconditioned foundation:

Pure Awareness: She is not merely an entity that possesses consciousness; She is consciousness itself (Citi). This is the static, eternal background light that illuminates all mental operations, thoughts, and physical movements without undergoing any change itself.

name: तत्पदलक्ष्यार्था
samasa: • तत् इति पदेन लक्षणीयः अर्थः यस्याः सा।
wordByWord: तत्पदलक्ष्यार्था — she who is the implied meaning of the word Tat
summary: i. One who is the connotation of the word Tat in the Mahavakya.
ii. In the Mahavakya Tat tvam asi, the word Tat indicates Brahman as the cause of creation and other cosmic functions. Its implied meaning is the same Brahman in its taskless, pure form.
iii. She is in the form of Brahman indicated by that implied meaning. The later name Tat may also be referred to.
searchText: 363 तत्पदलक्ष्यार्था • तत् इति पदेन लक्षणीयः अर्थः यस्याः सा। तत् — that; the word tat पदेन — by the word लक्षणीयः — indicated indirectly; implied अर्थः — meaning यस्याः — of whom; whose तत्पदलक्ष्यार्था — she who is the implied meaning of the word tat i. one who is the connotation of the word tat in the mahavakya. ii. in the mahavakya tat tvam asi, the word tat indicates brahman as the cause of creation and other cosmic functions. its implied meaning is the same brahman in its taskless, pure form. iii. she is in the form of brahman indicated by that implied meaning. the later name tat may also be referred to. bhāskararāya applies the standard vedāntic exegetical method of lakṣaṇā (indirect indication): the transcendent absolute: in the sentence "tat tvam asi" (that thou art), the literal meaning (vācyārtha) of tat is the omniscient, personal creator god (īśvara). however, when the structural limitations of cosmic veiling are stripped away, the implied meaning (lakṣyārtha) is the pure, non-dual, infinite consciousness (parabrahman). she is that ultimate reality.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya applies the standard Vedāntic exegetical method of Lakṣaṇā (indirect indication):

The Transcendent Absolute: In the sentence "Tat Tvam Asi" (That Thou Art), the literal meaning (vācyārtha) of Tat is the omniscient, personal creator God (Īśvara). However, when the structural limitations of cosmic veiling are stripped away, the implied meaning (lakṣyārtha) is the pure, non-dual, infinite consciousness (Parabrahman). She is that ultimate reality.

name: चिदेकरसरूपिणी
samasa: • चिदेव रसः येषां तानि रूपाणि अस्याः इति।
wordByWord: चिदेकरसरूपिणी — she whose forms have consciousness alone as their essence
summary: i. One who is completely in the form of knowledge.

ii. The attributes of bliss, consciousness and intellect are inseparable from her. Though she appears in different forms, her conscious form is full of knowledge, and knowledge itself is to be taken as the single essence.
iii. This is explained in the Panchapadika of Padmapadacharya: bliss, perception of objects and eternity, though inseparable from consciousness, appear as different attributes.
iv. She is never separated from Chit. Though they appear different by attributes, they are really one and the same.
searchText: 364 चिदेकरसरूपिणी • चिदेव रसः येषां तानि रूपाणि अस्याः इति। चित् — consciousness एव — alone; indeed रसः — essence रूपिणी — she whose form is चिदेकरसरूपिणी — she whose forms have consciousness alone as their essence i. one who is completely in the form of knowledge. ii. the attributes of bliss, consciousness and intellect are inseparable from her. though she appears in different forms, her conscious form is full of knowledge, and knowledge itself is to be taken as the single essence. iii. this is explained in the panchapadika of padmapadacharya: bliss, perception of objects and eternity, though inseparable from consciousness, appear as different attributes. iv. she is never separated from chit. though they appear different by attributes, they are really one and the same. in the saubhāgyabhāskara, bhāskararāya explains the term एकरस (homogenous flavor): homogenous reality: just as a vast body of salt water has only one taste throughout, her entire multi-layered manifestation has only one underlying flavor, substance, and essence: pure consciousness (cit). matter and mind are merely dense modifications of this single divine juice.
esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara, Bhāskararāya explains the term एकरस (homogenous flavor):

Homogenous Reality: Just as a vast body of salt water has only one taste throughout, Her entire multi-layered manifestation has only one underlying flavor, substance, and essence: pure consciousness (Cit). Matter and mind are merely dense modifications of this single divine juice.

name: स्वात्मानन्दलवीभूतब्रह्माद्यानन्दसन्ततिः
samasa: • स्वस्याः आत्मारूपः यः आनन्दः तस्य लवीभूताः ब्रह्मादीनां सन्ततयः यस्याः सा।
wordByWord: आत्मारूपः — in the form of the Self
स्वात्मानन्दलवीभूतब्रह्माद्यानन्दसन्ततिः — she whose own Self-bliss makes the bliss of Brahma and others only a minute particle
summary: i. The totality of the bliss of Brahma and others is only a minute portion of her own bliss.
ii. The three gods who perform creation, protection and destruction, as well as Indra and other devas, enjoy bliss; but this is only a drop when compared with the bliss of Sreedevee.

iii. The Taittiriya Upanishad says that other beings live on a mere part of this bliss. The grades of bliss described there increase up to Brahma, but remain limited. Her bliss is the unlimited bliss of Brahman.
iv. Lava means a particle. The name therefore shows that all other blisses are only small particles of her own Self-bliss.
searchText: 365 स्वात्मानन्दलवीभूतब्रह्माद्यानन्दसन्ततिः • स्वस्याः आत्मारूपः यः आनन्दः तस्य लवीभूताः ब्रह्मादीनां सन्ततयः यस्याः सा। स्वस्याः — of her own आत्मारूपः — in the form of the self आनन्दः — bliss लवीभूताः — become a tiny particle ब्रह्मादीनाम् — of brahma and others सन्ततयः — streams; continuities स्वात्मानन्दलवीभूतब्रह्माद्यानन्दसन्ततिः — she whose own self-bliss makes the bliss of brahma and others only a minute particle i. the totality of the bliss of brahma and others is only a minute portion of her own bliss. ii. the three gods who perform creation, protection and destruction, as well as indra and other devas, enjoy bliss; but this is only a drop when compared with the bliss of sreedevee. iii. the taittiriya upanishad says that other beings live on a mere part of this bliss. the grades of bliss described there increase up to brahma, but remain limited. her bliss is the unlimited bliss of brahman. iv. lava means a particle. the name therefore shows that all other blisses are only small particles of her own self-bliss. bhāskararāya anchors this directly in the taittirīya and bṛhadāraṇyaka upaniṣad evaluations of bliss (ānanda-mīmāṁsā): the ocean and the drop: the highest imaginable happiness in the relative universe is the bliss of the creator brahmā. yet, scriptural revelation states that his bliss is merely a tiny drop leaked from the infinite reservoir of brahman. she is that ocean; the ultimate spiritual ecstasy of the highest cosmic administrators is just an tiny splash of her inherent nature.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya anchors this directly in the Taittirīya and Bṛhadāraṇyaka Upaniṣad evaluations of bliss (Ānanda-mīmāṁsā):

The Ocean and the Drop: The highest imaginable happiness in the relative universe is the bliss of the creator Brahmā. Yet, scriptural revelation states that his bliss is merely a tiny drop leaked from the infinite reservoir of Brahman. She is that ocean; the ultimate spiritual ecstasy of the highest cosmic administrators is just an tiny splash of Her inherent nature.

name: परा
wordByWord: परा — supreme; transcendent; the highest form of speech
प्रसिद्धत्वात् — because of being well known
summary: i. One who is in the form of the sound called Para, the transcendent word.

ii. Speech is described in four forms: Para, Pashyanti, Madhyama and Vaikhari. The physical uttered speech expresses only gross form; the pure unconditioned Brahman is indicated through subtler levels of sound.

iii. In the process of manifestation, the causal bindu, nada and bija are discussed as stages of sound and creation. In the bodily aspect, the causal bindu is in the Muladhara and is known as Shakti or Kundalini.

iv. The first motionless sound in the Muladhara is called Para speech. From it arise the later stages: Pashyanti, Madhyama and Vaikhari. The Veda says that speech has four measured quarters; the wise know them, three are hidden, and human beings speak only the fourth.

v. From the name Tatpadalakshyartha up to Vaikhari-rupa, it is described that Sreedevee herself is in all four grades of speech. Tripura-siddhanta also explains Para as the supreme form and the source of grace.
searchText: 366 परा • परानन्दाभिधे तन्त्रे प्रसिद्धत्वाच्च सा परा। परा — supreme; transcendent; the highest form of speech परानन्द — supreme bliss प्रसिद्धत्वात् — because of being well known i. one who is in the form of the sound called para, the transcendent word. ii. speech is described in four forms: para, pashyanti, madhyama and vaikhari. the physical uttered speech expresses only gross form; the pure unconditioned brahman is indicated through subtler levels of sound. iii. in the process of manifestation, the causal bindu, nada and bija are discussed as stages of sound and creation. in the bodily aspect, the causal bindu is in the muladhara and is known as shakti or kundalini. iv. the first motionless sound in the muladhara is called para speech. from it arise the later stages: pashyanti, madhyama and vaikhari. the veda says that speech has four measured quarters; the wise know them, three are hidden, and human beings speak only the fourth. v. from the name tatpadalakshyartha up to vaikhari-rupa, it is described that sreedevee herself is in all four grades of speech. tripura-siddhanta also explains para as the supreme form and the source of grace. bhāskararāya decodes this name as the highest level of speech (vāk) within tantric linguistics: the unmanifest source: परा (parā) is the first, highest, and most subtle stage of sound manifestation. it resides completely unmanifested in the mūladhāra chakra as pure, undifferentiated potential energy. it is the silent, flashing seed of consciousness before a single ripple of thought or sound occurs.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya decodes this name as the highest level of speech (Vāk) within Tantric linguistics:

The Unmanifest Source: परा (Parā) is the first, highest, and most subtle stage of sound manifestation. It resides completely unmanifested in the Mūladhāra chakra as pure, undifferentiated potential energy. It is the silent, flashing seed of consciousness before a single ripple of thought or sound occurs.

name: प्रत्यक्चितीरूपा
samasa: • प्रतिकूलम् अञ्चतीति प्रतिची। सा च सा चिती च प्रत्यक्चिती। सा रूपं यस्याः सा।
wordByWord: प्रत्यक् — inward; turned toward the Self
प्रत्यक्चितीरूपा — she who is in the form of inner consciousness
summary: i. One who is in the form of inner consciousness.
ii. The nature of the sense organs is to move outward. Para, the great subtle form of sound, is recognised only by the mind turned inward. This inward turning is called Pratyak.

iii. With inward attention, the mind recognises the Para-knowledge in the form of Brahman without distinction of matter, energy and qualities. Thus Sreedevee is Pratyak-Chiti, the great inward knowledge.
searchText: 367 प्रत्यक्चितीरूपा • प्रतिकूलम् अञ्चतीति प्रतिची। सा च सा चिती च प्रत्यक्चिती। सा रूपं यस्याः सा। प्रत्यक् — inward; turned toward the self चिती — consciousness; knowledge रूपा — form प्रत्यक्चितीरूपा — she who is in the form of inner consciousness i. one who is in the form of inner consciousness. ii. the nature of the sense organs is to move outward. para, the great subtle form of sound, is recognised only by the mind turned inward. this inward turning is called pratyak. iii. with inward attention, the mind recognises the para-knowledge in the form of brahman without distinction of matter, energy and qualities. thus sreedevee is pratyak-chiti, the great inward knowledge. bhāskararāya highlights the exact direction of self-realization: the inward gaze: ordinary consciousness moves outward (parāñc) through the senses to perceive material objects. प्रत्यक् (pratyak) describes the exact reverse motion—turning the attention 180 degrees backward, away from the world, directly into the source of awareness. she is that specific internal witness textually realized when the mind rests in the self.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya highlights the exact direction of self-realization:

The Inward Gaze: Ordinary consciousness moves outward (parāñc) through the senses to perceive material objects. प्रत्यक् (pratyak) describes the exact reverse motion—turning the attention 180 degrees backward, away from the world, directly into the source of awareness. She is that specific internal witness textually realized when the mind rests in the self.

name: पश्यन्ती
wordByWord: पश्यन्ती — she who sees; the second subtle stage of speech
summary: i. One who is in the form of Pashyanti, speech in the inaudible stage.
ii. Earlier, under the name Para, the four stages of speech were discussed. The second stage is Pashyanti, and she is in that form.

iii. Saubhagya-sudhodaya says that because she sees all and sees all in herself, she is called Pashyanti. Since she rises above the path of action, she is also called Uttirna; the name Kulottirna may be referred to.
searchText: 368 पश्यन्ती • पश्यतीति पश्यन्ती। पश्यति — sees पश्यन्ती — she who sees; the second subtle stage of speech i. one who is in the form of pashyanti, speech in the inaudible stage. ii. earlier, under the name para, the four stages of speech were discussed. the second stage is pashyanti, and she is in that form. iii. saubhagya-sudhodaya says that because she sees all and sees all in herself, she is called pashyanti. since she rises above the path of action, she is also called uttirna; the name kulottirna may be referred to. bhāskararāya provides the second structural layer of phonic manifestation: the visualized thought: as the cosmic sound descends from the unmanifest parā state into the sahasrāra and maṇipūra regions, it becomes पश्यन्ती (paśyantī). at this stage, sound is not yet audible speech, but it exists as a luminous, holistic vision or a unified ideation within the cosmic mind. she is the witness of this primal creative vision.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya provides the second structural layer of phonic manifestation:

The Visualized Thought: As the cosmic sound descends from the unmanifest Parā state into the Sahasrāra and Maṇipūra regions, it becomes पश्यन्ती (Paśyantī). At this stage, sound is not yet audible speech, but it exists as a luminous, holistic vision or a unified ideation within the cosmic mind. She is the witness of this primal creative vision.

name: परदेवता
summary: i. One who is the object of supreme devotion.
ii. She is worshipped as the supreme deity and as the ultimate Godhead to be meditated upon.
searchText: 369 परदेवता • परा च असौ देवता च। परा — supreme देवता — deity; object of worship परदेवता — the supreme deity i. one who is the object of supreme devotion. ii. she is worshipped as the supreme deity and as the ultimate godhead to be meditated upon. bhāskararāya clarifies her ultimate hierarchical position in the universe: the sovereign object of worship: while there are numerous local, planetary, and functional deities responsible for individual aspects of nature, she is the paradevatā—the absolute, ultimate divine matrix from whom all other gods draw their authority, existence, and power. she is the final destination of all lines of devotion.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya clarifies Her ultimate hierarchical position in the universe:

The Sovereign Object of Worship: While there are numerous local, planetary, and functional deities responsible for individual aspects of nature, She is the Paradevatā—the absolute, ultimate divine matrix from whom all other gods draw their authority, existence, and power. She is the final destination of all lines of devotion.

name: मध्यमा
wordByWord: मध्यमा — the middle stage of speech
summary: i. One who is in the form of Madhyama, speech in the middle stage of its external expression.

ii. This stage is not like Pashyanti, which stops immediately after rising, nor like Vaikhari, in which articulation is fully developed. It is the intermediate stage between the two and is therefore called Madhyama.
searchText: 370 मध्यमा • मध्ये स्थिता। मध्ये — in the middle स्थिता — situated मध्यमा — the middle stage of speech i. one who is in the form of madhyama, speech in the middle stage of its external expression. ii. this stage is not like pashyanti, which stops immediately after rising, nor like vaikhari, in which articulation is fully developed. it is the intermediate stage between the two and is therefore called madhyama. bhāskararāya unpacks the third layer of the descent of sound: the mental articulation: located primarily in the region of the heart (anāhata), मध्यमा (madhyamā) is the intermediate stage of speech. here, the unified vision of paśyantī breaks down into specific words, sentences, and mental concepts, though it is still internal and silent to the outside world. she is the inner thinker structuring this mental language.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya unpacks the third layer of the descent of sound:

The Mental Articulation: Located primarily in the region of the heart (Anāhata), मध्यमा (Madhyamā) is the intermediate stage of speech. Here, the unified vision of Paśyantī breaks down into specific words, sentences, and mental concepts, though it is still internal and silent to the outside world. She is the inner thinker structuring this mental language.

name: वैखरीरूपा
samasa: • विशेषेण खरः विखरः। तस्य इयम् एव रूपं यस्याः सा।
wordByWord: वैखरीरूपा — she who is in the form of articulated speech
summary: i. One who is in the form of Vaikhari, the uttered audible speech.
ii. The hard and manifest form of letters, issuing as sound through the throat and mouth, is Vaikhari. Scattered sound, carried by air and made dense through articulation, becomes the form of words.
iii. Saubhagya-sudhodaya explains the word as that which clearly enters the cavity of the ear. Yoga texts also connect Vaikhari with the air that brings sound from the heart through the throat.
searchText: 371 वैखरीरूपा • विशेषेण खरः विखरः। तस्य इयम् एव रूपं यस्याः सा। विशेषेण — specially; distinctly खरः — hard; audible sound वैखरी — uttered speech रूपा — form वैखरीरूपा — she who is in the form of articulated speech i. one who is in the form of vaikhari, the uttered audible speech. ii. the hard and manifest form of letters, issuing as sound through the throat and mouth, is vaikhari. scattered sound, carried by air and made dense through articulation, becomes the form of words. iii. saubhagya-sudhodaya explains the word as that which clearly enters the cavity of the ear. yoga texts also connect vaikhari with the air that brings sound from the heart through the throat. bhāskararāya completes the magnificent four-fold phonetic sequence (from 366 to 371): audible communication: when sound passes up through the throat and is shaped by the tongue, teeth, and lips, it emerges as वैखरी (vaikharī)—gross, audible sound waves capable of being heard by the ears of others. bhāskararāya notes that every spoken word, sentence, and mantra in the universe is her physical voice in this dense state of material expression.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya completes the magnificent four-fold phonetic sequence (from 366 to 371):

Audible Communication: When sound passes up through the throat and is shaped by the tongue, teeth, and lips, it emerges as वैखरी (Vaikharī)—gross, audible sound waves capable of being heard by the ears of others. Bhāskararāya notes that every spoken word, sentence, and mantra in the universe is Her physical voice in this dense state of material expression.

name: भक्तमानसहंसिका
wordByWord: मानसे — in the mind; in the Manasa lake-like mind
भक्तमानसहंसिका — she who is like a swan in the minds of devotees
summary: i. One who is in the form of a swan in the lake-like minds of devotees.
ii. Brahma created the pure Manasa lake on Kailasha, where swans dwell. The pure minds of devotees are compared to that lake, and Sreedevee is compared to the swan dwelling there.

iii. Soundaryalahari also compares the divine pair to swans moving in the minds of the great. She is invisible to others, that is, to those who are not devotees. The name Muni-manasa-hamsika may also be referred to.
searchText: 372 भक्तमानसहंसिका • भक्तानां मानसे हंसीव हंसिका। भक्तानाम् — of devotees मानसे — in the mind; in the manasa lake-like mind हंसी इव — like a swan हंसिका — female swan भक्तमानसहंसिका — she who is like a swan in the minds of devotees i. one who is in the form of a swan in the lake-like minds of devotees. ii. brahma created the pure manasa lake on kailasha, where swans dwell. the pure minds of devotees are compared to that lake, and sreedevee is compared to the swan dwelling there. iii. soundaryalahari also compares the divine pair to swans moving in the minds of the great. she is invisible to others, that is, to those who are not devotees. the name muni-manasa-hamsika may also be referred to. bhāskararāya utilizes this classic aesthetic metaphor to describe her internal residence: sporting in the heart: a swan will only swim in perfectly clear, clean water, such as the sacred lake mānasarovara. similarly, she chooses to manifest her presence inside the lake of the devotee's mind (mānasa) only when it is cleared of the mud of worldly desire and kept perfectly serene, playing there as the breath of life (haṁsa).
esoteric: Bhāskararāya utilizes this classic aesthetic metaphor to describe Her internal residence:

Sporting in the Heart: A swan will only swim in perfectly clear, clean water, such as the sacred lake Mānasarovara. Similarly, She chooses to manifest Her presence inside the lake of the devotee's mind (mānasa) only when it is cleared of the mud of worldly desire and kept perfectly serene, playing there as the breath of life (Haṁsa).

name: कामेश्वरप्राणनाडी
wordByWord: कामेश्वरप्राणनाडी — she who is like the life-current of Kameshvara
summary: i. One who is like the life-current of Kameshvara.
ii. Prana is the life principle. A nadi carries that vital force. Sreedevee is so inseparably connected with Kameshvara that she is described as his very prana-nadi.
searchText: 373 कामेश्वरप्राणनाडी • कामेश्वरस्य प्राणनाडीव। कामेश्वरस्य — of kameshvara प्राणनाडी — life-current; vital nerve इव — like कामेश्वरप्राणनाडी — she who is like the life-current of kameshvara i. one who is like the life-current of kameshvara. ii. prana is the life principle. a nadi carries that vital force. sreedevee is so inseparably connected with kameshvara that she is described as his very prana-nadi. bhāskararāya highlights the absolute, inseparable nature of shiva and shakti: the vital breath of shiva: shiva (kāmeśvara) represents the foundational, static consciousness. without her presence as the kinetic vital channel (prāṇa-nāḍī), he remains unmanifest and inert. she is the very pulse, life, and breath that animates him, demonstrating that they are as inseparable as fire and heat.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya highlights the absolute, inseparable nature of Shiva and Shakti:

The Vital Breath of Shiva: Shiva (Kāmeśvara) represents the foundational, static consciousness. Without Her presence as the kinetic vital channel (Prāṇa-nāḍī), He remains unmanifest and inert. She is the very pulse, life, and breath that animates Him, demonstrating that they are as inseparable as fire and heat.

name: कृतज्ञा
wordByWord: कृते — what has been done; action performed
कृतज्ञा — she who knows what has been done; grateful one
summary: i. One who knows what has been done.
ii. She knows even the smallest service or worship performed by devotees. She is therefore grateful in the highest sense and never ignores the devotion shown to her.
searchText: 374 कृतज्ञा • कृते जानातीति। कृते — what has been done; action performed जानाति — knows कृतज्ञा — she who knows what has been done; grateful one i. one who knows what has been done. ii. she knows even the smallest service or worship performed by devotees. she is therefore grateful in the highest sense and never ignores the devotion shown to her. bhāskararāya illuminates her absolute attentiveness to the devotee's internal efforts: remembering small devotions: humans often forget favors done by others. she is kṛtajñā because she remembers even the smallest, most hidden act of devotion, mental surrender, or ritual offering performed by a seeker. she accounts for every step taken toward her, returning it with infinite grace.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya illuminates Her absolute attentiveness to the devotee's internal efforts:

Remembering Small Devotions: Humans often forget favors done by others. She is Kṛtajñā because She remembers even the smallest, most hidden act of devotion, mental surrender, or ritual offering performed by a seeker. She accounts for every step taken toward Her, returning it with infinite grace.

name: कामपूजिता
wordByWord: कामेन — by Kama; by desire; by Kameshvara
कामपूजिता — she who is worshipped by Kama
summary: i. One who is worshipped by Kama.
ii. Kama may indicate Manmatha or Kameshvara. She is worshipped by him and is also the supreme object sought by desire when desire becomes purified.
searchText: 375 कामपूजिता • कामेन पूजिता। कामेन — by kama; by desire; by kameshvara पूजिता — worshipped कामपूजिता — she who is worshipped by kama i. one who is worshipped by kama. ii. kama may indicate manmatha or kameshvara. she is worshipped by him and is also the supreme object sought by desire when desire becomes purified. bhāskararāya connects this directly to the foundational lineage of śrīvidyā: the kāmarāja lineage: kāmadeva is not merely a god of mundane desire; he is one of the twelve great founding seers (kādi-mata) who brought down the premier structural formulation of the fifteen-syllabled pañcadaśākṣarī mantra. his worship is sublime and purifies the creative drive, realigning it toward cosmic union.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya connects this directly to the foundational lineage of Śrīvidyā:

The Kāmarāja Lineage: Kāmadeva is not merely a god of mundane desire; he is one of the twelve great founding seers (kādi-mata) who brought down the premier structural formulation of the fifteen-syllabled Pañcadaśākṣarī Mantra. His worship is sublime and purifies the creative drive, realigning it toward cosmic union.

name: शृङ्गाररससम्पूर्णा
samasa: • शृङ्गम् अररं यस्याः सा शृङ्गाररा। सा एव सम्पूर्णेन ब्रह्मणा सह ससम्पूर्णा च।
wordByWord: शृङ्गाररस — the rasa of love and beauty
शृङ्गाररससम्पूर्णा — she who is full of the rasa of love and beauty
summary: i. One who is filled with the rasa of love and beauty.
ii. Shringara is the rasa of love. Sreedevee is complete in this rasa. The name also admits a deeper interpretation linking her completeness with Brahman.
searchText: 376 शृङ्गाररससम्पूर्णा • शृङ्गाराख्यरसेन सम्यक् पूर्णा। • शृङ्गम् अररं यस्याः सा शृङ्गाररा। सा एव सम्पूर्णेन ब्रह्मणा सह ससम्पूर्णा च। शृङ्गाररस — the rasa of love and beauty सम्पूर्णा — completely full ब्रह्मणा सह — together with brahman शृङ्गाररससम्पूर्णा — she who is full of the rasa of love and beauty i. one who is filled with the rasa of love and beauty. ii. shringara is the rasa of love. sreedevee is complete in this rasa. the name also admits a deeper interpretation linking her completeness with brahman. bhāskararāya defines śṛṅgāra past its common physical interpretation: the sentiment of union: śṛṅgāra is the premier aesthetic emotion (rasa-rāja). in its highest cosmic sense, it represents the absolute attraction and ecstatic love between the individual soul and the universal self, or between shiva and shakti. she is the infinite reservoir of this divine love.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya defines Śṛṅgāra past its common physical interpretation:

The Sentiment of Union: Śṛṅgāra is the premier aesthetic emotion (Rasa-rāja). In its highest cosmic sense, it represents the absolute attraction and ecstatic love between the individual soul and the universal self, or between Shiva and Shakti. She is the infinite reservoir of this divine love.

name: जया
summary: i. One who is victory itself.
ii. She is the victorious power, and through her grace the devotee gains victory over inner and outer obstacles.
searchText: 377 जया • जयस्वरूपा। जय — victory स्वरूपा — whose nature is जया — victory itself i. one who is victory itself. ii. she is the victorious power, and through her grace the devotee gains victory over inner and outer obstacles. in the saubhāgyabhāskara, bhāskararāya tracks her identity within ritual and historical frameworks: the energy of success: she is the absolute state of victory itself. she functions as the specific energy that enables righteousness to triumph over unrighteousness. furthermore, in the context of the śrī cakra, she represents the specialized protective force executing her sovereign commands.
esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara, Bhāskararāya tracks Her identity within ritual and historical frameworks:

The Energy of Success: She is the absolute state of victory itself. She functions as the specific energy that enables righteousness to triumph over unrighteousness. Furthermore, in the context of the Śrī Cakra, She represents the specialized protective force executing Her sovereign commands.

name: जालन्धरस्थिता
wordByWord: जालन्धरस्थिता — she who abides in Jalandhara
summary: i. One who is situated in Jalandhara.
ii. Jalandhara is one of the sacred seats connected with worship. She is present there and is worshipped in that pitha.
searchText: 378 जालन्धरस्थिता • जालन्धरे स्थिता। जालन्धरे — in jalandhara स्थिता — situated जालन्धरस्थिता — she who abides in jalandhara i. one who is situated in jalandhara. ii. jalandhara is one of the sacred seats connected with worship. she is present there and is worshipped in that pitha. bhāskararāya details both the geographical seat and the internal yogic lock: the external shrine: she is the presiding goddess of the historical jālandhara pīṭha, one of the primary sacred locations of the śākta tradition. the throat lock: in internal hatha/kuṇḍalinī yoga, जालन्धरबन्ध (jālandhara-bandha) is the precise throat lock performed to contain and redirect the upward-moving vital winds. she is the internal energy awakened and stabilized during this practice.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya details both the geographical seat and the internal yogic lock:
The External Shrine: She is the presiding goddess of the historical Jālandhara Pīṭha, one of the primary sacred locations of the Śākta tradition.

The Throat Lock: In internal Hatha/Kuṇḍalinī Yoga, जालन्धरबन्ध (Jālandhara-bandha) is the precise throat lock performed to contain and redirect the upward-moving vital winds. She is the internal energy awakened and stabilized during this practice.

name: ओड्याणपीठनिलया
samasa: • ओड्याणाख्यं पीठम् एव निलयः यस्याः सा।
wordByWord: ओड्याणपीठनिलया — she whose abode is the Odyana pitha
summary: i. One whose abode is the Odyana pitha.
ii. Odyana is one of the important pithas of Sreedevee worship. She is contemplated as dwelling there.
searchText: 379 ओड्याणपीठनिलया • ओड्याणाख्यं पीठम् एव निलयः यस्याः सा। ओड्याणाख्यम् — called odyana पीठम् — sacred seat निलयः — abode यस्याः — of whom; whose ओड्याणपीठनिलया — she whose abode is the odyana pitha i. one whose abode is the odyana pitha. ii. odyana is one of the important pithas of sreedevee worship. she is contemplated as dwelling there. bhāskararāya explains this premier station within śrīvidyā geometry: the central throne: geographically, it refers to the prominent oḍḍiyāna sacred site. esoterically, within the śrī cakra, the ओड्याणपीठ represents the highly guarded central triangle directly surrounding the bindu. she sits inside this core sanctuary, radiating her creative rays into the outer layers.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya explains this premier station within Śrīvidyā geometry:

The Central Throne: Geographically, it refers to the prominent Oḍḍiyāna sacred site. Esoterically, within the Śrī Cakra, the ओड्याणपीठ represents the highly guarded central triangle directly surrounding the Bindu. She sits inside this core sanctuary, radiating Her creative rays into the outer layers.

name: बिन्दुमण्डलवासिनी
wordByWord: बिन्दुमण्डलवासिनी — she who dwells in the Bindu-mandala
summary: i. One who dwells in the Bindu-mandala.
ii. The Bindu is the central and most subtle point in the Sri Chakra. Sreedevee is contemplated as dwelling in that supreme centre.
searchText: 380 बिन्दुमण्डलवासिनी • बिन्दुः एव मण्डलं तस्मिन् वसतीति। बिन्दुः — bindu; point मण्डलं — circle; field तस्मिन् — in that वसति — dwells बिन्दुमण्डलवासिनी — she who dwells in the bindu-mandala i. one who dwells in the bindu-mandala. ii. the bindu is the central and most subtle point in the sri chakra. sreedevee is contemplated as dwelling in that supreme centre. bhāskararāya illuminates the absolute center of meditation: the center of the universe: the बिन्दु (bindu) is the singular, mathematical point at the absolute center of the śrī cakra (the sarvānandamaya cakra). it represents the unmanifested, condensed union of shiva and shakti before creation explodes outward. she resides inside this point, functioning as the ultimate source of all cosmic dimensions.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya illuminates the absolute center of meditation:

The Center of the Universe: The बिन्दु (Bindu) is the singular, mathematical point at the absolute center of the Śrī Cakra (the Sarvānandamaya Cakra). It represents the unmanifested, condensed union of Shiva and Shakti before creation explodes outward. She resides inside this point, functioning as the ultimate source of all cosmic dimensions.

name: रहोयागक्रमाराध्या
samasa: • रहसि यागः तस्य तत्र वा क्रमेण आराध्या।
wordByWord: रहोयागक्रमाराध्या — she who is worshipped by the secret ritual order
summary: i. One who is worshipped by the procedure of secret worship.
ii. Rahoyaga is worship performed in secrecy according to the prescribed order. It is an inner form of worship directed to Sreedevee.
searchText: 381 रहोयागक्रमाराध्या • रहसि यागः तस्य तत्र वा क्रमेण आराध्या। रहसि — in secret यागः — worship; sacrifice क्रमेण — according to prescribed order आराध्या — to be worshipped रहोयागक्रमाराध्या — she who is worshipped by the secret ritual order i. one who is worshipped by the procedure of secret worship. ii. rahoyaga is worship performed in secrecy according to the prescribed order. it is an inner form of worship directed to sreedevee. bhāskararāya strips away any external, public ostentation: internalized sacrifice: रहसि (rahasi) denotes absolute interior privacy. this name refers strictly to antaryāga—the internal, mental sacrifice where the practitioner offers their ego, thoughts, and sensory functions as oblations into the fire of pure consciousness. this is the highest form of śrīvidyā practice, invisible to the outside world.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya strips away any external, public ostentation:

Internalized Sacrifice: रहसि (rahasi) denotes absolute interior privacy. This name refers strictly to Antaryāga—the internal, mental sacrifice where the practitioner offers their ego, thoughts, and sensory functions as oblations into the fire of pure consciousness. This is the highest form of Śrīvidyā practice, invisible to the outside world.

name: रहस्तर्पणतर्पिता
wordByWord: तर्पणेन — by offering of satisfaction; libation
रहस्तर्पणतर्पिता — she who is pleased by secret tarpana
summary: i. One who is satisfied by secret tarpana.
ii. She is pleased by offerings made in the inner and secret mode of worship.
searchText: 382 रहस्तर्पणतर्पिता • रहसि (कृतेन) तर्पणेन तर्पिता। रहसि — secretly तर्पणेन — by offering of satisfaction; libation तर्पिता — satisfied रहस्तर्पणतर्पिता — she who is pleased by secret tarpana i. one who is satisfied by secret tarpana. ii. she is pleased by offerings made in the inner and secret mode of worship. bhāskararāya continues the internal mystical methodology: the nectar of absorption: the "libation" (tarpaṇa) offered here is not physical water. it represents the downpour of the nectar of immortality (amṛta) generated when the kuṇḍalinī unites with shiva in the crown center. offering this supreme bliss back to the devi is what truly satisfies her cosmic nature.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya continues the internal mystical methodology:

The Nectar of Absorption: The "libation" (tarpaṇa) offered here is not physical water. It represents the downpour of the nectar of immortality (amṛta) generated when the Kuṇḍalinī unites with Shiva in the crown center. Offering this supreme bliss back to the Devi is what truly satisfies Her cosmic nature.

name: सद्यःप्रसादिनी
wordByWord: सद्यःप्रसादिनी — she who grants grace immediately
summary: i. One who is pleased immediately.
ii. Sreedevee responds quickly to sincere worship and devotion. Her grace is immediate when approached with proper devotion.
searchText: 383 सद्यःप्रसादिनी • सद्यः एव प्रसीदतीति। सद्यः — immediately प्रसीदति — becomes gracious; is pleased सद्यःप्रसादिनी — she who grants grace immediately i. one who is pleased immediately. ii. sreedevee responds quickly to sincere worship and devotion. her grace is immediate when approached with proper devotion. in the saubhāgyabhāskara, bhāskararāya contrasts her with other long, arduous paths: instantaneous grace: while other disciplines require multiple lifetimes of dry, mechanical effort to yield a minor result, her path of pure devotion and internal surrender (prapatti) is so direct that she clears the practitioner's karmic blockages and illuminates their consciousness instantly (sadyaḥ), the moment sincere connection is made.
esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara, Bhāskararāya contrasts Her with other long, arduous paths:

Instantaneous Grace: While other disciplines require multiple lifetimes of dry, mechanical effort to yield a minor result, Her path of pure devotion and internal surrender (prapatti) is so direct that She clears the practitioner's karmic blockages and illuminates their consciousness instantly (sadyaḥ), the moment sincere connection is made.

name: विश्वसाक्षिणी
summary: i. One who is the witness of the universe.
ii. She is the witness-consciousness before which the whole universe appears and functions.
searchText: 384 विश्वसाक्षिणी • विश्वस्य साक्षिणी। विश्वस्य — of the universe साक्षिणी — witness विश्वसाक्षिणी — witness of the universe i. one who is the witness of the universe. ii. she is the witness-consciousness before which the whole universe appears and functions. bhāskararāya applies the vedāntic concept of the uninvolved witness (sākṣī): the cosmic witness: the universe moves through cycles of creation, maintenance, and collapse. she stands as the constant, unmoving lens that witnesses all of these transitions simultaneously. she does not get entangled in the changes; she is the light that makes the perception of the universe possible.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya applies the Vedāntic concept of the uninvolved witness (Sākṣī):

The Cosmic Witness: The universe moves through cycles of creation, maintenance, and collapse. She stands as the constant, unmoving lens that witnesses all of these transitions simultaneously. She does not get entangled in the changes; She is the light that makes the perception of the universe possible.

name: साक्षिवर्जिता
summary: i. One who has no witness beyond herself.
ii. She is the witness of all, but there is no other witness to her. Since she is the ultimate consciousness, she is beyond being witnessed by another.
searchText: 385 साक्षिवर्जिता • साक्षिणः वर्जिता। साक्षिणः — witness वर्जिता — devoid of; beyond साक्षिवर्जिता — beyond any witness i. one who has no witness beyond herself. ii. she is the witness of all, but there is no other witness to her. since she is the ultimate consciousness, she is beyond being witnessed by another. bhāskararāya constructs another profound non-dual paradox by matching 384 and 385: the absolute subject: while she witnesses everything (viśvasākṣiṇī), there is absolutely no one else back behind her to witness her. she is the ultimate, final subjective consciousness (draṣṭā). there is no second entity above or beyond her to turn her into an object, confirming her status as the final absolute.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya constructs another profound non-dual paradox by matching 384 and 385:

The Absolute Subject: While She witnesses everything (Viśvasākṣiṇī), there is absolutely no one else back behind Her to witness Her. She is the ultimate, final subjective consciousness (Draṣṭā). There is no second entity above or beyond Her to turn Her into an object, confirming Her status as the final absolute.

name: षडङ्गदेवतायुक्ता
wordByWord: षडङ्गदेवतायुक्ता — she who is united with the deities of the six limbs
summary: i. One who is accompanied by the deities of the six limbs.
ii. The six limbs connected with mantra and worship have their presiding deities. She is united with those deities and is worshipped together with them.
searchText: 386 षडङ्गदेवतायुक्ता • षण्णाम् अङ्गानां देवताभिः युक्ता। • षण्णाम् अङ्गानां देवता। तेन युक्ता। षण्णाम् — of six अङ्गानाम् — limbs; auxiliaries देवताभिः — with deities युक्ता — joined; endowed षडङ्गदेवतायुक्ता — she who is united with the deities of the six limbs i. one who is accompanied by the deities of the six limbs. ii. the six limbs connected with mantra and worship have their presiding deities. she is united with those deities and is worshipped together with them. bhāskararāya decodes this through mantric anatomy and ritual architecture: the six angas: in mantra practice, a text is consecrated across six limbs: heart (hṛdaya), head (śiras), crown-lock (śikhā), armor (kavaca), eyes (netra), and weapon (astra). she is ṣaḍaṅgadevatāyuktā because her supreme presence is perfectly protected and expressed through these six secondary guardian forces.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya decodes this through Mantric anatomy and ritual architecture:

The Six Angas: In mantra practice, a text is consecrated across six limbs: heart (Hṛdaya), head (Śiras), crown-lock (Śikhā), armor (Kavaca), eyes (Netra), and weapon (Astra). She is Ṣaḍaṅgadevatāyuktā because Her supreme presence is perfectly protected and expressed through these six secondary guardian forces.

name: षाड्गुण्यपरिपूरिता
samasa: • षण्णां गुणानां समूहः तेन परितः पूरिता।
wordByWord: षाड्गुण्यपरिपूरिता — she who is fully endowed with the six qualities
summary: i. One who is filled with the six divine qualities.
ii. The six qualities are traditionally understood as complete lordly excellences. Sreedevee is fully endowed with them.
searchText: 387 षाड्गुण्यपरिपूरिता • षण्णां गुणानां समूहः तेन परितः पूरिता। षण्णाम् — of six गुणानाम् — qualities समूहः — aggregate परिपूरिता — completely filled षाड्गुण्यपरिपूरिता — she who is fully endowed with the six qualities i. one who is filled with the six divine qualities. ii. the six qualities are traditionally understood as complete lordly excellences. sreedevee is fully endowed with them. bhāskararāya enumerates the six absolute attributes (gunas) that define lordship: the six virtues: these are ऐश्वर्य (absolute sovereignty), वीर्य (unmatched potency), यशस् (infinite fame), श्री (total prosperity), ज्ञान (omniscience), and वैराग्य (complete dispassion). she contains all six in their absolute peak state, making her the personal lord of the cosmos.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya enumerates the six absolute attributes (Gunas) that define Lordship:

The Six Virtues: These are ऐश्वर्य (absolute sovereignty), वीर्य (unmatched potency), यशस् (infinite fame), श्री (total prosperity), ज्ञान (omniscience), and वैराग्य (complete dispassion). She contains all six in their absolute peak state, making Her the personal Lord of the cosmos.

name: नित्यक्लिन्ना
wordByWord: क्लिन्ना — moist; melted; softened with compassion
summary: i. One who is always softened or melted.
ii. The name indicates a state of constant compassion and tenderness toward devotees. It is also connected with the Nitya called Nityaklinna in Sri Vidya worship.
