searchText: 388 नित्यक्लिन्ना • नित्यं क्लिन्ना। नित्यम् — always क्लिन्ना — moist; melted; softened with compassion नित्यक्लिन्ना — ever softened; ever compassionate i. one who is always softened or melted. ii. the name indicates a state of constant compassion and tenderness toward devotees. it is also connected with the nitya called nityaklinna in sri vidya worship. bhāskararāya moves this from standard imagery to specific śrīvidyā nomenclature: perpetual compassion: to be "moist" means her heart never dries up into cold indifference. she is perpetually soft and melting with mercy for her children. the nityā goddess: it also explicitly names her identity as नित्यक्लिन्ना, the third nityā goddess governing the structural flow of lunar time and nectar within the śrī cakra.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya moves this from standard imagery to specific Śrīvidyā nomenclature:
Perpetual Compassion: To be "moist" means Her heart never dries up into cold indifference. She is perpetually soft and melting with mercy for Her children.
The Nityā Goddess: It also explicitly names Her identity as नित्यक्लिन्ना, the third Nityā goddess governing the structural flow of lunar time and nectar within the Śrī Cakra.

name: निरुपमा
wordByWord: निरुपमा — she who has no comparison
summary: i. One who is incomparable.
ii. There is no object or being equal to her, and no comparison can adequately describe her.
searchText: 389 निरुपमा • निर्गता उपमा यस्याः सा। निर्गता — gone; absent उपमा — comparison; equal यस्याः — of whom; whose निरुपमा — she who has no comparison i. one who is incomparable. ii. there is no object or being equal to her, and no comparison can adequately describe her. in the saubhāgyabhāskara, this states her absolute uniqueness: beyond comparison: a comparison can only occur if there are two similar things in the universe. since she is the unique, non-dual, unmatched absolute reality (advaya), there is absolutely nothing else in existence that can be placed next to her as an equal or an analogy.
esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara, this states Her absolute uniqueness:

Beyond Comparison: A comparison can only occur if there are two similar things in the universe. Since She is the unique, non-dual, unmatched absolute reality (Advaya), there is absolutely nothing else in existence that can be placed next to Her as an equal or an analogy.

name: निर्वाणसुखदायिनी
samasa: • निर्गतं बाणं यस्मिन् सुखे तत् सुखं ददातीति।
wordByWord: निर्वाणसुखदायिनी — giver of the happiness of liberation
summary: i. One who grants the bliss of Nirvana.
ii. Nirvana is liberation, the state beyond bondage. Sreedevee grants that supreme happiness to the devotee.
iii. Another explanation plays on the word as nir-bana, free from the arrow of desire or suffering, and she grants that freedom.
searchText: 390 निर्वाणसुखदायिनी • निर्वाणस्य सुखं ददातीति। • निर्गतं बाणं यस्मिन् सुखे तत् सुखं ददातीति। निर्वाणस्य — of liberation सुखम् — happiness; bliss ददाति — gives निर्वाणसुखदायिनी — giver of the happiness of liberation i. one who grants the bliss of nirvana. ii. nirvana is liberation, the state beyond bondage. sreedevee grants that supreme happiness to the devotee. iii. another explanation plays on the word as nir-bana, free from the arrow of desire or suffering, and she grants that freedom. bhāskararāya distinguishes her gifts from temporary pleasures: the bliss of extinction: worldly happiness is brief and followed by fatigue. nirvāṇa-sukha is the permanent, unshakeable bliss of the extinction of the individual ego. she is the ultimate dispenser of this final spiritual resting state.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya distinguishes Her gifts from temporary pleasures:

The Bliss of Extinction: Worldly happiness is brief and followed by fatigue. Nirvāṇa-sukha is the permanent, unshakeable bliss of the extinction of the individual ego. She is the ultimate dispenser of this final spiritual resting state.

name: नित्याषोडशिकारूपा
samasa: • नित्याश्च ताः षोडशिकाश्च तासां रूपाणि यस्याः सा।
wordByWord: नित्याषोडशिकारूपा — she whose forms are the sixteen Nityas
summary: i. One who is in the form of the sixteen Nityas.
ii. The sixteen Nitya deities are forms of Sreedevee. She is their collective and essential form.
searchText: 391 नित्याषोडशिकारूपा • नित्याश्च ताः षोडशिकाश्च तासां रूपाणि यस्याः सा। नित्याः — eternal deities; nityas षोडशिकाः — the sixteen forms रूपाणि — forms यस्याः — of whom; whose नित्याषोडशिकारूपा — she whose forms are the sixteen nityas i. one who is in the form of the sixteen nityas. ii. the sixteen nitya deities are forms of sreedevee. she is their collective and essential form. bhāskararāya unpacks the chronological and geometric mapping of the śrī cakra: the sixteen aspects of time: in śrīvidyā cosmology, the passage of time and lunar cycles is governed by sixteen specialized rays of energy known as the षोडश नित्याः (ṣoḍaśa nityāḥ), running from kameśvarī to citra. she is not separate from them; she is the collective matrix that embodies all sixteen phases of time.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya unpacks the chronological and geometric mapping of the Śrī Cakra:

The Sixteen Aspects of Time: In Śrīvidyā cosmology, the passage of time and lunar cycles is governed by sixteen specialized rays of energy known as the षोडश नित्याः (Ṣoḍaśa Nityāḥ), running from Kameśvarī to Citra. She is not separate from them; She is the collective matrix that embodies all sixteen phases of time.

name: श्रीकण्ठार्धशरीरिणी
samasa: • श्रीः कण्ठे यस्य सः श्रीकण्ठः। तस्य अर्धं शरीरम् अस्याः अस्तीति।
wordByWord: श्रीकण्ठार्धशरीरिणी — she who shares half the body of Srikantha
summary: i. One who has half the body of Srikantha.
ii. Srikantha is Shiva. Sreedevee shares his body and is inseparable from him. The name expresses the Ardhanarishvara principle, the unity of Shiva and Shakti.
searchText: 392 श्रीकण्ठार्धशरीरिणी • श्रीः कण्ठे यस्य सः श्रीकण्ठः। तस्य अर्धं शरीरम् अस्याः अस्तीति। • श्रीकण्ठेन अर्धशरीरम् अस्याः अस्तीति। श्रीकण्ठः — shiva, the auspicious-throated one अर्धम् — half शरीरम् — body अस्याः — of her श्रीकण्ठार्धशरीरिणी — she who shares half the body of srikantha i. one who has half the body of srikantha. ii. srikantha is shiva. sreedevee shares his body and is inseparable from him. the name expresses the ardhanarishvara principle, the unity of shiva and shakti. bhāskararāya details the sublime non-dual state of ardhanārīśvara: the fusion of being: she is the left half of shiva's physical and cosmic form. bhāskararāya explains that this represents the absolute structural equilibrium of reality: consciousness (shiva) and dynamic energy (shakti) do not exist as separate neighbors; they are fused together into a single, unified body.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya details the sublime non-dual state of Ardhanārīśvara:

The Fusion of Being: She is the left half of Shiva's physical and cosmic form. Bhāskararāya explains that this represents the absolute structural equilibrium of reality: consciousness (Shiva) and dynamic energy (Shakti) do not exist as separate neighbors; they are fused together into a single, unified body.

name: प्रभावती
summary: i. One who is radiant.
ii. Prabha is light and splendour. Sreedevee possesses supreme radiance.
searchText: 393 प्रभावती • प्रभा अस्या अस्तीति। प्रभा — light; radiance अस्ति — exists प्रभावती — she who possesses radiance i. one who is radiant. ii. prabha is light and splendour. sreedevee possesses supreme radiance. bhāskararāya moves this from a simple visual characteristic to her status as the source of light: the owner of splendor: she holds absolute possession over the creative rays of light. any object, star, or intellect that exhibits illumination or brilliance is merely borrowing a fraction of her baseline light (prabhā).
esoteric: Bhāskararāya moves this from a simple visual characteristic to Her status as the source of light:

The Owner of Splendor: She holds absolute possession over the creative rays of light. Any object, star, or intellect that exhibits illumination or brilliance is merely borrowing a fraction of Her baseline light (Prabhā).

name: प्रभारूपा
wordByWord: प्रभारूपा — she whose form is radiance
summary: i. One whose form is radiance.
ii. She is not merely radiant; radiance itself is her form.
searchText: 394 प्रभारूपा • प्रभा एव रूपं यस्याः सा। प्रभा — radiance एव — itself रूपम् — form यस्याः — of whom; whose प्रभारूपा — she whose form is radiance i. one whose form is radiance. ii. she is not merely radiant; radiance itself is her form. bhāskararāya builds a direct correction to the preceding name to prevent duality: identity with light: immediately after stating she possesses light (prabhāvatī), the text states she is light (prabhārūpā). bhāskararāya explains that she does not look like a person holding a lamp; her entire essential body is made of nothing but pure, unconditioned luminous energy.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya builds a direct correction to the preceding name to prevent duality:

Identity with Light: Immediately after stating She possesses light (Prabhāvatī), the text states She is light (Prabhārūpā). Bhāskararāya explains that She does not look like a person holding a lamp; Her entire essential body is made of nothing but pure, unconditioned luminous energy.

name: प्रसिद्धा
summary: i. One who is well known.
ii. She is known by all as the inner “I”. Because the awareness “I am” is common to all experience, she is famous or evident as the Self.
searchText: 395 प्रसिद्धा • (सर्वैः अहम् इति वेद्यत्वात्) प्रसिद्धा। सर्वैः — by all अहम् इति — as “i” वेद्यत्वात् — because of being known प्रसिद्धा — well known; evident i. one who is well known. ii. she is known by all as the inner “i”. because the awareness “i am” is common to all experience, she is famous or evident as the self. bhāskararāya provides one of his most brilliant philosophical insights for this simple name: the most immediate intimacy: how is the absolute devi "famous" or "well-known"? bhāskararāya explains that she is the most famous entity because she is experienced by every single living creature every second as the immediate, self-evident feeling of "अहम्" ("i am"). no one needs a text to prove their own existence; she is that baseline presence, making her the most instantly recognized reality.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya provides one of his most brilliant philosophical insights for this simple name:

The Most Immediate Intimacy: How is the absolute Devi "famous" or "well-known"? Bhāskararāya explains that She is the most famous entity because She is experienced by every single living creature every second as the immediate, self-evident feeling of "अहम्" ("I am"). No one needs a text to prove their own existence; She is that baseline presence, making Her the most instantly recognized reality.

name: परमेश्वरी
summary: i. One who is the supreme ruler.
ii. She is the highest Ishvari, the supreme sovereign power beyond all other rulers.
searchText: 396 परमेश्वरी • परमा च सा ईश्वरी च। परमा — supreme ईश्वरी — ruler; goddess परमेश्वरी — supreme ruler i. one who is the supreme ruler. ii. she is the highest ishvari, the supreme sovereign power beyond all other rulers. in the saubhāgyabhāskara, this states her position over the entire causal chain: the ultimate sovereign: lesser lords rule specific departments of matter. as parameśvarī, she is the supreme crown ruler sitting above all primary loops of causality, governing both the physical universes and the spiritual paths with absolute independence (svātantrya).
esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara, this states Her position over the entire causal chain:

The Ultimate Sovereign: Lesser lords rule specific departments of matter. As Parameśvarī, She is the supreme crown ruler sitting above all primary loops of causality, governing both the physical universes and the spiritual paths with absolute independence (Svātantrya).

name: मूलप्रकृतिः
summary: i. One who is the original Prakriti.
ii. She is the root nature from which manifestation proceeds. As the original Prakriti, she is the source of all names and forms.
searchText: 397 मूलप्रकृतिः • मूला प्रकृतिः। • मूलस्य प्रकृतिः। मूला — root; original प्रकृतिः — primordial nature मूलप्रकृतिः — the root primordial nature i. one who is the original prakriti. ii. she is the root nature from which manifestation proceeds. as the original prakriti, she is the source of all names and forms. bhāskararāya explains her identity with the foundational matrix of the sāṅkhya and śākta systems: the primordial womb: मूलप्रकृति (mūlaprakṛti) is the state where the three guṇas (sattva, rajas, tamas) exist in perfect, unmanifested equilibrium. she is the baseline material and kinetic cause out of which the entire physical universe subsequently grows. she is the root source that has no root before it.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya explains Her identity with the foundational matrix of the Sāṅkhya and Śākta systems:

The Primordial Womb: मूलप्रकृति (Mūlaprakṛti) is the state where the three guṇas (sattva, rajas, tamas) exist in perfect, unmanifested equilibrium. She is the baseline material and kinetic cause out of which the entire physical universe subsequently grows. She is the root source that has no root before it.

name: अव्यक्ता
summary: i. One who is unmanifest.
ii. Before manifestation she remains beyond perception and description. She is the unmanifest source.
searchText: 398 अव्यक्ता • न व्यक्ता। न — not व्यक्ता — manifest अव्यक्ता — unmanifest i. one who is unmanifest. ii. before manifestation she remains beyond perception and description. she is the unmanifest source. bhāskararāya defines her hidden, causal state: beyond sensory perception: because she is the primordial source, she cannot be perceived by the human eyes, touched by the hands, or grasped by the ordinary rational mind. she exists in her pristine, unmanifested state (avyakta), completely hidden behind the screen of her own cosmic projections.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya defines Her hidden, causal state:

Beyond Sensory Perception: Because She is the primordial source, She cannot be perceived by the human eyes, touched by the hands, or grasped by the ordinary rational mind. She exists in Her pristine, unmanifested state (Avyakta), completely hidden behind the screen of Her own cosmic projections.

name: व्यक्ताऽव्यक्तस्वरूपिणी
samasa: • व्यक्तं च अव्यक्तं स्वरूपम् अस्याः इति।
wordByWord: व्यक्ताऽव्यक्तस्वरूपिणी — she whose form is both manifest and unmanifest
summary: i. One whose form is both manifest and unmanifest.
ii. She appears as the manifested universe and also remains as the unmanifest cause. Both states are her own form.
searchText: 399 व्यक्ताऽव्यक्तस्वरूपिणी • व्यक्तं च अव्यक्तं स्वरूपम् अस्याः इति। व्यक्तम् — manifest अव्यक्तम् — unmanifest स्वरूपम् — essential form अस्याः — of her व्यक्ताऽव्यक्तस्वरूपिणी — she whose form is both manifest and unmanifest i. one whose form is both manifest and unmanifest. ii. she appears as the manifested universe and also remains as the unmanifest cause. both states are her own form. bhāskararāya reconciles the preceding structural tracks: the total synthesis: she is not only the unmanifested source (avyaktā). when the universe shoots forth into visible names, forms, and elements, that visible creation (vyakta) is also her body. she is simultaneously the hidden root and the visible tree, encompassing all dimensions of reality.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya reconciles the preceding structural tracks:

The Total Synthesis: She is not only the unmanifested source (Avyaktā). When the universe shoots forth into visible names, forms, and elements, that visible creation (Vyakta) is also Her body. She is simultaneously the hidden root and the visible tree, encompassing all dimensions of reality.

name: व्यापिनी
summary: i. One who pervades everything.
ii. She pervades all objects, beings, states and worlds as their inner reality.
searchText: 400 व्यापिनी • व्याप्नोतीति। व्याप्नोति — pervades व्यापिनी — all-pervading one i. one who pervades everything. ii. she pervades all objects, beings, states and worlds as their inner reality. in the saubhāgyabhāskara, bhāskararāya concludes this major block of names by stating her absolute omnipresence: absolute omnipresence: there is not a single point in space, a single atom of matter, or a single ripple of thought where she is absent. she completely saturates, fills, and encompasses every level of creation from the inside out, serving as the continuous, all-pervading fabric of existence.
esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara, Bhāskararāya concludes this major block of names by stating Her absolute omnipresence:

Absolute Omnipresence: There is not a single point in space, a single atom of matter, or a single ripple of thought where She is absent. She completely saturates, fills, and encompasses every level of creation from the inside out, serving as the continuous, all-pervading fabric of existence.

name: विविधाकारा
wordByWord: विविधाकारा — she who has manifold forms
summary: i. One who has various forms.
ii. The many forms seen in the universe are forms of Sreedevee. She appears in countless shapes according to the needs of creation and worship.
searchText: 401 विविधाकारा • विविधाः आकाराः यस्याः सा। विविधाः — various; manifold आकाराः — forms यस्याः — of whom; whose विविधाकारा — she who has manifold forms i. one who has various forms. ii. the many forms seen in the universe are forms of sreedevee. she appears in countless shapes according to the needs of creation and worship. in the saubhāgyabhāskara, bhāskararāya clarifies that her single, uniform essence of pure consciousness appears differentiated through the mirror of matter: the infinite manifestation: since everything in the universe—from gods to elements—is a projection of her dynamic energy (śakti), every shape, entity, and configuration in existence is a representation of her own body. beyond monolithic limits: this counters any view that limits the divine to a single, static appearance. she plays effortlessly through an infinity of structural variations.
esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara, Bhāskararāya clarifies that Her single, uniform essence of pure consciousness appears differentiated through the mirror of matter:

The Infinite Manifestation: Since everything in the universe—from gods to elements—is a projection of Her dynamic energy (Śakti), every shape, entity, and configuration in existence is a representation of Her own body.
Beyond Monolithic Limits: This counters any view that limits the divine to a single, static appearance. She plays effortlessly through an infinity of structural variations.

name: विद्याऽविद्यास्वरूपिणी
samasa: • विद्या च अविद्या च स्वरूपम् अस्याः अस्तीति।
wordByWord: विद्याऽविद्यास्वरूपिणी — she whose form is knowledge and ignorance
summary: i. One who is in the form of knowledge and ignorance.
ii. Vidya leads to liberation, while avidya is the basis of bondage and manifestation. Both function within her power and are forms of her cosmic play.
searchText: 402 विद्याऽविद्यास्वरूपिणी • विद्या च अविद्या च स्वरूपम् अस्याः अस्तीति। विद्या — knowledge अविद्या — ignorance स्वरूपम् — form अस्याः — of her विद्याऽविद्यास्वरूपिणी — she whose form is knowledge and ignorance i. one who is in the form of knowledge and ignorance. ii. vidya leads to liberation, while avidya is the basis of bondage and manifestation. both function within her power and are forms of her cosmic play. bhāskararāya details a profound, non-dual reconciliation of cosmic forces: the power of liberation: as विद्या (vidyā), she is the spiritual insight that turns the mind inward, dissolves the ego, and reveals the absolute identity of the self (mokṣa-pradā). the power of bondage: as अविद्या (avidyā), she operates as the cosmic obscuration that project differences, enabling the relative play of the world. bhāskararāya notes that both are functional shifts of the same singular divine matrix, keeping the cosmic rhythm intact.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya details a profound, non-dual reconciliation of cosmic forces:
The Power of Liberation: As विद्या (Vidyā), She is the spiritual insight that turns the mind inward, dissolves the ego, and reveals the absolute identity of the Self (Mokṣa-pradā).

The Power of Bondage: As अविद्या (Avidyā), She operates as the cosmic obscuration that project differences, enabling the relative play of the world. Bhāskararāya notes that both are functional shifts of the same singular divine matrix, keeping the cosmic rhythm intact.

name: महाकामेशनयनकुमुदाह्लादकौमुदी
samasa: • महाकामेशस्य नयने एव कुमुदे। तयोः आह्लादे कौमुदी इव।
wordByWord: महाकामेशनयनकुमुदाह्लादकौमुदी — she who is moonlight delighting the water-lily eyes of Mahakamesha
summary: i. One who is like moonlight that delights the lily-like eyes of Mahakamesha.
ii. The eyes of Mahakamesha are compared to kumuda flowers, which bloom in moonlight. Sreedevee is the moonlight that gives delight to those eyes.
searchText: 403 महाकामेशनयनकुमुदाह्लादकौमुदी • महाकामेशस्य नयने एव कुमुदे। तयोः आह्लादे कौमुदी इव। महाकामेशस्य — of mahakamesha नयने — eyes कुमुदे — water-lilies आह्लादे — in delighting कौमुदी — moonlight महाकामेशनयनकुमुदाह्लादकौमुदी — she who is moonlight delighting the water-lily eyes of mahakamesha i. one who is like moonlight that delights the lily-like eyes of mahakamesha. ii. the eyes of mahakamesha are compared to kumuda flowers, which bloom in moonlight. sreedevee is the moonlight that gives delight to those eyes. bhāskararāya highlights the intimate, non-dual delight of the absolute couple: the radiance of delight: the night-lotus (kumuda) opens only when struck by the cool rays of the moon. when mahākāmeśvara looks upon her supreme beauty, his eyes swell with immediate spiritual ecstasy. she is the source of that bliss, anchoring the baseline union of consciousness and energy.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya highlights the intimate, non-dual delight of the absolute couple:

The Radiance of Delight: The night-lotus (kumuda) opens only when struck by the cool rays of the moon. When Mahākāmeśvara looks upon Her supreme beauty, His eyes swell with immediate spiritual ecstasy. She is the source of that bliss, anchoring the baseline union of consciousness and energy.

name: भक्तहार्दतमोभेदभानुमद्भानुसंततिः
samasa: • भक्तानां हृदि भावनी यानि तमांसि तेषां भेदे भानुमतः भानुसंततिः।
wordByWord: भक्तहार्दतमोभेदभानुमद्भानुसंततिः — she who is a stream of sun-rays removing the darkness in devotees’ hearts
summary: i. One who removes the darkness in the hearts of devotees like the rays of the sun.
ii. The darkness in the heart is ignorance and sorrow. Her grace shines like sunlight and dispels that inner darkness.
searchText: 404 भक्तहार्दतमोभेदभानुमद्भानुसंततिः • भक्तानां हृदि भावनी यानि तमांसि तेषां भेदे भानुमतः भानुसंततिः। भक्तानाम् — of devotees हार्द — in the heart तमः — darkness भेद — breaking; removing भानुमत् — sun-like भानुसंततिः — stream of rays भक्तहार्दतमोभेदभानुमद्भानुसंततिः — she who is a stream of sun-rays removing the darkness in devotees’ hearts i. one who removes the darkness in the hearts of devotees like the rays of the sun. ii. the darkness in the heart is ignorance and sorrow. her grace shines like sunlight and dispels that inner darkness. in the saubhāgyabhāskara, bhāskararāya treats this as a formula for the removal of spiritual distress: the solar dispersion: the deep-seated ignorance (avidyā) and accumulated mental impressions (vāsanās) are like pitch darkness inside the heart cavity. sincere concentration on her works like a high-noon sun, vaporizing those dense blocks instantly and replacing them with clear light.
esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara, Bhāskararāya treats this as a formula for the removal of spiritual distress:

The Solar Dispersion: The deep-seated ignorance (avidyā) and accumulated mental impressions (vāsanās) are like pitch darkness inside the heart cavity. Sincere concentration on Her works like a high-noon sun, vaporizing those dense blocks instantly and replacing them with clear light.

name: शिवदूती
wordByWord: शिवदूती — she for whom Shiva is messenger; or the messenger of Shiva
summary: i. One whose messenger is Shiva, or who is the messenger of Shiva.
ii. The name recalls the form in which Shiva himself acts as her messenger. It also indicates her inseparable relation with Shiva.
searchText: 405 शिवदूती • शिवः दूतः यस्याः सा। • शिवस्य दूती। शिवः — shiva दूतः — messenger यस्याः — of whom; whose शिवदूती — she for whom shiva is messenger; or the messenger of shiva i. one whose messenger is shiva, or who is the messenger of shiva. ii. the name recalls the form in which shiva himself acts as her messenger. it also indicates her inseparable relation with shiva. bhāskararāya connects this name directly to the core texts of the devī māhātmya: the ultimate sovereign: during the cosmic battle against the demonic forces (specifically śumbha and niśumbha), she sent lord śiva himself as her official emissary to issue the final ultimatum. this demonstrates her immense absolute authority over even the highest tier of the trinity.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya connects this name directly to the core texts of the Devī Māhātmya:

The Ultimate Sovereign: During the cosmic battle against the demonic forces (specifically Śumbha and Niśumbha), She sent Lord Śiva Himself as Her official emissary to issue the final ultimatum. This demonstrates Her immense absolute authority over even the highest tier of the trinity.

name: शिवाराध्या
wordByWord: शिवाराध्या — she who is worshipped by Shiva
summary: i. One who is worshipped by Shiva.
ii. Even Shiva worships her. This establishes her supreme status as the power of Shiva himself.
searchText: 406 शिवाराध्या • शिवेन आराध्या। शिवेन — by shiva आराध्या — worshipped; adored शिवाराध्या — she who is worshipped by shiva i. one who is worshipped by shiva. ii. even shiva worships her. this establishes her supreme status as the power of shiva himself. bhāskararāya highlights the profound, reciprocal adoration between the two poles of reality: the contemplation of consciousness: śiva is the static, pure witness consciousness. to experience his own creative power and bliss, he must contemplate and worship her—the dynamic energy (śakti). this confirms that she is the ultimate object of adoration even for the lord of ascetics.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya highlights the profound, reciprocal adoration between the two poles of reality:

The Contemplation of Consciousness: Śiva is the static, pure witness consciousness. To experience His own creative power and bliss, He must contemplate and worship Her—the dynamic energy (Śakti). This confirms that She is the ultimate object of adoration even for the lord of ascetics.

name: शिवमूर्तिः
wordByWord: शिवमूर्तिः — she whose form is Shiva
summary: i. One whose form is Shiva.
ii. She is not different from Shiva. Her form is auspiciousness itself and is identical with Shiva-consciousness.
searchText: 407 शिवमूर्तिः • शिवः एव मूर्तिः यस्याः सा। शिवः — shiva; auspiciousness एव — itself मूर्तिः — form यस्याः — of whom; whose शिवमूर्तिः — she whose form is shiva i. one whose form is shiva. ii. she is not different from shiva. her form is auspiciousness itself and is identical with shiva-consciousness. bhāskararāya removes any remaining concept of separation between the two: non-dual identity: lest a practitioner think that śiva and śakti are separate entities competing for status, this name states that her very body is śiva. they are the structural twin aspects of a single, indivisible absolute, like the word and its meaning.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya removes any remaining concept of separation between the two:

Non-Dual Identity: Lest a practitioner think that Śiva and Śakti are separate entities competing for status, this name states that Her very body is Śiva. They are the structural twin aspects of a single, indivisible absolute, like the word and its meaning.

name: शिवङ्करी
summary: i. One who does good and makes all auspicious.
ii. She confers welfare and auspiciousness on devotees and turns life toward the good.
searchText: 408 शिवङ्करी • शिवं करोतीति। शिवम् — auspiciousness; welfare करोति — makes; does शिवङ्करी — she who causes auspiciousness i. one who does good and makes all auspicious. ii. she confers welfare and auspiciousness on devotees and turns life toward the good. in the saubhāgyabhāskara, bhāskararāya defines śivam as the highest welfare: the dispenser of peace: she transforms the chaotic, turbulent life of the practitioner into a state of deep, tranquil harmony. by dissolving negative karmic tendencies, she naturally generates peace, ultimately converting the individual soul into the status of śiva himself.
esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara, Bhāskararāya defines Śivam as the highest welfare:

The Dispenser of Peace: She transforms the chaotic, turbulent life of the practitioner into a state of deep, tranquil harmony. By dissolving negative karmic tendencies, She naturally generates peace, ultimately converting the individual soul into the status of Śiva Himself.

name: शिवप्रिया
wordByWord: शिवप्रिया — beloved of Shiva; she to whom Shiva is dear
summary: i. One who is dear to Shiva.
ii. She is beloved of Shiva, and Shiva is dear to her. The name expresses the mutual unity and love of Shiva and Shakti.
searchText: 409 शिवप्रिया • शिवस्य प्रिया। • शिवः प्रियः यस्याः सा। शिवस्य — of shiva प्रिया — beloved शिवः — shiva प्रियः — dear शिवप्रिया — beloved of shiva; she to whom shiva is dear i. one who is dear to shiva. ii. she is beloved of shiva, and shiva is dear to her. the name expresses the mutual unity and love of shiva and shakti. bhāskararāya explores this mutual, cosmic love: the inseparable bond: she is the eternal consort of śiva. this intense love signifies that energy cannot exist without a consciousness background, and consciousness cannot manifest without energy. their deep mutual attraction holds the structure of the cosmos together.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya explores this mutual, cosmic love:

The Inseparable Bond: She is the eternal consort of Śiva. This intense love signifies that energy cannot exist without a consciousness background, and consciousness cannot manifest without energy. Their deep mutual attraction holds the structure of the cosmos together.

name: शिवपरा
wordByWord: शिवपरा — she whose supreme object is Shiva
summary: i. One for whom Shiva is the supreme.
ii. She is established in Shiva as the supreme reality. The name also shows her non-difference from Shiva.
searchText: 410 शिवपरा • शिवः एव परः यस्याः सा। शिवः — shiva एव — alone परः — supreme goal यस्याः — of whom; whose शिवपरा — she whose supreme object is shiva i. one for whom shiva is the supreme. ii. she is established in shiva as the supreme reality. the name also shows her non-difference from shiva. bhāskararāya defines this through the lens of absolute devotion to the source: perpetual orientation: as the active, kinetic power, her entire operational intent is to lead everything back into the peaceful, unmoving baseline of static consciousness (śiva). she remains completely absorbed in him, demonstrating the model of perfect internal surrender.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya defines this through the lens of absolute devotion to the source:

Perpetual Orientation: As the active, kinetic power, Her entire operational intent is to lead everything back into the peaceful, unmoving baseline of static consciousness (Śiva). She remains completely absorbed in Him, demonstrating the model of perfect internal surrender.

name: शिष्टेष्टा
wordByWord: शिष्टैः — by the cultured and righteous
शिष्टेष्टा — she who is loved and worshipped by the noble
summary: i. One who is loved and worshipped by the noble and cultured.
ii. Shishta means the righteous, learned and disciplined. She is their chosen deity and object of worship.
searchText: 411 शिष्टेष्टा • शिष्टैः इष्टा। • शिष्टानि इष्टानि यस्याः सा। • शिष्टानाम् इष्टा। • शिष्टाः इष्टाः यस्याः सा। शिष्टैः — by the cultured and righteous इष्टा — loved; worshipped; desired शिष्टानाम् — of the noble शिष्टेष्टा — she who is loved and worshipped by the noble i. one who is loved and worshipped by the noble and cultured. ii. shishta means the righteous, learned and disciplined. she is their chosen deity and object of worship. bhāskararāya details the criteria for true alignment with her: the adoration of the wise: शिष्ट refers to those who strictly follow the structural disciplines of scripture and possess refined, clean intellects. she is the ultimate object of their desire. concurrently, she holds these virtuous seekers exceptionally dear, protecting their spiritual progress.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya details the criteria for true alignment with Her:

The Adoration of the Wise: शिष्ट refers to those who strictly follow the structural disciplines of scripture and possess refined, clean intellects. She is the ultimate object of their desire. Concurrently, She holds these virtuous seekers exceptionally dear, protecting their spiritual progress.

name: शिष्टपूजिता
wordByWord: शिष्टैः — by the noble; by the disciplined
summary: i. One who is worshipped by noble persons.
ii. The shishtas, those who follow right knowledge and conduct, worship her.
searchText: 412 शिष्टपूजिता • शिष्टैः पूजिता। शिष्टैः — by the noble; by the disciplined पूजिता — worshipped शिष्टपूजिता — worshipped by the noble i. one who is worshipped by noble persons. ii. the shishtas, those who follow right knowledge and conduct, worship her. bhāskararāya reinforces the validity of traditional lineages: the validated path: this title emphasizes that her worship is not an eccentric or lawless practice. the greatest seers, masters of grammar, and realized souls of history have systematically adored her, confirming that her path is grounded in absolute truth and moral rectitude.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya reinforces the validity of traditional lineages:

The Validated Path: This title emphasizes that Her worship is not an eccentric or lawless practice. The greatest seers, masters of grammar, and realized souls of history have systematically adored Her, confirming that Her path is grounded in absolute truth and moral rectitude.

name: अप्रमेया
wordByWord: प्रमेया — measurable; knowable as an object of proof
अप्रमेया — immeasurable; not an object of limited knowledge
summary: i. One who is immeasurable.
ii. She cannot be measured by the ordinary means of knowledge. Being the very basis of knowledge, she cannot be known as a limited object.
searchText: 413 अप्रमेया • न प्रमेया। न — not प्रमेया — measurable; knowable as an object of proof अप्रमेया — immeasurable; not an object of limited knowledge i. one who is immeasurable. ii. she cannot be measured by the ordinary means of knowledge. being the very basis of knowledge, she cannot be known as a limited object. bhāskararāya applies a fundamental rule of epistemology (pramāṇa-śāstra): beyond logic and proof: an object is understood when it is measured by instruments of knowledge like perception, inference, or logic (pramāṇa). but because she is the foundational subjective consciousness that animates those very instruments, she can never be turned into an external object of proof. she is the knower, never the known.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya applies a fundamental rule of epistemology (pramāṇa-śāstra):

Beyond Logic and Proof: An object is understood when it is measured by instruments of knowledge like perception, inference, or logic (pramāṇa). But because She is the foundational subjective consciousness that animates those very instruments, She can never be turned into an external object of proof. She is the knower, never the known.

name: स्वप्रकाशा
summary: i. One who is self-luminous.
ii. She shines by herself and does not need another light to reveal her. As consciousness, she reveals all things and is self-revealed.
searchText: 414 स्वप्रकाशा • स्वः प्रकाशः यस्याः सा। स्वः — self; own प्रकाशः — light; illumination यस्याः — of whom; whose स्वप्रकाशा — self-luminous one i. one who is self-luminous. ii. she shines by herself and does not need another light to reveal her. as consciousness, she reveals all things and is self-revealed. in the saubhāgyabhāskara, bhāskararāya sets this up as the resolution to the preceding name: independent light: if she cannot be known by ordinary proofs (aprameyā), does she remain in total darkness? no. she is svaprakāśā—she requires no external lamp, intellect, or text to illuminate her. just as the sun is seen by its own light, she is realized through her own self-revealing presence.
esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara, Bhāskararāya sets this up as the resolution to the preceding name:

Independent Light: If She cannot be known by ordinary proofs (Aprameyā), does She remain in total darkness? No. She is Svaprakāśā—She requires no external lamp, intellect, or text to illuminate Her. Just as the sun is seen by its own light, She is realized through Her own self-revealing presence.

name: मनोवाचामगोचरा
samasa: • मनांसि च वाचश्च तेषां तासां च अगोचरा।
wordByWord: • अगोचरा — beyond the field or range of perception.
• मनोवाचामगोचरा — She who is beyond the range of mind and speech.
summary: (i) One who is beyond the range of mind and speech.

(ii) That is, She is not comprehensible by mind and speech. Sruti also, in the Taittiriya Upanishad (II-9-2), says: from whence speech and mind turn away unable to reach — Yato Vacho Nivartante Aprapya Manasa Saha.
(iii) In Vishnu Purana, Prahlada says, “I bow down to the supreme Ishvaree who transcends speech and mind and who can be grasped by the wisdom of the wise alone.”
(iv) She is one in whom no object of thought or speech is to be found.
(v) By mentioning mind and speech, all the eleven organs and Para, etc., speeches are indicated.

(vi) These organs have their own ways of understanding objects. Only if the objects to be understood are within some limits can these organs recognise them. But whatever Sreedevee has is limitless, boundless and infinite. Hence She does not have anything which mind and other organs can recognise.

(vii) When read with the previous name, ‘A’ is to be prefixed to this name. Then it can be split as Amano + Vachma + Gochara. That is, She is beyond the reach of those whose mind and speech are immature. Alternatively, She can be reached only by those whose mind and speech are mature.

(viii) Kathopanishad says that by mind alone it should be perceived. This contradiction is removed in the Bhamati by adding “immature and mature” or “not purified and purified” respectively to the word “mind”.
searchText: 415 मनोवाचामगोचरा • मनांसि च वाचश्च तेषां तासां च अगोचरा। • मनांसि — minds. • वाचः — words, speech. • तेषाम् / तासाम् — of them. • अगोचरा — beyond the field or range of perception. • मनोवाचामगोचरा — she who is beyond the range of mind and speech. (i) one who is beyond the range of mind and speech. (ii) that is, she is not comprehensible by mind and speech. sruti also, in the taittiriya upanishad (ii-9-2), says: from whence speech and mind turn away unable to reach — yato vacho nivartante aprapya manasa saha. (iii) in vishnu purana, prahlada says, “i bow down to the supreme ishvaree who transcends speech and mind and who can be grasped by the wisdom of the wise alone.” (iv) she is one in whom no object of thought or speech is to be found. (v) by mentioning mind and speech, all the eleven organs and para, etc., speeches are indicated. (vi) these organs have their own ways of understanding objects. only if the objects to be understood are within some limits can these organs recognise them. but whatever sreedevee has is limitless, boundless and infinite. hence she does not have anything which mind and other organs can recognise. (vii) when read with the previous name, ‘a’ is to be prefixed to this name. then it can be split as amano + vachma + gochara. that is, she is beyond the reach of those whose mind and speech are immature. alternatively, she can be reached only by those whose mind and speech are mature. (viii) kathopanishad says that by mind alone it should be perceived. this contradiction is removed in the bhamati by adding “immature and mature” or “not purified and purified” respectively to the word “mind”. bhāskararāya evokes the definitive taittirīya upaniṣad text ("यतो वाचो निवर्तन्ते अप्राप्य मनसा सह"): transcendence of conceptions: ordinary words fail to describe her, and the finite human mind cracks when trying to compute her absolute immensity. she cannot be captured by verbal definitions or mental concepts, remaining as the transcendent witness behind them.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya evokes the definitive Taittirīya Upaniṣad text ("यतो वाचो निवर्तन्ते अप्राप्य मनसा सह"):

Transcendence of Conceptions: Ordinary words fail to describe Her, and the finite human mind cracks when trying to compute Her absolute immensity. She cannot be captured by verbal definitions or mental concepts, remaining as the transcendent witness behind them.

name: चिच्छक्तिः
summary: (i) One who is in the form of energy called Chit.
(ii) Chitshakti, the power of removing ignorance, is also termed chaitanya. Since Sreedevee is in the form of knowledge, She got this name.

(iii) According to the Svetasvatara Upanishad (V-1), in the imperishable and infinite highest universal absolute, wherein vidya and avidya are hidden, one is imperishable and the other is perishable; She who rules these two is different from either.
(iv) In Devee Bhagavatam it is said that in all beings there is shakti with all its powers. Any being devoid of that shakti becomes like a corpse. That Chit-Shakti is in all beings; that is Her form.

(v) Chit, according to Yaska, means comparison. Panini also uses it in this sense. This method describes an unknown object by comparing it with a known object. The knowledge of comparison is Sreedevee Herself.
(vi) In Saptashatee we read: Chitiroopena Ya Krutsnam Etad Vyapya Sthita Jagat.
searchText: 416 चिच्छक्तिः • चित्शक्तिः। • चित् — consciousness, knowledge. • शक्तिः — power, energy. • चिच्छक्तिः — the power of consciousness. (i) one who is in the form of energy called chit. (ii) chitshakti, the power of removing ignorance, is also termed chaitanya. since sreedevee is in the form of knowledge, she got this name. (iii) according to the svetasvatara upanishad (v-1), in the imperishable and infinite highest universal absolute, wherein vidya and avidya are hidden, one is imperishable and the other is perishable; she who rules these two is different from either. (iv) in devee bhagavatam it is said that in all beings there is shakti with all its powers. any being devoid of that shakti becomes like a corpse. that chit-shakti is in all beings; that is her form. (v) chit, according to yaska, means comparison. panini also uses it in this sense. this method describes an unknown object by comparing it with a known object. the knowledge of comparison is sreedevee herself. (vi) in saptashatee we read: chitiroopena ya krutsnam etad vyapya sthita jagat. bhāskararāya outlines the primary engine of creation: the kinetic consciousness: she is not a blind, unconscious energy matter (prakṛti). she is the supreme dynamic power operating in absolute synchronization with pure awareness. she is the capacity of brahman to know, experience, and manifest realities without losing its baseline purity.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya outlines the primary engine of creation:

The Kinetic Consciousness: She is not a blind, unconscious energy matter (prakṛti). She is the supreme dynamic power operating in absolute synchronization with pure awareness. She is the capacity of Brahman to know, experience, and manifest realities without losing its baseline purity.

name: चेतनारूपा
wordByWord: • चेतनारूपा — She whose form is consciousness.
summary: (i) One who is in the form of consciousness, chaitanya.

(ii) The chetana energy in all beings is Sreedevee only. In Saptashatee this has been prayed as Chetana. Chetana means inner sense. Only because of this can we recognise the objects shown by eye, ear and mind. Only through its action can we recognise its availability, not by any other means.
(iii) Kenopanishad says: Srotrasya Srotram Manaso Mano.
(iv) Samkshepa Sareerakam says that the pure chit shakti of the supreme lord is called chaitanya.
(v) In Gaudapada’s Sree Vidya Ratna Sutra also it is mentioned that Shakti is chaitanya itself.
(vi) The first verse of Devee Bhagavatam says that we meditate upon that Primeval Vidya which is the chaitanya of all and which guides our senses.
(vii) In the Taittiriya Upanishad commentary tradition, the letter ‘Sa’ is explained as being in the form of Chitshakti.
searchText: 417 चेतनारूपा • चेतना रूपं यस्याः सा। • चेतना — consciousness, sentience, inner awareness. • रूपम् — form. • यस्याः सा — whose; she whose. • चेतनारूपा — she whose form is consciousness. (i) one who is in the form of consciousness, chaitanya. (ii) the chetana energy in all beings is sreedevee only. in saptashatee this has been prayed as chetana. chetana means inner sense. only because of this can we recognise the objects shown by eye, ear and mind. only through its action can we recognise its availability, not by any other means. (iii) kenopanishad says: srotrasya srotram manaso mano. (iv) samkshepa sareerakam says that the pure chit shakti of the supreme lord is called chaitanya. (v) in gaudapada’s sree vidya ratna sutra also it is mentioned that shakti is chaitanya itself. (vi) the first verse of devee bhagavatam says that we meditate upon that primeval vidya which is the chaitanya of all and which guides our senses. (vii) in the taittiriya upanishad commentary tradition, the letter ‘sa’ is explained as being in the form of chitshakti. bhāskararāya tracks her presence across all living organisms: the animating pulse: cetanā is the visible spark of life and intelligence that differentiates a living body from a dead corpse. she is that very pulse of vitality. every thought, movement, and perception occurring in any biological form is her direct physical signature.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya tracks Her presence across all living organisms:

The Animating Pulse: Cetanā is the visible spark of life and intelligence that differentiates a living body from a dead corpse. She is that very pulse of vitality. Every thought, movement, and perception occurring in any biological form is Her direct physical signature.

name: जडशक्तिः
wordByWord: • जडशक्तिः — the power operating in inert creation.
summary: (i) One who manifests as the mechanical forces of the inanimate creation.
(ii) A certain modification of maya, correlative to the power which creates the animate world, simply denotes the creative energy.

(iii) The Vishnu Purana says that the energies of all beings are not grasped by our intellects. The creative energies of Brahman are a hundred times more difficult to grasp; they are in Him as heat is in fire. Brahman is only the instrumental cause of the creation of the world. The creative energies are the material cause. Except for the instrumental cause, the creative energies depend on nothing. An object becomes itself by its own energy.
searchText: 418 जडशक्तिः • जडाशक्तिः। • जडा — inert, insentient, mechanical. • शक्तिः — power, energy. • जडशक्तिः — the power operating in inert creation. (i) one who manifests as the mechanical forces of the inanimate creation. (ii) a certain modification of maya, correlative to the power which creates the animate world, simply denotes the creative energy. (iii) the vishnu purana says that the energies of all beings are not grasped by our intellects. the creative energies of brahman are a hundred times more difficult to grasp; they are in him as heat is in fire. brahman is only the instrumental cause of the creation of the world. the creative energies are the material cause. except for the instrumental cause, the creative energies depend on nothing. an object becomes itself by its own energy. bhāskararāya introduces a key philosophical transition to prevent cosmic exclusion: the matrix of matter: lest a practitioner think that the material, unconscious elements of the universe (like rocks, elements, and mechanical forces) belong to an evil or separate category, this name states she is jaḍaśakti. she assumes the form of inert matter to build the physical staging ground of the cosmos.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya introduces a key philosophical transition to prevent cosmic exclusion:

The Matrix of Matter: Lest a practitioner think that the material, unconscious elements of the universe (like rocks, elements, and mechanical forces) belong to an evil or separate category, this name states She is Jaḍaśakti. She assumes the form of inert matter to build the physical staging ground of the cosmos.

name: जडात्मिका
samasa: • जडं दृश्यमत्रम् आत्मा यस्याः मायायाः सा।
wordByWord: • दृश्यमात्रम् — mere visible object, object of perception.
• जडात्मिका — She who is the inner essence of inert manifestation.
summary: (i) One who is the innermost essence of all mechanical forces.
(ii) It has been mentioned above that this static universe is the specialty of cosmic manifestation.
searchText: 419 जडात्मिका • जडं दृश्यमत्रम् आत्मा यस्याः मायायाः सा। • जडम् — inert, insentient. • दृश्यमात्रम् — mere visible object, object of perception. • आत्मा — inner essence, nature. • माया — maya. • जडात्मिका — she who is the inner essence of inert manifestation. (i) one who is the innermost essence of all mechanical forces. (ii) it has been mentioned above that this static universe is the specialty of cosmic manifestation. in the saubhāgyabhāskara, bhāskararāya clarifies her identity with the objective world: the object universe: जड refers to the entire grid of dṛśya (the seen/perceivable objects). she embodies this dense, material layer through her power of māyā. she is the substance out of which the objective, tangible landscape of creation is spun.
esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara, Bhāskararāya clarifies Her identity with the objective world:

The Object Universe: जड refers to the entire grid of dṛśya (the seen/perceivable objects). She embodies this dense, material layer through Her power of Māyā. She is the substance out of which the objective, tangible landscape of creation is spun.

name: गायत्री
wordByWord: • गायन्तम् — the one who sings or recites.
• गोपकन्या — daughter of a cowherd / guardian of Veda.
• ब्रह्मणः कनिष्ठपत्नी — the junior wife of Brahma.
summary: (i) One who is in the form of Gayatree.

(ii) Veda says, Gayatreem Chandasam Mata: Gayatree mantra is the mother of all mantras. The Gayatree metre has 24 characters. In Sreemad Bhagavat Geeta (X-35), Lord Sreekrishna says: Gayatree Chandasam Aham. In Koorma Purana also the same is conveyed.

(iii) According to Padma Purana, Gayatree is a daughter of a shepherd and a junior wife of Brahma. When Brahma was performing a sacrificial fire, he called his wife Savitri. As she delayed, Brahma brought a daughter of a shepherd from Shakti and, with Vishnu’s consent, married her by the Gandharva method. For the word Gopakanya, Go has several meanings; one of them is Veda, and those who guard Veda are Gopas.

(iv) Gayatree protects those who recite her mantra. Gayatree Kalpa says: Gayantam Trayate Yasmat Gayatree Tena Gadyate. Other reasons are also given: Devee Purana says that Devas worship Gayatree by singing and hearing; Chhandogya Upanishad says Gayatree Cha Trayate Cha; Mahavashishta Ramayana says that because She is in the form of songs, She is called Gayatree.
(v) Padma Purana says that Sreedevee is in the form of Gayatree.

(vi) The ordinarily recited Gayatree mantra has three groups and the complete Gayatree mantra has four. The first three are said to be capsule forms of Rig, Yajur and Sama Vedas; the fourth is a gist of Atharvana Veda. It is said that Sree Bhaskara Raya also had Upanayana again and learnt Atharvana Veda.
(vii) It is well known that Sreemad Ramayana includes Gayatree mantra and that 24 verses are read as Gayatree Ramayana.
(viii) Tripura Tapini Upanishad advises that Panchadasi mantra has the meaning of Gayatree mantra in every group. This has also been explained by Sree Bhaskara Raya.
(ix) In Mahashodha Nyasa, it has been mentioned that She is in the form of three Gunas.
searchText: 420 गायत्री • छन्दसां चतुर्विंशत्यक्षरं गायत्री। • गायन्तं त्रायते यस्मादिति। • गायनाद् गमनाद् वा। • गायति च त्रायते च। • गायनात्मत्वात्। • गोपकन्या ब्रह्मणः कनिष्ठपत्नी। • छन्दसाम् — among metres. • चतुर्विंशत्यक्षरम् — having twenty-four syllables. • गायन्तम् — the one who sings or recites. • त्रायते — protects. • गायनात् — from singing. • गमनात् — from going or movement. • गायति — sings. • त्रायते — protects. • गोपकन्या — daughter of a cowherd / guardian of veda. • ब्रह्मणः कनिष्ठपत्नी — the junior wife of brahma. (i) one who is in the form of gayatree. (ii) veda says, gayatreem chandasam mata: gayatree mantra is the mother of all mantras. the gayatree metre has 24 characters. in sreemad bhagavat geeta (x-35), lord sreekrishna says: gayatree chandasam aham. in koorma purana also the same is conveyed. (iii) according to padma purana, gayatree is a daughter of a shepherd and a junior wife of brahma. when brahma was performing a sacrificial fire, he called his wife savitri. as she delayed, brahma brought a daughter of a shepherd from shakti and, with vishnu’s consent, married her by the gandharva method. for the word gopakanya, go has several meanings; one of them is veda, and those who guard veda are gopas. (iv) gayatree protects those who recite her mantra. gayatree kalpa says: gayantam trayate yasmat gayatree tena gadyate. other reasons are also given: devee purana says that devas worship gayatree by singing and hearing; chhandogya upanishad says gayatree cha trayate cha; mahavashishta ramayana says that because she is in the form of songs, she is called gayatree. (v) padma purana says that sreedevee is in the form of gayatree. (vi) the ordinarily recited gayatree mantra has three groups and the complete gayatree mantra has four. the first three are said to be capsule forms of rig, yajur and sama vedas; the fourth is a gist of atharvana veda. it is said that sree bhaskara raya also had upanayana again and learnt atharvana veda. (vii) it is well known that sreemad ramayana includes gayatree mantra and that 24 verses are read as gayatree ramayana. (viii) tripura tapini upanishad advises that panchadasi mantra has the meaning of gayatree mantra in every group. this has also been explained by sree bhaskara raya. (ix) in mahashodha nyasa, it has been mentioned that she is in the form of three gunas. bhāskararāya unpacks the absolute premier status of this identity: the mother of mantras: she is non-different from the sacred gāyatrī mantra, the foundational core of vedic revelation. bhāskararāya details the semantic root: गायन्तं त्रायते — she extends an immediate shield of spiritual protection over any seeker who sings or chants her name, transforming their intellect into pure light.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya unpacks the absolute premier status of this identity:

The Mother of Mantras: She is non-different from the sacred Gāyatrī Mantra, the foundational core of Vedic revelation. Bhāskararāya details the semantic root: गायन्तं त्रायते — She extends an immediate shield of spiritual protection over any seeker who sings or chants Her name, transforming their intellect into pure light.

name: व्याहृतिः
summary: (i) One who is in the form of Vyahrutis, invocations.

(ii) Some special mantras are called Vyahrutis. Before creating this world, Brahma chanted the Pranava mantra Om by joining the first letters of the three Vedas, A, U and M. Then he chanted Bhooh, Bhuvah and Suvah and created the world. It expanded to the words Mahah, Janah, Tapah and Satyam and as seven worlds. Hence the Pranava and the names of these seven worlds are called Vyahrutis. She is in this form.
(iii) According to Vayu Purana, She is in the form of Vyaharanam, that is pronunciation. “You were addressed by me and hence you approached me. Hence you are the Vyahruti.”
(iv) It can be said that this and the next two names respectively indicate the waking, dream and deep sleep states.
searchText: 421 व्याहृतिः • उच्चारणरूपा। • मन्त्रविशेषणरूपा। • उच्चारण — utterance, pronunciation. • रूपा — form. • मन्त्रविशेषण — qualifying or special mantra-expression. • व्याहृतिः — sacred utterance/invocation. (i) one who is in the form of vyahrutis, invocations. (ii) some special mantras are called vyahrutis. before creating this world, brahma chanted the pranava mantra om by joining the first letters of the three vedas, a, u and m. then he chanted bhooh, bhuvah and suvah and created the world. it expanded to the words mahah, janah, tapah and satyam and as seven worlds. hence the pranava and the names of these seven worlds are called vyahrutis. she is in this form. (iii) according to vayu purana, she is in the form of vyaharanam, that is pronunciation. “you were addressed by me and hence you approached me. hence you are the vyahruti.” (iv) it can be said that this and the next two names respectively indicate the waking, dream and deep sleep states. bhāskararāya connects this directly to the structural framework of vedic ritual: the sacred utterance: व्याहृति (vyāhṛti) refers specifically to the cosmic prefixes—भूः, भुवः, स्वः (bhūḥ, bhuvaḥ, svaḥ)—that anchor and unlock the gāyatrī mantra. she is the phonic reality of these syllables, representing the three foundational worlds of experience projected by her sound.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya connects this directly to the structural framework of Vedic ritual:

The Sacred Utterance: व्याहृति (Vyāhṛti) refers specifically to the cosmic prefixes—भूः, भुवः, स्वः (Bhūḥ, Bhuvaḥ, Svaḥ)—that anchor and unlock the Gāyatrī Mantra. She is the phonic reality of these syllables, representing the three foundational worlds of experience projected by Her sound.

name: संध्या
wordByWord: • ध्येयत्वात् — because she is fit to be meditated upon.
summary: (i) One who is in the form of Sandhya Devee, the deity of twilight.
(ii) Sandhya means sound meditation. She is worshipped with the idea of the non-separation of ourselves.

(iii) Sandhya means junction, the time of night and day, or meditation. The meeting of day and night occurs in the morning and evening; similar junctions occur at noon and midnight also. These junctions are worshipped by Brahmins with the integrated thought of chaitanya in the Sun. That kind of worship is the real meaning of Sandhya. In that way, worshipped Gayatree and Brahman are one and the same.
Madhava says that this word indicates the Devee to be worshipped during the junction of day and night.

Note: As per Tantra Sastras, during Sandhya period arghya has to be given aiming at the mainly worshipped deities, after the Vedic Sandhyavandanam. In this fashion, Sreevidyopasakas give arghya in all four Sandhyas. This is called Sree Vidya Sandhyavandanam according to Sakta Tantra Sastra.

(iv) Sandhya is the mind-born daughter of Brahma. When Brahma was meditating, a beautiful girl originated and was called Sandhya. She did penance and, by giving up her body, became Arundhati, wife of sage Vasishta.

(v) In Renuka Purana it is said that the three pulses Ida, Pingala and Susumna are integrated in the body. Similarly Sandhya is an integrated form of the three Devis Mahakalee, Mahalakshmee and Ekaveera.
(vi) According to Dhoumya, a one-year-old girl is called Sandhya. Sreedevee is in that form also.
(vii) The three names Vyahruti, Sandhya and Dvija Brunda Nishevita respectively indicate the awakened, dream and deep sleep states.
searchText: 422 संध्या • सम्यग् ध्यायन्ति अस्याम्। • सम्यग्ध्येयत्वात्। • सम्यक् — properly, completely. • ध्यायन्ति — they meditate. • अस्याम् — in her, upon her. • ध्येयत्वात् — because she is fit to be meditated upon. • संध्या — sandhya, junction, twilight meditation. (i) one who is in the form of sandhya devee, the deity of twilight. (ii) sandhya means sound meditation. she is worshipped with the idea of the non-separation of ourselves. (iii) sandhya means junction, the time of night and day, or meditation. the meeting of day and night occurs in the morning and evening; similar junctions occur at noon and midnight also. these junctions are worshipped by brahmins with the integrated thought of chaitanya in the sun. that kind of worship is the real meaning of sandhya. in that way, worshipped gayatree and brahman are one and the same. madhava says that this word indicates the devee to be worshipped during the junction of day and night. note: as per tantra sastras, during sandhya period arghya has to be given aiming at the mainly worshipped deities, after the vedic sandhyavandanam. in this fashion, sreevidyopasakas give arghya in all four sandhyas. this is called sree vidya sandhyavandanam according to sakta tantra sastra. (iv) sandhya is the mind-born daughter of brahma. when brahma was meditating, a beautiful girl originated and was called sandhya. she did penance and, by giving up her body, became arundhati, wife of sage vasishta. (v) in renuka purana it is said that the three pulses ida, pingala and susumna are integrated in the body. similarly sandhya is an integrated form of the three devis mahakalee, mahalakshmee and ekaveera. (vi) according to dhoumya, a one-year-old girl is called sandhya. sreedevee is in that form also. (vii) the three names vyahruti, sandhya and dvija brunda nishevita respectively indicate the awakened, dream and deep sleep states. bhāskararāya details both the ritual hour and the internal yogic junction: the hour of union: saṁdhyā represents the transitional junctions of the day (dawn, noon, and dusk) where the mind naturally settles into quietude. the internal junction: in the subtle body, she is the junction point where the dual flows of iḍā and piṅgalā dissolve into the central suṣumṇā, marking the state of absolute meditative absorption.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya details both the ritual hour and the internal yogic junction:
The Hour of Union: Saṁdhyā represents the transitional junctions of the day (dawn, noon, and dusk) where the mind naturally settles into quietude.
The Internal Junction: In the subtle body, She is the junction point where the dual flows of Iḍā and Piṅgalā dissolve into the central Suṣumṇā, marking the state of absolute meditative absorption.

name: द्विजवृन्दनिषेविता
summary: (i) One who is well worshipped by groups of the twice born.
(ii) She has been well meditated upon by all the twice born: Brahmins, Kshatriyas and Vyasyas. One birth is from the mother and the other is during Upanayanam to understand Gayatree mantra.

(iii) She is so worshipped because She is Sandhya. The Renuka Purana says that this Sandhya is to be worshipped by Devas, by the twice born and by great souls, in sitting down, lying down, moving about and eating, and that She is Renuka Herself.

A Brahmin who does not worship Sandhya is no more a Brahmin. He is unfit to do any other religious actions. Sastra says that even if he does any actions, they will be fruitless. The importance of worshipping Gayatree has been described in detail in Devee Bhagavatam.

(iv) It can be said that the three names Vyahruti, Sandhya and Dvija Brunda Nishevita respectively indicate waking, dream and deep sleep states. Vyahruti is the operation of speech and indicates waking. Sandhya, being between the two, indicates dream. Dvija also means birds; birds fly in the sky and return to their nests, just as tired jivas, quitting waking and dream states, are absorbed in the supreme Brahman in deep sleep.
searchText: 423 द्विजवृन्दनिषेविता • द्विजानां वृन्दम्। तैः निषेविता उपास्या। • द्विजानाम् — of the twice-born. • वृन्दम् — group, assembly. • तैः — by them. • निषेविता — served, worshipped. • उपास्या — to be worshipped. (i) one who is well worshipped by groups of the twice born. (ii) she has been well meditated upon by all the twice born: brahmins, kshatriyas and vyasyas. one birth is from the mother and the other is during upanayanam to understand gayatree mantra. (iii) she is so worshipped because she is sandhya. the renuka purana says that this sandhya is to be worshipped by devas, by the twice born and by great souls, in sitting down, lying down, moving about and eating, and that she is renuka herself. a brahmin who does not worship sandhya is no more a brahmin. he is unfit to do any other religious actions. sastra says that even if he does any actions, they will be fruitless. the importance of worshipping gayatree has been described in detail in devee bhagavatam. (iv) it can be said that the three names vyahruti, sandhya and dvija brunda nishevita respectively indicate waking, dream and deep sleep states. vyahruti is the operation of speech and indicates waking. sandhya, being between the two, indicates dream. dvija also means birds; birds fly in the sky and return to their nests, just as tired jivas, quitting waking and dream states, are absorbed in the supreme brahman in deep sleep. in the saubhāgyabhāskara, bhāskararāya clarifies the functional meaning of dvija: the double birth: dvija means twice-born—first biologically from the mother's womb, and second spiritually through the reception of the gāyatrī and agamic initiation. she is the central deity sought after by all those who have dedicated their corporate lives to the maintenance of scriptural order and daily contemplative practices.
esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara, Bhāskararāya clarifies the functional meaning of Dvija:

The Double Birth: Dvija means twice-born—first biologically from the mother's womb, and second spiritually through the reception of the Gāyatrī and Agamic initiation. She is the central deity sought after by all those who have dedicated their corporate lives to the maintenance of scriptural order and daily contemplative practices.

name: तत्त्वासना
wordByWord: • तत्त्वासना — She whose seat is constituted by the tattvas.
summary: (i) One who has a seat constituted by the Cosmic Elements, the 36 tatvas.

(ii) Sankhyas say that there are 24 tatvas from Moolaprakruti till earth. One religion says there are 96 tatvas. Thus the number varies in different schools. The details can be seen in Sreemad Bhagavatam. Saiva Sastras and Sakta Sastras unanimously accept that there are 36 tatvas from Shiva till Prithvee. She has these 36 tatvas as Her yoga seat.

(iii) One other meaning is that She pushes out the tatvas, i.e. She is beyond all these tatvas. For the 906th name Tatvadhika it has been interpreted that She continues even when these tatvas are destroyed.
searchText: 424 तत्त्वासना • षट्त्रिंशत् तत्त्वानि आसनं यस्याः सा। • षट्त्रिंशत् — thirty-six. • तत्त्वानि — cosmic principles or elements. • आसनम् — seat. • यस्याः सा — whose; she whose. • तत्त्वासना — she whose seat is constituted by the tattvas. (i) one who has a seat constituted by the cosmic elements, the 36 tatvas. (ii) sankhyas say that there are 24 tatvas from moolaprakruti till earth. one religion says there are 96 tatvas. thus the number varies in different schools. the details can be seen in sreemad bhagavatam. saiva sastras and sakta sastras unanimously accept that there are 36 tatvas from shiva till prithvee. she has these 36 tatvas as her yoga seat. (iii) one other meaning is that she pushes out the tatvas, i.e. she is beyond all these tatvas. for the 906th name tatvadhika it has been interpreted that she continues even when these tatvas are destroyed. bhāskararāya maps this across the complete taxonomy of the agamas: the throne of thirty-six principles: in the śākta/śaiva systems, reality is stratified into thirty-six progressive principles (tattvas), running from the grossest element earth (pṛthivī) up to pure śiva-tattva. she sits on top of this entire evolutionary ladder, controlling it as its sovereign source and ruler.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya maps this across the complete taxonomy of the Agamas:

The Throne of Thirty-Six Principles: In the Śākta/Śaiva systems, reality is stratified into thirty-six progressive principles (tattvas), running from the grossest element earth (pṛthivī) up to pure Śiva-tattva. She sits on top of this entire evolutionary ladder, controlling it as its sovereign source and ruler.

name: तत्
wordByWord: • तत् — That; the transcendent principle referred to in the Mahavakya.
• बुद्धिविपरिवृत्तिविषये — in the field of the transformation or revolution of intellect.
summary: (i) One who is in the form Tat.
(ii) The word Tat signifies the revolution of mind, as Sreedevee revolves in the Buddhis of all the souls. She is signified by the word Tat.
(iii) Sree Adi Sankara, in his commentary on Vishnu Sahasranamam for the names 730 to 733 — Yat Tat Padamanuttamam — has mentioned the same sense.
(iv) In usage of namavali, this name appears as Tasmai Nama.
searchText: 425 तत् • तत्पदस्य बुद्धिविपरिवृत्तिविषये शक्तिः। • तत्पदवाच्या। • तस्मै नमः। • तत् — that; the transcendent principle referred to in the mahavakya. • पदस्य — of the word. • बुद्धिविपरिवृत्तिविषये — in the field of the transformation or revolution of intellect. • शक्तिः — power. • वाच्या — directly denoted. • तस्मै नमः — salutation to that. (i) one who is in the form tat. (ii) the word tat signifies the revolution of mind, as sreedevee revolves in the buddhis of all the souls. she is signified by the word tat. (iii) sree adi sankara, in his commentary on vishnu sahasranamam for the names 730 to 733 — yat tat padamanuttamam — has mentioned the same sense. (iv) in usage of namavali, this name appears as tasmai nama. bhāskararāya connects this directly to the linguistic architecture of the mahāvākyas: the direct designation: in vedic syntax, तत् (tat) points directly to the unmanifested, omnipresent, transcendent cause of the universe. by declaring her name to be tat, the text removes any anthropomorphic limitations, confirming her identity as the absolute, non-dual brahman beyond time and space.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya connects this directly to the linguistic architecture of the Mahāvākyas:

The Direct Designation: In Vedic syntax, तत् (Tat) points directly to the unmanifested, omnipresent, transcendent cause of the universe. By declaring Her name to be Tat, the text removes any anthropomorphic limitations, confirming Her identity as the absolute, non-dual Brahman beyond time and space.

name: त्वम्
summary: (i) Thou.

(ii) In the 36 tatvas mentioned above, the Shiva tatva is called Para Samvit. This is the Supreme Being without any quality or blemish. Even though it is without any dispositions, with energy for creation. Hence, the Shiva Tatva without any dispositions and the Shakti Tatva, the cause of the dispositions on account of creation, are one and the same in the Para Samvit. Hence when Sreedevee is in the form of Parabrahmam, She is referred to as Prakasha Vimarsha Samarasya Roopinee. Kameshwaree is in the form of brightness. This form of knowledge is integrated with Aham, Idam, Etat and Tat.
(iii) This form is described in the 24th name in Trishatee, Etat-Tad-Ityanirdeshya.
(iv) In Pavanopanishad we read of Kameshvara as the Nirupadhika Samvid.
(v) Vasinee Devis explained the confident form of self in the previous name and the experimental form in this name.
(vi) Since it is said as Tatvamayee later in the 907th name, these 425th, 426th and 427th names are split into Tat, Tvam and Ayee.
(vii) In usage of namavali, this name appears as Tubhyam Nama.
searchText: 426 त्वम् • त्वम्पदवाच्या। • त्वम्पदवाच्या। • तुभ्यं नमः। • त्वम् — thou; you. • पदवाच्या — denoted by the word. • तुभ्यम् नमः — salutation to you. (i) thou. (ii) in the 36 tatvas mentioned above, the shiva tatva is called para samvit. this is the supreme being without any quality or blemish. even though it is without any dispositions, with energy for creation. hence, the shiva tatva without any dispositions and the shakti tatva, the cause of the dispositions on account of creation, are one and the same in the para samvit. hence when sreedevee is in the form of parabrahmam, she is referred to as prakasha vimarsha samarasya roopinee. kameshwaree is in the form of brightness. this form of knowledge is integrated with aham, idam, etat and tat. (iii) this form is described in the 24th name in trishatee, etat-tad-ityanirdeshya. (iv) in pavanopanishad we read of kameshvara as the nirupadhika samvid. (v) vasinee devis explained the confident form of self in the previous name and the experimental form in this name. (vi) since it is said as tatvamayee later in the 907th name, these 425th, 426th and 427th names are split into tat, tvam and ayee. (vii) in usage of namavali, this name appears as tubhyam nama. bhāskararāya reveals the staggering non-dual transition achieved by putting 425 and 426 together: the immediate presence: if she were only tat ("that"), she might seem like a cold, distant galactic absolute. therefore, the text immediately names her त्वम् ("thou"). she is the immediate, close, internal reality residing inside the seeker as their own subjective spark of life, completing the equation of realization.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya reveals the staggering non-dual transition achieved by putting 425 and 426 together:

The Immediate Presence: If She were only Tat ("That"), She might seem like a cold, distant galactic absolute. Therefore, the text immediately names Her त्वम् ("Thou"). She is the immediate, close, internal reality residing inside the seeker as their own subjective spark of life, completing the equation of realization.

name: अयी
wordByWord: • अयी — Ayi; affectionate form of address to the Mother.
• जनमातृत्वात् — because she is the mother of all beings.
• आमन्त्रणीया — to be addressed or invoked.
summary: (i) One who is referred as Ayee.
searchText: 427 अयी • अयी। जनमातृत्वात् जनैः आमन्त्रणीया। • अयी — ayi; affectionate form of address to the mother. • जनमातृत्वात् — because she is the mother of all beings. • जनैः — by people. • आमन्त्रणीया — to be addressed or invoked. (i) one who is referred as ayee. bhāskararāya details the emotional accessibility built into this sound: the spontaneous cry: अयी is the tender, emotional syllable used by a child to call out to its mother when in distress. by setting this as a formal name, it indicates that she requires no complex, mechanical linguistic formulations; she answers the simple, spontaneous cry of love arising from the heart.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya details the emotional accessibility built into this sound:

The Spontaneous Cry: अयी is the tender, emotional syllable used by a child to call out to its mother when in distress. By setting this as a formal name, it indicates that She requires no complex, mechanical linguistic formulations; She answers the simple, spontaneous cry of love arising from the heart.

name: पञ्चकोशांतरस्थिता
wordByWord: • पञ्चकोशांतरस्थिता — She who dwells amidst the five koshas, the psychological sheaths of the personality.
summary: (i) One who dwells amidst the five koshas, the psychological sheaths of the personality.

(ii) In Navavarna Pooja, after the ninth avarana above the bindu, Pancha Panchika Pooja is performed by imagining five thrones. These five Panchikas are respectively the five Lakshmis, five sheaths, five kalpalatas, five kamadhenus, and five gems. The five sheaths are named Sree Vidya, Paramjyoti, Paranishkala Shambhavi, Ajapadanda, and Matruka. These details are available in Jnanarnava Tantra.

(iii) Sree Vidya sheath is worshipped in the bindu. The other four are worshipped outside, differentiating the individual and the combined forms. Since Sree Vidya sheath is worshipped in the bindu amidst these four, this name is given to Sreedevee.

(iv) The results of these five Panchika Poojas are explained in detail in Saparya Paddhati Vasana by Sree Chidananda Natha. He explains the five positions as Samyam, Layam, Vinasham, Adyantabhavam, and Aikyam, corresponding to the five Panchikas.

(v) It has also been said that there are five sheaths in the body: Annamaya, Pranamaya, Manomaya, Vijnanamaya, and Anandamaya. Each is covered by the previous one. Sreedevee is in the integral form with Brahmam as the Anandamaya sheath.

(vi) Sree Adi Sankara states that Anandamaya is not itself the universal absolute Brahmam, but is to be understood as different from Brahmam. Based on this view, the name means that She is in the midst of the five sheaths: Brahmam is beyond Anandamaya, completing it, witnessing all actions, and being of the nature of truth and knowledge.
(vii) Brahma Geeta and the tradition of Durvasa also describe Sreedevee as the supreme light known through the five sheaths.
