(iii) sree vidya sheath is worshipped in the bindu. the other four are worshipped outside, differentiating the individual and the combined forms. since sree vidya sheath is worshipped in the bindu amidst these four, this name is given to sreedevee. (iv) the results of these five panchika poojas are explained in detail in saparya paddhati vasana by sree chidananda natha. he explains the five positions as samyam, layam, vinasham, adyantabhavam, and aikyam, corresponding to the five panchikas. (v) it has also been said that there are five sheaths in the body: annamaya, pranamaya, manomaya, vijnanamaya, and anandamaya. each is covered by the previous one. sreedevee is in the integral form with brahmam as the anandamaya sheath. (vi) sree adi sankara states that anandamaya is not itself the universal absolute brahmam, but is to be understood as different from brahmam. based on this view, the name means that she is in the midst of the five sheaths: brahmam is beyond anandamaya, completing it, witnessing all actions, and being of the nature of truth and knowledge. (vii) brahma geeta and the tradition of durvasa also describe sreedevee as the supreme light known through the five sheaths. bhāskararāya aligns this with the psycho-spiritual anatomy detailed in the taittirīya upaniṣad: the hidden jewel: the individual soul is covered by five dense layers: annamaya (food/physical), prāṇamaya (vital energy), manomaya (mind/emotion), vijñānamaya (intellect/discernment), and ānandamaya (causal bliss). she sits at the absolute center of these five layers as the ultimate, unblemished core—the ātman.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya aligns this with the psycho-spiritual anatomy detailed in the Taittirīya Upaniṣad:

The Hidden Jewel: The individual soul is covered by five dense layers: Annamaya (food/physical), Prāṇamaya (vital energy), Manomaya (mind/emotion), Vijñānamaya (intellect/discernment), and Ānandamaya (causal bliss). She sits at the absolute center of these five layers as the ultimate, unblemished core—the Ātman.

name: निस्सीममहिमा
samasa: • निष्कान्तः सीमानं सीमां वा निःसीमा नीःसीमो वा महिमा यस्याः सा।
wordByWord: • निस्सीममहिमा — She whose glory is boundless.
summary: (i) One whose glory is boundless.
(ii) Seema — boundary. Nisseema ~ boundless. Her pride and glory are boundless.
(iii) In many a book i has been printed as Nisseemamahimne Nama: SreeBhaskaraRaya — allows Nisseema Mahimayai Nama; also.
searchText: 429 निस्सीममहिमा • निष्कान्तः सीमानं सीमां वा निःसीमा नीःसीमो वा महिमा यस्याः सा। • निष्कान्तः — gone beyond, free from. • सीमानं — limit. • सीमां — boundary. • निःसीमा — without limit. • महिमा — glory, greatness. • निस्सीममहिमा — she whose glory is boundless. (i) one whose glory is boundless. (ii) seema — boundary. nisseema ~ boundless. her pride and glory are boundless. (iii) in many a book i has been printed as nisseemamahimne nama: sreebhaskararaya — allows nisseema mahimayai nama; also. in the saubhāgyabhāskara, bhāskararāya defines the scope of her glory: boundless sovereignty: human or divine greatness is always limited by space, time, or capacity. her majesty (mahimā) is nissīma—it has no border wall, no expiration date, and no structural limitation, expanding infinitely in all directions beyond calculation.
esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara, Bhāskararāya defines the scope of Her glory:

Boundless Sovereignty: Human or divine greatness is always limited by space, time, or capacity. Her majesty (Mahimā) is Nissīma—it has no border wall, no expiration date, and no structural limitation, expanding infinitely in all directions beyond calculation.

name: नित्ययौवना
wordByWord: • नित्ययौवना — She who is eternally young.
summary: (i) One who is eternally young.
(ii) She is juvenile and youthful without any growth or deterioration.
(iii) 136 — Nitya, 344 — Kshaya-vriddhi Vinirmukté, 358 ~ Taruni and 470 Vayovastha Vivarjita may be referred.

(i) One who is worshipped by those, devoted to the Koula tradition.

(ii) Margo, the path handed down by family tradition. One has to worship only following those methods. We read that only using those tradition Sreedevee is worshipped with sandal paste, offerings, ete; Yasya Yasya Hi YéDevee Kulamdgena Samsthita Tena Tena Cha Sé Poojya Boligandhénulepancr Il Naivedyair Vividhaishchaive Poojayet Kulamargeta: in worshipping Sree Vidya, there are three different modes viz. , Somaya, Koule and Mishro, The Sombitas of Sukor, Vesishtar, Sonakar, Sonondanar and Sonathkumorar(jointly called as Supdkama Panchakam) follow the Voideeka tradition. This is Samaya tradition to be followed by Vaideekas. The Misra tradition has been described in the eight Tontras, viz. , Candrokalé, Jyotssndvati, Kaléniti, Kuldrnavo, KulEshwari, Bhuvaneshwari, Bérhaspatyam and Durvasa. It is called mixed because it partakes of both the Samaya and Koula modes. Everyone is eligible for to use method. Aiming at the dwijas (Brahmins, Kshatriyas and Vysyas) the rightist method (Savye or Pradakshina method) has been suggested. And the leftist (Aprodokshina or Aposavya) method has been suggested for the Sudras. Relating to this, the names 98 — Samaydchdratatpordand 912 ~Savyopasavyomérgastho can be referred. The rightist method has been differently interpreted there.

(ii) that is, her form is full of thrivingness, nourishment, plenty and body full (complete in all aspects of organs and constituent parts of the body). this has to be read in conjunction with the previous name. in the saubhāgyabhāskara, bhāskararāya unpacks the macrocosmic structural authority of the divine mother: the mother of cosmic forces: as the parent matrix of both kārttikeya (representing kriyā-śakti or dynamic action) and gaṇeśa (representing jñāna-śakti or obstacles-mastery), she coordinates the defense and structural order of the universe. sensory control: under the second grammatical derivation, she functions as the absolute restraining force that binds the disruptive sensory urges (māragana) of an advanced practitioner, ensuring steadfast inward absorption.
esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara, Bhāskararāya unpacks the macrocosmic structural authority of the divine mother:

The Mother of Cosmic Forces: As the parent matrix of both Kārttikeya (representing Kriyā-śakti or dynamic action) and Gaṇeśa (representing Jñāna-śakti or obstacles-mastery), She coordinates the defense and structural order of the universe.

Sensory Control: Under the second grammatical derivation, She functions as the absolute restraining force that binds the disruptive sensory urges (māragana) of an advanced practitioner, ensuring steadfast inward absorption.

name: तुष्टिः
wordByWord: • सन्तोषरूपिणी — having the form of contentment.
• तुष्टिः — She who has the form of contentment.
summary: (i) One who has the form of contentment.

(ii) There are seven names here: contentment, nourishment, wisdom, fortitude, tranquillity, benevolence, and beauty. Sreedevee is praised as these forms. Whoever has these qualities may be understood to have received Her special blessing.
(iii) In Saptashatee also She is praised as Sree, Hree, Buddhi, Lajja, Pushti, Tushti, Shanti, and Kshanti.
(iv) In Devee Bhagavatam also the same idea is conveyed: the Mother is present in all beings as understanding, fame, firmness, prosperity, energy, faith, intelligence, and memory.
(v) According to Padma Purana, Tushti is the deity in Vasreshvara, Pushti is the deity in Devataru forest, and Dhruti is the deity in Pindaraka Kshetra.
(vi) Sree Bhaskara Raya cites a verse from Mallari Mahatmya to show that Paramashiva is also described in this manner.
searchText: 443 तुष्टिः • सन्तोषरूपिणी। • सन्तोषरूपिणी — having the form of contentment. • तुष्टिः — she who has the form of contentment. (i) one who has the form of contentment. (ii) there are seven names here: contentment, nourishment, wisdom, fortitude, tranquillity, benevolence, and beauty. sreedevee is praised as these forms. whoever has these qualities may be understood to have received her special blessing. (iii) in saptashatee also she is praised as sree, hree, buddhi, lajja, pushti, tushti, shanti, and kshanti. (iv) in devee bhagavatam also the same idea is conveyed: the mother is present in all beings as understanding, fame, firmness, prosperity, energy, faith, intelligence, and memory. (v) according to padma purana, tushti is the deity in vasreshvara, pushti is the deity in devataru forest, and dhruti is the deity in pindaraka kshetra. (vi) sree bhaskara raya cites a verse from mallari mahatmya to show that paramashiva is also described in this manner. bhāskararāya views this as her direct manifestation inside human experience: the sentiment of contentment: she is the baseline energy of satisfaction (santosa-rūpiṇī). true contentment does not arise from external objects, which are finite; it is a manifestation of the self. she resides as that internal placid state that marks a mind completely free from craving.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya views this as Her direct manifestation inside human experience:

The Sentiment of Contentment: She is the baseline energy of satisfaction (Santosa-rūpiṇī). True contentment does not arise from external objects, which are finite; it is a manifestation of the Self. She resides as that internal placid state that marks a mind completely free from craving.

name: पुष्टिः

(ii) her form is full of thrivingness, nourishment, plenty, and completeness in all limbs and constituent parts of the body. this has to be read in conjunction with the previous name. bhāskararāya defines her operational role as the cosmic nourisher: the nourishing principle: she is पोषणकारिणी (poṣaṇakāriṇī)—the energy that ensures the growth, stability, and vital sustenance of both the physical cosmos and the spiritual faculties of a practitioner. she feeds the biological framework with health and the spiritual framework with wisdom.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya defines Her operational role as the cosmic nourisher:

The Nourishing Principle: She is पोषणकारिणी (Poṣaṇakāriṇī)—the energy that ensures the growth, stability, and vital sustenance of both the physical cosmos and the spiritual faculties of a practitioner. She feeds the biological framework with health and the spiritual framework with wisdom.

(iii) saptashatee and devee bhagavatam praise sreedevee as present in all beings in forms such as contentment, nourishment, firmness, peace, and radiance. bhāskararāya defines her identity with absolute peace: the termination of fluctuations: she is शान्तिरूपिणी (śāntirūpiṇī)—the state of absolute stillness that remains when all mental modifications (vṛttis) and external agitations are thoroughly pacified. it is the signature of the quieted soul resting in the proximity of brahman.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya defines Her identity with absolute peace:

The Termination of Fluctuations: She is शान्तिरूपिणी (Śāntirūpiṇī)—the state of absolute stillness that remains when all mental modifications (vṛttis) and external agitations are thoroughly pacified. It is the signature of the quieted soul resting in the proximity of Brahman.

name: वदनत्रयसंयुता
wordByWord: • वदनत्रयसंयुता — She who has three faces.
summary: (i) One who has three faces.
(ii) The abdomen is the place of water. Hence ‘She has three faces To explain the three tattvas - ether air and water.

(ii) the abdomen is the place of water. hence ‘she has three faces to explain the three tattvas - ether air and water. bhāskararāya decodes the structural symbolism of the triple countenance at the navel: the triple gaze of fire: the three faces represent the triple dimensions of the fire element governed by this chakra—the digestive fire within the body (jaṭharāgni), the planetary fire in nature (daivāgni), and the internal fire of spiritual discrimination (jñānāgni). they look in three strategic directions to monitor the purification of lower physical drives.

They look in three strategic directions to monitor the purification of lower physical drives.

wordByWord: • रक्त्रवर्णा — She who is red/ blood coloured.

(i) one who is red/ blood coloured. in the saubhāgyabhāskara, bhāskararāya tracks the visual meditation profile of this center's deity: the radiant crimson: unlike the soft rosy hue of the throat, her complexion here is an intense, blazing crimson (rakta). this color directly signifies the high-velocity kinetic energy, heat, and consuming power of the primal fire element (tejas) that she commands at the navel.

name: मांसनिष्ठा
wordByWord: • मांसनिष्ठा — She who is fond of flesh/meat. Thus ends the sixth Kala called Ruchi.
summary: (i) One who is fond of flesh/meat. Thus ends the sixth Kala called Ruchi.

(i) one who is fond of flesh/meat. thus ends the sixth kala called ruchi. bhāskararāya details the biological mapping of the navel śakti: the controller of the muscular grid: within the distribution of the seven foundational tissues (dhātus) of the physical framework, the navel center governs the muscle tissue (māṁsa). she sits as the structural intelligence governing physical strength, movement, and structural tissue mass, linking muscular exertion directly to the metabolic fire of the maṇipūra.

name: गुडान्नप्रीतमानसा
samasa: • गुडेन मिश्रमन्नं तेन प्रीतं मानसं यस्याः सा।
wordByWord: • गुडान्नप्रीतमानसा — the complete name, understood through the samasa above.
summary: (i) One who gets satisfied with rice cooked with jaggery.

(i) one who gets satisfied with rice cooked with jaggery. bhāskararāya traces the exact dietary preferences that conclude the maṇipūra sequence: the alchemical sweetness: jaggery (guḍa) represents the earthy, unctuous sweetness required to cool and safely ground the violent flames of the digestive and energetic fire (agni) located at the navel center, ensuring that the intense kinetic heat does not exhaust the physical organs.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya traces the exact dietary preferences that conclude the Maṇipūra sequence:

name: समस्तभक्तसुखदा
wordByWord: • समस्तभक्तसुखदा — the complete name, understood through the samasa above.
summary: (i) One who bestows happiness on all devotees.
searchText: 502 समस्तभक्तसुखदा • समस्तेभ्यो भक्तेभ्यो सुखं ददातीति। • समस्तेभ्यो — to all. • भक्तेभ्यो — to devotees. • सुखं — happiness. • ददातीति — gives. • समस्तभक्तसुखदा — the complete name, understood through the samasa above. (i) one who bestows happiness on all devotees. in the saubhāgyabhāskara, bhāskararāya clarifies the scope of her benevolence: universal allocation: she does not restrict her gifts to a single class of high-level ascetics. she coordinates the baseline happiness for every tier of seeker, matching their capacity—granting material comfort to the worldly practitioner and non-dual spiritual bliss (mokṣa) to the advanced yogi.
esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara, Bhāskararāya clarifies the scope of Her benevolence:

name: लाकिन्यम्बास्वरूपिणी
wordByWord: • लाकिन्यम्बास्वरूपिणी — the complete name, understood through the samasa above.
summary: (i) One who is in the form of Lakini Devi.
searchText: 503 लाकिन्यम्बास्वरूपिणी • लाकिन्याख्याया अम्बायाः स्वरूपमस्याः अस्तीति। • लाकिन्याख्याया — called lakini. • अम्बायाः — of the mother. • स्वरूपमस्याः — her form. • अस्तीति — is. • लाकिन्यम्बास्वरूपिणी — the complete name, understood through the samasa above. (i) one who is in the form of lakini devi. bhāskararāya provides the definitive structural identification that seals the navel center (maṇipūra): the presiding śakti of the navel: she explicitly identifies herself here as लाकिणी (lākiṇī), the classical administrative śakti placed in supreme charge of the entire ten-petaled maṇipūra framework, consolidating all properties from nāma 495 to 502 directly to her person.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya provides the definitive structural identification that seals the navel center (Maṇipūra):

name: स्वाधिष्ठानाम्बुजगता

summary: (i) One who abides in the Svadhishthana chakra, in the lower abdomen of the body.
searchText: 504 स्वाधिष्ठानाम्बुजगता • स्वाधिष्ठानाख्यम् अम्बुजं गता। • स्वाधिष्ठानाख्यम् — called svadhishthana. • अम्बुजं — lotus. • गता — gone to, residing in. • स्वाधिष्ठानाम्बुजगता — the complete name, understood through the samasa above. (i) one who abides in the svadhishthana chakra, in the lower abdomen of the body. bhāskararāya moves the internal anatomical focus down to the pelvic chakra: the self-established base: she transitions her operation to the स्वाधिष्ठान (svādhiṣṭhāna) chakra, the six-petaled lotus located at the root of the genital organs. this center governs the water element (ap), baseline biological vitality, and represents the station where the individual soul's self-existence (sva-adhiṣṭhāna) is anchored.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya moves the internal anatomical focus down to the pelvic chakra:

The Self-Established Base: She transitions Her operation to the स्वाधिष्ठान (Svādhiṣṭhāna) chakra, the six-petaled lotus located at the root of the genital organs. name: चतुर्वक्त्रमनोहरा
wordByWord: • चतुर्वक्त्रमनोहरा — the complete name, understood through the samasa above.
summary: (i) One who is fascinating with Her four faces.

(ii) Svadhishthana is the place of water. Hence, starting from ether and ending with water, the four primary elements are represented by Her four faces. This is explained in the book by Sri Radhakrishna Sastrigal. The fourteenth verse of Soundaryalahari may also be referred to; it states Svadhishthana as the place of fire and Manipura as the place of water.
searchText: 505 चतुर्वक्त्रमनोहरा • चतुर्भिः वक्त्रैः मनः हरतीति। • चतुर्भिः — with four. • वक्त्रैः — faces. • मनः — mind. • हरतीति — captivates. • चतुर्वक्त्रमनोहरा — the complete name, understood through the samasa above. (i) one who is fascinating with her four faces. (ii) svadhishthana is the place of water. hence, starting from ether and ending with water, the four primary elements are represented by her four faces. this is explained in the book by sri radhakrishna sastrigal. the fourteenth verse of soundaryalahari may also be referred to; it states svadhishthana as the place of fire and manipura as the place of water. bhāskararāya decodes the structural layout of the four countenances at the pelvic base: the quadruple watch: the four faces represent the governance over the four functional paths of energy and fluid circulation within the lower trunk, ensuring that the water element remains pure and captivatingly ordered without stagnant decay.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya decodes the structural layout of the four countenances at the pelvic base:

name: शूलाद्यायुधसम्पन्ना
wordByWord: • शूलाद्यायुधसम्पन्ना — the complete name, understood through the samasa above.
summary: (i) One who has weapons such as the trident. Trident, noose, skull and goad are the weapons of Sreedevee.
searchText: 506 शूलाद्यायुधसम्पन्ना • शूलादिभिः आयुधैः सम्पन्ना। • शूलादिभिः — with trident and others. • आयुधैः — weapons. • सम्पन्ना — endowed. • शूलाद्यायुधसम्पन्ना — the complete name, understood through the samasa above. (i) one who has weapons such as the trident. trident, noose, skull and goad are the weapons of sreedevee. bhāskararāya details the active implements of the pelvic deity: the sharp cut of discerning will: the trident (śūla) acts as the primary weapon at this station. it signifies the piercing control over the triple guṇas and the aggressive striking down of basic lower animal fears, biological laziness, and base urges that cluster around the water center.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya details the active implements of the pelvic deity:

The Sharp Cut of Discerning Will: The trident (Śūla) acts as the primary weapon at this station. It signifies the piercing control over the triple guṇas and the aggressive striking down of basic lower animal fears, biological laziness, and base urges that cluster around the water center.

name: पीतवर्णा
wordByWord: • पीतवर्णा — the complete name, understood through the samasa above.
summary: (i) One who is yellow in hue, golden in colour.

(ii) The word Hiranyavarnam in Sree Sooktam may be remembered. Pita also means saffron flower. Hence it can also be taken that Sreedevee is rosy in colour like the saffron flower. The reddish or rosy colour of Sreedevee is described in many places, for example Sinduraruna Vigraham in the dhyana verse of this Sahasranama and Lauhityametasya Sarvasya Vimarshah in Bhavanopanishad.
searchText: 507 पीतवर्णा • पीतो वर्णो यस्याः सा। • पीतो — yellow. • वर्णो — colour. • यस्याः — whose. • सा — she. • पीतवर्णा — the complete name, understood through the samasa above. (i) one who is yellow in hue, golden in colour. (ii) the word hiranyavarnam in sree sooktam may be remembered. pita also means saffron flower. hence it can also be taken that sreedevee is rosy in colour like the saffron flower. the reddish or rosy colour of sreedevee is described in many places, for example sinduraruna vigraham in the dhyana verse of this sahasranama and lauhityametasya sarvasya vimarshah in bhavanopanishad. in the saubhāgyabhāskara, bhāskararāya tracks the visualization parameters of the pelvic goddess: the radiant gold: her skin glows with a rich, intense yellow radiance (pīta). this specific light frequency provides the grounding, stable energetic property required to balance and illuminate the heavy fluid dynamics of the water element.
esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara, Bhāskararāya tracks the visualization parameters of the pelvic goddess:

name: अतिगर्विता
wordByWord: • सौन्दर्यादिकृतो — caused by beauty and the like.
• अतिगर्विता — the complete name, understood through the samasa above.
summary: (i) One who is very proud or dignified.
(ii) She is proud because She is so beautiful.
(iii) In Saptashatee, in the chapter Doota Samvadam, we read Sreedevee's own words indicating that She has pride: Yo Me Darpam Vyapohati.
(iv) In the 158th name, Nirmada, it is stated that She is free from ordinary pride; here the dignity born of Her beauty and sovereignty is indicated.
searchText: 508 अतिगर्विता • अतीव सौन्दर्यादिकृतो गर्वो यस्याः सा। • अतीव — exceedingly. • सौन्दर्यादिकृतो — caused by beauty and the like. • गर्वो — pride, dignity. • यस्याः — whose. • सा — she. • अतिगर्विता — the complete name, understood through the samasa above. (i) one who is very proud or dignified. (ii) she is proud because she is so beautiful. (iii) in saptashatee, in the chapter doota samvadam, we read sreedevee's own words indicating that she has pride: yo me darpam vyapohati. (iv) in the 158th name, nirmada, it is stated that she is free from ordinary pride; here the dignity born of her beauty and sovereignty is indicated. bhāskararāya clarifies this state, removing any mundane psychological flaws: the pride of absolute sovereignty: her "pride" (garva) is the absolute aesthetic and functional consciousness of her own structural perfection and unmatched cosmic beauty. she bows to nothing external, standing completely secure in her independent status (svātantrya).
esoteric: Bhāskararāya clarifies this state, removing any mundane psychological flaws:

The Pride of Absolute Sovereignty: Her "pride" (garva) is the absolute aesthetic and functional consciousness of Her own structural perfection and unmatched cosmic beauty. She bows to nothing external, standing completely secure in Her independent status (Svātantrya).

name: मेदोनिष्ठा

summary: (i) One who is residing in, or presiding over, the fatty tissues of the body.
(ii) Since She is the presiding deity for these tissues, She was mentioned as pride in the previous name.
searchText: 509 मेदोनिष्ठा • मेदसि निष्ठा यस्याः सा। • मेदसि — in fat tissue. • निष्ठा — established, abiding. • यस्याः — whose. • सा — she. • मेदोनिष्ठा — the complete name, understood through the samasa above. (i) one who is residing in, or presiding over, the fatty tissues of the body. (ii) since she is the presiding deity for these tissues, she was mentioned as pride in the previous name. bhāskararāya details the biological tissue management at the pelvic center: the controller of lipids: within the metabolic distribution of bodily components, the svādhiṣṭhāna governs the fat tissue (medas). she operates as the structural intelligence governing lipid storage, physical insulation, and lubrication, directly linking fat retention to the water mechanics of this chakra.

name: मधुप्रीता
wordByWord: • मधुप्रीता — the complete name, understood through the samasa above.

(ii) Madhu also indicates honey. Sruti also says that one who presents an oblation with madhu pleases the great Devee: Yan Madhuna Juhoti Mahatimeva Taddevatam Prinati.

esoteric: Bhāskararāya transitions the entire system to its absolute transcendental peak center:

The Crown Kingdom: She moves past the six localized chakras directly into the सहस्रार (Sahasrāra) at the crown of the head. This is the shoreless kingdom of pure non-duality, where all individual element definitions dissolve into the infinite downpour of absolute consciousness and nectar.

name: सर्ववर्णोपशोभिता
samasa: • सर्वाणि वर्णानि (अक्षराणि) यासां ताभिः शक्तिभिः उप समीपे शोभिता।
wordByWord: • पाटलश्यामरक्तपीतादिरूपैः — having forms such as pink, dark, red and yellow.
• सर्ववर्णोपशोभिता — the complete name, understood through the samasa above.
summary: (i) One who shines with all colours.
(ii) All colours, or all letters, are included in Her retinue and form.
searchText: 529 सर्ववर्णोपशोभिता • सर्वैः वर्णैः (पाटलश्यामरक्तपीतादिरूपैः) उपशोभिता। चित्रवर्णा इत्यर्थः। • सर्वाणि वर्णानि (अक्षराणि) यासां ताभिः शक्तिभिः उप समीपे शोभिता। • सर्वैः — with all. • वर्णैः — colours or letters. • पाटलश्यामरक्तपीतादिरूपैः — having forms such as pink, dark, red and yellow. • उपशोभिता — adorned, made beautiful. • चित्रवर्णा — variegated in colour. • इत्यर्थः — this is the meaning. • सर्वाणि — all. • वर्णानि — letters/colours. • अक्षराणि — letters. • यासां — whose. • ताभिः — by them. • शक्तिभिः — with shaktis. • उप — near. • समीपे — near, close by. • शोभिता — adorned. • सर्ववर्णोपशोभिता — the complete name, understood through the samasa above. (i) one who shines with all colours. (ii) all colours, or all letters, are included in her retinue and form. bhāskararāya reconciles the visual and phonic architecture of the crown: the chromatic and phonic synthesis: in the crown center, all the color frequencies and all fifty-one phonic syllables (varṇas) that were previously scattered across the lower chakras return to their source, swirling together in a brilliant, holistic display of integrated light and sound.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya reconciles the visual and phonic architecture of the crown:

The Chromatic and Phonic Synthesis: In the crown center, all the color frequencies and all fifty-one phonic syllables (varṇas) that were previously scattered across the lower chakras return to their source, swirling together in a brilliant, holistic display of integrated light and sound.

name: सर्वायुधधरा
wordByWord: • सर्वायुधधरा — the complete name, understood through the samasa above.
summary: (i) One who is armed with all weapons. Vishnu Sahasranama names Sarvashastrabhrutavara and Sarvapraharanayudha may be compared.
searchText: 530 सर्वायुधधरा • सर्वेषाम् आयुधानां धरा। • सर्वेषाम् — of all. • आयुधानां — weapons. • धरा — bearer. • सर्वायुधधरा — the complete name, understood through the samasa above. (i) one who is armed with all weapons. vishnu sahasranama names sarvashastrabhrutavara and sarvapraharanayudha may be compared. in the saubhāgyabhāskara, bhāskararāya explains her total martial preparedness at the crown: the arsenal of supreme will: because the crown represents the absolute consolidation of all cosmic powers, she does not hold a limited selection of tools here. she holds the entire arsenal of divine weapons simultaneously, representing the absolute capacity to crush any tier of ignorance instantly.
esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara, Bhāskararāya explains Her total martial preparedness at the crown:

The Arsenal of Supreme Will: Because the crown represents the absolute consolidation of all cosmic powers, She does not hold a limited selection of tools here. She holds the entire arsenal of divine weapons simultaneously, representing the absolute capacity to crush any tier of ignorance instantly.

name: शुक्लसंस्थिता
wordByWord: • शुक्ले — in semen / the white principle.
• वीर्याख्यधातौ — in the bodily tissue called semen.
• शुक्लसंस्थिता — the complete name, understood through the samasa above.
summary: (i) One who is the presiding deity of semen.
(ii) According to the Bhavishyottara Purana, Shukla is the name of a kind of meditation at the time of sexual enjoyment. It can also be taken that She is in that form.
searchText: 531 शुक्लसंस्थिता • शुक्ले (वीर्याख्यधातौ) सम्यक् (तदभिमानितया) स्थिता। • [भविष्योत्तरपुराणे कथिते] शुक्लसंज्ञे ध्यानविशेषे स्थिता। • शुक्ले — in semen / the white principle. • वीर्याख्यधातौ — in the bodily tissue called semen. • सम्यक् — fully, properly. • तदभिमानितया — as its presiding deity. • स्थिता — situated. • भविष्योत्तरपुराणे — in bhavishyottara purana. • कथिते — stated. • शुक्लसंज्ञे — called shukla. • ध्यानविशेषे — in a particular meditation. • शुक्लसंस्थिता — the complete name, understood through the samasa above. (i) one who is the presiding deity of semen. (ii) according to the bhavishyottara purana, shukla is the name of a kind of meditation at the time of sexual enjoyment. it can also be taken that she is in that form. bhāskararāya details both the biological core and the meditative state: the essence of vitality: she governs the ultimate, highly concentrated vital fluid (śukla) that marks the physical transformation of food essence. the pure meditation: it denotes her permanent absorption in the śukla-dhyāna described in the bhaviṣyottara purāṇa—the pristine, unblemished white light meditation where no thought ripples can survive.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya details both the biological core and the meditative state:
The Essence of Vitality: She governs the ultimate, highly concentrated vital fluid (śukla) that marks the physical transformation of food essence.

The Pure Meditation: It denotes Her permanent absorption in the Śukla-dhyāna described in the Bhaviṣyottara Purāṇa—the pristine, unblemished white light meditation where no thought ripples can survive.

name: सर्वतोमुखी
wordByWord: • सर्वतोमुखी — the complete name, understood through the samasa above.
summary: (i) One who has faces in all directions.
(ii) In Srimad Bhagavad Gita it is said: Sarvatah akshi-shiro-mukham, everywhere eyes, heads and mouths.
(iii) In Sruti, in the Purusha Sooktam also, it is mentioned as Sahasrashirsha.
searchText: 532 सर्वतोमुखी • सर्वतः मुखानि यस्याः सा। • सर्वतः — on all sides. • मुखानि — faces. • यस्याः — whose. • सा — she. • सर्वतोमुखी — the complete name, understood through the samasa above. (i) one who has faces in all directions. (ii) in srimad bhagavad gita it is said: sarvatah akshi-shiro-mukham, everywhere eyes, heads and mouths. (iii) in sruti, in the purusha sooktam also, it is mentioned as sahasrashirsha. bhāskararāya connects this to her absolute spatial omnipresence: the omnidirectional vision: at the absolute peak of the crown, she is no longer limited by structural angles. she sees, handles, and interfaces with all coordinates of the multi-dimensional universe simultaneously. she is face-to-face with every atom in existence.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya connects this to Her absolute spatial omnipresence:

The Omnidirectional Vision: At the absolute peak of the crown, She is no longer limited by structural angles. She sees, handles, and interfaces with all coordinates of the multi-dimensional universe simultaneously. She is face-to-face with every atom in existence.

name: सर्वौदनप्रीतचित्ता
samasa: • सर्वैः ओदनैः प्रीतं चित्तं यस्याः सा।
wordByWord: • सर्वौदनप्रीतचित्ता — the complete name, understood through the samasa above.
summary: (i) One who gets pleased with all kinds of rice.
(ii) As mentioned earlier, right from payasa rice to yellow rice and other rice preparations, She is pleased with all kinds of rice.
searchText: 533 सर्वौदनप्रीतचित्ता • सर्वैः ओदनैः प्रीतं चित्तं यस्याः सा। • सर्वैः — with all. • ओदनैः — with rice preparations. • प्रीतं — pleased. • चित्तं — mind. • यस्याः — whose. • सा — she. • सर्वौदनप्रीतचित्ता — the complete name, understood through the samasa above. (i) one who gets pleased with all kinds of rice. (ii) as mentioned earlier, right from payasa rice to yellow rice and other rice preparations, she is pleased with all kinds of rice. bhāskararāya outlines the grand culinary synthesis at the crown peak: fulfillment of all nutritional frequencies: because she has climbed past the individual element restrictions of the lower centers (where she required distinct single foods), at the crown she accepts all categories of nourishment simultaneously, showing her complete satisfaction and integration of all material properties.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya outlines the grand culinary synthesis at the crown peak:

Fulfillment of All Nutritional Frequencies: Because She has climbed past the individual element restrictions of the lower centers (where She required distinct single foods), at the crown She accepts all categories of nourishment simultaneously, showing Her complete satisfaction and integration of all material properties.

name: याकिन्यम्बास्वरूपिणी
wordByWord: • याकिन्यम्बास्वरूपिणी — the complete name, understood through the samasa above.
summary: (i) One who appears in the form of Yakini.
(ii) It is the quality of Sreedevee to admire Paramashiva: Parashiva-rasikam.
(iii) Thus, having described Sreedevee under the forms of Yoginis, the text proceeds to describe Her other forms and qualities.
searchText: 534 याकिन्यम्बास्वरूपिणी • याकिन्याख्यायाः अम्बायाः स्वरूपं अस्याः अस्तीति। • याकिन्याख्यायाः — called yakini. • अम्बायाः — of the mother. • स्वरूपं — form. • अस्याः — of her. • अस्तीति — is. • याकिन्यम्बास्वरूपिणी — the complete name, understood through the samasa above. (i) one who appears in the form of yakini. (ii) it is the quality of sreedevee to admire paramashiva: parashiva-rasikam. (iii) thus, having described sreedevee under the forms of yoginis, the text proceeds to describe her other forms and qualities. bhāskararāya provides the grand capstone identification that seals the entire six-chakra structural layout: the master śakti of the crown: she explicitly identifies herself here as याकिणी (yākiṇī), the absolute, high-level administrative śakti placed in charge of the supreme sahasrāra matrix. this name triumphantly concludes the deep internal anatomical tour running from nāma 475 to 533, anchoring the complete subtle layout directly to her non-dual person.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya provides the grand capstone identification that seals the entire six-chakra structural layout:

The Master Śakti of the Crown: She explicitly identifies Herself here as याकिणी (Yākiṇī), the absolute, high-level administrative Śakti placed in charge of the supreme Sahasrāra matrix. This name triumphantly concludes the deep internal anatomical tour running from Nāma 475 to 533, anchoring the complete subtle layout directly to Her non-dual person.

name: स्वाहा
wordByWord: • स्वाहार्थरूपा — having the form of the meaning of Svaha.
• स्वाहा — the complete name, understood through the samasa above.
summary: (i) One who is in the form of Svaha, the sacred exclamation with which oblations are made in the sacrificial fire for gods.
searchText: 535 स्वाहा • स्वाहार्थरूपा। • सुष्ठु आहूयते अनया इति। • स्वान् आजिहीते (स्वीयत्वेन सम्यग्जानाति) इति। • स्वाहार्थरूपा — having the form of the meaning of svaha. • सुष्ठु — well, properly. • आहूयते — is invoked. • अनया — by this. • इति — thus. • स्वान् — one’s own. • आजिहीते — calls, brings near. • स्वीयत्वेन — as one’s own. • सम्यग्जानाति — knows properly. • स्वाहा — the complete name, understood through the samasa above. (i) one who is in the form of svaha, the sacred exclamation with which oblations are made in the sacrificial fire for gods. bhāskararāya details her function within the fire sacrifice (homa): the consumer of oblations: she is the phonic reality of the mantra स्वाहा (svāhā). without her presence, no offering dropped into the physical sacrificial fire can reach the devas. she acts as the dynamic alchemical bridge that transforms material oblations into subtle cosmic energy.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya details Her function within the fire sacrifice (Homa):

The Consumer of Oblations: She is the phonic reality of the mantra स्वाहा (Svāhā). Without Her presence, no offering dropped into the physical sacrificial fire can reach the Devas. She acts as the dynamic alchemical bridge that transforms material oblations into subtle cosmic energy.

name: स्वधा
wordByWord: • अं — the syllable/being indicated by am.
• स्वधा — the complete name, understood through the samasa above.
summary: (i) One who is in the form of Svadha.
(ii) The words Svaha and Svadha are used when oblations are offered: Svaha for the gods in the sacrificial fire and Svadha for the Pitrs, the ancestors. Devee Bhagavatam also states this distinction.
(iii) These names also indicate that She is in the form of the Devis represented by Svaha and Svadha.
searchText: 536 स्वधा • सुष्ठु आधीयते अनया इति। • सुष्ठु अं (विष्णुं स्वान् वा) दधातीति। • सुष्ठु — well, properly. • आधीयते — is placed or offered. • अनया — by this. • इति — thus. • अं — the syllable/being indicated by am. • विष्णुं — vishnu. • स्वान् — one’s own. • वा — or. • दधातीति — holds, sustains. • स्वधा — the complete name, understood through the samasa above. (i) one who is in the form of svadha. (ii) the words svaha and svadha are used when oblations are offered: svaha for the gods in the sacrificial fire and svadha for the pitrs, the ancestors. devee bhagavatam also states this distinction. (iii) these names also indicate that she is in the form of the devis represented by svaha and svadha. bhāskararāya coordinates her function within the ancestral rites (tarpaṇa): sustenance of the ancestors: she is the phonic power of the mantra स्वधा (svadhā), which unlocks the channel to the ancestral planes (pitrū-loka). she ensures the transmission of offerings to nourish the departed roots of the family line, holding ancestral order in equilibrium.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya coordinates Her function within the ancestral rites (Tarpaṇa):

Sustenance of the Ancestors: She is the phonic power of the mantra स्वधा (Svadhā), which unlocks the channel to the ancestral planes (Pitrū-loka). She ensures the transmission of offerings to nourish the departed roots of the family line, holding ancestral order in equilibrium.

name: अमतिः
wordByWord: • वृत्त्यात्मकविद्यारूपा — a form of knowledge as mental modification.
• सृष्टिरूपा — in the form of creation.
• अमतिः — the complete name, understood through the samasa above.
summary: (i) One who is in the form of ignorance or non-understanding, and also as the primordial movement connected with creation.
(ii) Amati is explained as avidya. It may also indicate a form of knowledge functioning through mental modifications, according to the grammatical and interpretive explanations.
searchText: 537 अमतिः • अविद्या। • [अल्पार्थे नञ् इत्यनेन] वृत्त्यात्मकविद्यारूपा। • अविद्यापूर्वा प्राथमिकी (विद्यापूर्वा च क्रमप्राप्ता) सृष्टिरूपा। • अविद्या — ignorance. • अल्पार्थे — in a minor/secondary sense. • नञ् — the negative particle nañ. • इत्यनेन — by this. • वृत्त्यात्मकविद्यारूपा — a form of knowledge as mental modification. • अविद्यापूर्वा — preceded by avidya. • प्राथमिकी — primary, initial. • विद्यापूर्वा — preceded by vidya. • च — and. • क्रमप्राप्ता — arrived in sequence. • सृष्टिरूपा — in the form of creation. • अमतिः — the complete name, understood through the samasa above. (i) one who is in the form of ignorance or non-understanding, and also as the primordial movement connected with creation. (ii) amati is explained as avidya. it may also indicate a form of knowledge functioning through mental modifications, according to the grammatical and interpretive explanations. bhāskararāya navigates a deep non-dual philosophical transition: the screen of unconsciousness: as amati, she represents the temporary suspension of active intellectual modifications—either as the baseline cosmic sleep before creation, or as the state of avidyā that allows the relative world to appear separate. it confirms that even the absence of active intellect is a corner of her power.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya navigates a deep non-dual philosophical transition:

The Screen of Unconsciousness: As Amati, She represents the temporary suspension of active intellectual modifications—either as the baseline cosmic sleep before creation, or as the state of Avidyā that allows the relative world to appear separate. It confirms that even the absence of active intellect is a corner of Her power.

name: मेधा
wordByWord: • विदिताखिलवेदसारा — one who knows the essence of all the Vedas.
• बुद्धिविशेषरूपा — in the form of a special intellect.
• मेधा — the complete name, understood through the samasa above.
summary: (i) One who is in the form of Medha, retentive intelligence.
(ii) Medha is the power that grasps and retains the essence of all the Vedas.
searchText: 538 मेधा • विदिताखिलवेदसारा। • धीर्धारणवती बुद्धिविशेषरूपा। • विदिताखिलवेदसारा — one who knows the essence of all the vedas. • धीर्धारणवती — intelligence that retains. • बुद्धिविशेषरूपा — in the form of a special intellect. • मेधा — the complete name, understood through the samasa above. (i) one who is in the form of medha, retentive intelligence. (ii) medha is the power that grasps and retains the essence of all the vedas. in the saubhāgyabhāskara, bhāskararāya defines this refined cognitive faculty: the power of spiritual retention: medhā is not simple data memory. it is the highly specialized capacity of the intellect (buddhi) to grasp, hold, and integrate the deepest essence of scriptural truth without forgetting or distorting it. she is that internal steel trap that preserves self-knowledge inside the seeker.
esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara, Bhāskararāya defines this refined cognitive faculty:

The Power of Spiritual Retention: Medhā is not simple data memory. It is the highly specialized capacity of the intellect (buddhi) to grasp, hold, and integrate the deepest essence of scriptural truth without forgetting or distorting it. She is that internal steel trap that preserves self-knowledge inside the seeker.

name: श्रुतिः
wordByWord: • वेदरूपा — in the form of the Veda.
• श्रुतिः — the complete name, understood through the samasa above.
summary: (i) One who is in the form of the Vedas.
searchText: 539 श्रुतिः • वेदरूपा। • वेदरूपा — in the form of the veda. • श्रुतिः — the complete name, understood through the samasa above. (i) one who is in the form of the vedas. bhāskararāya anchors her identity with the highest scriptural authority: the living revelation: she is the absolute phonic framework of the श्रुति (śruti — the four eternal vedas). they are not human books; they are the uncorrupted sound waves of truth heard by ancient rishis in deep absorption. she is the substance, code, and voice of this baseline divine revelation.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya anchors Her identity with the highest scriptural authority:

The Living Revelation: She is the absolute phonic framework of the श्रुति (Śruti — the four eternal Vedas). They are not human books; they are the uncorrupted sound waves of truth heard by ancient rishis in deep absorption. She is the substance, code, and voice of this baseline divine revelation.

name: स्मृतिः
wordByWord: • स्मृतिः — the complete name, understood through the samasa above.
summary: (i) One who is in the form of Smritis.
(ii) Sruti means hearing and Smriti means recollection. She is in the form of knowledge obtained through both.

(iii) It is mentioned in many places that the Vedas and Smritis, such as those of Manu, are Her form. Saptashatee and Koorma Purana also convey that the Vedas are the inherent form of Brahmam and the eternal Shakti.
searchText: 540 स्मृतिः • स्मरते सर्वकार्याणीति। • स्मरते — remembers. • सर्वकार्याणीति — all actions. • स्मृतिः — the complete name, understood through the samasa above. (i) one who is in the form of smritis. (ii) sruti means hearing and smriti means recollection. she is in the form of knowledge obtained through both. (iii) it is mentioned in many places that the vedas and smritis, such as those of manu, are her form. saptashatee and koorma purana also convey that the vedas are the inherent form of brahmam and the eternal shakti. bhāskararāya details the execution of cosmic and practical law: the structural memory: she is the power of memory (smṛti) that keeps the laws of human conduct and cosmic history intact. concurrently, she manifests as the authoritative smṛti texts (like manu or yājñavalkya) that translate the high revelations of the śruti into practical ethical systems for society.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya details the execution of cosmic and practical law:

The Structural Memory: She is the power of memory (Smṛti) that keeps the laws of human conduct and cosmic history intact. Concurrently, She manifests as the authoritative Smṛti texts (like Manu or Yājñavalkya) that translate the high revelations of the Śruti into practical ethical systems for society.

name: अनुत्तमा
samasa: • न नुत्ता परप्रेरिता मा बुद्धिरैश्वर्यं वा यस्या सा।
wordByWord: • यदपेक्षयोत्तममन्यद्वस्तु — a thing superior in comparison with whom.
• अनुत्तमा — the complete name, understood through the samasa above.
summary: (i) One who has none superior to Her.
(ii) There is nothing higher than Her. She is not dependent on praise, external prompting, or any other authority.
searchText: 541 अनुत्तमा • यदपेक्षयोत्तममन्यद्वस्तु नास्ति सा। • न नुत्ता परप्रेरिता मा बुद्धिरैश्वर्यं वा यस्या सा। • यदपेक्षयोत्तममन्यद्वस्तु — a thing superior in comparison with whom. • नास्ति — does not exist. • सा — she. • न — not. • नुत्ता — praised / prompted. • परप्रेरिता — impelled by another. • मा — measure, knowledge, or power. • बुद्धिरैश्वर्यं — intellect or lordship. • वा — or. • यस्या — whose. • अनुत्तमा — the complete name, understood through the samasa above. (i) one who has none superior to her. (ii) there is nothing higher than her. she is not dependent on praise, external prompting, or any other authority. bhāskararāya confirms her absolute position at the peak of reality: the unsurpassed absolute: if any entity existed above her, her sovereignty would be secondary. she is anuttamā because she stands as the definitive ceiling of existence. there is no higher reality, no superior consciousness, and no master back behind her station.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya confirms Her absolute position at the peak of reality:

The Unsurpassed Absolute: If any entity existed above Her, Her sovereignty would be secondary. She is Anuttamā because She stands as the definitive ceiling of existence. There is no higher reality, no superior consciousness, and no master back behind Her station.

name: पुण्यकीर्तिः
wordByWord: • पुण्यकीर्तिः — the complete name, understood through the samasa above.
summary: (i) One whose fame is holy and meritorious.
(ii) Her praise itself is auspicious and bestows merit.
searchText: 542 पुण्यकीर्तिः • पुण्या पुण्यप्रदा कीर्तिर्यस्याः सा। • पुण्या — holy, meritorious. • पुण्यप्रदा — bestower of merit. • कीर्तिर्यस्याः — whose fame. • सा — she. • पुण्यकीर्तिः — the complete name, understood through the samasa above. (i) one whose fame is holy and meritorious. (ii) her praise itself is auspicious and bestows merit. in the saubhāgyabhāskara, bhāskararāya details the physics of her renown: the purifying renown: ordinary fame feeds vanity. her fame is puṇya—it carries a tangible energetic frequency. by simply listening to, repeating, or celebrating her historical and cosmic triumphs, the heavy karmic impurities inside the listener's mind are dissolved, replaced by automatic merit.
esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara, Bhāskararāya details the physics of Her renown:

The Purifying Renown: Ordinary fame feeds vanity. Her fame is Puṇya—it carries a tangible energetic frequency. By simply listening to, repeating, or celebrating Her historical and cosmic triumphs, the heavy karmic impurities inside the listener's mind are dissolved, replaced by automatic merit.

name: पुण्यलभ्या
wordByWord: • पुण्यलभ्या — the complete name, understood through the samasa above.
summary: (i) One who can be attained only through virtue and righteousness.
(ii) Punya means good actions performed in previous births. Only through such punya can Her blessings be attained.

(iii) Devee Bhagavatam says that only those righteous ascetics devoted to wisdom see the beneficent Devee; men of desire do not. Sree Adi Sankara also conveys the same idea in his commentary on the first verse of Soundaryalahari.
searchText: 543 पुण्यलभ्या • पुण्यैः लभ्या। • पुण्यैः — through merit. • लभ्या — attainable. • पुण्यलभ्या — the complete name, understood through the samasa above. (i) one who can be attained only through virtue and righteousness. (ii) punya means good actions performed in previous births. only through such punya can her blessings be attained. (iii) devee bhagavatam says that only those righteous ascetics devoted to wisdom see the beneficent devee; men of desire do not. sree adi sankara also conveys the same idea in his commentary on the first verse of soundaryalahari. bhāskararāya clarifies the transactional alignment of spiritual access: the currency of merit: she cannot be purchased with money, captured by aggressive egoic intellect, or reached by deceptive minds. the only operational currency that unlocks her gate is puṇya—the slow, steady accumulation of virtuous deeds, mental purity, and selfless alignment with dharma over lifetimes.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya clarifies the transactional alignment of spiritual access:

The Currency of Merit: She cannot be purchased with money, captured by aggressive egoic intellect, or reached by deceptive minds. The only operational currency that unlocks Her gate is Puṇya—the slow, steady accumulation of virtuous deeds, mental purity, and selfless alignment with dharma over lifetimes.

name: पुण्यश्रवणकीर्तना
wordByWord: • विहितकर्मरूपे — in the form of prescribed action.
• पुण्यश्रवणकीर्तना — the complete name, understood through the samasa above.
summary: (i) One whose hearing and chanting are meritorious.
(ii) Listening to and reciting Her names are themselves holy acts.
searchText: 544 पुण्यश्रवणकीर्तना • पुण्ये विहितकर्मरूपे श्रवणकीर्तने यस्याः सा। • पुण्ये — in merit. • विहितकर्मरूपे — in the form of prescribed action. • श्रवणकीर्तने — hearing and chanting. • यस्याः — whose. • सा — she. • पुण्यश्रवणकीर्तना — the complete name, understood through the samasa above. (i) one whose hearing and chanting are meritorious. (ii) listening to and reciting her names are themselves holy acts. bhāskararāya re-emphasizes the ease of access built into her path: the sonic purification: one does not need to master complex yoga posture loops or live in isolated caves to start the alignment. by simply using the ears to hear her text (śravaṇa) and the tongue to recite her syllables (kīrtana), the practitioner's internal environment is realigned with cosmic order.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya re-emphasizes the ease of access built into Her path:

The Sonic Purification: One does not need to master complex yoga posture loops or live in isolated caves to start the alignment. By simply using the ears to hear Her text (śravaṇa) and the tongue to recite Her syllables (kīrtana), the practitioner's internal environment is realigned with cosmic order.

name: पुलोमजार्चिता
wordByWord: • पुलोमजार्चिता — the complete name, understood through the samasa above.
summary: (i) One who is worshipped by Pulomaja, Indrani, the daughter of Puloma.
searchText: 545 पुलोमजार्चिता • पुलोमतो जातया (इन्द्राण्या) अर्चिता • पुलोमतो — from puloma. • जातया — born of. • इन्द्राण्या — by indrani. • अर्चिता — worshipped. • पुलोमजार्चिता — the complete name, understood through the samasa above. (i) one who is worshipped by pulomaja, indrani, the daughter of puloma. bhāskararāya highlights her adoration across the highest levels of celestial sovereignty: the worship of the heavenly queen: indrāṇī, the empress of the gods, adores her to maintain the stability of the heavenly throne. it demonstrates that even those who have attained the absolute peak of celestial pleasure and position must bow before her to hold their office securely.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya highlights Her adoration across the highest levels of celestial sovereignty:

The Worship of the Heavenly Queen: Indrāṇī, the empress of the gods, adores Her to maintain the stability of the heavenly throne. It demonstrates that even those who have attained the absolute peak of celestial pleasure and position must bow before Her to hold their office securely.

name: बन्धमोचनी
wordByWord: • बन्धमोचनी — the complete name, understood through the samasa above.
summary: (i) One who releases beings from bondage.
searchText: 546 बन्धमोचनी • बन्धं मोचयतीति। • बन्धं — bondage. • मोचयतीति — releases. • बन्धमोचनी — the complete name, understood through the samasa above. (i) one who releases beings from bondage. in the saubhāgyabhāskara, bhāskararāya targets the ultimate human problem: the cutter of the karmic tether: बन्ध (bandha) is the structural imprisonment of the individual soul inside the cycle of birth and death, forged by ego, attachment, and the illusion of separation. as bandhamocanī, she snaps these chains cleanly, granting immediate spiritual liberation (mokṣa).
esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara, Bhāskararāya targets the ultimate human problem:

The Cutter of the Karmic Tether: बन्ध (bandha) is the structural imprisonment of the individual soul inside the cycle of birth and death, forged by ego, attachment, and the illusion of separation. As Bandhamocanī, She snaps these chains cleanly, granting immediate spiritual liberation (Mokṣa).

name: बन्धुरालका बर्बरालका
wordByWord: • बन्धुरालका बर्बरालका — the complete name, understood through the samasa above.
summary: (i) One who has charming, curly tresses.
searchText: 547 बन्धुरालका बर्बरालका • बन्धुरा बर्बरा अलकाः यस्याः सा। • बन्धुरा — beautiful. • बर्बरा — curly. • अलकाः — locks of hair. • यस्याः — whose. • सा — she. • बन्धुरालका बर्बरालका — the complete name, understood through the samasa above. (i) one who has charming, curly tresses. bhāskararāya moves from the poetic physical decoration to structural cosmology: the coiled waves of creation: her hair strands represent the infinite, curled, and interwoven rays of cosmic energy projected from her head. they appear wavy and complex (bandhura) to the human eye, symbolizing the intricate, multi-layered fabric of the manifest universe that she wears effortlessly.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya moves from the poetic physical decoration to structural cosmology:

The Coiled Waves of Creation: Her hair strands represent the infinite, curled, and interwoven rays of cosmic energy projected from Her head. They appear wavy and complex (bandhura) to the human eye, symbolizing the intricate, multi-layered fabric of the manifest universe that She wears effortlessly.

name: विमर्शरूपिणी
wordByWord: • विमर्शरूपिणी — the complete name, understood through the samasa above.
summary: (i) One who is of the form of Vimarsha.
(ii) Vimarsha is the self-awareness aspect of consciousness, without which pure Prakasha cannot manifest as experience.
searchText: 548 विमर्शरूपिणी • विमर्शः एव रूपम् अस्याः अस्तीति। • विमर्शः — reflective consciousness, self-awareness. • एव — itself, indeed. • अस्याः — of her. • अस्तीति — is. • विमर्शरूपिणी — the complete name, understood through the samasa above. (i) one who is of the form of vimarsha. (ii) vimarsha is the self-awareness aspect of consciousness, without which pure prakasha cannot manifest as experience. bhāskararāya details one of the absolute pillars of śrīvidyā/non-dual śākta theology: the dynamic mirror of consciousness: ultimate reality consists of two aspects: प्रकाश (prakāśa — the static, self-luminous light of pure consciousness, śiva) and विमर्श (vimarśa — the dynamic, self-reflective awareness of that light, śakti). without vimarśa, consciousness would remain inert, unable to know its own existence or project worlds. she is that critical cosmic mirror.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya details one of the absolute pillars of Śrīvidyā/Non-dual Śākta theology:

The Dynamic Mirror of Consciousness: Ultimate reality consists of two aspects: प्रकाश (Prakāśa — the static, self-luminous light of pure consciousness, Śiva) and विमर्श (Vimarśa — the dynamic, self-reflective awareness of that light, Śakti). Without Vimarśa, consciousness would remain inert, unable to know its own existence or project worlds. She is that critical cosmic mirror.

name: विद्या
wordByWord: • मोक्षप्रदज्ञानस्वरूपत्वाद्विद्या — Vidya because she is knowledge that grants liberation.
• विद्या — the complete name, understood through the samasa above.
summary: (i) She is the Vidya that confers salvation.
(ii) Saptashatee says that the holy supreme Devee is Vidya. Gaudapada also says in Sree Vidya Ratna Sutra that She herself is Vidya.
(iii) Vidya is the supreme knowledge by which the products of Maya are discriminated and the state of wisdom is recognized.
searchText: 549 विद्या • मोक्षप्रदज्ञानस्वरूपत्वाद्विद्या। • मोक्षप्रदज्ञानस्वरूपत्वाद्विद्या — vidya because she is knowledge that grants liberation. • विद्या — the complete name, understood through the samasa above. (i) she is the vidya that confers salvation. (ii) saptashatee says that the holy supreme devee is vidya. gaudapada also says in sree vidya ratna sutra that she herself is vidya. (iii) vidya is the supreme knowledge by which the products of maya are discriminated and the state of wisdom is recognized. bhāskararāya distinguishes her from ordinary informational data fields: the science of liberation: she is not aparā-vidyā (relative worldly information). she is परविद्या (parā-vidyā)—the direct, experiential non-dual insight that breaks the illusion of cosmic separation and reveals the identity of the self with brahman, acting as the immediate engine of mokṣa.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya distinguishes Her from ordinary informational data fields:

The Science of Liberation: She is not Aparā-vidyā (relative worldly information). She is परविद्या (Parā-vidyā)—the direct, experiential non-dual insight that breaks the illusion of cosmic separation and reveals the identity of the Self with Brahman, acting as the immediate engine of Mokṣa.

name: वियदादिजगत्प्रसूः
wordByWord: • वियदादिजगत्प्रसूः — the complete name, understood through the samasa above.
summary: (i) One who is the mother of the universe consisting of ether and other elements.

(ii) In Taittiriya Upanishad it is mentioned that ether arose from the Supreme Being, air from ether, fire from air, water from fire and earth from water. These are the primary elements for the creation of this dynamic universe.

(iii) Vyad, or ether, is perceived as close, yet seems farther as one approaches. Similarly the Supreme Being appears to be understood, yet remains beyond grasp. This status is indicated through ether, the first of the elements.
searchText: 550 वियदादिजगत्प्रसूः • वियद्व्योम आदिर्यस्य तज्जगत्प्रसूते इति। • वियद्व्योम — ether/space. • आदिर्यस्य — beginning with which. • तज्जगत्प्रसूते — mother/source of that world. • इति — thus. • वियदादिजगत्प्रसूः — the complete name, understood through the samasa above. (i) one who is the mother of the universe consisting of ether and other elements. (ii) in taittiriya upanishad it is mentioned that ether arose from the supreme being, air from ether, fire from air, water from fire and earth from water. these are the primary elements for the creation of this dynamic universe. (iii) vyad, or ether, is perceived as close, yet seems farther as one approaches. similarly the supreme being appears to be understood, yet remains beyond grasp. this status is indicated through ether, the first of the elements. in the saubhāgyabhāskara, bhāskararāya tracks the systematic cosmic evolution: the mother of elements: the universe evolves in a precise cascade: first space (ākāśa), then air, fire, water, and earth. she is the primordial matrix (prasūḥ) that stands before the first element, breathing out the space matrix within which the rest of creation can subsequently unfold.
esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara, Bhāskararāya tracks the systematic cosmic evolution:

The Mother of Elements: The universe evolves in a precise cascade: first space (Ākāśa), then air, fire, water, and earth. She is the primordial matrix (Prasūḥ) that stands before the first element, breathing out the space matrix within which the rest of creation can subsequently unfold.

name: सर्वव्याधिप्रशमनी
wordByWord: • सर्वव्याधिप्रशमनी — the complete name, understood through the samasa above.
summary: (i) One who cures or alleviates all illnesses and diseases.
(ii) The same message is conveyed in the 876th name, Niramaya.
searchText: 551 सर्वव्याधिप्रशमनी • सर्वेषां व्याधीनां प्रशमनी। • सर्वेषां — of all. • व्याधीनां — of diseases. • प्रशमनी — pacifier, remover. • सर्वव्याधिप्रशमनी — the complete name, understood through the samasa above. (i) one who cures or alleviates all illnesses and diseases. (ii) the same message is conveyed in the 876th name, niramaya. bhāskararāya details her healing capacity across both dense and subtle tracks: the absolute physician: vyādhi operates on two levels: physical biological diseases of the body, and the ultimate subtle disease—भवव्याधि (bhava-vyādhi—the chronic sickness of saṁsāric wandering). she forcefully eradicates both, cooling the fever of the physical organs and curing the spiritual blindness of the soul.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya details Her healing capacity across both dense and subtle tracks:

The Absolute Physician: Vyādhi operates on two levels: physical biological diseases of the body, and the ultimate subtle disease—भवव्याधि (bhava-vyādhi—the chronic sickness of saṁsāric wandering). She forcefully eradicates both, cooling the fever of the physical organs and curing the spiritual blindness of the soul.

name: सर्वमृत्युनिवारिणी
wordByWord: • सर्वमृत्युनिवारिणी — the complete name, understood through the samasa above.
summary: (i) One who dispels all forms of death.
(ii) Since all forms of death are mentioned, this includes untimely death and death due to old age. Untimely death may be due to accident or fatal disease; She prevents these.

(iii) Anyone born in this world must face death. Depending on actions during life, future births are decided. The fruits of actions are also called Mrityus. She saves one from these Mrityus by making one realise the Self and reach the nectar stage. The Srutis also advise the same: knowing Him, one breaks the noose of death.
searchText: 552 सर्वमृत्युनिवारिणी • सर्वमृत्यून् निवारयतीति। • सर्वमृत्यून् — all deaths. • निवारयतीति — wards off. • सर्वमृत्युनिवारिणी — the complete name, understood through the samasa above. (i) one who dispels all forms of death. (ii) since all forms of death are mentioned, this includes untimely death and death due to old age. untimely death may be due to accident or fatal disease; she prevents these. (iii) anyone born in this world must face death. depending on actions during life, future births are decided. the fruits of actions are also called mrityus. she saves one from these mrityus by making one realise the self and reach the nectar stage. the srutis also advise the same: knowing him, one breaks the noose of death. bhāskararāya decodes the prevention of untimely death: the shield against mortality: death can occur prematurely (apamṛtyu) due to accidents, planetary shifts, or curses before the full life-term is reached. she acts as the ultimate protective shield, blocking these accidental vectors. in the highest sense, she grants the deathless state of immortality by dissolving the ego before the body falls.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya decodes the prevention of untimely death:

The Shield Against Mortality: Death can occur prematurely (apamṛtyu) due to accidents, planetary shifts, or curses before the full life-term is reached. She acts as the ultimate protective shield, blocking these accidental vectors. In the highest sense, She grants the deathless state of immortality by dissolving the ego before the body falls.

name: अग्रगण्या
samasa: • गणं लब्ध्री गण्या। अग्रे च सा गण्या च।
wordByWord: • अग्रगण्या — the complete name, understood through the samasa above.
summary: (i) One who deserves to be counted first.
(ii) She is foremost among all, and therefore worthy of being placed at the head of any enumeration.
searchText: 553 अग्रगण्या • अग्रे गणयितुम् अर्हा। • गणं लब्ध्री गण्या। अग्रे च सा गण्या च। • अग्रे — first, foremost. • गणयितुम् — to be counted. • अर्हा — worthy. • गणं — group, category. • लब्ध्री — attainer. • गण्या — to be counted. • च — and. • सा — she. • अग्रगण्या — the complete name, understood through the samasa above. (i) one who deserves to be counted first. (ii) she is foremost among all, and therefore worthy of being placed at the head of any enumeration. in the saubhāgyabhāskara, bhāskararāya establishes her premier position across all evaluation metrics: the first principle: when scholars, sages, or scriptures sit down to list the foundational causes, supreme forces, or objects of worship in the cosmos, she must be counted at position number one (agra). she stands before all dependencies, anchoring the rest of the list.
esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara, Bhāskararāya establishes Her premier position across all evaluation metrics:

The First Principle: When scholars, sages, or scriptures sit down to list the foundational causes, supreme forces, or objects of worship in the cosmos, She must be counted at position number one (Agra). She stands before all dependencies, anchoring the rest of the list.

name: अचिन्त्यरूपा
wordByWord: • अचिन्त्यरूपा — the complete name, understood through the samasa above.
summary: (i) One whose form is beyond thought.
(ii) Her nature cannot be grasped by ordinary mental conception.
searchText: 554 अचिन्त्यरूपा • चिन्तयितुम् अशक्यं रूपं यस्याः सा। • चिन्तयितुम् — to think. • अशक्यं — impossible. • रूपं — form. • यस्याः — whose. • सा — she. • अचिन्त्यरूपा — the complete name, understood through the samasa above. (i) one whose form is beyond thought. (ii) her nature cannot be grasped by ordinary mental conception. bhāskararāya clarifies the limits of the ordinary rational intellect: beyond conceptual computing: the human mind can only think about things that are bound by time, space, and attributes. because her essential form is the infinite, non-dual, attribute-less brahman, the ordinary brain cannot frame her inside standard concepts. she must be realized through direct absorption, not thought out.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya clarifies the limits of the ordinary rational intellect:

Beyond Conceptual Computing: The human mind can only think about things that are bound by time, space, and attributes. Because Her essential form is the infinite, non-dual, attribute-less Brahman, the ordinary brain cannot frame Her inside standard concepts. She must be realized through direct absorption, not thought out.

name: कलिकल्मषनाशिनी
wordByWord: • कलिकल्मषनाशिनी — the complete name, understood through the samasa above.
summary: (i) One who destroys the sins and impurities of the Kali age.
searchText: 555 कलिकल्मषनाशिनी • कलियुगे कल्मषानां नाशिनी। • कलियुगे — in the kali age. • कल्मषानां — of impurities/sins. • नाशिनी — destroyer. • कलिकल्मषनाशिनी — the complete name, understood through the samasa above. (i) one who destroys the sins and impurities of the kali age. bhāskararāya details the modern corrective shield: the antidote to the dark age: the kali yuga is characterized by intense mental distraction, decline in moral rectitude (dharma), and heavy psychological impurities (kalmaṣa). as kalikalmaṣanāśinī, her specialized energetic layout acts as a high-velocity filter, clearing these dark age distortions from the mind of any seeker who calls upon her name.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya details the modern corrective shield:

The Antidote to the Dark Age: The Kali Yuga is characterized by intense mental distraction, decline in moral rectitude (Dharma), and heavy psychological impurities (kalmaṣa). As Kalikalmaṣanāśinī, Her specialized energetic layout acts as a high-velocity filter, clearing these dark age distortions from the mind of any seeker who calls upon Her name.

name: कात्यायनी
wordByWord: • कतो — from the sage named Kata/Katyayana.
• कात्यायनी — the complete name, understood through the samasa above.
summary: (i) One who is the daughter of the sage Katyayana.

(ii) The Vamana Purana says that this is the name of the Deity formed from the collective brightness of all the Devas, comparable to the account in Saptashati where the brightness of the Devas combines to form Mahalakshmi, the destroyer of Mahishasura.
(iii) According to Kalika Purana, She is Katyayani in Odyana Peetha, Kamakhya in Kamarupa, Purneshwari in Purnagiri, and Chandi in Jalandhara Peetha.
(iv) According to Devi Purana, She supports and pervades the whole world, and therefore receives the name Katyayani.
(v) The Gopika ladies worshipped Katyayani during Margasirsha month and obtained Krishna as their husband. In the Kavacha of Saptashati, Katyayani is also the sixth of the nine names of Durga.
searchText: 556 कात्यायनी • कतो नामर्षिः तस्यापत्यं स्त्री। • कतो — from the sage named kata/katyayana. • नामर्षिः — the sage so named. • तस्यापत्यं — his descendant/offspring. • स्त्री — female. • कात्यायनी — the complete name, understood through the samasa above. (i) one who is the daughter of the sage katyayana. (ii) the vamana purana says that this is the name of the deity formed from the collective brightness of all the devas, comparable to the account in saptashati where the brightness of the devas combines to form mahalakshmi, the destroyer of mahishasura. (iii) according to kalika purana, she is katyayani in odyana peetha, kamakhya in kamarupa, purneshwari in purnagiri, and chandi in jalandhara peetha. (iv) according to devi purana, she supports and pervades the whole world, and therefore receives the name katyayani. (v) the gopika ladies worshipped katyayani during margasirsha month and obtained krishna as their husband. in the kavacha of saptashati, katyayani is also the sixth of the nine names of durga. bhāskararāya unpacks her alignment within historical and ritual revelation: the unified mass of light: mythologically, when the tyrannical demonic forces could not be checked by individual gods, the collective angers and splendors of the entire trinity and deities fused together at the hermitage of sage kātyāyana. she emerged out of this unified mountain of blazing light as the ultimate defensive warrior goddess, demonstrating her status as the collective kinetic matrix of the cosmos.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya unpacks Her alignment within historical and ritual revelation:

The Unified Mass of Light: Mythologically, when the tyrannical demonic forces could not be checked by individual gods, the collective angers and splendors of the entire trinity and deities fused together at the hermitage of Sage Kātyāyana. She emerged out of this unified mountain of blazing light as the ultimate defensive warrior goddess, demonstrating Her status as the collective kinetic matrix of the cosmos.

name: कालहन्त्री
wordByWord: • कालहन्त्री — the complete name, understood through the samasa above.
summary: (i) One who is the destroyer of Time or Death.
(ii) Sruti, in the Svetasvatara Upanishad, says that Brahman is the Time of time and is all-knowing. She is therefore the destroyer even of Kala, the power of death.
searchText: 557 कालहन्त्री • कालस्य मृत्योर्हन्त्री। • कालस्य — of time/death. • कालहन्त्री — the complete name, understood through the samasa above. (i) one who is the destroyer of time or death. (ii) sruti, in the svetasvatara upanishad, says that brahman is the time of time and is all-knowing. she is therefore the destroyer even of kala, the power of death. in the saubhāgyabhāskara, bhāskararāya details her absolute supremacy over the governing dimension of mortality: the transcendent substratum: time (kāla) devours all created entities without exception. however, because she is the immutable eternal consciousness existing before the dawn of time, she ultimately devours time itself during the cosmic collapse (mahāpralaya). deliverance from mortality: for the realized practitioner, she acts as kālahantrī by slicing away the illusion of temporal change, rescuing the soul from the cyclical fear of death and anchoring it in deathless awareness.
esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara, Bhāskararāya details Her absolute supremacy over the governing dimension of mortality:

The Transcendent Substratum: Time (Kāla) devours all created entities without exception. However, because She is the immutable eternal consciousness existing before the dawn of time, She ultimately devours Time itself during the cosmic collapse (mahāpralaya).

Deliverance from Mortality: For the realized practitioner, She acts as Kālahantrī by slicing away the illusion of temporal change, rescuing the soul from the cyclical fear of death and anchoring it in deathless awareness.

name: कमलाक्षनिषेविता
wordByWord: • कमलाक्षनिषेविता — the complete name, understood through the samasa above.
summary: (i) One who is specially worshipped by the lotus-eyed Mahavishnu.
(ii) Padma Purana says that Vishnu always worships the sapphire-like Devi and thereby attains the state of Vishnu.

(iii) The story of Vishnu worshipping with lotuses and offering his own eye when one lotus was short may be remembered. Since Shiva and Shakti are not different, that worship may also be understood as worship of Sreedevee.
searchText: 558 कमलाक्षनिषेविता • कमलाक्षेण विष्णुना नितरां सेविता। • कमलाक्षेण — by the lotus-eyed one. • विष्णुना — by vishnu. • सेविता — worshipped. • कमलाक्षनिषेविता — the complete name, understood through the samasa above. (i) one who is specially worshipped by the lotus-eyed mahavishnu. (ii) padma purana says that vishnu always worships the sapphire-like devi and thereby attains the state of vishnu. (iii) the story of vishnu worshipping with lotuses and offering his own eye when one lotus was short may be remembered. since shiva and shakti are not different, that worship may also be understood as worship of sreedevee. bhāskararāya highlights her supreme position relative to the maintenance phase of the cosmos: the source of pervasive power: viṣṇu (kamalākṣa) is the all-pervading protector of the worlds. bhāskararāya notes that viṣṇu can execute his preservation duties strictly because he meditates upon and draws kinetic energy from her. his continuous adoration at her feet ensures the structural stability of cosmic dharma.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya highlights Her supreme position relative to the maintenance phase of the cosmos:

The Source of Pervasive Power: Viṣṇu (Kamalākṣa) is the all-pervading protector of the worlds. Bhāskararāya notes that Viṣṇu can execute his preservation duties strictly because he meditates upon and draws kinetic energy from Her. His continuous adoration at Her feet ensures the structural stability of cosmic Dharma.

name: ताम्बूलपूरितमुखी
wordByWord: • ताम्बूलपूरितमुखी — the complete name, understood through the samasa above.
summary: (i) One whose mouth is filled with betel leaves.
(ii) This is connected with the earlier description of the fragrance of Her tambula. The aroma from Her mouth is treated as a mark of royal enjoyment and auspicious beauty.
(iii) Legendary poets such as Kalidasa and others are said to have gained poetic excellence by receiving the juice of Sreedevee's tambula.
searchText: 559 ताम्बूलपूरितमुखी • ताम्बूलेन पूरितं मुखं यस्याः सा। • ताम्बूलेन — with betel leaf. • पूरितं — filled. • मुखं — mouth/face. • यस्याः — whose. • सा — she. • ताम्बूलपूरितमुखी — the complete name, understood through the samasa above. (i) one whose mouth is filled with betel leaves. (ii) this is connected with the earlier description of the fragrance of her tambula. the aroma from her mouth is treated as a mark of royal enjoyment and auspicious beauty. (iii) legendary poets such as kalidasa and others are said to have gained poetic excellence by receiving the juice of sreedevee's tambula. bhāskararāya strips this aesthetic description of any mundane parameters to reveal the śrīvidyā ritual indicators: the red flush of cosmic activity: the chewing of tāmbūla colors the mouth bright red and generates a rich fragrance. in tantric symbolism, the red color represents rāga—not mundane desire, but the intense, compassionate impetus to project and protect the universe. the speech matrix: the mouth filled with betel ingredients represents the unceasing, fragrant generation of sacred speech (vāk) and the containment of mantric seeds ready to be dispensed to seers.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya strips this aesthetic description of any mundane parameters to reveal the Śrīvidyā ritual indicators:

The Red Flush of Cosmic Activity: The chewing of Tāmbūla colors the mouth bright red and generates a rich fragrance. In Tantric symbolism, the red color represents Rāga—not mundane desire, but the intense, compassionate impetus to project and protect the universe.
The Speech Matrix: The mouth filled with betel ingredients represents the unceasing, fragrant generation of sacred speech (Vāk) and the containment of mantric seeds ready to be dispensed to seers.

name: दाडिमीकुसुमप्रभा
wordByWord: • दाडिमीकुसुमप्रभा — the complete name, understood through the samasa above.
summary: (i) One whose lustre is like the hue of a pomegranate flower.
(ii) There are varieties of pomegranate trees; the kind referred to here is known for its brilliant red flowers. Her complexion is compared to that vivid red colour.
searchText: 560 दाडिमीकुसुमप्रभा • दाडिमीवृक्षस्य कुसुमस्येव प्रभा यस्याः सा। • दाडिमीवृक्षस्य — of the pomegranate tree. • कुसुमस्येव — like the flower. • प्रभा — lustre. • यस्याः — whose. • सा — she. • दाडिमीकुसुमप्रभा — the complete name, understood through the samasa above. (i) one whose lustre is like the hue of a pomegranate flower. (ii) there are varieties of pomegranate trees; the kind referred to here is known for its brilliant red flowers. her complexion is compared to that vivid red colour. in the saubhāgyabhāskara, bhāskararāya maps this color frequency within visual concentration: the light of pure will: the pomegranate blossom exhibits a unique, intensely bright crimson-red color that stands out sharply in nature. this visual profile represents the pure, concentrated vibration of इच्छाशक्ति (icchā-śakti — divine will). meditating on this vibrant crimson light cleanses the practitioner's psychic channels, dispelling intellectual grayness.
esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara, Bhāskararāya maps this color frequency within visual concentration:

The Light of Pure Will: The pomegranate blossom exhibits a unique, intensely bright crimson-red color that stands out sharply in nature. This visual profile represents the pure, concentrated vibration of इच्छाशक्ति (Icchā-śakti — divine will). Meditating on this vibrant crimson light cleanses the practitioner's psychic channels, dispelling intellectual grayness.

name: मृगाक्षी
samasa: • मृगस्य अक्षिणी इव अक्षिणी यस्याः सा।
wordByWord: • मृगाक्षी — the complete name, understood through the samasa above.
summary: (i) One who has eyes like those of a deer.

(ii) The eyes of deer are not fixed in one place but move on all sides. Such wavering eyes add beauty. Sreedevee's eyes move in this manner so that Her compassionate glance may fall upon all devotees on every side.
(iii) The 103rd name of Trishati, Harinekshana, may be compared.
