searchText: 561 मृगाक्षी • मृगस्य अक्षिणी इव अक्षिणी यस्याः सा। • मृगस्य — of a deer. • अक्षिणी — eyes. • इव — as if. • यस्याः — whose. • सा — she. • मृगाक्षी — the complete name, understood through the samasa above. (i) one who has eyes like those of a deer. (ii) the eyes of deer are not fixed in one place but move on all sides. such wavering eyes add beauty. sreedevee's eyes move in this manner so that her compassionate glance may fall upon all devotees on every side. (iii) the 103rd name of trishati, harinekshana, may be compared. bhāskararāya details the quality of her gaze toward creation: the gaze of gentle compassion: a deer's eyes are exceptionally wide, dark, liquid, and gentle, entirely devoid of predatory intent. her glance (īkṣaṇa) toward bound souls reflects this exact spontaneous tenderness. concurrently, in subtle anatomy, it marks her constant surveillance over the restless, deer-like movements of the individual mind (manas), working to soothe it into stillness.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya details the quality of Her gaze toward creation:

The Gaze of Gentle Compassion: A deer's eyes are exceptionally wide, dark, liquid, and gentle, entirely devoid of predatory intent. Her glance (īkṣaṇa) toward bound souls reflects this exact spontaneous tenderness. Concurrently, in subtle anatomy, it marks Her constant surveillance over the restless, deer-like movements of the individual mind (Manas), working to soothe it into stillness.

name: मोहिनी
wordByWord: • मोहिनी — the complete name, understood through the samasa above.
summary: (i) One who enchants or bewitches.
(ii) Laghu Naradiya Purana says that She deludes the entire universe through Her beauty, and therefore She is called Mohini.
(iii) Vishnu is said to have assumed the Mohini form by meditating upon Sreedevee. Soundaryalahari also indicates that even great sages are enchanted through Her grace.
(iv) Mohini is also connected with certain sacred reckonings of time and with the presiding deity of the temple at Nivasapura on the banks of the river Pravara.
searchText: 562 मोहिनी • मोहयतीति। • मोहयतीति — enchants, deludes. • मोहिनी — the complete name, understood through the samasa above. (i) one who enchants or bewitches. (ii) laghu naradiya purana says that she deludes the entire universe through her beauty, and therefore she is called mohini. (iii) vishnu is said to have assumed the mohini form by meditating upon sreedevee. soundaryalahari also indicates that even great sages are enchanted through her grace. (iv) mohini is also connected with certain sacred reckonings of time and with the presiding deity of the temple at nivasapura on the banks of the river pravara. bhāskararāya explains a key functional aspect of her cosmic veiling power: the grand bewilderment: as mohinī, she is the mysterious power that throws the blanket of relative difference over consciousness, making the non-dual absolute appear as a fragmented world of objects. this enchantment keeps the cosmic game (līlā) moving. the saver of gods: mythologically, she is the enchanting form assumed by viṣṇu (who is identical with her energy) to delude the chaotic forces and protect the nectar of immortality.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya explains a key functional aspect of Her cosmic veiling power:

The Grand Bewilderment: As Mohinī, She is the mysterious power that throws the blanket of relative difference over consciousness, making the non-dual absolute appear as a fragmented world of objects. This enchantment keeps the cosmic game (Līlā) moving.
The Saver of Gods: Mythologically, She is the enchanting form assumed by Viṣṇu (who is identical with Her energy) to delude the chaotic forces and protect the nectar of immortality.

name: मुख्या
wordByWord: • मुखे — at the beginning / in the face.
• मुख्या — the complete name, understood through the samasa above.
summary: (i) One who is the first or principal.
(ii) When a child is born, the face is the first part to appear; therefore what is first is called mukhya.
(iii) She is first among all that has originated and foremost among all deities. Taittiriya Upanishad says, 'I am the first-born of truth.'
searchText: 563 मुख्या • मुखे सर्वादौ भवा। • मुखे — at the beginning / in the face. • सर्वादौ — first of all. • भवा — being, existing. • मुख्या — the complete name, understood through the samasa above. (i) one who is the first or principal. (ii) when a child is born, the face is the first part to appear; therefore what is first is called mukhya. (iii) she is first among all that has originated and foremost among all deities. taittiriya upanishad says, 'i am the first-born of truth.' in the saubhāgyabhāskara, bhāskararāya anchors her priority across evolutionary logic: the primal cause: she is mukhyā because she stands at the absolute head of the causal chain (prāthamikī). before a single element, word, or dimension is projected, she exists as the primary, uncaused foundation. she is the chief animating principle governing all subsequent secondary forces.
esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara, Bhāskararāya anchors Her priority across evolutionary logic:

The Primal Cause: She is Mukhyā because She stands at the absolute head of the causal chain (Prāthamikī). Before a single element, word, or dimension is projected, She exists as the primary, uncaused foundation. She is the chief animating principle governing all subsequent secondary forces.

name: मृडानी
wordByWord: • मृडानी — the complete name, understood through the samasa above.
summary: (i) One who is the consort of Mrida, Paramashiva.
(ii) The word Mrida, as a verbal root, indicates giving happiness. Since Paramashiva gives happiness to all, He is called Mrida, and She is His consort, Mridani.
(iii) Mridani is also indicated in Mahashodanyasa in the form of Paksha.
searchText: 564 मृडानी • मृडस्य परमशिवस्य पत्नी। • मृडस्य — of mrida, shiva. • परमशिवस्य — of paramashiva. • पत्नी — wife, consort. • मृडानी — the complete name, understood through the samasa above. (i) one who is the consort of mrida, paramashiva. (ii) the word mrida, as a verbal root, indicates giving happiness. since paramashiva gives happiness to all, he is called mrida, and she is his consort, mridani. (iii) mridani is also indicated in mahashodanyasa in the form of paksha. bhāskararāya explores the etymological root of this title: the power of pure mercy: मृड (mṛḍa) signifies the one who melts completely with compassion, pardoning errors and granting joy. she is the dynamic, non-dual power (śakti) that expresses this mercy. through her form as mṛḍānī, she absorbs the devotee's historical errors and realigns their life with divine order.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya explores the etymological root of this title:

The Power of Pure Mercy: मृड (Mṛḍa) signifies the one who melts completely with compassion, pardoning errors and granting joy. She is the dynamic, non-dual power (Śakti) that expresses this mercy. Through Her form as Mṛḍānī, She absorbs the devotee's historical errors and realigns their life with divine order.

name: मित्ररूपिणी
wordByWord: • मित्ररूपिणी — the complete name, understood through the samasa above.
summary: (i) One who is in the form of the Sun or in the form of a friend.
(ii) Mitra is one of the twelve names of the Sun. She is therefore the illuminating and sustaining principle.
(iii) In Sree Vidya Tantra, fire indicates Shiva, Moon indicates Sreedevee, and Sun indicates the unified form of both. Mitra also means friend; thus She is the friendly Self.
searchText: 565 मित्ररूपिणी • मित्राणां सूर्याणामिव रूपमस्याः अस्तीति। • मित्राणां — of mitras / suns / friends. • सूर्याणामिव — like the suns. • रूपमस्याः — her form. • अस्तीति — is. • मित्ररूपिणी — the complete name, understood through the samasa above. (i) one who is in the form of the sun or in the form of a friend. (ii) mitra is one of the twelve names of the sun. she is therefore the illuminating and sustaining principle. (iii) in sree vidya tantra, fire indicates shiva, moon indicates sreedevee, and sun indicates the unified form of both. mitra also means friend; thus she is the friendly self. bhāskararāya decodes this name across structural solar frameworks and moral attributes: the luminous solar matrix: मित्र (mitra) is a primary vedic name for the sun. she is identical with the twelve solar deities (dvādaśa-āditya) that radiate life-giving light and energy across the planetary grid. the universal friend: on a spiritual path, she functions as the ultimate, non-judgmental friend (mitra) who stays close to the soul, guiding it out of internal darkness.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya decodes this name across structural solar frameworks and moral attributes:

The Luminous Solar Matrix: मित्र (Mitra) is a primary Vedic name for the Sun. She is identical with the twelve solar deities (Dvādaśa-āditya) that radiate life-giving light and energy across the planetary grid.
The Universal Friend: On a spiritual path, She functions as the ultimate, non-judgmental friend (Mitra) who stays close to the soul, guiding it out of internal darkness.

name: नित्यतृप्ता
wordByWord: • स्वरूपानन्देन — by the bliss of her own nature.
• नित्यतृप्ता — the complete name, understood through the samasa above.
summary: (i) One who is eternally content.
(ii) She is complete in every respect and needs nothing. Therefore She is always satisfied.
(iii) She is contented by the eternal bliss of Her own true nature.
searchText: 566 नित्यतृप्ता • नित्यं तृप्ता। • नित्येन स्वरूपानन्देन तृप्ता। • नित्यं — always, eternally. • तृप्ता — satisfied. • नित्येन — by the eternal. • स्वरूपानन्देन — by the bliss of her own nature. • नित्यतृप्ता — the complete name, understood through the samasa above. (i) one who is eternally content. (ii) she is complete in every respect and needs nothing. therefore she is always satisfied. (iii) she is contented by the eternal bliss of her own true nature. in the saubhāgyabhāskara, bhāskararāya defines her absolute freedom from desire: satiation within the self: human satisfaction is brief and dependent on acquiring external objects. she is nityatṛptā because she experiences absolute fulfillment exclusively through her own intrinsic self-bliss (svarūpānanda). lacking nothing and containing everything, she remains completely full, untouched by the hunger of lack.
esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara, Bhāskararāya defines Her absolute freedom from desire:

Satiation within the Self: Human satisfaction is brief and dependent on acquiring external objects. She is Nityatṛptā because She experiences absolute fulfillment exclusively through Her own intrinsic self-bliss (Svarūpānanda). Lacking nothing and containing everything, She remains completely full, untouched by the hunger of lack.

name: भक्तनिधिः
wordByWord: • भक्तनिधिः — the complete name, understood through the samasa above.
summary: (i) One who is a treasure for devotees.
(ii) She grants whatever is asked by devotees. The name Vanchitartha Pradayini may be compared.
(iii) Soundaryalahari says that She gives even more than what the devotee asks.
searchText: 567 भक्तनिधिः • भक्तानां निधिः। • भक्तानां — of devotees. • निधिः — treasure. • भक्तनिधिः — the complete name, understood through the samasa above. (i) one who is a treasure for devotees. (ii) she grants whatever is asked by devotees. the name vanchitartha pradayini may be compared. (iii) soundaryalahari says that she gives even more than what the devotee asks. bhāskararāya details the immense spiritual wealth she represents: the ultimate storehouse: a nidhi is an inexhaustible, hidden treasure house that permanently eliminates poverty once uncovered. for those who possess real devotion (bhakti), she functions as this absolute treasure. finding her terminates all spiritual and mental poverty, granting immediate access to the riches of liberation.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya details the immense spiritual wealth She represents:

The Ultimate Storehouse: A Nidhi is an inexhaustible, hidden treasure house that permanently eliminates poverty once uncovered. For those who possess real devotion (bhakti), She functions as this absolute treasure. Finding Her terminates all spiritual and mental poverty, granting immediate access to the riches of liberation.

name: नियन्त्री
wordByWord: • नियन्त्री — the complete name, understood through the samasa above.
summary: (i) One who guides and controls the universe.
(ii) Because She governs and directs the whole universe, She is called Niyantri.
searchText: 568 नियन्त्री • जगन्नियामक्त्वात् नियन्त्री। • नियन्त्री — controller, governor. • नियन्त्री — the complete name, understood through the samasa above. (i) one who guides and controls the universe. (ii) because she governs and directs the whole universe, she is called niyantri. bhāskararāya details her absolute legislative control over the cosmos: the cosmic director: she is जगन्नियामिका (jaganniyāmikā) — the supreme inner controller who dictates and maintains the laws of nature, physics, karma, and time. not a single planet rotates and not a single atom vibrates outside her structural coordination. she holds the entire universe in disciplined order.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya details Her absolute legislative control over the cosmos:

The Cosmic Director: She is जगन्नियामिका (Jaganniyāmikā) — the supreme inner controller who dictates and maintains the laws of nature, physics, karma, and time. Not a single planet rotates and not a single atom vibrates outside Her structural coordination. She holds the entire universe in disciplined order.

name: निखिलेश्वरी
wordByWord: • निखिलेश्वरी — the complete name, understood through the samasa above.
summary: (i) One who is the ruler of all.
(ii) Since She controls the universe, She is the sovereign ruler of everything.
searchText: 569 निखिलेश्वरी • निखिलस्य ईश्वरी। • निखिलस्य — of all. • ईश्वरी — ruler, sovereign. • निखिलेश्वरी — the complete name, understood through the samasa above. (i) one who is the ruler of all. (ii) since she controls the universe, she is the sovereign ruler of everything. in the saubhāgyabhāskara, this confirms her boundless scope: sovereignty without remainder: a king's rule ends at his border wall. her sovereignty (īśvaritva) is nikhila — it covers the entire multi-dimensional grid of space, matter, souls, and unmanifest potentials. there is absolutely no corner of reality that sits outside her imperial command.
esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara, this confirms Her boundless scope:

Sovereignty without Remainder: A king's rule ends at his border wall. Her sovereignty (Īśvaritva) is Nikhila — it covers the entire multi-dimensional grid of space, matter, souls, and unmanifest potentials. There is absolutely no corner of reality that sits outside Her imperial command.

name: मैत्र्यादिवासनालभ्या
wordByWord: • मैत्र्यादिभिः — by friendliness and the like.
• मैत्र्यादिवासनालभ्या — the complete name, understood through the samasa above.
summary: (i) One who is attained through dispositions such as friendliness.

(ii) Yoga texts teach that bad tendencies should be removed and good dispositions cultivated: friendship towards the happy, compassion towards the suffering, joy in the righteous, and indifference towards the sinful.
(iii) Srimad Bhagavatam and Yoga Sutra describe these four dispositions, namely maitri, karuna, mudita, and upeksha. They purify the mind and make Sreedevee easy to attain.
searchText: 570 मैत्र्यादिवासनालभ्या • मैत्र्यादिभिः वासनाभिः लभ्या। • मैत्र्यादिभिः — by friendliness and the like. • वासनाभिः — by dispositions/tendencies. • लभ्या — attainable. • मैत्र्यादिवासनालभ्या — the complete name, understood through the samasa above. (i) one who is attained through dispositions such as friendliness. (ii) yoga texts teach that bad tendencies should be removed and good dispositions cultivated: friendship towards the happy, compassion towards the suffering, joy in the righteous, and indifference towards the sinful. (iii) srimad bhagavatam and yoga sutra describe these four dispositions, namely maitri, karuna, mudita, and upeksha. they purify the mind and make sreedevee easy to attain. bhāskararāya links this directly to the foundational psychological purifications noted in the patañjali yoga sūtras (citta-prasādana): the ethical access path: the four pure attitudes are मैत्री (friendliness toward the happy), करुणा (compassion toward the suffering), मुदिता (joy toward the virtuous), and उपेक्षा (neutrality toward the vicious). bhāskararāya notes that she cannot be reached by minds hardened by jealousy or hatred. cultivating these clean mental impressions clears the internal environment, allowing her light to emerge.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya links this directly to the foundational psychological purifications noted in the Patañjali Yoga Sūtras (Citta-prasādana):

The Ethical Access Path: The four pure attitudes are मैत्री (friendliness toward the happy), करुणा (compassion toward the suffering), मुदिता (joy toward the virtuous), and उपेक्षा (neutrality toward the vicious). Bhāskararāya notes that She cannot be reached by minds hardened by jealousy or hatred. Cultivating these clean mental impressions clears the internal environment, allowing Her light to emerge.

name: महाप्रलयसाक्षिणी
wordByWord: • महाप्रलयसाक्षिणी — the complete name, understood through the samasa above.
summary: (i) One who is the witness of the great dissolution.
(ii) Soundaryalahari describes the dissolution of Brahma and others. Sreedevee alone remains as the witness.
(iii) A witness does not participate in the action and is not affected by its result. She is such a witness of the great pralaya.
(iv) The names Maheshvara Mahakalpa Mahatandava Sakshini and Sakshivargita may also be compared.
searchText: 571 महाप्रलयसाक्षिणी • महाप्रलये साक्षिणी। • महाप्रलये — in the great dissolution. • साक्षिणी — witness. • महाप्रलयसाक्षिणी — the complete name, understood through the samasa above. (i) one who is the witness of the great dissolution. (ii) soundaryalahari describes the dissolution of brahma and others. sreedevee alone remains as the witness. (iii) a witness does not participate in the action and is not affected by its result. she is such a witness of the great pralaya. (iv) the names maheshvara mahakalpa mahatandava sakshini and sakshivargita may also be compared. bhāskararāya details her position at the absolute end of the cosmic movie: the sole survivor: during the mahāpralaya, the five elements dissolve, space collapses, time terminates, and even the creator deities (brahmā, viṣṇu, rudra) vanish into unmanifest matter. she stands entirely alone in this absolute darkness, functioning as the silent, unblinking witness (sākṣiṇī), proving her status as the eternal, independent reality.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya details Her position at the absolute end of the cosmic movie:

The Sole Survivor: During the Mahāpralaya, the five elements dissolve, space collapses, time terminates, and even the creator deities (Brahmā, Viṣṇu, Rudra) vanish into unmanifest matter. She stands entirely alone in this absolute darkness, functioning as the silent, unblinking witness (Sākṣiṇī), proving Her status as the eternal, independent reality.

name: पराशक्तिः
wordByWord: • परायाः — of Para / of the supreme mantra.
• पराशक्तिः — the complete name, understood through the samasa above.
summary: (i) One who is the Supreme Power.
(ii) Agamic texts describe certain bodily elements as arising from Shakti and others from Shiva; the tenth principle is called Parashakti.
(iii) Parashakti also means the very great energy. The principle of Shakta doctrine is that Shiva attains manifest greatness only through union with Shakti.
(iv) Sruti says that His supreme Shakti is known in manifold forms. Linga Purana also says that whatever energy is found in any substance is Sreedevee.
searchText: 572 पराशक्तिः • परा शक्तिः। • परायाः (मन्त्रस्य शक्तिः। • परा — supreme. • शक्तिः — power, energy. • परायाः — of para / of the supreme mantra. • मन्त्रस्य — of the mantra. • पराशक्तिः — the complete name, understood through the samasa above. (i) one who is the supreme power. (ii) agamic texts describe certain bodily elements as arising from shakti and others from shiva; the tenth principle is called parashakti. (iii) parashakti also means the very great energy. the principle of shakta doctrine is that shiva attains manifest greatness only through union with shakti. (iv) sruti says that his supreme shakti is known in manifold forms. linga purana also says that whatever energy is found in any substance is sreedevee. in the saubhāgyabhāskara, bhāskararāya details the operational engine of the absolute: the transcendent energy: she is the parā-śakti celebrated across all agamic revelation — the supreme, primordial kinetic power that belongs to brahman. she is the capacity through which non-dual awareness expresses its will, projects universes, and reveals its own hidden beauty, serving as the master key of the entire śrīvidyā system.
esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara, Bhāskararāya details the operational engine of the absolute:

The Transcendent Energy: She is the Parā-śakti celebrated across all Agamic revelation — the supreme, primordial kinetic power that belongs to Brahman. She is the capacity through which non-dual awareness expresses its will, projects universes, and reveals its own hidden beauty, serving as the master key of the entire Śrīvidyā system.

name: परानिष्ठा
wordByWord: • परानिष्ठा — the complete name, understood through the samasa above.
summary: (i) One who is the final end of speech, action, and thought.
(ii) Bhagavad Gita says that all actions culminate in knowledge.

(iii) Suta Samhita describes the highest state as firm knowledge, based on scripture, teacher, reasoning, and experience, that the Self alone is pure non-dual Brahman. This is the highest culmination of scriptural teaching and direct experience.
searchText: 573 परानिष्ठा • परा निष्ठा। • परायां निष्ठा यस्याः सा। • परा — supreme. • निष्ठा — final state, establishment. • परायां — in the supreme. • यस्याः — whose. • सा — she. • परानिष्ठा — the complete name, understood through the samasa above. (i) one who is the final end of speech, action, and thought. (ii) bhagavad gita says that all actions culminate in knowledge. (iii) suta samhita describes the highest state as firm knowledge, based on scripture, teacher, reasoning, and experience, that the self alone is pure non-dual brahman. this is the highest culmination of scriptural teaching and direct experience. bhāskararāya defines the absolute ceiling of spiritual attainment: the ultimate absorption: niṣṭhā describes a state of unshakeable, permanent fixity where the mind has completely ceased its external wandering. as parāniṣṭhā, she represents that final, non-dual absorption where all relative states dissolve into pure brahman awareness, a state that she simultaneously guides the seeker toward.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya defines the absolute ceiling of spiritual attainment:

The Ultimate Absorption: Niṣṭhā describes a state of unshakeable, permanent fixity where the mind has completely ceased its external wandering. As Parāniṣṭhā, She represents that final, non-dual absorption where all relative states dissolve into pure Brahman awareness, a state that She simultaneously guides the seeker toward.

name: प्रज्ञानघनरूपिणी
wordByWord: • प्रज्ञानघनरूपिणी — the complete name, understood through the samasa above.
summary: (i) One who is of the form of pure, concentrated, supreme knowledge.
(ii) Pra means superior and beyond ordinary mental modification. Jnana is eternal wisdom. Ghana means compact, solid, and not contaminated by ignorance.
(iii) Brihadaranyaka Upanishad compares the Self to a lump of salt, full of taste throughout. She is the solidified form of supreme wisdom.
searchText: 574 प्रज्ञानघनरूपिणी • प्रकृष्टेन ज्ञानेन घनं रूपमस्याः इति। • प्रकृष्टेन — by supreme, excellent. • ज्ञानेन — knowledge. • घनं — compact, solid mass. • रूपमस्याः — her form. • प्रज्ञानघनरूपिणी — the complete name, understood through the samasa above. (i) one who is of the form of pure, concentrated, supreme knowledge. (ii) pra means superior and beyond ordinary mental modification. jnana is eternal wisdom. ghana means compact, solid, and not contaminated by ignorance. (iii) brihadaranyaka upanishad compares the self to a lump of salt, full of taste throughout. she is the solidified form of supreme wisdom. bhāskararāya anchors this directly in the profound declarations of the māṇḍūkya and bṛhadāraṇyaka upaniṣads: the homogenous mass: प्रज्ञानघन (prajñāna-ghana) describes a reality that contains no internal gaps, no differences, and no foreign elements—just like a solid block of salt has only one taste throughout. her entire body is made of nothing but pure, unadulterated, dense consciousness, completely free from the filters of objective thoughts.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya anchors this directly in the profound declarations of the Māṇḍūkya and Bṛhadāraṇyaka Upaniṣads:

The Homogenous Mass: प्रज्ञानघन (Prajñāna-ghana) describes a reality that contains no internal gaps, no differences, and no foreign elements—just like a solid block of salt has only one taste throughout. Her entire body is made of nothing but pure, unadulterated, dense consciousness, completely free from the filters of objective thoughts.

name: माध्वीपानालसा
wordByWord: • माध्वीपानालसा — the complete name, understood through the samasa above.
summary: (i) One who is languid after drinking madhvi.
(ii) Madhvi is an alcoholic drink mixed with grapes and honey. Many names describe Sreedevee as connected with divine drink.
(iii) In these contexts madhu or liquor indicates supreme bliss. Therefore this name means that She is immersed in the bliss of the Supreme Self.
searchText: 575 माध्वीपानालसा • माध्व्या पानेनालसा। • माध्व्या — by madhvi drink. • माध्वीपानालसा — the complete name, understood through the samasa above. (i) one who is languid after drinking madhvi. (ii) madhvi is an alcoholic drink mixed with grapes and honey. many names describe sreedevee as connected with divine drink. (iii) in these contexts madhu or liquor indicates supreme bliss. therefore this name means that she is immersed in the bliss of the supreme self. bhāskararāya purges this name of any literal, crude intoxication perspectives: the ecstasy of self-absorption: माध्वी (mādhwī) refers to the supreme nectar of immortality (amṛta) that downpours from the sahasrāra crown center during high-level internal union. drinking this celestial essence causes her active, projective energy to become wonderfully relaxed and languid (alasā), drawing the practitioner's entire internal system into deep, ecstatic samādhi.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya purges this name of any literal, crude intoxication perspectives:

The Ecstasy of Self-Absorption: माध्वी (mādhwī) refers to the supreme nectar of immortality (amṛta) that downpours from the Sahasrāra crown center during high-level internal union. Drinking this celestial essence causes Her active, projective energy to become wonderfully relaxed and languid (alasā), drawing the practitioner's entire internal system into deep, ecstatic Samādhi.

name: मत्ता
wordByWord: • मत्ता — the complete name, understood through the samasa above.
summary: (i) One who appears intoxicated or unconscious.
(ii) As in the previous name, this indicates the state caused by divine drink, understood as supreme bliss.
(iii) Mat also indicates the Self and the sense of 'I'. Soundaryalahari describes Her as the ego-form of Paramashiva.
searchText: 576 मत्ता • मत्ता इव। • मद्भावः (अहम्भावः)। • मत्ता — intoxicated. • इव — as if. • मद्भावः — state of i-ness / self-sense. • अहम्भावः — ego-sense. • मत्ता — the complete name, understood through the samasa above. (i) one who appears intoxicated or unconscious. (ii) as in the previous name, this indicates the state caused by divine drink, understood as supreme bliss. (iii) mat also indicates the self and the sense of 'i'. soundaryalahari describes her as the ego-form of paramashiva. in the saubhāgyabhāskara, bhāskararāya decodes this state of absolute divine pride: the pride of being: she is mattā because she is completely filled with the ecstasy of her own infinite existence. bhāskararāya also highlights an advanced grammatical extraction: मद्-भाव (mad-bhāva) represents the absolute, uncorrupted cosmic ego—the pure, non-dual sense of "अहम्" ("i am") that characterizes the supreme absolute before it splits into objects.
esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara, Bhāskararāya decodes this state of absolute divine pride:

The Pride of Being: She is Mattā because She is completely filled with the ecstasy of Her own infinite existence. Bhāskararāya also highlights an advanced grammatical extraction: मद्-भाव (Mad-bhāva) represents the absolute, uncorrupted cosmic ego—the pure, non-dual sense of "अहम्" ("I am") that characterizes the supreme absolute before it splits into objects.

name: मातृकावर्णरूपिणी
samasa: • मातृका (अकारादिक्षकारान्ताः)। तासां वर्णाः। तान्येव रूपं स्वरूपमस्याः इति।
• मातृकावर्णा एव रूपा निरूपका वाचका यस्याः इति।
wordByWord: • मातृकावर्णरूपिणी — the complete name, understood through the samasa above.
summary: (i) One who is in the form of all Sanskrit letters.
(ii) She is in the form of the fifty-one letters. Since letters give form and colour to words, they are called varnas.
(iii) Tantric texts assign colours to the letters in the chakras and identify Her with the Shakti that gives individuality to letters.
(iv) She is also the garland of letters, the sound-Brahman, and the source of the matrika letters. Some traditions connect this name with the Kailasa Prastara form of Sreechakra.
searchText: 577 मातृकावर्णरूपिणी • मातृका (अकारादिक्षकारान्ताः)। तासां वर्णाः। तान्येव रूपं स्वरूपमस्याः इति। • मातृकावर्णा (एकपञ्चाशत्) एव रूपमस्या इति। • मातृकावर्णा एव रूपा निरूपका वाचका यस्याः इति। • मातृकावर्णान् रूपयति (जनयति) इति। • मातृका — letters of the alphabet. • अकारादिक्षकारान्ताः — from a to ksha. • तासां — of them. • वर्णाः — letters/colours. • तान्येव — those very. • स्वरूपमस्याः — her own form. • एव — itself. • यस्याः — whose. • रूपयति — forms, manifests. • जनयति — generates. • मातृकावर्णरूपिणी — the complete name, understood through the samasa above. (i) one who is in the form of all sanskrit letters. (ii) she is in the form of the fifty-one letters. since letters give form and colour to words, they are called varnas. (iii) tantric texts assign colours to the letters in the chakras and identify her with the shakti that gives individuality to letters. (iv) she is also the garland of letters, the sound-brahman, and the source of the matrika letters. some traditions connect this name with the kailasa prastara form of sreechakra. bhāskararāya details the foundational phonic framework of the cosmos: the phonic matrix of reality: मातृका (mātṛkā) represents the fifty-one sacred syllables of the sanskrit alphabet running from अ to क्ष. these letters are not human tools; they are the "little mothers" — the foundational acoustic blueprints out of which every object, name, and element in the universe is woven. she is this structural phonic grid, acting as the soul of all mantras.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya details the foundational phonic framework of the cosmos:

The Phonic Matrix of Reality: मातृका (Mātṛkā) represents the fifty-one sacred syllables of the Sanskrit alphabet running from अ to क्ष. These letters are not human tools; they are the "little mothers" — the foundational acoustic blueprints out of which every object, name, and element in the universe is woven. She is this structural phonic grid, acting as the soul of all mantras.

name: महाकैलासनिलया
wordByWord: • महाकैलासनिलया — the complete name, understood through the samasa above.
summary: (i) One who resides in the great Kailasa.
(ii) Puranas describe Mahakailasa as beyond the ordinary Kailasa, the abode of Paramashiva.
(iii) The word Maha is used by the Vagdevis to indicate very great ideas, things, and deities.
(iv) Kailasa is also interpreted as the Sahasrara in the Brahmarandhra, where Shiva in the form of bindu resides.
searchText: 578 महाकैलासनिलया • महाकैलास एव निलयो यस्याः सा। • महाकैलास — great kailasa. • एव — itself. • यस्याः — whose. • सा — she. • महाकैलासनिलया — the complete name, understood through the samasa above. (i) one who resides in the great kailasa. (ii) puranas describe mahakailasa as beyond the ordinary kailasa, the abode of paramashiva. (iii) the word maha is used by the vagdevis to indicate very great ideas, things, and deities. (iv) kailasa is also interpreted as the sahasrara in the brahmarandhra, where shiva in the form of bindu resides. bhāskararāya elevates this past the physical mountain range to its highest cosmic coordinate: the peak of non-dual union: while the earthly kailāsa is śiva's mountain, महाकैलास represents the supreme, unmanifested transcendental space where shiva and shakti exist in absolute, unbroken non-dual embrace. it corresponds internally to the luminous white space within the sahasrāra crown center.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya elevates this past the physical mountain range to its highest cosmic coordinate:

The Peak of Non-Dual Union: While the earthly Kailāsa is Śiva's mountain, महाकैलास represents the supreme, unmanifested transcendental space where Shiva and Shakti exist in absolute, unbroken non-dual embrace. It corresponds internally to the luminous white space within the Sahasrāra crown center.

name: मृणालमृदुदोर्लता
wordByWord: • मृणालमृदुदोर्लता — the complete name, understood through the samasa above.
summary: (i) One whose arms are soft and slender like lotus stalks.
(ii) The earlier names Bisatantu Taniyasi and Shatodari may be recalled, where Her subtle fineness and slenderness were similarly praised.
searchText: 579 मृणालमृदुदोर्लता • मृणालमिव (बिसतन्तुः इव) मृद्व्यो दोर्लता यस्याः। • मृणालमिव — like a lotus fibre/stalk. • बिसतन्तुः — lotus fibre. • इव — as if. • दोर्लता — creeper-like arms. • यस्याः — whose. • मृणालमृदुदोर्लता — the complete name, understood through the samasa above. (i) one whose arms are soft and slender like lotus stalks. (ii) the earlier names bisatantu taniyasi and shatodari may be recalled, where her subtle fineness and slenderness were similarly praised. in the saubhāgyabhāskara, bhāskararāya decodes the structural symbolism of her limbs: the soft grip of grace: her arms hold the cosmic weapons (the bow, arrows, noose, and goad). though these tools govern creation and alignment, her limbs are described as soft as a lotus fiber (mṛṇāla), reminding the devotee that her execution of cosmic law and correction is driven entirely by gentle maternal grace.
esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara, Bhāskararāya decodes the structural symbolism of Her limbs:

The Soft Grip of Grace: Her arms hold the cosmic weapons (the bow, arrows, noose, and goad). Though these tools govern creation and alignment, Her limbs are described as soft as a lotus fiber (mṛṇāla), reminding the devotee that Her execution of cosmic law and correction is driven entirely by gentle maternal grace.

name: महनीया
wordByWord: • पूज्यत्वात् — because of being worthy of worship.
• महनीया — the complete name, understood through the samasa above.
summary: (i) One who is worthy of worship by all.
(ii) The name Mahapujya may be compared. Vishnu Sahasranama names such as Stavya and Arha similarly indicate one who deserves worship and honour.
searchText: 580 महनीया • पूज्यत्वात् महनीया। • पूज्यत्वात् — because of being worthy of worship. • महनीया — venerable. • महनीया — the complete name, understood through the samasa above. (i) one who is worthy of worship by all. (ii) the name mahapujya may be compared. vishnu sahasranama names such as stavya and arha similarly indicate one who deserves worship and honour. bhāskararāya notes the natural response of the intellect to her presence: the object of supreme reverence: she is mahanīyā because her very presence commands absolute, instinctive reverence from every tier of intelligence in the cosmos. by meditating upon her adorable status, the practitioner's mind is cleared of superficiality, instilling deep spiritual humility.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya notes the natural response of the intellect to Her presence:

The Object of Supreme Reverence: She is Mahanīyā because Her very presence commands absolute, instinctive reverence from every tier of intelligence in the cosmos. By meditating upon Her adorable status, the practitioner's mind is cleared of superficiality, instilling deep spiritual humility.

name: दयामूर्तिः
wordByWord: • दयामूर्तिः — the complete name, understood through the samasa above.
summary: (i) One who is the personification of mercy and compassion.
(ii) Earlier names such as Karunarasasagara, Sandrakaruna, and Avyajakarunamurti convey the same sense.
(iii) The dhyana verse of this Sahasranama calls Her Karunatarangitakshi, one whose eyes move with waves of compassion.
(iv) Traditional hymns such as Daya Shatakam and Kshamashodashi describe compassion as the force that protects even those whose faults would otherwise disqualify them.
searchText: 581 दयामूर्तिः • दयैव मूर्तिः स्वरूपं यस्याः सा। • दयैव — compassion itself. • मूर्तिः — form, embodiment. • स्वरूपं — nature, form. • यस्याः — whose. • सा — she. • दयामूर्तिः — the complete name, understood through the samasa above. (i) one who is the personification of mercy and compassion. (ii) earlier names such as karunarasasagara, sandrakaruna, and avyajakarunamurti convey the same sense. (iii) the dhyana verse of this sahasranama calls her karunatarangitakshi, one whose eyes move with waves of compassion. (iv) traditional hymns such as daya shatakam and kshamashodashi describe compassion as the force that protects even those whose faults would otherwise disqualify them. bhāskararāya removes any misconception regarding the nature of the absolute ruler: compassion incarnate: she does not simply feel pity or perform merciful acts. her entire energetic and physical manifestation is nothing but compassion itself (dayā-svarūpa). every cosmic operation she runs—even dissolution and karmic correction—is structurally designed to evolve souls, demonstrating maternal mercy.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya removes any misconception regarding the nature of the absolute ruler:

Compassion Incarnate: She does not simply feel pity or perform merciful acts. Her entire energetic and physical manifestation is nothing but compassion itself (Dayā-svarūpa). Every cosmic operation She runs—even dissolution and karmic correction—is structurally designed to evolve souls, demonstrating maternal mercy.

name: महासाम्राज्यशालिनी
samasa: • महत् च तत् साम्राज्यं च। तस्य शालिनी।
wordByWord: • महासाम्राज्यशालिनी — the complete name, understood through the samasa above.
summary: (i) One who possesses a vast empire.
(ii) A Samrat controls many kings; a Mahasamrat controls many Samrats. This name is apt because She is not different from Parameshwara and is the Empress of the dominion of Mahakailasa.
searchText: 582 महासाम्राज्यशालिनी • महत् च तत् साम्राज्यं च। तस्य शालिनी। • महत् — great. • च — and. • तत् — that. • साम्राज्यं — empire. • शालिनी — possessing. • महासाम्राज्यशालिनी — the complete name, understood through the samasa above. (i) one who possesses a vast empire. (ii) a samrat controls many kings; a mahasamrat controls many samrats. this name is apt because she is not different from parameshwara and is the empress of the dominion of mahakailasa. in the saubhāgyabhāskara, bhāskararāya defines the structural scale of her throne: empress of the cosmic order: her empire (sāmrājyā) is not a physical territory on earth. she rules over the entire layout of the multi-dimensional universe. from her seat on the śrī cakra, she directs the laws of nature and the paths of liberation, shining with unmatched imperial authority.
esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara, Bhāskararāya defines the structural scale of Her throne:

Empress of the Cosmic Order: Her empire (Sāmrājyā) is not a physical territory on Earth. She rules over the entire layout of the multi-dimensional universe. From Her seat on the Śrī Cakra, She directs the laws of nature and the paths of liberation, shining with unmatched imperial authority.

name: आत्मविद्या
wordByWord: • आत्मविद्या — the complete name, understood through the samasa above.
summary: (i) One who is the doctrine that helps one understand the Self.
(ii) This Vidya instructs the form of Brahman. The name Shivajnana Pradayini also says that She bestows the knowledge of Brahman.
(iii) The teaching of Brahman to Indra in Kenopanishad may be remembered. Some traditions also take this as the Turiya Gayatri of Mahashodanyasa.
searchText: 583 आत्मविद्या • आत्मनः विद्या। • आत्मनः — of the self. • विद्या — knowledge, doctrine. • आत्मविद्या — the complete name, understood through the samasa above. (i) one who is the doctrine that helps one understand the self. (ii) this vidya instructs the form of brahman. the name shivajnana pradayini also says that she bestows the knowledge of brahman. (iii) the teaching of brahman to indra in kenopanishad may be remembered. some traditions also take this as the turiya gayatri of mahashodanyasa. bhāskararāya identifies her directly with the highest branch of spiritual science: the science of self-realization: she is not an external object or a distant deity to be analyzed. she is ātmavidyā — the sacred, internal path of insight that reveals the absolute identity of the individual soul with the universal self. realizing her is identical to recognizing one's own true nature.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya identifies Her directly with the highest branch of spiritual science:

The Science of Self-Realization: She is not an external object or a distant deity to be analyzed. She is Ātmavidyā — the sacred, internal path of insight that reveals the absolute identity of the individual soul with the universal Self. Realizing Her is identical to recognizing one's own true nature.

name: महाविद्या
wordByWord: • महाविद्या — the complete name, understood through the samasa above.
summary: (i) One who is the great doctrine.
(ii) The Atmavidya mentioned in the previous name is great because it teaches Brahman, and is therefore called Mahavidya.
(iii) She is also Mahati, the great one, because She removes all sorrows.
(iv) Navadurga Vidya, or Vanadurga Vidya in some books, and Chandi Navakshari Vidya are also connected with this name.
searchText: 584 महाविद्या • महती (सर्वानर्थनिवारका) च असौ विद्या च। • महती — great. • सर्वानर्थनिवारका — remover of all misfortunes. • च — and. • विद्या — knowledge, doctrine. • महाविद्या — the complete name, understood through the samasa above. (i) one who is the great doctrine. (ii) the atmavidya mentioned in the previous name is great because it teaches brahman, and is therefore called mahavidya. (iii) she is also mahati, the great one, because she removes all sorrows. (iv) navadurga vidya, or vanadurga vidya in some books, and chandi navakshari vidya are also connected with this name. bhāskararāya details the protective scale of this identity: the destroyer of miseries: she is the mahāvidyā because her path possesses the unique capacity to thoroughly eradicate the ultimate misery—सर्वानर्थ (sarvānartha — the systemic confusion of saṁsāra). it also references her position as the central matrix behind the ten great tantric wisdom lineages (daśa-mahāvidyā).
esoteric: Bhāskararāya details the protective scale of this identity:

The Destroyer of Miseries: She is the Mahāvidyā because Her path possesses the unique capacity to thoroughly eradicate the ultimate misery—सर्वानर्थ (sarvānartha — the systemic confusion of saṁsāra). It also references Her position as the central matrix behind the ten great Tantric wisdom lineages (Daśa-mahāvidyā).

name: श्रीविद्या
wordByWord: • श्रीविद्या — the complete name, understood through the samasa above.
summary: (i) One who is in the form of Panchadashi Vidya.
(ii) Vidya is explained in four forms: Yajna Vidya, Maha Vidya, Guhya Vidya, and Atma Vidya. Vishnu Purana says that She is all these forms and is the bestower of liberation.
(iii) Tripuratapini Upanishad says that each group of the Panchadashi mantra explains the meaning of the Gayatri mantra.
(iv) It is appropriate to call it Sree Vidya because it gives extraordinary meanings in very few letters. The fruit-section of Trishati says that Sree Vidya alone is the path to liberation.
searchText: 585 श्रीविद्या • श्रीश्चासौ विद्या च। • श्रीश्चासौ — she who is sri. • विद्या — knowledge, doctrine. • च — and. • श्रीविद्या — the complete name, understood through the samasa above. (i) one who is in the form of panchadashi vidya. (ii) vidya is explained in four forms: yajna vidya, maha vidya, guhya vidya, and atma vidya. vishnu purana says that she is all these forms and is the bestower of liberation. (iii) tripuratapini upanishad says that each group of the panchadashi mantra explains the meaning of the gayatri mantra. (iv) it is appropriate to call it sree vidya because it gives extraordinary meanings in very few letters. the fruit-section of trishati says that sree vidya alone is the path to liberation. in the saubhāgyabhāskara, bhāskararāya states the absolute identity of the system: the sovereign tradition: she is the physical and phonic embodiment of the श्रीविद्या (śrīvidyā) — the highly revered, fifteen-syllabled (pañcadaśī) or sixteen-syllabled (ṣoḍaśī) mantric system that acts as the absolute crown of the śākta tradition, seamlessly blending material abundance with final liberation.
esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara, Bhāskararāya states the absolute identity of the system:

The Sovereign Tradition: She is the physical and phonic embodiment of the श्रीविद्या (Śrīvidyā) — the highly revered, fifteen-syllabled (Pañcadaśī) or sixteen-syllabled (Ṣoḍaśī) mantric system that acts as the absolute crown of the Śākta tradition, seamlessly blending material abundance with final liberation.

name: कामसेविता
wordByWord: • कामसेविता — the complete name, understood through the samasa above.
summary: (i) One who is adored by Kama.
(ii) Kama here indicates Mahakameshwara, who worshipped Her. In mantra-shastras, Mahakameshwara and Kameshwari are said to have mutually acted as teacher and disciple.
(iii) Kama also indicates the bodiless god of love, the presiding sage of Kadi Vidya, another form of Panchadashi Vidya.
(iv) Soundaryalahari says that Cupid worshipped Sreedevee and received Her blessings; his power succeeds only through Her gracious glance.
searchText: 586 कामसेविता • कामेन सेविता (उपासिता)। • कामेन — by kama. • सेविता — worshipped. • उपासिता — adored. • कामसेविता — the complete name, understood through the samasa above. (i) one who is adored by kama. (ii) kama here indicates mahakameshwara, who worshipped her. in mantra-shastras, mahakameshwara and kameshwari are said to have mutually acted as teacher and disciple. (iii) kama also indicates the bodiless god of love, the presiding sage of kadi vidya, another form of panchadashi vidya. (iv) soundaryalahari says that cupid worshipped sreedevee and received her blessings; his power succeeds only through her gracious glance. bhāskararāya explores this name across the founding lineages of the tradition: the kāmarāja path: kāmadeva (the deity of love) is one of the premier founding seers who mastered her mantra, establishing the highly prominent कादि-मत (kādi-mata) lineage. concurrently, काम refers to paramaśiva himself, who constantly adores her dynamic energy to initiate cosmic play.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya explores this name across the founding lineages of the tradition:

The Kāmarāja Path: Kāmadeva (the deity of love) is one of the premier founding seers who mastered Her mantra, establishing the highly prominent कादि-मत (Kādi-mata) lineage. Concurrently, काम refers to Paramaśiva Himself, who constantly adores Her dynamic energy to initiate cosmic play.

name: श्रीषोडशाक्षरीविद्या
samasa: • षोडशानामक्षराणां समाहारः षोडशाक्षरी। श्रीयुक्ता षोडशाक्षरी। सा च असौ विद्या च।
• श्रीः इति षोडशमक्षरं यस्यां तादृशी च असौ विद्या च।
wordByWord: • श्रीषोडशाक्षरीविद्या — the complete name, understood through the samasa above.
summary: (i) One who is in the form of the sixteen-lettered mantra.
(ii) By adding one seed-letter to the Panchadashi mantra, the sixteen-lettered Vidya is obtained.
(iii) Mahashodashi has different versions in mantra-shastra and must be learnt through a proper teacher.
(iv) The names Atmavidya, Mahavidya, Sreevidya, and this name indicate successive forms of sacred knowledge, including the mantra-form and Parabrahman-form of Sreedevee.
searchText: 587 श्रीषोडशाक्षरीविद्या • षोडशानामक्षराणां समाहारः षोडशाक्षरी। श्रीयुक्ता षोडशाक्षरी। सा च असौ विद्या च। • श्रीः इति षोडशमक्षरं यस्यां तादृशी च असौ विद्या च। • समाहारः — collection. • षोडशाक्षरी — sixteen-lettered. • श्रीयुक्ता — endowed with sri. • सा — she. • च — and. • विद्या — knowledge, doctrine. • श्रीषोडशाक्षरीविद्या — the complete name, understood through the samasa above. (i) one who is in the form of the sixteen-lettered mantra. (ii) by adding one seed-letter to the panchadashi mantra, the sixteen-lettered vidya is obtained. (iii) mahashodashi has different versions in mantra-shastra and must be learnt through a proper teacher. (iv) the names atmavidya, mahavidya, sreevidya, and this name indicate successive forms of sacred knowledge, including the mantra-form and parabrahman-form of sreedevee. bhāskararāya details the most guarded mantric formula of the tradition: the sixteen-syllabled crown: this name explicitly points to the षोडश्यात्मक मन्त्र (ṣoḍaśī mantra). by adding the secret, highly potent sixteenth phonic seed—the syllable श्रीं (śrīṁ)—to the base fifteen-syllabled layout, the mantra reaches its ultimate structural peak. she is that exact phonic formula, which grants absolute liberation.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya details the most guarded mantric formula of the tradition:

The Sixteen-Syllabled Crown: This name explicitly points to the षोडश्यात्मक मन्त्र (Ṣoḍaśī Mantra). By adding the secret, highly potent sixteenth phonic seed—the syllable श्रीं (Śrīṁ)—to the base fifteen-syllabled layout, the mantra reaches its ultimate structural peak. She is that exact phonic formula, which grants absolute liberation.

name: त्रिकूटा
wordByWord: • वाग्भवादिकूटत्रयाभिन्ना — non-different from the three kutas beginning with Vagbhabha.
• त्रिकूटा — the complete name, understood through the samasa above.
summary: (i) One who has three groups or kutas.
(ii) Om has three parts A, U, and M. There are three deities, three worlds, three states of the soul, three bodies, three gunas, three times, and three mantra-kandas.
(iii) In Sree Vidya these are Vagbhabha Kuta, Kamaraja Kuta, and Shakti Kuta. She is the form of all that exists in groups of three.
searchText: 588 त्रिकूटा • त्रयाणां (ब्रह्मादीनामवस्थापीठलोकगुणादीनां वा) कूटं (समूहः) यस्यां सा। • वाग्भवादिकूटत्रयाभिन्ना। • त्रयाणां — of three. • कूटं — group, section. • समूहः — collection. • सा — she. • वाग्भवादिकूटत्रयाभिन्ना — non-different from the three kutas beginning with vagbhabha. • त्रिकूटा — the complete name, understood through the samasa above. (i) one who has three groups or kutas. (ii) om has three parts a, u, and m. there are three deities, three worlds, three states of the soul, three bodies, three gunas, three times, and three mantra-kandas. (iii) in sree vidya these are vagbhabha kuta, kamaraja kuta, and shakti kuta. she is the form of all that exists in groups of three. bhāskararāya decodes the triadic geography of her mantra and form: the three mantric peaks: in the śrīvidyā layout, the mantra is stratified into three distinct peaks or blocks (kūṭas): वाग्भव-कूट (vāgbhava-kūṭa), कामरौज-कूट (kāmarāja-kūṭa), and शक्ति-कूट (śakti-kūṭa). she is non-different from this triadic structure, which maps directly onto the structural states of consciousness.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya decodes the triadic geography of Her mantra and form:

The Three Mantric Peaks: In the Śrīvidyā layout, the mantra is stratified into three distinct peaks or blocks (kūṭas): वाग्भव-कूट (Vāgbhava-kūṭa), कामरौज-कूट (Kāmarāja-kūṭa), and शक्ति-कूट (Śakti-kūṭa). She is non-different from this triadic structure, which maps directly onto the structural states of consciousness.

name: कामकोटिका
wordByWord: • कामकोटिका — the complete name, understood through the samasa above.
summary: (i) One who is in the form of Kamakoti.
(ii) Since the Supreme Being is the unified form of Shiva and Shakti, Sreedevee is called Kamakotika.
(iii) She is also identified with the Kamakoti Peetha at Kanchipuram.
(iv) She is the limit or culmination of kama, understood as the highest desire, namely liberation. This is also interpreted as residence in Sreechakra.
searchText: 589 कामकोटिका • कामः (परशिवः) एव कोटिः (एकदेशः) यस्याः सा। • कामः — kama / desire / parashiva. • परशिवः — parashiva. • एव — itself. • कोटिः — limit, point, section. • एकदेशः — one part. • यस्याः — whose. • सा — she. • कामकोटिका — the complete name, understood through the samasa above. (i) one who is in the form of kamakoti. (ii) since the supreme being is the unified form of shiva and shakti, sreedevee is called kamakotika. (iii) she is also identified with the kamakoti peetha at kanchipuram. (iv) she is the limit or culmination of kama, understood as the highest desire, namely liberation. this is also interpreted as residence in sreechakra. in the saubhāgyabhāskara, bhāskararāya details the non-dual pinnacle: the apex of shiva-shakti: काम represents paramaśiva as pure, baseline attraction. कोटि means the absolute pinnacle or matching half. she is kāmakoṭikā because she sits at the absolute apex where dynamic energy fusions perfectly with static consciousness, anchoring the historical sanctuary at kāñcī.
esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara, Bhāskararāya details the non-dual pinnacle:

The Apex of Shiva-Shakti: काम represents Paramaśiva as pure, baseline attraction. कोटि means the absolute pinnacle or matching half. She is Kāmakoṭikā because She sits at the absolute apex where dynamic energy fusions perfectly with static consciousness, anchoring the historical sanctuary at Kāñcī.

name: कटाक्षकिङ्करीभूतकमलाकोटिसेविता
wordByWord: • कटाक्षकिङ्करीभूतकमलाकोटिसेविता — the complete name, understood through the samasa above.
summary: (i) One upon whom crores of Lakshmis, made servants by Her mere glance, wait and worship.
(ii) If the glance of Lakshmi for even a moment makes one wealthy, the greatness of Sreedevee, served by crores of Lakshmis, is beyond imagination.
(iii) If Her benign glance falls on a devotee, crores of Lakshmis will attend him. The greatness of Her glance is poetically described in Mooka Panchasati.
searchText: 590 कटाक्षकिङ्करीभूतकमलाकोटिसेविता • कटाक्षस्य किङ्करीभूताभिः कमलानां (लक्ष्मीणां) कोटिभिः सेविता। • कटाक्षस्य — of the glance. • किङ्करीभूताभिः — made into attendants. • कमलानां — of lakshmis. • लक्ष्मीणां — of lakshmis. • कोटिभिः — by crores. • सेविता — worshipped. • कटाक्षकिङ्करीभूतकमलाकोटिसेविता — the complete name, understood through the samasa above. (i) one upon whom crores of lakshmis, made servants by her mere glance, wait and worship. (ii) if the glance of lakshmi for even a moment makes one wealthy, the greatness of sreedevee, served by crores of lakshmis, is beyond imagination. (iii) if her benign glance falls on a devotee, crores of lakshmis will attend him. the greatness of her glance is poetically described in mooka panchasati. bhāskararāya clarifies the hierarchy of prosperity within her presence: the source of abundance: the goddess of wealth (lakṣmī) is highly sought after by humans. yet, bhāskararāya points out that millions of lakṣmīs stand ready as humble attendants to a mere fraction of her side-glance (kaṭākṣa). by simply receiving a glancing ray of her grace, the practitioner automatically obtains boundless material and spiritual wealth. block note: the five-station micro-cosmic meditation array from nāma 591 to 597, the text tracks a precise internal meditation layout, mapping her appearance across the vertical centers of the physical and subtle body as commented upon by bhāskararāya.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya clarifies the hierarchy of prosperity within Her presence:

The Source of Abundance: The goddess of wealth (Lakṣmī) is highly sought after by humans. Yet, Bhāskararāya points out that millions of Lakṣmīs stand ready as humble attendants to a mere fraction of Her side-glance (kaṭākṣa). By simply receiving a glancing ray of Her grace, the practitioner automatically obtains boundless material and spiritual wealth.
BLOCK NOTE: The Five-Station Micro-Cosmic Meditation Array
From Nāma 591 to 597, the text tracks a precise internal meditation layout, mapping Her appearance across the vertical centers of the physical and subtle body as commented upon by Bhāskararāya.

name: शिरःस्थिता
wordByWord: • शिरःस्थिता — the complete name, understood through the samasa above.
summary: (i) One who resides in the head.
(ii) She resides in the Brahmarandhra, assuming the form of a teacher.
(iii) She is also in the form of the final Mahabindu in the sound M of the seed Hrim; this is to be learnt through a teacher.
searchText: 591 शिरःस्थिता • शिरसि ब्रह्मरन्ध्रे स्थिता। • शिरसि — in the head. • ब्रह्मरन्ध्रे — in the brahmarandhra. • स्थिता — situated. • शिरःस्थिता — the complete name, understood through the samasa above. (i) one who resides in the head. (ii) she resides in the brahmarandhra, assuming the form of a teacher. (iii) she is also in the form of the final mahabindu in the sound m of the seed hrim; this is to be learnt through a teacher. bhāskararāya initiates the spatial sequence at the crown: the station of the crown nectar: she resides within the brahmarandhra at the absolute peak of the head. at this station, she sits as the pure white, unmanifested transcendent light, waiting to pour the nectar of immortality down through the subtle channels.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya initiates the spatial sequence at the crown:

The Station of the Crown Nectar: She resides within the Brahmarandhra at the absolute peak of the head. At this station, She sits as the pure white, unmanifested transcendent light, waiting to pour the nectar of immortality down through the subtle channels.

name: चन्द्रनिभा
wordByWord: • चन्द्रनिभा — the complete name, understood through the samasa above.
summary: (i) One whose lustre is like the Moon.
(ii) There is a Moon in the lower part of the Brahmarandhra, corresponding to the third division of the Panchadashi Vidya. Therefore She is called Chandranibha.
(iii) The name Chandramandalamadhyaga may also be compared.

(i) one whose lustre is like the moon. (ii) there is a moon in the lower part of the brahmarandhra, corresponding to the third division of the panchadashi vidya. therefore she is called chandranibha. (iii) the name chandramandalamadhyaga may also be compared. in the saubhāgyabhāskara, bhāskararāya maps the visual quality of the crown deity: the soothing radiance: her appearance at the head center is a cooling, soothing, unblemished white light, exactly like the full moon. this visual frequency cools the internal fires of stress and karmic friction, spreading deep tranquility through the practitioner.
esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara, Bhāskararāya maps the visual quality of the crown deity:

The Soothing Radiance: Her appearance at the head center is a cooling, soothing, unblemished white light, exactly like the full moon. This visual frequency cools the internal fires of stress and karmic friction, spreading deep tranquility through the practitioner.

name: भालस्था
wordByWord: • भालस्था — the complete name, understood through the samasa above.
summary: (i) One who resides in the forehead.
(ii) She abides in the Ajna chakra in the forehead in the form of the bindu of the syllable Hrim.
searchText: 593 भालस्था • भाले तिष्ठतीति। • भाले — in the forehead. • तिष्ठतीति — abides. • भालस्था — the complete name, understood through the samasa above. (i) one who resides in the forehead. (ii) she abides in the ajna chakra in the forehead in the form of the bindu of the syllable hrim. bhāskararāya moves the internal focus down to the forehead junction: the station of command: she positions her presence at the forehead, aligning with the ājñā chakra center. here, she functions as the sharp, inner eye of intuitive vision and intellect, directing the practitioner's internal discernment.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya moves the internal focus down to the forehead junction:

The Station of Command: She positions Her presence at the forehead, aligning with the Ājñā chakra center. Here, She functions as the sharp, inner eye of intuitive vision and intellect, directing the practitioner's internal discernment.

name: इन्द्रधनुष्प्रभा
wordByWord: • इन्द्रधनुष्प्रभा — the complete name, understood through the samasa above.
summary: (i) One whose brightness is like a rainbow.
(ii) Indradhanus means the bow of Indra, the rainbow.
(iii) Tantric texts describe the bindu and the crescent in the forehead as forms of light. Bhaskararaya also describes the bindu in the forehead as shining like a lamp, with the crescent above it.

(i) one whose brightness is like a rainbow. (ii) indradhanus means the bow of indra, the rainbow. (iii) tantric texts describe the bindu and the crescent in the forehead as forms of light. bhaskararaya also describes the bindu in the forehead as shining like a lamp, with the crescent above it. bhāskararāya decodes the chromatic shift at the forehead: the multi-hued light: while she was pure white at the crown, at the forehead she radiates like a brilliant rainbow (indradhanus). this signifies the splitting of pure light into the vibrant, functional energy channels (nāḍīs) that coordinate the intellectual operations of the brain.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya decodes the chromatic shift at the forehead:

The Multi-Hued Light: While She was pure white at the crown, at the forehead She radiates like a brilliant rainbow (Indradhanus). This signifies the splitting of pure light into the vibrant, functional energy channels (Nāḍīs) that coordinate the intellectual operations of the brain.

name: हृदयस्था
wordByWord: • हृदयस्था — the complete name, understood through the samasa above.
summary: (i) One who resides in the heart.
(ii) She is to be meditated upon in the heart.
(iii) Kalpasutra says that one who knows the heart of the Lord finds happiness at every step. Here the heart indicates the Para-bija.
(iv) Scriptures describe the entire moving and unmoving universe as present in seed-form in the heart.

esoteric: Bhāskararāya details the third station of internal descent:

The Sanctuary of the Witness: She moves Her focus into the Anāhata heart center. At this junction, She rests as the steady, internal witness light, pulsing gently as the soul of emotional balance and baseline devotion.

name: रविप्रख्या

(ii) In the heart there is a solar disc corresponding to the second division of the Panchadashi mantra. Therefore Sreedevee is called Raviprakhya.
(iii) Mooka Panchasati also indicates the same idea.
