(iii) mooka panchasati also indicates the same idea. in the saubhāgyabhāskara, bhāskararāya notes the solar frequency at the heart: the blazing solar center: at the heart center, her radiance shifts to a warm, brilliant, golden-red solar light (ravi). this intense light operates to burn away internal emotional blocks, fears, and doubts, illuminating the inner cave with clarity.
esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara, Bhāskararāya notes the solar frequency at the heart:

The Blazing Solar Center: At the heart center, Her radiance shifts to a warm, brilliant, golden-red solar light (Ravi). This intense light operates to burn away internal emotional blocks, fears, and doubts, illuminating the inner cave with clarity.

name: त्रिकोणान्तरदीपिका
wordByWord: • त्रिकोणान्तरदीपिका — the complete name, understood through the samasa above.
summary: (i) One who is like a light within a triangle.
(ii) In the pericarp of the Mooladhara there is a triangle, and within it a disc of fire corresponding to the first division of Panchadashi mantra. Hence She is called Trikonantaradipika.
(iii) Tantraraja Tantra describes fire in Mooladhara, the Sun in the heart, and the Moon below Brahmarandhra; together these form the threefold ancient mantra.
searchText: 597 त्रिकोणान्तरदीपिका • (मूलाधारे) त्रिकोणस्य आन्तरे दीपिका। • त्रिकोणस्य — of the triangle. • आन्तरे — inside. • दीपिका — lamp/light. • त्रिकोणान्तरदीपिका — the complete name, understood through the samasa above. (i) one who is like a light within a triangle. (ii) in the pericarp of the mooladhara there is a triangle, and within it a disc of fire corresponding to the first division of panchadashi mantra. hence she is called trikonantaradipika. (iii) tantraraja tantra describes fire in mooladhara, the sun in the heart, and the moon below brahmarandhra; together these form the threefold ancient mantra. bhāskararāya completes the meditation loop at the subterranean base: the lamp of the root: at the mūladhāra root center, she sits inside the inverted central triangle. she functions as a steady, unmoving lamp flame (dīpikā), representing the dormant kuṇḍalinī energy that holds the baseline vitality of the physical shell intact before its ascent.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya completes the meditation loop at the subterranean base:

The Lamp of the Root: At the Mūladhāra root center, She sits inside the inverted central triangle. She functions as a steady, unmoving lamp flame (Dīpikā), representing the dormant Kuṇḍalinī energy that holds the baseline vitality of the physical shell intact before its ascent.

name: दाक्षायणी
wordByWord: • दाक्षायणयज्ञरूपा — in the form of Dakshayana sacrifice.
• दाक्षायणी — the complete name, understood through the samasa above.
summary: (i) One who is the daughter of Daksha.
(ii) According to the dictionaries, Dakshayani means the wife of Shiva, and also refers to Rohini and other constellations, daughters of Daksha Prajapati.
(iii) Dakshayana is also a sacrifice involving repetition of Darsha and Purnamasa rites; She is in that form also.
searchText: 598 दाक्षायणी • दक्षस्य कन्या। • दाक्षायणयज्ञरूपा। • दक्षस्य — of daksha. • कन्या — daughter. • दाक्षायणयज्ञरूपा — in the form of dakshayana sacrifice. • दाक्षायणी — the complete name, understood through the samasa above. (i) one who is the daughter of daksha. (ii) according to the dictionaries, dakshayani means the wife of shiva, and also refers to rohini and other constellations, daughters of daksha prajapati. (iii) dakshayana is also a sacrifice involving repetition of darsha and purnamasa rites; she is in that form also. bhāskararāya unpacks her primary historical incarnation: the incarnation of honor: she historically manifested as satī, the daughter of king dakṣa, to execute a critical alignment of cosmic consciousness. bhāskararāya notes an advanced track: she embodies the structural framework of the sacrifice (yajna) itself, ensuring its moral validity.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya unpacks Her primary historical incarnation:

The Incarnation of Honor: She historically manifested as Satī, the daughter of King Dakṣa, to execute a critical alignment of cosmic consciousness. Bhāskararāya notes an advanced track: She embodies the structural framework of the sacrifice (yajna) itself, ensuring its moral validity.

name: दैत्यहन्त्री
wordByWord: • दैत्यहन्त्री — the complete name, understood through the samasa above.
summary: (i) One who slays demons such as Bhandasura.
(ii) The name Rakshasaghni may also be compared.
searchText: 599 दैत्यहन्त्री • दैत्यानां भण्डासुरादीनां हन्त्री। • दैत्यानां — of demons. • भण्डासुरादीनां — of bhandasura and others. • हन्त्री — destroyer. • दैत्यहन्त्री — the complete name, understood through the samasa above. (i) one who slays demons such as bhandasura. (ii) the name rakshasaghni may also be compared. bhāskararāya moves this martial title into the internal battlefield: the crusher of inner chaos: demons (daityas), such as bhaṇḍāsura, represent the dark, chaotic forces of ego and pride that attack the soul. as daityahantrī, her active energy tracks and eliminates these internal distortions, restoring the reign of spiritual law (dharma).
esoteric: Bhāskararāya moves this martial title into the internal battlefield:

The Crusher of Inner Chaos: Demons (Daityas), such as Bhaṇḍāsura, represent the dark, chaotic forces of ego and pride that attack the soul. As Daityahantrī, Her active energy tracks and eliminates these internal distortions, restoring the reign of spiritual law (Dharma).

name: दक्षयज्ञविनाशिनी
wordByWord: • दक्षयज्ञविनाशिनी — the complete name, understood through the samasa above.
summary: (i) One who destroyed the sacrifice of Daksha.
(ii) Daksha, out of ego, did not honour Shiva or give Him His proper offering. Therefore the sacrifice was destroyed.
(iii) Though Shiva and His attendants performed the destruction, Sreedevee was the instrument of that destruction, and hence the name is given to Her.
searchText: 600 दक्षयज्ञविनाशिनी • दक्षस्य (दक्षयोः) यज्ञं विनाशयितुं शीलमस्याः। • दक्षस्य — of daksha. • यज्ञं — sacrifice. • विनाशयितुं — to destroy. • शीलमस्याः — her nature is. • दक्षयज्ञविनाशिनी — the complete name, understood through the samasa above. (i) one who destroyed the sacrifice of daksha. (ii) daksha, out of ego, did not honour shiva or give him his proper offering. therefore the sacrifice was destroyed. (iii) though shiva and his attendants performed the destruction, sreedevee was the instrument of that destruction, and hence the name is given to her. in the saubhāgyabhāskara, bhāskararāya decodes the philosophical lesson behind this destruction: the dismantling of egoic ritualism: king dakṣa conducted a massive, opulent sacrifice but intentionally excluded lord śiva (pure consciousness), driven by pride. she manifested her terrifying power to systematically tear down that sacrifice. this establishes that any action, ritual, or business performed without honoring the baseline absolute consciousness is an expression of toxic ego that she will dismantle to protect cosmic balance.
esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara, Bhāskararāya decodes the philosophical lesson behind this destruction:

The Dismantling of Egoic Ritualism: King Dakṣa conducted a massive, opulent sacrifice but intentionally excluded Lord Śiva (pure consciousness), driven by pride. She manifested Her terrifying power to systematically tear down that sacrifice. This establishes that any action, ritual, or business performed without honoring the baseline absolute consciousness is an expression of toxic ego that She will dismantle to protect cosmic balance.

name: दरान्दोलितदीर्घाक्षी
samasa: • दरम् (ईषद्यथा स्यात्तथा) आन्दोलितानि (चञ्चलानि) दीर्घाणि अक्षीणि यस्याः सा।
• दरम् (भयम्) आन्दोलितं (नाशितं) यैः तादृशदीर्घाक्षीणि यस्याः सा।
wordByWord: • दरान्दोलितदीर्घाक्षी — the complete name, understood through the samasa above.
summary: (i) One whose long eyes, reaching up to the ears, slightly move.
(ii) Dara means a little; Her eyes gently move on all sides.
(iii) According to physiognomy, eyes extending to the ears are the best. Dara also means fear; Her eyes remove fear.
(iv) Her long wavering eyes allow Her compassionate glance to fall on all devotees.
searchText: 601 दरान्दोलितदीर्घाक्षी • दरम् (ईषद्यथा स्यात्तथा) आन्दोलितानि (चञ्चलानि) दीर्घाणि अक्षीणि यस्याः सा। • दरम् (भयम्) आन्दोलितं (नाशितं) यैः तादृशदीर्घाक्षीणि यस्याः सा। • दरम् — slightly / fear. • आन्दोलितानि — moving, wavering. • चञ्चलानि — restless. • दीर्घाणि — long. • अक्षीणि — eyes. • यस्याः — whose. • सा — she. • भयम् — fear. • नाशितं — destroyed. • दरान्दोलितदीर्घाक्षी — the complete name, understood through the samasa above. (i) one whose long eyes, reaching up to the ears, slightly move. (ii) dara means a little; her eyes gently move on all sides. (iii) according to physiognomy, eyes extending to the ears are the best. dara also means fear; her eyes remove fear. (iv) her long wavering eyes allow her compassionate glance to fall on all devotees. in the saubhāgyabhāskara, bhāskararāya unpacks this name across two distinct theological frameworks: the movement of vimarśa: under the first derivation, the slight, dynamic movement (दर-आन्दोलित) of her long eyes represents the active pulse of विमर्श (vimarśa — divine self-awareness) interacting with the static light of consciousness. the dissolution of saṁsāra: under the second track, दर means fear. her glance acts as a powerful shield that completely eradicates the baseline existential fear of death, birth, and transience inside the heart of the practitioner.
esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara, Bhāskararāya unpacks this name across two distinct theological frameworks:

The Movement of Vimarśa: Under the first derivation, the slight, dynamic movement (दर-आन्दोलित) of Her long eyes represents the active pulse of विमर्श (Vimarśa — divine self-awareness) interacting with the static light of consciousness.

The Dissolution of Saṁsāra: Under the second track, दर means fear. Her glance acts as a powerful shield that completely eradicates the baseline existential fear of death, birth, and transience inside the heart of the practitioner.

name: दरहासोज्ज्वलन्मुखी
wordByWord: • दरहासोज्ज्वलन्मुखी — the complete name, understood through the samasa above.
summary: (i) One whose face shines with a smile.
(ii) Darahasa means a gentle smile. Her physical beauty and inner bliss are both indicated by this smile.
(iii) The dhyana verse calls Her Smitamukhi. This name may be compared with Darasmera Mukhambuja and the Trishati name Ishatsmitanana.
(iv) Dara also means fear; in war, Her terrible laughter destroys demons.
searchText: 602 दरहासोज्ज्वलन्मुखी • दरहासेन उज्ज्वलत् मुखं यस्याः सा। • दरहासेन — by a gentle smile. • उज्ज्वलत् — shining. • मुखं — mouth/face. • यस्याः — whose. • सा — she. • दरहासोज्ज्वलन्मुखी — the complete name, understood through the samasa above. (i) one whose face shines with a smile. (ii) darahasa means a gentle smile. her physical beauty and inner bliss are both indicated by this smile. (iii) the dhyana verse calls her smitamukhi. this name may be compared with darasmera mukhambuja and the trishati name ishatsmitanana. (iv) dara also means fear; in war, her terrible laughter destroys demons. bhāskararāya details the intellectual transformations triggered by her expression: the dawn of vidyā: the gentle smile (darahāsa) is not a mundane emotional reaction. it represents the soft, luminous dawn of absolute knowledge (विद्या) breaking open. the radiance from her smile enters the dark, dense blockages of the student's intellect, instantly pacifying spiritual doubt and infusing the consciousness with baseline joy.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya details the intellectual transformations triggered by Her expression:

The Dawn of Vidyā: The gentle smile (darahāsa) is not a mundane emotional reaction. It represents the soft, luminous dawn of absolute knowledge (विद्या) breaking open. The radiance from Her smile enters the dark, dense blockages of the student's intellect, instantly pacifying spiritual doubt and infusing the consciousness with baseline joy.

name: गुरुमूर्तिः
wordByWord: • गुरुमूर्तिः — the complete name, understood through the samasa above.
summary: (i) One who assumes the form of a teacher.

(ii) Mantra-shastras say that the teacher, the mantra, and the deity are one and should not be seen as different. The Sreechakra and the Self are also to be understood in the same non-different manner.
(iii) Gu means the darkness of ignorance and ru removes it; therefore Guru means one who removes ignorance. Sreedevee comes as the Guru and gives mantra-upadesha.
searchText: 603 गुरुमूर्तिः • गुरुरेव मूर्तिः शरीरं यस्याः सा। • गुरुरेव — the guru himself. • मूर्तिः — form, embodiment. • शरीरं — body. • यस्याः — whose. • सा — she. • गुरुमूर्तिः — the complete name, understood through the samasa above. (i) one who assumes the form of a teacher. (ii) mantra-shastras say that the teacher, the mantra, and the deity are one and should not be seen as different. the sreechakra and the self are also to be understood in the same non-different manner. (iii) gu means the darkness of ignorance and ru removes it; therefore guru means one who removes ignorance. sreedevee comes as the guru and gives mantra-upadesha. bhāskararāya defines the core channel of dynamic grace within the śrīvidyā tradition: the human gateway: the absolute supreme reality is too vast to be conceptualized directly by bound souls. therefore, she assumes a tangible, human form as the श्रीगुरु (śrīguru) to execute the transmission of initiatory power (śaktipāta). bhāskararāya notes that the guru is never to be viewed as an ordinary mortal; the preceptor is the visible, functional body of the mother specialized for the liberation of the seeker.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya defines the core channel of dynamic grace within the Śrīvidyā tradition:

The Human Gateway: The absolute supreme reality is too vast to be conceptualized directly by bound souls. Therefore, She assumes a tangible, human form as the श्रीगुरु (Śrīguru) to execute the transmission of initiatory power (śaktipāta). Bhāskararāya notes that the Guru is never to be viewed as an ordinary mortal; the preceptor is the visible, functional body of the Mother specialized for the liberation of the seeker.

name: गुणनिधिः
samasa: • गुणो रज्जुः सा चेह नौकाबन्धनी वटीरिकानाम्नी लक्षणया गृह्यते। सा नितरां धीयतेऽस्यामिति गुणनिधिः (मात्स्यकालीपुराणम्)।
wordByWord: • गुणनिधिः — the complete name, understood through the samasa above.
summary: (i) One who is the treasure-house of qualities.
(ii) Sankhya doctrine speaks of three qualities, sattva, rajas, and tamas, with countless modifications. She is the repository of all such qualities.
(iii) Guna also means aggregate or vyuha; nine such vyuhas are described. Since She is the form of these, She is called Gunanidhi.
(iv) Guna also means rope; Puranic accounts of the rope used at pralaya are connected with Her stabilising power.
searchText: 604 गुणनिधिः • गुणानां निधिः। • गुणा व्यूहरूपा निधिसंख्या यस्याः सा। • गुणो रज्जुः सा चेह नौकाबन्धनी वटीरिकानाम्नी लक्षणया गृह्यते। सा नितरां धीयतेऽस्यामिति गुणनिधिः (मात्स्यकालीपुराणम्)। • गुणानां — of qualities. • निधिः — treasure. • यस्याः — whose. • सा — she. • गुणनिधिः — the complete name, understood through the samasa above. (i) one who is the treasure-house of qualities. (ii) sankhya doctrine speaks of three qualities, sattva, rajas, and tamas, with countless modifications. she is the repository of all such qualities. (iii) guna also means aggregate or vyuha; nine such vyuhas are described. since she is the form of these, she is called gunanidhi. (iv) guna also means rope; puranic accounts of the rope used at pralaya are connected with her stabilising power. bhāskararāya explores this across standard cosmic management and purāṇic alchemical metaphors: the reservoir of cosmic virtues: she is the infinite repository (nidhi) of all divine splendors, cosmic qualities, and manifestations. the anchor of the soul: under the mātsyakālī purāṇa track, guṇa denotes the heavy rope used to tie a vessel. she is the secure treasure dock that holds the binding rope of the individual soul's boat, keeping it firmly anchored and safe from capsizing in the turbulent storm waves of material illusion.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya explores this across standard cosmic management and Purāṇic alchemical metaphors:
The Reservoir of Cosmic Virtues: She is the infinite repository (nidhi) of all divine splendors, cosmic qualities, and manifestations.

The Anchor of the Soul: Under the Mātsyakālī Purāṇa track, guṇa denotes the heavy rope used to tie a vessel. She is the secure treasure dock that holds the binding rope of the individual soul's boat, keeping it firmly anchored and safe from capsizing in the turbulent storm waves of material illusion.

name: गोमाता
wordByWord: • गोमाता — the complete name, understood through the samasa above.
summary: (i) One who is in the form of Kamadhenu, the divine cow, mother of all cows, or the greatest of cows.
(ii) Dictionaries give many meanings for go, including speech, rays, and heaven. She is the root form of all these.
searchText: 605 गोमाता • गवां धेनूनां माता। • गवां — of cows. • धेनूनां — of cows. • माता — mother. • गोमाता — the complete name, understood through the samasa above. (i) one who is in the form of kamadhenu, the divine cow, mother of all cows, or the greatest of cows. (ii) dictionaries give many meanings for go, including speech, rays, and heaven. she is the root form of all these. in the saubhāgyabhāskara, bhāskararāya elevates this past standard agricultural perspectives to encompass three major linguistic and physical planes: the matrix of words, earth, and senses: according to classical lexicon definitions, गो (go) signifies the earth, speech (vāk), and the human sensory apparatus. as gomātā, she is the primordial matrix that gives birth to, nourishes, and sustains all three layers, functioning as the ultimate wish-fulfilling cow (kāmadhenu) for her children.
esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara, Bhāskararāya elevates this past standard agricultural perspectives to encompass three major linguistic and physical planes:

The Matrix of Words, Earth, and Senses: According to classical lexicon definitions, गो (go) signifies the earth, speech (Vāk), and the human sensory apparatus. As Gomātā, She is the primordial matrix that gives birth to, nourishes, and sustains all three layers, functioning as the ultimate wish-fulfilling cow (Kāmadhenu) for Her children.

name: गुहजन्मभूः
wordByWord: • गुहजन्मभूः — the complete name, understood through the samasa above.
summary: (i) One who is the mother of Guha, Lord Muruga or Subrahmanya.
(ii) The root guh means to cover. Souls veiled by ignorance are called guhas. As sparks arise from hot iron, souls arise from the Self; therefore She is also the source of such guhas.

The Parent of the Dynamic Power: Mythologically, She is the mother of Skanda (Guha), the lord of intense spiritual action.

name: देवेशी
wordByWord: • देवेशी — the complete name, understood through the samasa above.
summary: (i) One who is the ruler of the gods.
(ii) She is the head of all divine powers such as Brahma, Vishnu, Shiva, and others.
searchText: 607 देवेशी • देवानाम् ईशी। • देवानाम् — of the gods. • ईशी — ruler. • देवेशी — the complete name, understood through the samasa above. (i) one who is the ruler of the gods. (ii) she is the head of all divine powers such as brahma, vishnu, shiva, and others. bhāskararāya defines her ultimate position over the cosmic administration: the sovereign over light forces: the devas represent the cosmic forces of light, law, and balance. she is their absolute īśī (empress). they draw their specific functional capacities exclusively from her kinetic field, and they execute their duties strictly under her supreme governance.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya defines Her ultimate position over the cosmic administration:

The Sovereign over Light Forces: The Devas represent the cosmic forces of light, law, and balance. She is their absolute Īśī (empress). They draw their specific functional capacities exclusively from Her kinetic field, and they execute their duties strictly under Her supreme governance.

esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara, Bhāskararāya details Her alignment with cosmic law:

The Anchor of Moral Law: दण्डनीति represents the absolute metric of governance and corrective justice that maintains moral balance across the universe. She sits at the core of this system, ensuring that the law of cause and effect (Karma) operates with neutral accuracy, returning the exact consequences of actions to maintain universal harmony.

name: दहराकाशरूपिणी
wordByWord: • दहराकाशरूपिणी — the complete name, understood through the samasa above.
summary: (i) One who is the subtle ether in the cave of the heart.
(ii) In the heart of every being there is a subtle hidden space; She dwells there as the inner ether.
(iii) The Chandogya Upanishad describes the small lotus-house in the city of Brahman and the subtle ether within it. Brahma Sutra identifies this dahara-akasha with Brahman.
searchText: 609 दहराकाशरूपिणी • दहरमाकाशं रूपमस्याः इति। • रूपमस्याः — her form. • दहराकाशरूपिणी — the complete name, understood through the samasa above. (i) one who is the subtle ether in the cave of the heart. (ii) in the heart of every being there is a subtle hidden space; she dwells there as the inner ether. (iii) the chandogya upanishad describes the small lotus-house in the city of brahman and the subtle ether within it. brahma sutra identifies this dahara-akasha with brahman. bhāskararāya aligns this directly with the premier meditations of the chāndogya upaniṣad: the sacred internal sky: दहराकाश (daharākāśa) is the tiny, luminous ether chamber hidden deep inside the cavity of the human heart. the upaniṣad reveals that this internal space contains the entire macrocosm. she is that exact pristine, unblemished inner sky where the absolute can be directly realized when the outward senses are quieted.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya aligns this directly with the premier meditations of the Chāndogya Upaniṣad:

The Sacred Internal Sky: दहराकाश (Daharākāśa) is the tiny, luminous ether chamber hidden deep inside the cavity of the human heart. The Upaniṣad reveals that this internal space contains the entire macrocosm. She is that exact pristine, unblemished inner sky where the absolute can be directly realized when the outward senses are quieted.

name: प्रतिपन्मुख्यराकान्ततिथिमण्डलपूजिता
samasa: • प्रतिपत्तिथिः मुख्या यस्मिन्, राकातिथिः अन्ते यस्मिन् तिथिमण्डले तस्मिन् पूजिता।
wordByWord: • तिथिमण्डले — in the circle of lunar days.
• प्रतिपन्मुख्यराकान्ततिथिमण्डलपूजिता — the complete name, understood through the samasa above.
summary: (i) One who is worshipped in the circle of lunar days beginning with Pratipat and ending with the full moon.
(ii) Pratipat is the first lunar day and Raka is the full-moon day. She is worshipped through all fifteen days according to tantric rites.
(iii) Each tithi has its presiding Nitya Devi. The sixteen include the fifteen Tithi Nityas and Lalita Mahanitya in the bindu. Tithinitya worship is an important part of Sree Vidya worship.
searchText: 610 प्रतिपन्मुख्यराकान्ततिथिमण्डलपूजिता • प्रतिपत्तिथिः मुख्या यस्मिन्, राकातिथिः अन्ते यस्मिन् तिथिमण्डले तस्मिन् पूजिता। • प्रतिपत्तिथिः — the first lunar day. • मुख्या — principal, beginning. • यस्मिन् — in which. • राकातिथिः — the full-moon day. • अन्ते — at the end. • तिथिमण्डले — in the circle of lunar days. • तस्मिन् — in that. • पूजिता — worshipped. • प्रतिपन्मुख्यराकान्ततिथिमण्डलपूजिता — the complete name, understood through the samasa above. (i) one who is worshipped in the circle of lunar days beginning with pratipat and ending with the full moon. (ii) pratipat is the first lunar day and raka is the full-moon day. she is worshipped through all fifteen days according to tantric rites. (iii) each tithi has its presiding nitya devi. the sixteen include the fifteen tithi nityas and lalita mahanitya in the bindu. tithinitya worship is an important part of sree vidya worship. bhāskararāya details the integration of time and mantric geometry within this name: the mistress of lunar time: in śrīvidyā cosmology, she is non-different from time itself. the fifteen lunar days are governed by fifteen specific crescent energy deities known as the तिथि-नित्याः (tithi-nityāḥ), running from pratipat to pūrṇimā. she stands as the sixteenth absolute background light (ṣoḍaśī) that animates this complete cycle, completely surrounding and permeating the calendar array.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya details the integration of time and mantric geometry within this name:

The Mistress of Lunar Time: In Śrīvidyā cosmology, She is non-different from Time itself. The fifteen lunar days are governed by fifteen specific crescent energy deities known as the तिथि-नित्याः (Tithi-Nityāḥ), running from Pratipat to Pūrṇimā. She stands as the sixteenth absolute background light (Ṣoḍaśī) that animates this complete cycle, completely surrounding and permeating the calendar array.

name: कलात्मिका
summary: (i) One who is in the form of kalās.

(ii) Kalā indicates several divisions of divine power: the kalās of fire, Sun, Moon, Brahmā, Viṣṇu, Rudra, Īśvara and Sadāśiva, as well as the well-known sixty-four arts. All these kalās are forms of Śrīdevī.
(iii) The states such as waking, dream, deep sleep and turīya are also related to different kalās. Their real form is Śrīdevī.
searchText: 611 कलात्मिका कला आत्मा स्वरूपं यस्याः सा। कला — art; kalā; divine digit/power आत्मा — self; essence स्वरूपं — own form; essential nature यस्याः — of whom; whose (i) one who is in the form of kalās. (ii) kalā indicates several divisions of divine power: the kalās of fire, sun, moon, brahmā, viṣṇu, rudra, īśvara and sadāśiva, as well as the well-known sixty-four arts. all these kalās are forms of śrīdevī. (iii) the states such as waking, dream, deep sleep and turīya are also related to different kalās. their real form is śrīdevī. in the saubhāgyabhāskara, bhāskararāya unpacks the term कला (kalā) across multiple systematic tracks: the manifested fractions: kalā represents the digits of the moon, the sixty-four cultural arts, or the functional fractions into which the absolute energy divides itself to construct the material universe. she is the soul of all these diverse expressions, animating the components while remaining internally undivided.
esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara, Bhāskararāya unpacks the term कला (Kalā) across multiple systematic tracks:

The Manifested Fractions: Kalā represents the digits of the moon, the sixty-four cultural arts, or the functional fractions into which the absolute energy divides itself to construct the material universe. She is the soul of all these diverse expressions, animating the components while remaining internally undivided.

name: कलानाथा
summary: (i) She is the head of all kalās.
(ii) The Moon is called the lord of kalās. Since the Śrīcakra itself is contemplated in the form of the lunar sphere, the name Kalānāthā is appropriate for Śrīdevī.
searchText: 612 कलानाथा कलानां नाथा। कलानां — of the kalās (i) she is the head of all kalās. (ii) the moon is called the lord of kalās. since the śrīcakra itself is contemplated in the form of the lunar sphere, the name kalānāthā is appropriate for śrīdevī. bhāskararāya defines her operational control over temporal and artistic rays: the controller of time and expression: as kalānāthā, she governs the light digits that determine the movement of time and lunar calendars. concurrently, she rules as the supreme source behind all fine arts and creative intelligence, directing how aesthetic energy is formulated.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya defines Her operational control over temporal and artistic rays:

The Controller of Time and Expression: As Kalānāthā, She governs the light digits that determine the movement of time and lunar calendars. Concurrently, She rules as the supreme source behind all fine arts and creative intelligence, directing how aesthetic energy is formulated.

name: काव्यालापविमोदिनी
summary: (i) One who gets delighted with poetical speech, dialogue and description.
(ii) Kāvyas are works of poetry. Their rasa, beautiful speech and figures of speech delight Her.
(iii) A devotee who worships Śrīdevī methodically gains the capacity for poetic expression, as in the examples of Kālidāsa, Mūka and other great devotees.
searchText: 613 काव्यालापविमोदिनी काव्यानाम् आलापेन मोदिनी। काव्यानाम् — of poems; poetic works आलापेन — by speech; discourse (i) one who gets delighted with poetical speech, dialogue and description. (ii) kāvyas are works of poetry. their rasa, beautiful speech and figures of speech delight her. (iii) a devotee who worships śrīdevī methodically gains the capacity for poetic expression, as in the examples of kālidāsa, mūka and other great devotees. bhāskararāya details her relationship with high aesthetic culture: the muse of divine literature: true poetry (kāvya) is not ordinary wording; it is the harmonious expression of cosmic truth and beauty captured by inspired seers (kavis). she functions as the inner muse behind this literature and is deeply pleased when her children engage in refined, elevating artistic compositions that mirror divine order.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya details Her relationship with high aesthetic culture:

The Muse of Divine Literature: True poetry (Kāvya) is not ordinary wording; it is the harmonious expression of cosmic truth and beauty captured by inspired seers (kavis). She functions as the inner muse behind this literature and is deeply pleased when Her children engage in refined, elevating artistic compositions that mirror divine order.

name: सचामररमावाणीसव्यदक्षिणसेविता
samasa: चामराभ्यां सहिते सचामरे। तादृशीभ्यां रमावाणीभ्यां लक्ष्मीसरस्वतीभ्यां सव्ये दक्षिणे क्रमेण सेविता।
summary: (i) One who is attended on either side by Lakṣmī and Sarasvatī holding cāmaras.
(ii) Lakṣmī and Sarasvatī serve Śrīdevī on both sides. A devotee who becomes merged in devotion to Śrīdevī is also blessed by Lakṣmī and Sarasvatī.
(iii) The reference is also supported by the Soundaryalaharī statement that Brahmā and Hari sport with Sarasvatī and Lakṣmī.
searchText: 614 सचामररमावाणीसव्यदक्षिणसेविता चामराभ्यां सहिते सचामरे। तादृशीभ्यां रमावाणीभ्यां लक्ष्मीसरस्वतीभ्यां सव्ये दक्षिणे क्रमेण सेविता। चामराभ्यां — by two cāmaras/fans सहिते — accompanied by रमावाणीभ्यां — by ramā/lakṣmī and vāṇī/sarasvatī सव्ये — on the left दक्षिणे — on the right क्रमेण — in order (i) one who is attended on either side by lakṣmī and sarasvatī holding cāmaras. (ii) lakṣmī and sarasvatī serve śrīdevī on both sides. a devotee who becomes merged in devotion to śrīdevī is also blessed by lakṣmī and sarasvatī. (iii) the reference is also supported by the soundaryalaharī statement that brahmā and hari sport with sarasvatī and lakṣmī. in the saubhāgyabhāskara, bhāskararāya clarifies the hierarchy of cosmic powers: sovereignty over wealth and wisdom: humans spend lifetimes pursuing either material wealth (lakṣmī) or intellectual learning (sarasvatī). this name establishes that when a practitioner approaches the absolute center, both cosmic principles stand submissively as entry-level attendants holding fans at her throne, demonstrating her absolute status as the supreme source of all capability.
esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara, Bhāskararāya clarifies the hierarchy of cosmic powers:

Sovereignty over Wealth and Wisdom: Humans spend lifetimes pursuing either material wealth (Lakṣmī) or intellectual learning (Sarasvatī). This name establishes that when a practitioner approaches the absolute center, both cosmic principles stand submissively as entry-level attendants holding fans at Her throne, demonstrating Her absolute status as the supreme source of all capability.

name: आदिशक्तिः
summary: (i) One who is the original power.
(ii) She is the primordial Śakti and the root cause before all later manifestations.
(iii) The later names describe many forms and functions, but this name points to the causal power from which they arise.
searchText: 615 आदिशक्तिः आदिः कारणभूता च सा शक्तिश्च। आदिः — beginning; primordial cause कारणभूता — being the cause सा — she (i) one who is the original power. (ii) she is the primordial śakti and the root cause before all later manifestations. (iii) the later names describe many forms and functions, but this name points to the causal power from which they arise. bhāskararāya identifies the initial engine behind all physical and subtle operations: the uncaused kinetic cause: she is ādi-śakti because she exists prior to the first movement of cosmic manifestation. she is the baseline energy matrix that belongs to the absolute brahman, acting as the primary source from which all secondary forces (like gravity, light, and electricity) branch out.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya identifies the initial engine behind all physical and subtle operations:

The Uncaused Kinetic Cause: She is Ādi-śakti because She exists prior to the first movement of cosmic manifestation. She is the baseline energy matrix that belongs to the absolute Brahman, acting as the primary source from which all secondary forces (like gravity, light, and electricity) branch out.

name: अमेया
summary: (i) One who cannot be measured.
(ii) She cannot be limited by thought, word, number, place, time or object.
(iii) The name indicates that Her form is beyond the reach of ordinary means of measurement or description.
searchText: 616 अमेया मातुमशक्या। मेयं न विद्यते यस्याः सा। मेयं — measurable object विद्यते — exists यस्याः — of whom; whose (i) one who cannot be measured. (ii) she cannot be limited by thought, word, number, place, time or object. (iii) the name indicates that her form is beyond the reach of ordinary means of measurement or description. bhāskararāya applies a fundamental rule of non-dual epistemology: beyond mathematical quantification: finite objects can be measured, weighted, and fully computed by human instruments. but because she is the infinite, shoreless background awareness within which space itself exists, she can never be turned into an object to be measured. she remains immeasurable, accessible only through direct absorption.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya applies a fundamental rule of non-dual epistemology:

Beyond Mathematical Quantification: Finite objects can be measured, weighted, and fully computed by human instruments. But because She is the infinite, shoreless background awareness within which space itself exists, She can never be turned into an object to be measured. She remains immeasurable, accessible only through direct absorption.

name: आत्मा
samasa: यच्चाप्नोति यदादत्तेयच्चात्ति विषयानिह। यच्चास्य सततो भावस्तस्मादात्मेति कीर्त्यते॥
wordByWord: Word meanings are read directly from the samāsa expression above.
summary: (i) One who is in the form of Ātman or jīva.
(ii) This name indicates the jīvātman, while the following name indicates Paramātman.
(iii) Ātman may mean body, mind, Brahman, nature, firmness and intelligence; as She is present in all these, the name is apt.
searchText: 617 आत्मा जीवरूपा। यच्चाप्नोति यदादत्तेयच्चात्ति विषयानिह। यच्चास्य सततो भावस्तस्मादात्मेति कीर्त्यते॥ word meanings are read directly from the samāsa expression above. (i) one who is in the form of ātman or jīva. (ii) this name indicates the jīvātman, while the following name indicates paramātman. (iii) ātman may mean body, mind, brahman, nature, firmness and intelligence; as she is present in all these, the name is apt. in the saubhāgyabhāskara, bhāskararāya quotes the ancient semantic verses defining the soul: the pervasive subject: she is ātmā because she fulfills the four criteria of the self: she pervades all space (आप्नोति), absorbs all elements into herself (आदत्ते), experiences and illuminates all objective fields (अत्ति), and exists constantly as the baseline background layer of reality. she is the true inner identity of every living being.
esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara, Bhāskararāya quotes the ancient semantic verses defining the Soul:

The Pervasive Subject: She is Ātmā because She fulfills the four criteria of the Self: She pervades all space (आप्नोति), absorbs all elements into Herself (आदत्ते), experiences and illuminates all objective fields (अत्ति), and exists constantly as the baseline background layer of reality. She is the true inner identity of every living being.

name: परमा
wordByWord: Word meanings are read directly from the samāsa expression above.
summary: (i) One who is supreme and great in all ways.
(ii) She reveals and limits for us the immeasurable Brahman.
(iii) For those without devotion She seems distant; in relation to the previous name as jīvātman, this name points to Paramātman.
searchText: 618 परमा परब्रह्मरूपा। word meanings are read directly from the samāsa expression above. (i) one who is supreme and great in all ways. (ii) she reveals and limits for us the immeasurable brahman. (iii) for those without devotion she seems distant; in relation to the previous name as jīvātman, this name points to paramātman. bhāskararāya positions this name at the definitive peak of reality: the absolute ceiling: she is paramā because she marks the absolute ceiling of existence. there is no higher state, no finer consciousness, and no authority back behind her station, identifying her directly with the unconditioned parabrahman.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya positions this name at the definitive peak of reality:

The Absolute Ceiling: She is Paramā because She marks the absolute ceiling of existence. There is no higher state, no finer consciousness, and no authority back behind Her station, identifying Her directly with the unconditioned Parabrahman.

name: पावनाकृतिः
summary: (i) One whose body and form are holy and sanctifying.
(ii) The very thought of Śrīdevī washes away sins and makes one pure.
(iii) Kṛti may also mean action; therefore the name may also indicate one whose actions are pure and sanctifying.
searchText: 619 पावनाकृतिः पावनी आकृतिः यस्याः सा। पावनी — purifying; sanctifying आकृतिः — form यस्याः — of whom; whose (i) one whose body and form are holy and sanctifying. (ii) the very thought of śrīdevī washes away sins and makes one pure. (iii) kṛti may also mean action; therefore the name may also indicate one whose actions are pure and sanctifying. bhāskararāya details the immediate alchemical effect of her visual geometry: the purifying presence: her structural form—whether visualized as the anthropomorphic mother or as the geometric framework of the śrī cakra—possesses a natural purifying frequency (pāvanī). simply anchoring the attention upon her structure cleanses the practitioner's subconscious, burning away mental impurities.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya details the immediate alchemical effect of Her visual geometry:

The Purifying Presence: Her structural form—whether visualized as the anthropomorphic mother or as the geometric framework of the Śrī Cakra—possesses a natural purifying frequency (pāvanī). Simply anchoring the attention upon Her structure cleanses the practitioner's subconscious, burning away mental impurities.

name: अनेककोटिब्रह्माण्डजननी
samasa: अनेका अनन्ताः कोटयो ये ब्रह्माण्डास्तेषां जननी जनयित्री।
अनेककोटयः ब्रह्माण्डाः येषां विराडादीनां ते। तेषां जन
summary: (i) One who gave birth to many crores of worlds.
(ii) Virāṭ, Hiraṇyagarbha and Īśvara create crores of worlds, and She is the mother even of them.
(iii) Brahmāṇḍa arises from the gross elements and their modifications; She is the source of all such cosmic spheres.
searchText: 620 अनेककोटिब्रह्माण्डजननी अनेका अनन्ताः कोटयो ये ब्रह्माण्डास्तेषां जननी जनयित्री। अनेककोटयः ब्रह्माण्डाः येषां विराडादीनां ते। तेषां जन अनेका — many अनन्ताः — endless जननी — mother ब्रह्माण्डाः — cosmic eggs; universes (i) one who gave birth to many crores of worlds. (ii) virāṭ, hiraṇyagarbha and īśvara create crores of worlds, and she is the mother even of them. (iii) brahmāṇḍa arises from the gross elements and their modifications; she is the source of all such cosmic spheres. in the saubhāgyabhāskara, bhāskararāya strips away any localized planetary limits: the architect of the multiverse: she is not the mother of a single world or an isolated galaxy. her creative womb projects infinite systems of multiverses (aneka-koṭi-brahmāṇḍa) simultaneously. they float within her vast space like tiny foam bubbles on an ocean, showcasing her shoreless creative scale.
esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara, Bhāskararāya strips away any localized planetary limits:

The Architect of the Multiverse: She is not the mother of a single world or an isolated galaxy. Her creative womb projects infinite systems of multiverses (aneka-koṭi-brahmāṇḍa) simultaneously. They float within Her vast space like tiny foam bubbles on an ocean, showcasing Her shoreless creative scale.

name: दिव्यविग्रहा
samasa: दिव्यो रमणीयो विग्रहो देहो यस्याः सा।
दिव अन्तरिक्षे भवो दिव्यो विग्रहो रणो यस्याः सा।
summary: (i) One who has a divine and beautiful form.
(ii) The name may also recall the Purāṇic description of Devī appearing in a divine body and fighting even in the sky.
(iii) Her form is not ordinary or material but divya, fit for divine contemplation.
searchText: 621 दिव्यविग्रहा दिव्यो रमणीयो विग्रहो देहो यस्याः सा। दिव अन्तरिक्षे भवो दिव्यो विग्रहो रणो यस्याः सा। यस्याः — of whom; whose (i) one who has a divine and beautiful form. (ii) the name may also recall the purāṇic description of devī appearing in a divine body and fighting even in the sky. (iii) her form is not ordinary or material but divya, fit for divine contemplation. bhāskararāya explores this across visual appearance and martial metaphysics: the body of light: her form (vigraha) is not composed of dense biological tissue; it is made of pure, self-luminous light energy (aprakṛta). the high battlefield: alternatively, her cosmic battle (vigraha) against ignorance is conducted in the subtle space of pure consciousness, clearing the internal heavens for the seeker.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya explores this across visual appearance and martial metaphysics:
The Body of Light: Her form (vigraha) is not composed of dense biological tissue; it is made of pure, self-luminous light energy (aprakṛta).
The High Battlefield: Alternatively, Her cosmic battle (vigraha) against ignorance is conducted in the subtle space of pure consciousness, clearing the internal heavens for the seeker.

name: क्लींकारी
summary: (i) One who is the personification of the letter klīṃ, the Kāmarāja bīja.
(ii) Klīṃ is the seed connected with Kāma or Manmatha and also with Kāmeśvara.
(iii) The bīja indicates the union of Śiva and Śakti, and Śrīdevī is its living form.
searchText: 622 क्लींकारी क्लीं कामं बीजं करोतीति। कामबीजस्वरूपेति। क्लींकारस्य शिवकामस्य स्त्रीति। क्लीं — klīṃ bīja कामं — kāma; desire बीजं — seed syllable (i) one who is the personification of the letter klīṃ, the kāmarāja bīja. (ii) klīṃ is the seed connected with kāma or manmatha and also with kāmeśvara. (iii) the bīja indicates the union of śiva and śakti, and śrīdevī is its living form. bhāskararāya details the operational force of this bīja within the tradition: the seed of absolute attraction: क्लीं is the celebrated kāmarāja-bīja — the syllable of divine attraction, love, and creative desire. she is klīṁkārī because she animates the primal vibration that draws elements together to form worlds and calls the individual soul back into non-dual union with śiva.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya details the operational force of this bīja within the tradition:

The Seed of Absolute Attraction: क्लीं is the celebrated Kāmarāja-bīja — the syllable of divine attraction, love, and creative desire. She is Klīṁkārī because She animates the primal vibration that draws elements together to form worlds and calls the individual soul back into non-dual union with Śiva.

name: केवला
wordByWord: Word meanings are read directly from the samāsa expression above.
summary: (i) One who is absolute, alone and devoid of all attributes.
(ii) Kevala means the state not mixed with any other attribute. It is the remaining truth of non-duality.
(iii) In relation to the klīṃ bīja, the name is also connected with the inner secret of kāmakalā and the turīya state leading to kaivalya.
searchText: 623 केवला निखिलधर्मराहित्येनैकाकित्वात्केवला। पूर्वोक्तकामबीजे केवलत्वं ककारलकारात्मकव्यञ्जनराहित्यम्। तेन केवलतुर्यस्वरूपा। word meanings are read directly from the samāsa expression above. (i) one who is absolute, alone and devoid of all attributes. (ii) kevala means the state not mixed with any other attribute. it is the remaining truth of non-duality. (iii) in relation to the klīṃ bīja, the name is also connected with the inner secret of kāmakalā and the turīya state leading to kaivalya. in the saubhāgyabhāskara, bhāskararāya provides a profound dual track covering philosophy and mantric acoustics: the attribute-less absolute: she is kevalā because she stands completely independent, devoid of all material properties and dualities (nirguṇa). the vowel light: in advanced mantra grammar, kevala describes the extraction of the pure vowel sound after dropping the outer consonant frames (क and ल). this identifies her as the unconditioned transcendental light behind all spoken syllables.
esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara, Bhāskararāya provides a profound dual track covering philosophy and mantric acoustics:
The Attribute-less Absolute: She is Kevalā because She stands completely independent, devoid of all material properties and dualities (Nirguṇa).

The Vowel Light: In advanced mantra grammar, kevala describes the extraction of the pure vowel sound after dropping the outer consonant frames (क and ल). This identifies Her as the unconditioned transcendental light behind all spoken syllables.

name: गुह्या
summary: (i) One who is most secret in form and meaning.
(ii) Guhyā also means one who dwells in the cave of the heart.
(iii) She is to be known by inward search, as the secret reality of jīvātman, Paramātman and their unity.
searchText: 624 गुह्या गुहां प्रविष्टा। गुहां — cave; secret place (i) one who is most secret in form and meaning. (ii) guhyā also means one who dwells in the cave of the heart. (iii) she is to be known by inward search, as the secret reality of jīvātman, paramātman and their unity. bhāskararāya tracks the exact coordinates where she remains concealed: the secret core: she is guhyā because she chooses to remain hidden away from casual, superficial minds. she resides deep inside the secret cavern (guhā) of the heart and within the highly guarded, internal instructions of the śrīvidyā tradition, accessible strictly through refined devotion.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya tracks the exact coordinates where She remains concealed:

The Secret Core: She is Guhyā because She chooses to remain hidden away from casual, superficial minds. She resides deep inside the secret cavern (guhā) of the heart and within the highly guarded, internal instructions of the Śrīvidyā tradition, accessible strictly through refined devotion.

name: कैवल्यपददायिनी
samasa: कैवल्यं च पदानि च स्थानानि सालोक्यादिमुक्तिचतुष्टयं च दातुं शीलमस्याः इति।
summary: (i) One who bestows kaivalya, the solitary non-dual state of liberation.
(ii) The states of liberation such as sālokya, sārūpya, sāmīpya, sāyujya and kaivalya are mentioned; She grants these states.
(iii) Kaivalya is the final state of singleness without attributes, the direct understanding of the Self.
searchText: 625 कैवल्यपददायिनी कैवल्यं मुक्तिस्तदेव पदं ददाति। कैवल्यं च पदानि च स्थानानि सालोक्यादिमुक्तिचतुष्टयं च दातुं शीलमस्याः इति। कैवल्यं — kaivalya; non-dual liberation पदं — state; abode (i) one who bestows kaivalya, the solitary non-dual state of liberation. (ii) the states of liberation such as sālokya, sārūpya, sāmīpya, sāyujya and kaivalya are mentioned; she grants these states. (iii) kaivalya is the final state of singleness without attributes, the direct understanding of the self. bhāskararāya maps her capacity to dispense the final resting states of the soul: the giver of non-dual freedom: कैवल्य (kaivalya) is the ultimate state of liberation where the illusion of duality dissolves completely, leaving the soul in its pristine isolation as pure brahman. bhāskararāya notes she also systematically grants the preparatory stages of liberation—sālokya (sharing the realm), sāmīpya (proximity), sārūpya (similarity of form), and sāyujya (absorption).
esoteric: Bhāskararāya maps Her capacity to dispense the final resting states of the soul:

The Giver of Non-Dual Freedom: कैवल्य (Kaivalya) is the ultimate state of liberation where the illusion of duality dissolves completely, leaving the soul in its pristine isolation as pure Brahman. Bhāskararāya notes She also systematically grants the preparatory stages of liberation—Sālokya (sharing the realm), Sāmīpya (proximity), Sārūpya (similarity of form), and Sāyujya (absorption).

name: त्रिपुरा
wordByWord: Word meanings are read directly from the samāsa expression above.
summary: (i) One who is older than the three gods Brahmā, Viṣṇu and Rudra.
(ii) She assumes the three forms for creation, protection and destruction and is therefore prior to them.
(iii) Tripurā also indicates Her presence in the three states, three cities and threefold manifestations.
searchText: 626 त्रिपुरा तिसृभ्यो मूर्तिभ्यः पुरातनत्वात् त्रिपुरा। word meanings are read directly from the samāsa expression above. (i) one who is older than the three gods brahmā, viṣṇu and rudra. (ii) she assumes the three forms for creation, protection and destruction and is therefore prior to them. (iii) tripurā also indicates her presence in the three states, three cities and threefold manifestations. in the saubhāgyabhāskara, bhāskararāya defines her radical pre-existence to all triadic structures: prior to the triads: she is tripurā because she exists before (पुरत) the emergence of the foundational triads (त्रि) that build the universe. she stands before the three gods (brahmā, viṣṇu, rudra), the three primary channels (iḍā, piṅgalā, suṣumṇā), the three guṇas, and the three states of consciousness, serving as their shared unmanifested source.
esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara, Bhāskararāya defines Her radical pre-existence to all triadic structures:

Prior to the Triads: She is Tripurā because She exists before (पुरत) the emergence of the foundational triads (त्रि) that build the universe. She stands before the three gods (Brahmā, Viṣṇu, Rudra), the three primary channels (Iḍā, Piṅgalā, Suṣumṇā), the three guṇas, and the three states of consciousness, serving as their shared unmanifested source.

name: त्रिजगद्वन्द्या
summary: (i) One who is worshipped by the three worlds.
(ii) The beings of heaven, earth and the intermediate world bow to Her.
(iii) The name indicates universal reverence toward Śrīdevī.
searchText: 627 त्रिजगद्वन्द्या त्रिभिर्जगद्भिर्वन्द्या। त्रयो जगद्वन्द्या यस्याः सा। यस्याः — of whom; whose (i) one who is worshipped by the three worlds. (ii) the beings of heaven, earth and the intermediate world bow to her. (iii) the name indicates universal reverence toward śrīdevī. bhāskararāya confirms her universal authority across all dimensions of space: the object of universal adoration: the three worlds—the celestial realms (svarga), the terrestrial plane (bhūmi), and the subterranean zones (pātāla)—all bow before her authority. not a single plane of existence is independent of her law, making her the central destination for all expressions of devotion.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya confirms Her universal authority across all dimensions of space:

The Object of Universal Adoration: The three worlds—the celestial realms (svarga), the terrestrial plane (bhūmi), and the subterranean zones (pātāla)—all bow before Her authority. Not a single plane of existence is independent of Her law, making Her the central destination for all expressions of devotion.

name: त्रिमूर्तिः
wordByWord: Word meanings are read directly from the samāsa expression above.
summary: (i) One who has the three forms.
(ii) These may be understood as Brahmā, Viṣṇu and Rudra; or as the three Śaktis and other triads described in the tradition.
(iii) The threefold universe and its functions are Her own manifestation.
searchText: 628 त्रिमूर्तिः त्रैवर्षिककन्यारूपत्वात्रिमूर्तित्वम्। रक्तशुक्लमिश्रात्मकचरणमूर्तित्रयरूपा। ब्रह्मादिमूर्तित्रयं वामादित्रयमिच्छादिमूर्तित्रयं चास्या एवेति। word meanings are read directly from the samāsa expression above. (i) one who has the three forms. (ii) these may be understood as brahmā, viṣṇu and rudra; or as the three śaktis and other triads described in the tradition. (iii) the threefold universe and its functions are her own manifestation. bhāskararāya unpacks the magnificent triple configurations running through this name: the triple manifestation: she embodies the three core administrative faces of the absolute (brahmā, viṣṇu, rudra) and the three creative drives (icchā, jñāna, kriyā). the alchemical colors: she represents the three foundational rays of the feet: red, white, and mixed. ritualistically, it also maps her onto the pure energy of a three-year-old maiden (traivarṣika-kanyā) within tantric liturgy.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya unpacks the magnificent triple configurations running through this name:
The Triple Manifestation: She embodies the three core administrative faces of the absolute (Brahmā, Viṣṇu, Rudra) and the three creative drives (Icchā, Jñāna, Kriyā).

The Alchemical Colors: She represents the three foundational rays of the feet: red, white, and mixed. Ritualistically, it also maps Her onto the pure energy of a three-year-old maiden (Traivarṣika-kanyā) within Tantric liturgy.

name: त्रिदशेश्वरी
samasa: त्रिदशानां देवानां तिसृणां वा दशानामवस्थानां त्रियुक्तदशानां त्रयोदशानां विश्वेषां देवानां वा त्रिगुणितदशानां त्रिंशता लक्षणया त्रयस्त्रिंशद्गणानां चेश्वरी स्वामिनी।
wordByWord: Word meanings are read directly from the samāsa expression above.
summary: (i) One who is the head of the devas.
(ii) Tridaśa refers to the gods, including the well-known groups such as Vasus, Ādityas, Rudras and Viśvedevas.
(iii) She is the sovereign over all such divine hosts and their powers.
searchText: 629 त्रिदशेश्वरी त्रिदशानां देवानां तिसृणां वा दशानामवस्थानां त्रियुक्तदशानां त्रयोदशानां विश्वेषां देवानां वा त्रिगुणितदशानां त्रिंशता लक्षणया त्रयस्त्रिंशद्गणानां चेश्वरी स्वामिनी। word meanings are read directly from the samāsa expression above. (i) one who is the head of the devas. (ii) tridaśa refers to the gods, including the well-known groups such as vasus, ādityas, rudras and viśvedevas. (iii) she is the sovereign over all such divine hosts and their powers. in the saubhāgyabhāskara, bhāskararāya details multiple mathematical and structural insights: the sovereign over celestial hierarchies: त्रिदश (tridaśa) is a classic vedic code for the gods, referencing the thirty-three primary administrative classes of deities. she is their absolute empress. the ruler of states: alternatively, it represents the three states of consciousness (jāgrat, svapna, suṣupti) multiplied across the ten operational directions of space, all governed by her background light.
esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara, Bhāskararāya details multiple mathematical and structural insights:
The Sovereign over Celestial Hierarchies: त्रिदश (Tridaśa) is a classic Vedic code for the gods, referencing the thirty-three primary administrative classes of deities. She is their absolute empress.

The Ruler of States: Alternatively, it represents the three states of consciousness (jāgrat, svapna, suṣupti) multiplied across the ten operational directions of space, all governed by Her background light.

name: त्र्यक्षरी
wordByWord: Word meanings are read directly from the samāsa expression above.
summary: (i) One who is of three letters.
(ii) The three letters may be understood as the seed-forms connected with speech, desire and power.
(iii) The name points to Her mantra-form and to the triadic structure of Śrīvidyā.
searchText: 630 त्र्यक्षरी त्रयाणामक्षराणां वाक्कामशक्तिबीजात्मकानां समाहारः। word meanings are read directly from the samāsa expression above. (i) one who is of three letters. (ii) the three letters may be understood as the seed-forms connected with speech, desire and power. (iii) the name points to her mantra-form and to the triadic structure of śrīvidyā. bhāskararāya decodes the internal mantric layout of the core śrīvidyā formula: the triadic sound formula: the name points directly to the three supreme seed-syllables that build the vāgbhava, kāmarāja, and śakti blocks of her text—specifically the sounds ऐं, क्लीं, सौः (aiṁ, klīṁ, sauḥ). she is the direct phonic embodiment of this triadic formula, which contains the complete energetic geometry of creation.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya decodes the internal mantric layout of the core Śrīvidyā formula:

The Triadic Sound Formula: The name points directly to the three supreme seed-syllables that build the Vāgbhava, Kāmarāja, and Śakti blocks of Her text—specifically the sounds ऐं, क्लीं, सौः (Aiṁ, Klīṁ, Sauḥ). She is the direct phonic embodiment of this triadic formula, which contains the complete energetic geometry of creation.

name: दिव्यगन्धाढ्या
samasa: दिवि भवा दिव्या देवादयश्चेतनाचेतनात्मकपदार्थसमूहाः। तेषां गन्धैः सम्बन्धैराढ्या परिपूर्णा।
summary: (i) One who is rich in divine fragrance.
(ii) Divya-gandha may mean celestial sandal and other divine perfumes.
(iii) It may also indicate that the divine beings and their relationships are pervaded by Her grace.
searchText: 631 दिव्यगन्धाढ्या दिवि भवा दिव्या देवादयश्चेतनाचेतनात्मकपदार्थसमूहाः। तेषां गन्धैः सम्बन्धैराढ्या परिपूर्णा। दिव्यगन्धो हरिचन्दनादिपरिमलः, तेनाढ्या। दिव्यगन्धा आढ्याः सम्पन्ना यया प्रसादात्सा। दिवि — in heaven भवा — existing गन्धैः — by fragrances/relations (i) one who is rich in divine fragrance. (ii) divya-gandha may mean celestial sandal and other divine perfumes. (iii) it may also indicate that the divine beings and their relationships are pervaded by her grace. bhāskararāya strips this of superficial cosmetic definitions to reveal structural ontology: the fabric of relations: while it physically marks her perfume as divine sandalwood (hari-candana), bhāskararāya notes an advanced track: गन्ध (gandha) means connection or relational alignment. she is divyagandhāḍhyā because she is perfectly filled with the invisible, interconnected fabric that ties every element, space coordinate, and soul together in cosmic harmony.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya strips this of superficial cosmetic definitions to reveal structural ontology:

The Fabric of Relations: While it physically marks Her perfume as divine sandalwood (Hari-candana), Bhāskararāya notes an advanced track: गन्ध (gandha) means connection or relational alignment. She is Divyagandhāḍhyā because She is perfectly filled with the invisible, interconnected fabric that ties every element, space coordinate, and soul together in cosmic harmony.

name: सिन्दूरतिलकाञ्चिता
summary: (i) One adorned with a vermilion mark.
(ii) The sindūra tilaka beautifies Her forehead and is a sign of auspiciousness.
(iii) The name is connected with the traditional contemplation of Devī as adorned with auspicious marks.
searchText: 632 सिन्दूरतिलकाञ्चिता सिन्दूरस्य तिलकेन चित्रकेणाञ्चिता युक्ता। सिन्दूरतिलकाभ्यामञ्चिता। सिन्दूरस्य — of vermilion तिलकेन — by a forehead mark (i) one adorned with a vermilion mark. (ii) the sindūra tilaka beautifies her forehead and is a sign of auspiciousness. (iii) the name is connected with the traditional contemplation of devī as adorned with auspicious marks. bhāskararāya details the energetic placement of this symbol: the radiance of the brow center: the bright red vermilion mark (sindūra-tilaka) sits directly over the ājñā chakra center. it visually represents the blazing, active presence of her creative intent and protective force anchored at the command bridge of the subtle body, radiating auspiciousness to the seeker.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya details the energetic placement of this symbol:

The Radiance of the Brow Center: The bright red vermilion mark (sindūra-tilaka) sits directly over the Ājñā chakra center. It visually represents the blazing, active presence of Her creative intent and protective force anchored at the command bridge of the subtle body, radiating auspiciousness to the seeker.

name: उमा
samasa: उः उत्तमा च सा मा चित्तवृत्तिश्चेति।
अकारोकारमकारा मदीये प्रणवे स्थिताः।उकारं च मकारं च अकारं च क्रमेरितम्॥
summary: (i) Umā is interpreted in many ways.
(ii) U may indicate Śiva and mā may indicate Lakṣmī or measuring power; thus the name contains Śiva-Śakti meanings.
(iii) The name is also connected with the praṇava and with traditional interpretations of Umā in Śaiva and Śākta texts.
searchText: 633 उमा उकारः शिववाचकस्तस्य मा लक्ष्मीः। उं परशिवं माति परिच्छिनत्तीति। उः उत्तमा च सा मा चित्तवृत्तिश्चेति। अकारोकारमकारा मदीये प्रणवे स्थिताः।उकारं च मकारं च अकारं च क्रमेरितम्॥ उकारः — the syllable u सा — she (i) umā is interpreted in many ways. (ii) u may indicate śiva and mā may indicate lakṣmī or measuring power; thus the name contains śiva-śakti meanings. (iii) the name is also connected with the praṇava and with traditional interpretations of umā in śaiva and śākta texts. in the saubhāgyabhāskara*, bhāskararāya provides three profound insights for this short name: the mirror of shiva: उ (u) signifies lord śiva (pure consciousness), and मा (mā) means his light, wealth, or mirror. she is the dynamic reflection that allows consciousness to know itself. the synthesis of the praṇava: he notes her structural identity within the syllables of ओम् (aum), where the sounds are inverted and integrated to prove her non-differentiation from the ultimate word.
esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara*, Bhāskararāya provides three profound insights for this short name:
The Mirror of Shiva: उ (u) signifies Lord Śiva (pure consciousness), and मा (mā) means His light, wealth, or mirror. She is the dynamic reflection that allows consciousness to know itself.
The Synthesis of the Praṇava: He notes Her structural identity within the syllables of ओम् (Aum), where the sounds are inverted and integrated to prove Her non-differentiation from the ultimate word.

name: शैलेन्द्रतनया
summary: (i) Daughter of the lord of mountains, Himavān.
(ii) The name identifies Her as Pārvatī, born as the daughter of the mountain king.
(iii) It also recalls Her manifest form for devotees.
searchText: 634 शैलेन्द्रतनया शैलेन्द्रस्य पर्वतराजस्य हिमवतस्तनया पुत्री। शैलेन्द्रस्य — of the mountain lord (i) daughter of the lord of mountains, himavān. (ii) the name identifies her as pārvatī, born as the daughter of the mountain king. (iii) it also recalls her manifest form for devotees. bhāskararāya details the structural meaning behind her mountainous birth choice: the spine of earthly stability: she chose to manifest as pārvatī, daughter of the himālayas (śailendra). mountains represent the firm, unmoving, structural anchors of the earth element. by anchoring her physical manifestation to this range, she establishes the firm, grounded channel within the spine of the earth, aligning with the macrocosmic suṣumṇā.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya details the structural meaning behind Her mountainous birth choice:

The Spine of Earthly Stability: She chose to manifest as Pārvatī, daughter of the Himālayas (Śailendra). Mountains represent the firm, unmoving, structural anchors of the earth element. By anchoring Her physical manifestation to this range, She establishes the firm, grounded channel within the spine of the earth, aligning with the macrocosmic Suṣumṇā.

name: गौरी
wordByWord: Word meanings are read directly from the samāsa expression above.
summary: (i) One who is fair or radiant.
(ii) Gaurī means the bright or fair one.
(iii) The name points to Her auspicious and shining form.
searchText: 635 गौरी गौरवर्णत्वाद्गौरी। word meanings are read directly from the samāsa expression above. (i) one who is fair or radiant. (ii) gaurī means the bright or fair one. The Pristine Radiance: As Gaurī, Her skin radiates a brilliant, unblemished golden-white light. She is the light that contains no dark corners of ignorance, radiating absolute clarity.

name: गन्धर्वसेविता
summary: (i) One served by Gandharvas.
(ii) The celestial musicians praise and worship Her.
(iii) Their service shows that divine music and praise belong to Her worship.
searchText: 636 गन्धर्वसेविता गन्धर्वैः सेविता। गन्धर्वैः — by gandharvas (i) one served by gandharvas. (ii) the celestial musicians praise and worship her. (iii) their service shows that divine music and praise belong to her worship. bhāskararāya tracks her sovereignty over the acoustic arts: the sovereign of harmonics: the gandharvas are the celestial masters of music, fine melody, and rhythm. their constant service at her throne indicates that every perfect musical note, scale, and harmonic frequency in the universe is merely a faint echo of her inner cosmic voice, adoring her as the source of sound.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya tracks Her sovereignty over the acoustic arts:

The Sovereign of Harmonics: The Gandharvas are the celestial masters of music, fine melody, and rhythm. Their constant service at Her throne indicates that every perfect musical note, scale, and harmonic frequency in the universe is merely a faint echo of Her inner cosmic voice, adoring Her as the source of sound.

name: विश्वगर्भा
summary: (i) One who has the universe in Her womb.
(ii) The entire manifest world is contained in Her before and during manifestation.
(iii) She is therefore the cosmic mother and source of all worlds.
searchText: 637 विश्वगर्भा विश्वं प्रपञ्चजातं गर्भे यस्याः सा। विश्वं — universe गर्भे — in the womb यस्याः — of whom; whose (i) one who has the universe in her womb. (ii) the entire manifest world is contained in her before and during manifestation. (iii) she is therefore the cosmic mother and source of all worlds. in the saubhāgyabhāskara, bhāskararāya clarifies the internal position of creation: the safe embryo of consciousness: the universe does not sit outside of her space like an independent house built by a carpenter. the entire projection remains permanently inside her causal womb (garbha), drawing its baseline existence and nourishment directly from her field, fully protected like a child within its mother.
esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara, Bhāskararāya clarifies the internal position of creation:

The Safe Embryo of Consciousness: The universe does not sit outside of Her space like an independent house built by a carpenter. The entire projection remains permanently inside Her causal womb (garbha), drawing its baseline existence and nourishment directly from Her field, fully protected like a child within its mother.

name: स्वर्णगर्भा
samasa: अर्णानां वर्णानां मातृकाणां गर्भः शोभनो यया सा।
स्वर्णाः शोभनार्णा मन्त्रा गर्भे यस्याः सा।
summary: (i) One whose womb is golden or who contains hiraṇya.
(ii) The name may also be read in relation to varṇas, mātr̥kās and auspicious mantras held within Her.
(iii) She is the womb of radiant creation and mantra-consciousness.
searchText: 638 स्वर्णगर्भा स्वर्णं हिरण्यं गर्भे यस्याः सा। स्वर्णस्य गर्भभवा। अर्णानां वर्णानां मातृकाणां गर्भः शोभनो यया सा। स्वर्णाः शोभनार्णा मन्त्रा गर्भे यस्याः सा। स्वर्णं — gold हिरण्यं — golden principle गर्भे — in the womb यस्याः — of whom; whose (i) one whose womb is golden or who contains hiraṇya. (ii) the name may also be read in relation to varṇas, mātr̥kās and auspicious mantras held within her. (iii) she is the womb of radiant creation and mantra-consciousness. bhāskararāya unpacks the dual material and phonetic layers of this title: the cosmic light source: she is identical with हिरण्यगर्भ (hiraṇyagarbha — the golden womb of cosmic light that initiates creation). the mantric repository: under the phonetic track, सु-वर्ण means beautiful, potent letters. she is svarṇagarbhā because she contains the complete matrix of all brilliant, hidden mantras inside her causal field, holding their formatting codes safe.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya unpacks the dual material and phonetic layers of this title:
The Cosmic Light Source: She is identical with हिरण्यगर्भ (Hiraṇyagarbha — the golden womb of cosmic light that initiates creation).

The Mantric Repository: Under the phonetic track, सु-वर्ण means beautiful, potent letters. She is Svarṇagarbhā because She contains the complete matrix of all brilliant, hidden mantras inside Her causal field, holding their formatting codes safe.

name: अवरदा
samasa: अवरदेति चतुरक्षरं नाम। अवराननार्यानसुरान् द्यति खण्डयतीति।
अवन्तीत्यवाः कान्तिमन्तो रदा दन्ता यस्याः सा।
summary: (i) The name is explained in more than one way.
(ii) It may indicate one who destroys the ignoble or demonic; it may also be connected with beautiful, shining teeth.
(iii) The traditional explanations preserve both a protective and a beauty-related sense.
searchText: 639 अवरदा अवरदेति चतुरक्षरं नाम। अवराननार्यानसुरान् द्यति खण्डयतीति। अवन्तीत्यवाः कान्तिमन्तो रदा दन्ता यस्याः सा। यस्याः — of whom; whose (i) the name is explained in more than one way. (ii) it may indicate one who destroys the ignoble or demonic; it may also be connected with beautiful, shining teeth. (iii) the traditional explanations preserve both a protective and a beauty-related sense. bhāskararāya details her defensive and aesthetic parameters: the dissolver of low intent: अवर (avara) refers to the dark, chaotic, unrighteous demonic forces that attack spiritual balance. she is the power that cuts down (dyati) these deviations. alternatively, her teeth are beautifully radiant, acting as a metaphor for the sharp, defensive boundary walls that guard her countenance.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya details Her defensive and aesthetic parameters:

The Dissolver of Low Intent: अवर (avara) refers to the dark, chaotic, unrighteous demonic forces that attack spiritual balance. She is the power that cuts down (dyati) these deviations. Alternatively, Her teeth are beautifully radiant, acting as a metaphor for the sharp, defensive boundary walls that guard Her countenance.

name: वागधीश्वरी
summary: (i) Sovereign of speech.
(ii) All words, expression and learning stand under Her rule.
(iii) As the power behind speech, She is worshipped as the supreme source of vāṇī.
searchText: 640 वागधीश्वरी वाचाम् अधीश्वरी। वाचाम् — of speech/words (i) sovereign of speech. (ii) all words, expression and learning stand under her rule. (iii) as the power behind speech, she is worshipped as the supreme source of vāṇī. in the saubhāgyabhāskara, this states her supreme legislative control over communication: the commander of language: she is the absolute director governing the entire grid of human and divine expression (vāk). she rules over grammar, semantic translation, and philosophical formulation. every word spoken or written operates strictly under her structural coordination, making her the ultimate master of wisdom.
esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara, this states Her supreme legislative control over communication:

The Commander of Language: She is the absolute director governing the entire grid of human and divine expression (Vāk). She rules over grammar, semantic translation, and philosophical formulation. Every word spoken or written operates strictly under Her structural coordination, making Her the ultimate master of wisdom.

name: ध्यानगम्या
summary: (i) One who is reached through meditation.
(ii) She is known by dhyāna and inner contemplation, not by external perception alone.
(iii) By meditating on Her, the devotee becomes absorbed in Her presence.
searchText: 641 ध्यानगम्या ध्यानेन विभावनेन गम्या ज्ञेया। ध्यानेन — by meditation गम्या — attainable; knowable (i) one who is reached through meditation. (ii) she is known by dhyāna and inner contemplation, not by external perception alone. (iii) by meditating on her, the devotee becomes absorbed in her presence. bhāskararāya clarifies the exact mechanism required to reach the center: the internal approach: she cannot be uncovered by external physical travel, ostentatious public displays, or dry intellectual debate. she is dhyānagamyā—accessible exclusively when the practitioner turns the attention inward, quietens the cognitive ripples, and enters a state of deep, continuous mental absorption (dhyāna).
esoteric: Bhāskararāya clarifies the exact mechanism required to reach the center:

The Internal Approach: She cannot be uncovered by external physical travel, ostentatious public displays, or dry intellectual debate. She is Dhyānagamyā—accessible exclusively when the practitioner turns the attention inward, quietens the cognitive ripples, and enters a state of deep, continuous mental absorption (dhyāna).

name: अपरिच्छेद्या
wordByWord: Word meanings are read directly from the samāsa expression above.
summary: (i) One who cannot be delimited.
(ii) She cannot be divided by space, time, object or thought.
(iii) Her nature is unlimited and beyond all finite boundaries.
searchText: 642 अपरिच्छेद्या न परिच्छेद्या। word meanings are read directly from the samāsa expression above. (i) one who cannot be delimited. (ii) she cannot be divided by space, time, object or thought. (iii) her nature is unlimited and beyond all finite boundaries. bhāskararāya details her absolute structural infinity: beyond partition: material objects can be chopped into pieces, limited by boundary walls, or cut off by the passage of time. as the unconditioned absolute reality, she possesses no edges. she cannot be partitioned by spatial lines, split by historical eras, or fragmented by mental concepts, remaining eternally whole.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya details Her absolute structural infinity:

Beyond Partition: Material objects can be chopped into pieces, limited by boundary walls, or cut off by the passage of time. As the unconditioned absolute reality, She possesses no edges. She cannot be partitioned by spatial lines, split by historical eras, or fragmented by mental concepts, remaining eternally whole.

name: ज्ञानदा
summary: (i) Giver of knowledge.
(ii) She grants knowledge of both worldly and spiritual truths.
(iii) Ultimately She gives the liberating knowledge of the Self.
searchText: 643 ज्ञानदा ज्ञानं ददातीति। ज्ञानं — knowledge (i) giver of knowledge. (ii) she grants knowledge of both worldly and spiritual truths. (iii) ultimately she gives the liberating knowledge of the self. in the saubhāgyabhāskara, bhāskararāya outlines her ultimate gift to the seeker: the granting of awakening: the individual soul is trapped by the illusion of separation. she acts as jñānadā, directly bestowing ब्रह्मज्ञान (brahma-jñāna — non-dual self-knowledge). this gift instantly vaporizes the structural ignorance of the seeker, dissolving their bondage and granting immediate spiritual freedom.
esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara, Bhāskararāya outlines Her ultimate gift to the seeker:

The Granting of Awakening: The individual soul is trapped by the illusion of separation. She acts as Jñānadā, directly bestowing ब्रह्मज्ञान (Brahma-jñāna — non-dual self-knowledge). This gift instantly vaporizes the structural ignorance of the seeker, dissolving their bondage and granting immediate spiritual freedom.

name: ज्ञानविग्रहा
samasa: ज्ञानस्य विग्रहो विस्तारो यस्याः सकाशात् सा।
summary: (i) One whose very body is knowledge.
(ii) Knowledge is not merely Her attribute but Her form itself.
(iii) The expansion of knowledge proceeds from Her.
searchText: 644 ज्ञानविग्रहा ज्ञानमेव विग्रहः शरीरं यस्याः सा। ज्ञानस्य विग्रहो विस्तारो यस्याः सकाशात् सा। ज्ञानमेव — knowledge itself विग्रहः — body; form यस्याः — of whom; whose (i) one whose very body is knowledge. (ii) knowledge is not merely her attribute but her form itself. (iii) the expansion of knowledge proceeds from her. bhāskararāya constructs a direct non-dual refinement to prevent structural duality: identity with wisdom: lest a student think that she is a person who simply holds information, this name states her body is made of nothing but pure knowledge itself (jñāna-svarūpa). alternatively, she is the primary fountainhead out of which the complete expansion (vigraha) of all sciences and arts streams into the cosmos.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya constructs a direct non-dual refinement to prevent structural duality:

Identity with Wisdom: Lest a student think that She is a person who simply holds information, this name states Her body is made of nothing but pure knowledge itself (Jñāna-svarūpa). Alternatively, She is the primary fountainhead out of which the complete expansion (vigraha) of all sciences and arts streams into the cosmos.

name: सर्ववेदान्तसंवेद्या
wordByWord: Word meanings are read directly from the samāsa expression above.
summary: (i) One known by all Vedānta.
(ii) The Upaniṣads and Vedānta texts point to Her as the supreme reality.
(iii) She is the truth to be realised through the culmination of scriptural enquiry.
searchText: 645 सर्ववेदान्तसंवेद्या सर्वैर्वेदान्तैरुपनिषत्समूहैः समयग्वेद्या। word meanings are read directly from the samāsa expression above. (i) one known by all vedānta. (ii) the upaniṣads and vedānta texts point to her as the supreme reality. (iii) she is the truth to be realised through the culmination of scriptural enquiry. bhāskararāya tracks her identity to the final conclusion of vedic revelation: the core subject of non-duality: वेदान्त (vedānta) represents the peak upanishadic records. bhāskararāya notes that every riddle, statement, and paradox found inside the upanishads has only one ultimate target. their corporate intent is to point directly to her essential nature as the non-dual absolute consciousness baseline of existence.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya tracks Her identity to the final conclusion of Vedic revelation:

The Core Subject of Non-Duality: वेदान्त (Vedānta) represents the peak Upanishadic records. Bhāskararāya notes that every riddle, statement, and paradox found inside the Upanishads has only one ultimate target. Their corporate intent is to point directly to Her essential nature as the non-dual absolute consciousness baseline of existence.

name: सत्यानन्दस्वरूपिणी
summary: (i) One whose form is truth and bliss.
(ii) Satya and ānanda are Her own nature.
(iii) The name points to Her as the blissful reality beyond false appearances.
searchText: 646 सत्यानन्दस्वरूपिणी सत्यमानन्दश्च स्वरूपं यस्याः सा। स्वरूपं — own form; essential nature यस्याः — of whom; whose (i) one whose form is truth and bliss. (ii) satya and ānanda are her own nature. (iii) the name points to her as the blissful reality beyond false appearances. in the saubhāgyabhāskara, bhāskararāya connects this directly to the description of brahman: the reality of bliss: she embodies the two primary operational attributes that define the absolute absolute reality—सत्य (satya — uncorrupted existence/truth) and आनन्द (ānanda — unconditioned bliss). she is the living expression of this reality, comforting the practitioner that the source of existence is full of ecstatic joy.
esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara, Bhāskararāya connects this directly to the description of Brahman:

The Reality of Bliss: She embodies the two primary operational attributes that define the absolute absolute reality—सत्य (Satya — uncorrupted existence/truth) and आनन्द (Ānanda — unconditioned bliss). She is the living expression of this reality, comforting the practitioner that the source of existence is full of ecstatic joy.

name: लोपामुद्रार्चिता
summary: (i) Worshipped by Lopāmudrā, the wife of Agastya.
(ii) Lopāmudrā is one of the great Śrīvidyā upāsakās.
(iii) The name links Her worship to the lineage of Agastya and Lopāmudrā.
searchText: 647 लोपामुद्रार्चिता लोपामुद्रया अगस्त्यपत्न्या कर्त्र्या अर्चिता उपासिता। लोपामुद्रया — by lopāmudrā अर्चिता — worshipped (i) worshipped by lopāmudrā, the wife of agastya. (ii) lopāmudrā is one of the great śrīvidyā upāsakās.
