(iii) the name links her worship to the lineage of agastya and lopāmudrā. bhāskararāya validates her position within the core historical lineages of the tradition: the hādi lineage crown: lopāmudrā, the consort of sage agastya, is one of the premier master seers of the śrīvidyā tradition, credited with bringing down the highly revered हादि-मत (hādi-mata) formulation of the core mantra. her intense adoration confirms the absolute authority and scriptural validity of this branch of practice.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya validates Her position within the core historical lineages of the tradition:

The Hādi Lineage Crown: Lopāmudrā, the consort of Sage Agastya, is one of the premier master seers of the Śrīvidyā tradition, credited with bringing down the highly revered हादि-मत (Hādi-mata) formulation of the core mantra. Her intense adoration confirms the absolute authority and scriptural validity of this branch of practice.

name: लीलाकॢप्तब्रह्माण्डमण्डला
summary: (i) One who playfully creates the spheres of countless Brahmāṇḍas.
(ii) Creation is only līlā for Her, done without effort.
(iii) The cosmic egg-clusters arise by Her mere sport.
searchText: 648 लीलाकॢप्तब्रह्माण्डमण्डला लीलामात्रेणानायासेन कॢप्तानि ब्रह्माण्डमण्डलानि यस्याः सा। कॢप्तानि — created; fashioned यस्याः — of whom; whose (i) one who playfully creates the spheres of countless brahmāṇḍas. (ii) creation is only līlā for her, done without effort. (iii) the cosmic egg-clusters arise by her mere sport. bhāskararāya details her absolute ease of operation, removing any concepts of cosmic labor: effortless cosmic architecture: the creation, structuring, and alignment of infinite planetary grids and universal caskets require no heavy labor, planning committees, or structural fatigue from her end. it is a spontaneous, effortless overflow of her playful energy (līlā), projected as easily as a child breathing out soap bubbles.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya details Her absolute ease of operation, removing any concepts of cosmic labor:

Effortless Cosmic Architecture: The creation, structuring, and alignment of infinite planetary grids and universal caskets require no heavy labor, planning committees, or structural fatigue from Her end. It is a spontaneous, effortless overflow of Her playful energy (līlā), projected as easily as a child breathing out soap bubbles.

name: अदृश्या
wordByWord: Word meanings are read directly from the samāsa expression above.
summary: (i) One who is invisible.
(ii) She is different from all visible objects and is not grasped by the eyes or senses.
(iii) Her reality is to be known inwardly, not by ordinary sight.
searchText: 649 अदृश्या दृश्यविलक्षणत्वाददृश्या। चक्षुरादीन्द्रियायोग्या। word meanings are read directly from the samāsa expression above. (i) one who is invisible. (ii) she is different from all visible objects and is not grasped by the eyes or senses. (iii) her reality is to be known inwardly, not by ordinary sight. in the saubhāgyabhāskara, bhāskararāya details the ultimate direction of sensory alignment: the invisible subject: human eyes and sensory channels are designed strictly to capture external, material objects (dṛśya). because she is the foundational subjective consciousness that sits behind the eyes, enabling them to see in the first place, she can never be seen as an external object. she is the ultimate seer, permanently invisible to outer sight.
esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara, Bhāskararāya details the ultimate direction of sensory alignment:

The Invisible Subject: Human eyes and sensory channels are designed strictly to capture external, material objects (dṛśya). Because She is the foundational subjective consciousness that sits behind the eyes, enabling them to see in the first place, She can never be seen as an external object. She is the ultimate seer, permanently invisible to outer sight.

name: दृश्यरहिता
summary: (i) One who is free from the visible.
(ii) She is not an object of perception and is not limited by what can be seen.
(iii) The visible world depends on Her, but She transcends it.
searchText: 650 दृश्यरहिता दृश्येण रहिता रहिता — devoid of (i) one who is free from the visible. (ii) she is not an object of perception and is not limited by what can be seen. (iii) the visible world depends on her, but she transcends it. bhāskararāya details the absolute state of non-dual realization that concludes this block: the resolution of duality: दृश्य (dṛśya) represents the entire field of changing, objective material appearances. she is dṛṣyarahitā because in her ultimate essential state (svarūpa), the illusion of a separate objective universe completely dissolves. when the practitioner achieves the highest absorption (nirvikalpa-samādhi), the movie of the world vanishes, leaving her to shine alone in her pristine, non-dual subjective purity.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya details the absolute state of non-dual realization that concludes this block:

The Resolution of Duality: दृश्य (dṛśya) represents the entire field of changing, objective material appearances. She is Dṛṣyarahitā because in Her ultimate essential state (Svarūpa), the illusion of a separate objective universe completely dissolves. When the practitioner achieves the highest absorption (Nirvikalpa-Samādhi), the movie of the world vanishes, leaving Her to shine alone in Her pristine, non-dual subjective purity.

name: विज्ञात्री
summary: (i) One who is the knower.
(ii) She is the special intelligence behind all knowledge.
(iii) Though She is the power of knowing, She is distinct from every known object.
searchText: 651 विज्ञात्री विशेषेण जानातीति विज्ञात्री। विशेषेण — especially (i) one who is the knower. (ii) she is the special intelligence behind all knowledge. (iii) though she is the power of knowing, she is distinct from every known object. in the saubhāgyabhāskara, bhāskararāya defines this name as the supreme subjective consciousness: the absolute knower: she is the ultimate subject (draṣṭā) behind all acts of perception. while ordinary intellects analyze separate objects, she is the non-dual baseline awareness that comprehends the entire cosmos comprehensively and distinctively, without error or modification.
esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara, Bhāskararāya defines this name as the supreme subjective consciousness:

The Absolute Knower: She is the ultimate subject (draṣṭā) behind all acts of perception. While ordinary intellects analyze separate objects, She is the non-dual baseline awareness that comprehends the entire cosmos comprehensively and distinctively, without error or modification.

name: वेद्यवर्जिता
summary: (i) One who is devoid of anything to be known apart from Herself.
(ii) There is no separate object by which She can be fully known.
(iii) As the ultimate knower, She is beyond the category of the knowable.
searchText: 652 वेद्यवर्जिता वेद्येन वेद्यस्य वा वर्जिता। वेद्येन — by the knowable (i) one who is devoid of anything to be known apart from herself. (ii) there is no separate object by which she can be fully known. (iii) as the ultimate knower, she is beyond the category of the knowable. bhāskararāya construct an immediate non-dual paradox following vijñātrī: beyond knower and known: though she functions as the ultimate knower (vijñātrī), she is simultaneously vedyavarjitā because there is no independent second object outside of her own nature to be known. in her essential state of absolute absorption (samādhi), the triad of knower, knowing, and known dissolves into pure, unified awareness.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya construct an immediate non-dual paradox following Vijñātrī:

Beyond Knower and Known: Though She functions as the ultimate Knower (Vijñātrī), She is simultaneously Vedyavarjitā because there is no independent second object outside of Her own nature to be known. In Her essential state of absolute absorption (Samādhi), the triad of knower, knowing, and known dissolves into pure, unified awareness.

name: योगिनी
samasa: योगः विषयसङ्गः भोगः, सः अस्याः अस्तीति भोक्त्री।
summary: (i) One who is in the form of yoga.
(ii) Yoga means the union or identity of the individual and the supreme.
(iii) It may also be explained as bhoga in another interpretive line; She is present in both yoga and experience.
searchText: 653 योगिनी योगः एकत्वभावना अस्याः अस्तीति। योगः विषयसङ्गः भोगः, सः अस्याः अस्तीति भोक्त्री। योगः — yoga; union एकत्वभावना — contemplation of oneness (i) one who is in the form of yoga. (ii) yoga means the union or identity of the individual and the supreme. (iii) it may also be explained as bhoga in another interpretive line; she is present in both yoga and experience. bhāskararāya details both the philosophical path of realization and the agamic framework: the spirit of union: योग represents the intense internal cultivation of oneness (ekatva-bhāvanā) between the individual soul and the universal self. the cosmic consumer: alternatively, yoga can denote cosmic experience and sensory contact (bhoga). she is the ultimate consumer who experiences the universe through all forms while remaining untainted. in agamic geometry, it also names her as the supreme source behind all attendant yogini energy hosts.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya details both the philosophical path of realization and the Agamic framework:
The Spirit of Union: योग represents the intense internal cultivation of oneness (ekatva-bhāvanā) between the individual soul and the universal Self.

The Cosmic Consumer: Alternatively, Yoga can denote cosmic experience and sensory contact (bhoga). She is the ultimate consumer who experiences the universe through all forms while remaining untainted. In Agamic geometry, it also names Her as the supreme source behind all attendant yogini energy hosts.

name: योगदा
summary: (i) One who gives yoga.
(ii) She grants union with the supreme reality.
(iii) In another sense She also gives the experiences appropriate to the devotee.
searchText: 654 योगदा योगं ददातीति। योगः विषयसङ्गः भोगः, तं ददातीति। योगं — yoga योगः — yoga; union (i) one who gives yoga. (ii) she grants union with the supreme reality. (iii) in another sense she also gives the experiences appropriate to the devotee. in the saubhāgyabhāskara, bhāskararāya defines her capacity to distribute ultimate states: the dispenser of liberation and enjoyment: she is the dynamic force that grants the gift of spiritual union (yoga), linking the disciplined seeker back to the absolute. concurrently, for those on the path of integrated practice, she perfectly measures out the appropriate external alignment and cosmic experience (bhoga) needed for overall evolution.
esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara, Bhāskararāya defines Her capacity to distribute ultimate states:

The Dispenser of Liberation and Enjoyment: She is the dynamic force that grants the gift of spiritual union (Yoga), linking the disciplined seeker back to the absolute. Concurrently, for those on the path of integrated practice, She perfectly measures out the appropriate external alignment and cosmic experience (bhoga) needed for overall evolution.

name: योग्या
summary: (i) One who is fit to be attained through yoga.
(ii) She is the proper object of spiritual union and worship.
(iii) In an alternate interpretation, the name also connects with bhogya, that which is fit for enjoyment or experience.
searchText: 655 योग्या योगः विषयसङ्गः भोगः, योग्यत्वात् भोग्या। योगः — yoga; union (i) one who is fit to be attained through yoga. (ii) she is the proper object of spiritual union and worship. (iii) in an alternate interpretation, the name also connects with bhogya, that which is fit for enjoyment or experience. bhāskararāya highlights her status as the ultimate target of meditation: the proper object of realization: she is yogyā because she is the only reality truly fit (yogya) to be sought after, adored, and meditated upon by advanced intellects. every other material object provides transient distraction, while she represents the ultimate, correct destination of human consciousness.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya highlights Her status as the ultimate target of meditation:

The Proper Object of Realization: She is Yogyā because She is the only reality truly fit (yogya) to be sought after, adored, and meditated upon by advanced intellects. Every other material object provides transient distraction, while She represents the ultimate, correct destination of human consciousness.

name: योगानन्दा
samasa: योगः सङ्गः न विद्यते यस्याः सा।
summary: (i) One who is the bliss obtained through yoga.
(ii) The union of Śiva and Śakti is yoga, and its bliss is yogānanda.
(iii) The name is also interpreted through sleep, Yogānanda Nṛsiṃha, and alternate readings such as Ayogā and Nandā.
searchText: 656 योगानन्दा योगस्य आनन्दः यस्याः सा। निद्रायाः आनन्दप्रधानत्वात् योगनिद्रा। योगानन्दः नृसिंहः तद्रूपा। /अयोगा। योगः सङ्गः न विद्यते यस्याः सा। अयेन शुभावहविधिना उं परशिवं गच्छतीति। /नन्दा। अलकनन्दात्मकगङ्गारूपा। नन्दजा नन्दा मूर्तिविशेषरूपा। योगस्य — of yoga आनन्दः — bliss यस्याः — of whom; whose योगः — yoga; union विद्यते — exists (i) one who is the bliss obtained through yoga. (ii) the union of śiva and śakti is yoga, and its bliss is yogānanda. (iii) the name is also interpreted through sleep, yogānanda nṛsiṃha, and alternate readings such as ayogā and nandā. bhāskararāya unpacks the immense layers built into these paths: the bliss of yoga: as yogānandā, she is the ecstatic bliss experienced when the mind enters perfect stillness (samādhi), or identical to the cosmic rest state yoga-nidrā. the unconnected absolute: as ayogā, she possesses no binding contact (saṅga) with material mutations. alternatively, she leads practitioners to lord śiva through pure auspicious pathways (ayena). the source of delight: as nandā, she manifests as the cleansing current of the gaṅgā or the ancient protective structural deity born in the nanda lineage.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya unpacks the immense layers built into these paths:
The Bliss of Yoga: As Yogānandā, She is the ecstatic bliss experienced when the mind enters perfect stillness (Samādhi), or identical to the cosmic rest state Yoga-nidrā.
The Unconnected Absolute: As Ayogā, She possesses no binding contact (saṅga) with material mutations. Alternatively, She leads practitioners to Lord Śiva through pure auspicious pathways (ayena).
The Source of Delight: As Nandā, She manifests as the cleansing current of the Gaṅgā or the ancient protective structural deity born in the Nanda lineage.

name: युगन्धरा
summary: (i) One who supports the yuga or era.
(ii) Yuga has several meanings, including an era, a pair, a yoke and a measure.
(iii) The Kr̥ta and other yugas are supported only by the Śiva-Śakti form; therefore She is Yugandharā.
searchText: 657 युगन्धरा युगं धारयतीति। युगं — era; yuga (i) one who supports the yuga or era. (ii) yuga has several meanings, including an era, a pair, a yoke and a measure. (iii) the kr̥ta and other yugas are supported only by the śiva-śakti form; therefore she is yugandharā. in the saubhāgyabhāskara, bhāskararāya details her control over linear macrocosmic time: the upholder of time cycles: the universe passes through the massive cycles of the four eras (yugas — satya, tretā, dvāpara, and kali). she is yugandharā because she functions as the absolute background axis that bears the weight of these epochal shifts, ensuring the continuity of cosmic balance across vast expanses of time.
esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara, Bhāskararāya details Her control over linear macrocosmic time:

The Upholder of Time Cycles: The universe passes through the massive cycles of the four eras (yugas — Satya, Tretā, Dvāpara, and Kali). She is Yugandharā because She functions as the absolute background axis that bears the weight of these epochal shifts, ensuring the continuity of cosmic balance across vast expanses of time.

name: इच्छाशक्तिज्ञानशक्तिक्रियाशक्तिस्वरूपिणी
wordByWord: Word meanings are read directly from the samāsa expression above.
summary: (i) One who is in the form of the three powers: desire, knowledge and action.
(ii) Every task needs icchā-śakti, jñāna-śakti and kriyā-śakti, and all three are Śrīdevī.
(iii) These three powers are also seen as the forms behind creation, protection and destruction.
searchText: 658 इच्छाशक्तिज्ञानशक्तिक्रियाशक्तिस्वरूपिणी इच्छाज्ञानक्रियारूपगुणत्रयं स्वरूपमस्याः अस्तीति। word meanings are read directly from the samāsa expression above. (i) one who is in the form of the three powers: desire, knowledge and action. (ii) every task needs icchā-śakti, jñāna-śakti and kriyā-śakti, and all three are śrīdevī. (iii) these three powers are also seen as the forms behind creation, protection and destruction. bhāskararāya details the primary engine behind all cosmic manifestation and physics: the triadic force matrix: she is the absolute consolidation of the three baseline functional energies that project the universe. as इच्छाशक्ति (icchā-śakti), she provides the initial divine intent to create. as ज्ञानशक्ति (jñāna-śakti), she maintains the intellectual blueprint of design. as क्रियाशक्ति (kriyā-śakti), she executes the actual kinetic movement into dense material elements, proving her status as the full dynamic expression of reality.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya details the primary engine behind all cosmic manifestation and physics:

The Triadic Force Matrix: She is the absolute consolidation of the three baseline functional energies that project the universe. As इच्छाशक्ति (Icchā-śakti), She provides the initial divine intent to create. As ज्ञानशक्ति (Jñāna-śakti), She maintains the intellectual blueprint of design. As क्रियाशक्ति (Kriyā-śakti), She executes the actual kinetic movement into dense material elements, proving Her status as the full dynamic expression of reality.

name: सर्वाधारा
summary: (i) One who supports all.
(ii) The three powers mentioned above are the support of everything happening in the world.
(iii) She has all supports as Her form and is the underlying basis of the universe.
searchText: 659 सर्वाधारा अन्तर्यामित्वात् सर्वमाधारः यस्याः सा। सर्वेषाम् आधारा। यस्याः — of whom; whose सर्वेषाम् — of all (i) one who supports all. (ii) the three powers mentioned above are the support of everything happening in the world. (iii) she has all supports as her form and is the underlying basis of the universe. bhāskararāya clarifies her position as the absolute container of space: the inner controller: she does not simply support things externally. as the omnipresent अन्तर्यामी (antaryāmī — inner controller), she permeates the inside and outside of every atom. the entire cosmos rests upon her fabric, drawing its space, existence, and stability directly from her baseline field.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya clarifies Her position as the absolute container of space:

The Inner Controller: She does not simply support things externally. As the omnipresent अन्तर्यामी (Antaryāmī — inner controller), She permeates the inside and outside of every atom. The entire cosmos rests upon Her fabric, drawing its space, existence, and stability directly from Her baseline field.

name: सुप्रतिष्ठा
summary: (i) One who is firmly and auspiciously established.
(ii) She is established as the support of the universe and gives auspiciousness to all.
(iii) Supratiṣṭhā is also a metrical name, and She is of that form as well.
searchText: 660 सुप्रतिष्ठा जगतः शोभना प्रतिष्ठा अधिष्ठानम्। जगतः — of the world प्रतिष्ठा — foundation; establishment (i) one who is firmly and auspiciously established. (ii) she is established as the support of the universe and gives auspiciousness to all. (iii) supratiṣṭhā is also a metrical name, and she is of that form as well. in the saubhāgyabhāskara, bhāskararāya anchors her as the ultimate reality: the immutable substratum: material objects shift and collapse because they lack a permanent foundation. she is supratiṣṭhā because she is the perfectly established, unmoving, and absolute base (adhibhṭhāna) of the entire universe. she remains constant across all cosmic transitions, providing the secure anchor for existence.
esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara, Bhāskararāya anchors Her as the ultimate reality:

The Immutable Substratum: Material objects shift and collapse because they lack a permanent foundation. She is Supratiṣṭhā because She is the perfectly established, unmoving, and absolute base (adhibhṭhāna) of the entire universe. She remains constant across all cosmic transitions, providing the secure anchor for existence.

name: सदसद्रूपधारिणी
wordByWord: Word meanings are read directly from the samāsa expression above.
summary: (i) One who assumes the form of being and non-being.
(ii) Sat is the eternal Brahman; asat is the universe which appears and changes.
(iii) From Her standpoint both are resolved in the one reality of Śrīdevī.
searchText: 661 सदसद्रूपधारिणी सदसद्र्रूपे धारयतीति। word meanings are read directly from the samāsa expression above. (i) one who assumes the form of being and non-being. (ii) sat is the eternal brahman; asat is the universe which appears and changes. (iii) from her standpoint both are resolved in the one reality of śrīdevī. bhāskararāya details a profound, non-dual synthesis of ontology: the encompasser of reality and potential: सत् (sat) represents the visible, tangible, and currently manifested world of objects. असत् (asat) represents the hidden, unmanifested causal potentials. she is sadasadrūpadāriṇī because both conditions are waves of her own dynamic energy. she holds the visible universe and the dark unmanifested vacuum together in absolute synchronization.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya details a profound, non-dual synthesis of ontology:

The Encompasser of Reality and Potential: सत् (Sat) represents the visible, tangible, and currently manifested world of objects. असत् (Asat) represents the hidden, unmanifested causal potentials. She is Sadasadrūpadāriṇī because both conditions are waves of Her own dynamic energy. She holds the visible universe and the dark unmanifested vacuum together in absolute synchronization.

name: अष्टमूर्तिः
samasa: लक्ष्मीर्मेधा धरा पुष्टिर्गौरी तुष्टिः प्रभा धृतिः सरस्वती चेति अष्टौ मूर्तयो यस्याः सा।
जीवात्मा अन्तरात्मा परमात्मा निर्मलः शुद्धात्मा ज्ञानरूपात्मा महात्मा भूतात्मा चेति अष्टौ मूर्तयो यस्याः सा।
पञ्चमहाभूतानि सूर्याचन्द्रमसौ जीवात्मस्वर्गदीक्षितानामन्यतम एक इत्यष्टौ मूर्तयो यस्याः सा।
summary: (i) One who has eight forms.
(ii) The eight forms are explained in many ways: eight mothers, eight Vāgdevatās, eight forms of Śiva, eight parts of nature and other traditional groups.
(iii) Thus many octads are treated as Her own forms.
searchText: 662 अष्टमूर्तिः लक्ष्मीर्मेधा धरा पुष्टिर्गौरी तुष्टिः प्रभा धृतिः सरस्वती चेति अष्टौ मूर्तयो यस्याः सा। जीवात्मा अन्तरात्मा परमात्मा निर्मलः शुद्धात्मा ज्ञानरूपात्मा महात्मा भूतात्मा चेति अष्टौ मूर्तयो यस्याः सा। पञ्चमहाभूतानि सूर्याचन्द्रमसौ जीवात्मस्वर्गदीक्षितानामन्यतम एक इत्यष्टौ मूर्तयो यस्याः सा। अष्टौ — eight यस्याः — of whom; whose (i) one who has eight forms. (ii) the eight forms are explained in many ways: eight mothers, eight vāgdevatās, eight forms of śiva, eight parts of nature and other traditional groups. (iii) thus many octads are treated as her own forms. bhāskararāya unpacks three distinct traditional frameworks mapping her macrocosmic and psychological design: the eight divine attributes: she manifests as the eight corporate energies of well-being (lakṣmī, medhā, etc.). the eight strata of soul: she governs the eight layers of internal consciousness running from the individual soul (jīvātmā) up to the absolute paramātmā. the shiva-anatomy grid: she is identical with the aṣṭamūrti layout of lord śiva, where the body consists of the five base elements (earth, water, fire, air, space) along with the sun, the moon, and the conscious observer (jīva), proving her total immanence in nature.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya unpacks three distinct traditional frameworks mapping Her macrocosmic and psychological design:
The Eight Divine Attributes: She manifests as the eight corporate energies of well-being (Lakṣmī, Medhā, etc.).
The Eight Strata of Soul: She governs the eight layers of internal consciousness running from the individual soul (Jīvātmā) up to the absolute Paramātmā.

The Shiva-Anatomy Grid: She is identical with the Aṣṭamūrti layout of Lord Śiva, where the body consists of the five base elements (earth, water, fire, air, space) along with the Sun, the Moon, and the conscious observer (Jīva), proving Her total immanence in nature.

name: अजाजैत्री
wordByWord: Word meanings are read directly from the samāsa expression above.
summary: (i) One who conquers ajā, meaning avidyā or ignorance.
(ii) She is knowledge itself and enables devotees to conquer ignorance.
(iii) The name therefore indicates the victory of wisdom over avidyā.
searchText: 663 अजाजैत्री अविद्यारूपायाः अजायाः जैत्री। word meanings are read directly from the samāsa expression above. (i) one who conquers ajā, meaning avidyā or ignorance. (ii) she is knowledge itself and enables devotees to conquer ignorance. (iii) the name therefore indicates the victory of wisdom over avidyā. in the saubhāgyabhāskara, bhāskararāya details the mechanics of final illumination: conquering the primordial veil: अजा (ajā) is a classical scriptural code for अविद्या (avidyā — primordial ignorance or cosmic illusion), which has no birth date and veils the true self. she is ajājaitrī because her supreme non-dual light forcefully conquers and dismantles this ancient illusion, dissolving the veil to reveal the pristine truth of brahman.
esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara, Bhāskararāya details the mechanics of final illumination:

Conquering the Primordial Veil: अजा (Ajā) is a classical scriptural code for अविद्या (Avidyā — primordial ignorance or cosmic illusion), which has no birth date and veils the true Self. She is Ajājaitrī because Her supreme non-dual light forcefully conquers and dismantles this ancient illusion, dissolving the veil to reveal the pristine truth of Brahman.

name: लोकयात्राविधायिनी
samasa: लोकानां चतुर्दशसङ्ख्यानां यात्रां (प्रलयं संरक्षणं वा) विधातुं शीलमस्याः अस्तीति।
summary: (i) One who directs the continuous course of the worlds.
(ii) The world is constantly arising, continuing and ending in countless individual forms.
(iii) She has the power to regulate this cyclic travel of the universe.
searchText: 664 लोकयात्राविधायिनी लोकानां चतुर्दशसङ्ख्यानां यात्रां (प्रलयं संरक्षणं वा) विधातुं शीलमस्याः अस्तीति। लोकानां — of worlds यात्रां — course; movement (i) one who directs the continuous course of the worlds. (ii) the world is constantly arising, continuing and ending in countless individual forms. (iii) she has the power to regulate this cyclic travel of the universe. bhāskararāya details her administrative management over the multi-dimensional realms: the conductor of the cosmic journey: लोकयात्रा refers to the continuous operational journey, evolutionary progression, and historical transitions of the fourteen structural realms of creation. she acts as the master director who organizes these cycles, ensuring that the laws of karma, natural physics, and spiritual preservation run smoothly across all planes.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya details Her administrative management over the multi-dimensional realms:

The Conductor of the Cosmic Journey: लोकयात्रा refers to the continuous operational journey, evolutionary progression, and historical transitions of the fourteen structural realms of creation. She acts as the master director who organizes these cycles, ensuring that the laws of karma, natural physics, and spiritual preservation run smoothly across all planes.

name: एकाकिनी
wordByWord: द्वितीयराहित्यात् — because of absence of a second
summary: (i) One who is alone.
(ii) She has no second and needs no support. The name Kevalā may be compared.
(iii) The Upaniṣadic teaching 'ekam eva advitīyam' is recalled: She alone is the non-dual truth.
searchText: 665 एकाकिनी द्वितीयराहित्यात् एका (५.३.५२ एकादाकिनिच्चासहाये)। द्वितीयराहित्यात् — because of absence of a second एका — one; alone (i) one who is alone. (ii) she has no second and needs no support. the name kevalā may be compared. (iii) the upaniṣadic teaching 'ekam eva advitīyam' is recalled: she alone is the non-dual truth. bhāskararāya defines her absolute non-dual foundation: the matchless absolute: she is ekākinī because she stands entirely alone in her ultimate essential nature, completely devoid of any second entity (dvitīya-rāhitā). before creation and after dissolution, she shines as the singular, non-dual baseline awareness. there are no neighbors, no rivals, and no companions competing with her absolute status.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya defines Her absolute non-dual foundation:

The Matchless Absolute: She is Ekākinī because She stands entirely alone in Her ultimate essential nature, completely devoid of any second entity (dvitīya-rāhitā). Before creation and after dissolution, She shines as the singular, non-dual baseline awareness. There are no neighbors, no rivals, and no companions competing with Her absolute status.

name: भूमरूपा
summary: (i) One who is the aggregate of all existing things.
(ii) The word Bhūmā means Brahman. She is in the form of Brahman. Bhūmā also means the many; though She was described as one in the previous name, here She is described as many.
(iii) What is seen as one Brahman in the supreme state appears as many in the nature of this universe. Many forms of Śrīdevī are described in the Devī Purāṇa.
searchText: 666 भूमरूपा ब्रह्मरूपा। एकाकिनी अपि बहुरूपा। एकाकिनी — alone अपि — also; even (i) one who is the aggregate of all existing things. (ii) the word bhūmā means brahman. she is in the form of brahman. bhūmā also means the many; though she was described as one in the previous name, here she is described as many. (iii) what is seen as one brahman in the supreme state appears as many in the nature of this universe. many forms of śrīdevī are described in the devī purāṇa. in the saubhāgyabhāskara, bhāskararāya anchors this directly in the profound declarations of the chāndogya upaniṣad (bhūma-vidyā): the infinite vastness: the upaniṣad declares: यो वै भूमा तत्सुखं नाल्पे सुखमस्ति ("the infinite vastness alone is bliss; there is no joy in the finite"). she is that bhūmā — the shoreless, unconditioned, and all-encompassìng absolute reality. bhāskararāya notes the sublime paradox: though she is completely alone (ekākinī), her infinite vastness encompasses every single form in existence.
esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara, Bhāskararāya anchors this directly in the profound declarations of the Chāndogya Upaniṣad (Bhūma-vidyā):

The Infinite Vastness: The Upaniṣad declares: यो वै भूमा तत्सुखं नाल्पे सुखमस्ति ("The infinite vastness alone is bliss; there is no joy in the finite"). She is that Bhūmā — the shoreless, unconditioned, and all-encompassìng absolute reality. Bhāskararāya notes the sublime paradox: though She is completely alone (Ekākinī), Her infinite vastness encompasses every single form in existence.

name: निर्द्वैता
wordByWord: Word meanings are read directly from the samāsa expression above.
summary: (i) One who is non-dual, without duality.
(ii) Dvaita is the feeling that another exists in addition to the Self. She does not have such duality.

(iii) When the soul is thought of as different from the Supreme Being, that is duality. When this is gone, only Brahman indicated by Ekākinī and Kevalā remains; such is Śrīdevī, or She endows that state.
searchText: 667 निर्द्वैता निर्गतं द्वैतं यस्यां सा। word meanings are read directly from the samāsa expression above. (i) one who is non-dual, without duality. (ii) dvaita is the feeling that another exists in addition to the self. she does not have such duality. (iii) when the soul is thought of as different from the supreme being, that is duality. when this is gone, only brahman indicated by ekākinī and kevalā remains; such is śrīdevī, or she endows that state. bhāskararāya targets the ultimate removal of division: the non-dual screen: duality (dvaita) is the root cause of confusion, fear, and moral judgment. she is nirdvaitā because her essential nature contains no internal cleavages, no splits, and no subject-object distinctions. she is the unified screen upon which the appearance of duality is projected, remaining unblemished.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya targets the ultimate removal of division:

The Non-Dual Screen: Duality (dvaita) is the root cause of confusion, fear, and moral judgment. She is Nirdvaitā because Her essential nature contains no internal cleavages, no splits, and no subject-object distinctions. She is the unified screen upon which the appearance of duality is projected, remaining unblemished.

name: द्वैतवर्जिता
summary: (i) One who is free from the state of duality.
(ii) This name stresses more clearly the message of the previous name.
(iii) The state of duality is unreal. At no point of time is there anything other than Śrīdevī, who is the form of Brahman.
searchText: 668 द्वैतवर्जिता द्वैतेन वर्जिता। द्वैतेन — by duality (i) one who is free from the state of duality. (ii) this name stresses more clearly the message of the previous name. (iii) the state of duality is unreal. at no point of time is there anything other than śrīdevī, who is the form of brahman. bhāskararāya reinforces this status to clear any remaining subtle dualistic doubts: exclusion of separation: by placing nirdvaitā and dvaitavarjitā side-by-side, the text eliminates any misconception that duality has a real, independent foothold in reality. she thoroughly rejects and stands free from the boundaries of separation, anchoring the practitioner firmly in non-dual absorption.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya reinforces this status to clear any remaining subtle dualistic doubts:

Exclusion of Separation: By placing Nirdvaitā and Dvaitavarjitā side-by-side, the text eliminates any misconception that duality has a real, independent foothold in reality. She thoroughly rejects and stands free from the boundaries of separation, anchoring the practitioner firmly in non-dual absorption.

name: अन्नदा
wordByWord: Word meanings are read directly from the samāsa expression above.
summary: (i) One who gives food to the entire universe.
(ii) As the Mother of the universe, She protects the universe by giving food. In this function She is Annapūrṇā, holding a vessel full of food and a ladle.
(iii) Before taking food, if one prays to Her and thinks that Śrīdevī alone has given the food, the blemish of food will not affect one and strength, knowledge and zeal will be gained.
searchText: 669 अन्नदा अन्नं ददातीति। word meanings are read directly from the samāsa expression above. (i) one who gives food to the entire universe. (ii) as the mother of the universe, she protects the universe by giving food. in this function she is annapūrṇā, holding a vessel full of food and a ladle. (iii) before taking food, if one prays to her and thinks that śrīdevī alone has given the food, the blemish of food will not affect one and strength, knowledge and zeal will be gained. in the saubhāgyabhāskara, bhāskararāya details her nutritional and material support: the giver of nourishment: she is the cosmic mother who provides the physical food (anna) needed to maintain the biological frameworks of all living beings. bhāskararāya notes an advanced upanishadic track: अन्न represents that which is consumed and that which consumes. she is the material fuel that feeds the physical sheet (annamaya-kośa), keeping life viable so that spiritual growth can occur.
esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara, Bhāskararāya details Her nutritional and material support:

The Giver of Nourishment: She is the cosmic mother who provides the physical food (Anna) needed to maintain the biological frameworks of all living beings. Bhāskararāya notes an advanced Upanishadic track: अन्न represents that which is consumed and that which consumes. She is the material fuel that feeds the physical sheet (Annamaya-kośa), keeping life viable so that spiritual growth can occur.

name: वसुदा
summary: (i) One who gives wealth to all.
(ii) Vasu has many meanings, such as wealth, gem and gold; She gives all these.
(iii) The essence of this name is the feeling that all enjoyable wealth obtained in this world is due only to Her blessings. The Veda also speaks of annam and vasu in this connection.
searchText: 670 वसुदा वसु धनं रत्नं च ददातीति। वसु — wealth; treasure (i) one who gives wealth to all. (ii) vasu has many meanings, such as wealth, gem and gold; she gives all these. (iii) the essence of this name is the feeling that all enjoyable wealth obtained in this world is due only to her blessings. the veda also speaks of annam and vasu in this connection. bhāskararāya explores this across material and structural solar frameworks: the dispenser of riches: वसु (vasu) refers to gold, physical wealth, and precious gems. she dispenses these assets to maintain societal balance. the ray matrix: alternatively, vasu signifies the vital light rays projected by the sun and the specific group of foundational atmospheric deities (vasus). she gives this light energy to sustain planetary vitality.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya explores this across material and structural solar frameworks:
The Dispenser of Riches: वसु (Vasu) refers to gold, physical wealth, and precious gems. She dispenses these assets to maintain societal balance.

The Ray Matrix: Alternatively, Vasu signifies the vital light rays projected by the sun and the specific group of foundational atmospheric deities (Vasus). She gives this light energy to sustain planetary vitality.

name: वृद्धा
summary: (i) One who is senior in age.
(ii) Since She exists before everything else, She is the most ancient and the oldest.
(iii) She may also be understood as the completely grown and matured world, or as one who nourishes the world.
searchText: 671 वृद्धा सर्वज्येष्ठत्वात्। वर्धयति जगत् इति। वर्धयति — nourishes; increases जगत् — world (i) one who is senior in age. (ii) since she exists before everything else, she is the most ancient and the oldest. (iii) she may also be understood as the completely grown and matured world, or as one who nourishes the world. bhāskararāya clears this name of any connotations of physical biological aging: the primordial eldest: she is vṛddhā because she possesses absolute temporal priority over everything (सर्वज्येष्ठत्वात्). she exists before the oldest ancestors, the first elements, and the creator deities. concurrently, the root signifies her active role in causing the expansion and growth (vardhayati) of the universe from its seed state.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya clears this name of any connotations of physical biological aging:

The Primordial Eldest: She is Vṛddhā because She possesses absolute temporal priority over everything (सर्वज्येष्ठत्वात्). She exists before the oldest ancestors, the first elements, and the creator deities. Concurrently, the root signifies Her active role in causing the expansion and growth (vardhayati) of the universe from its seed state.

name: ब्रह्मात्मैक्यस्वरूपिणी
samasa: ब्रह्मणा चैतन्येन सह आत्मनां जीवानामैक्यमेव स्वं निजं रूपमास्याः अस्तीति।
summary: (i) One who is in the form of the unity of Brahman and Ātman.
(ii) Mahāvākyas such as Aham Brahmāsmi and mantras like Soham indicate that Brahman is to be realised in the breath of every soul. That is the real form of Śrīdevī.
(iii) The Haṃsa mantra unites the soul with Śiva. The natural breath itself becomes ajapa mantra, and this meaning is also described in the Triśatī name Haṃsamantrārtha-rūpiṇī.
searchText: 672 ब्रह्मात्मैक्यस्वरूपिणी ब्रह्मणा चैतन्येन सह आत्मनां जीवानामैक्यमेव स्वं निजं रूपमास्याः अस्तीति। ब्रह्मणा — with brahman चैतन्येन — with consciousness (i) one who is in the form of the unity of brahman and ātman. (ii) mahāvākyas such as aham brahmāsmi and mantras like soham indicate that brahman is to be realised in the breath of every soul. that is the real form of śrīdevī. (iii) the haṃsa mantra unites the soul with śiva. the natural breath itself becomes ajapa mantra, and this meaning is also described in the triśatī name haṃsamantrārtha-rūpiṇī. in the saubhāgyabhāskara, bhāskararāya highlights this name as the absolute philosophical zenith of the entire text: the formula of liberation: this name states the ultimate core truth of advaita vedānta and high-tier śrīvidyā. she is not an external ruler to be feared; she is the very realization of the absolute identity (aikya) between the seeker's core identity (ātman) and the universal absolute (brahman). she is the experience of that non-dual bridge, vanishing the illusion of separation forever.
esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara, Bhāskararāya highlights this name as the absolute philosophical zenith of the entire text:

The Formula of Liberation: This name states the ultimate core truth of Advaita Vedānta and high-tier Śrīvidyā. She is not an external ruler to be feared; She is the very realization of the absolute identity (aikya) between the seeker's core identity (Ātman) and the universal absolute (Brahman). She is the experience of that non-dual bridge, vanishing the illusion of separation forever.

name: बृहती
wordByWord: Word meanings are read directly from the samāsa expression above.
summary: (i) One whose form is immensely great.
(ii) The Veda says Mahato Mahīyān — greater than the greatest.
(iii) Bṛhat-sāman occurs in the Sāma Veda, and a metre of thirty-six letters is called Bṛhatī. She is in those forms also.
searchText: 673 बृहती महतो महीयत्वाद्बृहती। word meanings are read directly from the samāsa expression above. (i) one whose form is immensely great. (ii) the veda says mahato mahīyān — greater than the greatest. (iii) bṛhat-sāman occurs in the sāma veda, and a metre of thirty-six letters is called bṛhatī. she is in those forms also. bhāskararāya anchors this in the upanishadic metrics of scale: the boundless expansion: she is bṛhatī because her scale surpasses the highest imaginable metric of greatness (महतो महीयत्वात्). she cannot be contained by any boundary line, expanding infinitely beyond cosmic horizons, serving as the vast space within which multiverses float.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya anchors this in the Upanishadic metrics of scale:

The Boundless Expansion: She is Bṛhatī because Her scale surpasses the highest imaginable metric of greatness (महतो महीयत्वात्). She cannot be contained by any boundary line, expanding infinitely beyond cosmic horizons, serving as the vast space within which multiverses float.

name: ब्राह्मणी
summary: (i) One who is in the form of a Brāhmin lady.
(ii) The word Brāhmaṇī has several meanings, including a Brāhmin lady, a plant and wisdom. She is of all these forms.
(iii) Since Śiva, Her consort, is spoken of as Brāhmaṇa and the deity of Brāhmaṇas, She is Brāhmaṇī.
searchText: 674 ब्राह्मणी द्विजस्त्रीमात्ररूपा। संविद्विशेषरूपा। शिवस्य ब्राह्मणत्वजातिमत्त्वाद्ब्राह्मणी। शिवस्य — of śiva (i) one who is in the form of a brāhmin lady. (ii) the word brāhmaṇī has several meanings, including a brāhmin lady, a plant and wisdom. she is of all these forms. (iii) since śiva, her consort, is spoken of as brāhmaṇa and the deity of brāhmaṇas, she is brāhmaṇī. bhāskararāya details the alignment with pure, uncorrupted knowledge: the consort of the knower: lord śiva represents the ultimate, pristine repository of vedic wisdom—the archetypal brahman. she is his non-dual dynamic energy (brāhmaṇī). concurrently, she represents the refined, highly specialized state of pure internal awareness (saṁvit-viśeṣa-rūpā) that marks an illumined mind.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya details the alignment with pure, uncorrupted knowledge:

The Consort of the Knower: Lord Śiva represents the ultimate, pristine repository of Vedic wisdom—the archetypal Brahman. She is His non-dual dynamic energy (Brāhmaṇī). Concurrently, She represents the refined, highly specialized state of pure internal awareness (Saṁvit-viśeṣa-rūpā) that marks an illumined mind.

name: ब्राह्मी
samasa: अविद्यातिरिक्तजडजातिसद्भावे मानाभाव इत्यत आह ब्राह्मी। (ब्राह्मो जाताविति निपातनात्साधुः)।
wordByWord: Word meanings are read directly from the samāsa expression above.
summary: (i) One who is in the form of Sarasvatī, the consort of Brahmā.
(ii) She is in the form of speech and may be understood as Vāgīśvarī.
(iii) She is also the senior-most among the eight mothers, shining with the swan, japa-mālā and kamaṇḍalu.
searchText: 675 ब्राह्मी अविद्यातिरिक्तजडजातिसद्भावे मानाभाव इत्यत आह ब्राह्मी। (ब्राह्मो जाताविति निपातनात्साधुः)। वागात्मिका। word meanings are read directly from the samāsa expression above. (i) one who is in the form of sarasvatī, the consort of brahmā. (ii) she is in the form of speech and may be understood as vāgīśvarī. (iii) she is also the senior-most among the eight mothers, shining with the swan, japa-mālā and kamaṇḍalu. in the saubhāgyabhāskara, bhāskararāya provides a rigorous logical and mantric insight: the non-dual substance: because there is no valid proof (mānābhāva) for the existence of any independent material matter separate from the absolute, the entire universe is fundamentally nothing but brahman. she is brāhmī — the expression of this non-dual reality. concurrently, she is the power of articulation (vāg-ātmikā) that animates creative sound.
esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara, Bhāskararāya provides a rigorous logical and mantric insight:

The Non-Dual Substance: Because there is no valid proof (mānābhāva) for the existence of any independent material matter separate from the absolute, the entire universe is fundamentally nothing but Brahman. She is Brāhmī — the expression of this non-dual reality. Concurrently, She is the power of articulation (Vāg-ātmikā) that animates creative sound.

name: ब्रह्मानन्दा
summary: (i) One who is in the form of supreme bliss.
(ii) She has Brahman itself as Her bliss.
(iii) The form of Śrīdevī is described as frozen or condensed bliss, just as Śrī Kṛṣṇa is described as one whose body is supreme bliss.
searchText: 676 ब्रह्मानन्दा ब्रह्मैवानन्दो यस्याः सगुणायाः। यस्याः — of whom; whose (i) one who is in the form of supreme bliss. (ii) she has brahman itself as her bliss. (iii) the form of śrīdevī is described as frozen or condensed bliss, just as śrī kṛṣṇa is described as one whose body is supreme bliss. bhāskararāya defines the quality of her joy: the bliss of the absolute: she is the living embodiment of that supreme, unconditioned happiness (ānanda) that defines brahman. when she assumes a qualified form (saguṇā) for the sake of the devotee, she radiates this unadulterated cosmic joy, lifting the practitioner out of earthly grief.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya defines the quality of Her joy:

The Bliss of the Absolute: She is the living embodiment of that supreme, unconditioned happiness (Ānanda) that defines Brahman. When She assumes a qualified form (Saguṇā) for the sake of the devotee, She radiates this unadulterated cosmic joy, lifting the practitioner out of earthly grief.

name: बलिप्रिया
samasa: बलिनो विद्यानिराससमर्थाः कामादिशत्रुजेतारः प्रिया दयापात्रं यस्याः सा।
बलिनामको राजा प्रियो यस्य वामनस्य तदभिन्ना।
summary: (i) One who is delighted with the mighty.
(ii) Dhīras or balis are those who overcome ignorance and the enemies such as desire. She is fond of them and blesses them.
(iii) The name also recalls Viṣṇu's compassion for king Bali in the Vāmana incarnation, and also the bali offerings made during worship, which She accepts with joy.
searchText: 677 बलिप्रिया बलिनो विद्यानिराससमर्थाः कामादिशत्रुजेतारः प्रिया दयापात्रं यस्याः सा। बलिनामको राजा प्रियो यस्य वामनस्य तदभिन्ना। पूजोपहाराः प्रिया यस्याः सा। विद्यानिराससमर्थाः — able to remove ignorance प्रिया — dear यस्याः — of whom; whose पूजोपहाराः — worship offerings (i) one who is delighted with the mighty. (ii) dhīras or balis are those who overcome ignorance and the enemies such as desire. she is fond of them and blesses them. (iii) the name also recalls viṣṇu's compassion for king bali in the vāmana incarnation, and also the bali offerings made during worship, which she accepts with joy. bhāskararāya decodes these three functional paths: the love for spiritual warriors: बली refers to those strong, heroic practitioners who have conquered their internal enemies—lust, anger, and greed (kāmādi-śatru-jetāraḥ). they are her favorite children. ritual and mythological mappings: she accepts pure ritual offerings (upahāra) surrendered with devotion, and is non-different from the pervasive viṣṇu form that blessed king bali.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya decodes these three functional paths:

The Love for Spiritual Warriors: बली refers to those strong, heroic practitioners who have conquered their internal enemies—lust, anger, and greed (kāmādi-śatru-jetāraḥ). They are Her favorite children.
Ritual and Mythological Mappings: She accepts pure ritual offerings (upahāra) surrendered with devotion, and is non-different from the pervasive Viṣṇu form that blessed King Bali.

name: भाषारूपा
summary: (i) One who is the form of languages.
(ii) Since She is the head of speech, all languages are Her form.
(iii) Her form is identified through languages; She can be recognised by speech.
searchText: 678 भाषारूपा संस्कृतप्राकृतादिभाषा रूपं यस्याः सा। भाषाभिर्निरूप्यत इति। यस्याः — of whom; whose (i) one who is the form of languages. (ii) since she is the head of speech, all languages are her form. (iii) her form is identified through languages; she can be recognised by speech. in the saubhāgyabhāskara, bhāskararāya details her presence across all linguistic systems: the matrix of articulation: she manifests directly as the structural architecture of language—including classical sanskrit, natural prākṛt, and every linguistic dialect spoken across the planetary grid. every word uttered is her voice. concurrently, she is the ultimate reality targeted and defined (nirūpyate) by all sacred philosophical descriptions.
esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara, Bhāskararāya details Her presence across all linguistic systems:

The Matrix of Articulation: She manifests directly as the structural architecture of language—including classical Sanskrit, natural Prākṛt, and every linguistic dialect spoken across the planetary grid. Every word uttered is Her voice. Concurrently, She is the ultimate reality targeted and defined (nirūpyate) by all sacred philosophical descriptions.

name: बृहत्सेना
samasa: बृहती अपारा सेना चतुरङ्गबलं यस्याः सा।
summary: (i) One who has a mighty army.
(ii) Though She can perform creation, protection and destruction by Herself, She creates a vast army and completes Her tasks through it, giving share and pride to those who serve.
(iii) Bṛhatsenā is also the name of a certain kingly line; She is in that form too.
searchText: 679 बृहत्सेना बृहती अपारा सेना चतुरङ्गबलं यस्याः सा। बृहती — great; vast अपारा — boundless सेना — army यस्याः — of whom; whose (i) one who has a mighty army. (ii) though she can perform creation, protection and destruction by herself, she creates a vast army and completes her tasks through it, giving share and pride to those who serve. (iii) bṛhatsenā is also the name of a certain kingly line; she is in that form too. bhāskararāya details her unmatched defensive readiness: the boundless army of light: her "army" (senā) represents the infinite arrays of protective guardian energies, śaktis, and defensive rays that surround her throne. this boundless force stands perpetually mobilized to defend cosmic order and protect the practitioner from internal negative invasions.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya details Her unmatched defensive readiness:

The Boundless Army of Light: Her "army" (Senā) represents the infinite arrays of protective guardian energies, Śaktis, and defensive rays that surround Her throne. This boundless force stands perpetually mobilized to defend cosmic order and protect the practitioner from internal negative invasions.

name: भावाभावविवर्जिता
wordByWord: Word meanings are read directly from the samāsa expression above.
summary: (i) One who is beyond existence and non-existence.
(ii) Bhāva means existence and abhāva means non-existence. Everything in the world passes between these states.
(iii) Śrīdevī does not belong completely to either state; the Supreme Being is eternal.
searchText: 680 भावाभावविवर्जिता भावा द्रव्यगुणादयोऽभावाः प्रागभावादयस्तैरुभयैरपि विवर्जिता। word meanings are read directly from the samāsa expression above. (i) one who is beyond existence and non-existence. (ii) bhāva means existence and abhāva means non-existence. everything in the world passes between these states. (iii) śrīdevī does not belong completely to either state; the supreme being is eternal. bhāskararāya details her transcendence over all logical and material categories: beyond conceptual dialectics: in logical systems (nyāya/vaiśeṣika), the universe is divided into positive categories of being (bhāva — substance, quality, action) and states of negation (abhāva — prior non-existence, destruction). as the absolute transcendent consciousness, she stands completely outside this binary dualism. she is the baseline screen that precedes logical divisions,
esoteric: Bhāskararāya details Her transcendence over all logical and material categories:

Beyond Conceptual Dialectics: In logical systems (Nyāya/Vaiśeṣika), the universe is divided into positive categories of being (bhāva — substance, quality, action) and states of negation (abhāva — prior non-existence, destruction). As the absolute transcendent consciousness, She stands completely outside this binary dualism. She is the baseline screen that precedes logical divisions,

name: सुखाराध्या
summary: (i) One who can be worshipped comfortably, without much effort.
(ii) She can be worshipped without bodily pain such as fasting and without harsh restrictions on meditation.
(iii) To worship Her, it is enough to imagine any form in the mind as Śrīdevī with devotion. Hence Her worship is very easy.
searchText: 681 सुखाराध्या सुखेन (उपवासादिरूपकायक्लेशं ध्येयस्वरूपनियमनिर्बन्धादिकं चान्तरेण) आराध्या । सुखेन — comfortably आराध्या — worshipped (i) one who can be worshipped comfortably, without much effort. (ii) she can be worshipped without bodily pain such as fasting and without harsh restrictions on meditation. (iii) to worship her, it is enough to imagine any form in the mind as śrīdevī with devotion. hence her worship is very easy. in the saubhāgyabhāskara, bhāskararāya details the accessibility of her devotional path: worship free from physical friction: she does not require harsh physical mortification, extreme fasting (upavāsa), or torturous bodily postures (kāya-kleśa). she is sukhārādhyā because she can be worshipped with ease (sukhena), yielding her presence to sincere internal devotion and simple love rather than complex mechanical constraints.
esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara, Bhāskararāya details the accessibility of Her devotional path:

Worship Free from Physical Friction: She does not require harsh physical mortification, extreme fasting (upavāsa), or torturous bodily postures (kāya-kleśa). She is Sukhārādhyā because She can be worshipped with ease (sukhena), yielding Her presence to sincere internal devotion and simple love rather than complex mechanical constraints.

name: शुभकरी
wordByWord: Word meanings are read directly from the samāsa expression above.
summary: (i) One who grants auspiciousness to Her devotees.
(ii) After saying that She can be worshipped easily, this name removes the doubt that lack of fasting or strict observance may cause sin.
(iii) She bestows auspiciousness on Her devotees.
searchText: 682 शुभकरी शुभं पुण्यमेव करोति। word meanings are read directly from the samāsa expression above. (i) one who grants auspiciousness to her devotees. (ii) after saying that she can be worshipped easily, this name removes the doubt that lack of fasting or strict observance may cause sin. (iii) she bestows auspiciousness on her devotees. bhāskararāya defines her continuous generation of virtue: the engine of auspiciousness: she is śubhakarī because her continuous influence automatically transforms the seeker's mental environment, producing auspicious thoughts, moral purity (puṇya), and benign outer circumstances that support spiritual growth.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya defines Her continuous generation of virtue:

The Engine of Auspiciousness: She is Śubhakarī because Her continuous influence automatically transforms the seeker's mental environment, producing auspicious thoughts, moral purity (puṇya), and benign outer circumstances that support spiritual growth.

name: शोभनासुलभागतिः
samasa: मोक्षादिपुरुषार्थरूपत्वाच्छोभना। गम्यत इति गतिः फलम्। सुखोपास्यत्वात्सुलभा च गतिः प्राप्तव्यं स्थानम्।
wordByWord: Word meanings are read directly from the samāsa expression above.
summary: (i) One whose path is beautiful and easy to attain.
(ii) Gati means goal, path, destiny, knowledge or salvation. Śobhanā means auspicious and sulabhā means easily attainable.
(iii) The name indicates that even the difficult-to-attain freedom from rebirth becomes attainable by Her grace.
searchText: 683 शोभनासुलभागतिः मोक्षादिपुरुषार्थरूपत्वाच्छोभना। गम्यत इति गतिः फलम्। सुखोपास्यत्वात्सुलभा च गतिः प्राप्तव्यं स्थानम्। word meanings are read directly from the samāsa expression above. (i) one whose path is beautiful and easy to attain. (ii) gati means goal, path, destiny, knowledge or salvation. śobhanā means auspicious and sulabhā means easily attainable. (iii) the name indicates that even the difficult-to-attain freedom from rebirth becomes attainable by her grace. in the saubhāgyabhāskara, bhāskararāya reconciles the supreme value of liberation with its ease of attainment: the magnificent goal: her station (gati) represents the highest human aim (puruṣārtha)—liberation itself, making it शोभना (śobhanā — glorious). yet, because her path is grounded in grace rather than friction, this ultimate state remains सुलभा (sulabhā — easily reachable) for those who take refuge in her.
esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara, Bhāskararāya reconciles the supreme value of liberation with its ease of attainment:

The Magnificent Goal: Her station (gati) represents the highest human aim (puruṣārtha)—liberation itself, making it शोभना (śobhanā — glorious). Yet, because Her path is grounded in grace rather than friction, this ultimate state remains सुलभा (sulabhā — easily reachable) for those who take refuge in Her.

name: राजराजेश्वरी
wordByWord: Word meanings are read directly from the samāsa expression above.
summary: (i) One who is the ruler of the king of kings.
(ii) She is the head of the rulers of the eight directions and also of Brahmā, Viṣṇu and Śiva.
(iii) Kubera is also called Rājarāja; She is the ruler even of him.
searchText: 684 राजराजेश्वरी राज्ञां देवराजादीनां ये राजानो ब्रह्मविष्णुरुद्रास्तेषामपीश्वरी। राजराजस्य कुबेरस्येश्वरी। word meanings are read directly from the samāsa expression above. (i) one who is the ruler of the king of kings. (ii) she is the head of the rulers of the eight directions and also of brahmā, viṣṇu and śiva. (iii) kubera is also called rājarāja; she is the ruler even of him. bhāskararāya establishes her absolute imperial hierarchy over macrocosmic administration: sovereign over the trinity: secondary gods like indra rule the heavens, but their rulers are the primary trinity—brahmā, viṣṇu, and rudra. she is rājarājeśvarī because she sits as the supreme empress over this trinity itself. concurrently, she governs kubera (rājarāja), holding total command over all cosmic treasuries.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya establishes Her absolute imperial hierarchy over macrocosmic administration:

Sovereign over the Trinity: Secondary gods like Indra rule the heavens, but their rulers are the primary trinity—Brahmā, Viṣṇu, and Rudra. She is Rājarājeśvarī because She sits as the supreme empress over this trinity itself. Concurrently, She governs Kubera (Rājarāja), holding total command over all cosmic treasuries.

name: राज्यदायिनी
wordByWord: Word meanings are read directly from the samāsa expression above.
summary: (i) One who bestows dominion.
(ii) Rājya indicates liberation, Vaikuṇṭha or Kailāsa; She grants these.
(iii) The posts and roles of the directional lords and other rulers are also given by Her. Rājya is described in several forms such as vairājya, bhaujya and sāmrājya.
searchText: 685 राज्यदायिनी राज्यं स्वाराज्यवैकुण्ठकैलासाधिपत्यादिकं दातुं शीलमस्याः अस्तीति। word meanings are read directly from the samāsa expression above. (i) one who bestows dominion. (ii) rājya indicates liberation, vaikuṇṭha or kailāsa; she grants these. (iii) the posts and roles of the directional lords and other rulers are also given by her. rājya is described in several forms such as vairājya, bhaujya and sāmrājya. in the saubhāgyabhāskara, bhāskararāya expands the definition of "kingdom": the dispenser of heavenly realms: she does not merely grant temporal earthly kingdoms. she is rājyadāyinī because she dispenses spiritual sovereignty—including absolute self-rule (svārājya), access to the celestial realms of viṣṇu (vaikuṇṭha) and śiva (kailāsa), and ultimate dominion over one's internal senses.
esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara, Bhāskararāya expands the definition of "kingdom":

The Dispenser of Heavenly Realms: She does not merely grant temporal earthly kingdoms. She is Rājyadāyinī because She dispenses spiritual sovereignty—including absolute self-rule (svārājya), access to the celestial realms of Viṣṇu (Vaikuṇṭha) and Śiva (Kailāsa), and ultimate dominion over one's internal senses.

name: राज्यवल्लभा
summary: (i) One to whom dominions are dear.
(ii) The previous name indicates kingdoms and their rulers such as Brahmā, Indra and others. Śrīdevī has affection for all such kings.
(iii) It is said in Āryā Dviśatī that such kings wait in Śrīpura for the sight of Śrīdevī.
searchText: 686 राज्यवल्लभा राज्यानि वल्लभानि प्रियाणि यस्याः सा। वल्लभानि — dear; beloved यस्याः — of whom; whose (i) one to whom dominions are dear. (ii) the previous name indicates kingdoms and their rulers such as brahmā, indra and others. śrīdevī has affection for all such kings. (iii) it is said in āryā dviśatī that such kings wait in śrīpura for the sight of śrīdevī. bhāskararāya details her affection for structured governance and order: the guardian of sovereign order: she is rājyavallabhā because she delights in righteous governance, order, and the expanding sovereignty of her devotees. she protects organized, righteous realms (rājya) so that wisdom and dharma can flourish without chaotic disruption.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya details Her affection for structured governance and order:

The Guardian of Sovereign Order: She is Rājyavallabhā because She delights in righteous governance, order, and the expanding sovereignty of Her devotees. She protects organized, righteous realms (rājya) so that wisdom and Dharma can flourish without chaotic disruption.

name: राजत्कृपा
summary: (i) One whose compassion shines gloriously.
(ii) Since She is the form of a mother, She is full of compassion.
(iii) Her grace shines and protects the devotees.
searchText: 687 राजत्कृपा राजन्ती शोभमाना कृपा यस्याः सा। राजन्ती — shining कृपा — compassion यस्याः — of whom; whose (i) one whose compassion shines gloriously. (ii) since she is the form of a mother, she is full of compassion. (iii) her grace shines and protects the devotees. in the saubhāgyabhāskara, bhāskararāya highlights the visible brilliance of her grace: the radiant grace: her compassion (kṛpā) is not a hidden or passive sentiment; it is राजन्ती (rājantī — splendidly active and luminous). it shines out continuously to illuminate dark places in the seeker's mind, dispelling fear and granting immediate protection.
esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara, Bhāskararāya highlights the visible brilliance of Her grace:

The Radiant Grace: Her compassion (kṛpā) is not a hidden or passive sentiment; it is राजन्ती (rājantī — splendidly active and luminous). It shines out continuously to illuminate dark places in the seeker's mind, dispelling fear and granting immediate protection.

name: राजपीठनिवेशितनिजाश्रिता
samasa: राज्ञां नृपाणामिन्द्रादीनां च पीठेषु सिंहासनेषु निवेशिता निजा आश्रिता यया सा  ।
wordByWord: आश्रिता — those who have taken refuge
summary: (i) One who raises those who resort to Her to royal status.
(ii) She gives the posts of Indra and others to Her devotees; examples are known from stories such as Dhruva and Suratha.
(iii) True devotees do not accept even such posts as final; She grants them the still higher state of Brahman.
searchText: 688 राजपीठनिवेशितनिजाश्रिता राज्ञां नृपाणामिन्द्रादीनां च पीठेषु सिंहासनेषु निवेशिता निजा आश्रिता यया सा  । सिंहासनेषु — on thrones आश्रिता — those who have taken refuge सा — she (i) one who raises those who resort to her to royal status. (ii) she gives the posts of indra and others to her devotees; examples are known from stories such as dhruva and suratha. (iii) true devotees do not accept even such posts as final; she grants them the still higher state of brahman. bhāskararāya details her capacity to elevate the humble: the elevating power of grace: she takes those who seek refuge in her (nijāśritā) and installs them upon the thrones (rājapīṭha) of worldly governance, heavenly positions (like indra's seat), or spiritual mastery. her refuge transforms the spiritually destitute into sovereign masters over life.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya details Her capacity to elevate the humble:

The Elevating Power of Grace: She takes those who seek refuge in Her (nijāśritā) and installs them upon the thrones (rājapīṭha) of worldly governance, heavenly positions (like Indra's seat), or spiritual mastery. Her refuge transforms the spiritually destitute into sovereign masters over life.

name: राज्यलक्ष्मीः
wordByWord: Word meanings are read directly from the samāsa expression above.
summary: (i) One who is the goddess of sovereignty.
(ii) Rājyalakṣmī indicates the entire wealth of all kings and devas such as Indra.
(iii) She is that form, and the mantra of Rājyalakṣmī is described in Tantrarāja.
searchText: 689 राज्यलक्ष्मीः राज्याभिमानिनी लक्ष्मी। word meanings are read directly from the samāsa expression above. (i) one who is the goddess of sovereignty. (ii) rājyalakṣmī indicates the entire wealth of all kings and devas such as indra. (iii) she is that form, and the mantra of rājyalakṣmī is described in tantrarāja. in the saubhāgyabhāskara, bhāskararāya maps her as the vital splendor behind power: the splendor of rulership: power without grace leads to destruction. she is rājyalakṣmīḥ because she is the living fortune, majesty, and dignity (lakṣmī) that animates a kingdom. when her presence leaves a reign, its wealth and stability crumble; when she resides within it, prosperity flourishes.
esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara, Bhāskararāya maps Her as the vital splendor behind power:

The Splendor of Rulership: Power without grace leads to destruction. She is Rājyalakṣmīḥ because She is the living fortune, majesty, and dignity (lakṣmī) that animates a kingdom. When Her presence leaves a reign, its wealth and stability crumble; when She resides within it, prosperity flourishes.

name: कोशनाथा
samasa: कोशस्य भाण्डागारस्य दिव्यस्य अन्नमयादेर्वा नाथा स्वामिनी।
summary: (i) One who presides over treasure.
(ii) Kośa means a treasury; She is the head of all wealth.
(iii) Kośa also refers to the five sheaths of the body — annamaya, prāṇamaya, manomaya, vijñānamaya and ānandamaya. As their head, She blesses devotees with life, thought, knowledge and happiness.
searchText: 690 कोशनाथा कोशस्य भाण्डागारस्य दिव्यस्य अन्नमयादेर्वा नाथा स्वामिनी। कोशस्य — of treasury or sheath भाण्डागारस्य — of the treasury नाथा — mistress; lord (i) one who presides over treasure. (ii) kośa means a treasury; she is the head of all wealth. (iii) kośa also refers to the five sheaths of the body — annamaya, prāṇamaya, manomaya, vijñānamaya and ānandamaya. as their head, she blesses devotees with life, thought, knowledge and happiness. bhāskararāya unpacks both the material and internal vedāntic treasuries: ruler of material and subtle sheaths: materially, she is the master (nāthā) of all royal treasuries and stores (bhāṇḍāgāra). esoterically, kośa refers to the five sheaths of human existence (annamaya, prāṇamaya, manomaya, vijñānamaya, ānandamaya). she is kośanāthā because she commands these internal sheaths, opening their hidden wealth to the inward-bound practitioner.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya unpacks both the material and internal Vedāntic treasuries:

Ruler of Material and Subtle Sheaths: Materially, She is the master (nāthā) of all royal treasuries and stores (bhāṇḍāgāra). Esoterically, Kośa refers to the five sheaths of human existence (Annamaya, Prāṇamaya, Manomaya, Vijñānamaya, Ānandamaya). She is Kośanāthā because She commands these internal sheaths, opening their hidden wealth to the inward-bound practitioner.

name: चतुरङ्गबलेश्वरी
summary: (i) One who rules the fourfold army.
(ii) The fourfold army consists of cavalry, elephants, chariots and infantry. For the soul, the four faculties are mind, intellect, volition and ego.
(iii) The four aggregates are also explained through the deities connected with creation, protection and destruction; She is the head of all that is fourfold.
searchText: 691 चतुरङ्गबलेश्वरी चत्वारि हस्त्यश्वरथपादातरूपाण्यङ्गानि येषां तेषां बलानामीश्वरी। अङ्गान्येव बलं येषां तेऽङ्गबला व्यूहाः। चत्वारि — four (i) one who rules the fourfold army. (ii) the fourfold army consists of cavalry, elephants, chariots and infantry. for the soul, the four faculties are mind, intellect, volition and ego. (iii) the four aggregates are also explained through the deities connected with creation, protection and destruction; she is the head of all that is fourfold. in the saubhāgyabhāskara, bhāskararāya maps this royal martial layout across cosmic and psychological fields: the mistress of the four divisions: traditionally, a complete imperial army consists of elephants, cavalry, chariots, and infantry. she is the absolute empress (īśvarī) directing this four-fold defensive grid. esoterically, she commands the four internal functional divisions of the cognitive apparatus (अन्तःकरण) — mind, intellect, memory, and ego — marshaling them to protect the practitioner's path.
esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara, Bhāskararāya maps this royal martial layout across cosmic and psychological fields:

The Mistress of the Four Divisions: Traditionally, a complete imperial army consists of elephants, cavalry, chariots, and infantry. She is the absolute empress (Īśvarī) directing this four-fold defensive grid. Esoterically, She commands the four internal functional divisions of the cognitive apparatus (अन्तःकरण) — mind, intellect, memory, and ego — marshaling them to protect the practitioner's path.

name: साम्राज्यदायिनी
wordByWord: Word meanings are read directly from the samāsa expression above.
summary: (i) One who bestows imperial dominion on Her devotees.
(ii) As indicated earlier in Rājyadāyinī, She directly blesses the devotees with sāmrājya, greater than other forms of rulership.
(iii) She bestows the status of emperor or maṇḍaleśvara.
searchText: 692 साम्राज्यदायिनी साम्राज्यं [ताच्छील्येन] दत्ते इति। word meanings are read directly from the samāsa expression above. (i) one who bestows imperial dominion on her devotees. (ii) as indicated earlier in rājyadāyinī, she directly blesses the devotees with sāmrājya, greater than other forms of rulership. (iii) she bestows the status of emperor or maṇḍaleśvara. bhāskararāya distinguishes her gifts from temporary political fortunes: the giver of spiritual sovereignty: while she can grant external material kingdoms, her primary gift as sāmrājyadāyinī is स्वाराज्य (svārājya — the inner empire of self-mastery). she bestows that high state of realization where the practitioner rules over their own senses, mind, and destinies, completely liberated from external dependency.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya distinguishes Her gifts from temporary political fortunes:

The Giver of Spiritual Sovereignty: While She can grant external material kingdoms, Her primary gift as Sāmrājyadāyinī is स्वाराज्य (Svārājya — the inner empire of self-mastery). She bestows that high state of realization where the practitioner rules over their own senses, mind, and destinies, completely liberated from external dependency.

name: सत्यसन्धा
samasa: सत्ये अनुल्लङ्घ्ये सन्धे प्रतिज्ञामर्यादे यस्याः सा।
summary: (i) One whose promises and vows are unbroken.
(ii) The promises of Śrīdevī and Her limits of protection cannot be broken by anyone.
(iii) She has vowed to protect the entire universe and firmly keeps Her word.
searchText: 693 सत्यसन्धा सत्ये अनुल्लङ्घ्ये सन्धे प्रतिज्ञामर्यादे यस्याः सा। सत्ये — in truth सन्धे — in vow; promise यस्याः — of whom; whose (i) one whose promises and vows are unbroken. (ii) the promises of śrīdevī and her limits of protection cannot be broken by anyone. (iii) she has vowed to protect the entire universe and firmly keeps her word. bhāskararāya details the unshakeable reliability of her cosmic pacts: the absolute guarantee: her vows and universal laws are अनुल्लङ्घ्य (anullaṅghya — inviolable/unbreakable). when she promises protection, grace, or eventual liberation to a devotee who surrenders to her path, that vow is absolute truth. cosmic laws may bend, but her covenant with her children remains unyielding.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya details the unshakeable reliability of Her cosmic pacts:

The Absolute Guarantee: Her vows and universal laws are अनुल्लङ्घ्य (anullaṅghya — inviolable/unbreakable). When She promises protection, grace, or eventual liberation to a devotee who surrenders to Her path, that vow is absolute truth. Cosmic laws may bend, but Her covenant with Her children remains unyielding.

name: सागरमेखला
samasa: सागराः समुद्रा एव मेखला काञ्ची यस्याः सा।
summary: (i) One whose girdle is the ocean.
(ii) The oceans themselves form Her girdle.
(iii) This points to Her cosmic body, in which the seas encircle the worlds as an ornament.
searchText: 694 सागरमेखला सागराः समुद्रा एव मेखला काञ्ची यस्याः सा। सागराः — oceans मेखला — girdle यस्याः — of whom; whose (i) one whose girdle is the ocean. (ii) the oceans themselves form her girdle. (iii) this points to her cosmic body, in which the seas encircle the worlds as an ornament. in the saubhāgyabhāskara, bhāskararāya clarifies her planetary immanence: the sovereign of the earth body: the physical earth is her manifest body, wrapped around the center by the vast oceans acting as a royal waistband (mekhalā). this reinforces her absolute presence across the terrestrial grid, showing that the physical structures of geography are components of her form.
esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara, Bhāskararāya clarifies Her planetary immanence:

The Sovereign of the Earth Body: The physical earth is Her manifest body, wrapped around the center by the vast oceans acting as a royal waistband (mekhalā). This reinforces Her absolute presence across the terrestrial grid, showing that the physical structures of geography are components of Her form.

name: दीक्षिता
samasa: दीक्षा अस्य सञ्जाता इति दीक्षितः। तदभिन्ना।
summary: (i) One who helps others gain knowledge through initiation.
(ii) Dīkṣā destroys the sins of disciples through the giving of mantra. This is the duty of the teacher.
(iii) Since Śrīdevī is Gurumūrti, She is Dīkṣitā. There is no difference between the teacher, mantra and presiding deity.
searchText: 695 दीक्षिता दीक्षा। धियं ज्ञानं क्षिणोति प्रापयतीति। शिष्येभ्यो मन्त्रदानेन पापं क्षपयतीति। दीक्षा अस्य सञ्जाता इति दीक्षितः। तदभिन्ना। दीक्षा — initiation (i) one who helps others gain knowledge through initiation. (ii) dīkṣā destroys the sins of disciples through the giving of mantra. this is the duty of the teacher. (iii) since śrīdevī is gurumūrti, she is dīkṣitā. there is no difference between the teacher, mantra and presiding deity. bhāskararāya provides an extensive breakdown of the syllable दीक्षा (dīkṣā): the core of initiatory power: दी signifies the bestowal of spiritual knowledge (dhiyaṁ jñānaṁ dadāti), and क्षा signifies the absolute destruction of past binding sins (pāpaṁ kṣapayati). she is dīkṣitā because she is the internal source and the non-dual power running through the initiation process, performing the purification of the student's soul.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya provides an extensive breakdown of the syllable दीक्षा (Dīkṣā):

The Core of Initiatory Power: दी signifies the bestowal of spiritual knowledge (dhiyaṁ jñānaṁ dadāti), and क्षा signifies the absolute destruction of past binding sins (pāpaṁ kṣapayati). She is Dīkṣitā because She is the internal source and the non-dual power running through the initiation process, performing the purification of the student's soul.

name: दैत्यशमनी
summary: (i) One who destroys or pacifies demons.
(ii) Daityas such as Bhaṇḍāsura are subdued by Her.
(iii) The name indicates Her protective power that removes the forces opposed to divine order.
searchText: 696 दैत्यशमनी दैत्यानां भण्डादीनां शमनी नाशिका। शमनी — pacifier; destroyer (i) one who destroys or pacifies demons. (ii) daityas such as bhaṇḍāsura are subdued by her. (iii) the name indicates her protective power that removes the forces opposed to divine order. bhāskararāya moves this martial title into the internal chambers of rectification: the pacification of egotism: demons (daityas) represent the wild, aggressive distortions of rajas and tamas inside the mind. she does not just fight them; she pacifies (śamanī) them, drawing the chaotic energy back into its baseline source, converting the practitioner's inner turbulence into tranquil silence.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya moves this martial title into the internal chambers of rectification:

The Pacification of Egotism: Demons (Daityas) represent the wild, aggressive distortions of rajas and tamas inside the mind. She does not just fight them; She pacifies (śamanī) them, drawing the chaotic energy back into its baseline source, converting the practitioner's inner turbulence into tranquil silence.

name: सर्वलोकवशङ्करी
summary: (i) One who brings all worlds under Her control.
(ii) She makes all worlds act according to Her will.
(iii) All lokas and beings are governed by Her sovereign power.
searchText: 697 सर्वलोकवशङ्करी सर्वान् लोकान् स्ववशे कुरुते। सर्वान् — all लोकान् — worlds स्ववशे — under one’s control (i) one who brings all worlds under her control. (ii) she makes all worlds act according to her will. (iii) all lokas and beings are governed by her sovereign power. in the saubhāgyabhāskara, bhāskararāya details her absolute magnetic authority: the supreme fascination: she does not enforce control through external tyrannical violence. she is sarvalokavaśaṅkarī because her essential nature is so boundlessly beautiful, blissful, and full of truth that every realm, deity, and living soul is naturally fascinated, drawn inward, and aligned under her command.
esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara, Bhāskararāya details Her absolute magnetic authority:

The Supreme Fascination: She does not enforce control through external tyrannical violence. She is Sarvalokavaśaṅkarī because Her essential nature is so boundlessly beautiful, blissful, and full of truth that every realm, deity, and living soul is naturally fascinated, drawn inward, and aligned under Her command.

name: सर्वार्थदात्री
wordByWord: Word meanings are read directly from the samāsa expression above.
summary: (i) One who gives all aims of life.
(ii) She grants the four puruṣārthas: dharma, artha, kāma and mokṣa.
(iii) Thus every true human aim is obtained through Her grace.
searchText: 698 सर्वार्थदात्री सर्वेषां चतुर्णामर्थानां पुरुषार्थानां दात्री। word meanings are read directly from the samāsa expression above. (i) one who gives all aims of life. (ii) she grants the four puruṣārthas: dharma, artha, kāma and mokṣa.
