(iii) thus every true human aim is obtained through her grace. bhāskararāya outlines her complete, balanced fulfillment of human aspiration: the dispenser of the four aims: she handles the complete grid of the four human objectives: धर्म (righteousness), अर्थ (wealth), काम (desire), and मोक्ष (liberation). she ensures that her devotees do not suffer material lack while seeking spirit, nor get trapped in wealth without spirit, organizing a balanced distribution of all human aims.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya outlines Her complete, balanced fulfillment of human aspiration:

The Dispenser of the Four Aims: She handles the complete grid of the four human objectives: धर्म (righteousness), अर्थ (wealth), काम (desire), and मोक्ष (liberation). She ensures that Her devotees do not suffer material lack while seeking spirit, nor get trapped in wealth without spirit, organizing a balanced distribution of all human aims.

name: सावित्री
wordByWord: Word meanings are read directly from the samāsa expression above.
summary: (i) She is Sāvitrī.
(ii) Savitṛ is the creator or impeller of the world; She is the Śakti of that supreme source.
(iii) The name also recalls the Gāyatrī-Sāvitrī contemplation connected with the Sun and the supreme being.
searchText: 699 सावित्री सवितुर्जगत्प्रसूतेः परशिवस्येयं सावित्री। word meanings are read directly from the samāsa expression above. (i) she is sāvitrī. (ii) savitṛ is the creator or impeller of the world; she is the śakti of that supreme source. (iii) the name also recalls the gāyatrī-sāvitrī contemplation connected with the sun and the supreme being. bhāskararāya details her identity with the absolute source of light: the power of the divine creator: सवीतृ (savitā) refers to the supreme generator of the universe—paramaśiva. she is his non-dual dynamic power (sāvitrī). this confirms her absolute identity with the inner essence of the gāyatrī mantra, acting as the brilliant light that awakens the spiritual intellect.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya details Her identity with the absolute source of light:

The Power of the Divine Creator: सवीतृ (Savitā) refers to the supreme generator of the universe—Paramaśiva. She is His non-dual dynamic power (Sāvitrī). This confirms Her absolute identity with the inner essence of the Gāyatrī Mantra, acting as the brilliant light that awakens the spiritual intellect.

name: सच्चिदानन्दरूपिणी
wordByWord: Word meanings are read directly from the samāsa expression above.
summary: (i) One whose form is existence, consciousness and bliss.
(ii) Sat, cit and ānanda together constitute Brahman.
(iii) That Brahman is Śrīdevī; this completes the group of kalās described here.
searchText: 700 सच्चिदानन्दरूपिणी सत्त्वं चित्त्वमानन्दश्च रूपं स्वरूपमस्या इति। word meanings are read directly from the samāsa expression above. (i) one whose form is existence, consciousness and bliss. (ii) sat, cit and ānanda together constitute brahman. (iii) that brahman is śrīdevī; this completes the group of kalās described here. in the saubhāgyabhāskara, bhāskararāya concludes this major sequence by stating her absolute philosophical definition: the supreme definition of brahman: this name frames her identity with the unconditioned absolute. she is not a passing manifestation; her entire essential body is made of nothing but pure, uncorrupted, eternal existence (sat), infinite, self-luminous awareness (cit), and boundless, unshakeable bliss (ānanda), serving as the ultimate refuge of reality.
esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara, Bhāskararāya concludes this major sequence by stating Her absolute philosophical definition:

The Supreme Definition of Brahman: This name frames Her identity with the unconditioned absolute. She is not a passing manifestation; Her entire essential body is made of nothing but pure, uncorrupted, eternal existence (Sat), infinite, self-luminous awareness (Cit), and boundless, unshakeable bliss (Ānanda), serving as the ultimate refuge of reality.

name: देशकालापरिच्छिन्ना
wordByWord: Word meanings are read directly from the samāsa expression above.
summary: (i) One who is not limited by space and time.
(ii) Some things are limited by place or time; She is eternal and available at all times, so such distinctions do not apply to Her.
(iii) The meanings of Nityā, Nirnāśā and Vyāpinī are brought together in this name.
searchText: 701 देशकालापरिच्छिन्ना देशकालाभ्यामपरिच्छिन्ना। word meanings are read directly from the samāsa expression above. (i) one who is not limited by space and time. (ii) some things are limited by place or time; she is eternal and available at all times, so such distinctions do not apply to her. (iii) the meanings of nityā, nirnāśā and vyāpinī are brought together in this name. in the saubhāgyabhāskara, bhāskararāya defines her absolute freedom from spatial and temporal limitations (pariccheda): spatial and temporal infinity: material entities exist at a specific location and endure for a finite duration. she is deśakālāparicchinnā because she stands completely outside these structural coordinates. she lacks spatial boundaries, meaning she is omnipresent; and she lacks temporal boundaries, meaning she is eternal. she acts as the baseline screen within which space and time themselves unfold.
esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara, Bhāskararāya defines Her absolute freedom from spatial and temporal limitations (Pariccheda):

Spatial and Temporal Infinity: Material entities exist at a specific location and endure for a finite duration. She is Deśakālāparicchinnā because She stands completely outside these structural coordinates. She lacks spatial boundaries, meaning She is omnipresent; and She lacks temporal boundaries, meaning She is eternal. She acts as the baseline screen within which space and time themselves unfold.

name: सर्वगा
wordByWord: Word meanings are read directly from the samāsa expression above.
summary: (i) One who pervades all.
(ii) She reaches all things by non-difference and is present everywhere.
(iii) No place or object is outside Her presence.
searchText: 702 सर्वगा सर्वं वस्तुमात्रं गच्छत्यभेदेन प्राप्नोतीति सर्वगा। word meanings are read directly from the samāsa expression above. (i) one who pervades all. (ii) she reaches all things by non-difference and is present everywhere. (iii) no place or object is outside her presence. bhāskararāya details the mechanics of her non-dual pervasiveness: the pervasive substance: she does not simply move from place to place. she is sarvagā because she reaches and permeates every single objective entity (vastu-mātra) through absolute structural identity (अभेदेन). there is no gap between her consciousness field and the material forms it animates; she is the essential substance inside every coordinate of the universe.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya details the mechanics of Her non-dual pervasiveness:

The Pervasive Substance: She does not simply move from place to place. She is Sarvagā because She reaches and permeates every single objective entity (vastu-mātra) through absolute structural identity (अभेदेन). There is no gap between Her consciousness field and the material forms it animates; She is the essential substance inside every coordinate of the universe.

name: सर्वमोहिनी
samasa: सर्वान्प्राकृतजनान् भेदभाने सत्यत्वं मन्यमानान् मोहयति अद्वैतविषयकज्ञानविधुरान् कुरुते इति।
summary: (i) One who bewilders all.
(ii) She bewilders ordinary people who take apparent duality as real and deprives them of the knowledge of unity.
(iii) The apparent difference between Brahman and the universe is illusory; this power of bewilderment is described also in Saptashatī.
searchText: 703 सर्वमोहिनी सर्वान्प्राकृतजनान् भेदभाने सत्यत्वं मन्यमानान् मोहयति अद्वैतविषयकज्ञानविधुरान् कुरुते इति। मोहयति — bewilders (i) one who bewilders all. (ii) she bewilders ordinary people who take apparent duality as real and deprives them of the knowledge of unity. (iii) the apparent difference between brahman and the universe is illusory; this power of bewilderment is described also in saptashatī. bhāskararāya provides a rigorous philosophical analysis of her veiling power: the magic of duality: as sarvamohinī, she binds those who view the relative world as an absolute reality (bheda-bhāna). she temporarily veils their non-dual realization (advaita-jñāna-vidhura), causing them to see differences instead of unity. this enchantment is the foundational mechanism that allows the cosmic play (līlā) to appear real to the individual observer.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya provides a rigorous philosophical analysis of Her veiling power:

The Magic of Duality: As Sarvamohinī, She binds those who view the relative world as an absolute reality (bheda-bhāna). She temporarily veils their non-dual realization (advaita-jñāna-vidhura), causing them to see differences instead of unity. This enchantment is the foundational mechanism that allows the cosmic play (Līlā) to appear real to the individual observer.

name: सरस्वती
samasa: सरो नीरं तद्वत् रसो वा अस्त्यस्या इति।
summary: (i) Sarasvatī.
(ii) She is the form of knowledge and speech, and the deity presiding over knowledge.
(iii) She is like an ocean of knowledge and is also connected with the traditional form of Sarasvatī.
searchText: 704 सरस्वती सरो नीरं तद्वत् रसो वा अस्त्यस्या इति। द्विवर्षा तु सरस्वतीति धौम्यवचनात्तादृशकन्यारूपति। विषयानवच्छिन्नज्ञानरूपेति। ज्ञानाभिमानिनी देवता ज्ञानसमुद्ररूपेत्यर्थः। सरो — lake; water (i) sarasvatī. (ii) she is the form of knowledge and speech, and the deity presiding over knowledge. (iii) she is like an ocean of knowledge and is also connected with the traditional form of sarasvatī. in the saubhāgyabhāskara, bhāskararāya unpacks this name across three distinct dimensions of wisdom and ritual: the shoreless ocean of wisdom: she is identical to the supreme goddess of wisdom, sarasvatī. she represents unconditioned awareness (viṣayānavacchinna-jñāna), flowing like a vast, fluid ocean of insight. ritual maidens: in the liturgical framework of kumārī-pūjā defined by sage dhaumya, she manifests as the pristine energy of a two-year-old girl, representing pure potential before worldly conditioning.
esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara, Bhāskararāya unpacks this name across three distinct dimensions of wisdom and ritual:

The Shoreless Ocean of Wisdom: She is identical to the supreme goddess of wisdom, Sarasvatī. She represents unconditioned awareness (viṣayānavacchinna-jñāna), flowing like a vast, fluid ocean of insight.

Ritual Maidens: In the liturgical framework of Kumārī-Pūjā defined by Sage Dhaumya, She manifests as the pristine energy of a two-year-old girl, representing pure potential before worldly conditioning.

name: शास्त्रमयी
wordByWord: Word meanings are read directly from the samāsa expression above.
summary: (i) One who is composed of śāstra.
(ii) The scriptures beginning with teachings such as sarvaṃ khalvidaṃ brahma are centred on Her.
(iii) Her body is understood as made of scripture, or as the source and transformation of śāstra.
searchText: 705 शास्त्रमयी सर्वं खल्विदं ब्रह्मेत्यादिशास्त्रप्रधाना। शास्त्रात्मकस्वावयवशालिविग्रहत्वाच्छास्त्रविकारेत्यप्यर्थः। word meanings are read directly from the samāsa expression above. (i) one who is composed of śāstra. (ii) the scriptures beginning with teachings such as sarvaṃ khalvidaṃ brahma are centred on her. (iii) her body is understood as made of scripture, or as the source and transformation of śāstra. bhāskararāya details her non-differentiation from scriptural truth: the body of the sacred word: she is śāstramayī because her subtle body is woven out of the foundational declarations of non-duality, such as “sarvaṁ khalvidaṁ brahma” ("all this is indeed brahman"). bhāskararāya notes that every sacred science, text, and structural formula is a limb of her manifest form, making her the literal substance of divine revelation.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya details Her non-differentiation from scriptural truth:

The Body of the Sacred Word: She is Śāstramayī because Her subtle body is woven out of the foundational declarations of non-duality, such as “Sarvaṁ khalvidaṁ Brahma” ("All this is indeed Brahman"). Bhāskararāya notes that every sacred science, text, and structural formula is a limb of Her manifest form, making Her the literal substance of divine revelation.

name: गुहाम्बा
summary: (i) One who dwells in the cave.
(ii) The cave is the heart. The Upaniṣadic idea of shadow and light in the cave of the heart is discussed here.
(iii) The name may also mean the mother of Guha or Subrahmaṇya, as narrated in Purāṇic accounts.
searchText: 706 गुहाम्बा गुहायां स्थिताम्बा। गुहस्य स्कन्दस्याम्बा माता गुहस्य — of guha/subrahmaṇya माता — mother (i) one who dwells in the cave. (ii) the cave is the heart. the upaniṣadic idea of shadow and light in the cave of the heart is discussed here. (iii) the name may also mean the mother of guha or subrahmaṇya, as narrated in purāṇic accounts. bhāskararāya explores this across both historical mythology and deep internal coordinates: the guardian of the secret cavern: mythologically, she is the mother of the warrior lord skanda (guha). esoterically, गुहा (guhā) refers to the hidden, secret cavern of the human heart. she is the primordial, protective mother energy (ambā) that sits inside this internal cave, guarding the individual spark of consciousness.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya explores this across both historical mythology and deep internal coordinates:

The Guardian of the Secret Cavern: Mythologically, She is the mother of the warrior lord Skanda (Guha). Esoterically, गुहा (guhā) refers to the hidden, secret cavern of the human heart. She is the primordial, protective mother energy (Ambā) that sits inside this internal cave, guarding the individual spark of consciousness.

name: गुह्यरूपिणी
samasa: गुहायां स्थितं गुह्यं परमरहस्यं व्यावहारिकदृष्ट्ययोग्यं ज्ञानमेव रूपमस्याः।
wordByWord: Word meanings are read directly from the samāsa expression above.
summary: (i) One whose form is most secret.
(ii) The permanent truth is secret, beyond the phenomenal universe and beyond the senses.
(iii) She is the secret knowledge residing in the secret place, beloved by secret devotees.
searchText: 707 गुह्यरूपिणी गुहायां स्थितं गुह्यं परमरहस्यं व्यावहारिकदृष्ट्ययोग्यं ज्ञानमेव रूपमस्याः। word meanings are read directly from the samāsa expression above. (i) one whose form is most secret. (ii) the permanent truth is secret, beyond the phenomenal universe and beyond the senses. (iii) she is the secret knowledge residing in the secret place, beloved by secret devotees. in the saubhāgyabhāskara, bhāskararāya defines the hidden nature of her ultimate reality: the transcendent secret: ordinary physical vision is tuned exclusively to capture relative, external material objects. her true, essential form is guhya — a supreme secret that cannot be objectified by common, worldly intellects (vyāvahārika-dṛṣṭi-ayogya). she is the unmanifested, mystical core of reality, realized solely through internal absorption.
esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara, Bhāskararāya defines the hidden nature of Her ultimate reality:

The Transcendent Secret: Ordinary physical vision is tuned exclusively to capture relative, external material objects. Her true, essential form is Guhya — a supreme secret that cannot be objectified by common, worldly intellects (vyāvahārika-dṛṣṭi-ayogya). She is the unmanifested, mystical core of reality, realized solely through internal absorption.

name: सर्वोपाधिविनिर्मुक्ता
samasa: सर्वैः उपाधिभिः विशिष्य निःशेषेण मुक्ता त्यक्ता धर्मसम्बन्धशून्या।
summary: (i) One who is free from all limitations and adjuncts.
(ii) Every attribute becomes a limiting adjunct of place, time or object. She is free from all such upādhis.
(iii) The relation of qualities to the qualified thing is ultimately illusory; She, as Brahman, is unrelated to every limiting quality.
searchText: 708 सर्वोपाधिविनिर्मुक्ता सर्वैः उपाधिभिः विशिष्य निःशेषेण मुक्ता त्यक्ता धर्मसम्बन्धशून्या। तार्किकप्रसिद्धोपाधिशून्या तादृशसद्धेतुगम्येति। सर्वैः — by all उपाधिभिः — by limiting adjuncts मुक्ता — free (i) one who is free from all limitations and adjuncts. (ii) every attribute becomes a limiting adjunct of place, time or object. she is free from all such upādhis. (iii) the relation of qualities to the qualified thing is ultimately illusory; she, as brahman, is unrelated to every limiting quality. bhāskararāya navigates a critical non-dual philosophical absolute: absolute freedom from adjuncts: an upādhi is a conditioning lens—like a piece of red glass that makes a clear crystal appear red. space, time, names, forms, and elements are all cosmic lenses. she is sarvopādhivinirmuktā because in her pristine essential nature as parabrahman, she stands completely free from all these attributes (धर्मसम्बन्धशून्या), remaining unconditioned and pure.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya navigates a critical non-dual philosophical absolute:

Absolute Freedom from Adjuncts: An Upādhi is a conditioning lens—like a piece of red glass that makes a clear crystal appear red. Space, time, names, forms, and elements are all cosmic lenses. She is Sarvopādhivinirmuktā because in Her pristine essential nature as Parabrahman, She stands completely free from all these attributes (धर्मसम्बन्धशून्या), remaining unconditioned and pure.

name: सदाशिवपतिव्रता
samasa: शिव एव पतिरिति व्रतं नियमः सदा सार्वकालिको यस्याः सा।
summary: (i) One who is the devoted spouse of Sadāśiva.
(ii) She remains always with Śiva in all three times.
(iii) This name establishes Her equality with, and inseparability from, Brahman while preserving the devotional image of Śiva and Śakti.
searchText: 709 सदाशिवपतिव्रता शिव एव पतिरिति व्रतं नियमः सदा सार्वकालिको यस्याः सा। सदाशिवस्य पतिव्रतेति। सदा — always यस्याः — of whom; whose (i) one who is the devoted spouse of sadāśiva. (ii) she remains always with śiva in all three times. (iii) this name establishes her equality with, and inseparability from, brahman while preserving the devotional image of śiva and śakti. bhāskararāya elevations this past mundane human marital configurations to reveal cosmic physics: the eternal fusion of consciousness and energy: this names the absolute, unbreakable dynamic law of non-duality. she (śakti) possesses an unceasing, timeless vow (sada-vrata) to never separate herself from sadāśiva (pure, static consciousness). energy cannot exist without consciousness, and consciousness cannot express itself without energy. they remain in an eternal, unbroken embrace.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya elevations this past mundane human marital configurations to reveal cosmic physics:

The Eternal Fusion of Consciousness and Energy: This names the absolute, unbreakable dynamic law of non-duality. She (Śakti) possesses an unceasing, timeless vow (sada-vrata) to never separate Herself from Sadāśiva (pure, static consciousness). Energy cannot exist without consciousness, and consciousness cannot express itself without energy. They remain in an eternal, unbroken embrace.

name: सम्प्रदायेश्वरी
samasa: सम्यक् शिष्येभ्यः प्रदीयत इति सम्प्रदायः। तत्रेश्वरी समर्था समर्थनक्षमा।
summary: (i) One who is the ruler of the tradition.
(ii) Sampradāya is that which is properly handed down from teacher to disciple.
(iii) She is the authority and power behind that lineage and its transmission.
searchText: 710 सम्प्रदायेश्वरी सम्यक् शिष्येभ्यः प्रदीयत इति सम्प्रदायः। तत्रेश्वरी समर्था समर्थनक्षमा। शिष्येभ्यः — to disciples (i) one who is the ruler of the tradition. (ii) sampradāya is that which is properly handed down from teacher to disciple. (iii) she is the authority and power behind that lineage and its transmission. in the saubhāgyabhāskara, bhāskararāya defines the structural transmission of grace: the mistress of the lineage: सम्प्रदाय (sampradāya) is the uninterrupted chain through which sacred wisdom is correctly and directly transmitted from master to disciple (samyak pradīyate). she is the absolute ruler and animating spirit behind this entire pedagogical pipeline, ensuring that the initiatory power stays alive and uncorrupted across generations.
esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara, Bhāskararāya defines the structural transmission of grace:

The Mistress of the Lineage: सम्प्रदाय (Sampradāya) is the uninterrupted chain through which sacred wisdom is correctly and directly transmitted from master to disciple (samyak pradīyate). She is the absolute ruler and animating spirit behind this entire pedagogical pipeline, ensuring that the initiatory power stays alive and uncorrupted across generations.

name: साध्वी
wordByWord: Word meanings are read directly from the samāsa expression above.
summary: (i) One who is virtuous and gives the good path.
(ii) Sādhvī means the one of noble conduct.
(iii) She leads devotees to the right and auspicious path.
searchText: 711 साध्वी सद्गतिदायिनी। word meanings are read directly from the samāsa expression above. (i) one who is virtuous and gives the good path. (ii) sādhvī means the one of noble conduct. (iii) she leads devotees to the right and auspicious path. bhāskararāya connects her virtue to her capacity to elevate the seeker: the guide to the highest goal: she is sādhvī because her operations are entirely aligned with uncorrupted cosmic order (ṛta). for the practitioner, she acts as सद्गतिदायिनी (sadgati-dāyinī) — the force that safely guides the soul along the upward path, ensuring they reach the ultimate destination of liberation without falling into spiritual deviation.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya connects Her virtue to Her capacity to elevate the seeker:

The Guide to the Highest Goal: She is Sādhvī because Her operations are entirely aligned with uncorrupted cosmic order (Ṛta). For the practitioner, She acts as सद्गतिदायिनी (Sadgati-dāyinī) — the force that safely guides the soul along the upward path, ensuring they reach the ultimate destination of liberation without falling into spiritual deviation.

name: ई
wordByWord: Word meanings are read directly from the samāsa expression above.
summary: (i) The one-letter name Ī.
(ii) This single syllable is connected with the turīya state and kāmakalā.
(iii) It is a secret mantra-name and is to be understood through the proper tradition.
searchText: 712 ई तुर्यस्वरूपमेकाक्षरं कामकलासंज्ञकमिदं नाम। word meanings are read directly from the samāsa expression above. (i) the one-letter name ī. (ii) this single syllable is connected with the turīya state and kāmakalā. (iii) it is a secret mantra-name and is to be understood through the proper tradition. bhāskararāya decodes one of the most guarded acoustic coordinates of high śrīvidyā theology: the monosyllable of kāmakalā: the vowel ई (ī) is the phonetic seed-syllable that represents कामकला (kāmakalā) — the primal, creative flash of divine desire where shiva and shakti recognize their union. it is the fourth transcendent state (turya-svarūpa). she is the direct phonic reality of this single syllable, which contains the complete blueprint of creation within its resonance.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya decodes one of the most guarded acoustic coordinates of high Śrīvidyā theology:

The Monosyllable of Kāmakalā: The vowel ई (Ī) is the phonetic seed-syllable that represents कामकला (Kāmakalā) — the primal, creative flash of divine desire where Shiva and Shakti recognize their union. It is the fourth transcendent state (Turya-svarūpa). She is the direct phonic reality of this single syllable, which contains the complete blueprint of creation within its resonance.

name: गुरुमण्डलरूपिणी
samasa: गुरवः परमशिवादिस्वस्वगुरुपर्यन्तास्तेषां मण्डलं परम्परा सैव रूपं निरूपणमस्याः अस्तीति।
summary: (i) One who is in the form of the circle of Gurus.
(ii) The secret kāmakalā form must be learnt through a proper teacher and not merely from books.
(iii) The lineage from Paramaśiva down to one's own Guru is the Gurumaṇḍala; She is in that form.
searchText: 713 गुरुमण्डलरूपिणी गुरवः परमशिवादिस्वस्वगुरुपर्यन्तास्तेषां मण्डलं परम्परा सैव रूपं निरूपणमस्याः अस्तीति। गुरवः — gurus परम्परा — tradition; succession (i) one who is in the form of the circle of gurus. (ii) the secret kāmakalā form must be learnt through a proper teacher and not merely from books. (iii) the lineage from paramaśiva down to one's own guru is the gurumaṇḍala; she is in that form. in the saubhāgyabhāskara, bhāskararāya details the human-divine matrix of the preceptors: the corporate chain of preceptors: the guru lineage in śrīvidyā is stratified into three distinct tiers: दिव्यौघ (divyaugha — celestial masters), सिद्धौघ (siddhaugha — perfected masters), and मानवौघ (mānavaugha — human masters), initiating from paramaśiva himself. she is gurumaṇḍalarūpiṇī because she is the continuous light running through this entire corporate chain, proving that every true guru is an execution of her presence.
esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara, Bhāskararāya details the human-divine matrix of the preceptors:

The Corporate Chain of Preceptors: The Guru lineage in Śrīvidyā is stratified into three distinct tiers: दिव्यौघ (Divyaugha — celestial masters), सिद्धौघ (Siddhaugha — perfected masters), and मानवौघ (Mānavaugha — human masters), initiating from Paramaśiva Himself. She is Gurumaṇḍalarūpiṇī because She is the continuous light running through this entire corporate chain, proving that every true guru is an execution of Her presence.

name: कुलोत्तीर्णा
wordByWord: Word meanings are read directly from the samāsa expression above.
summary: (i) One who has crossed beyond kula.
(ii) Kula is here the group of senses. She is beyond recognition through the senses.
(iii) She can be reached only through imagination, devotion and knowledge; related names such as Bhāvanāgamyā and Bhaktigamyā are recalled.
searchText: 714 कुलोत्तीर्णा कुलमिन्द्रियसमूहमुत्तीर्णा अतिक्रान्ता। word meanings are read directly from the samāsa expression above. (i) one who has crossed beyond kula. (ii) kula is here the group of senses. she is beyond recognition through the senses. (iii) she can be reached only through imagination, devotion and knowledge; related names such as bhāvanāgamyā and bhaktigamyā are recalled. bhāskararāya details the transition from manifest structures to transcendent space: beyond the sensory matrix: in internal alignment, कुल (kula) denotes the network of the senses, the physical elements, and the structured system of the lower chakras. she is kulottīrṇā because while she animates these systems, she simultaneously stands completely past them (atikrāntā). she represents the shoreless, unmanifested sky of pure consciousness (akula) that remains untouched by material mechanics.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya details the transition from manifest structures to transcendent space:

Beyond the Sensory Matrix: In internal alignment, कुल (Kula) denotes the network of the senses, the physical elements, and the structured system of the lower chakras. She is Kulottīrṇā because while She animates these systems, She simultaneously stands completely past them (atikrāntā). She represents the shoreless, unmanifested sky of pure consciousness (Akula) that remains untouched by material mechanics.

name: भगाराध्या
summary: (i) One who is to be worshipped in the Sun's disc.
(ii) Bhaga indicates the Sun's disc, and She is secretly worshipped there; this recalls Bhānumaṇḍala-madhyasthā.
(iii) The triangular letter Ī and the union of Śiva and Śakti are also connected with this name, and the meaning must be learnt through a proper teacher.
searchText: 715 भगाराध्या भगे सवितृमण्डले आराध्योपास्या। भगेन एकाकारेण आराध्या। भगे — in bhaga; solar disc/triangle सवितृमण्डले — in the solar orb (i) one who is to be worshipped in the sun's disc. (ii) bhaga indicates the sun's disc, and she is secretly worshipped there; this recalls bhānumaṇḍala-madhyasthā. (iii) the triangular letter ī and the union of śiva and śakti are also connected with this name, and the meaning must be learnt through a proper teacher. bhāskararāya unpacks the solar and non-dual tracks of this name: the meditation in the sun: भग (bhaga) refers directly to the radiant, life-giving orb of the sun (savitṛ-maṇḍala). she is the inner consciousness worshipped at the absolute center of this solar light. the unified adoration: alternatively, it denotes that she is correctly approached and adored through a completely undivided, unified state of internal awareness (ekākāra), where the practitioner sheds all sense of fragmented tracking.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya unpacks the solar and non-dual tracks of this name:

The Meditation in the Sun: भग (Bhaga) refers directly to the radiant, life-giving orb of the sun (Savitṛ-maṇḍala). She is the inner consciousness worshipped at the absolute center of this solar light.

The Unified Adoration: Alternatively, it denotes that She is correctly approached and adored through a completely undivided, unified state of internal awareness (ekākāra), where the practitioner sheds all sense of fragmented tracking.

name: माया
wordByWord: Word meanings are read directly from the samāsa expression above.
summary: (i) One who is the form of māyā.
(ii) Māyā is the power that hides what is clear and makes appear what is not available. Dreams and supernatural appearances are also connected with māyā.
(iii) The Devī Purāṇa, Varāha Purāṇa, Bhagavad Gītā, Śāṇḍilya Sūtra, Saptashatī and Śākta tradition explain this power. In Śrīvidyā it is the Śakti of Brahman; by worshipping Her, māyā is crossed.
searchText: 716 माया प्रसिद्धतरस्याप्यप्रकटीकरणानुकूला शक्तिर्माया। word meanings are read directly from the samāsa expression above. (i) one who is the form of māyā. (ii) māyā is the power that hides what is clear and makes appear what is not available. dreams and supernatural appearances are also connected with māyā. (iii) the devī purāṇa, varāha purāṇa, bhagavad gītā, śāṇḍilya sūtra, saptashatī and śākta tradition explain this power. in śrīvidyā it is the śakti of brahman; by worshipping her, māyā is crossed. in the saubhāgyabhāskara, bhāskararāya provides a brilliant, technical definition of this power: the engine of selective concealment: māyā is not a simple hallucination. bhāskararāya defines it as that highly specialized, cosmic kinetic capacity that makes the most glaringly obvious truth—the non-dual reality of the self—appear completely hidden (अप्रकटीकरणानुकूला शक्तिः), while simultaneously making the transient world of objects appear absolutely real. she commands this operational filter to sustain the cosmic projection.
esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara, Bhāskararāya provides a brilliant, technical definition of this power:

The Engine of Selective Concealment: Māyā is not a simple hallucination. Bhāskararāya defines it as that highly specialized, cosmic kinetic capacity that makes the most glaringly obvious truth—the non-dual reality of the Self—appear completely hidden (अप्रकटीकरणानुकूला शक्तिः), while simultaneously making the transient world of objects appear absolutely real. She commands this operational filter to sustain the cosmic projection.

name: मधुमती
samasa: मधु मद्यं पुष्परसः क्षौद्रं वा पूजनादिसमये अस्याः अस्तीति।
summary: (i) One who is connected with madhu, meaning honey or wine.
(ii) Madhu may mean honey, wine or sweetness, and such offerings are part of certain forms of worship.
(iii) The name is also related to Vedic and Upaniṣadic statements about honey, to the yogic stage called Madhumatī, and to a river of that name.
searchText: 717 मधुमती मधु मद्यं पुष्परसः क्षौद्रं वा पूजनादिसमये अस्याः अस्तीति। आदित्यो वै देवमध्विति श्रुतौ विहितमधुमत्याख्यविद्याविशेषरूपा। मधु — honey; wine पुष्परसः — flower-juice; honey (i) one who is connected with madhu, meaning honey or wine. (ii) madhu may mean honey, wine or sweetness, and such offerings are part of certain forms of worship. (iii) the name is also related to vedic and upaniṣadic statements about honey, to the yogic stage called madhumatī, and to a river of that name. bhāskararāya details both the ritual and the high-level upaniṣadic layers of this name: the solar honey: the chāndogya upaniṣad celebrates the sun as the divine honey of the cosmos (adityo vai devamadhu). she is the living essence of this मधुमती विद्या (madhumatī vidyā), the high scriptural science that tracks this solar nectar. the internal sweetness: in internal yoga, it represents her presence within the ultimate sweet fluid that floods the channels during deep concentration.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya details both the ritual and the high-level Upaniṣadic layers of this name:

The Solar Honey: The Chāndogya Upaniṣad celebrates the sun as the divine honey of the cosmos (Adityo vai devamadhu). She is the living essence of this मधुमती विद्या (Madhumatī Vidyā), the high scriptural science that tracks this solar nectar.
The Internal Sweetness: In internal yoga, it represents Her presence within the ultimate sweet fluid that floods the channels during deep concentration.

name: मही
wordByWord: Word meanings are read directly from the samāsa expression above.
summary: (i) One who is in the form of the earth.
(ii) Though previous names spoke of secret forms, this name says that She is clearly visible like the earth.
(iii) She supports the whole universe like the earth. Devī Purāṇa also calls eternal nature Mahī; She is that form and also the river called Mahī.
searchText: 718 मही रहस्यरूपापि पृथ्वीवदतिप्रकटा। मह्याख्यनदीविशेषरूपा। महद्व्याप्य स्थिता। word meanings are read directly from the samāsa expression above. (i) one who is in the form of the earth. (ii) though previous names spoke of secret forms, this name says that she is clearly visible like the earth. (iii) she supports the whole universe like the earth. devī purāṇa also calls eternal nature mahī; she is that form and also the river called mahī. in the saubhāgyabhāskara, bhāskararāya resolves a beautiful paradox between the secret and the manifest: the hyper-manifest reality: though she is highly secret (rahasya-rūpā), as mahī, she is simultaneously as blindingly obvious and tangible as the solid earth beneath our feet (pṛthivīvat ati-prakaṭā). she occupies and pervades the entire physical expansion (mahad-vyāpya), showing her absolute accessibility within the material elements.
esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara, Bhāskararāya resolves a beautiful paradox between the secret and the manifest:

The Hyper-Manifest Reality: Though She is highly secret (rahasya-rūpā), as Mahī, She is simultaneously as blindingly obvious and tangible as the solid earth beneath our feet (pṛthivīvat ati-prakaṭā). She occupies and pervades the entire physical expansion (mahad-vyāpya), showing Her absolute accessibility within the material elements.

name: गणाम्बा
summary: (i) One who is the mother of hosts.
(ii) Gaṇa means a group; She is the mother of the Pramatha and other hosts of Paramaśiva.
(iii) Since the head of Śiva's hosts is Gaṇeśa, She is also understood as his mother.
searchText: 719 गणाम्बा गणस्य प्रमथादेः अम्बा। गणस्य गजाननस्य अम्बा। गणस्य — of the host प्रमथादेः — of pramatha and others गजाननस्य — of gaṇeśa (i) one who is the mother of hosts. (ii) gaṇa means a group; she is the mother of the pramatha and other hosts of paramaśiva. (iii) since the head of śiva's hosts is gaṇeśa, she is also understood as his mother. bhāskararāya details her matrix relationship with cosmic hosts and mathematical structures: the mother of assemblies: mythologically, she is the mother of the elephant-faced lord gaṇapati, who commands the dynamic forces. esoterically, गण (gaṇa) represents the groupings, categories, and mathematical sets of phonemes and elements that construct the universe. she sits as the original maternal source (ambā) that gives birth to and organizes these sets.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya details Her matrix relationship with cosmic hosts and mathematical structures:

The Mother of Assemblies: Mythologically, She is the mother of the elephant-faced lord Gaṇapati, who commands the dynamic forces. Esoterically, गण (gaṇa) represents the groupings, categories, and mathematical sets of phonemes and elements that construct the universe. She sits as the original maternal source (Ambā) that gives birth to and organizes these sets.

name: गुह्यकाराध्या
wordByWord: Word meanings are read directly from the samāsa expression above.
summary: (i) One who is worshipped by Guhyakas.
(ii) Kubera, the god of wealth, is the sage for one variety of Pañcadaśī; his hosts are called Guhyakas. She is worshipped by Kubera and those hosts.
(iii) In the Kaula method, Guhyaka also indicates worship in a secret place, unknown to others.
searchText: 720 गुह्यकाराध्या गुह्यकैर्देवविशेषैः आराध्या। अज्ञातरहस्यस्थले आराध्या। word meanings are read directly from the samāsa expression above. (i) one who is worshipped by guhyakas. (ii) kubera, the god of wealth, is the sage for one variety of pañcadaśī; his hosts are called guhyakas. she is worshipped by kubera and those hosts. (iii) in the kaula method, guhyaka also indicates worship in a secret place, unknown to others. bhāskararāya tracks her adoration within hidden classes and secret dimensions: adoration by the hidden hosts: the guhyakas are a highly specialized class of protective, subterranean celestial beings who guard the hidden treasures of the cosmos under kubera. their adoration establishes her status as the ruler of unmanifested wealth. the secret sanctuary: alternatively, it confirms that she is correctly approached and adored within the most private, silent, and deeply interior chambers of spiritual realization.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya tracks Her adoration within hidden classes and secret dimensions:

Adoration by the Hidden Hosts: The Guhyakas are a highly specialized class of protective, subterranean celestial beings who guard the hidden treasures of the cosmos under Kubera. Their adoration establishes Her status as the ruler of unmanifested wealth.
The Secret Sanctuary: Alternatively, it confirms that She is correctly approached and adored within the most private, silent, and deeply interior chambers of spiritual realization.

name: कोमलाङ्गी
summary: (i) One whose form is delicate and pleasing.
(ii) The message given in the name Nalinī is stressed here. Her body is gentle and soft, as praised in devotional poetry such as Śyāmala Daṇḍaka.
searchText: 721 कोमलाङ्गी कोमलानि सुकुमाराण्यङ्गानि यस्याः सा। कोमलानि — delicate; soft यस्याः — whose; of whom (i) one whose form is delicate and pleasing. (ii) the message given in the name nalinī is stressed here. her body is gentle and soft, as praised in devotional poetry such as śyāmala daṇḍaka. in the saubhāgyabhāskara, bhāskararāya clarifies the spiritual refinement of her layout: the softness of grace: her limbs and geometric lines are described as tender (sukumāra), reminding the practitioner that her interaction with the universe is free from crude, heavy violence. even her massive cosmic operations are driven by a highly refined, delicate grace, ensuring that the alignment of souls occurs with gentle precision.
esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara, Bhāskararāya clarifies the spiritual refinement of Her layout:

The Softness of Grace: Her limbs and geometric lines are described as tender (sukumāra), reminding the practitioner that Her interaction with the universe is free from crude, heavy violence. Even Her massive cosmic operations are driven by a highly refined, delicate grace, ensuring that the alignment of souls occurs with gentle precision.

name: गुरुप्रिया
summary: (i) One who has affection for the Guru.
(ii) She has great love for Parameśvara, the great teacher of the world.
(iii) She is pleased with the teachers who initiate eligible disciples into Śrīvidyā and keep the worshipping tradition unbroken.

(iv) She is also understood as the consort of the Guru of every worshipper. This implies that the Guru's wife is to be treated as Śrīdevī herself; in this worship every lady is to be regarded as Śrīdevī.
(v) Guru can also mean Bṛhaspati, the teacher of the Devas; She has care for him also.
searchText: 722 गुरुप्रिया गुरुः प्रियो यस्याः सा। गुरुपत्न्यभिन्ना। जगद्गुरोः शिवस्य पत्नी। गुरुः — guru; teacher यस्याः — whose; of whom शिवस्य — of śiva (i) one who has affection for the guru. (ii) she has great love for parameśvara, the great teacher of the world. (iii) she is pleased with the teachers who initiate eligible disciples into śrīvidyā and keep the worshipping tradition unbroken. (iv) she is also understood as the consort of the guru of every worshipper. this implies that the guru's wife is to be treated as śrīdevī herself; in this worship every lady is to be regarded as śrīdevī. (v) guru can also mean bṛhaspati, the teacher of the devas; she has care for him also. bhāskararāya details the foundational teacher-consort framework: the beloved of the universal master: lord śiva is the eternal, primordial जगद्गुरु (jagadguru — teacher of the worlds). she is his beloved non-dual consort (gurupriyā). concurrently, this name establishes that she is deeply affectionate toward any true preceptor who correctly preserves and teaches the sacred lineage keys, holding them close to her grace.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya details the foundational teacher-consort framework:

The Beloved of the Universal Master: Lord Śiva is the eternal, primordial जगद्गुरु (Jagadguru — teacher of the worlds). She is His beloved non-dual consort (Gurupriyā). Concurrently, this name establishes that She is deeply affectionate toward any true preceptor who correctly preserves and teaches the sacred lineage keys, holding them close to Her grace.

name: स्वतन्त्रा
summary: (i) One who acts on Her own, without depending on others.
(ii) She does not require the help of others for any activity. She does not even need instruments; by Her mere will or declaration She acts.
(iii) She owns Her consort Parameśvara, and He owns Her; they mutually belong to each other.
(iv) She is in the form of the Nitya Tantra called Svatantra. This name may also be connected with the Svatantra mentioned in Soundaryalaharī.
(v) All tantras are ultimately Her own, because even worship of other deities is made possible only by the life-energy that is Śrīdevī.
searchText: 723 स्वतन्त्रा स्वस्य तन्त्रं प्राधान्यं यस्यां सा। स्वान्यात्मीयानि तन्त्राणि यस्याः सा। कारकपारतन्त्र्यमन्तरेणैव सर्वकर्तृत्वात्। स्वतन्त्राख्यनित्यातन्त्ररूपा। स्व आत्मीयः स्वाधीनः परशिवस्तत्तन्त्रा तदधीना। स्वस्य — of herself तन्त्राणि — tantras यस्याः — whose; of whom (i) one who acts on her own, without depending on others. (ii) she does not require the help of others for any activity. she does not even need instruments; by her mere will or declaration she acts. (iii) she owns her consort parameśvara, and he owns her; they mutually belong to each other. (iv) she is in the form of the nitya tantra called svatantra. this name may also be connected with the svatantra mentioned in soundaryalaharī. (v) all tantras are ultimately her own, because even worship of other deities is made possible only by the life-energy that is śrīdevī. bhāskararāya details the absolute metaphysical independence (svātantrya) of the śakti: absolute sovereignty: she is svatantrā because she operates with absolute freedom, entirely unconditioned by external factors or causal dependencies (कारकपारतन्त्र्यमन्तरेण सर्वकर्तृत्वात्). she rules her own systems. the mirror of shiva: alternatively, स्व refers to her own non-dual counterpart, paramaśiva, meaning her entire operational system is fully synchronized and dependent solely on him, tracking her onto the svatantrā nityā deity of the lunar calendar.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya details the absolute metaphysical independence (Svātantrya) of the Śakti:

Absolute Sovereignty: She is Svatantrā because She operates with absolute freedom, entirely unconditioned by external factors or causal dependencies (कारकपारतन्त्र्यमन्तरेण सर्वकर्तृत्वात्). She rules Her own systems.

The Mirror of Shiva: Alternatively, स्व refers to Her own non-dual counterpart, Paramaśiva, meaning Her entire operational system is fully synchronized and dependent solely on Him, tracking Her onto the Svatantrā Nityā deity of the lunar calendar.

name: सर्वतन्त्रेशी
summary: (i) One who is the head of all tantras.
(ii) She establishes and supports the sixty-four tantras and makes them meaningful.
searchText: 724 सर्वतन्त्रेशी सर्वाणि चतुःषष्टिसंख्याकानि तन्त्राणीष्टे समर्थयतीति। सर्वाणि — all (i) one who is the head of all tantras. (ii) she establishes and supports the sixty-four tantras and makes them meaningful. in the saubhāgyabhāskara, this confirms her legislative mastery over all traditions: the overlord of the sixty-four lineages: the agamic universe features sixty-four primary textual systems and lineages (catuḥṣaṣṭi-tantra). she is sarvatantreśī because she sits at the absolute peak apex above all sixty-four books. they are merely partial, specialized charts of her energy, drawing their functional authority exclusively from her supreme command.
esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara, this confirms Her legislative mastery over all traditions:

The Overlord of the Sixty-Four Lineages: The Agamic universe features sixty-four primary textual systems and lineages (Catuḥṣaṣṭi-tantra). She is Sarvatantreśī because She sits at the absolute peak apex above all sixty-four books. They are merely partial, specialized charts of Her energy, drawing their functional authority exclusively from Her supreme command.

name: दक्षिणामूर्तिरूपिणी
samasa: शिवस्य दक्षिणाभिमुखी मूर्तिर्ब्रह्मनारायणादेरध्यापकत्वेन प्रसिद्धा। यस्या मन्त्रास्तन्त्रेषु प्रसिद्धाः स एव रूपमस्याः अस्तीति।
summary: (i) One who is in the form of Dakṣiṇāmūrti.
(ii) Dakṣiṇāmūrti teaches the principle of non-duality.

(iii) Śiva and Śrīdevī appear in individual forms, in the integrated form such as Ardhanārīśvara, and in forms where one is merged in the other. Śrīdevī is also understood as merged in the form of Dakṣiṇāmūrti.
(iv) The Dakṣiṇāmūrti idea continues in the following names.
searchText: 725 दक्षिणामूर्तिरूपिणी शिवस्य दक्षिणाभिमुखी मूर्तिर्ब्रह्मनारायणादेरध्यापकत्वेन प्रसिद्धा। यस्या मन्त्रास्तन्त्रेषु प्रसिद्धाः स एव रूपमस्याः अस्तीति। शिवस्य — of śiva दक्षिणाभिमुखी — south-facing (i) one who is in the form of dakṣiṇāmūrti. (ii) dakṣiṇāmūrti teaches the principle of non-duality. (iii) śiva and śrīdevī appear in individual forms, in the integrated form such as ardhanārīśvara, and in forms where one is merged in the other. śrīdevī is also understood as merged in the form of dakṣiṇāmūrti. (iv) the dakṣiṇāmūrti idea continues in the following names. bhāskararāya reveals a profound, identity equation crossing standard gender definitions: the soul of the silent teacher: dakṣiṇāmūrti is lord śiva sitting in absolute silence under the banyan tree, instructing the ancient rishis through unuttered resonance. this name reveals that she is non-different from this supreme masculine teaching form. she is the internal illumination, the code, and the processing power that allows that silent instruction to break open inside the disciple's mind.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya reveals a profound, identity equation crossing standard gender definitions:

The Soul of the Silent Teacher: Dakṣiṇāmūrti is Lord Śiva sitting in absolute silence under the banyan tree, instructing the ancient rishis through unuttered resonance. This name reveals that She is non-different from this supreme masculine teaching form. She is the internal illumination, the code, and the processing power that allows that silent instruction to break open inside the disciple's mind.

name: सनकादिसमाराध्या
wordByWord: Word meanings are read directly from the samāsa expression above.
summary: (i) One who is worshipped by Sanaka and other sages.
(ii) The four sages Sanaka, Sanandana, Sanātana and Sanatkumāra contemplated Śrīdevī in the form of Dakṣiṇāmūrti and received instruction from Śiva.
(iii) These sages are the mind-born sons of Brahmā, great yogins and highly knowledgeable disciples.
(iv) They are included in the lineage of teachers and are known in connection with the Samaya method of Śrīvidyā worship.
searchText: 726 सनकादिसमाराध्या सनकसनन्दनादिभिः सम्यगाराध्योपास्या। word meanings are read directly from the samāsa expression above. (i) one who is worshipped by sanaka and other sages. (ii) the four sages sanaka, sanandana, sanātana and sanatkumāra contemplated śrīdevī in the form of dakṣiṇāmūrti and received instruction from śiva. (iii) these sages are the mind-born sons of brahmā, great yogins and highly knowledgeable disciples. (iv) they are included in the lineage of teachers and are known in connection with the samaya method of śrīvidyā worship. bhāskararāya tracks her adoration across the highest levels of internal asceticism: the meditation of the primeval ascetics: sages like sanaka represent the absolute pinnacle of lifelong celibacy, internal detachment, and unshakeable absorption in brahman. their intense adoration (samārādhyā) indicates that she is not a deity sought merely for temporary worldly gain, but the ultimate, non-dual absolute targeted by the most advanced ascetics in existence.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya tracks Her adoration across the highest levels of internal asceticism:

The Meditation of the Primeval Ascetics: Sages like Sanaka represent the absolute pinnacle of lifelong celibacy, internal detachment, and unshakeable absorption in Brahman. Their intense adoration (samārādhyā) indicates that She is not a deity sought merely for temporary worldly gain, but the ultimate, non-dual absolute targeted by the most advanced ascetics in existence.

name: शिवज्ञानप्रदायिनी
summary: (i) One who bestows knowledge relating to Śiva.
(ii) Śiva means auspiciousness; She gives the knowledge of Brahman.
(iii) The pure and peaceful Śiva can be known only through Śrīdevī, His vibratory power.
(iv) It may also be understood that Śiva gives knowledge about Śrīdevī. This continues the Dakṣiṇāmūrti meaning of the previous name.
searchText: 727 शिवज्ञानप्रदायिनी शिवविषयकं ज्ञानं प्रकर्षेण दत्ते। शिवो ज्ञानप्रदायी यस्याः सा। प्रकर्षेण — excellently; fully यस्याः — whose; of whom (i) one who bestows knowledge relating to śiva. (ii) śiva means auspiciousness; she gives the knowledge of brahman. (iii) the pure and peaceful śiva can be known only through śrīdevī, his vibratory power. (iv) it may also be understood that śiva gives knowledge about śrīdevī. this continues the dakṣiṇāmūrti meaning of the previous name. in the saubhāgyabhāskara, bhāskararāya outlines the ultimate crown of insight: the granting of absolute awakening: the individual soul can know many relative sciences, but it remains bound until it uncovers the true nature of śiva (pure consciousness). she acts as śivajñānapradāyinī, directly delivering this supreme, non-dual realization. she reveals śiva, vanishing the illusion of separation and initiating final liberation.
esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara, Bhāskararāya outlines the ultimate crown of insight:

The Granting of Absolute Awakening: The individual soul can know many relative sciences, but it remains bound until it uncovers the true nature of Śiva (pure consciousness). She acts as Śivajñānapradāyinī, directly delivering this supreme, non-dual realization. She reveals Śiva, vanishing the illusion of separation and initiating final liberation.

name: चित्कला
samasa: चिदेव कला सच्चिदानन्दात्मनो ब्रह्मण एकदेश इव यस्यां सा।
अन्तःकरणोपाधिकं चैतन्यं निरुपाधिकायाश्चितः कला एकदेश इति।
summary: (i) One who is with divine consciousness.
(ii) Cit means knowledge or consciousness, and kalā means a part.
(iii) Brahman cannot be fully objectified, but Sat, Cit and Ānanda are spoken of as its inseparable aspects. Here She is the Cit aspect.
(iv) The consciousness present in the inner self of the soul is described as a part of Brahman; She is that Cit-kalā.

name: आनन्दकलिका
samasa: आनन्द एव कलैकदेश इव यस्याः सा।
summary: (i) One who is the bud of divine bliss.
(ii) As the consciousness of the soul is a part of Brahman-consciousness, the bliss of the soul is a part of Brahman-bliss.
(iii) She is the form of supreme bliss, Sat-cit-ānanda.
(iv) Kalikā means a bud. The bliss latent in the soul is like a bud that can, at the proper time, open into supreme bliss.
searchText: 729 आनन्दकलिका आनन्द एव कलैकदेश इव यस्याः सा। जीवगतानन्दैकदेशरूपा। (स्वार्थे कः)। यस्याः — whose; of whom (i) one who is the bud of divine bliss. (ii) as the consciousness of the soul is a part of brahman-consciousness, the bliss of the soul is a part of brahman-bliss. (iii) she is the form of supreme bliss, sat-cit-ānanda. (iv) kalikā means a bud. the bliss latent in the soul is like a bud that can, at the proper time, open into supreme bliss. bhāskararāya maps the internal experience of joy inside bound souls: the blossom of joy: the unconditioned bliss of brahman is massive and shoreless. inside individual living beings (jīvas), this joy is experienced as a tiny, subtle fraction—a delicate bud of happiness (ānanda-aika-deśa-rūpā) that breaks open during moments of deep love or peace. she is that exact internal blossom of joy, waiting to expand into full cosmic realization.

She is that exact internal blossom of joy, waiting to expand into full cosmic realization.

name: प्रेमरूपा
samasa: प्रेम स्नेहो भक्तिरेव स्वरूपं यस्याः सा।
summary: (i) One who is in the form of love.
(ii) The love or devotion of the devotees towards Her is indicated as Śrīdevī herself.
(iii) Love takes different names according to its object: devotion towards elders, love towards equals, affection towards children. She is present in all these forms.
searchText: 730 प्रेमरूपा प्रेम स्नेहो भक्तिरेव स्वरूपं यस्याः सा। प्रेम — love यस्याः — whose; of whom (i) one who is in the form of love. (ii) the love or devotion of the devotees towards her is indicated as śrīdevī herself. (iii) love takes different names according to its object: devotion towards elders, love towards equals, affection towards children. she is present in all these forms. in the saubhāgyabhāskara, bhāskararāya concludes this major sequence by defining the emotional essence of the absolute: pure devotion incarnate: she does not simply feel affection or receive love. her entire essential substance is nothing but pure, unadulterated divine love itself (prema-svarūpa). this love is the absolute emotional expression of non-duality—the intense attraction that pulls the fragment back into the whole, comforting the seeker that the heart of reality is pure love.

name: प्रियङ्करी
summary: (i) One who grants what is dear to Her devotees.
(ii) She gives what devotees seek, according to their inner disposition, whether enjoyment or liberation.
(iii) As soul-consciousness, She causes interest in worldly objects through the senses, and also gives the knowledge that even this interest arises because of the Self.
searchText: 731 प्रियङ्करी प्रियं करोतीति। प्रियं — dear; desired (i) one who grants what is dear to her devotees. name: नन्दिविद्या
summary: (i) One who is in the form of the mantra taught by Nandikeśvara.
(ii) Among the sages connected with Śrīvidyā, Nandi is also counted along with Manu, Candra, Kubera, Lopāmudrā, Agastya, Agni, Sūrya, Indra, Skanda, Śiva and others.
searchText: 733 नन्दिविद्या नन्दिनो नन्दिकेश्वरस्य विद्या तदुपासितविद्या। विद्या — knowledge; mantra (i) one who is in the form of the mantra taught by nandikeśvara. name: नटेश्वरी

(iii) Chidambara may mean the sacred place or the ether of the heart.
(iv) She dances together with Naṭeśa, as indicated in the Soundaryalaharī verse describing the dance of Śiva and Śakti.

name: मिथ्याजगदधिष्ठाना
samasa: मिथ्यारूपस्य जगतोऽधिष्ठानं भानाधिकरणं रजतस्येव शुक्तिः। (गुणत्रयापचयविवक्षया स्त्रीलिङ्गम्।)
जगदधिष्ठानरूपं ब्रह्मैव अनः प्राणो यस्याः सा।
summary: (i) One who is the substratum of the falsely appearing world.
(ii) Just as a shell is mistaken for silver until true knowledge arises, the changing universe appears on the real substratum, Śrīdevī as the Supreme Being.
(iii) Scriptural statements are cited to show that duality is ultimately illusory and that the Supreme alone is real.
(iv) This name can also be interpreted as She whose breath is Brahman, the support of the universe.
(v) In the tantric view, the universe is not different from Brahman, just as a pot is not different from clay; perceived difference is only apparent.
searchText: 735 मिथ्याजगदधिष्ठाना मिथ्यारूपस्य जगतोऽधिष्ठानं भानाधिकरणं रजतस्येव शुक्तिः। (गुणत्रयापचयविवक्षया स्त्रीलिङ्गम्।) जगदधिष्ठानरूपं ब्रह्मैव अनः प्राणो यस्याः सा। यस्याः — whose; of whom (i) one who is the substratum of the falsely appearing world. (ii) just as a shell is mistaken for silver until true knowledge arises, the changing universe appears on the real substratum, śrīdevī as the supreme being. (iii) scriptural statements are cited to show that duality is ultimately illusory and that the supreme alone is real. (iv) this name can also be interpreted as she whose breath is brahman, the support of the universe. (v) in the tantric view, the universe is not different from brahman, just as a pot is not different from clay; perceived difference is only apparent. bhāskararāya navigates a critical, high-tier advaita vedānta non-dual equation: the reality behind the appearance: in classical philosophy, an illusion requires a real base to appear—just as a silver illusion (rajata) requires the actual mother-of-pearl shell (śukti) beneath it. the material universe is mithyā (transient, relative, and non-permanent). she is mithyājagadadhiṣṭhānā because she is the absolute, unchanging consciousness baseline that supports this entire changing projection, allowing it to be perceived.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya navigates a critical, high-tier Advaita Vedānta non-dual equation:

The Reality behind the Appearance: In classical philosophy, an illusion requires a real base to appear—just as a silver illusion (rajata) requires the actual mother-of-pearl shell (śukti) beneath it. The material universe is mithyā (transient, relative, and non-permanent). She is Mithyājagadadhiṣṭhānā because She is the absolute, unchanging consciousness baseline that supports this entire changing projection, allowing it to be perceived.

name: मुक्तिदा

summary: (i) One who gives liberation.
(ii) The Purāṇas say that those desirous of freedom should seek refuge in Parvatī, Parameśvarī, the Self of all beings and of Śiva.
(iii) Those who worship the supreme Śakti, whether by regular or irregular methods, do not remain bound in saṃsāra; they are indeed liberated.

name: मुक्तिरूपिणी
wordByWord: Word meanings are read directly from the samāsa expression above.
summary: (i) One who is the form of liberation itself.
(ii) Liberation is not merely an external result but the manifestation of one's own bliss when ignorance is removed.
(iii) The true nature of the Self is hidden by ignorance; when that ignorance goes, the self-bliss shines forth. She is that true bliss.
(iv) Knowledge does not create liberation; it reveals the Self that was veiled.
searchText: 737 मुक्तिरूपिणी मुक्तिरेव रूपमस्याः अस्तीति। word meanings are read directly from the samāsa expression above. (i) one who is the form of liberation itself. (ii) liberation is not merely an external result but the manifestation of one's own bliss when ignorance is removed. esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara, Bhāskararāya constructs an immediate non-dual refinement to prevent structural duality:

Realizing Her person is identical to entering the state of unconditioned, boundless freedom.

name: लास्यप्रिया
summary: (i) One who is fond of dance.
(ii) The dance performed by women is called lāsya, while male dance is called tāṇḍava.
(iii) Śrīdevī Herself dances and is also pleased with the dance of others.
(iv) As in royal courts and temples, dance and music are offered before the Empress Śrīdevī. She is pleased by dance performed with rhythm, song and devotion.
searchText: 738 लास्यप्रिया लास्यं नर्तनं प्रियं यस्याः सा। तालैर्नृत्यगीतयोः समकालपरिच्छेदो वा लयः। तस्य करी कर्त्री। प्रियं — dear; desired यस्याः — whose; of whom (i) one who is fond of dance. (ii) the dance performed by women is called lāsya, while male dance is called tāṇḍava. (iii) śrīdevī herself dances and is also pleased with the dance of others. (iv) as in royal courts and temples, dance and music are offered before the empress śrīdevī. she is pleased by dance performed with rhythm, song and devotion. bhāskararāya details the gentle, creative properties of her energy: the joy of harmonious expression: while the masculine tāṇḍava represents fierce, destructive kinetic velocity, लास्य (lāsya) represents the highly refined, graceful, and harmonizing dance frequency associated with creation and nourishment. she is deeply affectionate toward this gentle expression, reflecting her mode of uplifting the cosmos through pure grace.

name: लयकरी

summary: (i) One who causes absorption.
(ii) Laya is a special state of mind, greater than ordinary meditation.
(iii) When the mind becomes wholly absorbed in seeing, hearing or contemplation, it forgets everything else. Such absorption in the Supreme Being is granted by Her.
(iv) Laya also refers to the measured time of music and dance; She is that form also.

(iv) laya also refers to the measured time of music and dance; she is that form also. bhāskararāya details three profound operational paths for this title: cosmic dissolution: she safely draws the manifest universe back into its unmanifest source during the grand collapse. rhythmic absorption: she acts as the soul of time-keeping (laya) that binds music and dance into harmony. mental stillness: for the practitioner, she coordinates that unique, quiet state of consciousness (citta-avasthā-viśeṣa) where all cognitive ripples dissolve, leading to deep samādhi.

Cosmic Dissolution: She safely draws the manifest universe back into its unmanifest source during the grand collapse.
Rhythmic Absorption: She acts as the soul of time-keeping (laya) that binds music and dance into harmony.
Mental Stillness: For the practitioner, She coordinates that unique, quiet state of consciousness (citta-avasthā-viśeṣa) where all cognitive ripples dissolve, leading to deep Samādhi.

name: लज्जा

(iv) The word Hrīṃ is connected with modesty and is an important bīja of Śrīdevī. Thus lajjā also points cryptically to that bīja.

(ii) From this name onwards the beautiful form of Śrīdevī is described in a series of names.
