name: भवदावसुधावृष्टिः
samasa: भवः संसार एव दावो वनवह्निस्तस्य शामकत्वात्सुधावृष्टिः पीयूषवर्षमिव।
भवः संसार एव दावोऽरण्यम्। तस्य पुङ्खानुपुङ्खतया उज्जीवनात्पीयूषवृष्टिरिव।
भवं परशिवं दत्ते वसु रत्नं धनं च धत्ते एतादृशी वृष्टिरिति त्रिपदं नाम वा।
summary: (i) One who is the rain of nectar falling on the forest-fire of worldly existence.
(ii) Bhava means worldly existence and dāva means forest fire. She is the nectar-rain that extinguishes that fire.
(iii) The name may also be split to mean She who grants Śiva, wealth and auspicious abundance.
(iv) For the great devotees of Śrīdevī, both enjoyment and liberation are available by Her grace.
searchText: 742 भवदावसुधावृष्टिः भवः संसार एव दावो वनवह्निस्तस्य शामकत्वात्सुधावृष्टिः पीयूषवर्षमिव। भवः संसार एव दावोऽरण्यम्। तस्य पुङ्खानुपुङ्खतया उज्जीवनात्पीयूषवृष्टिरिव। भवं परशिवं दत्ते वसु रत्नं धनं च धत्ते एतादृशी वृष्टिरिति त्रिपदं नाम वा। भवः — worldly existence दत्ते — gives नाम — name (i) one who is the rain of nectar falling on the forest-fire of worldly existence. (ii) bhava means worldly existence and dāva means forest fire. she is the nectar-rain that extinguishes that fire. (iii) the name may also be split to mean she who grants śiva, wealth and auspicious abundance. (iv) for the great devotees of śrīdevī, both enjoyment and liberation are available by her grace. bhāskararāya coordinates the alchemical transformations triggered by her grace: the extinguisher of rebirth: under the primary track, the cyclical friction of worldly existence (bhava) is a blazing forest fire (dāva) burning the bound soul. her downpour of non-dual nectar (sudhā-vṛṣṭi) cools this internal panic. the nourisher of spirit: alternatively, the world is a parched forest; her nectar acts as the refreshing irrigation that brings dead spiritual channels back to life. under the split-word key, she functions as a continuous downpour that systematically delivers the direct realization of lord śiva alongside all material assets.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya coordinates the alchemical transformations triggered by Her grace:

The Extinguisher of Rebirth: Under the primary track, the cyclical friction of worldly existence (Bhava) is a blazing forest fire (dāva) burning the bound soul. Her downpour of non-dual nectar (sudhā-vṛṣṭi) cools this internal panic.

The Nourisher of Spirit: Alternatively, the world is a parched forest; Her nectar acts as the refreshing irrigation that brings dead spiritual channels back to life. Under the split-word key, She functions as a continuous downpour that systematically delivers the direct realization of Lord Śiva alongside all material assets.

name: पापारण्यदवानला
samasa: पापान्येवारण्यानि दुःखजनकत्वात्। तेषां दवानल इव नाशकत्वात्।
पापारण्यानां ये दवाः दावाग्नयो नाशनोपायभूता उपास्त्यादयस्तेषां अनान् प्राणान् लाति आदत्ते तान् जीवयतीति।
summary: (i) One who is the forest fire that burns down the forest of sins.
(ii) She destroys sins just as a forest fire consumes a forest.
(iii) Sins such as prārabdha, sañcita and āgāmi cannot be destroyed merely by worldly enjoyment; by Her grace and true knowledge they are ruined.
(iv) Remembering the feet of the supreme Śakti is taught as the highest expiation for sins committed knowingly or unknowingly.
searchText: 743 पापारण्यदवानला पापान्येवारण्यानि दुःखजनकत्वात्। तेषां दवानल इव नाशकत्वात्। पापारण्यानां दवानलो यस्या नाम सा। पापारण्यानां ये दवाः दावाग्नयो नाशनोपायभूता उपास्त्यादयस्तेषां अनान् प्राणान् लाति आदत्ते तान् जीवयतीति। नाम — name (i) one who is the forest fire that burns down the forest of sins. (ii) she destroys sins just as a forest fire consumes a forest. (iii) sins such as prārabdha, sañcita and āgāmi cannot be destroyed merely by worldly enjoyment; by her grace and true knowledge they are ruined. (iv) remembering the feet of the supreme śakti is taught as the highest expiation for sins committed knowingly or unknowingly. in the saubhāgyabhāskara, bhāskararāya maps her aggressive corrective purification: the incineration of accumulated karmas: sins (pāpa) resemble a dense, dark, overgrown jungle because they form an interconnected tangle that creates existential blockages and blocks out the light of the self. she acts as davānalā—a high-velocity fire that leaves no branch unburned, reducing the seeker's historical load of negative impressions (saṁskāras) to pure ash.
esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara, Bhāskararāya maps Her aggressive corrective purification:

The Incineration of Accumulated Karmas: Sins (pāpa) resemble a dense, dark, overgrown jungle because they form an interconnected tangle that creates existential blockages and blocks out the light of the Self. She acts as Davānalā—a high-velocity fire that leaves no branch unburned, reducing the seeker's historical load of negative impressions (saṁskāras) to pure ash.

name: दौर्भाग्यतूलवातूला
samasa: दौर्भाग्यमेव तूलं कार्पासं तस्य वातूल इव वात्येव दूरं निरासिकेति।
दौर्भाग्यं तूलं येषां ते दौर्भाग्यतूलाः दौर्भाग्यस्य तूलस्येव निरासका धर्मविशेषाः। अत एव ते धर्मा एव वातूले गौण्यावृत्त्या तेन दौर्भाग्यतूलः वातूला यस्याः सकाशात् सा।
दौर्भाग्यनिरासकानि कर्माणि यस्याः सकाशादुद्भवन्ति यस्याः सम्बन्धीनि वा सा।
summary: (i) One who is the cyclone that drives away the cotton-bundles of misfortune.
(ii) Misfortune from actions of previous births may appear huge, like bundles of cotton, but Her force scatters it like a strong wind.
(iii) The actions that remove such misfortune arise from Her and are connected with Her grace.
searchText: 744 दौर्भाग्यतूलवातूला दौर्भाग्यमेव तूलं कार्पासं तस्य वातूल इव वात्येव दूरं निरासिकेति। दौर्भाग्यं तूलं येषां ते दौर्भाग्यतूलाः दौर्भाग्यस्य तूलस्येव निरासका धर्मविशेषाः। अत एव ते धर्मा एव वातूले गौण्यावृत्त्या तेन दौर्भाग्यतूलः वातूला यस्याः सकाशात् सा। दौर्भाग्यनिरासकानि कर्माणि यस्याः सकाशादुद्भवन्ति यस्याः सम्बन्धीनि वा सा। यस्याः — whose; of whom (i) one who is the cyclone that drives away the cotton-bundles of misfortune. (ii) misfortune from actions of previous births may appear huge, like bundles of cotton, but her force scatters it like a strong wind. (iii) the actions that remove such misfortune arise from her and are connected with her grace. bhāskararāya details the immediate shift in internal and external alignment: the cyclone of auspiciousness: misfortune (daurbhāgya) has no solid ontological reality; it is merely a weightless collection of negative conditions, like cotton flakes (tūla). she acts as a powerful whirlwind (vātūlā), blowing these scattered blocks entirely out of the practitioner's path, replacing structural obstacles with instant alignment.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya details the immediate shift in internal and external alignment:

The Cyclone of Auspiciousness: Misfortune (daurbhāgya) has no solid ontological reality; it is merely a weightless collection of negative conditions, like cotton flakes (tūla). She acts as a powerful whirlwind (vātūlā), blowing these scattered blocks entirely out of the practitioner's path, replacing structural obstacles with instant alignment.

name: जराध्वान्तरविप्रभा
wordByWord: Word meanings are read directly from the samāsa expression above.
summary: (i) One who removes the darkness of old age by Her light.
(ii) Childhood, youth and old age belong to the body, not to the eternal Self.
(iii) When the knowledge that body and soul are different arises, the fear of old age and death is removed.
(iv) She is like the sunlight that removes the darkness called old age.
searchText: 745 जराध्वान्तरविप्रभा जरैव ध्वान्तमन्धकारं तस्य नाशकत्वाद्रवेः प्रभेव। word meanings are read directly from the samāsa expression above. (i) one who removes the darkness of old age by her light. (ii) childhood, youth and old age belong to the body, not to the eternal self. (iii) when the knowledge that body and soul are different arises, the fear of old age and death is removed. (iv) she is like the sunlight that removes the darkness called old age. bhāskararāya purges this name of simple superficial external physical formatting: the ray of timeless vitality: jarā represents the dark creeping rust of time, decay, mental fog, and biological slowing down that veils the body's internal radiance. she is raviprabhā — the blinding, rejuvenating morning sun ray that cuts through this internal stagnation, restoring the pristine, timeless vitality of pure consciousness inside the subtle matrix.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya purges this name of simple superficial external physical formatting:

The Ray of Timeless Vitality: Jarā represents the dark creeping rust of time, decay, mental fog, and biological slowing down that veils the body's internal radiance. She is Raviprabhā — the blinding, rejuvenating morning sun ray that cuts through this internal stagnation, restoring the pristine, timeless vitality of pure consciousness inside the subtle matrix.

name: भाग्याब्धिचन्द्रिका
wordByWord: Word meanings are read directly from the samāsa expression above.
summary: (i) One who is the moonlight that illumines the ocean of good fortune.
(ii) Good fortune comes from virtue done in previous births. When others recognise a person's good fortune, the joy swells like the ocean under moonlight.
(iii) By Her compassion, She makes the good fortune of Her devotees expand and become recognised.
searchText: 746 भाग्याब्धिचन्द्रिका भाग्यलक्षणस्याब्धेरुल्लासकत्वाच्चन्द्रिका कौमुदीव। word meanings are read directly from the samāsa expression above. (i) one who is the moonlight that illumines the ocean of good fortune. (ii) good fortune comes from virtue done in previous births. when others recognise a person's good fortune, the joy swells like the ocean under moonlight. (iii) by her compassion, she makes the good fortune of her devotees expand and become recognised. in the saubhāgyabhāskara, bhāskararāya decodes the internal dynamics of spiritual expansion: the tidal surge of grace: when the full moon shines, the ocean naturally experiences massive gravitational tidal surges. similarly, when her moon-like light (candrikā) rises inside the practitioner's consciousness, the dormant ocean of inner spiritual and material merit (bhāgya) expands dramatically, flooding the entire system with active prosperity.
esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara, Bhāskararāya decodes the internal dynamics of spiritual expansion:

The Tidal Surge of Grace: When the full moon shines, the ocean naturally experiences massive gravitational tidal surges. Similarly, when Her moon-like light (Candrikā) rises inside the practitioner's consciousness, the dormant ocean of inner spiritual and material merit (bhāgya) expands dramatically, flooding the entire system with active prosperity.

name: भक्तचित्तकेकिघनाघना
wordByWord: Word meanings are read directly from the samāsa expression above.
summary: (i) One who is the cloud that excites the peacocks which are the minds of Her devotees.
(ii) Just as peacocks dance when they see dark rainclouds, devotees' minds rejoice on seeing Śrīdevī.
(iii) Her continuous life-work nourishes the devotees spiritually, like rainclouds sustaining life.
searchText: 747 भक्तचित्तकेकिघनाघना भक्तानां चित्तान्येव केकिनो मयूरास्तेषामुल्लासकत्वाद्घनाघना मेघस्वरूपा। word meanings are read directly from the samāsa expression above. (i) one who is the cloud that excites the peacocks which are the minds of her devotees. (ii) just as peacocks dance when they see dark rainclouds, devotees' minds rejoice on seeing śrīdevī. (iii) her continuous life-work nourishes the devotees spiritually, like rainclouds sustaining life. bhāskararāya tracks the emotional and structural relief of the spiritual heart: the relief of the monsoon: peacocks suffer during the hot summer months and burst into ecstatic dance the exact moment they hear the first low thunder of a dark monsoon rain-cloud (ghanāghana). the devotee's mind, parched by the anxieties of worldly life, experiences this exact cooling relief upon her entry, bursting into natural, joyous spiritual alignment.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya tracks the emotional and structural relief of the spiritual heart:

The Relief of the Monsoon: Peacocks suffer during the hot summer months and burst into ecstatic dance the exact moment they hear the first low thunder of a dark monsoon rain-cloud (ghanāghana). The devotee's mind, parched by the anxieties of worldly life, experiences this exact cooling relief upon Her entry, bursting into natural, joyous spiritual alignment.

name: रोगपर्वतदम्भोलिः
samasa: रोगा एव पर्वताः स्थूलत्वात्तेषां दम्भोलिर्वज्र इव।
summary: (i) One who is the thunderbolt that strikes the mountain of diseases.
(ii) Diseases are compared to mountains, and Śrīdevī to Indra's thunderbolt that can shatter them.
(iii) This recalls the name Sarvavyādhi-praśamanī, the pacifier of all diseases.
searchText: 748 रोगपर्वतदम्भोलिः रोगा एव पर्वताः स्थूलत्वात्तेषां दम्भोलिर्वज्र इव। पर्वताः — mountains (i) one who is the thunderbolt that strikes the mountain of diseases. (ii) diseases are compared to mountains, and śrīdevī to indra's thunderbolt that can shatter them. (iii) this recalls the name sarvavyādhi-praśamanī, the pacifier of all diseases. in the saubhāgyabhāskara, bhāskararāya defines her absolute therapeutic precision: the shattering of disease vectors: chronic physical illnesses and deep-seated subconscious mental complexes behave like massive, unmoving stone mountains (parvata), blocking path progression. she is the piercing, high-voltage diamond thunderbolt (dambholi — indra's vajra) that strikes these structural densities, shattering the root vectors of disease instantly.
esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara, Bhāskararāya defines Her absolute therapeutic precision:

The Shattering of Disease Vectors: Chronic physical illnesses and deep-seated subconscious mental complexes behave like massive, unmoving stone mountains (parvata), blocking path progression. She is the piercing, high-voltage diamond thunderbolt (Dambholi — Indra's Vajra) that strikes these structural densities, shattering the root vectors of disease instantly.

name: मृत्युदारुकुठारिका
wordByWord: Word meanings are read directly from the samāsa expression above.
summary: (i) One who is the axe that cuts down the tree of death.
(ii) Death and related pains grow like a tree rooted in birth and time; She cuts it down like an axe.
(iii) A devotee who firmly thinks, "I worship Śrīdevī and will gain liberation," is freed from the fear of death.
(iv) Therefore death is aptly compared to a tree and Śrīdevī to the axe.
searchText: 749 मृत्युदारुकुठारिका मृत्युरूपदारूणां कुठारा इव। word meanings are read directly from the samāsa expression above. (i) one who is the axe that cuts down the tree of death. (ii) death and related pains grow like a tree rooted in birth and time; she cuts it down like an axe. (iii) a devotee who firmly thinks, "i worship śrīdevī and will gain liberation," is freed from the fear of death. (iv) therefore death is aptly compared to a tree and śrīdevī to the axe. bhāskararāya details the termination of systemic mortality: the cutter of the rebirth trunk: physical death (mṛtyu) is the heavy, stubborn log framework that keeps the soul trapped in the engine of rebirth. as kuṭhārikā, she strikes directly at the base root of this wood, slicing the illusion of mortality cleanly away and anchoring the practitioner in non-dual permanence.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya details the termination of systemic mortality:

The Cutter of the Rebirth Trunk: Physical death (Mṛtyu) is the heavy, stubborn log framework that keeps the soul trapped in the engine of rebirth. As Kuṭhārikā, She strikes directly at the base root of this wood, slicing the illusion of mortality cleanly away and anchoring the practitioner in non-dual permanence.

name: महेश्वरी
summary: (i) One who is the supreme sovereign.
(ii) This name differs from the earlier Māheśvarī by the long and short form of the vowel, and therefore is not a mere repetition.
searchText: 750 महेश्वरी महती च सेश्वरी च। महती — great (i) one who is the supreme sovereign. (ii) this name differs from the earlier māheśvarī by the long and short form of the vowel, and therefore is not a mere repetition. in the saubhāgyabhāskara, bhāskararāya concludes this structural sequence by establishing her premier position over cosmic leadership: sovereignty past the lesser gods: a local ruler governs a territory; the secondary deities govern specific cosmic departments. she is maheśvarī because her sovereign rule (īśvaritva) is completely unbounded (mahat). she stands as the absolute, non-dual empress who formats the laws of nature, directs the motion of universes, and acts as the supreme consort of maheśvara, sealing all cosmic authority directly to her person.
esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara, Bhāskararāya concludes this structural sequence by establishing Her premier position over cosmic leadership:

Sovereignty Past the Lesser Gods: A local ruler governs a territory; the secondary deities govern specific cosmic departments. She is Maheśvarī because Her sovereign rule (Īśvaritva) is completely unbounded (Mahat). She stands as the absolute, non-dual empress who formats the laws of nature, directs the motion of universes, and acts as the supreme consort of Maheśvara, sealing all cosmic authority directly to Her person.

name: महाकाली
summary: (i) One who is great and in the form of Kāli.
(ii) Kāli may mean She who determines time; Mahākāli determines even the time of Yama, the deity of death.
(iii) She is worshipped as Mahākāli, the consort of Mahākāla, and as the deity of the first story of Saptashatī.

(iv) In Her fierce form She appears dark. Death itself is explained as an act of compassion that temporarily releases beings from the bondage of action and prepares the way for peace and future growth.
searchText: 751 महाकाली महती च सा काली च। महाकालस्य स्त्री। महती — great सा — she काली — kālī महाकालस्य — of mahākāla (i) one who is great and in the form of kāli. (ii) kāli may mean she who determines time; mahākāli determines even the time of yama, the deity of death. (iii) she is worshipped as mahākāli, the consort of mahākāla, and as the deity of the first story of saptashatī. (iv) in her fierce form she appears dark. death itself is explained as an act of compassion that temporarily releases beings from the bondage of action and prepares the way for peace and future growth. in the saubhāgyabhāskara, bhāskararāya reveals the non-dual baseline tracking time and energy: the devourer of time: mahākāla is the supreme aspect of consciousness that dissolves the entire universe at the end of an epoch. she is mahākālī because she is the dynamic kinetic capacity that acts as the container and consumer of even that ultimate time-principle. she marks the final absolute rest state where all temporal mutations return to stillness.
esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara, Bhāskararāya reveals the non-dual baseline tracking time and energy:

The Devourer of Time: Mahākāla is the supreme aspect of consciousness that dissolves the entire universe at the end of an epoch. She is Mahākālī because She is the dynamic kinetic capacity that acts as the container and consumer of even that ultimate time-principle. She marks the final absolute rest state where all temporal mutations return to stillness.

name: महाग्रासा
summary: (i) One who is the great devourer.
(ii) The Upaniṣad speaks of Brahmins and Kṣatriyas as food and death as condiment for the Supreme.
(iii) At dissolution She, as the supreme consuming power, swallows the universe and the thirty-six tattvas.
searchText: 752 महाग्रासा महानपरिमितो ग्रासः कवलो यस्याः सा। ग्रासः — mouthful; devouring यस्याः — whose; of whom (i) one who is the great devourer. (ii) the upaniṣad speaks of brahmins and kṣatriyas as food and death as condiment for the supreme. (iii) at dissolution she, as the supreme consuming power, swallows the universe and the thirty-six tattvas. bhāskararāya defines her absolute ease during cosmic reabsorption: the single morsel of creation: during the grand dissolution (mahāpralaya), the creation of names, forms, elements, and structural spatial coordinates is not dissolved slowly or with friction. she takes the entire multi-dimensional cosmos as a single, effortless mouthful (grāsa), absorbing it completely back into her non-dual unmanifested matrix.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya defines Her absolute ease during cosmic reabsorption:

The Single Morsel of Creation: During the grand dissolution (mahāpralaya), the creation of names, forms, elements, and structural spatial coordinates is not dissolved slowly or with friction. She takes the entire multi-dimensional cosmos as a single, effortless mouthful (grāsa), absorbing it completely back into Her non-dual unmanifested matrix.

name: महाशना
summary: (i) One who is a great eater.
(ii) Continuing the previous name, She consumes the entire animate and inanimate universe.
(iii) This name differs from the earlier Mahāsanā and should not be confused with it.
searchText: 753 महाशना महच्चराचरकर्मकत्वादशनं यस्याः सा। यस्याः — whose; of whom (i) one who is a great eater. (ii) continuing the previous name, she consumes the entire animate and inanimate universe. (iii) this name differs from the earlier mahāsanā and should not be confused with it. in the saubhāgyabhāskara, this reinforces her ultimate consuming authority: the absolute appetite: complementing mahāgrāsā, she is mahāśanā because her ultimate consumption (aśana) encompasses the entire created universe. nothing is left over as waste matter outside her field; every single spark of objective appearance is digested, showing her status as the unified final terminal for all cosmic properties.
esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara, this reinforces Her ultimate consuming authority:

The Absolute Appetite: Complementing Mahāgrāsā, She is Mahāśanā because Her ultimate consumption (aśana) encompasses the entire created universe. Nothing is left over as waste matter outside Her field; every single spark of objective appearance is digested, showing Her status as the unified final terminal for all cosmic properties.

name: अपर्णा
summary: (i) One who is without debt, or one who ate not even a leaf.
(ii) She gives more than devotees expect and is therefore not indebted to them.
(iii) As Pārvatī, while performing penance to marry Śiva, She did not eat even a leaf; hence the name Aparṇā.
(iv) The name may also mean one without falling or decline.
searchText: 754 अपर्णा अपगतमृणं यस्याः सा। पर्णं पतनमिति नैरुक्तात्पतनरहितेति। पर्णमप्यदनीयत्वेन न विद्यते यस्याः सा। यस्याः — whose; of whom (i) one who is without debt, or one who ate not even a leaf. (ii) she gives more than devotees expect and is therefore not indebted to them. (iii) as pārvatī, while performing penance to marry śiva, she did not eat even a leaf; hence the name aparṇā. (iv) the name may also mean one without falling or decline. bhāskararāya details the foundational asceticism and non-dual perfection of this name: the pinnacle of tapas: historically, during her intense tapas to fuse with shiva, she abandoned consuming even fallen leaves (parṇa). the fallless substratum: under the etymological track, parṇa denotes falling (patana); she is entirely free from falling into spatial or temporal degradation. the debtless absolute: she has no outstanding liabilities or cosmic debts (ṛṇa), existing completely secure in her independent status.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya details the foundational asceticism and non-dual perfection of this name:
The Pinnacle of Tapas: Historically, during Her intense tapas to fuse with Shiva, She abandoned consuming even fallen leaves (parṇa).
The Fallless Substratum: Under the etymological track, parṇa denotes falling (patana); She is entirely free from falling into spatial or temporal degradation.
The Debtless Absolute: She has no outstanding liabilities or cosmic debts (ṛṇa), existing completely secure in Her independent status.

name: चण्डिका
summary: (i) One who is fierce or angry.
(ii) She is angry with those who lack devotion.
(iii) Anger is also described as a characteristic of Brahman in certain scriptural passages.
(iv) In Saptashatī the combined form of Mahākāli, Mahālakṣmī and Mahāsarasvatī is called Caṇḍikā.
searchText: 755 चण्डिका अभक्तेषु कोपनत्वात् चण्डयतीति। अभक्तेषु — towards non-devotees (i) one who is fierce or angry. (ii) she is angry with those who lack devotion. (iii) anger is also described as a characteristic of brahman in certain scriptural passages. (iv) in saptashatī the combined form of mahākāli, mahālakṣmī and mahāsarasvatī is called caṇḍikā. bhāskararāya details the active mechanics of her protective wrath: the fierce filter of justice: her anger is not a mundane psychological distortion. she is caṇḍikā because she expresses an intense, high-velocity defensive fury (caṇḍatva) directed specifically at unrighteous entities and stubborn internal blockages (abhaktas). this fierce light tears down the structures of ego, providing the necessary shocks to realign the soul.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya details the active mechanics of Her protective wrath:

The Fierce Filter of Justice: Her anger is not a mundane psychological distortion. She is Caṇḍikā because She expresses an intense, high-velocity defensive fury (caṇḍatva) directed specifically at unrighteous entities and stubborn internal blockages (abhaktas). This fierce light tears down the structures of ego, providing the necessary shocks to realign the soul.

name: चण्डमुण्डासुरनिषूदिनी
samasa: चण्डश्च मुण्डश्च, तौ च तावसुरौ च, तौ निषूदयतीति।
wordByWord: Word meanings are read directly from the samāsa expression above.
summary: (i) One who destroyed the demons Caṇḍa and Muṇḍa.
(ii) In the form of Kālī, She destroyed the demons Caṇḍa and Muṇḍa of the group of Śumbha and Niśumbha; hence She was named Cāmuṇḍā.
(iii) Other Purāṇic explanations also connect the name Cāmuṇḍā with the slaying of demons and the carrying of their heads.
(iv) This name recalls one of the forms among the eight mothers.
searchText: 756 चण्डमुण्डासुरनिषूदिनी चण्डश्च मुण्डश्च, तौ च तावसुरौ च, तौ निषूदयतीति। word meanings are read directly from the samāsa expression above. (i) one who destroyed the demons caṇḍa and muṇḍa. (ii) in the form of kālī, she destroyed the demons caṇḍa and muṇḍa of the group of śumbha and niśumbha; hence she was named cāmuṇḍā. (iii) other purāṇic explanations also connect the name cāmuṇḍā with the slaying of demons and the carrying of their heads. (iv) this name recalls one of the forms among the eight mothers. in the saubhāgyabhāskara, bhāskararāya moves this historical battle into internal psychological coordinates: the extraction of passion and delusion: in the internal battlefield of the mind, चण्ड represents the violent force of aggressive passion (rajas), and मुण्ड represents the low, stubborn weight of intellectual blindness and delusion (tamas). as niṣūdinī, she extracts these twin root distortions from the subconscious channels, freeing the practitioner's path.
esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara, Bhāskararāya moves this historical battle into internal psychological coordinates:

The Extraction of Passion and Delusion: In the internal battlefield of the mind, चण्ड represents the violent force of aggressive passion (Rajas), and मुण्ड represents the low, stubborn weight of intellectual blindness and delusion (Tamas). As Niṣūdinī, She extracts these twin root distortions from the subconscious channels, freeing the practitioner's path.

name: क्षराक्षरात्मिका
samasa: क्षराण्यनियतसंख्यान्यक्षराणि वर्णानि आत्मा स्वरूपं यस्याः सा।
क्षरः सर्वाणि भूतानि कूटस्थोऽक्षर उच्यते। तदुभयमात्मा स्वरूपं यस्याः सा।
summary: (i) One whose form is both the perishable and the imperishable.
(ii) It may mean She is the form of countless letters or syllables, since mantras are formed from letters.
(iii) It may also mean She is the Self of both kṣara, the changing beings, and akṣara, the unchanging principle.
(iv) Thus She is the one and many, the source of mantra and the basis of all beings.
searchText: 757 क्षराक्षरात्मिका क्षराण्यनियतसंख्यान्यक्षराणि वर्णानि आत्मा स्वरूपं यस्याः सा। क्षरः सर्वाणि भूतानि कूटस्थोऽक्षर उच्यते। तदुभयमात्मा स्वरूपं यस्याः सा। वर्णानि — letters आत्मा — self; essence यस्याः — whose; of whom क्षरः — perishable सर्वाणि — all (i) one whose form is both the perishable and the imperishable. (ii) it may mean she is the form of countless letters or syllables, since mantras are formed from letters. (iii) it may also mean she is the self of both kṣara, the changing beings, and akṣara, the unchanging principle. (iv) thus she is the one and many, the source of mantra and the basis of all beings. bhāskararāya coordinates this directly with the master taxonomy of the bhagavad gītā (puruṣottama yoga): the encompasser of perishable and imperishable: kṣara represents the changing, material world of moving elements; akṣara represents the still, changeless, and hidden spiritual witness (kūṭastha). she is kṣarākṣarātmikā because both conditions are expressions of her non-dual body, acting as the bridge that synthesizes form and formlessness.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya coordinates this directly with the master taxonomy of the Bhagavad Gītā (Puruṣottama Yoga):

The Encompasser of Perishable and Imperishable: Kṣara represents the changing, material world of moving elements; Akṣara represents the still, changeless, and hidden spiritual witness (Kūṭastha). She is Kṣarākṣarātmikā because both conditions are expressions of Her non-dual body, acting as the bridge that synthesizes form and formlessness.

name: सर्वलोकेशी
wordByWord: Word meanings are read directly from the samāsa expression above.
summary: (i) One who is the ruler of all worlds.
(ii) All lokas are under Her authority; She is their sovereign mistress.
searchText: 758 सर्वलोकेशी सर्वेषां लोकानामीशी ईश्वरी। word meanings are read directly from the samāsa expression above. (i) one who is the ruler of all worlds. (ii) all lokas are under her authority; she is their sovereign mistress. bhāskararāya outlines the unbounded scope of her legislative throne: universal imperial sovereignty: her administrative authority is not bounded by a single solar system or isolated dimension. she rules over the complete multi-dimensional network of the fourteen operational worlds (sarva-loka), directing their laws of karma and evolutionary paths with neutral accuracy.

name: विश्वधारिणी
wordByWord: Word meanings are read directly from the samāsa expression above.
summary: (i) One who supports the universe.
(ii) The whole universe is borne, sustained and held by Her power.
searchText: 759 विश्वधारिणी विश्वं धारयतीति। word meanings are read directly from the samāsa expression above. (i) one who supports the universe. (ii) the whole universe is borne, sustained and held by her power. in the saubhāgyabhāskara, bhāskararāya details the physics of cosmic suspension: the structural pillar of existence: the universe does not hang in a vacuum by chance. she is viśvadhāriṇī because her dynamic energy field acts as the permanent, invisible structural support that holds planetary spheres, gravity metrics, and spatial dimensions perfectly in place, preventing cosmic collapse.
esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara, Bhāskararāya details the physics of cosmic suspension:

name: त्रिवर्गदात्री
samasa: त्रयाणां धर्मार्थकामानां वर्गः समूहः। (त्रिवर्गो धर्मकामार्थैश्चतुर्वर्गः समोक्षकैरित्यमरः)। तस्य दात्री।
wordByWord: Word meanings are read directly from the samāsa expression above.
summary: (i) One who gives the threefold aims of life.
(ii) Trivarga means dharma, artha and kāma. She grants these worldly aims.
(iii) Since mokṣa has already been described separately, this name here points especially to the three aims connected with worldly life.
searchText: 760 त्रिवर्गदात्री त्रयाणां धर्मार्थकामानां वर्गः समूहः। (त्रिवर्गो धर्मकामार्थैश्चतुर्वर्गः समोक्षकैरित्यमरः)। तस्य दात्री। word meanings are read directly from the samāsa expression above. (i) one who gives the threefold aims of life. (ii) trivarga means dharma, artha and kāma. she grants these worldly aims. (iii) since mokṣa has already been described separately, this name here points especially to the three aims connected with worldly life. bhāskararāya outlines her balanced coordination of earthly life: the organization of earthly aspiration: before a soul is ready for final liberation (mokṣa), it requires balanced evolution across the three mundane planes: धर्म (moral alignment), अर्थ (material stability), and काम (refined desire). she systematically dispenses the resources for this triple framework (trivarga), ensuring her children do not experience unnatural lack during their earthly journey.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya outlines Her balanced coordination of earthly life:

The Organization of Earthly Aspiration: Before a soul is ready for final liberation (Mokṣa), it requires balanced evolution across the three mundane planes: धर्म (moral alignment), अर्थ (material stability), and काम (refined desire). She systematically dispenses the resources for this triple framework (trivarga), ensuring Her children do not experience unnatural lack during their earthly journey.

name: सुभगा
summary: (i) One who is supremely fortunate and bestows auspicious affluence.
(ii) Bhaga includes prosperity, desire, greatness, power, effort and fame.
(iii) All affluence in moving and unmoving beings is due to Her.
(iv) Worship of auspicious women is an important part of Śrīvidyā practice because Śrīdevī Herself is present in such auspiciousness.
searchText: 761 सुभगा शोभना श्रीकाममाहात्म्यवीर्ययत्नकीर्त्यादयो भगाः यस्यां सा। शोभनो भगः सूर्यो यया सा। शोभना — auspicious; beautiful भगाः — fortunes; powers (i) one who is supremely fortunate and bestows auspicious affluence. (ii) bhaga includes prosperity, desire, greatness, power, effort and fame. (iii) all affluence in moving and unmoving beings is due to her. (iv) worship of auspicious women is an important part of śrīvidyā practice because śrīdevī herself is present in such auspiciousness. in the saubhāgyabhāskara*, bhāskararāya defines the internal components of भग (bhaga): the six divine splendors: classical lexicon rules define bhaga as a corporate set of six attributes: absolute wealth, virtue, fame, beauty, wisdom, and effortless intent. she is subhagā because she contains these traits in their most refined condition. the solar engine: alternatively, she is the inner consciousness that gives the sun (bhaga) its brilliant capacity to illuminate worlds.
esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara*, Bhāskararāya defines the internal components of भग (Bhaga):

The Six Divine Splendors: Classical lexicon rules define Bhaga as a corporate set of six attributes: absolute wealth, virtue, fame, beauty, wisdom, and effortless intent. She is Subhagā because She contains these traits in their most refined condition.
The Solar Engine: Alternatively, She is the inner consciousness that gives the sun (Bhaga) its brilliant capacity to illuminate worlds.

name: त्र्यम्बका
summary: (i) One who is three-eyed.
(ii) Her three eyes are the Sun, Moon and Fire.
(iii) The same meaning is conveyed in the names Trinayanā and Trilocanā.
(iv) She may also be understood as the mother of the three gods, Brahmā, Viṣṇu and Rudra.
searchText: 762 त्र्यम्बका त्रीण्यम्बकानि नेत्राणि यस्याः सा। त्रयाणां ब्रह्मविष्णुरुद्राणामम्बिका माता। नेत्राणि — eyes यस्याः — whose; of whom (i) one who is three-eyed. (ii) her three eyes are the sun, moon and fire. (iii) the same meaning is conveyed in the names trinayanā and trilocanā. (iv) she may also be understood as the mother of the three gods, brahmā, viṣṇu and rudra. bhāskararāya decodes the structural vision and parental priority built into this title: the vision past linear time: her three eyes correspond to the sun, the moon, and fire, scanning past, present, and future simultaneously. the parent of the trinity: under the second precise track, अम्बका means mother. she is tryambakā because she is the original maternal source (ambikā) that gives birth to and formats the entire administrative trinity of the cosmos—brahmā, viṣṇu, and rudra.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya decodes the structural vision and parental priority built into this title:
The Vision Past Linear Time: Her three eyes correspond to the Sun, the Moon, and Fire, scanning past, present, and future simultaneously.

The Parent of the Trinity: Under the second precise track, अम्बका means mother. She is Tryambakā because She is the original maternal source (Ambikā) that gives birth to and formats the entire administrative trinity of the cosmos—Brahmā, Viṣṇu, and Rudra.

name: त्रिगुणात्मिका
wordByWord: Word meanings are read directly from the samāsa expression above.
summary: (i) One who is in the form of the three guṇas: sattva, rajas and tamas.
(ii) Since the three deities are connected with these three qualities, She includes them all.
(iii) Though She manifests as the three guṇas, She is also described elsewhere as beyond guṇas.
searchText: 763 त्रिगुणात्मिका त्रिगुणात्मिका सत्त्वरजस्तमोरूपगुणत्रयसाम्यविग्रहा। word meanings are read directly from the samāsa expression above. (i) one who is in the form of the three guṇas: sattva, rajas and tamas. (ii) since the three deities are connected with these three qualities, she includes them all. (iii) though she manifests as the three guṇas, she is also described elsewhere as beyond guṇas. bhāskararāya details her identity with primordial nature (mūlaprakṛti): the matrix of material form: before the universe is structured, energy exists as a perfectly balanced, unmanifested equilibrium of the three qualities (guṇas). she is triguṇātmikā because her manifest body forms the very fabric of this triadic matrix, directing how light (sattva), movement (rajas), and mass (tamas) combine to weave the material universe.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya details Her identity with primordial nature (Mūlaprakṛti):

The Matrix of Material Form: Before the universe is structured, energy exists as a perfectly balanced, unmanifested equilibrium of the three qualities (guṇas). She is Triguṇātmikā because Her manifest body forms the very fabric of this triadic matrix, directing how light (Sattva), movement (Rajas), and mass (Tamas) combine to weave the material universe.

name: स्वर्गापवर्गदा
samasa: क्षयिष्णु सुखं वर्गः। नित्यं तु सुखमपवर्गः। तदुभयं दत्ते इति।
summary: (i) One who bestows heaven and liberation.
(ii) Heaven is happiness earned by righteous deeds, but it is not permanent.
(iii) When accumulated merit is exhausted, one returns to mortal life. Apavarga means permanent liberation. She grants both.
searchText: 764 स्वर्गापवर्गदा क्षयिष्णु सुखं वर्गः। नित्यं तु सुखमपवर्गः। तदुभयं दत्ते इति। दत्ते — gives (i) one who bestows heaven and liberation. (ii) heaven is happiness earned by righteous deeds, but it is not permanent. (iii) when accumulated merit is exhausted, one returns to mortal life. apavarga means permanent liberation. she grants both. in the saubhāgyabhāskara, bhāskararāya clarifies the transactional alignment of experiences: fulfillment of all degrees of joy: svarga represents the temporary, structural rewards achieved through virtuous action in the higher planes. apavarga represents the permanent, non-dual bliss of final awakening. she acts as the singular portal for both paths, giving the appropriate worldly experience to the relative seeker and absolute liberation to the advanced ascetic.
esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara, Bhāskararāya clarifies the transactional alignment of experiences:

Fulfillment of All Degrees of Joy: Svarga represents the temporary, structural rewards achieved through virtuous action in the higher planes. Apavarga represents the permanent, non-dual bliss of final awakening. She acts as the singular portal for both paths, giving the appropriate worldly experience to the relative seeker and absolute liberation to the advanced ascetic.

name: शुद्धा
wordByWord: Word meanings are read directly from the samāsa expression above.
summary: (i) One who is ever pure.
(ii) She is free from the impurity called ignorance.
(iii) In truth She is the form of knowledge.
searchText: 765 शुद्धा आविद्यकमालिन्यशून्या। word meanings are read directly from the samāsa expression above. (i) one who is ever pure. (ii) she is free from the impurity called ignorance. (iii) in truth she is the form of knowledge. bhāskararāya strips this of simple moral definitions to reveal ontological purity: untouched by cosmic ignorance: material entities gather stains and undergo decay due to time and contact. she is śuddhā because her essential nature stands completely free from the ultimate stain—आविद्यकमालिन्य (āvidyaka-mālinya — the structural rust of cosmic ignorance or illusion). she remains eternally pristine, clear, and unblemished.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya strips this of simple moral definitions to reveal ontological purity:

Untouched by Cosmic Ignorance: Material entities gather stains and undergo decay due to time and contact. She remains eternally pristine, clear, and unblemished.

name: जपापुष्पनिभाकृतिः
samasa: जपापुष्पनिभा ओड्रपुष्पेण तुल्या आकृतिः स्वरूपं यस्याः सा।
summary: (i) One whose form is like the hibiscus flower.

name: ओजोवती
samasa: ओजः अष्टमधातुः इन्द्रियसामर्थ्यं तेजः बलम् दीप्तिः च अस्यां सन्तीति।
summary: (i) One who possesses ojas, the vitality of the body.
(ii) Ojas is the vital essence that protects the body in times of difficulty.
(iii) It also means light, strength, splendour and radiance; She possesses all these.

name: द्युतिधरा
wordByWord: Word meanings are read directly from the samāsa expression above.
summary: (i) One who bears splendour.
(ii) The brightness arising from the satisfaction of mind and body is called lustre, and She has that radiance.
(iii) This recalls the earlier name Kāntimatī.
searchText: 768 द्युतिधरा धरतीति धरा। द्युतेः कान्तेर्धरा। word meanings are read directly from the samāsa expression above. (i) one who bears splendour. (ii) the brightness arising from the satisfaction of mind and body is called lustre, and she has that radiance. (iii) this recalls the earlier name kāntimatī. bhāskararāya details her relationship with the cosmic light field: the pillar of luminous aura: she does not simply receive external light rays. she is dyutidharā because she acts as the active container and sustainer of the universal aura (kānti). every luminous frequency projected by the stars, fire, or awakened human intellects is a ray supported by her field.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya details Her relationship with the cosmic light field:

The Pillar of Luminous Aura: She does not simply receive external light rays. She is Dyutidharā because She acts as the active container and sustainer of the universal aura (kānti). Every luminous frequency projected by the stars, fire, or awakened human intellects is a ray supported by Her field.

name: यज्ञरूपा
samasa: यज्ञो वै विष्णुरिति श्रुतेः तदभिन्ना देवी यज्ञरूपा।
wordByWord: Word meanings are read directly from the samāsa expression above.
summary: (i) One who is the embodiment of sacrifice.
(ii) The five sacrifices prescribed for human beings are Deva-yajña, Pitṛ-yajña, Brahma-yajña, Manuṣya-yajña and Bhūta-yajña; She is the form of all these.
(iii) Offering knowledge and experience gained through the senses is also a jñāna-yajña, and She is that form.
(iv) The Gītā and Veda also identify the highest forms of yajña with the divine.
searchText: 769 यज्ञरूपा यज्ञो वै विष्णुरिति श्रुतेः तदभिन्ना देवी यज्ञरूपा। यज्ञ एव रूपमस्याः अस्तीति। word meanings are read directly from the samāsa expression above. (i) one who is the embodiment of sacrifice. (ii) the five sacrifices prescribed for human beings are deva-yajña, pitṛ-yajña, brahma-yajña, manuṣya-yajña and bhūta-yajña; she is the form of all these. (iii) offering knowledge and experience gained through the senses is also a jñāna-yajña, and she is that form. (iv) the gītā and veda also identify the highest forms of yajña with the divine. bhāskararāya anchors this directly in the profound declarations of the veda: identity with the sacrificial engine: the śruti explicitly declares “yajño vai viṣṇuḥ” ("the sacrifice is indeed viṣṇu"). she is yajñarūpā because she is non-different from this cosmic alignment mechanism. she is the intention, the action, the offering, and the alchemical transformation running through every holy rite, linking humanity to the absolute.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya anchors this directly in the profound declarations of the Veda:

Identity with the Sacrificial Engine: The Śruti explicitly declares “Yajño vai Viṣṇuḥ” ("The sacrifice is indeed Viṣṇu"). She is Yajñarūpā because She is non-different from this cosmic alignment mechanism. She is the intention, the action, the offering, and the alchemical transformation running through every holy rite, linking humanity to the absolute.

name: प्रियव्रता
summary: (i) One who is fond of vows.
(ii) A vow is the determination to begin and complete an act, such as Ekādaśī, Ṣaṣṭhī or Varalakṣmī vrata.
(iii) She is pleased with all vows, even those formally made to other deities, because the whole universe is Śiva-Śakti-maya.
searchText: 770 प्रियव्रता प्रियाणि व्रतान्यविशेषात्सर्वदेवताविषयकाणि यस्याः सा। यस्याः — whose; of whom (i) one who is fond of vows. (ii) a vow is the determination to begin and complete an act, such as ekādaśī, ṣaṣṭhī or varalakṣmī vrata. (iii) she is pleased with all vows, even those formally made to other deities, because the whole universe is śiva-śakti-maya. in the saubhāgyabhāskara, bhāskararāya concludes this structural sequence by defining her affinity for spiritual discipline: the affection for dedicated rules: she is priyavratā because she values the structural sincerity of the practitioner. it does not matter which entry-level deity a student targets; if they perform their vows (vrata) with continuous discipline and internal cleanliness, she accepts that focused energy, using it to clear their path toward non-dual awakening.

name: दुराराध्या
summary: (i) One who is difficult to worship.
(ii) She is difficult for the incompetent, for those who cannot control the senses, and for the fickle-minded.
(iii) Control of the senses and concentration of mind require great effort, and therefore Her worship is not easy for all.
searchText: 771 दुराराध्या दुःखेन आराध्या। दुःखेन — with difficulty (i) one who is difficult to worship. (ii) she is difficult for the incompetent, for those who cannot control the senses, and for the fickle-minded. (iii) control of the senses and concentration of mind require great effort, and therefore her worship is not easy for all. bhāskararāya clarifies this challenging title, stripping it of any concept of divine caprice: the filter against hypocrisy: she is not durārādhyā because she is harsh or unpredictable. rather, she cannot be accessed by superficial minds, deceptive intellects, or ostentatious public displays performed for vanity. her gate remains locked against egoic manipulation, requiring absolute internal transparency and intense, prolonged purification before swinging open.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya clarifies this challenging title, stripping it of any concept of divine caprice:

Her gate remains locked against egoic manipulation, requiring absolute internal transparency and intense, prolonged purification before swinging open.

name: दुराधर्षा
summary: (i) One who is hard to resist or subdue.
(ii) She cannot be attracted by luxury or strength.
(iii) She is attained only through devotion.
searchText: 772 दुराधर्षा दुःखरूप आधर्षः स्वायत्तीकरणं यस्याः सा। यस्याः — whose; of whom (i) one who is hard to resist or subdue. (ii) she cannot be attracted by luxury or strength. (iii) she is attained only through devotion. in the saubhāgyabhāskara, bhāskararāya details her unyielding independence: unconquerable by egoic assault: the individual ego often tries to conquer spiritual energy using aggressive manipulation, raw intellectual pride, or forced mechanical loops. she is durādharṣā because her field is completely unassailable. she bows to no external command, remaining independent (svātantrya) and accessible only through total surrender (prapatti).

Unconquerable by Egoic Assault: The individual ego often tries to conquer spiritual energy using aggressive manipulation, raw intellectual pride, or forced mechanical loops. name: पाटलीकुसुमप्रिया
summary: (i) One who is fond of the pāṭalī flower.
(ii) This flower has a mixed red and white colour, suggesting the union of Śiva and Śakti.
(iii) Other names also mention flowers dear to Śrīdevī, such as Kadamba, Campaka, Mandāra and the flower of consciousness.

name: महती
wordByWord: Word meanings are read directly from the samāsa expression above.
summary: (i) One who is very great.
(ii) She is greater than the greatest, as indicated by the Upaniṣadic expression Mahato Mahīyān.
(iii) Other things can be measured only through Her.
(iv) The name also means one worthy of worship. Mahatī is also the name of Nārada's vīṇā; She is in that form also.
searchText: 774 महती परममहत्परिमाणत्वात्। महान्कस्मान्मानेनान्याञ्जहतीति। महनीयो भवतीति। नारदमुनेर्वीणाविशेषो, तत्स्वरूपा। word meanings are read directly from the samāsa expression above. (i) one who is very great. (ii) she is greater than the greatest, as indicated by the upaniṣadic expression mahato mahīyān. (iii) other things can be measured only through her. (iv) the name also means one worthy of worship. mahatī is also the name of nārada's vīṇā; she is in that form also. bhāskararāya details three profound operational paths for this single-word title: the shoreless scale: she is mahatī because her metric of size represents absolute, infinite expansion, leaving nothing outside herself (मानेन अन्यान् जहतीति). the absolute object of worship: she commands automatic, timeless reverence from every tier of intelligence. the lute of resonance: mythologically, she is identical to the highly sacred lute (mahatī) played by sage nārada, acting as the soul of all spiritual harmonics.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya details three profound operational paths for this single-word title:
The Shoreless Scale: She is Mahatī because Her metric of size represents absolute, infinite expansion, leaving nothing outside Herself (मानेन अन्यान् जहतीति).
The Absolute Object of Worship: She commands automatic, timeless reverence from every tier of intelligence.
The Lute of Resonance: Mythologically, She is identical to the highly sacred lute (Mahatī) played by Sage Nārada, acting as the soul of all spiritual harmonics.

name: मेरुनिलया
samasa: नवाक्षरो मन्त्रो मेरुपदवाच्यः स एव निलयः सर्वमन्त्रोद्भवस्थानं यस्याः सा।
summary: (i) One who dwells in Meru.
(ii) Meru is the golden mountain; Śrīdevī's Cintāmaṇi house is said to be on the central peak.
(iii) After the destruction of Bhaṇḍāsura, the divine architects constructed the cities and abodes of Śrīdevī on the peaks and regions connected with Meru.
(iv) In Śrīcakra worship, Meru-prastāra is one of the modes of contemplation, and She has that Meru as Her dwelling.
(v) Meru may also mean the nine-letter mantra from which all mantras arise, or the central point in a rosary or counting method; She dwells there also.
searchText: 775 मेरुनिलया मेरुरेव निलयो यस्याः सा। नवाक्षरो मन्त्रो मेरुपदवाच्यः स एव निलयः सर्वमन्त्रोद्भवस्थानं यस्याः सा। यस्याः — whose; of whom निलयः — abode (i) one who dwells in meru. (ii) meru is the golden mountain; śrīdevī's cintāmaṇi house is said to be on the central peak. (iii) after the destruction of bhaṇḍāsura, the divine architects constructed the cities and abodes of śrīdevī on the peaks and regions connected with meru. (iv) in śrīcakra worship, meru-prastāra is one of the modes of contemplation, and she has that meru as her dwelling. (v) meru may also mean the nine-letter mantra from which all mantras arise, or the central point in a rosary or counting method; she dwells there also. in the saubhāgyabhāskara, bhāskararāya elevates this past physical geography to reveal high mantric maps: the axis of the cosmos and mantras: geographically, she sits at the peak of mount meru, the golden center axis of planetary worlds. internally, meru corresponds to the vertical pillar of the suṣumṇā channel. critically, in high mantra grammar, meru refers to the supreme nine-syllabled code matrix that acts as the original source for all subsequent mantras. she sits at the absolute hub of this formula.

Internally, Meru corresponds to the vertical pillar of the Suṣumṇā channel. Critically, in high mantra grammar, Meru refers to the supreme nine-syllabled code matrix that acts as the original source for all subsequent mantras. She sits at the absolute hub of this formula.

name: मन्दारकुसुमप्रिया
samasa: मन्दारो देवतरुः श्वेतार्को वा तस्य कुसुमं प्रियं यस्याः सा।
summary: (i) One who is fond of Mandara flowers.
(ii) Mandara is a celestial tree and is also identified with the white arka flower. Flowers used in worship represent pure and fragrant devotion offered to Sreedevee.
(iii) As She was earlier described as dwelling in Meru, the heavenly Mandara flower is fittingly mentioned as dear to Her.
searchText: 776 मन्दारकुसुमप्रिया मन्दारो देवतरुः श्वेतार्को वा तस्य कुसुमं प्रियं यस्याः सा। मन्दारो — mandara tree or flower देवतरुः — celestial tree प्रियं — dear (i) one who is fond of mandara flowers. (ii) mandara is a celestial tree and is also identified with the white arka flower. flowers used in worship represent pure and fragrant devotion offered to sreedevee. (iii) as she was earlier described as dwelling in meru, the heavenly mandara flower is fittingly mentioned as dear to her. bhāskararāya unpacks the alchemical value of this offering: the ray of unfading light: the mandāra is a legendary tree of the heavenly worlds whose blossoms never fade. earthly liturgy maps it onto the highly potent śvetārka (white calotropis). this flower represents pure, uncorrupted, and resilient solar energy. offering this frequency back to her signifies the surrender of one's immortal potential, stabilizing the internal centers.

This flower represents pure, uncorrupted, and resilient solar energy. Offering this frequency back to Her signifies the surrender of one's immortal potential, stabilizing the internal centers.

name: वीराराध्या
summary: (i) One who is worshipped by heroes.
(ii) Vira means a heroic worshipper, one who has steadiness, courage and fitness for the path of worship.
(iii) Sreedevee is adored by such qualified upasakas who are firm in practice and are not overcome by fear or weakness.
searchText: 777 वीराराध्या वीरैः आराध्या। वीरैः — by heroes (i) one who is worshipped by heroes. (ii) vira means a heroic worshipper, one who has steadiness, courage and fitness for the path of worship. (iii) sreedevee is adored by such qualified upasakas who are firm in practice and are not overcome by fear or weakness. bhāskararāya defines the internal character tier required for this path: the path of heroic consecration: in agamic classification, practitioners are stratified into three temperaments: paśu (bound/animalistic), vīra (heroic/dynamic), and divya (divine/absorbed). she is vīrārādhyā because she is correctly approached through the वीर-भाव (vīra-bhāva) — the path of intense, courageous, and uncompromising internal discipline where the seeker treats their own egoic doubts as enemies to be crushed, fighting forward without fear.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya defines the internal character tier required for this path:

The Path of Heroic Consecration: In Agamic classification, practitioners are stratified into three temperaments: Paśu (bound/animalistic), Vīra (heroic/dynamic), and Divya (divine/absorbed). She is Vīrārādhyā because She is correctly approached through the वीर-भाव (Vīra-bhāva) — the path of intense, courageous, and uncompromising internal discipline where the seeker treats their own egoic doubts as enemies to be crushed, fighting forward without fear.

name: विराड्रूपा
summary: (i) One whose form is the Virat.
(ii) The Virat is the cosmic form, the gross universal body. All beings and worlds are included in that vast manifestation.
(iii) Sreedevee is not merely an individual deity-form; She is the universal form itself.
searchText: 778 विराड्रूपा विराड् रूपं यस्याः सा। विराड् — cosmic form; virat (i) one whose form is the virat. (ii) the virat is the cosmic form, the gross universal body. all beings and worlds are included in that vast manifestation. (iii) sreedevee is not merely an individual deity-form; she is the universal form itself. in the saubhāgyabhāskara, bhāskararāya details her complete immanence across the visible plane: the visible garment of god: lest a practitioner think that she remains hidden only in secret unmanifested corners, this name establishes that the entire visible, physical universe—stars, mountains, oceans, and living shapes—is her macrocosmic body (virāṭ-sūtra). every atom seen is a cell of her physical form, proving her presence in the element layer.
esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara, Bhāskararāya details Her complete immanence across the visible plane:

The Visible Garment of God: Lest a practitioner think that She remains hidden only in secret unmanifested corners, this name establishes that the entire visible, physical universe—stars, mountains, oceans, and living shapes—is Her macrocosmic body (Virāṭ-sūtra). Every atom seen is a cell of Her physical form, proving Her presence in the element layer.

name: विरजा
summary: (i) One who is free from rajas or impurity.
(ii) Rajas may mean dust, passion, activity or sin. She is untouched by these impurities.
(iii) She is pure consciousness, beyond the taint of karma and the agitations born of rajas.
searchText: 779 विरजा विगतं रजः पापं यस्याः सा। विगतं — gone; free from रजः — rajas; dust; impurity; sin (i) one who is free from rajas or impurity. (ii) rajas may mean dust, passion, activity or sin. she is untouched by these impurities. (iii) she is pure consciousness, beyond the taint of karma and the agitations born of rajas. bhāskararāya defines the pristine clarity of the absolute baseline: the spotless screen: रजस् (rajas) represents the dust of mental agitation, binding desires, and the stains of karmic impurity (pāpa). she is virajā because her essential nature is completely free from this friction. she remains an unblemished screen, allowing worlds to turn without gathering any dark residue.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya defines the pristine clarity of the absolute baseline:

The Spotless Screen: रजस् (Rajas) represents the dust of mental agitation, binding desires, and the stains of karmic impurity (pāpa). She is Virajā because Her essential nature is completely free from this friction. She remains an unblemished screen, allowing worlds to turn without gathering any dark residue.

name: विश्वतोमुखी
summary: (i) One whose face is everywhere.
(ii) The name indicates that She faces all directions and is present in all forms.
(iii) Nothing is outside Her awareness; all beings are seen, sustained and governed by Her.
searchText: 780 विश्वतोमुखी विश्वतः सर्वावच्छेदेन मुखं यस्याः सा। विश्वतः — on all sides मुखं — face (i) one whose face is everywhere. (ii) the name indicates that she faces all directions and is present in all forms. (iii) nothing is outside her awareness; all beings are seen, sustained and governed by her. in the saubhāgyabhāskara, bhāskararāya concludes this major block by defining her absolute omnidirectional interfaces: the omnidirectional portal: she does not possess a limited front or back orientation. as viśvatomukhī, she stands face-to-face with every single coordinate of the universe simultaneously (सर्वावच्छेदेन). wherever a devotee calls out, and whichever atom experiences a transition, she is already looking, listening, and interfacing at that exact spot, showcasing her omnipresence.
esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara, Bhāskararāya concludes this major block by defining Her absolute omnidirectional interfaces:

The Omnidirectional Portal: She does not possess a limited front or back orientation. Wherever a devotee calls out, and whichever atom experiences a transition, She is already looking, listening, and interfacing at that exact spot, showcasing Her omnipresence.

(iii) She is not merely an external object of worship but the innermost consciousness in the worshipper.
searchText: 781 प्रत्यग्रूपा प्रत्यगवलोक्यमानं रूपं स्वरूपं यस्याः सा। प्रत्यगवलोक्यमानं — component from the samasa above रूपं — component from the samasa above स्वरूपं — component from the samasa above यस्याः — component from the samasa above सा। — component from the samasa above (i) one whose form is the inner self. She is the ultimate inner subject, found by tracking the source of the "I" sensation.

name: पराकाशा
samasa: पराकशब्दः कृच्छ्रविशेषवाचकः संस्तपोमात्रोपलक्षकः तस्य आशा दिक्।
परे उत्कृष्टे अके पापदुःखे अश्नाति नाशयतीति।
summary: (i) One who is the supreme ether.
(ii) Akasha is space; Parakasha is the higher and subtler space beyond ordinary physical space.
(iii) She is the supreme expanse of consciousness in which everything appears and into which everything resolves.
(iv) The samasa also allows meanings connected with removal of sin and suffering through supreme tapas.
searchText: 782 पराकाशा पर उत्कृष्टश्चासावाकाशश्च। पराकशब्दः कृच्छ्रविशेषवाचकः संस्तपोमात्रोपलक्षकः तस्य आशा दिक्। परे उत्कृष्टे अके पापदुःखे अश्नाति नाशयतीति। पर — higher; supreme (i) one who is the supreme ether. (ii) akasha is space; parakasha is the higher and subtler space beyond ordinary physical space. (iii) she is the supreme expanse of consciousness in which everything appears and into which everything resolves. (iv) the samasa also allows meanings connected with removal of sin and suffering through supreme tapas. in the saubhāgyabhāskara, bhāskararāya details her position as the absolute container of consciousness: the ultimate sky of spirit: she is the parākāśa — the pristine, unconditioned, and high-velocity space of pure consciousness (cidākāśa) that sits above and inside the material space element. the purifier of distress: alternatively, she functions as the absolute target of intense physical penance (parāka-tapas), and acts as the active force that devours (aśnati) the practitioner's stored sins and existential suffering (aka).

The Purifier of Distress: Alternatively, She functions as the absolute target of intense physical penance (parāka-tapas), and acts as the active force that devours (aśnati) the practitioner's stored sins and existential suffering (aka).

name: प्राणदा
summary: (i) One who gives life-breath.
(ii) Prana is the vital force by which living beings function.
(iii) Sreedevee grants and sustains prana in all beings; life itself is Her gift.
searchText: 783 प्राणदा प्राणान् दत्ते इति। प्राणान् — life-breaths दत्ते — gives (i) one who gives life-breath. (ii) prana is the vital force by which living beings function. (iii) sreedevee grants and sustains prana in all beings; life itself is her gift. bhāskararāya details the mechanics of biological activation: the fountain of life: physical bodies are merely cold combinations of element matter until they are animated by the vital breaths (prāṇa). she is prāṇadā because she dispenses this underlying life-force currency, breathing her active energy into the biological shell to initiate heartbeat, breath, and sensory transmission.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya details the mechanics of biological activation:

name: प्राणरूपिणी
summary: (i) One who is in the form of prana.
(ii) Prana is also understood as Brahman in the scriptures.
(iii) She is the life-principle itself, not merely the giver of life.
searchText: 784 प्राणरूपिणी प्राणः ब्रह्म रूपं यस्याः सा। प्राणः — life-breath; vital force ब्रह्म — brahman (i) one who is in the form of prana. Every internal movement of energy running through the Nāḍīs is the literal pulsing of Her cosmic form inside the practitioner.

searchText: 785 मार्ताण्डभैरवाराध्या मार्ताण्डभैरवाभ्यामाराध्या। मार्ताण्डभैरवाभ्यामाराध्या। — component from the samasa above (i) one who is worshipped by martanda bhairava. (ii) martanda is connected with the sun and bhairava with the fierce form of shiva. (iii) even such powerful deities worship her, showing her supreme status in the tradition. bhāskararāya decodes the internal administrative tracking of her throne: adoration by the solar commander: mārtāṇḍa bhairava represents the high-velocity, blazing solar consciousness that commands the defense of the energetic grid. his adoration at her feet demonstrates that even the most brilliant, crushing solar forces in nature act as entry-level ministers submissive to her central throne.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya decodes the internal administrative tracking of Her throne:

Adoration by the Solar Commander: Mārtāṇḍa Bhairava represents the high-velocity, blazing solar consciousness that commands the defense of the energetic grid. His adoration at Her feet demonstrates that even the most brilliant, crushing solar forces in nature act as entry-level ministers submissive to Her central throne.

name: मन्त्रिणीन्यस्तराज्यधूः
samasa: मन्त्रिणः नयतीति मन्त्रिणी। तस्मिन् न्यस्ता राज्यस्य स्वसाम्राज्यरूपस्यैक्यरहस्यस्य धूः तज्जनकतावच्छेदको धर्मो यया सा।
summary: (i) One who placed the burden of the kingdom upon Mantrini.
(ii) Mantrini, the minister-deity of Sreedevee, is entrusted with the administration of Her empire.
(iii) The name indicates the royal order of Sreedevee's kingdom and the confidence with which She delegates the work of governance to Her ministerial power.
searchText: 786 मन्त्रिणीन्यस्तराज्यधूः मन्त्रिणः नयतीति मन्त्रिणी। तस्मिन् न्यस्ता राज्यस्य स्वसाम्राज्यरूपस्यैक्यरहस्यस्य धूः तज्जनकतावच्छेदको धर्मो यया सा। न्यस्ता — entrusted; placed राज्यस्य — of kingdom (i) one who placed the burden of the kingdom upon mantrini. (ii) mantrini, the minister-deity of sreedevee, is entrusted with the administration of her empire. (iii) the name indicates the royal order of sreedevee's kingdom and the confidence with which she delegates the work of governance to her ministerial power. in the saubhāgyabhāskara, bhāskararāya defines her absolute detachment from administrative labor: the delegation of governance: she sits at the absolute center of the śrī cakra as pure, unmoving, non-dual bliss. to run the heavy, complex mechanics of universe administration and scriptural instruction, she delegates the complete operational burden (dhura) to her trusted minister mantriṇī (goddess śyāmalā). this allows the practitioner to approach the minister first to gain clean access to the queen.
esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara, Bhāskararāya defines Her absolute detachment from administrative labor:

The Delegation of Governance: She sits at the absolute center of the Śrī Cakra as pure, unmoving, non-dual bliss. To run the heavy, complex mechanics of universe administration and scriptural instruction, She delegates the complete operational burden (dhura) to Her trusted minister Mantriṇī (Goddess Śyāmalā). This allows the practitioner to approach the minister first to gain clean access to the Queen.

She is non-different from this structural goddess, commanding the fulfillment of spiritual aspirations.

name: जयत्सेना
summary: (i) One whose army is victorious.
(ii) Her army consists of divine Shaktis who always overcome hostile forces.
(iii) The victory of Her army symbolises the victory of divine knowledge and power over ignorance and obstruction.
searchText: 788 जयत्सेना जयन्ती सेना शक्तिसमूहो यस्याः सा। जयन्ती — victorious सेना — army (i) one whose army is victorious. (ii) her army consists of divine shaktis who always overcome hostile forces. (iii) the victory of her army symbolises the victory of divine knowledge and power over ignorance and obstruction. in the saubhāgyabhāskara, bhāskararāya details her unmatched defensive readiness: the victorious host of shaktis: her "army" (senā) is the corporate assembly of internal energy channels, guardian deities, and structural light rays that fight against ignorance. this host is jayantī — it cannot be checked, defeated, or turned back by chaotic forces, ensuring the eventual victory of the sincere seeker.
esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara, Bhāskararāya details Her unmatched defensive readiness:

The Victorious Host of Shaktis: Her "army" (Senā) is the corporate assembly of internal energy channels, guardian deities, and structural light rays that fight against ignorance. This host is jayantī — it cannot be checked, defeated, or turned back by chaotic forces, ensuring the eventual victory of the sincere seeker.

name: निस्त्रैगुण्या
summary: (i) One who is beyond the three gunas.
(ii) Sattva, rajas and tamas belong to Prakriti and to the manifested world.
(iii) Though She manifests through them, in Her supreme nature She is free from their limitation.
searchText: 789 निस्त्रैगुण्या निर्गतं त्रैगुण्यं गुणत्रयवत्त्वं यस्याः  सा। निर्गतं — gone; free from (i) one who is beyond the three gunas. (ii) sattva, rajas and tamas belong to prakriti and to the manifested world. (iii) though she manifests through them, in her supreme nature she is free from their limitation. bhāskararāya anchors this in the highest non-dual goal described in the gītā: beyond the element play: while she manifests the three qualities to construct forms (triguṇātmikā), her essential, unconditioned nature as parabrahman sits completely past this triad (nirguṇa). she instructs the practitioner to step out of the shifting currents of light, motion, and mass, anchoring themselves in her clean, quality-less space.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya anchors this in the highest non-dual goal described in the Gītā:

Beyond the Element Play: While She manifests the three qualities to construct forms (Triguṇātmikā), Her essential, unconditioned nature as Parabrahman sits completely past this triad (Nirguṇa). She instructs the practitioner to step out of the shifting currents of light, motion, and mass, anchoring themselves in Her clean, quality-less space.

summary: (i) One who is both para and apara.
(ii) Para means the supreme, and apara means the lower or manifested form.
(iii) She is both the transcendental Reality and the manifested universe; therefore She is Para-apara.
searchText: 790 परापरा परशब्दोऽपरशब्दः परापरशब्दो वा यस्य वाचकस्तत्स्वरूपा। परशब्दोऽपरशब्दः — component from the samasa above परापरशब्दो — component from the samasa above वा — component from the samasa above यस्य — component from the samasa above वाचकस्तत्स्वरूपा। — component from the samasa above (i) one who is both para and apara. (ii) para means the supreme, and apara means the lower or manifested form. (iii) she is both the transcendental reality and the manifested universe; therefore she is para-apara. in the saubhāgyabhāskara, bhāskararāya concludes this structural sequence by stating her synthesis of all frameworks: the non-dual integration: she is both परा (parā — the hidden, formless, unconditioned absolute) and अपरा (aparā — the visible, multiform, material universe of objects). she does not exclude the relative world to stay holy, nor does she lose her absolute status when projecting forms. she is the holistic reality that integrates both dimensions simultaneously, leaving zero room for structural duality.
esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara, Bhāskararāya concludes this structural sequence by stating Her synthesis of all frameworks:

The Non-Dual Integration: She is both परा (Parā — the hidden, formless, unconditioned absolute) and अपरा (Aparā — the visible, multiform, material universe of objects). She does not exclude the relative world to stay holy, nor does She lose Her absolute status when projecting forms. She is the holistic reality that integrates both dimensions simultaneously, leaving zero room for structural duality.

name: सत्यज्ञानानन्दरूपा
samasa: सती सद्विद्या तद्विषये अज्ञा अनभिज्ञा ये तेषामनानन्दमानन्दभिन्नं दुःखमेव रूउपयति ददातीति।
सत्याविद्या देव्या अज्ञानं येषां ते सत्यज्ञानाः तेषामानन्दाख्यान् लोकान् रूपयतीति।
summary: (i) One whose form is truth, knowledge and bliss.
(ii) Satya, jnana and ananda are used to indicate the nature of Brahman.
(iii) Sreedevee is that Brahman-form, the reality that is true, conscious and blissful.
searchText: 791 सत्यज्ञानानन्दरूपा सत्यं ज्ञानमानन्दश्च रूपं यस्याः सा। सती सद्विद्या तद्विषये अज्ञा अनभिज्ञा ये तेषामनानन्दमानन्दभिन्नं दुःखमेव रूउपयति ददातीति। सत्याविद्या देव्या अज्ञानं येषां ते सत्यज्ञानाः तेषामानन्दाख्यान् लोकान् रूपयतीति। सत्यं — truth सती — true; devoted wife (i) one whose form is truth, knowledge and bliss. (ii) satya, jnana and ananda are used to indicate the nature of brahman. (iii) sreedevee is that brahman-form, the reality that is true, conscious and blissful. bhāskararāya anchors this directly in the profound declarations of the taittirīya upaniṣad (satyaṁ jñānam anantaṁ brahma): the absolute identity of spirit: her essential substance consists of the core triad that defines the absolute reality—existence-truth (satya), luminous awareness (jñāna), and boundless bliss (ananda). bhāskararāya notes the corrective physics: to those who turn away from her light, reality appears as fragmented friction; to those who recognize her, she opens the gates of absolute joy.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya anchors this directly in the profound declarations of the Taittirīya Upaniṣad (Satyaṁ Jñānam Anantaṁ Brahma):

The Absolute Identity of Spirit: Her essential substance consists of the core triad that defines the absolute reality—Existence-Truth (Satya), Luminous Awareness (Jñāna), and Boundless Bliss (Ananda). Bhāskararāya notes the corrective physics: to those who turn away from Her light, reality appears as fragmented friction; to those who recognize Her, She opens the gates of absolute joy.

name: सामरस्यपरायणा

रस्यन्ते गीयन्त इति रस्यानि सामानि च तानि रस्यानि च तानि परायणानि अभीष्टानि यस्याः सा।

(ii) Samarasa means the non-difference or equal essence of Shiva and Shakti.
(iii) She abides in the perfect harmony where Shiva and Shakti are not separate.
(iv) The name also suggests that sacred chants and songs become desirable because they lead to this unity.
searchText: 792 सामरस्यपरायणा समो न्यूनानधिको रसो ययोस्तयोः शिवशक्त्योर्भावः सामरस्यमेव परमयनं स्थानं यस्याः सा। सामरस्य लोकस्य परायणमाश्रय:। रस्यन्ते गीयन्त इति रस्यानि सामानि च तानि रस्यानि च तानि परायणानि अभीष्टानि यस्याः सा। समो — component from the samasa above न्यूनानधिको — component from the samasa above रसो — component from the samasa above ययोस्तयोः — component from the samasa above शिवशक्त्योर्भावः — component from the samasa above सामरस्यमेव — component from the samasa above (i) one who is devoted to perfect unity or sameness of essence. (ii) samarasa means the non-difference or equal essence of shiva and shakti. (iii) she abides in the perfect harmony where shiva and shakti are not separate. (iv) the name also suggests that sacred chants and songs become desirable because they lead to this unity. in the saubhāgyabhāskara, bhāskararāya outlines the theological crown of the śrīvidyā system: the equilibrium of consciousness and energy: सामरस्य (sāmarasya) describes a state of chemical and spiritual equilibrium where two elements fuse so perfectly that they cannot be separated—like water mixed with milk. shiva (consciousness) and shakti (energy) exist in this exact condition of balanced, non-dual fusion. she is parāyaṇā because she is permanently anchored at this peak junction point, serving as the ultimate refuge where the seeker's individual duality is dissolved into the whole.
esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara, Bhāskararāya outlines the theological crown of the Śrīvidyā system:

The Equilibrium of Consciousness and Energy: सामरस्य (Sāmarasya) describes a state of chemical and spiritual equilibrium where two elements fuse so perfectly that they cannot be separated—like water mixed with milk. Shiva (consciousness) and Shakti (energy) exist in this exact condition of balanced, non-dual fusion. She is Parāyaṇā because She is permanently anchored at this peak junction point, serving as the ultimate refuge where the seeker's individual duality is dissolved into the whole.

(iii) it also indicates her association with the purifying matted locks from which even ganga becomes sacred. bhāskararāya tracks her alignment with the untamed, pristine channels of consciousness: the containment of the cosmic current: कपर्द refers to the coiled, sacred matted hair worn by ascetics and lord śiva. as kapardinī, her hair strands represent the interwoven, high-velocity currents of cosmic energy and inspiration. just as shiva's hair caught and safely tamed the violent downward crash of the river gaṅgā, her structural matted channels contain and direct the vast currents of spiritual power, preventing them from destroying the practitioner's nervous system.
