The Containment of the Cosmic Current: कपर्द refers to the coiled, sacred matted hair worn by ascetics and Lord Śiva. As Kapardinī, Her hair strands represent the interwoven, high-velocity currents of cosmic energy and inspiration. Just as Shiva's hair caught and safely tamed the violent downward crash of the river Gaṅgā, Her structural matted channels contain and direct the vast currents of spiritual power, preventing them from destroying the practitioner's nervous system.

name: कलामाला
samasa: कलां लावण्यं मां शोभां च लातीति।
summary: (i) One who is the garland of kalas.
(ii) Kalas are arts, digits, powers or phases.
(iii) She is the continuous series and source of all kalas, including the sixty-four arts and all forms of beauty and skill.
searchText: 794 कलामाला कलानां चतुःषष्ट्यादिरूपाणां माला परम्परा। कलां लावण्यं मां शोभां च लातीति। माला — garland; series (i) one who is the garland of kalas. (ii) kalas are arts, digits, powers or phases. (iii) she is the continuous series and source of all kalas, including the sixty-four arts and all forms of beauty and skill. in the saubhāgyabhāskara, bhāskararāya maps her presence across all creative configurations: the unbroken chain of creative rays: she wears the entire network of the sixty-four cultural arts (catuḥ-ṣaṣṭhi-kalā), the digits of lunar time, and the operational phases of element evolution as a continuous, beautiful garland (mālā). every single expression of aesthetic brilliance, mathematical phase, or design ray is a link in her corporate chain.
esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara, Bhāskararāya maps Her presence across all creative configurations:

The Unbroken Chain of Creative Rays: She wears the entire network of the sixty-four cultural arts (catuḥ-ṣaṣṭhi-kalā), the digits of lunar time, and the operational phases of element evolution as a continuous, beautiful garland (mālā). Every single expression of aesthetic brilliance, mathematical phase, or design ray is a link in Her corporate chain.

name: कामधुक्
summary: (i) One who fulfils desires like the wish-yielding cow.
(ii) Kamadhuk means one who milks or yields desired objects.
(iii) Like Kamadhenu, She grants the legitimate desires of devotees and supports their spiritual and worldly needs.
searchText: 795 कामधुक् कामान् दोग्धीति कामधुक्। कामधेनुस्वरूपा। कामान् — desires (i) one who fulfils desires like the wish-yielding cow. (ii) kamadhuk means one who milks or yields desired objects. (iii) like kamadhenu, she grants the legitimate desires of devotees and supports their spiritual and worldly needs. bhāskararāya defines her absolute material and spiritual responsiveness: the wish-fulfilling fountain: she functions as the ultimate cosmic kāmadhenu — the celestial cow that effortlessly yields the fulfillment of whatever a sincere heart requires. bhāskararāya notes that she does not delay or force painful bargaining; if the seeker's alignment is clean, she milks out the necessary resource immediately, providing overall support for the path.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya defines Her absolute material and spiritual responsiveness:

The Wish-Fulfilling Fountain: She functions as the ultimate cosmic Kāmadhenu — the celestial cow that effortlessly yields the fulfillment of whatever a sincere heart requires. Bhāskararāya notes that She does not delay or force painful bargaining; if the seeker's alignment is clean, She milks out the necessary resource immediately, providing overall support for the path.

name: कामरूपिणी
summary: (i) One whose form is desire or the supreme Kama.
(ii) Kama here may indicate Parashiva, the supreme beloved, or the power that assumes forms according to will.
(iii) All forms desired in worship are forms of Her.
searchText: 796 कामरूपिणी कामः परशिव एव रूपमस्याः अस्तीति। कामं यथेच्छं वा रूपाण्यस्याः अस्तीति। कामः — desire; parashiva (i) one whose form is desire or the supreme kama. (ii) kama here may indicate parashiva, the supreme beloved, or the power that assumes forms according to will. (iii) all forms desired in worship are forms of her. in the saubhāgyabhāskara, bhāskararāya unpacks the dual structural and optional layers: the body of divine desire: काम represents paramaśiva as pure, unconditioned cosmic attraction. she is her non-dual counterpart, giving visible expression to that baseline consciousness. absolute morphological freedom: alternatively, she is kāmarūpiṇī because she possesses the absolute freedom to project and assume any physical or geometric form whatsoever (yatheccha-rūpā) to guide and protect her devotees.
esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara, Bhāskararāya unpacks the dual structural and optional layers:
The Body of Divine Desire: काम represents Paramaśiva as pure, unconditioned cosmic attraction. She is Her non-dual counterpart, giving visible expression to that baseline consciousness.

Absolute Morphological Freedom: Alternatively, She is Kāmarūpiṇī because She possesses the absolute freedom to project and assume any physical or geometric form whatsoever (yatheccha-rūpā) to guide and protect Her devotees.

(ii) She is the repository of many kinds of arts, powers and digits.
(iii) The Upanishadic idea of the sixteenfold Self is also suggested; the souls and their powers are contained in Her.
searchText: 797 कलानिधिः कलानां नानाविधतया निधिः। आत्मैवास्य षोडशी कलेति बृहदारण्यकोक्तेरात्मनां जीवानां निधिः। कलाः कर्माणि निधीयन्तेऽस्यामिति। कलानां — component from the samasa above नानाविधतया — component from the samasa above निधिः। — component from the samasa above आत्मैवास्य — component from the samasa above षोडशी — component from the samasa above कलेति — component from the samasa above (i) one who is the treasure-house of kalas. (ii) she is the repository of many kinds of arts, powers and digits. (iii) the upanishadic idea of the sixteenfold self is also suggested; the souls and their powers are contained in her. bhāskararāya links this directly to the high declarations of the bṛhadāraṇyaka upaniṣad: the storehouse of the sixteen digits: the upaniṣad states that the true self constitutes the ultimate sixteenth digit (षोडशी कला) of existence. she is kalānidhi because she functions as the absolute treasure house where all these individual sparks of living consciousness (jīvas) are stored, protected, and nourished during cosmic cycles, keeping their potential intact.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya links this directly to the high declarations of the Bṛhadāraṇyaka Upaniṣad:

The Storehouse of the Sixteen Digits: The Upaniṣad states that the true Self constitutes the ultimate sixteenth digit (षोडशी कला) of existence. She is Kalānidhi because She functions as the absolute treasure house where all these individual sparks of living consciousness (Jīvas) are stored, protected, and nourished during cosmic cycles, keeping their potential intact.

name: काव्यकला
summary: (i) One who is the art of poetry.
(ii) She is the power that produces poetry, learning, refined expression and inspired speech.
(iii) The life-giving power of poetic art and the revival of expression arise from Her grace.
searchText: 798 काव्यकला काव्यं च सा कला च। काव्योत्पादककलारूपा। काव्यस्य शुक्रस्य मृतसञ्जीवन्याख्यकलारूपा कला — art; digit; power (i) one who is the art of poetry. (ii) she is the power that produces poetry, learning, refined expression and inspired speech. (iii) the life-giving power of poetic art and the revival of expression arise from her grace. bhāskararāya details the artistic and alchemical properties of this name: the genius behind literature: she is the highly specialized, creative ray (kalā) that breaks open inside the intellect of a seer (kavi), allowing them to compose sublime, immortal literature that captures cosmic truth. the ray of resurrection: mythologically, kāvya is sage śukrācārya; she is the hidden, internal dynamic power behind his celebrated मृतसञ्जीवनी विद्या (mṛta-sañjīvanī vidyā — the science that restores life to the dead), demonstrating her capacity to revive stagnant systems.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya details the artistic and alchemical properties of this name:

The Genius behind Literature: She is the highly specialized, creative ray (kalā) that breaks open inside the intellect of a seer (kavi), allowing them to compose sublime, immortal literature that captures cosmic truth.

The Ray of Resurrection: Mythologically, Kāvya is Sage Śukrācārya; She is the hidden, internal dynamic power behind his celebrated मृतसञ्जीवनी विद्या (Mṛta-sañjīvanī Vidyā — the science that restores life to the dead), demonstrating Her capacity to revive stagnant systems.

name: रसज्ञा
summary: (i) One who knows rasa.
(ii) Rasa means aesthetic flavour such as shringara and other poetic sentiments, and also taste.
(iii) She knows, enjoys and grants all rasa, both in art and in spiritual experience.
searchText: 799 रसज्ञा रसान् शृङ्गारादिभेदेन दशविधान् जानातीति। रसनेन्द्रियस्वरूपा। रसान् — aesthetic flavours; tastes (i) one who knows rasa. (ii) rasa means aesthetic flavour such as shringara and other poetic sentiments, and also taste. (iii) she knows, enjoys and grants all rasa, both in art and in spiritual experience. in the saubhāgyabhāskara, bhāskararāya details her appreciation of human and divine emotion: the master connoisseur of consciousness flavor: रस (rasa) represents the ten refined categories of aesthetic and spiritual emotion (love, peace, heroism, etc.). she is rasajñā because she perfectly understands, feels, and evaluates the exact flavor profile of the devotee's heart. concurrently, she manifests as the foundational sensory capacity of taste (rasanendriya), allowing life to experience sweetness.
esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara, Bhāskararāya details Her appreciation of human and divine emotion:

The Master Connoisseur of Consciousness Flavor: रस (Rasa) represents the ten refined categories of aesthetic and spiritual emotion (love, peace, heroism, etc.). She is Rasajñā because She perfectly understands, feels, and evaluates the exact flavor profile of the devotee's heart. Concurrently, She manifests as the foundational sensory capacity of taste (rasanendriya), allowing life to experience sweetness.

name: रसशेवधिः
samasa: कलानां रसनां च निधिः शेवधिर्यस्याः सा।
summary: (i) One who is the treasure-house of rasa.
(ii) The highest rasa is the nectar of Brahman.
(iii) She is the storehouse of that nectar and of all forms of sweetness, taste and experience.
searchText: 800 रसशेवधिः रसस्य ब्रह्मामृतस्य शेवधिर्निधिः। कलानां रसनां च निधिः शेवधिर्यस्याः सा। निधिः — treasure; store (i) one who is the treasure-house of rasa. (ii) the highest rasa is the nectar of brahman. (iii) she is the storehouse of that nectar and of all forms of sweetness, taste and experience. bhāskararāya concludes this major numerical sequence by framing her position as the absolute container of joy: the reservoir of the non-dual nectar: the word शेवधि (śevadhi) denotes an inexhaustible, highly guarded divine treasure house. she is rasaśevadhi because she is the absolute reservoir that holds the infinite, sweet nectar of spiritual immortality (ब्रह्मामृत) generated within the sahasrāra crown center. finding her presence gives the practitioner immediate, permanent access to this source of unconditioned joy, concluding the 800-name milestone with absolute satisfaction.

The Reservoir of the Non-Dual Nectar: The word शेवधि (Śevadhi) denotes an inexhaustible, highly guarded divine treasure house. Finding Her presence gives the practitioner immediate, permanent access to this source of unconditioned joy, concluding the 800-name milestone with absolute satisfaction.

searchText: 801 पुष्टा षट्त्रिंशत्तत्त्वविग्रहशीलत्वात्। बहुभिर्गुणैर्ब्रह्मरसेन ब्राह्मणैर्वा पुष्टा। षट्त्रिंशत्तत्त्वविग्रहशीलत्वात्। — component from the samasa above बहुभिर्गुणैर्ब्रह्मरसेन — component from the samasa above ब्राह्मणैर्वा — component from the samasa above पुष्टा। — component from the samasa above (i) one who is full and nourished. (ii) she is complete with the thirty-six tattvas and with countless qualities. (iii) she nourishes the universe and is herself the fullness from which nourishment comes. in the saubhāgyabhāskara, bhāskararāya details the metaphysical expansion behind this fullness: the fullness of the thirty-six tattvas: she is puṣṭā because her manifest body is perfectly filled and nourished by the complete deployment of the thirty-six foundational principles of reality (षट्त्रिंशत्तत्त्व). the essence of brahman: alternatively, she is continually nourished and amplified by absolute virtues, the nectar of brahman-bliss (brahma-rasa), and the spiritual realizations of realized seers, remaining absolute without decay.

The Fullness of the Thirty-Six Tattvas: She is Puṣṭā because Her manifest body is perfectly filled and nourished by the complete deployment of the thirty-six foundational principles of reality (षट्त्रिंशत्तत्त्व).

The Essence of Brahman: Alternatively, She is continually nourished and amplified by absolute virtues, the nectar of Brahman-bliss (brahma-rasa), and the spiritual realizations of realized seers, remaining absolute without decay.

name: पुरातना
summary: (i) One who is ancient.
(ii) She is prior to all beings and is the origin of everything.
(iii) Since all qualities and worlds arise in Her, She is called Puratana, the ancient one.
searchText: 802 पुरातना सर्वेषामादिभूतत्वात्पुरातना। पुरातना गुणा अस्यां सन्तीति। पुरातना — ancient (i) one who is ancient. (ii) she is prior to all beings and is the origin of everything. (iii) since all qualities and worlds arise in her, she is called puratana, the ancient one. bhāskararāya defines her radical priority over the cosmic timeline: the primeval source: she is purātanā because she exists prior to the first movement of cosmic manifestation (सर्वेषाम आदिभूतत्वात्). before space formats itself and before time begins its count, she is already present as the timeless, uncreated background awareness.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya defines Her radical priority over the cosmic timeline:

The Primeval Source: She is Purātanā because She exists prior to the first movement of cosmic manifestation (सर्वेषाम आदिभूतत्वात्). Before space formats itself and before time begins its count, She is already present as the timeless, uncreated background awareness.

searchText: 805 पुष्करेक्षणा पुष्कराणीव कमलानीवेक्षणानि नयनानि यस्याः सा। पुष्करशब्दः पृथिवीपरः। मह्यादिविषये क्षण उत्सवो निर्व्यापारस्थितिर्वा यस्याः सा। पुष्करशब्दो न्यग्रोधवृक्षपरः। न्यग्रोधपर्णशायित्वाद्विष्णुरत्र लक्षणया गृह्यते। तत्रेक्षणं कृपानिरीक्षणं यस्याः सा। पुष्कराणीव — component from the samasa above कमलानीवेक्षणानि — component from the samasa above नयनानि — component from the samasa above यस्याः — component from the samasa above सा। — component from the samasa above पुष्करशब्दः — component from the samasa above (i) one whose eyes are like lotuses. (iii) the name also permits meanings connected with earth, sacred trees and the compassionate glance of vishnu, all absorbed in her power. bhāskararāya decodes the administrative and compassionate quality of her gaze: the gaze of sustenance: her eyes operate like wide, liquid lotus petals. when her glance fixes upon the earth plane, it initiates an internal festival of stability (utsava). when her glance fixes upon the preserving energy of viṣṇu resting upon the banyan leaf, it feeds him the dynamic compassion needed to maintain planetary order.

(iii) she is the highest resting-place and the supreme brilliance sought by worshippers. in the saubhāgyabhāskara, bhāskararāya anchors her as the final terminal: the ultimate destination: dhāma signifies both a blinding, infinite energy field (tejas) and the final spiritual coordinate where the soul rests. she is paraṁdhāma because she marks the absolute ceiling of spiritual attainment—the shoreless, non-dual home from which no traveler returns to bondage.
esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara, Bhāskararāya anchors Her as the final terminal:

esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara, Bhāskararāya decodes the geometry of Her left lower limb:

name: पाशहन्त्री
summary: (i) One who destroys bonds.

(iii) She cuts the bonds that bind the individual soul, including ignorance, desire and limitation.
searchText: 811 पाशहन्त्री पाशानां हन्त्री नाशिका। हन्त्री — destroyer (i) one who destroys bonds. (ii) pasha means bondage or fetter. (iii) she cuts the bonds that bind the individual soul, including ignorance, desire and limitation. bhāskararāya targets the systematic extraction of existential limitations: the cutter of the eight tethers: individual souls are trapped by the eight tethers (pāśas — shame, doubt, fear, pride, lineage, custom, status, and judgment). as pāśahantrī, her sharp kinetic energy cuts these specific chords, breaking the energetic cages of the subconscious and instantly liberating the soul.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya targets the systematic extraction of existential limitations:

The Cutter of the Eight Tethers: Individual souls are trapped by the eight tethers (pāśas — shame, doubt, fear, pride, lineage, custom, status, and judgment). name: परमन्त्रविभेदिनी
samasa: परेषां स्वोपासकद्विषां मन्त्रान्, परैरभिचाराद्यर्थं प्रयुक्तो मनुः परमन्त्रः, तान् वा, विशेषाद्भिनत्तीति।
परा उत्कृष्टा ये मन्तारो मननकर्तारस्तेषामवीन् पापानि भेदयति नाशयति।
summary: (i) One who breaks hostile or external mantras.
(ii) She destroys mantras used by enemies of Her worshippers, including harmful rites.
(iii) She also reveals or unfolds the supreme mantra, the Panchadashi, and destroys the sins of worthy contemplators.
searchText: 812 परमन्त्रविभेदिनी परेषां स्वोपासकद्विषां मन्त्रान्, परैरभिचाराद्यर्थं प्रयुक्तो मनुः परमन्त्रः, तान् वा, विशेषाद्भिनत्तीति। पर उत्कृष्टो मन्त्रः पञ्चदशीरूपस्तं विभेदयति। परा उत्कृष्टा ये मन्तारो मननकर्तारस्तेषामवीन् पापानि भेदयति नाशयति। मन्त्रान् — mantras पर — higher; supreme (i) one who breaks hostile or external mantras. (ii) she destroys mantras used by enemies of her worshippers, including harmful rites. (iii) she also reveals or unfolds the supreme mantra, the panchadashi, and destroys the sins of worthy contemplators. in the saubhāgyabhāskara, bhāskararāya navigates her defensive and instructional operations: the shield against subtle aggression: she completely deactivates and dismantles hostile energetic actions (abhicāra) aimed at her practitioners. the pierce of the core formula: concurrently, she breaks open and reveals the hidden meanings inside the supreme fifteen-syllabled pañcadaśī formula, clearing away intellectual blocks.
esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara, Bhāskararāya navigates Her defensive and instructional operations:
The Shield against Subtle Aggression: She completely deactivates and dismantles hostile energetic actions (abhicāra) aimed at Her practitioners.
The Pierce of the Core Formula: Concurrently, She breaks open and reveals the hidden meanings inside the supreme fifteen-syllabled Pañcadaśī formula, clearing away intellectual blocks.

name: मूर्ता
summary: (i) One who has form.
(ii) All formed objects and the gross elements are Her forms.
(iii) She is therefore worshipped in visible and tangible forms also.
searchText: 813 मूर्ता रूपवद्वस्तु मूर्तम्, तद्रूपा। पञ्चीकृतानि महाभूतानि मूर्तानि, तद्रूपा। मूर्तम् — formed (i) one who has form. (ii) all formed objects and the gross elements are her forms. (iii) she is therefore worshipped in visible and tangible forms also. bhāskararāya details her absolute immanence within gross matter: the dense fabric of nature: she is mūrtā because she forms the physical fabric of the five gross elements after they have undergone the process of quintuplication (पञ्चीकरण). every physical object, planetary body, and tangible biological shell is her solidified form, showing her total presence in the material plane.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya details Her absolute immanence within gross matter:

The Dense Fabric of Nature: She is Mūrtā because She forms the physical fabric of the five gross elements after they have undergone the process of quintuplication (पञ्चीकरण). Every physical object, planetary body, and tangible biological shell is Her solidified form, showing Her total presence in the material plane.

summary: (i) One who is formless.
(ii) Air, space and the subtle elements are without gross form; She is in those also.
(iii) Thus She is both formed and formless, gross and subtle.
searchText: 814 अमूर्ता वाय्वाकाशादिकममूर्तम्, तद्रूपा। अपञ्चीकृतानि तु भूतसूक्ष्माण्यमूर्तानि, तद्रूपा।। वाय्वाकाशादिकममूर्तम् — component from the samasa above तद्रूपा। — component from the samasa above अपञ्चीकृतानि — component from the samasa above तु — component from the samasa above भूतसूक्ष्माण्यमूर्तानि — component from the samasa above तद्रूपा।। — component from the samasa above (i) one who is formless. (ii) air, space and the subtle elements are without gross form; she is in those also. (iii) thus she is both formed and formless, gross and subtle. bhāskararāya constructs an immediate non-dual paradox following mūrtā: the subtle substratum: while she is the visible object (mūrtā), she is simultaneously amūrtā — the formless, invisible sky of air and ether (ākāśa) and the unquintupled, subtle elemental seeds (tanmātras). she confirms that the unmanifest vacuum and the dense world are waves of a single energy field.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya constructs an immediate non-dual paradox following Mūrtā:

The Subtle Substratum: While She is the visible object (Mūrtā), She is simultaneously Amūrtā — the formless, invisible sky of air and ether (ākāśa) and the unquintupled, subtle elemental seeds (tanmātras). She confirms that the unmanifest vacuum and the dense world are waves of a single energy field.

name: अनित्यतृप्ता
samasa: इति एवंप्रकारेण अतृप्ता न तृप्ता न भवतीति न। (ईदृशः प्रकारो नास्ति येन तृप्ता न स्यात्। अपि तु सर्वैरपि प्रकारैस्तृप्तैव)।
summary: (i) One who is satisfied even by impermanent offerings.
(ii) Since She is pleased by devotion alone, even simple and temporary offerings satisfy Her.
(iii) Another interpretation says there is no manner in which She is not satisfied; She is always fulfilled by every sincere mode of worship.
searchText: 815 अनित्यतृप्ता अनित्यैरेवोपचारैस्तृप्तेति पञ्चाक्षरं नाम भक्तिमात्रप्रियत्वात्। अनिति श्वसतीति जीवोऽनितिपदार्थः। तै: अतृप्ता। इति एवंप्रकारेण अतृप्ता न तृप्ता न भवतीति न। (ईदृशः प्रकारो नास्ति येन तृप्ता न स्यात्। अपि तु सर्वैरपि प्रकारैस्तृप्तैव)। तृप्ता — satisfied (i) one who is satisfied even by impermanent offerings. (ii) since she is pleased by devotion alone, even simple and temporary offerings satisfy her. (iii) another interpretation says there is no manner in which she is not satisfied; she is always fulfilled by every sincere mode of worship. in the saubhāgyabhāskara, bhāskararāya decodes the transactional dynamics of simple devotion: sincerity over opulence: she does not demand permanent, impossible physical monuments; she is perfectly satisfied by transient, fleeting daily offerings (anitya-upacāra) if driven by pure devotion. the double negation: under the logical track, it is impossible for her to experience lack; she remains fully satisfied across every parameter and condition in existence.
esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara, Bhāskararāya decodes the transactional dynamics of simple devotion:
Sincerity over Opulence: She does not demand permanent, impossible physical monuments; She is perfectly satisfied by transient, fleeting daily offerings (anitya-upacāra) if driven by pure devotion.
The Double Negation: Under the logical track, it is impossible for Her to experience lack; She remains fully satisfied across every parameter and condition in existence.

(ii) the minds of munis are like manasa lake, and she moves there like a swan. (iii) she is honoured in the esteem of sages and dwells in their pure contemplation. bhāskararāya details the interior habitation of her grace: the swan of the interior lake: a pristine swan (haṁsī) leaves muddy swamps to swim exclusively in the clear waters of lake mānasa. similarly, she bypasses minds clouded by worldly desires, choosing to glide exclusively within the clear, calm, and undisturbed intellectual waters (mānasa) of deep, contemplative meditators.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya details the interior habitation of Her grace:

The Swan of the Interior Lake: A pristine swan (haṁsī) leaves muddy swamps to swim exclusively in the clear waters of Lake Mānasa. Similarly, She bypasses minds clouded by worldly desires, choosing to glide exclusively within the clear, calm, and undisturbed intellectual waters (mānasa) of deep, contemplative meditators.

name: सत्यव्रता
samasa: सत्यं ब्रह्मैव व्रतं भक्ष्यमुपचारात्तद्वत्प्रियं यस्याः सा।
सत्यव्रतोनाम ब्राह्मणः सूकरभयात् ऐ ऐ इत्युच्चार्य तावतैव तपसा महाकविर्देवीभक्तो जातस्तदभेदात्सत्यव्रता।
summary: (i) One whose vow is truth.
(ii) Truth, Brahman itself, is Her vow and Her delight.
(iii) The name also recalls vows that quickly bear fruit, and the tradition of Satyavrata, who became a great poet and devotee through the power of the Goddess.
searchText: 817 सत्यव्रता सत्यं ब्रह्मैव व्रतं भक्ष्यमुपचारात्तद्वत्प्रियं यस्याः सा। सत्यमेव व्रतं यस्याः सा। सत्यानि शीघ्रफलदानि व्रतानि यस्याः सा। सत्यव्रतोनाम ब्राह्मणः सूकरभयात् ऐ ऐ इत्युच्चार्य तावतैव तपसा महाकविर्देवीभक्तो जातस्तदभेदात्सत्यव्रता। सत्यं — truth (i) one whose vow is truth. (ii) truth, brahman itself, is her vow and her delight. (iii) the name also recalls vows that quickly bear fruit, and the tradition of satyavrata, who became a great poet and devotee through the power of the goddess. bhāskararāya maps the historical and philosophical accuracy of this title: the absolute vow: her essential code of operation is absolute truth (satya). the power of the seed-syllable: under the historical track, she references a mute brahmin (satyavrata) who accidentally vibrated the primary wisdom seed-syllable ऐं (aiṁ) out of fear, instantly unlocking her presence and transforming into a master poet, proving the immediate responsiveness of her sound keys.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya maps the historical and philosophical accuracy of this title:
The Absolute Vow: Her essential code of operation is absolute truth (Satya).

The Power of the Seed-Syllable: Under the historical track, She references a mute Brahmin (Satyavrata) who accidentally vibrated the primary wisdom seed-syllable ऐं (Aiṁ) out of fear, instantly unlocking Her presence and transforming into a master poet, proving the immediate responsiveness of Her sound keys.

name: सत्यरूपा
summary: (i) One whose form is truth.
(ii) Truth is that which is not contradicted in the three periods of time.
(iii) She is that unchanging reality.
searchText: 818 सत्यरूपा सत्यं कालत्रयाबाध्यं रूपं यस्याः सा। सत्यं — truth (i) one whose form is truth. (ii) truth is that which is not contradicted in the three periods of time. (iii) she is that unchanging reality. in the saubhāgyabhāskara, bhāskararāya provides the definitive technical validation of सत्य: untouched by the three tenses: satya is defined as that reality which can never be contradicted, altered, or negated across the past, present, or future (kālatrayābādhya). every material object shifts and fails; she is satyarūpā because her baseline consciousness remains constant across all universal transitions.
esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara, Bhāskararāya provides the definitive technical validation of सत्य:

Untouched by the Three Tenses: Satya is defined as that reality which can never be contradicted, altered, or negated across the past, present, or future (kālatrayābādhya). Every material object shifts and fails; She is Satyarūpā because Her baseline consciousness remains constant across all universal transitions.

(iii) she governs from within as consciousness, life and law. bhāskararāya details the mechanics of her omnipresent governance: the thread behind the puppets: she does not govern the cosmos from a distant exterior sky. as the antaryāmiṇī, she sits inside the internal cognitive core of every single living soul, atom, and element. she directs their internal movements, formats their karmic translations, and animates their baseline existence from within.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya details the mechanics of Her omnipresent governance:

The Thread behind the Puppets: She does not govern the cosmos from a distant exterior sky. As the Antaryāmiṇī, She sits inside the internal cognitive core of every single living soul, atom, and element. She directs their internal movements, formats their karmic translations, and animates their baseline existence from within.

The Substance of Being: She is Satī because She is the non-dual expression of pure existence (Sad-rūpa). Mythologically, She is the historical daughter of Dakṣa who chose absolute fusion with Lord Śiva, proving that uncorrupted devotion and absolute reality are one substance.

The Foundation of Bliss: The Upaniṣad describes the structure of bliss, concluding with “Brahma pucchaṁ pratiṣṭhā” ("Brahman is the tail or structural foundation"). As Brahmāṇī, She functions as that absolute anchor point (puccha/aṇī) that stabilizes the entire framework of cosmic bliss, giving life to the creator deity himself.

name: ब्रह्म
summary: (i) One who is Brahman.
(ii) Brahman is the knowledge attained by the liberated and is not different from the Self.
(iii) She is that supreme Brahman itself.
searchText: 822 ब्रह्म ब्रह्म यन्मुक्तप्राप्यं तत् स्वात्माभिन्नं ज्ञानम्। ब्रह्म — brahman (i) one who is brahman. (ii) brahman is the knowledge attained by the liberated and is not different from the self. (iii) she is that supreme brahman itself. bhāskararāya eliminates any remaining dualistic filters: identity with the whole: lest a student think she is merely a secondary power belonging to a higher absolute, this name establishes she is the unconditioned, non-dual परब्रह्म (parabrahman). she is that non-dual knowledge (jñāna) that is accessed by liberated ascetics, completely identical with one's own core self (svātmābhinna).
esoteric: Bhāskararāya eliminates any remaining dualistic filters:

Identity with the Whole: Lest a student think She is merely a secondary power belonging to a higher absolute, this name establishes She is the unconditioned, non-dual परब्रह्म (Parabrahman). She is that non-dual knowledge (jñāna) that is accessed by liberated ascetics, completely identical with one's own core Self (svātmābhinna).

name: बहुरूपा
summary: (i) One who has many forms.
(ii) She assumes innumerable forms according to the needs of creation, worship and experience.
(iii) The many deities and the many beings are Her expressions.
searchText: 824 बहुरूपा बहूनि रूपाणि यस्याः सा। बहूनि — many रूपाणि — forms (i) one who has many forms. (ii) she assumes innumerable forms according to the needs of creation, worship and experience. (iii) the many deities and the many beings are her expressions. bhāskararāya details the ultimate morphological paradox: the singular multitude: though she is completely alone and non-dual (ekākinī), she is simultaneously bahurūpā because every single changing form, planet, cell, and creature in the universe is a mask assumed by her energy field. duality is merely her unity displaying its infinite potential.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya details the ultimate morphological paradox:

The Singular Multitude: Though She is completely alone and non-dual (Ekākinī), She is simultaneously Bahurūpā because every single changing form, planet, cell, and creature in the universe is a mask assumed by Her energy field. Duality is merely Her unity displaying its infinite potential.

esoteric: Bhāskararāya clarifies the tier of appreciation targeted here:

The Worship of the Discerning: Budha refers to those who have crossed past superficial emotionalism and dry logic, anchoring themselves in direct spiritual discernment (jñānīs). Their structured adoration confirms that Her path satisfies the highest operational demands of the human intellect.

(ii) her messengers and powers are intense and capable of destroying hostile forces. esoteric: Bhāskararāya details Her aggressive corrective filters:

The Force of Pure Protection: She is Pracaṇḍā because Her protective heralds and defensive energy lines operate with unyielding, terrifying velocity (caṇḍatva) to crush egoic deviations. Botanically, Her connection to the cooling Śaṅkhapuṣpī flower demonstrates that this intense fire serves to restore long-term internal peace.

name: आज्ञा

summary: (i) One who is command.

(iii) The universe functions by Her command.
searchText: 828 आज्ञा भगवदिच्छारूपा। भगवदिच्छारूपा। — component from the samasa above (i) one who is command. (ii) she is the very form of divine will and order. (iii) the universe functions by her command. bhāskararāya identifies the core law of universal motion: the executive decree of consciousness: she is the living embodiment of the supreme command (ājñā) issued by the absolute consciousness. this command is भगवदिच्छा (bhagavad-icchā — divine will), the unalterable cosmic law that dictates planetary orbits, elements, and karmic metrics. nothing moves outside this structural decree.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya identifies the core law of universal motion:

The Executive Decree of Consciousness: She is the living embodiment of the supreme command (Ājñā) issued by the absolute consciousness. This command is भगवदिच्छा (Bhagavad-icchā — divine will), the unalterable cosmic law that dictates planetary orbits, elements, and karmic metrics. Nothing moves outside this structural decree.

विश्वमिति। — component from the samasa above
summary: (i) One who is the foundation.
(ii) The whole universe stands established in Her.
(iii) She is the support in which all existence rests.
searchText: 829 प्रतिष्ठा प्रतितिष्ठत्यस्यां विश्वमिति। प्रतितिष्ठत्यस्यां — component from the samasa above विश्वमिति। — component from the samasa above (i) one who is the foundation. (ii) the whole universe stands established in her. (iii) she is the support in which all existence rests. in the saubhāgyabhāskara, bhāskararāya details the physics of universal containment: the substratum of the world movie: a moving picture requires an unmoving, white screen to be projected upon. she is pratiṣṭhā because she is that exact stable screen within which the entire manifest universe (viśvam) stands, operates, and resides, remaining untouched by the transformations of the movie.
esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara, Bhāskararāya details the physics of universal containment:

The Substratum of the World Movie: A moving picture requires an unmoving, white screen to be projected upon. She is Pratiṣṭhā because She is that exact stable screen within which the entire manifest universe (viśvam) stands, operates, and resides, remaining untouched by the transformations of the movie.

name: प्रकटाकृतिः
samasa: प्रकटा सर्वैरनुभूयमाना आकृतिः रूपं यस्याः सा।
summary: (i) One whose form is manifest.
(ii) Her form is clearly experienced by all through the universe and through worship.
(iii) Though supreme and subtle, She also appears openly as the visible form of all existence.
searchText: 830 प्रकटाकृतिः प्रकटा सर्वैरनुभूयमाना आकृतिः रूपं यस्याः सा। प्रकटा — manifest आकृतिः — form (i) one whose form is manifest. (ii) her form is clearly experienced by all through the universe and through worship. (iii) though supreme and subtle, she also appears openly as the visible form of all existence. bhāskararāya concludes this sub-sequence by breaking the illusion of her distance: the obvious reality: sages often describe her as hidden (guhyā). however, bhāskararāya highlights the ultimate non-dual baseline: she is simultaneously prakaṭākṛtiḥ because her form is being directly and continuously experienced by every single living creature every second. every flash of sensory awareness, every breath, and every pulse of existence is her direct, manifest appearance, blindingly obvious to anyone who truly looks.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya concludes this sub-sequence by breaking the illusion of Her distance:

The Obvious Reality: Sages often describe Her as hidden (Guhyā). However, Bhāskararāya highlights the ultimate non-dual baseline: She is simultaneously Prakaṭākṛtiḥ because Her form is being directly and continuously experienced by every single living creature every second. Every flash of sensory awareness, every breath, and every pulse of existence is Her direct, manifest appearance, blindingly obvious to anyone who truly looks.

name: प्राणेश्वरी
wordByWord: प्राणानाम् — of the pranas or vital powers
summary: (i) One who is the ruler of the pranas.
(ii) Prana also denotes the vital functions and the senses. Sreedevee presides over them and directs their activity.
(iii) She is also the deity indicated by the Vedic sound, which is the highest form of sacred speech.
searchText: 831 प्राणेश्वरी प्राणानामिन्द्रियाणामधिष्ठातृत्वादीश्वरी। प्राणस्य पञ्चवृत्तिकस्याधिपतिर्वा। प्रकृष्टोऽणः शब्दो वेदरूपस्तदीश्वरी तत्प्रतिपाद्यदेवता। प्राणानाम् — of the pranas or vital powers इन्द्रियाणाम् — of the senses अधिष्ठातृत्वात् — because of presiding over ईश्वरी — ruler; governing goddess प्राणस्य — of prana पञ्चवृत्तिकस्य — having five functions अधिपतिः — lord; ruler (i) one who is the ruler of the pranas. (ii) prana also denotes the vital functions and the senses. sreedevee presides over them and directs their activity. (iii) she is also the deity indicated by the vedic sound, which is the highest form of sacred speech. bhāskararāya details her biological and scriptural governance: the ruler of the five breaths: she is the supreme director governing the five-fold life current (prāṇa, apāna, vyāna, udāna, samāna). concurrently, she rules over the sensory apparatus networks (indriyas). under the phonic key, she is the inner consciousness targeted by the refined words of the veda (pra-aṇa).
esoteric: Bhāskararāya details Her biological and scriptural governance:

The Ruler of the Five Breaths: She is the supreme director governing the five-fold life current (prāṇa, apāna, vyāna, udāna, samāna). Concurrently, She rules over the sensory apparatus networks (indriyas). Under the phonic key, She is the inner consciousness targeted by the refined words of the Veda (pra-aṇa).

name: प्राणदात्री
summary: (i) One who gives prana.
(ii) All life-breath and vitality proceed from Her.
(iii) The living being functions because She grants the vital force.
searchText: 832 प्राणदात्री प्राणानां दात्री। प्राणानाम् — of the pranas दात्री — giver; bestower (i) one who gives prana. (ii) all life-breath and vitality proceed from her. (iii) the living being functions because she grants the vital force. in the saubhāgyabhāskara, bhāskararāya maps her continuous life support: the dispenser of vitality: she does not simply watch over the energy fields; she actively dispenses the actual currency of life-force (prāṇa) required to keep the physical tissues viable, ensuring that the biological framework remains integrated so the soul can complete its evolution.
esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara, Bhāskararāya maps Her continuous life support:

The Dispenser of Vitality: She does not simply watch over the energy fields; She actively dispenses the actual currency of life-force (Prāṇa) required to keep the physical tissues viable, ensuring that the biological framework remains integrated so the soul can complete its evolution.

name: पञ्चाशत्पीठरूपिणी
summary: (i) One whose form is the fifty-one pithas.
(ii) The sacred seats beginning with Kamarupa and ending with Chayachatra are Her own forms.
(iii) Thus the entire field of Shakti worship is included in Her body.
searchText: 833 पञ्चाशत्पीठरूपिणी कामरूपादिच्छायाछत्रान्तैकपञ्चाशत्पीठानि रूपमस्याः अस्तीति। पञ्चाशत् — fifty पीठ — sacred seat or pitha रूपिणी — whose form is कामरूपादि — beginning with kamarupa छायाछत्रान्त — ending with chayachatra पीठानि — pithas; sacred seats (i) one whose form is the fifty-one pithas. (ii) the sacred seats beginning with kamarupa and ending with chayachatra are her own forms. (iii) thus the entire field of shakti worship is included in her body. bhāskararāya details the absolute geographical and internal mapping of this tantric grid: the geography of energy: the manifest landscape features fifty-one highly strategic energy vortexes (śākta-pīṭhas), running from kāmarūpa down to chāyāchhatra. internally, these seats correspond perfectly to the fifty-one phonic letters of the sanskrit alphabet (mātṛkā) distributed across the petals of the subtle body. she is the integrated grid that coordinates this entire layout.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya details the absolute geographical and internal mapping of this Tantric grid:

The Geography of Energy: The manifest landscape features fifty-one highly strategic energy vortexes (Śākta-Pīṭhas), running from Kāmarūpa down to Chāyāchhatra. Internally, these seats correspond perfectly to the fifty-one phonic letters of the Sanskrit alphabet (Mātṛkā) distributed across the petals of the subtle body. She is the integrated grid that coordinates this entire layout.

name: विश‍ृङ्खला
wordByWord: विगतशृङ्खला — one whose chain has gone
summary: (i) One who is free from chains.
(ii) A chain indicates bondage, dependence or limitation.
(iii) She is unbound, independent and beyond every fetter.
searchText: 834 विश‍ृङ्खला विगता शृङ्खला यस्याः सा। वि — without; free from शृङ्खला — chain; fetter विगतशृङ्खला — one whose chain has gone यस्याः सा — she whose (i) one who is free from chains. (ii) a chain indicates bondage, dependence or limitation. (iii) she is unbound, independent and beyond every fetter. bhāskararāya defines her absolute freedom from systemic constraints: the unconditioned infinite: human minds are bound by the iron chains of linear logic, social convention, and emotional filters. the physical world is bound by laws of limitation. she is viśṛṅkhalā because her essential nature stands completely past these boundary lines, operating with absolute, unrestricted freedom (svātantrya).
esoteric: Bhāskararāya defines Her absolute freedom from systemic constraints:

The Unconditioned Infinite: Human minds are bound by the iron chains of linear logic, social convention, and emotional filters. The physical world is bound by laws of limitation. She is Viśṛṅkhalā because Her essential nature stands completely past these boundary lines, operating with absolute, unrestricted freedom (Svātantrya).

name: विविक्तस्था
wordByWord: विविक्ते — in a pure, solitary or discriminating place
विजने स्थले — in a secluded place
आत्मानात्मविवेकशीलेषु — in those who discriminate Self and non-Self
summary: (i) One who abides in solitude and purity.
(ii) Vivikta also means discrimination between the Self and the non-Self.
(iii) She is present in the pure place and in the minds of those established in discrimination.
searchText: 835 विविक्तस्था विविक्ते पवित्रे विजने स्थले तिष्ठतीति। विविक्ते आत्मानात्मविवेकशीलेषु तिष्ठतीति। विविक्ते — in a pure, solitary or discriminating place पवित्रे — in purity विजने स्थले — in a secluded place तिष्ठति — abides आत्मानात्मविवेकशीलेषु — in those who discriminate self and non-self (i) one who abides in solitude and purity. (ii) vivikta also means discrimination between the self and the non-self. (iii) she is present in the pure place and in the minds of those established in discrimination. in the saubhāgyabhāskara, bhāskararāya details both the physical and psychological coordinates of her presence: the coordinate of discernment: geometrically and physically, she loves silent, pure, and undisturbed spaces free from chaotic noise. internally, vivikta refers to the sharp, highly refined capacity to differentiate the eternal self from the transient non-self (आत्मानात्मविवेक). she stations herself directly inside intellects that possess this clarity.
esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara, Bhāskararāya details both the physical and psychological coordinates of Her presence:

The Coordinate of Discernment: Geometrically and physically, She loves silent, pure, and undisturbed spaces free from chaotic noise. Internally, Vivikta refers to the sharp, highly refined capacity to differentiate the eternal Self from the transient non-self (आत्मानात्मविवेक). She stations Herself directly inside intellects that possess this clarity.

name: वीरमाता
samasa: वीरा उपासकधुरन्धरा, रणे अभिमुखे हता वा। तेषां माता जननी।
वीरं मद्यस्य भाजने इति विश्वकोशात्पानपात्रं तन्मातीति
wordByWord: रणे अभिमुखे हताः — those who face battle and are slain
summary: (i) Mother of heroes.
(ii) Vira means the heroic upasaka, one fit for the demanding path of worship.
(iii) She is the mother and source of such courageous devotees.
searchText: 836 वीरमाता वीरा उपासकधुरन्धरा, रणे अभिमुखे हता वा। तेषां माता जननी। वीरं मद्यस्य भाजने इति विश्वकोशात्पानपात्रं तन्मातीति वीराः — heroes; qualified worshippers उपासकधुरन्धराः — eminent upasakas माता — mother जननी — mother; source रणे अभिमुखे हताः — those who face battle and are slain (i) mother of heroes. (ii) vira means the heroic upasaka, one fit for the demanding path of worship. (iii) she is the mother and source of such courageous devotees. bhāskararāya details her relationship with the elite vanguard of seekers: the mother of the fearless: vīras are those advanced, courageous practitioners (upāsaka-dhurandharāḥ) who treat spiritual practice as a martial campaign against their own internal limitations. she acts as their proud maternal source (mātā). under the agamic key, she is the intelligence that formats the sacred ritual vessel, containing the nectar of transformation.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya details Her relationship with the elite vanguard of seekers:

The Mother of the Fearless: Vīras are those advanced, courageous practitioners (upāsaka-dhurandharāḥ) who treat spiritual practice as a martial campaign against their own internal limitations. She acts as their proud maternal source (Mātā). Under the Agamic key, She is the intelligence that formats the sacred ritual vessel, containing the nectar of transformation.

name: वियत्प्रसूः
summary: (i) Mother of space.
(ii) Viyat means akasha, the element of space.
(iii) Space itself is born from Her; therefore She is the source of the subtle elements.
searchText: 837 वियत्प्रसूः वियत आकाशस्य प्रसूर्जनिका। वियत् — space; akasha प्रसूः — mother; progenitress जनिका — producer; mother (i) mother of space. (ii) viyat means akasha, the element of space. (iii) space itself is born from her; therefore she is the source of the subtle elements. bhāskararāya tracks the primordial sequence of manifestation: the sky before the sky: space (akāśa) is the first element projected during creation, providing the room within which the other elements can step down. she is viyatprasūḥ because she stands before this first boundary, acting as the parent consciousness that breathes out the space matrix itself.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya tracks the primordial sequence of manifestation:

The Sky before the Sky: Space (Akāśa) is the first element projected during creation, providing the room within which the other elements can step down. She is Viyatprasūḥ because She stands before this first boundary, acting as the parent consciousness that breathes out the space matrix itself.

name: मुकुन्दा
wordByWord: विष्णुरूपत्वात् — because of being in the form of Vishnu
summary: (i) One who gives liberation.
(ii) Mukunda is one who grants mukti.
(iii) The name also recalls Her identity with Vishnu, who is traditionally called Mukunda.
searchText: 838 मुकुन्दा मुक्तिं ददातीति मुकुन्दा। विष्णुरूपत्वाद्वा। मुक्तिम् — liberation ददाति — gives मुकुन्दा — giver of liberation विष्णुरूपत्वात् — because of being in the form of vishnu (i) one who gives liberation. (ii) mukunda is one who grants mukti. (iii) the name also recalls her identity with vishnu, who is traditionally called mukunda. in the saubhāgyabhāskara, bhāskararāya reconciles her across traditional sectarian lines: the non-dual pervasive savior: mukunda is a celebrated title for lord viṣṇu as the savior who grants liberation. she is mukundā because she is completely identical with this preserving and liberating light field. she steps across nominal boundaries, confirming that the energy of viṣṇu is her own dynamic presence.
esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara, Bhāskararāya reconciles Her across traditional sectarian lines:

The Non-Dual Pervasive Savior: Mukunda is a celebrated title for Lord Viṣṇu as the savior who grants liberation. She is Mukundā because She is completely identical with this preserving and liberating light field. She steps across nominal boundaries, confirming that the energy of Viṣṇu is Her own dynamic presence.

name: मुक्तिनिलया
samasa: मुक्तीनां पञ्चविधमोक्षाणां निलय आकरो यस्यां सा।
wordByWord: पञ्चविधमोक्षाणाम् — of the five kinds of moksha
summary: (i) The abode of liberation.
(ii) The five kinds of liberation rest in Her.
(iii) She is both the giver and the resting-place of moksha.
searchText: 839 मुक्तिनिलया मुक्तीनां पञ्चविधमोक्षाणां निलय आकरो यस्यां सा। मुक्तीनाम् — of liberations पञ्चविधमोक्षाणाम् — of the five kinds of moksha निलयः — abode आकरः — mine; source यस्याम् सा — she in whom (i) the abode of liberation. (ii) the five kinds of liberation rest in her. (iii) she is both the giver and the resting-place of moksha. bhāskararāya details the complete taxonomy of final release: the storehouse of the five freedoms: liberation is processed through five distinct functional stages: sālokya (sharing the realm), sāmīpya (proximity), sārūpya (similarity of form), sāyujya (complete absorption), and kaivalya (absolute non-dual oneness). she is muktinilayā because she acts as the definitive reservoir (ākaraḥ) containing all five states, serving as the final dock for the soul.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya details the complete taxonomy of final release:

The Storehouse of the Five Freedoms: Liberation is processed through five distinct functional stages: Sālokya (sharing the realm), Sāmīpya (proximity), Sārūpya (similarity of form), Sāyujya (complete absorption), and Kaivalya (absolute non-dual oneness). She is Muktinilayā because She acts as the definitive reservoir (ākaraḥ) containing all five states, serving as the final dock for the soul.

name: मूलविग्रहरूपिणी

name: भावज्ञा

name: भवरोगघ्नी
samasa: भवः संसार एव रोगस्तं हन्ति इति।

summary: (i) One who destroys the disease of worldly existence.
(ii) Samsara is compared to a disease because it causes repeated suffering.
(iii) She cures that disease by giving knowledge and liberation.
searchText: 842 भवरोगघ्नी भवः संसार एव रोगस्तं हन्ति इति। भवः — worldly existence; samsara संसारः — the cycle of worldly life रोगः — disease घ्नी — destroyer तं हन्ति — destroys that (i) one who destroys the disease of worldly existence. (ii) samsara is compared to a disease because it causes repeated suffering. (iii) she cures that disease by giving knowledge and liberation. in the saubhāgyabhāskara, bhāskararāya maps her absolute therapeutic role: the medicine for rebirth: worldly existence (bhava) is not viewed merely as a political space; it is classified as a chronic, systemic psycho-spiritual disease (roga) that causes the soul to wander in friction. she acts as the ultimate physician, forcefully eradicating this root sickness and restoring the soul to its healthy state of pure consciousness.

name: भवचक्रप्रवर्तिनी

searchText: 843 भवचक्रप्रवर्तिनी भवचक्रं संसारमण्डलं प्रवर्ततीति। भवचक्रवत्प्रवर्तयतीति। भवचक्रम् — wheel of becoming; wheel of samsara संसारमण्डलम् — circle of worldly existence प्रवर्तते — moves; turns प्रवर्तयति — sets in motion (i) one who sets the wheel of becoming in motion. (iii) she turns this wheel as the power behind creation and worldly experience. bhāskararāya details her control over macrocosmic dynamics: the director of the cosmic wheel: the universe passes through massive cyclical rotations—birth, growth, decay, and death—known as the भवचक्र (bhava-cakra — the wheel of shifting becoming). she stands at the absolute hub of this wheel, acting as the dynamic axis that keeps the entire wheel spinning to execute cosmic evolution.

name: शास्त्रसारा
samasa: शास्त्रं शास्त्रे वा सारो यस्याः सा।
summary: (i) The essence of the scriptures.
(ii) The shastras have value because they reveal Her and lead the devotee to Her.
(iii) She is the core teaching contained in all sacred texts.
searchText: 845 शास्त्रसारा शास्त्रं शास्त्रे वा सारो यस्याः सा। शास्त्रम् — scripture शास्त्रे — in scripture सारः — essence यस्याः सा — she whose (i) the essence of the scriptures. (iii) she is the core teaching contained in all sacred texts. in the saubhāgyabhāskara, bhāskararāya defines her as the target of all literature: the ultimate scriptural conclusion: sacred texts contain extensive procedural instructions, debates, and local charts. she is śāstrasārā because if one strips away the conditional wrappers of all these sciences, the ultimate, non-dual essence left at the bottom is strictly her own nature, serving as the shared target of all true knowledge.
esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara, Bhāskararāya defines Her as the target of all literature:

name: मन्त्रसारा
summary: (i) The essence of mantras.
(ii) All mantras obtain their power from Her.
(iii) She is the inner potency and meaning of every mantra.
searchText: 846 मन्त्रसारा मन्त्राणि सारो यस्याः सा। मन्त्राणि — mantras सारः — essence यस्याः सा — she whose (i) the essence of mantras. (ii) all mantras obtain their power from her. (iii) she is the inner potency and meaning of every mantra. bhāskararāya unpacks the acoustic hierarchy of the śrīvidyā tradition: the potency inside the syllables: thousands of mantras exist across the global grid. she is mantrasārā because she is the hidden, activating spark and the ultimate resonance (nāda) that gives these phonic structures their actual power, functioning as the crown energy inside the syllables.

name: तलोदरी

name: उदारकीर्तिः
samasa: उत्कृष्टा आसमन्ताद्व्याप्ता च अरा शीघ्रसाध्या च कीर्तिर्यदुपासनया यस्याः सा।
summary: (i) One of noble fame.
(ii) Her glory is vast, excellent and widely spread.
(iii) Worship of Her quickly leads to honour, merit and spiritual fruit.
searchText: 848 उदारकीर्तिः उदारा महत्तरा कीर्तिर्यस्याः सा। उत्कृष्टा आसमन्ताद्व्याप्ता च अरा शीघ्रसाध्या च कीर्तिर्यदुपासनया यस्याः सा। उदारा — noble; great महत्तरा — very great कीर्तिः — fame व्याप्ता — spread; pervading उपासना — worship (i) one of noble fame. (ii) her glory is vast, excellent and widely spread. (iii) worship of her quickly leads to honour, merit and spiritual fruit. bhāskararāya details the rapid operational parameters of her renown: the fame of boundless grace: her renown is udāra because it spreads without boundaries (vyāptā). bhāskararāya notes an advanced operational breakdown: her practice is अरा (arā — swiftly successful). by aligning with her system, the practitioner's inner merit and pure spiritual voice are amplified across the cosmic grid with extreme speed.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya details the rapid operational parameters of Her renown:

By aligning with Her system, the practitioner's inner merit and pure spiritual voice are amplified across the cosmic grid with extreme speed.

name: उद्दामवैभवा
samasa: दाम बन्धनरज्जुः परिच्छेत्री तदुत्क्रान्तमुद्दामेयत्तानवच्छिन्नं वैभवं यस्याः सा।
summary: (i) One whose splendour is unbounded.
(ii) Dama is a limiting cord; uddama means beyond limitation.
(iii) Her majesty cannot be confined or measured.
searchText: 849 उद्दामवैभवा दाम बन्धनरज्जुः परिच्छेत्री तदुत्क्रान्तमुद्दामेयत्तानवच्छिन्नं वैभवं यस्याः सा। दाम — cord; binding rope बन्धनरज्जुः — rope of bondage उद्दाम — beyond restraint; unbounded वैभवम् — splendour; majesty अनवच्छिन्नम् — unlimited (i) one whose splendour is unbounded. (ii) dama is a limiting cord; uddama means beyond limitation. (iii) her majesty cannot be confined or measured. in the saubhāgyabhāskara, bhāskararāya defines her freedom from quantification: sovereignty past the rope of metric: a dāma is a cord used to measure boundaries or bind animals. her cosmic majesty, structural capacity, and infinite glory (vaibhava) have completely broken past this rope (इयत्तानवच्छिन्नम्). her power cannot be computed, quantified, or restricted by any conceptual grid, standing completely absolute.
esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara, Bhāskararāya defines Her freedom from quantification:

Sovereignty Past the Rope of Metric: A dāma is a cord used to measure boundaries or bind animals. Her power cannot be computed, quantified, or restricted by any conceptual grid, standing completely absolute.

name: वर्णरूपिणी
summary: (i) One whose form is the letters.
(ii) The letters of the Sanskrit alphabet are Her forms.
(iii) Speech, mantra and sacred sound all arise as Her manifestation.
searchText: 850 वर्णरूपिणी वर्णाश्चतुःषष्टिसंख्याका रूपमस्याः सा। वर्णाः — letters or colours चतुःषष्टिसंख्यकाः — sixty-four in number रूपम् — form यस्याः सा — she whose (i) one whose form is the letters. (ii) the letters of the sanskrit alphabet are her forms. (iii) speech, mantra and sacred sound all arise as her manifestation. bhāskararāya concludes this complete block by revealing the foundational acoustic and visual substance of the śakti: the phonic and chromatic synthesis: वर्ण (varṇa) operates on two simultaneous non-dual tracks: it signifies the absolute matrix of the sanskrit phonic alphabet (mātṛkā) that constructs all spoken mantras, and it signifies the primary color ray frequencies that format the visible universe. she is varṇarūpiṇī because her entire manifest body is composed of this integrated grid of light and sound, ensuring that every word spoken and every ray seen is a literal cell of her cosmic form.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya concludes this complete block by revealing the foundational acoustic and visual substance of the Śakti:

The Phonic and Chromatic Synthesis: वर्ण (Varṇa) operates on two simultaneous non-dual tracks: it signifies the absolute matrix of the Sanskrit phonic alphabet (Mātṛkā) that constructs all spoken mantras, and it signifies the primary color ray frequencies that format the visible universe. She is Varṇarūpiṇī because Her entire manifest body is composed of this integrated grid of light and sound, ensuring that every word spoken and every ray seen is a literal cell of Her cosmic form.

name: जन्ममृत्युजरातप्तजनविश्रान्तिदायिनी
samasa: जन्ममृत्युजराभिः तप्तेभ्यो जनेभ्यो विश्रान्तिभिः विश्रान्तिं दत्ते इति।
summary: (i) One who gives rest to those scorched by birth, death and old age.
(ii) Human life is afflicted by repeated birth, decay and death.
(iii) She grants repose, relief and final peace to those tormented by this cycle.
searchText: 851 जन्ममृत्युजरातप्तजनविश्रान्तिदायिनी जन्ममृत्युजराभिः तप्तेभ्यो जनेभ्यो विश्रान्तिभिः विश्रान्तिं दत्ते इति। जन्म — birth मृत्यु — death जरा — old age तप्तेभ्यः — to those afflicted जनेभ्यः — to people विश्रान्तिम् — rest; repose दत्ते — gives (i) one who gives rest to those scorched by birth, death and old age. (ii) human life is afflicted by repeated birth, decay and death. (iii) she grants repose, relief and final peace to those tormented by this cycle. in the saubhāgyabhāskara, bhāskararāya defines her role as the ultimate alchemical sanctuary: the oasis of saṁsāra: worldly existence is characterized by a three-fold chronic friction—the trauma of birth, the anxiety of decay, and the terror of physical termination. living beings are continuously scorched (tapta) by this existential heat. she does not offer temporary worldly distractions; she acts as viśrāntidāyinī — the deep, cooling sanctuary of non-dual consciousness where the exhausted soul can dissolve its burdens and rest permanently.
esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara, Bhāskararāya defines Her role as the ultimate alchemical sanctuary:

The Oasis of Saṁsāra: Worldly existence is characterized by a three-fold chronic friction—the trauma of birth, the anxiety of decay, and the terror of physical termination. Living beings are continuously scorched (tapta) by this existential heat. She does not offer temporary worldly distractions; She acts as Viśrāntidāyinī — the deep, cooling sanctuary of non-dual consciousness where the exhausted soul can dissolve its burdens and rest permanently.

name: सर्वोपनिषदुद्घुष्टा
summary: (i) One who is loudly proclaimed by all Upanishads.
(ii) The secret teaching of the Upanishads points to Her supreme nature.
(iii) She is the Reality established by the highest Vedantic revelation.
searchText: 852 सर्वोपनिषदुद्घुष्टा सर्वास्वैतरेयादिषूपनिषत्सु रहस्यभूतासु उत्कर्षेण धुष्टा प्रतिपाद्या। सर्वासु — in all उपनिषत्सु — in the upanishads रहस्यभूतासु — being secret or esoteric उत्कर्षेण — excellently; emphatically प्रतिपाद्या — taught; expounded (i) one who is loudly proclaimed by all upanishads. (ii) the secret teaching of the upanishads points to her supreme nature. (iii) she is the reality established by the highest vedantic revelation. bhāskararāya anchors her identity as the singular target of vedic literature: the shared voice of the secrets: the upaniṣads (like the aitareya, taittirīya, or bṛhadāraṇyaka) are the highly guarded, secret texts concluding the vedas. bhāskararāya notes that she is not a localized, secondary goddess mentioned in passing. she is sarvopaniṣadudghuṣṭā because every single upaniṣadic dialogue, paradox, and mahāvākya is loudly proclaiming her essential nature as the non-dual absolute brahman.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya anchors Her identity as the singular target of Vedic literature:

The Shared Voice of the Secrets: The Upaniṣads (like the Aitareya, Taittirīya, or Bṛhadāraṇyaka) are the highly guarded, secret texts concluding the Vedas. Bhāskararāya notes that She is not a localized, secondary goddess mentioned in passing. She is Sarvopaniṣadudghuṣṭā because every single Upaniṣadic dialogue, paradox, and mahāvākya is loudly proclaiming Her essential nature as the non-dual absolute Brahman.

name: शान्त्यतीतकलात्मिका
samasa: आकाशनिष्ठा कला शान्त्यतीतेत्युच्यते। शान्तिम् अतीता। सा च असौ कला च। तदात्मिका तदभिन्ना।
summary: (i) One whose form is the kala beyond Shanti.
(ii) Shantyatita is the state beyond the Shanti-kala and is associated with the highest subtle principle.
(iii) She is identical with that transcendent kala.
searchText: 853 शान्त्यतीतकलात्मिका आकाशनिष्ठा कला शान्त्यतीतेत्युच्यते। शान्तिम् अतीता। सा च असौ कला च। तदात्मिका तदभिन्ना। शान्तिम् — peace or shanti-kala अतीता — beyond कला — kala; digit; spiritual power आत्मिका — whose nature is तदभिन्ना — not different from that (i) one whose form is the kala beyond shanti. (ii) shantyatita is the state beyond the shanti-kala and is associated with the highest subtle principle. (iii) she is identical with that transcendent kala. bhāskararāya unpacks the advanced layout of cosmic energy strata (kalās): the strata past cosmic peace: in śāiva-śākta cosmology, the creative manifestation is mapped across five sequential energetic zones or digits: nivṛtti, pratiṣṭhā, vidyā, śānti, and शान्त्यतीता (śāntyatītā — that which completely passes beyond peace). the śānti layer governs the ether element and cosmic stillness. she is śāntyatītakalātmikā because she embodies the absolute, highest fifth stratum, anchored directly in the unconditioned transcendent space beyond structural stillness.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya unpacks the advanced layout of cosmic energy strata (Kalās):

The Strata past Cosmic Peace: In Śāiva-Śākta cosmology, the creative manifestation is mapped across five sequential energetic zones or digits: Nivṛtti, Pratiṣṭhā, Vidyā, Śānti, and शान्त्यतीता (Śāntyatītā — that which completely passes beyond peace). The Śānti layer governs the ether element and cosmic stillness. She is Śāntyatītakalātmikā because She embodies the absolute, highest fifth stratum, anchored directly in the unconditioned transcendent space beyond structural stillness.

name: गम्भीरा
wordByWord: महाह्रदस्वरूपा — of the nature of a great lake
summary: (i) One who is profound.
(ii) Her depth is compared to an immeasurable great lake.
(iii) Because She is infinite, Her nature cannot be sounded by ordinary thought.
searchText: 854 गम्भीरा आनन्त्याद्गम्भीरा। महाह्रदस्वरूपेत्यर्थः आनन्त्यात् — because of infinitude गम्भीरा — deep; profound महाह्रदस्वरूपा — of the nature of a great lake (i) one who is profound. (ii) her depth is compared to an immeasurable great lake. (iii) because she is infinite, her nature cannot be sounded by ordinary thought. in the saubhāgyabhāskara, bhāskararāya details the unfathomable depth of her field: the great cosmic lake: she is gambhīrā because her consciousness field possesses absolute, shoreless infinity (ānantya). she resembles a massive, completely still, and bottomless deep lake (mahā-hrada). worldly thoughts and elements ripple only on the very surface; her depths remain unperturbed, solid, and silent, holding the universe in equilibrium.
esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara, Bhāskararāya details the unfathomable depth of Her field:

The Great Cosmic Lake: She is Gambhīrā because Her consciousness field possesses absolute, shoreless infinity (ānantya). She resembles a massive, completely still, and bottomless deep lake (mahā-hrada). Worldly thoughts and elements ripple only on the very surface; Her depths remain unperturbed, solid, and silent, holding the universe in equilibrium.

name: गगनान्तस्था
samasa: गगनस्य दहराकाशस्य भूताकाशस्य पराकाशस्य वान्तर्मध्ये तिष्ठतीति।
wordByWord: दहराकाशस्य — of the inner subtle space
summary: (i) One who abides at the end of space.
(ii) She exists within and beyond the spaces called daharakasha, bhutakasha and parakasha.
(iii) Even at the dissolution of space She remains.
searchText: 855 गगनान्तस्था गगनस्य दहराकाशस्य भूताकाशस्य पराकाशस्य वान्तर्मध्ये तिष्ठतीति। गगनस्यान्ते नाशकालेऽपि तिष्ठतीति। गगनस्य — of space दहराकाशस्य — of the inner subtle space भूताकाशस्य — of physical space पराकाशस्य — of supreme space अन्ते — at the end तिष्ठति — abides (i) one who abides at the end of space. (ii) she exists within and beyond the spaces called daharakasha, bhutakasha and parakasha. (iii) even at the dissolution of space she remains. bhāskararāya explores the spatial and temporal thresholds of this name: the space inside space: she positions herself inside (antara) every tier of space—the physical sky (bhūtākāśa), the internal heart sky (daharākāśa), and the supreme space of consciousness (parākāśa). the survivor of dissolution: alternatively, anta signifies termination. when the cosmic sky collapses and dissolves at the end of time, she continues to stand (sthā) past the destruction point, showing her absolute independence.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya explores the spatial and temporal thresholds of this name:
The Space inside Space: She positions Herself inside (antara) every tier of space—the physical sky (bhūtākāśa), the internal heart sky (daharākāśa), and the supreme space of consciousness (parākāśa).

The Survivor of Dissolution: Alternatively, anta signifies termination. When the cosmic sky collapses and dissolves at the end of time, She continues to stand (sthā) past the destruction point, showing Her absolute independence.

name: गर्विता
samasa: गर्वो विश्वनिर्माणविषयिणी पराहन्ता सास्याः सञ्जाता इति।
wordByWord: विश्वनिर्माणविषयिणी — relating to creation of the universe
summary: (i) One who possesses the supreme pride of creation.
(ii) This is not ordinary egoistic pride but the cosmic ahamta by which the universe is manifested.
(iii) She stands as the sovereign power behind creation.
searchText: 856 गर्विता गर्वो विश्वनिर्माणविषयिणी पराहन्ता सास्याः सञ्जाता इति। गर्वः — pride; majesty; self-assertion विश्वनिर्माणविषयिणी — relating to creation of the universe पराहन्ता — supreme i-consciousness सञ्जाता — arisen (i) one who possesses the supreme pride of creation. (ii) this is not ordinary egoistic pride but the cosmic ahamta by which the universe is manifested. (iii) she stands as the sovereign power behind creation. bhāskararāya purges this of mundane human psychological flaws to reveal high non-dual metaphysics: the absolute pride of creation: her "pride" (garva) is the magnificent cosmic consciousness of her own independent existence—the uncorrupted sense of पराहन्ता (parāhantā — the absolute cosmic "i"). it is the ecstatic awareness: "i am the entire universe, and i alone project it." this majestic self-recognition initiates the creation process.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya purges this of mundane human psychological flaws to reveal high non-dual metaphysics:

The Absolute Pride of Creation: Her "pride" (garva) is the magnificent cosmic consciousness of Her own independent existence—the uncorrupted sense of पराहन्ता (Parāhantā — the absolute cosmic "I"). It is the ecstatic awareness: "I am the entire universe, and I alone project it." This majestic self-recognition initiates the creation process.

name: गानलोलुपा
samasa: गानं ततानद्धसुषिरघनचतुष्टयसमुच्चयात्मकम्, वादित्रादिकं वा, शरीरं गान्धर्वं वा, साम वा तयोर्लोलुपा सतृष्णा।
summary: (i) One who is fond of music.
(ii) Music includes vocal song, instruments, the Gandharva art and the Saman.
(iii) She delights in sacred music and is pleased by musical worship.
searchText: 857 गानलोलुपा गानं ततानद्धसुषिरघनचतुष्टयसमुच्चयात्मकम्, वादित्रादिकं वा, शरीरं गान्धर्वं वा, साम वा तयोर्लोलुपा सतृष्णा। गानम् — music or singing वादित्रादिकम् — instruments and the like गान्धर्वम् — gandharva music साम — saman chant लोलुपा — fond of; eager for (i) one who is fond of music. (ii) music includes vocal song, instruments, the gandharva art and the saman. (iii) she delights in sacred music and is pleased by musical worship. in the saubhāgyabhāskara, bhāskararāya maps her relationship with harmonic sound structures: the affection for rhythmic order: गान (gāna) encompasses the four classical structural classes of musical instruments (stringed, percussion, wind, and solid bronze), the celestial gandharva arts, and the sacred melodies of the sāma veda. she is gānalolupā because these perfect acoustic structures are reflections of her own inner harmonic law, absorbing her attention into their beauty.
esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara, Bhāskararāya maps Her relationship with harmonic sound structures:

The Affection for Rhythmic Order: गान (Gāna) encompasses the four classical structural classes of musical instruments (stringed, percussion, wind, and solid bronze), the celestial Gandharva arts, and the sacred melodies of the Sāma Veda. She is Gānalolupā because these perfect acoustic structures are reflections of Her own inner harmonic law, absorbing Her attention into their beauty.

name: कल्पनारहिता
samasa: नरस्य इमे नाराः जीवाः। कल्पे अपि नाराणां हिताः।
summary: (i) One who is free from imagination or mental construction.
(ii) Kalpana is the flow of vasana-born images and notions.
(iii) Her supreme nature is beyond such mental projections.
searchText: 858 कल्पनारहिता कल्पना वासनामय्यो दृश्यवीचयस्ताभी रहिता। नरस्य इमे नाराः जीवाः। कल्पे अपि नाराणां हिताः। कल्पना — imagination; mental construction वासनामय्यः — made of vasanas दृश्यवीचयः — waves of perceived forms रहिता — free from (i) one who is free from imagination or mental construction. (ii) kalpana is the flow of vasana-born images and notions. (iii) her supreme nature is beyond such mental projections. bhāskararāya details the pristine stillness of the absolute baseline: the screen free from projections: कल्पना (kalpanā) refers to the subconscious ripples, latent impressions (vāsanās), and conceptual partitions that fragment pure consciousness into objects. she is kalpanārahitā because her essential nature is completely still and unconditioned. she is the baseline awareness screen that lacks any internal projections, remaining perfectly whole.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya details the pristine stillness of the absolute baseline:

The Screen Free from Projections: कल्पना (Kalpanā) refers to the subconscious ripples, latent impressions (vāsanās), and conceptual partitions that fragment pure consciousness into objects. She is Kalpanārahitā because Her essential nature is completely still and unconditioned. She is the baseline awareness screen that lacks any internal projections, remaining perfectly whole.

name: काष्ठा
samasa: अष्टादशनिमेषात्मकः कालः काष्ठा। दारुहरिद्रापि काष्ठा। तदुभयरूपा।
wordByWord: काष्ठा — measure of time; limit; culmination
वेदान्तवाक्यार्थतत्त्वनिष्कर्षः — final essence of Vedantic sentences
summary: (i) One who is the limit or culmination.
(ii) Kashtha may mean a measure of time, wood or turmeric, and also the final conclusion of Vedantic truth.
(iii) She is the ultimate limit to which scriptural enquiry leads.
searchText: 859 काष्ठा अष्टादशनिमेषात्मकः कालः काष्ठा। दारुहरिद्रापि काष्ठा। तदुभयरूपा। वेदान्तवाक्यार्थतत्त्वनिष्कर्षोऽपि काष्ठा। गगनात्मकस्य भीमनामकस्य परशिवस्य पत्नी। काष्ठा — measure of time; limit; culmination कालः — time दारुहरिद्रा — wood-turmeric वेदान्तवाक्यार्थतत्त्वनिष्कर्षः — final essence of vedantic sentences (i) one who is the limit or culmination. (ii) kashtha may mean a measure of time, wood or turmeric, and also the final conclusion of vedantic truth. (iii) she is the ultimate limit to which scriptural enquiry leads. bhāskararāya details the temporal and philosophical pinnacles contained in this word: the pinnacle of realization: kāṣṭhā denotes the absolute ceiling, ultimate limit, or final summary of a pursuit. she is the final conclusion (niṣkarṣa) targeted by all vedāntic statements. the pulse of time and form: she manifests as the precise micro-unit of temporal measurement (18 blinks of an eye), formats the cleansing properties of nature's herbs, and acts as the consort to shiva's expansive cosmic sky form (bhīma).
esoteric: Bhāskararāya details the temporal and philosophical pinnacles contained in this word:
The Pinnacle of Realization: Kāṣṭhā denotes the absolute ceiling, ultimate limit, or final summary of a pursuit. She is the final conclusion (niṣkarṣa) targeted by all Vedāntic statements.

The Pulse of Time and Form: She manifests as the precise micro-unit of temporal measurement (18 blinks of an eye), formats the cleansing properties of nature's herbs, and acts as the consort to Shiva's expansive cosmic sky form (Bhīma).

name: अकान्ता
samasa: अकं पापे च दुःखे च तयोरन्तो नाशो यया सा।
summary: (i) One who ends sin and sorrow.
(ii) The name may be read as She by whom the end of sin and suffering is brought about.
(iii) She removes the pain and impurities of devotees.
searchText: 860 अकान्ता अकं पापे च दुःखे च तयोरन्तो नाशो यया सा। अकम् — sin and sorrow पापे — in sin दुःखे — in sorrow अन्तः — end; destruction नाशः — destruction यया सा — she by whom (i) one who ends sin and sorrow. (ii) the name may be read as she by whom the end of sin and suffering is brought about. (iii) she removes the pain and impurities of devotees. in the saubhāgyabhāskara, bhāskararāya decodes the structural removal of distress: the solvent of karmic pain: अक (aka) is a classical lexicon code for the dual friction of moral error (pāpa) and its resulting existential pain (duḥkha). she is akāntā because her entry into the practitioner's consciousness acts as a powerful solvent that systematically brings this entire duplicate channel of suffering to an absolute end (anta).
esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara, Bhāskararāya decodes the structural removal of distress:

The Solvent of Karmic Pain: अक (aka) is a classical lexicon code for the dual friction of moral error (pāpa) and its resulting existential pain (duḥkha). She is Akāntā because Her entry into the practitioner's consciousness acts as a powerful solvent that systematically brings this entire duplicate channel of suffering to an absolute end (anta).

name: कान्तार्धविग्रहा
samasa: कान्तस्यार्धं कान्तार्धम्, कान्तार्धं विग्रहो यस्याः सा।
ककारस्यान्तः कान्तः खकारस्तेन द्यौर्लक्ष्यते। अर्धशब्दो भागमात्रपरः। तेन द्यौः शरीरैकदेशो यस्याः सा।
wordByWord: कान्तार्धविग्रहा — she whose body is half of Her beloved
summary: (i) One whose body is half of Her beloved.
(ii) Her beloved is Parashiva, and She shares His form.
(iii) The name indicates the non-separation of Shiva and Shakti.
searchText: 861 कान्तार्धविग्रहा कान्तः परशिव एवार्धविग्रहो यस्याः सा। कान्तस्यार्धं कान्तार्धम्, कान्तार्धं विग्रहो यस्याः सा। ककारस्यान्तः कान्तः खकारस्तेन द्यौर्लक्ष्यते। अर्धशब्दो भागमात्रपरः। तेन द्यौः शरीरैकदेशो यस्याः सा। कान्तः — beloved; parashiva अर्ध — half विग्रहः — body; form कान्तार्धविग्रहा — she whose body is half of her beloved (i) one whose body is half of her beloved. (ii) her beloved is parashiva, and she shares his form. (iii) the name indicates the non-separation of shiva and shakti. bhāskararāya decodes the ultimate structural integration of consciousness and energy: the sovereign half: this name highlights the iconic अर्धनारीश्वर (ardhanārīśvara) manifestation where shiva and shakti occupy a single structural body. she forms the left half of the display. bhāskararāya notes a high-tier grammatical track: कान्त also means the letter kha, which is a code for the infinite sky (dyauḥ), proving that her body incorporates the spatial heavens.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya decodes the ultimate structural integration of consciousness and energy:

The Sovereign Half: This name highlights the iconic अर्धनारीश्वर (Ardhanārīśvara) manifestation where Shiva and Shakti occupy a single structural body. She forms the left half of the display. Bhāskararāya notes a high-tier grammatical track: कान्त also means the letter Kha, which is a code for the infinite sky (Dyauḥ), proving that Her body incorporates the spatial heavens.

name: कार्यकारणनिर्मुक्ता
summary: (i) One who is free from cause and effect.
(ii) Effects such as mahat and causes such as mulaprakriti do not bind Her.
(iii) She transcends the entire chain of causation.
searchText: 862 कार्यकारणनिर्मुक्ता कार्याणि महत्त्वादीनि कारणं मूलप्रकृतिः तैर्निर्मुक्ता। कार्याणि — effects महत्त्वादीनि — mahat and the rest कारणम् — cause मूलप्रकृतिः — primal nature निर्मुक्ता — free from (i) one who is free from cause and effect. (ii) effects such as mahat and causes such as mulaprakriti do not bind her. (iii) she transcends the entire chain of causation. in the saubhāgyabhāskara, bhāskararāya details her position past evolutionary loops: beyond causal dependency: in material transformation (sāṅkhya cosmology), the universe shifts from the unmanifested cause (mūlaprakṛti) down to the manifested effects (mahat, elements). she is kāryakāraṇanirmuktā because her essential unconditioned reality as parabrahman sits entirely outside this wheel of dependency. she remains unaffected by changes, causes, or historical outcomes.
esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara, Bhāskararāya details Her position past evolutionary loops:

Beyond Causal Dependency: In material transformation (Sāṅkhya cosmology), the universe shifts from the unmanifested cause (Mūlaprakṛti) down to the manifested effects (Mahat, elements). She is Kāryakāraṇanirmuktā because Her essential unconditioned reality as Parabrahman sits entirely outside this wheel of dependency. She remains unaffected by changes, causes, or historical outcomes.

name: कामकेलितरङ्गिता
samasa: कामस्य कामेश्वरस्य केलीनां क्रीडाविलासानां तरङ्गाः परम्पराः सञ्जाता अस्याः इति।
summary: (i) One who is rippled by the play of Kameshwara.
(ii) The sport of Kameshwara is expressed as waves of divine play.
(iii) She is moved by that current of love and creative delight.
searchText: 863 कामकेलितरङ्गिता कामस्य कामेश्वरस्य केलीनां क्रीडाविलासानां तरङ्गाः परम्पराः सञ्जाता अस्याः इति। कामस्य — of kama or kameshwara केलीनाम् — of sports or plays क्रीडाविलासानाम् — of playful enjoyments तरङ्गाः — waves परम्पराः — series; succession (i) one who is rippled by the play of kameshwara. (ii) the sport of kameshwara is expressed as waves of divine play.
