(iii) she is moved by that current of love and creative delight. bhāskararāya details the continuous surge of cosmic generation: the waves of absolute delight: कामकेलि refers to the unceasing, ecstatic creative interactions between her dynamic energy and lord kāmeśvara (pure consciousness). this interaction is not a static event; it surges in continuous, interwoven waves (taraṅga) that manifest as the unfolding patterns of universes, filling reality with dynamic joy.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya details the continuous surge of cosmic generation:

The Waves of Absolute Delight: कामकेलि refers to the unceasing, ecstatic creative interactions between Her dynamic energy and Lord Kāmeśvara (pure consciousness). This interaction is not a static event; it surges in continuous, interwoven waves (taraṅga) that manifest as the unfolding patterns of universes, filling reality with dynamic joy.

name: कनत्कनकताटङ्का
samasa: कनती दीप्यमाने कनकस्य सुवर्णमये ताटङ्के कर्णाभरणविशेषौ यस्याः सा।
summary: (i) One who wears shining golden Tatankas.
(ii) Her earrings are made of bright gold and shine beautifully.
(iii) The name continues the description of Her auspicious ornamented form.
searchText: 864 कनत्कनकताटङ्का कनती दीप्यमाने कनकस्य सुवर्णमये ताटङ्के कर्णाभरणविशेषौ यस्याः सा। कनत् — shining कनक — gold ताटङ्के — large earrings कर्णाभरणविशेषौ — special ear ornaments यस्याः सा — she whose (i) one who wears shining golden tatankas. (ii) her earrings are made of bright gold and shine beautifully. (iii) the name continues the description of her auspicious ornamented form. in the saubhāgyabhāskara, bhāskararāya decodes the stellar and solar geometry of her ears: the orbs of sun and moon: her circular ear-ornaments (tāṭaṅka) are not mundane jewelry; they are the literal visual manifestations of the सूर्य (sun) and चन्द्र (moon) orbs spinning at the sides of her countenance. their continuous, radiant golden vibration (kanat) metrics illuminate and maintain the boundaries of time across the macrocosmic grid.
esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara, Bhāskararāya decodes the stellar and solar geometry of Her ears:

The Orbs of Sun and Moon: Her circular ear-ornaments (tāṭaṅka) are not mundane jewelry; they are the literal visual manifestations of the सूर्य (Sun) and चन्द्र (Moon) orbs spinning at the sides of Her countenance. Their continuous, radiant golden vibration (kanat) metrics illuminate and maintain the boundaries of time across the macrocosmic grid.

name: लीलाविग्रहधारिणी
summary: (i) One who assumes forms as divine play.
(ii) She takes incarnations and special forms effortlessly, without compulsion.
(iii) Her manifestations are lilas, acts of spontaneous grace.
searchText: 865 लीलाविग्रहधारिणी लीलयाऽनायासेन विग्रहानवतारविशेषान्धारयतीति। पद्मराजस्य महिषी लीलादेवी तद्विग्रहधारिणीति। लीलया — playfully; effortlessly अनायासेन — without effort विग्रहान् — forms; embodiments अवतारविशेषान् — special incarnations धारयति — assumes; bears (i) one who assumes forms as divine play. (ii) she takes incarnations and special forms effortlessly, without compulsion. (iii) her manifestations are lilas, acts of spontaneous grace. bhāskararāya removes any concepts of cosmic friction or labor during incarnation: effortless morphological descent: when cosmic balance requires an anthropomorphic descent (avatāra), she does not experience structural constraint or karmic binding. it is a spontaneous, completely effortless expression of her playful energy (līlā). she assumes physical geometry as easily as a actor changes garments, maintaining her complete transcendent purity underneath.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya removes any concepts of cosmic friction or labor during incarnation:

Effortless Morphological Descent: When cosmic balance requires an anthropomorphic descent (Avatāra), She does not experience structural constraint or karmic binding. It is a spontaneous, completely effortless expression of Her playful energy (līlā). She assumes physical geometry as easily as a actor changes garments, maintaining Her complete transcendent purity underneath.

name: अजा
summary: (i) One who is unborn.
(ii) Aja means that She is never born.
(iii) Though She manifests in many forms, in truth She is beyond birth.
searchText: 866 अजा न जायते इति अजा। अजा — unborn न जायते — is not born (i) one who is unborn. (ii) aja means that she is never born. (iii) though she manifests in many forms, in truth she is beyond birth. in the saubhāgyabhāskara, bhāskararāya anchors her past the rules of birth: the uncreated source: everything in the material world has a start date, an origin point, and a cause that brought it forth. she is ajā because she stands completely outside this birth framework. lacking a beginning, she is the eternal, uncreated source that brings forth creation while remaining unborn herself.
esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara, Bhāskararāya anchors Her past the rules of birth:

The Uncreated Source: Everything in the material world has a start date, an origin point, and a cause that brought it forth. She is Ajā because She stands completely outside this birth framework. Lacking a beginning, She is the eternal, uncreated source that brings forth creation while remaining unborn Herself.

name: क्षयविनिर्मुक्ता
samasa: क्षये गृह एव विशिष्य निर्मुक्ता यया सा।
summary: (i) One who is free from decay and destruction.
(ii) Kshaya means wasting, death or loss.
(iii) She is untouched by decay and remains ever complete.
searchText: 867 क्षयविनिर्मुक्ता क्षयेण मरणेन विनिर्मुक्ता। क्षये गृह एव विशिष्य निर्मुक्ता यया सा। क्षयेण — by decay or death मरणेन — by death विनिर्मुक्ता — completely free (i) one who is free from decay and destruction. (ii) kshaya means wasting, death or loss. (iii) she is untouched by decay and remains ever complete. bhāskararāya details her immunity to time-based reduction: the exhaustion-free baseline: physical elements wear out, stars exhaust their fuel, and biological systems undergo systemic decay (kṣaya). she is kṣayavinirmuktā because her unconditioned consciousness field is completely immune to reduction. she remains whole, unexhausted, and structurally stable across all transitions.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya details Her immunity to time-based reduction:

The Exhaustion-Free Baseline: Physical elements wear out, stars exhaust their fuel, and biological systems undergo systemic decay (kṣaya). She is Kṣayavinirmuktā because Her unconditioned consciousness field is completely immune to reduction. She remains whole, unexhausted, and structurally stable across all transitions.

name: मुग्धा/अमुग्धा
samasa: अकारप्रश्लेषेण न सन्ति मूढा यस्या इति अमुग्धा।
wordByWord: मुग्धा — one who releases; also innocent
summary: (i) One who is Mugdha or Amugdha.
(ii) Mugdha can indicate one who releases bondage, while Amugdha means not deluded.
(iii) She is free from dullness and removes the delusion of devotees.
searchText: 868 मुग्धा/अमुग्धा सङ्गं मोमोक्षतीति मुग्धा। अकारप्रश्लेषेण न सन्ति मूढा यस्या इति अमुग्धा। मुग्धा — one who releases; also innocent सङ्गम् — attachment मोमोक्षति — seeks to release अमुग्धा — not deluded मूढाः — deluded ones (i) one who is mugdha or amugdha. (ii) mugdha can indicate one who releases bondage, while amugdha means not deluded. (iii) she is free from dullness and removes the delusion of devotees. in the saubhāgyabhāskara, bhāskararāya reconciles her visual charm with her supreme awareness: artless grace and direct clarity: as mugdhā, she displays a beautiful, pristine, and artless grace that captivates the cosmos. when read as amugdhā, it confirms that she never experiences internal confusion or intellectual delusion (mūḍha-rahitā), standing secure in her absolute clarity.
esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara, Bhāskararāya reconciles Her visual charm with Her supreme awareness:

Artless Grace and Direct Clarity: As Mugdhā, She displays a beautiful, pristine, and artless grace that captivates the cosmos. When read as Amugdhā, it confirms that She never experiences internal confusion or intellectual delusion (mūḍha-rahitā), standing secure in Her absolute clarity.

name: क्षिप्रप्रसादिनी
summary: (i) One who is quickly pleased.
(ii) She grants grace in a short time to sincere worshippers.
(iii) Her compassion is immediate when worship is offered with devotion.
searchText: 869 क्षिप्रप्रसादिनी क्षिप्रं स्वल्पदिनैरेव प्रसीदतीति। क्षिप्रम् — quickly स्वल्पदिनैः — in a short time प्रसीदति — becomes pleased प्रसादिनी — gracious one (i) one who is quickly pleased. (ii) she grants grace in a short time to sincere worshippers. (iii) her compassion is immediate when worship is offered with devotion. bhāskararāya clarifies the rapid response mechanics of her system: the high-velocity response: some spiritual paths require lifetimes of complex mechanical loops before a signal of grace is received. she is k kṣipraprasādinī because if the practitioner's surrender is authentic and their internal alignment is clean, her response is immediate. she does not delay, instantly opening her gate to elevate the seeker.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya clarifies the rapid response mechanics of Her system:

The High-Velocity Response: Some spiritual paths require lifetimes of complex mechanical loops before a signal of grace is received. She is K Kṣipraprasādinī because if the practitioner's surrender is authentic and their internal alignment is clean, Her response is immediate. She does not delay, instantly opening Her gate to elevate the seeker.

name: अन्तर्मुखसमाराध्या
samasa: अन्तःस्वात्मप्रणवं मुखं चित्तवृत्तिर्येषां ते। तैः सम्यगाराध्या।
summary: (i) One who is worshipped by those turned inward.
(ii) The inward-facing worshipper has the mind directed to the inner Self and pranava.
(iii) She is rightly worshipped through inward contemplation.
searchText: 870 अन्तर्मुखसमाराध्या अन्तःस्वात्मप्रणवं मुखं चित्तवृत्तिर्येषां ते। तैः सम्यगाराध्या। अन्तः — within स्वात्मप्रणवम् — inner self and pranava मुखम् — face; direction चित्तवृत्तिः — mental mode सम्यक् आराध्या — properly worshipped (i) one who is worshipped by those turned inward. (ii) the inward-facing worshipper has the mind directed to the inner self and pranava. (iii) she is rightly worshipped through inward contemplation. in the saubhāgyabhāskara, bhāskararāya concludes this sub-sequence by defining the directional orientation of practice: the internal alignment: antarmukha describes that condition where the active mental modifications (citta-vṛtti) have ceased chasing external objects, turning 180 degrees backward to track the internal self. she is samārādhyā within this specific field. she cannot be captured by outward displays, opening her presence strictly to those who explore the inner silence.
esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara, Bhāskararāya concludes this sub-sequence by defining the directional orientation of practice:

The Internal Alignment: Antarmukha describes that condition where the active mental modifications (citta-vṛtti) have ceased chasing external objects, turning 180 degrees backward to track the internal Self. She is Samārādhyā within this specific field. She cannot be captured by outward displays, opening Her presence strictly to those who explore the inner silence.

name: बहिर्मुखसुदुर्लभा
samasa: बहिर्विषयैकप्रवणं मुखं येषां ते। तेषां सुष्ठु दुर्लभा।
summary: (i) One who is very difficult for the outward-facing.
(ii) Those whose minds run only after external objects do not easily attain Her.
(iii) She is found by inwardness, not by outward distraction.
searchText: 871 बहिर्मुखसुदुर्लभा बहिर्विषयैकप्रवणं मुखं येषां ते। तेषां सुष्ठु दुर्लभा। बहिः — outward विषयैकप्रवणम् — inclined only to objects मुखम् — face; orientation सुदुर्लभा — very difficult to attain (i) one who is very difficult for the outward-facing. (ii) those whose minds run only after external objects do not easily attain her. (iii) she is found by inwardness, not by outward distraction. bhāskararāya details the structural barrier confronting superficial tracking: the locked gate of externalism: when an observer's intellect remains completely driven by external objects, sensory desires, and material differences (bahir-mukha), they are moving away from the source. she is sudurlabhā to this state of mind. no amount of aggressive external calculation can uncover her, as long as the internal coordinates remain neglected.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya details the structural barrier confronting superficial tracking:

The Locked Gate of Externalism: When an observer's intellect remains completely driven by external objects, sensory desires, and material differences (bahir-mukha), they are moving away from the source. She is Sudurlabhā to this state of mind. No amount of aggressive external calculation can uncover Her, as long as the internal coordinates remain neglected.

name: त्रयी
summary: (i) One who is the three Vedas.
(ii) Trayi refers to the Vedic triad.
(iii) She is the very form of Vedic revelation.
searchText: 872 त्रयी वेदत्रयरूपा। त्रयी — the three vedas वेदत्रय — the triad of vedas रूपा — whose form is (i) one who is the three vedas. (ii) trayi refers to the vedic triad. (iii) she is the very form of vedic revelation. in the saubhāgyabhāskara, bhāskararāya anchors her identity with the foundational sound engine of tradition: the soul of the three vedas: she is the living phonic embodiment of the त्रयी (trayī — the corporate triad of the ṛg, yajur, and sāma vedas). she is not a post-vedic addition; she is the code, the metric grid, and the dynamic resonance running through these primary revelations, formatting the laws of nature.
esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara, Bhāskararāya anchors Her identity with the foundational sound engine of tradition:

The Soul of the Three Vedas: She is the living phonic embodiment of the त्रयी (Trayī — the corporate triad of the Ṛg, Yajur, and Sāma Vedas). She is not a post-Vedic addition; She is the code, the metric grid, and the dynamic resonance running through these primary revelations, formatting the laws of nature.

name: त्रिवर्गनिलया
samasa: त्रिवर्गो धर्मकामार्थैरिति कोशः। तस्य निलयः स्थानं यस्यां सा।
summary: (i) The abode of the three aims.
(ii) Trivarga means dharma, artha and kama.
(iii) These aims of life rest in Her and are fulfilled through Her grace.
searchText: 873 त्रिवर्गनिलया त्रिवर्गो धर्मकामार्थैरिति कोशः। तस्य निलयः स्थानं यस्यां सा। त्रिवर्गः — dharma, artha and kama धर्म — righteousness काम — desire अर्थ — wealth or purpose निलयः — abode (i) the abode of the three aims. (ii) trivarga means dharma, artha and kama. (iii) these aims of life rest in her and are fulfilled through her grace. bhāskararāya details her position as the container of all human pursuits: the sanctuary of balanced aspiration: she does not simply stand as a distant destination for ascetics. she is trivarganilayā because she acts as the absolute home and organizing source within which धर्म (virtue), अर्थ (stability), and काम (desire) find their proper coordinates, keeping human life in equilibrium.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya details Her position as the container of all human pursuits:

The Sanctuary of Balanced Aspiration: She does not simply stand as a distant destination for ascetics. She is Trivarganilayā because She acts as the absolute home and organizing source within which धर्म (virtue), अर्थ (stability), and काम (desire) find their proper coordinates, keeping human life in equilibrium.

name: त्रिस्था
samasa: त्रिषु भूतादिकालेष्वकारोकारमकारेषु वा स्था स्थितिर्यस्याः सा।
wordByWord: अकारोकारमकारेषु — in A, U and M
लोकादिषु — in worlds and the like
summary: (i) One who abides in the three.
(ii) She stands in the three times, the three letters a-u-m, or the three worlds.
(iii) She pervades every threefold principle without separation.
searchText: 874 त्रिस्था त्रिषु भूतादिकालेष्वकारोकारमकारेषु वा स्था स्थितिर्यस्याः सा। त्रिषु लोकादिष्वभेदेन तिष्ठतीति। त्रिषु — in three भूतादिकालेषु — in the three times अकारोकारमकारेषु — in a, u and m स्थितिः — abiding लोकादिषु — in worlds and the like (i) one who abides in the three. (ii) she stands in the three times, the three letters a-u-m, or the three worlds. (iii) she pervades every threefold principle without separation. in the saubhāgyabhāskara, bhāskararāya unpacks the triadic integration of this name: the pervader of the triple states: she stands anchored uniformly across the three tenses of time (past, present, future), lives inside the three phonemes of the praṇava (अ, उ, म), and permeates the three states of human consciousness (jāgrat, svapna, suṣupti) without structural cleavage.
esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara, Bhāskararāya unpacks the triadic integration of this name:

The Pervader of the Triple States: She stands anchored uniformly across the three tenses of time (past, present, future), lives inside the three phonemes of the Praṇava (अ, उ, म), and permeates the three states of human consciousness (jāgrat, svapna, suṣupti) without structural cleavage.

name: त्रिपुरमालिनी
samasa: त्रीणि पुराणि दलावृत्तयोयस्याः सा। सा चासौ मालिनी च।
wordByWord: त्रिपुरमालिनी — Malini of the three cities
summary: (i) Tripuramalini.
(ii) She is related to the three cities or the three enclosures and is also Malini.
(iii) The name belongs to the Sri Vidya tradition and points to Her as the garlanded power of Tripura.
searchText: 875 त्रिपुरमालिनी त्रीणि पुराणि दलावृत्तयोयस्याः सा। सा चासौ मालिनी च। त्रीणि — three पुराणि — cities or enclosures मालिनी — garlanded one; malini त्रिपुरमालिनी — malini of the three cities (i) tripuramalini. (ii) she is related to the three cities or the three enclosures and is also malini. (iii) the name belongs to the sri vidya tradition and points to her as the garlanded power of tripura. bhāskararāya details the geometric and structural mapping of the śrī cakra: the ruler of the triadic garland: in the layout of the śrī yantra, tripuramālinī is the specific presiding goddess placed in absolute charge of the sixth major enclosure—the अन्तरदशार चक्र (antardaśāra cakra — the inner circle of ten radiant triangles). she represents that exact protective frequency, wearing the triadic garland of cosmic letters.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya details the geometric and structural mapping of the Śrī Cakra:

The Ruler of the Triadic Garland: In the layout of the Śrī Yantra, Tripuramālinī is the specific presiding goddess placed in absolute charge of the sixth major enclosure—the अन्तरदशार चक्र (Antardaśāra Cakra — the inner circle of ten radiant triangles). She represents that exact protective frequency, wearing the triadic garland of cosmic letters.

name: निरामया
summary: (i) One who is free from disease.
(ii) Amaya means disease or affliction.
(iii) She is free from every defect and removes the diseases of worldly life.
searchText: 876 निरामया निर्गता आमया रोगा यया सा। निर्गता — gone; removed आमयाः — diseases रोगाः — illnesses निरामया — free from disease (i) one who is free from disease. (ii) amaya means disease or affliction. (iii) she is free from every defect and removes the diseases of worldly life. bhāskararāya details her absolute structural health: the distortion-free screen: आमय (amaya) encompasses all physical biological illnesses, mental anxieties, and the ultimate sickness of spiritual confusion. she is niramayā because her essential unconditioned reality contains no internal cleavages or wear. she acts as a perfectly healthy screen that remains unaffected by the distortions projected upon it.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya details Her absolute structural health:

The Distortion-Free Screen: आमय (Amaya) encompasses all physical biological illnesses, mental anxieties, and the ultimate sickness of spiritual confusion. She is Niramayā because Her essential unconditioned reality contains no internal cleavages or wear. She acts as a perfectly healthy screen that remains unaffected by the distortions projected upon it.

name: निरालम्बा
summary: (i) One who is without support.
(ii) Alambana is a prop or support.
(iii) She depends on nothing, while everything depends on Her.
searchText: 877 निरालम्बा निर्गतम् आलम्बनं यस्याः सा। निर्गतम् — gone; absent आलम्बनम् — support; prop निरालम्बा — one without support (i) one who is without support. (ii) alambana is a prop or support. (iii) she depends on nothing, while everything depends on her. in the saubhāgyabhāskara, bhāskararāya defines her absolute philosophical independence (svātantrya): the independent substratum: finite objects require an external base to support their weight. she is nirālambā because she stands completely independent. lacking any neighbor or foundation back behind her station, she is the primary, uncaused foundation that supports the entire weight of the universe.
esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara, Bhāskararāya defines Her absolute philosophical independence (Svātantrya):

The Independent Substratum: Finite objects require an external base to support their weight. She is Nirālambā because She stands completely independent. Lacking any neighbor or foundation back behind Her station, She is the primary, uncaused foundation that supports the entire weight of the universe.

name: स्वात्मारामा
samasa: स्वमात्मीयं जगच्च आत्मा ब्रह्म च अनयोरारामः क्रीडनं विहरणं वृत्तित्वं यस्याः सा।
summary: (i) One who rejoices in Her own Self.
(ii) She sports in the Self alone.
(iii) The universe and Brahman are also Her field of play, but Her joy is self-complete.
searchText: 878 स्वात्मारामा स्वात्मन्येवारामः क्रीडनं यस्याः सा। स्वमात्मीयं जगच्च आत्मा ब्रह्म च अनयोरारामः क्रीडनं विहरणं वृत्तित्वं यस्याः सा। स्वात्मनि — in her own self आरामः — delight; play क्रीडनम् — playing जगत् — universe ब्रह्म — brahman (i) one who rejoices in her own self. (ii) she sports in the self alone. (iii) the universe and brahman are also her field of play, but her joy is self-complete. bhāskararāya defines her complete freedom from external acquisition: the joy of self-absorption: human happiness is dependent on acquiring external objects, making it brief and fragile. she is svātmārāmā because she experiences absolute fulfillment entirely through her own intrinsic self-bliss (svarūpānanda). bhāskararāya notes an advanced track: she balances the relative world (svam) and the absolute brahman (ātmā) as waves of a single, non-dual play.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya defines Her complete freedom from external acquisition:

The Joy of Self-Absorption: Human happiness is dependent on acquiring external objects, making it brief and fragile. She is Svātmārāmā because She experiences absolute fulfillment entirely through Her own intrinsic self-bliss (Svarūpānanda). Bhāskararāya notes an advanced track: She balances the relative world (svam) and the absolute Brahman (ātmā) as waves of a single, non-dual play.

name: सुधासृतिः
samasa: सुधायाः सहस्रारकर्णिकाचन्द्रगतायाः स्रुतिः स्रवणं यया कुण्डलिन्या सा।
summary: (i) One from whom nectar flows.
(ii) Sudha is the nectar from the moon in the sahasrara.
(iii) As Kundalini, She causes that nectar to flow and grants bliss to the yogin.
searchText: 879 सुधासृतिः सुधायाः सहस्रारकर्णिकाचन्द्रगतायाः स्रुतिः स्रवणं यया कुण्डलिन्या सा। सुधायाः स्रुतिरेव। सुधायाः — of nectar सहस्रार — thousand-petalled lotus चन्द्रगतायाः — located in the moon स्रुतिः — flow स्रवणम् — oozing; flowing (i) one from whom nectar flows. (ii) sudha is the nectar from the moon in the sahasrara. (iii) as kundalini, she causes that nectar to flow and grants bliss to the yogin. in the saubhāgyabhāskara, bhāskararāya details the subtle internal anatomy of the crown downpour: the stream of immortality: when the internal kuṇḍalinī climbs past the lower centers, it triggers the lunar orb located inside the sahasrāra crown center. this trigger causes a massive downpour of cooling, alchemical nectar (sudhā). she is both the downpour itself and the active channel (srutiḥ) that distributes this essence throughout the practitioner's subtle channels, rejuvenating the entire system.
esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara, Bhāskararāya details the subtle internal anatomy of the crown downpour:

The Stream of Immortality: When the internal Kuṇḍalinī climbs past the lower centers, it triggers the lunar orb located inside the Sahasrāra crown center. This trigger causes a massive downpour of cooling, alchemical nectar (sudhā). She is both the downpour itself and the active channel (srutiḥ) that distributes this essence throughout the practitioner's subtle channels, rejuvenating the entire system.

name: संसारपङ्कनिर्मग्नसमुद्धरणपण्डिता
samasa: संसारलक्षणे कर्दमे निःशेषेण मग्नानां जनानां सम्यगुद्धरणे पण्डिता कुशला।
summary: (i) One skilled in lifting those sunk in the mire of samsara.
(ii) Samsara is like a muddy marsh in which beings are deeply trapped.
(iii) She expertly rescues devotees from that bondage.
searchText: 880 संसारपङ्कनिर्मग्नसमुद्धरणपण्डिता संसारलक्षणे कर्दमे निःशेषेण मग्नानां जनानां सम्यगुद्धरणे पण्डिता कुशला। संसार — worldly existence पङ्क — mire; mud निर्मग्नानाम् — of those deeply sunk समुद्धरणे — in lifting up पण्डिता — skilled; expert (i) one skilled in lifting those sunk in the mire of samsara. (ii) samsara is like a muddy marsh in which beings are deeply trapped. (iii) she expertly rescues devotees from that bondage. bhāskararāya details her tactical salvage capacity: the expert rescuer: worldly delusion behaves like thick, heavy quicksand or mud (paṅka)—the more an individual struggles using simple egoic calculations, the deeper they sink. she is paṇḍitā (expertly skilled) at this rescue operation. she knows the exact leverage points, subtle frequencies, and initiatory channels needed to hook the bound soul and pull it safely out of the mud, establishing her status as the ultimate savior.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya details Her tactical salvage capacity:

The Expert Rescuer: Worldly delusion behaves like thick, heavy quicksand or mud (paṅka)—the more an individual struggles using simple egoic calculations, the deeper they sink. She is Paṇḍitā (expertly skilled) at this rescue operation. She knows the exact leverage points, subtle frequencies, and initiatory channels needed to hook the bound soul and pull it safely out of the mud, establishing Her status as the ultimate savior.

name: यज्ञप्रिया
wordByWord: यज्ञप्रिया — She to whom sacrifices are dear
summary: (i) One who loves sacrifice.
(ii) Yajnas are dear to Her, and yajna is also identified with Vishnu in the Veda.
(iii) Sacred offering, rightly performed, is pleasing to Her.
searchText: 881 यज्ञप्रिया यज्ञाः प्रिया यस्याः सा। यज्ञो वै विष्णुरिति श्रुतेस्तत्प्रियेति। यज्ञाः — sacrifices प्रियाः — dear विष्णुः — vishnu यज्ञप्रिया — she to whom sacrifices are dear (i) one who loves sacrifice. (ii) yajnas are dear to her, and yajna is also identified with vishnu in the veda. (iii) sacred offering, rightly performed, is pleasing to her. in the saubhāgyabhāskara, bhāskararāya connects her presence to the alchemical fire loop: the affinity for dedicated rites: a sacrifice (yajña) is the structural mechanism through which humans surrender material assets to nourish cosmic forces. she is yajñapriyā because she values this dedicated loop of mutual alignment. concurrently, she matches the declaration “yajño vai viṣṇuḥ”, showing her affinity for the cosmic preservation force.
esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara, Bhāskararāya connects Her presence to the alchemical fire loop:

The Affinity for Dedicated Rites: A sacrifice (Yajña) is the structural mechanism through which humans surrender material assets to nourish cosmic forces. She is Yajñapriyā because She values this dedicated loop of mutual alignment. Concurrently, She matches the declaration “Yajño vai Viṣṇuḥ”, showing Her affinity for the cosmic preservation force.

name: यज्ञकर्त्री
samasa: यज्ञस्य कर्ता यजमानात्मको दीक्षितमूर्तिः परमशिवस्तस्य पत्नी।
wordByWord: यजमानात्मकः — in the form of the sacrificer
summary: (i) One connected with the performer of sacrifice.
(ii) The yajna-karta is the sacrificer, ultimately identified with Parashiva.
(iii) She is His Shakti and the power by which sacrifice is performed.
searchText: 882 यज्ञकर्त्री यज्ञस्य कर्ता यजमानात्मको दीक्षितमूर्तिः परमशिवस्तस्य पत्नी। यज्ञस्य — of sacrifice कर्ता — performer यजमानात्मकः — in the form of the sacrificer दीक्षितमूर्तिः — initiated form परमशिवः — parashiva पत्नी — consort (i) one connected with the performer of sacrifice. (ii) the yajna-karta is the sacrificer, ultimately identified with parashiva. (iii) she is his shakti and the power by which sacrifice is performed. bhāskararāya decodes the non-dual agency behind ritual action: the power behind the executor: lord śiva manifests as the archetypal consecrated sacrificer (dīkṣita-mūrti) who runs the macrocosmic sacrifice to maintain order. she is his non-dual dynamic energy (yajñakartrī). without her presence, the executor lacks the kinetic capacity to initiate, manage, or complete a single rite, making her the true inner engine of action.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya decodes the non-dual agency behind ritual action:

The Power behind the Executor: Lord Śiva manifests as the archetypal consecrated sacrificer (Dīkṣita-mūrti) who runs the macrocosmic sacrifice to maintain order. She is His non-dual dynamic energy (Yajñakartrī). Without Her presence, the executor lacks the kinetic capacity to initiate, manage, or complete a single rite, making Her the true inner engine of action.

name: यजमानस्वरूपिणी
samasa: यजमानश्च स्वश्च यजमानस्वौ दीक्षितात्मानौ तौ रूपे अस्या इति।
summary: (i) One whose form is the yajamana.
(ii) The sacrificer and the initiated self are Her own forms.
(iii) In sacrifice, the deity, act and worshipper are resolved in Her.
searchText: 883 यजमानस्वरूपिणी यजमानश्च स्वश्च यजमानस्वौ दीक्षितात्मानौ तौ रूपे अस्या इति। यजमानः — sacrificer स्वः — self; one's own दीक्षितात्मानौ — initiated selves रूपे — forms अस्याः — of her (i) one whose form is the yajamana. (ii) the sacrificer and the initiated self are her own forms. (iii) in sacrifice, the deity, act and worshipper are resolved in her. in the saubhāgyabhāskara, bhāskararāya constructs an immediate non-dual equation to remove ritual duality: identity with the conscious observer: lest a practitioner think that she remains only a distant deity waiting to receive an offering from a human performer, this name establishes she is the very person performing the rite (yajamāna). she is the conscious intention and the internal observer driving the sacrifice, proving that the giver, the gift, and the receiver are one substance.
esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara, Bhāskararāya constructs an immediate non-dual equation to remove ritual duality:

Identity with the Conscious Observer: Lest a practitioner think that She remains only a distant deity waiting to receive an offering from a human performer, this name establishes She is the very person performing the rite (Yajamāna). She is the conscious intention and the internal observer driving the sacrifice, proving that the giver, the gift, and the receiver are one substance.

name: धर्माधारा
summary: (i) The support of dharma.
(ii) Dharma flows everywhere because of Her.
(iii) She is both the foundation of righteousness and the power by which it is sustained.
searchText: 884 धर्माधारा धर्माणामासमन्तात्सर्वदेशेषु धारा निरर्गलप्रवाहः। धर्म आधारो यस्याः सा। धर्म आधारो यया सा। धर्माणाम् — of dharmas धारा — stream; flow आधारः — support यया — by whom धर्माधारा — support of dharma (i) the support of dharma. (ii) dharma flows everywhere because of her. (iii) she is both the foundation of righteousness and the power by which it is sustained. bhāskararāya details the physics of universal moral balance: the unbroken current of law: she acts as the unceasing, unblocked stream (dhārā) that distributes moral law and systemic justice across all coordinates of space. concurrently, she functions as the absolute foundation (ādhāra) that keeps dharma stable, ensuring that the laws of karma and cosmic cause-and-effect remain unyielding.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya details the physics of universal moral balance:

The Unbroken Current of Law: She acts as the unceasing, unblocked stream (dhārā) that distributes moral law and systemic justice across all coordinates of space. Concurrently, She functions as the absolute foundation (ādhāra) that keeps Dharma stable, ensuring that the laws of karma and cosmic cause-and-effect remain unyielding.

name: धनाध्यक्षा
summary: (i) The superintendent of wealth.
(ii) She is the mistress and controller of all wealth.
(iii) All forms of prosperity are under Her authority.
searchText: 885 धनाध्यक्षा धनस्याध्यक्षा स्वामिनी। धनस्य — of wealth अध्यक्षा — superintendent; overseer स्वामिनी — mistress; owner (i) the superintendent of wealth. (ii) she is the mistress and controller of all wealth. (iii) all forms of prosperity are under her authority. in the saubhāgyabhāskara, bhāskararāya establishes her financial and material priority: the overlord of the cosmic treasury: humans spend lifetimes tracking and accumulating physical assets. she is the absolute adhyakṣā (sovereign supervisor) over the complete universal repository of wealth. kubera and lakṣmī manage local departments under her executive decree, confirming her status as the ultimate owner of all resources.
esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara, Bhāskararāya establishes Her financial and material priority:

The Overlord of the Cosmic Treasury: Humans spend lifetimes tracking and accumulating physical assets. She is the absolute Adhyakṣā (sovereign supervisor) over the complete universal repository of wealth. Kubera and Lakṣmī manage local departments under Her executive decree, confirming Her status as the ultimate owner of all resources.

name: धनधान्यविवर्धिनी
summary: (i) One who increases wealth and grain.
(ii) Dhana means wealth and dhanya means grain or food-supply.
(iii) She causes prosperity, nourishment and material welfare to grow for Her devotees.
searchText: 886 धनधान्यविवर्धिनी धनानि धान्यानि च विशेष्य वर्धयतीति। धनानि — wealths धान्यानि — grains; food supplies विशेष्य — especially; distinctly वर्धयति — increases; causes to grow (i) one who increases wealth and grain. (ii) dhana means wealth and dhanya means grain or food-supply. (iii) she causes prosperity, nourishment and material welfare to grow for her devotees. bhāskararāya maps her supportive material responsiveness: the multiplier of sustenance: she does not ignore the material needs of her children. as vivardhinī, she actively multiplies their financial reserves (dhana) and foundational food nutrition (dhānya), realigning their external environment to ensure they do not suffer material panic while pursuing spiritual goals.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya maps Her supportive material responsiveness:

The Multiplier of Sustenance: She does not ignore the material needs of Her children. As Vivardhinī, She actively multiplies their financial reserves (dhana) and foundational food nutrition (dhānya), realigning their external environment to ensure they do not suffer material panic while pursuing spiritual goals.

name: विप्रप्रिया
summary: (i) One who is dear to learned Brahmins.
(ii) Vipra means a Brahmin, a learned person or one who knows sacred knowledge.
(iii) She is beloved of such knowers and worshippers of the Veda.
searchText: 887 विप्रप्रिया विप्राः प्रिया अभीष्टा यस्याः सा। विप्राः — learned brahmins; sacred knowers प्रियाः — dear; beloved अभीष्टाः — cherished; desired यस्याः सा — she whose (i) one who is dear to learned brahmins. (ii) vipra means a brahmin, a learned person or one who knows sacred knowledge. (iii) she is beloved of such knowers and worshippers of the veda. in the saubhāgyabhāskara, bhāskararāya removes any concepts of rigid social caste, defining the intellectual class: the affinity for the seekers of truth: विप्र (vipra) signifies those whose life is dedicated to the intense study, recitation, and contemplation of scriptural truth and the absolute brahman. she holds this specific class close to her grace because their internal efforts mirror her own radiant wisdom field.
esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara, Bhāskararāya removes any concepts of rigid social caste, defining the intellectual class:

The Affinity for the Seekers of Truth: विप्र (Vipra) signifies those whose life is dedicated to the intense study, recitation, and contemplation of scriptural truth and the absolute Brahman. She holds this specific class close to Her grace because their internal efforts mirror Her own radiant wisdom field.

name: विप्ररूपा
summary: (i) One whose form is the Vipras.
(ii) The learned ones are regarded as Her own form because sacred knowledge shines through them.
(iii) She also nourishes and restores the Vipras to their proper spiritual form.
searchText: 888 विप्ररूपा विप्राः रूपं स्वरूपं यस्याः सा। विप्रान् रूपयति पूर्वरूपवतः करोत्याप्याययतीति। विप्राः — learned brahmins रूपम् — form स्वरूपम् — own form यस्याः सा — she whose रूपयति — forms; restores आप्याययति — nourishes; strengthens (i) one whose form is the vipras. (ii) the learned ones are regarded as her own form because sacred knowledge shines through them. (iii) she also nourishes and restores the vipras to their proper spiritual form. bhāskararāya constructs an immediate non-dual refinement to close the loop of knowledge: identity with the wisdom seers: she is non-different from the very seers who contemplate her nature. she is the internal processing power and the light of the intellect (buddhi) that enables them to comprehend truth. alternatively, she acts as the force that continuously nourishes (āpyāyayati) and restores their energetic form.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya constructs an immediate non-dual refinement to close the loop of knowledge:

Identity with the Wisdom Seers: She is non-different from the very seers who contemplate Her nature. She is the internal processing power and the light of the intellect (buddhi) that enables them to comprehend truth. Alternatively, She acts as the force that continuously nourishes (āpyāyayati) and restores their energetic form.

name: विश्वभ्रमणकारिणी
samasa: विश्वेषां ब्रह्माण्डानां भ्रमणं सृष्टिस्थितिनाशरूपं यातायातं कारयति।
summary: (i) One who causes the movement of the universe.
(ii) The movement of countless worlds includes creation, maintenance and dissolution.
(iii) She is the power that makes the universe revolve through these cosmic processes.
searchText: 889 विश्वभ्रमणकारिणी विश्वेषां ब्रह्माण्डानां भ्रमणं सृष्टिस्थितिनाशरूपं यातायातं कारयति। विश्वस्य विष्णोः भ्रमणकारिणी। विश्वेषाम् — of the worlds ब्रह्माण्डानाम् — of the cosmic eggs भ्रमणम् — movement; revolution सृष्टि — creation स्थिति — maintenance नाश — dissolution कारयति — causes (i) one who causes the movement of the universe. (ii) the movement of countless worlds includes creation, maintenance and dissolution. (iii) she is the power that makes the universe revolve through these cosmic processes. in the saubhāgyabhāskara, bhāskararāya details the mechanics of cosmic kinetic motion: the engine of cosmic rotation: the multiverses (viśva) do not sit static; they spin in continuous cycles of generation, orbit, and reabsorption (bhramaṇa). she is the dynamic kinetic engine (kāriṇī) that drives this entire movement. concurrently, she coordinates the various shifts and incarnations assumed by the pervasive energy of viṣṇu.
esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara, Bhāskararāya details the mechanics of cosmic kinetic motion:

The Engine of Cosmic Rotation: The multiverses (viśva) do not sit static; they spin in continuous cycles of generation, orbit, and reabsorption (bhramaṇa). She is the dynamic kinetic engine (kāriṇī) that drives this entire movement. Concurrently, She coordinates the various shifts and incarnations assumed by the pervasive energy of Viṣṇu.

name: विश्वग्रासा
summary: (i) One who swallows the universe.
(ii) At dissolution, the moving and unmoving universe is withdrawn into Her.
(iii) She is the power of final absorption in whom all manifestation is consumed.
searchText: 890 विश्वग्रासा विश्वं चराचरं ग्रसतीति। विश्वम् — the universe चराचरम् — moving and unmoving ग्रसति — swallows; absorbs (i) one who swallows the universe. (ii) at dissolution, the moving and unmoving universe is withdrawn into her. (iii) she is the power of final absorption in whom all manifestation is consumed. bhāskararāya concludes this sub-sequence by defining the final rest of the cosmos: the total reabsorption: complementing her consuming titles, she is viśvagrāsā because during the grand dissolution, she pulls the entire manifest world back into her causal womb. nothing is left as an independent object outside her field; she digests the elements, space, and time, leaving her non-dual light to shine alone in the silence.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya concludes this sub-sequence by defining the final rest of the cosmos:

The Total Reabsorption: Complementing Her consuming titles, She is Viśvagrāsā because during the grand dissolution, She pulls the entire manifest world back into Her causal womb. Nothing is left as an independent object outside Her field; She digests the elements, space, and time, leaving Her non-dual light to shine alone in the silence.

name: विद्रुमाभा
summary: (i) One whose lustre is like coral.
(ii) Vidruma means coral, bright red in colour.
(iii) Her complexion is described as red and shining like coral.
searchText: 891 विद्रुमाभा विद्रूमाः प्रवालास्तद्वदारक्ता। विद्रुमाः — corals प्रवालाः — corals तद्वत् — like that आरक्ता — reddish (i) one whose lustre is like coral. (ii) vidruma means coral, bright red in colour. (iii) her complexion is described as red and shining like coral. bhāskararāya maps this stable color frequency within visual concentration: the unblemished crimson aura: unlike passing emotional states, her complexion here is described as the rich, steady, unblemished red hue of high-grade coral (vidruma). this specific frequency represents the absolute fusion of इच्छाशक्ति (divine will) and क्रियाशक्ति (divine action) flowing together to protect the practitioner's psychic field, dispelling mental lethargy.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya maps this stable color frequency within visual concentration:

The Unblemished Crimson Aura: Unlike passing emotional states, Her complexion here is described as the rich, steady, unblemished red hue of high-grade coral (vidruma). This specific frequency represents the absolute fusion of इच्छाशक्ति (Divine Will) and क्रियाशक्ति (Divine Action) flowing together to protect the practitioner's psychic field, dispelling mental lethargy.

name: वैष्णवी
summary: (i) One who belongs to Vishnu, or one who is Vaishnavi.
(ii) She is the Shakti associated with Vishnu and is not different from the divine preserving power.
(iii) This name points to Her identity with the Vaishnavi aspect.
searchText: 892 वैष्णवी विष्णोरियम्। विष्णोः — of vishnu इयम् — this; belonging to (i) one who belongs to vishnu, or one who is vaishnavi. (ii) she is the shakti associated with vishnu and is not different from the divine preserving power. (iii) this name points to her identity with the vaishnavi aspect. in the saubhāgyabhāskara, bhāskararāya details her presence within the matrix of the seven mothers (mātṛkās): the pervasive protective power: she is the dynamic, all-pervading kinetic capacity (śakti) that belongs to lord viṣṇu. in the agamic defensive framework, she manifests as vaiṣṇavī — the corporate goddess who commands the maintenance of order, stands ready with the discus weapon, and destroys unrighteous distortions.
esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara, Bhāskararāya details Her presence within the matrix of the seven mothers (Mātṛkās):

The Pervasive Protective Power: She is the dynamic, all-pervading kinetic capacity (Śakti) that belongs to Lord Viṣṇu. In the Agamic defensive framework, She manifests as Vaiṣṇavī — the corporate goddess who commands the maintenance of order, stands ready with the discus weapon, and destroys unrighteous distortions.

name: विष्णुरूपिणी
summary: (i) One whose form is Vishnu.
(ii) Vishnu himself is Her form, for the powers of preservation and pervasion are included in Her.
(iii) She is not separate from Vishnu's sustaining principle.
searchText: 893 विष्णुरूपिणी विष्णुरेव रूपमस्या अस्तीति। विष्णुः — vishnu एव — itself रूपम् — form अस्याः — of her अस्तीति — is (i) one whose form is vishnu. (ii) vishnu himself is her form, for the powers of preservation and pervasion are included in her. (iii) she is not separate from vishnu's sustaining principle. bhāskararāya constructs an immediate non-dual identity equation across traditional sectarian lines: identity with the preserver: lest a student think that she is a localized goddess who simply belongs to a higher male deity, this name establishes her body is the very person of lord viṣṇu (viṣṇu-svarūpa). the pervasive strength that supports the planets and maintains cosmic dharma is the literal expression of her form.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya constructs an immediate non-dual identity equation across traditional sectarian lines:

Identity with the Preserver: Lest a student think that She is a localized goddess who simply belongs to a higher male deity, this name establishes Her body is the very person of Lord Viṣṇu (Viṣṇu-svarūpa). The pervasive strength that supports the planets and maintains cosmic Dharma is the literal expression of Her form.

name: अयोनिः
summary: (i) One who has no womb or source.
(ii) Yoni means womb, source or cause.
(iii) She has no prior cause and is not born from anything else.
searchText: 894 अयोनिः अविद्यमाना योनिः कारणं यस्याः सा। अविद्यमाना — non-existing; absent योनिः — womb; source; cause कारणम् — cause यस्याः सा — she whose (i) one who has no womb or source. (ii) yoni means womb, source or cause. (iii) she has no prior cause and is not born from anything else. in the saubhāgyabhāskara, bhāskararāya defines her absolute priority: the uncaused primary cause: material objects are born from a specific source womb (yoni). she is ayoniḥ because she stands completely outside this dependency loop. having no mother, no creator, and no prior cause back behind her station, she exists as the absolute, self-existent root of reality.
esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara, Bhāskararāya defines Her absolute priority:

The Uncaused Primary Cause: Material objects are born from a specific source womb (yoni). She is Ayoniḥ because She stands completely outside this dependency loop. Having no mother, no creator, and no prior cause back behind Her station, She exists as the absolute, self-existent root of reality.

name: योनिनिलया
samasa: निलीयते जगद्यस्यामिति निलया। योनिश्चासौ निलया च।
योनीनां जगत्कारणानां ब्रह्मादीनां नितरां लयो यस्यां सा।
summary: (i) One who is the abode of the yoni.
(ii) The universe dissolves in Her as in its source.
(iii) Maya, the causes of the world and the deities such as Brahma finally rest in Her.
searchText: 895 योनिनिलया निलीयते जगद्यस्यामिति निलया। योनिश्चासौ निलया च। योनिर्मायैव निलयः परिच्छेदिका यस्याः सा। योनीनां जगत्कारणानां ब्रह्मादीनां नितरां लयो यस्यां सा। निलीयते — dissolves; is absorbed जगत् — the world यस्याम् — in whom योनिः — womb; source निलया — abode माया — maya लयः — dissolution (i) one who is the abode of the yoni. (ii) the universe dissolves in her as in its source. (iii) maya, the causes of the world and the deities such as brahma finally rest in her. bhāskararāya reconciles her as both the womb and the terminal of existence: the matrix and the terminal: she is the primordial womb (yoni) within which the entire universe rests safely (nilaya). concurrently, she functions as the absolute terminal where all secondary causal matrices, elements, and creator deities (brahmā, viṣṇu, rudra) are drawn back and completely dissolved (layam) at the end of time.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya reconciles Her as both the womb and the terminal of existence:

The Matrix and the Terminal: She is the primordial womb (Yoni) within which the entire universe rests safely (nilaya). Concurrently, She functions as the absolute terminal where all secondary causal matrices, elements, and creator deities (Brahmā, Viṣṇu, Rudra) are drawn back and completely dissolved (layam) at the end of time.

name: कूटस्था
samasa: कूटयति छलयत्यात्मानमानन्दादिकमावृत्य संसारे पातयतीति कूटमज्ञानं तदध्यक्षतया तत्र तिष्ठतीति।
summary: (i) One who is established in the kuta.
(ii) Kuta is explained as ignorance that covers the Self, as a mountain peak, as groups of worlds, as the three mantra-kutas and as the triangle of the inner worship.
(iii) She stands as the witness and support of all these meanings, while remaining unmoving and supreme.
searchText: 896 कूटस्था कूटयति छलयत्यात्मानमानन्दादिकमावृत्य संसारे पातयतीति कूटमज्ञानं तदध्यक्षतया तत्र तिष्ठतीति। कूटस्याज्ञानस्य स्था स्थितिर्यस्यां सा। कूटो गिरिशृङ्गं तद्वन्निष्क्रियतया तिष्ठतीति। कूटानां विश्वसमूहानां स्थितिर्यस्यां सा। वाग्भवादिकूटत्रये तिष्ठतीति। कूटं पुरद्वारं स्त्रीचक्रान्तर्गतत्रिकोणं तत्र तिष्ठतीति। कूटम् — mass; peak; deception; mantra-section अज्ञानम् — ignorance अध्यक्षतया — as presiding witness तिष्ठति — stands; abides वाग्भवादिकूटत्रये — in the three mantra-kutas (i) one who is established in the kuta. (ii) kuta is explained as ignorance that covers the self, as a mountain peak, as groups of worlds, as the three mantra-kutas and as the triangle of the inner worship. (iii) she stands as the witness and support of all these meanings, while remaining unmoving and supreme. in the saubhāgyabhāskara, bhāskararāya delivers one of the most comprehensive philosophical analyses of this title: the unmoving witness on the peak: like an anvil (kūṭa) that stays completely still while the blacksmith beats iron shapes upon it, or a high mountain peak that watches changes below, she is the unmoving, changeless witness consciousness (kūṭastha). the mantric and geometric hub: she resides perfectly inside the three acoustic blocks (kūṭas) of her mantra and sits as the illuminating lamp inside the central inverted triangle of the śrī cakra.
esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara, Bhāskararāya delivers one of the most comprehensive philosophical analyses of this title:

The Unmoving Witness on the Peak: Like an anvil (kūṭa) that stays completely still while the blacksmith beats iron shapes upon it, or a high mountain peak that watches changes below, She is the unmoving, changeless witness consciousness (Kūṭastha).
The Mantric and Geometric Hub: She resides perfectly inside the three acoustic blocks (kūṭas) of Her mantra and sits as the illuminating lamp inside the central inverted triangle of the Śrī Cakra.

name: कुलरूपिणी
samasa: कुलं कौलमार्गो बाह्यपूजा वंश आचारो वा तद्रूपिणी।
summary: (i) One whose form is Kula.
(ii) Kula may mean the Kaula path, external worship, family lineage or sacred conduct.
(iii) She assumes the form of the Kula tradition and is its inner reality.
searchText: 897 कुलरूपिणी कुलं कौलमार्गो बाह्यपूजा वंश आचारो वा तद्रूपिणी। कुलम् — kula; lineage; kaula path कौलमार्गः — kaula path बाह्यपूजा — external worship वंशः — lineage आचारः — conduct रूपिणी — whose form is (i) one whose form is kula. (ii) kula may mean the kaula path, external worship, family lineage or sacred conduct. (iii) she assumes the form of the kula tradition and is its inner reality. bhāskararāya details the integration of manifest structures: the soul of the kaula lineage: कुल (kula) denotes the manifest universe of elements, body lineage, and the integrated agamic system of worship known as the kaula path. she is kularūpiṇī because she is the internal formatting code running through this entire structure, confirming that the visible matrix is an expression of her body.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya details the integration of manifest structures:

The Soul of the Kaula Lineage: कुल (Kula) denotes the manifest universe of elements, body lineage, and the integrated Agamic system of worship known as the Kaula path. She is Kularūpiṇī because She is the internal formatting code running through this entire structure, confirming that the visible matrix is an expression of Her body.

name: वीरगोष्ठीप्रिया
samasa: वीराणां गोष्ठी सभा संल्लापो वा प्रिया यस्याः सा।
summary: (i) One who loves the assembly of heroes.
(ii) Vira means the heroic worshipper; goshthi means assembly, gathering or sacred conversation.
(iii) She delights in the company and worship of qualified heroic upasakas.
searchText: 898 वीरगोष्ठीप्रिया वीराणां गोष्ठी सभा संल्लापो वा प्रिया यस्याः सा। वीराणाम् — of heroes; heroic worshippers गोष्ठी — assembly; gathering सभा — council संल्लापः — conversation प्रिया — dear (i) one who loves the assembly of heroes. (ii) vira means the heroic worshipper; goshthi means assembly, gathering or sacred conversation. (iii) she delights in the company and worship of qualified heroic upasakas. in the saubhāgyabhāskara, bhāskararāya defines the internal character environment she values: the affection for heroic fellowship: vīras are those advanced, disciplined practitioners who approach practice with courage, cutting away their internal limitations. she is deeply affectionate toward their corporate gatherings (goṣṭhī) and technical spiritual dialogues. when these practitioners sit together to analyze the lineage keys, she manifests inside their circle to elevate their understanding.
esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara, Bhāskararāya defines the internal character environment She values:

The Affection for Heroic Fellowship: Vīras are those advanced, disciplined practitioners who approach practice with courage, cutting away their internal limitations. She is deeply affectionate toward their corporate gatherings (goṣṭhī) and technical spiritual dialogues. When these practitioners sit together to analyze the lineage keys, She manifests inside their circle to elevate their understanding.

name: वीरा
wordByWord: वीर्यवत्त्वात् — because of possessing heroic power
summary: (i) One who is herself heroic.
(ii) Vira also means one endowed with strength and potency.
(iii) She is the very embodiment of divine courage and power.
searchText: 899 वीरा स्वयमपि वीर्यवत्त्वाद्वीरा। वीर्यवत्त्वात् — because of possessing heroic power वीरा — heroic one (i) one who is herself heroic. (ii) vira also means one endowed with strength and potency. (iii) she is the very embodiment of divine courage and power. bhāskararāya defines her absolute kinetic potency: the ultimate hero: she is vīrā because she possesses absolute, unchecked vital energy and sovereign power (vīryavattvāt). she bows to no external threat, contains zero internal fear, and operates as the supreme warrior who strikes down cosmic distortions, serving as the template of courage for the seeker.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya defines Her absolute kinetic potency:

The Ultimate Hero: She is Vīrā because She possesses absolute, unchecked vital energy and sovereign power (vīryavattvāt). She bows to no external threat, contains zero internal fear, and operates as the supreme warrior who strikes down cosmic distortions, serving as the template of courage for the seeker.

name: नैष्कर्म्या
samasa: निर्गतानि कर्माणि यस्मात्स निष्कर्मा तस्य भावो नैष्कर्म्यं
summary: (i) One who is free from karma.
(ii) Naishkarmya is the state in which actions have gone and do not bind.
(iii) She is beyond action and the fruit of action, though all action proceeds through Her power.
searchText: 900 नैष्कर्म्या निर्गतानि कर्माणि यस्मात्स निष्कर्मा तस्य भावो नैष्कर्म्यं निर्गतानि — gone; removed कर्माणि — actions निष्कर्मा — actionless भावः — state (i) one who is free from karma. (ii) naishkarmya is the state in which actions have gone and do not bind. (iii) she is beyond action and the fruit of action, though all action proceeds through her power. in the saubhāgyabhāskara, bhāskararāya concludes this major 900-name milestone by defining her freedom from karmic bondage: the actionless substratum: material entities are driven by the mechanical laws of action and reaction (karma), gathering binding residue. she is naiṣkarmyā because her essential unconditioned reality sits completely past this mechanical framework. though she runs the kinetic engine of universes, she remains anchored in absolute, non-dual stillness, free from the stains of action, and guiding the practitioner toward this same resting space of liberation.
esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara, Bhāskararāya concludes this major 900-name milestone by defining Her freedom from karmic bondage:

The Actionless Substratum: Material entities are driven by the mechanical laws of action and reaction (Karma), gathering binding residue. She is Naiṣkarmyā because Her essential unconditioned reality sits completely past this mechanical framework. Though She runs the kinetic engine of universes, She remains anchored in absolute, non-dual stillness, free from the stains of action, and guiding the practitioner toward this same resting space of liberation.

name: नादरूपिणी
wordByWord: प्रणवशिरस्थितः — situated at the head of Pranava
summary: (i) One whose form is Nada.
(ii) Nada is the subtle sound associated with Pranava.
(iii) She is the subtle sound-principle from which mantra and speech arise.
searchText: 901 नादरूपिणी नादः प्रणवशिरस्थितस्तद्रूपा। नादे रूपमस्या अस्तीति। नादः — subtle sound प्रणवशिरस्थितः — situated at the head of pranava तद्रूपा — of that form (i) one whose form is nada. (ii) nada is the subtle sound associated with pranava. (iii) she is the subtle sound-principle from which mantra and speech arise. in the saubhāgyabhāskara, bhāskararāya details the sonic stratification of consciousness: the apex of the praṇava: nāda represents the highly refined, subtle sound vibration located at the crown apex of the syllable ओम् (praṇava-śiras). it marks the transition point where sound dissolves back into silent awareness. she is nādarūpiṇī because she is the kinetic energy that flows through this acoustic ladder, acting as the uncreated stream that translates divine intent into universal manifestation.
esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara, Bhāskararāya details the sonic stratification of consciousness:

The Apex of the Praṇava: Nāda represents the highly refined, subtle sound vibration located at the crown apex of the syllable ओम् (Praṇava-śiras). It marks the transition point where sound dissolves back into silent awareness. She is Nādarūpiṇī because She is the kinetic energy that flows through this acoustic ladder, acting as the uncreated stream that translates divine intent into universal manifestation.

name: विज्ञानकलना
wordByWord: स्वात्मसाक्षात्कारः — realization of one’s own Self
summary: (i) One who is the realization of knowledge.
(ii) Vijnana here points to direct realization of Brahman.
(iii) She is the inner realization by which the Self is known.
searchText: 902 विज्ञानकलना विज्ञानस्य ब्रह्मसाक्षात्कारस्य कलना स्वात्मसाक्षात्कारः। विज्ञानस्य — of direct knowledge ब्रह्मसाक्षात्कारस्य — of realization of brahman कलना — realization; reckoning स्वात्मसाक्षात्कारः — realization of one’s own self (i) one who is the realization of knowledge. (ii) vijnana here points to direct realization of brahman. (iii) she is the inner realization by which the self is known. bhāskararāya defines the shift from intellectual computation to direct non-dual realization: the dawn of self-experience: vijñāna is not dry, theoretical academic knowledge (jñāna); it is the direct, unmediated flash of cosmic self-realization (ब्रह्मसाक्षात्कार). she is vijñānakalanā because she acts as the precise internal spark and kinetic process (kalanā) that converts a theoretical understanding of the self into an immediate, lived reality.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya defines the shift from intellectual computation to direct non-dual realization:

The Dawn of Self-Experience: Vijñāna is not dry, theoretical academic knowledge (jñāna); it is the direct, unmediated flash of cosmic self-realization (ब्रह्मसाक्षात्कार). She is Vijñānakalanā because She acts as the precise internal spark and kinetic process (kalanā) that converts a theoretical understanding of the Self into an immediate, lived reality.

name: कल्या
wordByWord: कादम्बर्यादिरूपा — of forms such as Kadambari
summary: (i) One who is Kalya.
(ii) The name is connected with kala, fitness for being known, and also with forms such as Kadambari.
(iii) She is fit to be contemplated through the kalas and is auspiciously knowable.
searchText: 903 कल्या कलासु साधुः। कलयितुमर्हा। कादम्बर्यादिरूपा। कलासु — in the kalas साधुः — good; fit कलयितुम् — to reckon; to know अर्हा — worthy कादम्बर्यादिरूपा — of forms such as kadambari (i) one who is kalya. (ii) the name is connected with kala, fitness for being known, and also with forms such as kadambari. (iii) she is fit to be contemplated through the kalas and is auspiciously knowable. in the saubhāgyabhāskara, bhāskararāya unpacks the three multi-layered structural tracks: the master of design: she is kalyā because she possesses perfect, flawless mastery over all the creative arts and phases of time (कलासु साधुः). the attainable horizon: concurrently, she represents the ultimate, pure state of consciousness that is worthy of being measured and realized by refined minds, acting as the radiant dawn of the soul.
esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara, Bhāskararāya unpacks the three multi-layered structural tracks:
The Master of Design: She is Kalyā because She possesses perfect, flawless mastery over all the creative arts and phases of time (कलासु साधुः).
The Attainable Horizon: Concurrently, She represents the ultimate, pure state of consciousness that is worthy of being measured and realized by refined minds, acting as the radiant dawn of the soul.

name: विदग्धा
samasa: विशेषेण दग्धा इति विग्रहः। अर्थस्तु सौभाग्यभास्करे चातुर्यशीला इति। (कोशे अपि निपुणपरः विदग्धशब्दः)।
summary: (i) One who is Vidagdha.
(ii) Vidagdha is explained in the sense of clever, skilful and refined.
(iii) She is the one of perfect skill and divine intelligence.
searchText: 904 विदग्धा विशेषेण दग्धा इति विग्रहः। अर्थस्तु सौभाग्यभास्करे चातुर्यशीला इति। (कोशे अपि निपुणपरः विदग्धशब्दः)। विशेषेण — especially दग्धा — burnt; refined चातुर्यशीला — skilful; clever विदग्ध — clever; accomplished (i) one who is vidagdha. (ii) vidagdha is explained in the sense of clever, skilful and refined. (iii) she is the one of perfect skill and divine intelligence. bhāskararāya purges this of mundane definitions to highlight her flawless intelligence: the perfection of clever grace: while the literal grammatical derivation means "thoroughly burned," the philosophical meaning denotes a highly refined, brilliant capacity of mind (चातुर्यशीला). she is vidagdhā because her consciousness manages the trillions of structural calculations required to run the multiverse with flawless, sharp, and graceful precision, leaving no room for operational error.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya purges this of mundane definitions to highlight Her flawless intelligence:

The Perfection of Clever Grace: While the literal grammatical derivation means "thoroughly burned," the philosophical meaning denotes a highly refined, brilliant capacity of mind (चातुर्यशीला). She is Vidagdhā because Her consciousness manages the trillions of structural calculations required to run the multiverse with flawless, sharp, and graceful precision, leaving no room for operational error.

name: बैन्दवासना
samasa: भ्रूवोरुपरिभागे वृत्तसन्निवेशो बैन्दवं तदासनं यस्याः सा।
बिन्दुनां समूहो बैन्दवं तदेवासनं तदभिधेयार्थानामाधारोऽभिधायको यस्याः सा।
summary: (i) One whose seat is Baindava.
(ii) Baindava may be the circular arrangement above the eyebrows, the Bindu-related Sarvanandamaya chakra, or the aggregate of bindus as support of meanings.
(iii) She is seated in the Bindu and in the supreme centre of bliss.
searchText: 905 बैन्दवासना भ्रूवोरुपरिभागे वृत्तसन्निवेशो बैन्दवं तदासनं यस्याः सा। बिन्दुसम्बन्धिचक्रं सर्वानन्दमयाख्यमेवासनं यस्याः सा। बिन्दुनां समूहो बैन्दवं तदेवासनं तदभिधेयार्थानामाधारोऽभिधायको यस्याः सा। भ्रूवोः — of the eyebrows उपरिभागे — above वृत्तसन्निवेशः — circular arrangement बिन्दु — bindu आसनम् — seat सर्वानन्दमय — all-blissful आधारः — support (i) one whose seat is baindava. (ii) baindava may be the circular arrangement above the eyebrows, the bindu-related sarvanandamaya chakra, or the aggregate of bindus as support of meanings. (iii) she is seated in the bindu and in the supreme centre of bliss. in the saubhāgyabhāskara, bhāskararāya delivers a major layout mapping tantric geometry: the ultimate hub of the śrī yantra: geometrically, baindava refers to the absolute central dot—the बिन्दु (bindu)—of the śrī yantra, which is the internal ninth enclosure known as the सर्वानन्दमय चक्र (sarvānandamaya cakra). she is baindavāsanā because she sits enthroned at this exact center point. it is the unmoving anchor where all acoustic sounds and geometric lines dissolve into absolute non-dual unity.
esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara, Bhāskararāya delivers a major layout mapping Tantric geometry:

The Ultimate Hub of the Śrī Yantra: Geometrically, Baindava refers to the absolute central dot—the बिन्दु (Bindu)—of the Śrī Yantra, which is the internal ninth enclosure known as the सर्वानन्दमय चक्र (Sarvānandamaya Cakra). She is Baindavāsanā because She sits enthroned at this exact center point. It is the unmoving anchor where all acoustic sounds and geometric lines dissolve into absolute non-dual unity.

name: तत्त्वाधिका
summary: (i) One who is beyond the tattvas.
(ii) The thirty-six tattvas remain only until dissolution.
(iii) She transcends them and remains even when they are dissolved.
searchText: 906 तत्त्वाधिका तत्त्वानि प्रलयपर्यन्तस्थायिवस्तूनि षट्त्रिंशत्संख्यान्येव। तेभ्योऽधिका तन्नाशेऽप्यवस्थानात्। तत्त्वप्रचुरा। तत्त्वानि — principles; tattvas षट्त्रिंशत् — thirty-six तेभ्यः — beyond them अधिका — greater; beyond नाशे अपि — even at dissolution अवस्थानात् — because of remaining (i) one who is beyond the tattvas. (ii) the thirty-six tattvas remain only until dissolution. (iii) she transcends them and remains even when they are dissolved. bhāskararāya details her total survival past universal collapse: the transcendent remainder: the manifest universe is structured out of thirty-six foundational blocks of reality (tattvas), running from the gross earth element up to the shiva-shakti layer. these elements are durable but eventually dissolve during the grand collapse (mahāpralaya). she is tattvādhikā because she sits completely above and past this entire taxonomy. when the thirty-six principles are wiped out, she remains unaffected as the baseline awareness background.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya details Her total survival past universal collapse:

The Transcendent Remainder: The manifest universe is structured out of thirty-six foundational blocks of reality (tattvas), running from the gross earth element up to the Shiva-Shakti layer. These elements are durable but eventually dissolve during the grand collapse (mahāpralaya). She is Tattvādhikā because She sits completely above and past this entire taxonomy. When the thirty-six principles are wiped out, She remains unaffected as the baseline awareness background.

name: तत्त्वमयी
summary: (i) One who is full of the tattvas.
(ii) Though She is beyond the tattvas, She also pervades them.
(iii) The whole range of principles is filled with Her presence.
searchText: 907 तत्त्वमयी तत्त्वप्रचुरा। तत्त्व — principle; tattva प्रचुरा — abundant; full of (i) one who is full of the tattvas. (ii) though she is beyond the tattvas, she also pervades them. (iii) the whole range of principles is filled with her presence. in the saubhāgyabhāskara, bhāskararāya presents the immediate non-dual paradox following tattvādhikā: immanence within the blocks: while she transcends the elements (tattvādhikā), she is simultaneously tattvamayī because she is the actual substance that constitutes them. she does not separate herself from the world to stay holy; every single element block is saturated with her consciousness. she is the material and spiritual fabric of everything that exists.
esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara, Bhāskararāya presents the immediate non-dual paradox following Tattvādhikā:

Immanence within the Blocks: While She transcends the elements (Tattvādhikā), She is simultaneously Tattvamayī because She is the actual substance that constitutes them. She does not separate Herself from the world to stay holy; every single element block is saturated with Her consciousness. She is the material and spiritual fabric of everything that exists.

name: तत्त्वमर्थस्वरूपिणी
wordByWord: शिवजीवौ — Shiva and the individual soul
summary: (i) One whose form is the meaning of Tat and Tvam.
(ii) Tat indicates Shiva and Tvam indicates the individual soul.
(iii) She is the identity of the two meanings revealed in the great saying.
searchText: 908 तत्त्वमर्थस्वरूपिणी तत्पदत्वम्पदयोरर्थौ शिवजीवौ स्वरूपमस्याः अस्तीति। तत्पद — the word tat त्वम्पद — the word tvam अर्थौ — meanings शिवजीवौ — shiva and the individual soul स्वरूपम् — form (i) one whose form is the meaning of tat and tvam. (ii) tat indicates shiva and tvam indicates the individual soul. (iii) she is the identity of the two meanings revealed in the great saying. bhāskararāya anchors this directly in the ultimate realization of the upaniṣads (tat tvam asi): the living bridge of non-duality: in the grand vedic statement "tat tvam asi" ("that thou art"), तत् (tat) represents the transcendent absolute consciousness (shiva), and त्वम् (tvam) represents the individualized localized living soul (jīva). she is tattvamarthasvarūpiṇī because she is the single, non-dual essence that bridges and unifies these two apparently distinct poles, showing they are one substance.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya anchors this directly in the ultimate realization of the Upaniṣads (Tat Tvam Asi):

The Living Bridge of Non-Duality: In the grand Vedic statement "Tat Tvam Asi" ("That Thou Art"), तत् (Tat) represents the transcendent absolute consciousness (Shiva), and त्वम् (Tvam) represents the individualized localized living soul (Jīva). She is Tattvamarthasvarūpiṇī because She is the single, non-dual essence that bridges and unifies these two apparently distinct poles, showing they are one substance.

name: सामगानप्रिया
wordByWord: सामगानम् — singing of the Sama Veda
summary: (i) One who loves the singing of the Sama Veda.
(ii) Sama-gana is sacred Vedic song.
(iii) She is pleased by the chanting of the Sama, and by sacred music directed to Her.
searchText: 909 सामगानप्रिया सामगानं प्रियं यस्याः सा। सामगानम् — singing of the sama veda प्रियम् — dear यस्याः सा — she whose (i) one who loves the singing of the sama veda. (ii) sama-gana is sacred vedic song. (iii) she is pleased by the chanting of the sama, and by sacred music directed to her. in the saubhāgyabhāskara, bhāskararāya details her relationship with sacred acoustic geometry: the joy of the perfect melody: lord krishna states in the gītā, "among the vedas, i am the sāma veda." the sāma chants represent the ultimate musical and rhythmic arrangement of vedic light metrics. she is sāmagānapriyā because these precise, soothing melodic patterns match the internal harmony of her own creative field, quickly calming the seeker's mind.
esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara, Bhāskararāya details Her relationship with sacred acoustic geometry:

The Joy of the Perfect Melody: Lord Krishna states in the Gītā, "Among the Vedas, I am the Sāma Veda." The Sāma chants represent the ultimate musical and rhythmic arrangement of Vedic light metrics. She is Sāmagānapriyā because these precise, soothing melodic patterns match the internal harmony of Her own creative field, quickly calming the seeker's mind.

name: सोम्या/सौम्या
summary: (i) One who is Somya or Saumya.
(ii) Somya is connected with Soma sacrifice, with Uma united with Soma, and with the cooling delight of the moon.
(iii) She is gentle, pleasing and fit for sacred worship.
searchText: 910 सोम्या/सौम्या सोम्या। सोमयागार्हा। उमया सहितः सोमोऽवयवोऽस्या अस्तीति। सोम इवाह्लादिका। सौम्या। सोम्या एव सौम्या (चातुर्वर्ण्यादित्वात्स्वार्थे ष्यञ्)।तद्वती। (अर्शादित्वान्मत्वर्थीयोऽप्रत्ययः। स्वार्थे वा ष्यङ्।) सोम्या — connected with soma; gentle सोमयागार्हा — fit for soma sacrifice उमया सहितः — united with uma आह्लादिका — delight-giving सौम्या — gentle; pleasing (i) one who is somya or saumya. (ii) somya is connected with soma sacrifice, with uma united with soma, and with the cooling delight of the moon. (iii) she is gentle, pleasing and fit for sacred worship. bhāskararāya coordinates the cooling, alchemical properties of her presence: the cooling radiance of consciousness: she is saumyā because she reflects the gentle, cooling, and blissful illumination of the moon (soma), soothing the existential burning of the mind. under the internal ritual key, she is the target who accepts the alchemical soma offering, and manifests as the unified form of shiva coupled with umā (स-उमा).
esoteric: Bhāskararāya coordinates the cooling, alchemical properties of Her presence:

The Cooling Radiance of Consciousness: She is Saumyā because She reflects the gentle, cooling, and blissful illumination of the moon (Soma), soothing the existential burning of the mind. Under the internal ritual key, She is the target who accepts the alchemical Soma offering, and manifests as the unified form of Shiva coupled with Umā (स-उमा).

name: सदाशिवकुटुम्बिनी
wordByWord: कुटुम्बिनी — wife; lady of the household
summary: (i) One who is the family lady of Sadashiva.
(ii) Kutumbini means the wife or lady of the household.
(iii) She is inseparably associated with Sadashiva.
searchText: 911 सदाशिवकुटुम्बिनी सदाशिवस्य कुटुम्बिनी। सदाशिवस्य — of sadashiva कुटुम्बिनी — wife; lady of the household (i) one who is the family lady of sadashiva. (ii) kutumbini means the wife or lady of the household. (iii) she is inseparably associated with sadashiva. in the saubhāgyabhāskara, bhāskararāya defines her equal partnership at the absolute tier: the manager of the cosmic household: sadāśiva represents the absolute, unmoving consciousness baseline. she is his kuṭumbinī — his direct domestic consort. this role implies that while he remains the silent witness, she takes full charge of managing the vast corporate "household" of the universe, translating his presence into dynamic form.
esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara, Bhāskararāya defines Her equal partnership at the absolute tier:

The Manager of the Cosmic Household: Sadāśiva represents the absolute, unmoving consciousness baseline. She is His Kuṭumbinī — His direct domestic consort. This role implies that while He remains the silent witness, She takes full charge of managing the vast corporate "household" of the universe, translating His presence into dynamic form.

name: सव्यापसव्यमार्गस्था
samasa: सव्यो देवयानः। अपसव्यः पितृयाणः। मार्गेषु स्था स्थितिर्यस्मात् इति विष्णुलोकपरः।ईदृशमार्गत्रयरूपेत्यर्थः।
summary: (i) One who abides in the right-hand and left-hand paths.
(ii) Savya and apasavya indicate different ritual paths and also paths such as devayana and pitriyana.
(iii) She is present in all legitimate paths and transcends the distinction between them.
searchText: 912 सव्यापसव्यमार्गस्था सव्यश्चापसव्यश्च मार्गश्च सव्यापसव्यमार्गाः। तेषु स्थिताधिकृता। सव्यो देवयानः। अपसव्यः पितृयाणः। मार्गेषु स्था स्थितिर्यस्मात् इति विष्णुलोकपरः।ईदृशमार्गत्रयरूपेत्यर्थः। सव्यः — right-hand path अपसव्यः — left-hand path मार्गः — path स्थित — abiding देवयानः — path of the gods पितृयाणः — path of the ancestors (i) one who abides in the right-hand and left-hand paths. (ii) savya and apasavya indicate different ritual paths and also paths such as devayana and pitriyana. (iii) she is present in all legitimate paths and transcends the distinction between them. bhāskararāya maps her all-inclusive control over spiritual methodologies: the master of all vehicles: in practice taxonomy, savya is the orthodox right-hand path (dakṣiṇācāra), and apasvya is the radical left-hand path (vāmācāra). in eschatology, they mark the solar path of the gods (devayāna) and the lunar path of ancestors (pitṛyāna). she is savyāpasavyamārgasthā because she is the sovereign director of both grids, ensuring that whichever road a soul travels, they ultimately interface with her energy field.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya maps Her all-inclusive control over spiritual methodologies:

The Master of All Vehicles: In practice taxonomy, Savya is the orthodox right-hand path (Dakṣiṇācāra), and Apasvya is the radical left-hand path (Vāmācāra). In eschatology, they mark the solar path of the gods (Devayāna) and the lunar path of ancestors (Pitṛyāna). She is Savyāpasavyamārgasthā because She is the sovereign director of both grids, ensuring that whichever road a soul travels, they ultimately interface with Her energy field.

name: सर्वापद्विनिवारिणी
summary: (i) One who removes all calamities.
(ii) Apad means danger, distress or misfortune.
(iii) She completely wards off the troubles of Her devotees.
searchText: 913 सर्वापद्विनिवारिणी सर्वा आपदो विशिष्य नितरां वारयतीति। सर्वाः — all आपदः — calamities; dangers वारयति — wards off विनिवारिणी — remover (i) one who removes all calamities. (ii) apad means danger, distress or misfortune. (iii) she completely wards off the troubles of her devotees. in the saubhāgyabhāskara, bhāskararāya details her active defensive intervention: the absolute shield: distresses (āpad) can strike from physical, planetary, or internal psychological vectors. she does not offer partial protection. as vinivāriṇī, she operates as a high-velocity shield that completely dismantles these incoming crises at their root energetic levels, securing the practitioner's workspace.
esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara, Bhāskararāya details Her active defensive intervention:

The Absolute Shield: Distresses (āpad) can strike from physical, planetary, or internal psychological vectors. She does not offer partial protection. As Vinivāriṇī, She operates as a high-velocity shield that completely dismantles these incoming crises at their root energetic levels, securing the practitioner's workspace.

name: स्वस्था
summary: (i) One who abides in Her own Self.
(ii) Svastha means established in oneself; it may also indicate abiding in heaven or a beautiful state.
(iii) She is self-established and gives stability to worshippers.
searchText: 914 स्वस्था स्वस्मिन्तिष्ठतीति। स्वरित्यव्ययं स्वर्गवाचि। तत्र तिष्ठत्यध्यक्षतयेति। शोभना अस्था स्थित्यभावो गतिर्यया सा। स्वस्मिन् — in one’s own self तिष्ठति — abides स्वः — heaven स्थ — abiding स्थितिः — state (i) one who abides in her own self. (ii) svastha means established in oneself; it may also indicate abiding in heaven or a beautiful state. (iii) she is self-established and gives stability to worshippers. bhāskararāya explores the deep non-dual definition of absolute health: the anchor of self-existence: most entities depend on external objects to feel stable. she is svasthā because she rests completely within her own pristine nature (स्वस्मिन् तिष्ठति). in language, this denotes perfect health and ease. she is completely free from external disturbance, demonstrating the state of independent rest.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya explores the deep non-dual definition of absolute health:

The Anchor of Self-Existence: Most entities depend on external objects to feel stable. She is Svasthā because She rests completely within Her own pristine nature (स्वस्मिन् तिष्ठति). In language, this denotes perfect health and ease. She is completely free from external disturbance, demonstrating the state of independent rest.

name: स्वभावमधुरा
samasa: स्व आत्मीयो भावोऽवस्थानं यस्यां सा च सा मधुरापुरी च।
स्वभामात्प्रज्ञानं ये वमन्ति सृजन्ति साधयन्ति तेषु धूरुत्तमा
स्वेषां भावेन भक्त्या मधु सत्फलं रातीति।
summary: (i) One whose nature is sweet.
(ii) Her own state, Her devotees' feeling, and the good fruit She grants are all described through sweetness.
(iii) She is naturally gentle and gracious.
