searchText: 915 स्वभावमधुरा स्व आत्मीयो भावोऽवस्थानं यस्यां सा च सा मधुरापुरी च। स्वभामात्प्रज्ञानं ये वमन्ति सृजन्ति साधयन्ति तेषु धूरुत्तमा स्वेषां भावेन भक्त्या मधु सत्फलं रातीति। स्व — own भावः — nature; state मधुरा — sweet भक्त्या — through devotion सत्फलम् — good fruit (i) one whose nature is sweet. (ii) her own state, her devotees' feeling, and the good fruit she grants are all described through sweetness. (iii) she is naturally gentle and gracious. in the saubhāgyabhāskara, bhāskararāya details her effortless charm: intrinsic absolute sweetness: she does not require external ornaments or artificial updates to appear attractive. her core essence is pure, unconditioned bliss (madhura). like honey is naturally sweet without effort, her presence is automatically soothing and charming to any mind that touches her field.
esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara, Bhāskararāya details Her effortless charm:

Intrinsic Absolute Sweetness: She does not require external ornaments or artificial updates to appear attractive. Her core essence is pure, unconditioned bliss (madhura). Like honey is naturally sweet without effort, Her presence is automatically soothing and charming to any mind that touches Her field.

name: धीरा
summary: (i) One who is wise and steady.
(ii) Dhira means learned, firm and courageous; it may also mean one who grants non-dual understanding.
(iii) She is the giver of wisdom and steadiness.
searchText: 916 धीरा धीरा पण्डिता। धैर्यवती। धियमद्वैतबुद्धिं राति ददातीति। धीप्रदा इरा दशमीतिथिर्यस्यां सा। धीरा — wise; steady पण्डिता — learned धैर्यवती — courageous; firm धियम् — understanding ददाति — gives (i) one who is wise and steady. (ii) dhira means learned, firm and courageous; it may also mean one who grants non-dual understanding. (iii) she is the giver of wisdom and steadiness. bhāskararāya tracks the cognitive gifts delivered by this single-word title: the giver of non-dual intellect: she is dhīra because she possesses unyielding courage and absolute wisdom. critically, she acts as the engine that dispenses non-dual intellect (धियम् अद्वैतबुद्धिं राति), breaking down the mental habits of fragmentation and anchoring the seeker in unity.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya tracks the cognitive gifts delivered by this single-word title:

The Giver of Non-Dual Intellect: She is Dhīra because She possesses unyielding courage and absolute wisdom. Critically, She acts as the engine that dispenses non-dual intellect (धियम् अद्वैतबुद्धिं राति), breaking down the mental habits of fragmentation and anchoring the seeker in unity.

name: धीरसमर्चिता
summary: (i) One who is worshipped by the wise.
(ii) Dhira also points to knowledge and bliss.
(iii) She is adored by those who seek the rasa of knowledge and the joy of realization.
searchText: 917 धीरसमर्चिता धीसंज्ञं ज्ञानाभिन्नं रसमानन्दमुद्दिश्यार्चिता। धी — knowledge; understanding रस — essence; bliss आनन्दम् — bliss अर्चिता — worshipped (i) one who is worshipped by the wise. (ii) dhira also points to knowledge and bliss. (iii) she is adored by those who seek the rasa of knowledge and the joy of realization. in the saubhāgyabhāskara, bhāskararāya tracks the ultimate target of this worship: worship with intellect and bliss: dhīra refers to those steady, unwavering seekers who cannot be distracted by worldly illusions. their worship is not based on blind emotion; they adore her by targeting the absolute fusion of pure knowledge (dhī) and non-dual bliss (rasa), matching her own nature.
esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara, Bhāskararāya tracks the ultimate target of this worship:

Worship with Intellect and Bliss: Dhīra refers to those steady, unwavering seekers who cannot be distracted by worldly illusions. Their worship is not based on blind emotion; they adore Her by targeting the absolute fusion of pure knowledge (dhī) and non-dual bliss (rasa), matching Her own nature.

name: चैतन्यार्घ्यसमाराध्या
summary: (i) One who is worshipped with consciousness as the offering.
(ii) Chaitanya itself becomes the arghya, the water of worship.
(iii) She is truly worshipped by the knowledge and direct experience of the Self.
searchText: 918 चैतन्यार्घ्यसमाराध्या चैतन्यमेवार्घ्यं पूजायोग्यं जलादि तेन सम्यगाराध्या। चैतन्यस्यात्मनोऽर्घ्येण ज्ञानेन स्वानुभूत्या सम्यगाराध्या। चैतन्यम् — consciousness अर्घ्यम् — ritual offering पूजायोग्यम् — fit for worship ज्ञानेन — by knowledge स्वानुभूत्या — by direct self-experience (i) one who is worshipped with consciousness as the offering. (ii) chaitanya itself becomes the arghya, the water of worship. (iii) she is truly worshipped by the knowledge and direct experience of the self. bhāskararāya strips this of external ritual materials to reveal internal mysticism: the offering of direct awareness: in external ritualism, one offers clean water (arghya) to honor a guest. in the high non-dual layout, the practitioner realizes that physical elements are insufficient. she is correctly worshipped when the seeker offers their own pure, unconditioned consciousness (caitanya) and direct self-experience (svānubhūti) back into her field, dissolving the separation between worshipper and worshipped.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya strips this of external ritual materials to reveal internal mysticism:

The Offering of Direct Awareness: In external ritualism, one offers clean water (arghya) to honor a guest. In the high non-dual layout, the practitioner realizes that physical elements are insufficient. She is correctly worshipped when the seeker offers their own pure, unconditioned consciousness (Caitanya) and direct self-experience (svānubhūti) back into Her field, dissolving the separation between worshipper and worshipped.

name: चैतन्यकुसुमप्रिया
summary: (i) One who loves the flower of consciousness.
(ii) The proper flower offered to Her is not merely external; it is the flower of pure awareness.
(iii) Consciousness offered in worship is dear to Her.
searchText: 919 चैतन्यकुसुमप्रिया चैतन्यरूपकुसुमं प्रिया यस्याः सा। चैतन्य — consciousness कुसुमम् — flower प्रिया — dear (i) one who loves the flower of consciousness. (ii) the proper flower offered to her is not merely external; it is the flower of pure awareness. (iii) consciousness offered in worship is dear to her. in the saubhāgyabhāskara, bhāskararāya guides the internal selection of offerings: the flower that never wears out: material flowers fade, dry up, and carry physical limitations. she is caitanyakusumapriyā because her favorite blossom is the glowing flower of internal awareness (caitanya). offering her a mind that is clear, still, and alert is the ultimate flower offering, satisfying her completely.
esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara, Bhāskararāya guides the internal selection of offerings:

The Flower that Never Wears Out: Material flowers fade, dry up, and carry physical limitations. She is Caitanyakusumapriyā because Her favorite blossom is the glowing flower of internal awareness (Caitanya). Offering Her a mind that is clear, still, and alert is the ultimate flower offering, satisfying Her completely.

name: सदोदिता
summary: (i) One who is always risen.
(ii) She is ever manifest and never subject to setting or disappearance.
(iii) Among the good and noble also, Her glory is constantly rising.
searchText: 920 सदोदिता सदा नित्यं उदिता उदयवती। सत्सु सज्जनेषु आसमन्तादतिशयेनोदिता। सदा — always नित्यम् — eternally उदिता — risen; manifest सज्जनेषु — among good people (i) one who is always risen. (ii) she is ever manifest and never subject to setting or disappearance. (iii) among the good and noble also, her glory is constantly rising. bhāskararāya concludes this sub-sequence by breaking the laws of cosmic cycles: the sun that never sets: physical stars and mental moods have rising and setting times. she is sadoditā because her consciousness sun is permanently risen. lacking any dark interval or setting phase, she shines continuously. for the practitioner, she stands instantly visible the exact moment the clouds of ego are cleared away.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya concludes this sub-sequence by breaking the laws of cosmic cycles:

The Sun that Never Sets: Physical stars and mental moods have rising and setting times. She is Sadoditā because Her consciousness sun is permanently risen. Lacking any dark interval or setting phase, She shines continuously. For the practitioner, She stands instantly visible the exact moment the clouds of ego are cleared away.

name: सदातुष्टा
summary: (i) One who is always satisfied.
(ii) She is pleased by good people and true devotion.
(iii) Being complete in Herself, She is ever content.
searchText: 921 सदातुष्टा सद्भिः जनैः आसमन्तात्तुष्टा। सद्भिः — by good people जनैः — by persons तुष्टा — satisfied; pleased (i) one who is always satisfied. (ii) she is pleased by good people and true devotion. (iii) being complete in herself, she is ever content. in the saubhāgyabhāskara, bhāskararāya details her absolute independence from external inputs: eternally full: duality requires external acquisition to feel satisfied. she is sadātuṣṭā because her essential nature is permanently anchored in absolute fulfillment (tṛpti). lacking any internal void or unfulfilled desire, she remains continuously pleased. alternatively, she finds delight in the pure heart movements of virtuous seekers.
esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara, Bhāskararāya details Her absolute independence from external inputs:

Eternally Full: Duality requires external acquisition to feel satisfied. She is Sadātuṣṭā because Her essential nature is permanently anchored in absolute fulfillment (tṛpti). Lacking any internal void or unfulfilled desire, She remains continuously pleased. Alternatively, She finds delight in the pure heart movements of virtuous seekers.

name: तरुणादित्यपाटला
summary: (i) One who is red like the young sun.
(ii) Taruna Aditya is the rising or young sun, and patala denotes a whitish-red or rosy colour.
(iii) Her complexion shines with that tender red radiance.
searchText: 922 तरुणादित्यपाटला तरुणो मध्याह्नकालिक आदित्यः सूर्यस्तद्वत्पाटला श्वेतरक्ता। तरुणः — young आदित्यः — sun पाटला — rosy-white; red-tinged (i) one who is red like the young sun. (ii) taruna aditya is the rising or young sun, and patala denotes a whitish-red or rosy colour. (iii) her complexion shines with that tender red radiance. bhāskararāya maps this vibrant light frequency within visual meditation: the blinding rosy effulgence: पाटल (pāṭala) denotes a highly specialized color profile—a brilliant mix of white and intense red, similar to the blinding light of a clear sun. this color frequency represents the absolute activation of ज्ञानशक्ति (divine knowledge) and क्रियाशक्ति (divine action) blending together to flood the practitioner's subtle channels, burning away internal dark inertia.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya maps this vibrant light frequency within visual meditation:

The Blinding Rosy Effulgence: पाटल (Pāṭala) denotes a highly specialized color profile—a brilliant mix of white and intense red, similar to the blinding light of a clear sun. This color frequency represents the absolute activation of ज्ञानशक्ति (Divine Knowledge) and क्रियाशक्ति (Divine Action) blending together to flood the practitioner's subtle channels, burning away internal dark inertia.

name: दक्षिणादक्षिणाराध्या
wordByWord: दक्षिणया — with dakshina; through right path
अदक्षिणैः — by unskilled or left-hand worshippers
summary: (i) One worshipped by the dakshina and adakshina paths.
(ii) She is worshipped by the skilled and the unskilled, by the learned and ordinary people, and by both right-hand and left-hand worshippers.
(iii) She accepts worship through many modes.
searchText: 923 दक्षिणादक्षिणाराध्या दक्षिणया दक्षिणैः कुशलैराराध्या। दक्षिणैः पण्डितैरदक्षिणैर्मूर्खैश्च आराध्या। दक्षिणामार्गोपासकेन वाममार्गोपासकेन च आराध्या। दक्षिणया — with dakshina; through right path दक्षिणैः — by skilled persons अदक्षिणैः — by unskilled or left-hand worshippers आराध्या — worshipped (i) one worshipped by the dakshina and adakshina paths. (ii) she is worshipped by the skilled and the unskilled, by the learned and ordinary people, and by both right-hand and left-hand worshippers. (iii) she accepts worship through many modes. in the saubhāgyabhāskara, bhāskararāya decodes her dynamic accessibility across all paths: the target of every seeker: she does not reject any class of seeker. she is accessed by the highly refined master (dakṣiṇa) and the simple, unlettered devotee (adakṣiṇa). critically, she is the ultimate target inside both the structured right-hand vedic path (dakṣiṇāmārga) and the intense, radical left-hand agamic path (vāmāmārga), accepting sincere devotion from all sides.
esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara, Bhāskararāya decodes Her dynamic accessibility across all paths:

The Target of Every Seeker: She does not reject any class of seeker. She is accessed by the highly refined master (dakṣiṇa) and the simple, unlettered devotee (adakṣiṇa). Critically, She is the ultimate target inside both the structured right-hand Vedic path (Dakṣiṇāmārga) and the intense, radical left-hand Agamic path (Vāmāmārga), accepting sincere devotion from all sides.

name: दरस्मेरमुखाम्बुजा
samasa: दरमीषद्यथा तथा स्मेरं स्मितवन्मुखाम्बुजं यस्याः सा।
मुखमम्बुजं यस्मिस्तन्मुखाम्बुजं ग्रीवावृत्तं दरवत् शङ्खवत्स्मेरं शोभमानं मुखाम्बुजं यस्याः सा।
summary: (i) One whose lotus face has a gentle smile.
(ii) Dara means slight; smera means smiling.
(iii) Her face is like a lotus softly illumined by a delicate smile.
searchText: 924 दरस्मेरमुखाम्बुजा दरमीषद्यथा तथा स्मेरं स्मितवन्मुखाम्बुजं यस्याः सा। मुखमम्बुजं यस्मिस्तन्मुखाम्बुजं ग्रीवावृत्तं दरवत् शङ्खवत्स्मेरं शोभमानं मुखाम्बुजं यस्याः सा। दरम् — slightly स्मेरम् — smiling मुखाम्बुजम् — lotus-face यस्याः सा — she whose (i) one whose lotus face has a gentle smile. (ii) dara means slight; smera means smiling. (iii) her face is like a lotus softly illumined by a delicate smile. bhāskararāya decodes the absolute reassurance built into her expression: the smile of cosmic reassurance: her countenance does not display fierce tension or cold indifference. she wears a continuous, gentle smile (dara-smera). this smile signifies her absolute confidence over cosmic laws and her deep compassion for the struggles of living beings, sending an immediate signal of safety to the heart of the meditator.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya decodes the absolute reassurance built into Her expression:

The Smile of Cosmic Reassurance: Her countenance does not display fierce tension or cold indifference. She wears a continuous, gentle smile (dara-smera). This smile signifies Her absolute confidence over cosmic laws and Her deep compassion for the struggles of living beings, sending an immediate signal of safety to the heart of the meditator.

name: कौलिनीकेवला
summary: (i) One who is alone with the Kaulinis.
(ii) The Kaulinis are knowers of the Kaula tradition.
(iii) She is attained by the Kaulinis and stands as the unique essence of that knowledge.
searchText: 925 कौलिनीकेवला कौलिन्यः केवला ज्ञानवत्यो यया सा। कौलिनीभिः केवला। कौलिन्यः — kaulini worshippers or knowers केवला — alone; unique ज्ञानवत्यः — endowed with knowledge (i) one who is alone with the kaulinis. (ii) the kaulinis are knowers of the kaula tradition. (iii) she is attained by the kaulinis and stands as the unique essence of that knowledge. in the saubhāgyabhāskara*, bhāskararāya defines the internal destination of agamic liturgy: the core of the kaula system: kaulinī refers to the inner energy running through the elements and the specialized kaula lineage. she is kevalā within this system because she is the absolute, unconditioned consciousness that sits at the very end of the path. she is pure awareness itself, free from external rituals.
esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara*, Bhāskararāya defines the internal destination of Agamic liturgy:

The Core of the Kaula System: Kaulinī refers to the inner energy running through the elements and the specialized Kaula lineage. She is Kevalā within this system because She is the absolute, unconditioned consciousness that sits at the very end of the path. She is pure awareness itself, free from external rituals.

name: अनर्घ्यकैवल्यपददायिनी/अनर्घ्यकैवल्यफलदायिनी
samasa: अनर्घ्यममूल्यमपरिच्छिन्नं यत्कैवल्याख्यं पदम्/फलम्।  तद्दातुं शीलमस्याः अस्तीति।
अनर्घ्येति दीर्घान्तपाठे आसमन्ताद्व्याप्तं यत्कैवल्यमिति वा पदम्/फलम्।
न विद्यते कैवल्यं यस्मात्तदकैवल्यमीदृशं यत्पदमिति वा पदम्/फलम्।
wordByWord: दातुं शीलम् — having the nature of giving
summary: (i) One who grants the priceless state or fruit of liberation.
(ii) Anarghya means beyond price; kaivalya is liberation.
(iii) She bestows the immeasurable fruit of absolute freedom.
searchText: 926 अनर्घ्यकैवल्यपददायिनी/अनर्घ्यकैवल्यफलदायिनी अनर्घ्यममूल्यमपरिच्छिन्नं यत्कैवल्याख्यं पदम्/फलम्।  तद्दातुं शीलमस्याः अस्तीति। अनर्घ्येति दीर्घान्तपाठे आसमन्ताद्व्याप्तं यत्कैवल्यमिति वा पदम्/फलम्। न विद्यते कैवल्यं यस्मात्तदकैवल्यमीदृशं यत्पदमिति वा पदम्/फलम्। अनर्घ्यम् — priceless कैवल्य — liberation पदम् — state फलम् — fruit दातुं शीलम् — having the nature of giving (i) one who grants the priceless state or fruit of liberation. (ii) anarghya means beyond price; kaivalya is liberation. (iii) she bestows the immeasurable fruit of absolute freedom. bhāskararāya defines the definitive ceiling of spiritual reward: the priceless gift: worldly gains have price tags and expire over time. she is padadāyinī of कैवल्य (kaivalya — absolute, unconditioned non-dual liberation). this state is anarghya (priceless) because it cannot be bought with material currency, marking the absolute end of all individual lack and limitation.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya defines the definitive ceiling of spiritual reward:

The Priceless Gift: Worldly gains have price tags and expire over time. She is Padadāyinī of कैवल्य (Kaivalya — absolute, unconditioned non-dual liberation). This state is Anarghya (priceless) because it cannot be bought with material currency, marking the absolute end of all individual lack and limitation.

name: स्तोत्रप्रिया
summary: (i) One who loves hymns of praise.
(ii) Stotra is praise offered through sacred words.
(iii) She is pleased by sincere recitation and glorification.
searchText: 927 स्तोत्रप्रिया स्तोत्रं प्रियं यस्याः सा। स्तोत्रम् — hymn of praise प्रियं — dear यस्याः सा — she whose (i) one who loves hymns of praise. (ii) stotra is praise offered through sacred words. (iii) she is pleased by sincere recitation and glorification. in the saubhāgyabhāskara, bhāskararāya clarifies the sound mechanics of praise: the resonance of realignment: she does not seek public praise to satisfy an ego. she is stotrapriyā because when a practitioner recites a hymn (stotra), the poetic metrics and focused language gather their scattered mental energies. this process realigns their subtle nervous system, making it easy for her grace to flood their consciousness.
esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara, Bhāskararāya clarifies the sound mechanics of praise:

The Resonance of Realignment: She does not seek public praise to satisfy an ego. She is Stotrapriyā because when a practitioner recites a hymn (stotra), the poetic metrics and focused language gather their scattered mental energies. This process realigns their subtle nervous system, making it easy for Her grace to flood their consciousness.

name: स्तुतिमती
summary: (i) One who is endowed with praise.
(ii) Stuti is praise; the name indicates that praise properly belongs to Her.
(iii) She is the worthy object of hymns and adoration.
searchText: 928 स्तुतिमती स्तुतिरस्यां कर्मतासम्बन्धेनास्तीति स्तुतिमती। स्तुत्या मतिश्च ईश्च यस्याः सा। स्तुतिः — praise अस्याम् — in her मतिः — thought; regard ईः — sovereignty or goddesshood (i) one who is endowed with praise. (ii) stuti is praise; the name indicates that praise properly belongs to her. (iii) she is the worthy object of hymns and adoration. bhāskararāya constructs an immediate non-dual identity equation: identity with the praise: lest a seeker think that she is an external entity who simply listens to independent words of praise, this name establishes she is the very substance of the praise itself (stuti-svarūpā). every word of adoration, every pure intent, and the capacity to praise are expressions of her own energy field.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya constructs an immediate non-dual identity equation:

Identity with the Praise: Lest a seeker think that She is an external entity who simply listens to independent words of praise, this name establishes She is the very substance of the praise itself (Stuti-svarūpā). Every word of adoration, every pure intent, and the capacity to praise are expressions of Her own energy field.

name: श्रुतिसंस्तुतवैभवा
samasa: श्रुतिभिः सम्यक् स्तुतं परिचितं वा वैभवं विभुत्वं यस्याः सा।
श्रुतिशब्दश्चतुःसंख्यापरो वा। (युगाब्धयोऽर्धाः श्रुतयश्चतस्र इति छन्दःसुधाकरात्।) तेन चतुर्धा परिचितं वैभवं यस्याः सा।
wordByWord: श्रुतिभिः — by the Shrutis or Vedas
summary: (i) One whose glory is praised by the Shrutis.
(ii) Shruti means Veda, and may also be connected with the number four.
(iii) Her greatness is fully celebrated and known through the Vedic revelation.
searchText: 929 श्रुतिसंस्तुतवैभवा श्रुतिभिः सम्यक् स्तुतं परिचितं वा वैभवं विभुत्वं यस्याः सा। श्रुतिशब्दश्चतुःसंख्यापरो वा। (युगाब्धयोऽर्धाः श्रुतयश्चतस्र इति छन्दःसुधाकरात्।) तेन चतुर्धा परिचितं वैभवं यस्याः सा। श्रुतिभिः — by the shrutis or vedas सम्यक् — well; fully स्तुतम् — praised वैभवम् — glory; greatness विभुत्वम् — sovereignty (i) one whose glory is praised by the shrutis. (ii) shruti means veda, and may also be connected with the number four. (iii) her greatness is fully celebrated and known through the vedic revelation. in the saubhāgyabhāskara, bhāskararāya details the foundational scriptural validation of her throne: the confirmed majesty: she is not a localized or modern invention. her majestic cosmic power (vaibhava) is thoroughly analyzed and validated across the ancient texts of the vedas (śrutis). bhāskararāya notes an advanced track where śruti means the numeral four: her glory is perfectly distributed across the four foundational levels of speech (parā, paśyantī, madhyamā, vaikharī).
esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara, Bhāskararāya details the foundational scriptural validation of Her throne:

The Confirmed Majesty: She is not a localized or modern invention. Her majestic cosmic power (vaibhava) is thoroughly analyzed and validated across the ancient texts of the Vedas (Śrutis). Bhāskararāya notes an advanced track where Śruti means the numeral four: Her glory is perfectly distributed across the four foundational levels of speech (Parā, Paśyantī, Madhyamā, Vaikharī).

name: मनस्विनी
summary: (i) One who is Manasvini.
(ii) Her mind stands free and independent.
(iii) She is self-willed, sovereign and endowed with supreme inner power.
searchText: 930 मनस्विनी मनोऽस्याः स्वतन्त्रतया तिष्ठतीति। मनः — mind अस्याः — of her स्वतन्त्रतया — independently तिष्ठति — stands (i) one who is manasvini. (ii) her mind stands free and independent. (iii) she is self-willed, sovereign and endowed with supreme inner power. bhāskararāya details the absolute independence of her cosmic intent: the independent will: in human beings, the mind is often pushed around by external sensory inputs and karmic impressions. she is manasvinī because her cosmic mind operates with absolute, unrestricted freedom (स्वतन्त्रतया तिष्ठति). her intent (icchā-śakti) is independent, directing the laws of nature without being bound by them.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya details the absolute independence of Her cosmic intent:

The Independent Will: In human beings, the mind is often pushed around by external sensory inputs and karmic impressions. She is Manasvinī because Her cosmic mind operates with absolute, unrestricted freedom (स्वतन्त्रतया तिष्ठति). Her intent (Icchā-śakti) is independent, directing the laws of nature without being bound by them.

name: मानवती
samasa: मानश्चित्तसमुन्नतिरादरणं वा प्रियापराधसूचिका चेष्टा वा प्रमाणं वा प्रमितिर्वास्यामस्तीति।
summary: (i) One who is Manavati.
(ii) Mana may mean honour, mental elevation, loving gesture, measure or valid knowledge.
(iii) She possesses these forms of dignity, measure and knowledge.
searchText: 931 मानवती मानश्चित्तसमुन्नतिरादरणं वा प्रियापराधसूचिका चेष्टा वा प्रमाणं वा प्रमितिर्वास्यामस्तीति। मानः — honour; measure; mental elevation चित्तसमुन्नतिः — elevation of mind आदरणम् — respect प्रमाणम् — means of knowledge प्रमितिः — valid cognition (i) one who is manavati. (ii) mana may mean honour, mental elevation, loving gesture, measure or valid knowledge. (iii) she possesses these forms of dignity, measure and knowledge. in the saubhāgyabhāskara, bhāskararāya decodes the cognitive tracking of this title: the matrix of valid knowledge: māna represents dignity, but philosophically it denotes प्रमिति (pramiti — valid, uncorrupted knowledge). she is mānavatī because she is the container and baseline source that validates all true information, allowing human intellects to comprehend reality accurately.
esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara, Bhāskararāya decodes the cognitive tracking of this title:

The Matrix of Valid Knowledge: Māna represents dignity, but philosophically it denotes प्रमिति (pramiti — valid, uncorrupted knowledge). She is Mānavatī because She is the container and baseline source that validates all true information, allowing human intellects to comprehend reality accurately.

name: महेशी
summary: (i) The consort of Mahesha.
(ii) Mahesha is the great Lord, Shiva.
(iii) She is His Shakti and inseparable consort.
searchText: 932 महेशी महेशस्य स्त्री। महेशस्य — of mahesha स्त्री — wife; consort (i) the consort of mahesha. (ii) mahesha is the great lord, shiva. (iii) she is his shakti and inseparable consort. bhāskararāya establishes her premier position over cosmic leadership: the shared throne of absolute power: maheśa is the great lord who oversees cosmic dissolution and transformation. she is his non-different dynamic energy (maheśī). she shares his supreme throne, executing the laws of nature while remaining anchored in transcendent stillness.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya establishes Her premier position over cosmic leadership:

The Shared Throne of Absolute Power: Maheśa is the great lord who oversees cosmic dissolution and transformation. She is His non-different dynamic energy (Maheśī). She shares His supreme throne, executing the laws of nature while remaining anchored in transcendent stillness.

name: मङ्गलाकृतिः
summary: (i) One whose form is auspiciousness.
(ii) Mangala means auspicious, blessed and beneficent.
(iii) Her very form and appearance are auspicious.
searchText: 933 मङ्गलाकृतिः मङ्गलरूपा आकृतिर्यस्याः सा। मङ्गल — auspiciousness रूपा — form आकृतिः — appearance (i) one whose form is auspiciousness. (ii) mangala means auspicious, blessed and beneficent. (iii) her very form and appearance are auspicious. in the saubhāgyabhāskara, bhāskararāya removes any superficial visual filters: the geometry of absolute benevolence: she is maṅgalākṛtiḥ because her very shape is composed of pure auspiciousness. her geometric forms (the śrī yantra) and her physical descriptions are not designed for simple display; they are precise energy grids meant to clear away negative currents and bring harmony to the seeker's space.
esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara, Bhāskararāya removes any superficial visual filters:

The Geometry of Absolute Benevolence: She is Maṅgalākṛtiḥ because Her very shape is composed of pure auspiciousness. Her geometric forms (the Śrī Yantra) and Her physical descriptions are not designed for simple display; they are precise energy grids meant to clear away negative currents and bring harmony to the seeker's space.

name: विश्वमाता
summary: (i) Mother of the universe.
(ii) Vishva means the world or Vishnu.
(iii) She is the mother of the whole universe and of all that pervades it.
searchText: 934 विश्वमाता विश्वस्य जगतो विष्णोर्वा माता। विश्वस्य — of the universe जगतः — of the world विष्णोः वा — or of vishnu माता — mother (i) mother of the universe. (ii) vishva means the world or vishnu. (iii) she is the mother of the whole universe and of all that pervades it. bhāskararāya details her compassionate parental priority: the universal womb: she is the viśvamātā because every single creature, element, star, and dimensional space was generated inside her womb. she watches over this macrocosmic creation with unbiased, protective care, ensuring that every soul is provided with the path inputs needed for its eventual evolution.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya details Her compassionate parental priority:

The Universal Womb: She is the Viśvamātā because every single creature, element, star, and dimensional space was generated inside Her womb. She watches over this macrocosmic creation with unbiased, protective care, ensuring that every soul is provided with the path inputs needed for its eventual evolution.

name: जगद्धात्री
wordByWord: भूरूपेण — in the form of the earth
summary: (i) Supporter of the universe.
(ii) She bears the world in the form of the earth.
(iii) The universe is sustained by Her supporting power.
searchText: 935 जगद्धात्री जगद्धत्ते भूरूपेणेति जगद्धात्री। जगत् — the universe धत्ते — holds; supports भूरूपेण — in the form of the earth धात्री — supporter; bearer (i) supporter of the universe. (ii) she bears the world in the form of the earth. (iii) the universe is sustained by her supporting power. in the saubhāgyabhāskara, bhāskararāya details the physics of cosmic support: the supporting earth substratum: the world does not float by chance or hang unsupported. she is jagaddhātrī because she manifests directly as the grounding force—exemplified by the stabilizing element of the earth (भूरूपेण) — that holds the weight of living beings, protecting them from structural collapse.
esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara, Bhāskararāya details the physics of cosmic support:

The Supporting Earth Substratum: The world does not float by chance or hang unsupported. She is Jagaddhātrī because She manifests directly as the grounding force—exemplified by the stabilizing element of the Earth (भूरूपेण) — that holds the weight of living beings, protecting them from structural collapse.

name: विशालाक्षी
summary: (i) One with large eyes.
(ii) Her eyes are wide and expansive.
(iii) The name may also indicate that the great sacred seat is the place of Her vision.
searchText: 936 विशालाक्षी विशाले विस्तीर्णे अक्षिणी यस्याः सा। विशालपीठमेवाक्षिस्थानं यस्याः सा। विशाले — large; expansive विस्तीर्णे — wide अक्षिणी — eyes विशालपीठम् — the vishala sacred seat अक्षिस्थानम् — eye-place (i) one with large eyes. (ii) her eyes are wide and expansive. (iii) the name may also indicate that the great sacred seat is the place of her vision. bhāskararāya decodes the absolute scale of her vision and sacred geography: the wide gaze of infinite awareness: her eyes are described as viṣāla (immense) because her vision is completely unbounded. she tracks the entire universe at a single glance. geographically, she is non-different from the primary deity विशालाक्षी (viśālākṣī) presiding over the sacred city of kashi (varanasi), marking a key energy point on the earth grid.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya decodes the absolute scale of Her vision and sacred geography:

The Wide Gaze of Infinite Awareness: Her eyes are described as viṣāla (immense) because Her vision is completely unbounded. She tracks the entire universe at a single glance. Geographically, She is non-different from the primary deity विशालाक्षी (Viśālākṣī) presiding over the sacred city of Kashi (Varanasi), marking a key energy point on the earth grid.

name: विरागिणी
summary: (i) One who is detached.
(ii) Viraga means dispassion or freedom from attachment.
(iii) Though She governs all, She remains untouched and free from worldly craving.
searchText: 937 विरागिणी विरागो वैराग्यमस्या अस्तीति। विरागः — detachment वैराग्यम् — dispassion अस्याः — of her अस्तीति — is (i) one who is detached. (ii) viraga means dispassion or freedom from attachment. (iii) though she governs all, she remains untouched and free from worldly craving. in the saubhāgyabhāskara, bhāskararāya details her freedom from sensory attachment: the detached ruler of pleasures: though she manifests as the source of all cosmic attraction and play (līlā), her essential unconditioned nature remains completely detached (vairāgya). she does not cling to projects or seek external gratification, standing as a pristine template of dispassion that guides the seeker away from emotional dependency.
esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara, Bhāskararāya details Her freedom from sensory attachment:

The Detached Ruler of Pleasures: Though She manifests as the source of all cosmic attraction and play (Līlā), Her essential unconditioned nature remains completely detached (vairāgya). She does not cling to projects or seek external gratification, standing as a pristine template of dispassion that guides the seeker away from emotional dependency.

name: प्रगल्भा
wordByWord: सृष्ट्यादिकर्मसु — in creation and other functions
प्रौढत्वात् — because of maturity or greatness
summary: (i) One who is mature and bold.
(ii) Pragalbha means fully capable or expert.
(iii) She is supremely competent in the acts of creation and the other cosmic functions.
searchText: 938 प्रगल्भा सृष्ट्यादिकर्मसु प्रौढत्वात्प्रगल्भा। सृष्ट्यादिकर्मसु — in creation and other functions प्रौढत्वात् — because of maturity or greatness प्रगल्भा — bold; competent (i) one who is mature and bold. (ii) pragalbha means fully capable or expert. (iii) she is supremely competent in the acts of creation and the other cosmic functions. bhāskararāya details her supreme competence during cosmic creation: the master executive: pragalbhā describes an expert who operates with absolute confidence and maturity (prauḍhatva), completely free from hesitation. she is pragalbhā because she executes the massive, complex processes of universal creation, preservation, and dissolution effortlessly, demonstrating absolute operational mastery.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya details Her supreme competence during cosmic creation:

The Master Executive: Pragalbhā describes an expert who operates with absolute confidence and maturity (prauḍhatva), completely free from hesitation. She is Pragalbhā because She executes the massive, complex processes of universal creation, preservation, and dissolution effortlessly, demonstrating absolute operational mastery.

name: परमोदारा/अपरमोदारा
samasa: परमा च सोदारा च महती च। (उदारो दातृमहतोरित्यमरः।)
परमाण्युदानि जलानि यस्मिन्स परमोदः समुद्रः, स च प्रकृते भवसमुद्ररूपः, तस्यारा आयुधविशेषो यस्याः सा।
अपगता रमा येषां तेऽपरमा दरिद्रास्तेभ्य उदारा ऐश्वर्यप्रदेति।
summary: (i) One who is supremely generous, or who is generous to those deprived of wealth.
(ii) Paramodara is explained as great, noble, and giver of supreme joy; it may also refer to the ocean of worldly existence and Her special weapon connected with it.
(iii) Aparamodara is explained as the one who grants prosperity to the poor and destitute.
searchText: 939 परमोदारा/अपरमोदारा परमोदारा। परमा च सोदारा च महती च। (उदारो दातृमहतोरित्यमरः।) परं प्रकृष्टं मोदमानन्दमासमन्ताद्रातीति। परमाण्युदानि जलानि यस्मिन्स परमोदः समुद्रः, स च प्रकृते भवसमुद्ररूपः, तस्यारा आयुधविशेषो यस्याः सा। अपरमोदारा। अपगता रमा येषां तेऽपरमा दरिद्रास्तेभ्य उदारा ऐश्वर्यप्रदेति। परमा — supreme उदारा — generous; noble महतī — great मोदम् — joy आनन्दम् — bliss दरिद्राः — poor people ऐश्वर्यप्रदा — giver of prosperity (i) one who is supremely generous, or who is generous to those deprived of wealth. (ii) paramodara is explained as great, noble, and giver of supreme joy; it may also refer to the ocean of worldly existence and her special weapon connected with it. (iii) aparamodara is explained as the one who grants prosperity to the poor and destitute. in the saubhāgyabhāskara, bhāskararāya maps the boundless scale of her giving: the sovereign giver: she is paramodārā because her generosity (udāratva) knows no bounds. she dispenses the highest, most refined bliss (paramam modam) to advanced ascetics. when read as aparamodārā, it highlights her active compassion toward the materially or spiritually destitute (aparama), flooding their lives with sudden abundance to balance their path.
esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara, Bhāskararāya maps the boundless scale of Her giving:

The Sovereign Giver: She is Paramodārā because Her generosity (udāratva) knows no bounds. She dispenses the highest, most refined bliss (paramam modam) to advanced ascetics. When read as Aparamodārā, it highlights Her active compassion toward the materially or spiritually destitute (aparama), flooding their lives with sudden abundance to balance their path.

name: परामोदा
samasa: पर उत्कृष्ट आमोदः परिमलः कीर्तिः यस्याः सा।
summary: (i) One whose fragrance, fame or delight is supreme.
(ii) Para-amoda means excellent fragrance or renown; it also means one whose joy is all-pervading.
(iii) She spreads supreme delight and auspicious fame everywhere.
searchText: 940 परामोदा पर उत्कृष्ट आमोदः परिमलः कीर्तिः यस्याः सा। आ समन्तान्मोदः सन्तोषो यस्याः सा। परः — excellent; supreme आमोदः — fragrance; delight; fame परिमलः — fragrance कीर्तिः — fame सन्तोषः — satisfaction; joy (i) one whose fragrance, fame or delight is supreme. (ii) para-amoda means excellent fragrance or renown; it also means one whose joy is all-pervading. (iii) she spreads supreme delight and auspicious fame everywhere. bhāskararāya tracks the sensory and spiritual fragrance of the absolute: the fragrance of pure consciousness: āmoda refers to a rich, captivating fragrance that spreads automatically. she is parāmodā because her spiritual presence acts as the ultimate fragrance, sweetening the internal channels of anyone who remembers her. concurrently, she rests in a state of continuous, shoreless satisfaction and delight (moda), free from internal friction.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya tracks the sensory and spiritual fragrance of the absolute:

The Fragrance of Pure Consciousness: Āmoda refers to a rich, captivating fragrance that spreads automatically. She is Parāmodā because Her spiritual presence acts as the ultimate fragrance, sweetening the internal channels of anyone who remembers Her. Concurrently, She rests in a state of continuous, shoreless satisfaction and delight (moda), free from internal friction.

name: मनोमयी
samasa: मनःप्रधाना। (मनसैवानुद्रष्टव्य इति स्वज्ञाने जननीये मनस एव प्राधान्यात्)
summary: (i) One who has the mind itself as Her form.
(ii) The Maha Vasishta Ramayana says that Bhairava, Shiva, is Chidakasha, and His own active energy, Spanda Shakti, is known as Manomayi.
(iii) The Brhadaranyaka Upanishad says, “By mind alone it is to be seen.” Thus the mind is the chief instrument in creating self-knowledge.
searchText: 941 मनोमयी शुद्धस्य ब्रह्मणो मनःस्थानीयत्वात्। मनःप्रधाना। (मनसैवानुद्रष्टव्य इति स्वज्ञाने जननीये मनस एव प्राधान्यात्) शुद्धस्य — of the pure मनोमयी। — component of the name (i) one who has the mind itself as her form. (ii) the maha vasishta ramayana says that bhairava, shiva, is chidakasha, and his own active energy, spanda shakti, is known as manomayi. (iii) the brhadaranyaka upanishad says, “by mind alone it is to be seen.” thus the mind is the chief instrument in creating self-knowledge. in the saubhāgyabhāskara, bhāskararāya anchors this within the cognitive physics of realization: the gateway of the refined mind: the scripture states, "manasaivānudraṣṭavyam" ("through the mind alone is the self to be realized"). while the absolute absolute is past the senses, it is discovered when the mind becomes perfectly clear, still, and reflective. she is manomayī because she acts as the very substance of this highly purified, refined intellect, functioning as the direct gateway to non-dual awakening.
esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara, Bhāskararāya anchors this within the cognitive physics of realization:

The Gateway of the Refined Mind: The scripture states, "Manasaivānudraṣṭavyam" ("Through the mind alone is the Self to be realized"). While the absolute absolute is past the senses, it is discovered when the mind becomes perfectly clear, still, and reflective. She is Manomayī because She acts as the very substance of this highly purified, refined intellect, functioning as the direct gateway to non-dual awakening.

name: व्योमकेशी
summary: (i) One whose hair is the form of ether, that is, the form of Virat.
(ii) Or She is the consort of Vyomakesha, Shiva. Since the directions are said to be His wives, She may be considered as the form of the directions.
(iii) Vyoma means ether. With the suffix ka it indicates small ether; She is present there also. Thus She is omnipresent.
searchText: 942 व्योमकेशी व्योमैव केशा यस्या विराड्रूपायाः सा। व्योमकेशस्य शिवस्य स्त्री। शिवस्य — of shiva व्योमकेशी। — component of the name (i) one whose hair is the form of ether, that is, the form of virat. (ii) or she is the consort of vyomakesha, shiva. since the directions are said to be his wives, she may be considered as the form of the directions. (iii) vyoma means ether. with the suffix ka it indicates small ether; she is present there also. thus she is omnipresent. bhāskararāya details the macrocosmic scale of her body configuration: the canopy of space: in her macrocosmic manifest body (virāṭ-rūpa), the entire open blue sky and cosmic space elements are nothing but the single strands of her loose hair (keśa). this imagery shows her vast containment of the planets. as the consort of shiva who bears the matted hair of space, she coordinates the cosmic fields with perfect balance.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya details the macrocosmic scale of Her body configuration:

The Canopy of Space: In Her macrocosmic manifest body (Virāṭ-rūpa), the entire open blue sky and cosmic space elements are nothing but the single strands of Her loose hair (keśa). This imagery shows Her vast containment of the planets. As the consort of Shiva who bears the matted hair of space, She coordinates the cosmic fields with perfect balance.

name: विमानस्था
samasa: विशेषेण मान आदरणे स्था स्थितिर्यस्याः सा।
विशिष्टा मा कान्तिर्यस्य तद्विमं तादृशं अनः शकटं किरिचक्रादिरूपो रथस्तत्र तिष्ठतीति।
विगतं मानं परिमाणं यस्य तदपरिच्छिन्नं ब्रह्म तन्निष्ठा।
माने परिमाणे स्था स्थितिः परिमाणाविच्छिन्नता विगता यस्याः सा।
विशिष्य माति निष्कृष्य प्रमां जनयतीति विमानो वेदस्तत्र  प्रतिपाद्यतया तिष्ठतीति वा।
विशिष्टेषु वेदाविरुद्धेषु मानेषु प्रमाणेषु धर्मब्रह्मरूपेण तिष्ठतीति।
विशिष्य मानं पुराणन्यायमीमांसेत्यादिना तद्व्यक्तित्वेन रूपेण परिगणनं येषां तेषु चतुर्दशस्वेव विद्याधर्मस्थानेषु तिष्ठतीति।
summary: (i) One who is no different from the Devas in the celestial chariot or flights.
(ii) She is omniscient and supports Her devotees with great interest.

(iii) She is present in the chariots such as Geya Chakra, Kiri Chakra and Chakra Raja, in the immeasurable Brahman, in the Vedas, in valid evidences not opposed to the Vedas, and in the fourteen Vidyas.
searchText: 943 विमानस्था विमानो व्योमयानं तत्र तिष्ठतीति। विशेषेण मान आदरणे स्था स्थितिर्यस्याः सा। विशिष्टा मा कान्तिर्यस्य तद्विमं तादृशं अनः शकटं किरिचक्रादिरूपो रथस्तत्र तिष्ठतीति। विगतं मानं परिमाणं यस्य तदपरिच्छिन्नं ब्रह्म तन्निष्ठा। माने परिमाणे स्था स्थितिः परिमाणाविच्छिन्नता विगता यस्याः सा। विशिष्य माति निष्कृष्य प्रमां जनयतीति विमानो वेदस्तत्र  प्रतिपाद्यतया तिष्ठतीति वा। विशिष्टेषु वेदाविरुद्धेषु मानेषु प्रमाणेषु धर्मब्रह्मरूपेण तिष्ठतीति। विशिष्य मानं पुराणन्यायमीमांसेत्यादिना तद्व्यक्तित्वेन रूपेण परिगणनं येषां तेषु चतुर्दशस्वेव विद्याधर्मस्थानेषु तिष्ठतीति। विमानस्था। — component of the name (i) one who is no different from the devas in the celestial chariot or flights. (ii) she is omniscient and supports her devotees with great interest. (iii) she is present in the chariots such as geya chakra, kiri chakra and chakra raja, in the immeasurable brahman, in the vedas, in valid evidences not opposed to the vedas, and in the fourteen vidyas. in the saubhāgyabhāskara, bhāskararāya delivers a deep vedāntic analysis: established in the unmeasurable: while human eyes map her onto a celestial vehicle (vimāna), the high non-dual interpretation reveals that she is vimānasthā because she stands permanently anchored within that absolute brahman which is completely free from measurement or limitation (विगतं मानं यस्य). concurrently, she resides at the core of the vedas (vimāna) as the ultimate truth they reveal.
esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara, Bhāskararāya delivers a deep Vedāntic analysis:

Established in the Unmeasurable: While human eyes map Her onto a celestial vehicle (vimāna), the high non-dual interpretation reveals that She is Vimānasthā because She stands permanently anchored within that absolute Brahman which is completely free from measurement or limitation (विगतं मानं यस्य). Concurrently, She resides at the core of the Vedas (Vimāna) as the ultimate truth they reveal.

name: वज्रिणी
samasa: महद्भयं वज्रमुद्यतमिति श्रुतौ वज्रपदस्य ब्रह्मपरत्वात् परिच्छेदकत्वसम्बन्धेन तद्वती।
summary: (i) One who is the consort of Indra, the possessor of the Vajra.
(ii) Or one who has the thunderbolt in hand.
(iii) Or one adorned with diamond-like gems. The Katha Upanishad uses Vajra in the sense of Brahman, and She is related to Brahman as its limiting power.
searchText: 944 वज्रिणी वज्रिण इन्द्रस्य स्त्री। वज्रम् अस्याः अस्तीति। वज्राख्यरत्नैर्भूषिता। महद्भयं वज्रमुद्यतमिति श्रुतौ वज्रपदस्य ब्रह्मपरत्वात् परिच्छेदकत्वसम्बन्धेन तद्वती। इन्द्रस्य — of indra वज्रिणी। — component of the name (i) one who is the consort of indra, the possessor of the vajra. (ii) or one who has the thunderbolt in hand. (iii) or one adorned with diamond-like gems. the katha upanishad uses vajra in the sense of brahman, and she is related to brahman as its limiting power. bhāskararāya links this directly to the profound structural declarations of the śruti: the awesome power of brahman: the kaṭha upaniṣad states, "mahad bhayaṁ vajram udyatam" ("the absolute is a great, raised thunderbolt"). this image denotes the unyielding, unchangeable laws of nature that ensure cosmic order. she is vajriṇī because she is the living embodiment of this unyielding law, holding the ultimate diamond power to shatter egoic deviations.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya links this directly to the profound structural declarations of the Śruti:

The Awesome Power of Brahman: The Kaṭha Upaniṣad states, "Mahad bhayaṁ vajram udyatam" ("The absolute is a great, raised thunderbolt"). This image denotes the unyielding, unchangeable laws of nature that ensure cosmic order. She is Vajriṇī because She is the living embodiment of this unyielding law, holding the ultimate diamond power to shatter egoic deviations.

name: वामकेश्वरी
samasa: वामा वाममार्गरताः। त एव कुत्सिता इति वामकाः।
summary: (i) One who is in the form of the Vamakeshvara Tantra.
(ii) This tantra describes the worship of Sree Devee, and Sree Bhaskararya’s Sethu Bandha is connected with its Nityashodashikarnava portion.
(iii) She is the head of Vamaka worshippers, and also of those creators such as Daksha who bring forth the world.
searchText: 945 वामकेश्वरी वामकेश्वरतन्त्ररूपा। वामकस्य तन्त्रस्य प्रतिपाद्या। वामकानामीश्वरी। वामा वाममार्गरताः। त एव कुत्सिता इति वामकाः। वमन्ति जगत्सृजन्तीति वा वामका दक्षाद्याः। वामकेश्वरी। — component of the name (i) one who is in the form of the vamakeshvara tantra. (ii) this tantra describes the worship of sree devee, and sree bhaskararya’s sethu bandha is connected with its nityashodashikarnava portion. (iii) she is the head of vamaka worshippers, and also of those creators such as daksha who bring forth the world. in the saubhāgyabhāskara, bhāskararāya tracks her authority across the core text of the lineage: the soul of the core tantra: she is the living embodiment and primary target of the highly sacred वामकेश्वर तन्त्र (vāmakeśvara tantra), which contains the secret geometry and mantra formulas of śrīvidyā. under the operational key, she acts as the supreme queen (īśvarī) governing those radical practitioners who follow the left-hand path (vāmakas), using intense methods to realign their awareness.
esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara, Bhāskararāya tracks Her authority across the core text of the lineage:

The Soul of the Core Tantra: She is the living embodiment and primary target of the highly sacred वामकेश्वर तन्त्र (Vāmakeśvara Tantra), which contains the secret geometry and mantra formulas of Śrīvidyā. Under the operational key, She acts as the supreme queen (Īśvarī) governing those radical practitioners who follow the left-hand path (Vāmakas), using intense methods to realign their awareness.

name: पञ्चयज्ञप्रिया
samasa: पञ्चसंख्याका यज्ञाः पञ्चयज्ञाः। ते प्रिया यस्याः सा।
summary: (i) One who loves the five kinds of sacrifices.

(ii) Different scriptures list the five sacrifices differently: Vedic sacrifices, Smriti sacrifices, Pancharatra practices, Kulagama forms, Nitya Tantra forms, and the Agnihotra-related five offerings.
(iii) She is fond of all these forms of yajna.
searchText: 946 पञ्चयज्ञप्रिया पञ्चसंख्याका यज्ञाः पञ्चयज्ञाः। ते प्रिया यस्याः सा। यज्ञाः — sacrifices प्रिया — dear यस्याः — whose (i) one who loves the five kinds of sacrifices. (ii) different scriptures list the five sacrifices differently: vedic sacrifices, smriti sacrifices, pancharatra practices, kulagama forms, nitya tantra forms, and the agnihotra-related five offerings. (iii) she is fond of all these forms of yajna. bhāskararāya details the daily alignment of energy: the harmony of daily sacrifice: the scripture mandates the pañca-mahā-yajñas (sacrifice to the absolute, to ancestors, to deities, to humans, and to animals) to clear existential debts and balance the earth space. she is pañcayajñapriyā because this daily practice keeps human activity in harmony with the cosmos, turning routine life into a field of pure devotion.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya details the daily alignment of energy:

The Harmony of Daily Sacrifice: The scripture mandates the Pañca-Mahā-Yajñas (sacrifice to the absolute, to ancestors, to deities, to humans, and to animals) to clear existential debts and balance the earth space. She is Pañcayajñapriyā because this daily practice keeps human activity in harmony with the cosmos, turning routine life into a field of pure devotion.

name: पञ्चप्रेतमञ्चाधिशायिनी
samasa: ब्रह्मादयश्चत्वारः प्रेताः पादाः। सदाशिवाख्यः प्रेतः फलकमेतादृशं मञ्चं पर्यङ्कमधिशेते।
summary: (i) One who reclines on a couch made of five corpses.

(ii) To show that She is greater than the five cosmic functions, the performers of those functions are treated as the legs and plank of Her couch. Brahma, Vishnu, Rudra and Eshvara are the four legs, and Sadashiva is the plank or sheet.
(iii) Without their Shaktis they cannot act; therefore they are described as corpses. This bed is praised in Bhairavayamala, Lalita Stavaratna, Soundaryalahari, Rudrayamala and other texts.
searchText: 947 पञ्चप्रेतमञ्चाधिशायिनी ब्रह्मादयश्चत्वारः प्रेताः पादाः। सदाशिवाख्यः प्रेतः फलकमेतादृशं मञ्चं पर्यङ्कमधिशेते। प्रेताः — corpses पञ्चप्रेतमञ्चाधिशायिनी। — component of the name (i) one who reclines on a couch made of five corpses. (ii) to show that she is greater than the five cosmic functions, the performers of those functions are treated as the legs and plank of her couch. brahma, vishnu, rudra and eshvara are the four legs, and sadashiva is the plank or sheet. (iii) without their shaktis they cannot act; therefore they are described as corpses. this bed is praised in bhairavayamala, lalita stavaratna, soundaryalahari, rudrayamala and other texts. in the saubhāgyabhāskara, bhāskararāya delivers one of the most celebrated and profound iconographic analyses of the tradition: the supreme throne past the five functions: the couch where she rests at the center of the royal chamber is constructed out of the five cosmic administrators: ब्रह्मा (creation), विष्णु (preservation), रुद्र (dissolution), and ईश्वर (concealment) serve as the four vertical legs, while सदाशिव (grace) forms the flat seating plank. without her dynamic energy field, these five deities are inert like corpses (preta). she sits above them, demonstrating her status as the supreme, non-dual source that gives life to cosmic functions.
esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara, Bhāskararāya delivers one of the most celebrated and profound iconographic analyses of the tradition:

The Supreme Throne past the Five Functions: The couch where She rests at the center of the royal chamber is constructed out of the five cosmic administrators: ब्रह्मा (Creation), विष्णु (Preservation), रुद्र (Dissolution), and ईश्वर (Concealment) serve as the four vertical legs, while सदाशिव (Grace) forms the flat seating plank. Without Her dynamic energy field, these five deities are inert like corpses (preta). She sits above them, demonstrating Her status as the supreme, non-dual source that gives life to cosmic functions.

name: पञ्चमी
summary: (i) One who is the consort of Sadashiva.
(ii) Brahma, Vishnu, Rudra, Eshvara and Sadashiva are described together; She is the Shakti of the fifth, Sadashiva.
(iii) The name also relates to the fifth principle among the five.
searchText: 948 पञ्चमी ब्रह्मादिषु पञ्चसु पञ्चमस्य सदाशिवस्य स्त्री। मकारेषु पञ्चमस्यानन्दरूपत्वात्तद्रूपा। पञ्चानां मानां मकाराणां समाहारः। पञ्चमी। — component of the name (i) one who is the consort of sadashiva. (ii) brahma, vishnu, rudra, eshvara and sadashiva are described together; she is the shakti of the fifth, sadashiva. (iii) the name also relates to the fifth principle among the five. bhāskararāya details the structural placement of her energy: the power of the fifth order: within the administrative framework, she is pañcamī because she is the dynamic consort of the absolute fifth principle—sadāśiva (the lord of grace). in the specialized vocabulary of agamic practice, she represents the critical fifth element—आनन्द (ananda — supreme non-dual bliss) — which functions as the crown energy that completes the purification process.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya details the structural placement of Her energy:

The Power of the Fifth Order: Within the administrative framework, She is Pañcamī because She is the dynamic consort of the absolute fifth principle—Sadāśiva (the Lord of Grace). In the specialized vocabulary of Agamic practice, She represents the critical fifth element—आनन्द (Ananda — supreme non-dual bliss) — which functions as the crown energy that completes the purification process.

name: पञ्चभूतेशी
summary: (i) One who is the ruler of the five elements.
(ii) The five elements are earth, water, fire, air and ether.
(iii) She is their presiding power and inner controller.
searchText: 949 पञ्चभूतेशी पञ्चसंख्यानां भूतानां पृथिव्यादीनामीशी। पञ्चभूतेशी। — component of the name (i) one who is the ruler of the five elements. (ii) the five elements are earth, water, fire, air and ether. (iii) she is their presiding power and inner controller. in the saubhāgyabhāskara, bhāskararāya details her absolute legislative rule over physical matter: the governor of the material grid: the entire physical universe is constructed out of the combinations of the five gross elements (pañca-mahābhūta). she is the absolute īśvarī (empress) who designs and governs their laws, formatting how gravity, fluid dynamics, heat exchange, atmospheric friction, and spatial containment operate across the macrocosmic grid.
esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara, Bhāskararāya details Her absolute legislative rule over physical matter:

The Governor of the Material Grid: The entire physical universe is constructed out of the combinations of the five gross elements (pañca-mahābhūta). She is the absolute Īśvarī (empress) who designs and governs their laws, formatting how gravity, fluid dynamics, heat exchange, atmospheric friction, and spatial containment operate across the macrocosmic grid.

name: पञ्चसंख्योपचारिणी
summary: (i) One who is worshipped with five upacharas.
(ii) The five forms of worship are connected with the elements and with the prescribed offerings.
(iii) She is pleased by worship performed through these five modes.
searchText: 950 पञ्चसंख्योपचारिणी पञ्चसंख्या उपचारा अस्याः अस्तीति। पञ्चसंख्योपचारिणी। — component of the name (i) one who is worshipped with five upacharas. (ii) the five forms of worship are connected with the elements and with the prescribed offerings. (iii) she is pleased by worship performed through these five modes. bhāskararāya concludes this major numerical sequence by mapping the elemental alignment of ritual worship: the cosmos offered back to the source: the five primary ritual offerings—गन्ध (sandalwood paste), पुष्प (flowers), धूप (incense), दीप (light), and नैवेद्य (food)—are not simple external objects. in the internal alignment matrix, they correspond perfectly to the five elements: earth, space, air, fire, and water. she is pañcasaṅkhyopacāriṇī because when a practitioner performs this five-fold worship (pañcopacāra), they are systematically offering the entire element fabric of the cosmos back into her non-dual light, completing the loop of realization.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya concludes this major numerical sequence by mapping the elemental alignment of ritual worship:

The Cosmos Offered Back to the Source: The five primary ritual offerings—गन्ध (Sandalwood paste), पुष्प (Flowers), धूप (Incense), दीप (Light), and नैवेद्य (Food)—are not simple external objects. In the internal alignment matrix, they correspond perfectly to the five elements: Earth, Space, Air, Fire, and Water. She is Pañcasaṅkhyopacāriṇī because when a practitioner performs this five-fold worship (pañcopacāra), they are systematically offering the entire element fabric of the cosmos back into Her non-dual light, completing the loop of realization.

name: शाश्वती
summary: (i) One who is eternal.
(ii) Shashvati also indicates that which is repeatedly practised or remembered.
(iii) She is ever-existing and worthy of repeated contemplation.
searchText: 951 शाश्वती शश्वत् पौनःपुन्यं तत्सम्बन्धिनी पुनःपुनरभ्यस्यमानेत्यर्थः। नित्या। शाश्वती। — component of the name (i) one who is eternal. (ii) shashvati also indicates that which is repeatedly practised or remembered. (iii) she is ever-existing and worthy of repeated contemplation. in the saubhāgyabhāskara, bhāskararāya details the temporal stability of the absolute: the continuous awakening: she is śāśvatī because her existence is completely unaffected by linear time modifications. under the practice track, she is the continuous phonic presence discovered through repetitive, rhythmic recitation (pounahpunya), remaining as the permanent substrate behind transient thoughts.
esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara, Bhāskararāya details the temporal stability of the absolute:

The Continuous Awakening: She is Śāśvatī because Her existence is completely unaffected by linear time modifications. Under the practice track, She is the continuous phonic presence discovered through repetitive, rhythmic recitation (pounahpunya), remaining as the permanent substrate behind transient thoughts.

name: शाश्वतैश्वर्या
summary: (i) One whose sovereignty is eternal.
(ii) Her lordship is not temporary or dependent.
(iii) It may be compared with the earlier description of the Panchapreta couch, where all divine powers depend on Her.
searchText: 952 शाश्वतैश्वर्या शाश्वतं नित्यमैश्वर्यं यस्याः सा। शाश्वतं — eternal यस्याः — whose शाश्वतैश्वर्या। — component of the name (i) one whose sovereignty is eternal. (ii) her lordship is not temporary or dependent. (iii) it may be compared with the earlier description of the panchapreta couch, where all divine powers depend on her. bhāskararāya contrasts her imperial throne with the localized power grids of the universe: unbounded sovereignty: earthly rulers and secondary deities hold conditional power (aiśvarya) that updates or expires based on cosmic cycles. she is śāśvataiśvaryā because her executive legislative capacity is uncreated and permanent, running the matrices of creation without external authorization.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya contrasts Her imperial throne with the localized power grids of the universe:

Unbounded Sovereignty: Earthly rulers and secondary deities hold conditional power (aiśvarya) that updates or expires based on cosmic cycles. She is Śāśvataiśvaryā because Her executive legislative capacity is uncreated and permanent, running the matrices of creation without external authorization.

name: शर्मदा
summary: (i) One who gives happiness.
(ii) Sharma means happiness, comfort or blessedness.
(iii) She bestows such happiness on Her devotees.
searchText: 953 शर्मदा शर्म सुखं दत्ते। शर्म — happiness शर्मदा। — component of the name (i) one who gives happiness. (ii) sharma means happiness, comfort or blessedness. (iii) she bestows such happiness on her devotees. in the saubhāgyabhāskara, bhāskararāya clarifies the tier of pleasure delivered: the rest of the soul: śarma does not signify localized, passing sensory excitement. it refers to the absolute, unconditioned peace and comfort of resting within the true self (svarūpānanda). she acts as the direct channel that dispenses this baseline rest, terminating individual lack.
esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara, Bhāskararāya clarifies the tier of pleasure delivered:

The Rest of the Soul: Śarma does not signify localized, passing sensory excitement. It refers to the absolute, unconditioned peace and comfort of resting within the true Self (Svarūpānanda). She acts as the direct channel that dispenses this baseline rest, terminating individual lack.

name: शम्भुमोहिनी
summary: (i) One who enchants Shambhu.
(ii) Even Shiva, the auspicious Lord, is attracted by Her beauty and power.
(iii) This name praises Her power to delight and enchant Shambhu Himself.
searchText: 954 शम्भुमोहिनी शम्भुं मोहयतीति। शम्भुमोहिनी। — component of the name (i) one who enchants shambhu. (ii) even shiva, the auspicious lord, is attracted by her beauty and power. (iii) this name praises her power to delight and enchant shambhu himself. bhāskararāya decodes the non-dual mechanics of cosmic manifestation: the enchantment of stillness: lord śiva as śambhu is the still, self-contained, and unmanifest absolute. he would remain permanently unmoving without an internal stir. she is śambhumohinī because her dynamic, enchanting energy field charms him into self-awareness, drawing him out to witness the unfolding of the multiverse.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya decodes the non-dual mechanics of cosmic manifestation:

The Enchantment of Stillness: Lord Śiva as Śambhu is the still, self-contained, and unmanifest absolute. He would remain permanently unmoving without an internal stir. She is Śambhumohinī because Her dynamic, enchanting energy field charms Him into self-awareness, drawing Him out to witness the unfolding of the multiverse.

name: धरा
summary: (i) One who is Earth.
(ii) Dhara also means one who bears or supports.
(iii) She supports the whole universe as the earth supports all beings.
searchText: 955 धरा धरा पृथ्वीस्वरूपा। सर्वस्य जगतो धारणात्। धरा — earth; supporter धरा। — component of the name (i) one who is earth. (ii) dhara also means one who bears or supports. (iii) she supports the whole universe as the earth supports all beings. in the saubhāgyabhāskara, bhāskararāya details the foundational physics of systemic containment: the absolute support: she is dharā because she is the structural capacity that bears and anchors the entire manifest universe (jagat). materially, she manifests as the foundational element of earth (pṛthivī), providing the stabilizing ground metric that supports living things.
esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara, Bhāskararāya details the foundational physics of systemic containment:

The Absolute Support: She is Dharā because She is the structural capacity that bears and anchors the entire manifest universe (jagat). Materially, She manifests as the foundational element of Earth (Pṛthivī), providing the stabilizing ground metric that supports living things.

name: धरसुता
summary: (i) One who is the daughter of the mountain.
(ii) Dhara is the mountain, especially Himavan.
(iii) She is praised as the daughter of the mountain king.
searchText: 956 धरसुता धरस्य हिमवत्पर्वतस्य सुता। धरसुता। — component of the name (i) one who is the daughter of the mountain. (ii) dhara is the mountain, especially himavan. (iii) she is praised as the daughter of the mountain king. bhāskararāya links structural physical biography to cosmic solidity: the offspring of solidity: mythologically, she takes birth as pārvatī, the daughter of the stable mountain king. metaphysically, a mountain represents unmoving structural stability, internal concentration, and spiritual altitude. by emerging as dharasutā, she confirms that her energy forms the peak of absolute, unbending meditation.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya links structural physical biography to cosmic solidity:

The Offspring of Solidity: Mythologically, She takes birth as Pārvatī, the daughter of the stable mountain king. Metaphysically, a mountain represents unmoving structural stability, internal concentration, and spiritual altitude. By emerging as Dharasutā, She confirms that Her energy forms the peak of absolute, unbending meditation.

name: धन्या
summary: (i) One who is blessed and fulfilled.
(ii) Dhanya also means one who is beneficial for wealth, one who obtains wealth, a particular Yogini, and a form of meditation.
(iii) She is the auspicious and fulfilled one who grants blessedness.
searchText: 957 धन्या कृतार्था। धनाय हिता। धनं लब्ध्री। योगिनीविशेषरूपा। धन्याख्यध्यानरूपा। धन्या। — component of the name (i) one who is blessed and fulfilled. (ii) dhanya also means one who is beneficial for wealth, one who obtains wealth, a particular yogini, and a form of meditation. (iii) she is the auspicious and fulfilled one who grants blessedness. in the saubhāgyabhāskara, bhāskararāya maps the parameters of spiritual fulfillment: the state of perfect completion: she is dhanyā because she exists in a state of absolute, self-contained fulfillment (kṛtārthā), lacking zero internal assets. yogically, she manifests as a specialized meditative light state (dhanyā-dhyāna) that clears mental fog, aligning the intellect with pristine metrics.
esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara, Bhāskararāya maps the parameters of spiritual fulfillment:

The State of Perfect Completion: She is Dhanyā because She exists in a state of absolute, self-contained fulfillment (kṛtārthā), lacking zero internal assets. Yogically, She manifests as a specialized meditative light state (dhanyā-dhyāna) that clears mental fog, aligning the intellect with pristine metrics.

name: धर्मिणी
summary: (i) One who possesses dharma.
(ii) Dharma is Her nature and conduct.
(iii) She embodies righteousness and sacred order.
searchText: 958 धर्मिणी धर्मः शीलमस्याः अस्तीति। धर्मः — dharma; righteousness धर्मिणी। — component of the name (i) one who possesses dharma. (ii) dharma is her nature and conduct. (iii) she embodies righteousness and sacred order. bhāskararāya anchors virtue directly within ontological identity: the embodiment of righteousness: righteousness (dharma) is not an external code of conduct she follows out of duty. she is dharmiṇī because moral order, universal justice, and structural integrity constitute her baseline nature and character (śīla). she is the law itself.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya anchors virtue directly within ontological identity:

The Embodiment of Righteousness: Righteousness (Dharma) is not an external code of conduct She follows out of duty. She is Dharmiṇī because moral order, universal justice, and structural integrity constitute Her baseline nature and character (śīla). She is the law itself.

name: धर्मवर्धिनी
summary: (i) One who increases dharma.
(ii) She causes righteousness to grow.
(iii) She also cuts away what obstructs true dharma.
searchText: 959 धर्मवर्धिनी धर्मं वर्धयतीति। धर्ममात्रं दृश्यजातं छेदयतीति। धर्मवर्धिनी। — component of the name (i) one who increases dharma. (ii) she causes righteousness to grow. (iii) she also cuts away what obstructs true dharma. in the saubhāgyabhāskara, bhāskararāya details her active evolutionary adjustments: the cultivator of justice: as vivardhinī, she actively amplifies the parameters of spiritual virtue (dharma) inside the heart of the seeker. under the second technical track, she acts as the sharp intelligence that cuts down the false reality of phenomenal properties (dharma-mātram), revealing the non-dual background.
esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara, Bhāskararāya details Her active evolutionary adjustments:

The Cultivator of Justice: As Vivardhinī, She actively amplifies the parameters of spiritual virtue (Dharma) inside the heart of the seeker. Under the second technical track, She acts as the sharp intelligence that cuts down the false reality of phenomenal properties (dharma-mātram), revealing the non-dual background.

name: लोकातीता
summary: (i) One who transcends the worlds.
(ii) She stands beyond all lokas and beyond individual beings.
(iii) No world can limit Her.
searchText: 960 लोकातीता लोकानतीतातिक्रम्य स्थिता। लोकाञ्जीवान्वाऽतीता। लोकातीता। — component of the name (i) one who transcends the worlds. (ii) she stands beyond all lokas and beyond individual beings. (iii) no world can limit her. bhāskararāya details her unconditioned status past material maps: the transcendent sky: the fourteen functional worlds (lokas) format the spaces for karmic transaction. she is lokātītā because her essential nature as parabrahman sits entirely outside and above this dimensional network, remaining unconfined by local coordinates.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya details Her unconditioned status past material maps:

The Transcendent Sky: The fourteen functional worlds (lokas) format the spaces for karmic transaction. She is Lokātītā because Her essential nature as Parabrahman sits entirely outside and above this dimensional network, remaining unconfined by local coordinates.

name: गुणातीता
summary: (i) One who is beyond the gunas.
(ii) The three gunas belong to Prakriti.
(iii) She transcends them completely.
searchText: 961 गुणातीता गुणानतीता। गुणातीता। — component of the name (i) one who is beyond the gunas. (ii) the three gunas belong to prakriti. (iii) she transcends them completely. in the saubhāgyabhāskara, bhāskararāya defines the unconditioned baseline of spirit: beyond the material triad: shifting material properties create colors, forms, and changes inside the mind. she is guṇātītā because her ultimate reality is completely unconditioned (nirguṇa). she instructs the practitioner to look past the movements of light and mass, anchoring themselves in her unblemished, quality-less space.
esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara, Bhāskararāya defines the unconditioned baseline of spirit:

Beyond the Material Triad: Shifting material properties create colors, forms, and changes inside the mind. She is Guṇātītā because Her ultimate reality is completely unconditioned (Nirguṇa). She instructs the practitioner to look past the movements of light and mass, anchoring themselves in Her unblemished, quality-less space.

name: सर्वातीता
summary: (i) One who is beyond everything.
(ii) She surpasses all objects and all words.
(iii) No category or description can contain Her.
searchText: 962 सर्वातीता सर्वमतीता। सर्वान् शब्दानतीता। सर्वातीता। — component of the name (i) one who is beyond everything. (ii) she surpasses all objects and all words. (iii) no category or description can contain her. bhāskararāya delivers the absolute statement of spiritual transcendence: the shoreless remainder: lest any conceptual boundary be left unaddressed, this title confirms that she is sarvātītā — completely beyond everything that can be named, counted, or computed. critically, she passes beyond all spoken language and definitions (śabda-ātītā), existing as the silent, unconditioned truth that sits past description.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya delivers the absolute statement of spiritual transcendence:

The Shoreless Remainder: Lest any conceptual boundary be left unaddressed, this title confirms that She is Sarvātītā — completely beyond everything that can be named, counted, or computed. Critically, She passes beyond all spoken language and definitions (śabda-ātītā), existing as the silent, unconditioned truth that sits past description.

name: शमात्मिका
samasa: शमः प्रपञ्चोपशम एवात्मा स्वरूपं यस्याः सा।
summary: (i) One whose nature is peace.
(ii) Shama means the quieting or subsidence of the universe.
(iii) Her own form is the peaceful cessation of the entire phenomenal display.
searchText: 963 शमात्मिका शमः प्रपञ्चोपशम एवात्मा स्वरूपं यस्याः सा। शमः — peace; quiescence यस्याः — whose शमात्मिका। — component of the name (i) one whose nature is peace. (ii) shama means the quieting or subsidence of the universe. (iii) her own form is the peaceful cessation of the entire phenomenal display. in the saubhāgyabhāskara, bhāskararāya maps the definitive peace of the absolute: the quietude of the screen: śama is not a simple emotional calculation of calmness. it refers to the absolute, grand quietude that occurs when the entire movie of manifest worlds dissolves back into unmanifest stillness (प्रपञ्चोपशम). she is śamātmikā because her body is this pristine, unperturbed silence, offering a home where all mental friction is terminated.
esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara, Bhāskararāya maps the definitive peace of the absolute:

The Quietude of the Screen: Śama is not a simple emotional calculation of calmness. It refers to the absolute, grand quietude that occurs when the entire movie of manifest worlds dissolves back into unmanifest stillness (प्रपञ्चोपशम). She is Śamātmikā because Her body is this pristine, unperturbed silence, offering a home where all mental friction is terminated.

name: बन्धूककुसुमप्रख्या
samasa: बन्धूको महावृक्षस्तस्य कुसुमं पुष्पं तस्येव प्रख्या कान्तिर्यस्याः सा।
summary: (i) One whose complexion is like the Bandhuka flower.
(ii) The Bandhuka blossom is bright and red.
(iii) Her radiance is compared with that flower.
searchText: 964 बन्धूककुसुमप्रख्या बन्धूको महावृक्षस्तस्य कुसुमं पुष्पं तस्येव प्रख्या कान्तिर्यस्याः सा। प्रख्या — resembling; lustre बन्धूककुसुमप्रख्या। — component of the name (i) one whose complexion is like the bandhuka flower. (ii) the bandhuka blossom is bright and red. (iii) her radiance is compared with that flower. bhāskararāya maps this vibrant color frequency within visual concentration: the flash of active awareness: the bandhūka blossom exhibits an exceptionally deep, unblemished crimson-red hue. in the physics of visualization, this frequency represents the absolute activation of विमर्शशक्ति (vimarśa-śakti — active self-recognition) overflowing with compassionate intent. meditating on this color pattern burns away internal lethargy, tuning the channels to clear light.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya maps this vibrant color frequency within visual concentration:

The Flash of Active Awareness: The Bandhūka blossom exhibits an exceptionally deep, unblemished crimson-red hue. In the physics of visualization, this frequency represents the absolute activation of विमर्शशक्ति (Vimarśa-śakti — active self-recognition) overflowing with compassionate intent. Meditating on this color pattern burns away internal lethargy, tuning the channels to clear light.

name: बाला
summary: (i) One who is a young girl.
(ii) Bala means maiden or child.
(iii) The name also points to Her youthful and worshipful Bala form.
searchText: 965 बाला कुमारी। बाला। — component of the name (i) one who is a young girl. (ii) bala means maiden or child. (iii) the name also points to her youthful and worshipful bala form. in the saubhāgyabhāskara, bhāskararāya decodes the playful youth and mantric hierarchy of this name: the ever-youthful core: she is bālā because her energy remains permanently fresh, vibrant, and untouched by biological aging or structural decay. in the initiatory lineage, she is non-different from बाला त्रिपुरसुन्दरी (bālā tripurasundarī) — the highly potent three-syllabled entry gateway deity whose mantra formats the foundational channels of the student.
esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara, Bhāskararāya decodes the playful youth and mantric hierarchy of this name:

The Ever-Youthful Core: She is Bālā because Her energy remains permanently fresh, vibrant, and untouched by biological aging or structural decay. In the initiatory lineage, She is non-different from बाला त्रिपुरसुन्दरी (Bālā Tripurasundarī) — the highly potent three-syllabled entry gateway deity whose mantra formats the foundational channels of the student.

name: लीलाविनोदिनी
samasa: पद्यराजस्य भार्या लीलाख्या योगवासिष्ठे प्रसिद्धा तां विशिष्य सत्कर्मसु नोदयतीति।
summary: (i) One who delights in sport.
(ii) The play of the universe itself is Her pastime.
(iii) Scriptural passages such as “Lokavattu Leela Kaivalyam” are used to explain that creation is Her divine play.
searchText: 966 लीलाविनोदिनी लीला प्रापञ्चिकी क्रीडैव विनोदो यस्याः सा। पद्यराजस्य भार्या लीलाख्या योगवासिष्ठे प्रसिद्धा तां विशिष्य सत्कर्मसु नोदयतीति। लीला — play; sport यस्याः — whose लीलाविनोदिनी। — component of the name (i) one who delights in sport. (ii) the play of the universe itself is her pastime. (iii) scriptural passages such as “lokavattu leela kaivalyam” are used to explain that creation is her divine play. bhāskararāya strips cosmic action of any concept of labor or friction: the delight of the play: the generation, maintenance, and dissolution of trillions of stellar systems is not a source of stress or heavy labor for her consciousness. it is handled as a spontaneous, completely effortless game (līlā). she watches this planetary deployment with pure amusement (vinoda), remaining detached and joyful.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya strips cosmic action of any concept of labor or friction:

The Delight of the Play: The generation, maintenance, and dissolution of trillions of stellar systems is not a source of stress or heavy labor for Her consciousness. It is handled as a spontaneous, completely effortless game (līlā). She watches this planetary deployment with pure amusement (vinoda), remaining detached and joyful.

name: सुमङ्गली
summary: (i) One who is very auspicious.
(ii) Sumangali also denotes a woman whose husband is alive.
(iii) Since auspiciousness indicates Brahman, She is the form of auspicious Brahman and removes sorrow.
searchText: 967 सुमङ्गली शोभनं मङ्गलमस्या अस्तीति। शोभनं मङ्गलं ब्रह्मैवेति। सुमङ्गली। — component of the name (i) one who is very auspicious. (ii) sumangali also denotes a woman whose husband is alive. (iii) since auspiciousness indicates brahman, she is the form of auspicious brahman and removes sorrow. in the saubhāgyabhāskara, bhāskararāya connects ultimate good fortune to pure spirit: the auspicious anchor: she is sumaṅgalī because her presence generates overall protection, prosperity, and balance across the workspace. critically, bhāskararāya notes that the ultimate, uncorrupted auspiciousness (maṅgalam) is the absolute brahman itself. she is the literal embodiment of this baseline goodness.
esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara, Bhāskararāya connects ultimate good fortune to pure spirit:

The Auspicious Anchor: She is Sumaṅgalī because Her presence generates overall protection, prosperity, and balance across the workspace. Critically, Bhāskararāya notes that the ultimate, uncorrupted auspiciousness (maṅgalam) is the absolute Brahman itself. She is the literal embodiment of this baseline goodness.

name: सुखकरी
summary: (i) One who gives happiness.
(ii) This repeats the same message seen in names such as Sharmadayini, Sukhaprada and Sharmada.
(iii) She grants joy and blessedness.
searchText: 968 सुखकरी सुखं करोति तच्छीलम्। करोति — makes; causes सुखकरी। — component of the name (i) one who gives happiness. (ii) this repeats the same message seen in names such as sharmadayini, sukhaprada and sharmada.
