(iii) she grants joy and blessedness. bhāskararāya defines the permanent alignment of her benevolence: the manufacturer of ease: her baseline operations are naturally formatted to convert the friction of material existence into steady, soul-level happiness (sukha). she does not distribute temporary psychological thrill; she sets the conditions that allow the seeker's mind to rest in ease.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya defines the permanent alignment of Her benevolence:

The Manufacturer of Ease: Her baseline operations are naturally formatted to convert the friction of material existence into steady, soul-level happiness (sukha). She does not distribute temporary psychological thrill; She sets the conditions that allow the seeker's mind to rest in ease.

name: सुवेषाढ्या
summary: (i) One decked with beautiful garments and ornaments.
(ii) Since She is Sumangali, She is adorned with auspicious raiment and ornaments.
(iii) She is richly decorated with jewels and gold ornaments.
searchText: 969 सुवेषाढ्या शोभनेन वेषेणाढ्या युक्ता। सुवेषाढ्या। — component of the name (i) one decked with beautiful garments and ornaments. (ii) since she is sumangali, she is adorned with auspicious raiment and ornaments. (iii) she is richly decorated with jewels and gold ornaments. in the saubhāgyabhāskara, bhāskararāya decodes the energetic vestures of the absolute: the raiment of pure radiance: her "vestures" (veṣa) are not woven fabrics. they are the beautiful visual rays of इच्छा, ज्ञान, क्रिया (will, knowledge, action) that drape around her form. she is suveṣāḍhyā because she is richly wrapped in these flawless aesthetic geometries, which serve to clear away dark, chaotic currents from the field of view.
esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara, Bhāskararāya decodes the energetic vestures of the absolute:

The Raiment of Pure Radiance: Her "vestures" (veṣa) are not woven fabrics. They are the beautiful visual rays of इच्छा, ज्ञान, क्रिया (Will, Knowledge, Action) that drape around Her form. She is Suveṣāḍhyā because She is richly wrapped in these flawless aesthetic geometries, which serve to clear away dark, chaotic currents from the field of view.

name: सुवासिनी
summary: (i) One who lives with Her husband.
(ii) Living with one’s husband is praised as a great blessing for ladies, and the word Suvasini indicates that auspicious state.
(iii) Texts such as Srimad Ramayana are cited to praise this sacred condition.
searchText: 970 सुवासिनी सुवासिनी। सुवासिनीनामर्चनेन प्रीता। सुवासिनी। — component of the name (i) one who lives with her husband. (ii) living with one’s husband is praised as a great blessing for ladies, and the word suvasini indicates that auspicious state. (iii) texts such as srimad ramayana are cited to praise this sacred condition. bhāskararāya unpacks the multi-layered cosmic and social layout of this title: the living shield of auspiciousness: suvāsinī denotes a woman who embodies active marital protection and auspicious vitality. metaphysically, she is suvāsinī because her eternal partner, paramaśiva, is perpetually alive and present. concurrently, she is the intelligence that perfumes (vāsa) the entire multiverse with the fragrance of consciousness, abiding securely inside all elements.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya unpacks the multi-layered cosmic and social layout of this title:

The Living Shield of Auspiciousness: Suvāsinī denotes a woman who embodies active marital protection and auspicious vitality. Metaphysically, She is Suvāsinī because Her eternal partner, Paramaśiva, is perpetually alive and present. Concurrently, She is the intelligence that perfumes (vāsa) the entire multiverse with the fragrance of consciousness, abiding securely inside all elements.

name: सुवासिन्यर्चनप्रीता
summary: (i) One who is pleased by the worship done by Suvasinis.
(ii) Or one who is pleased by doing archana to Suvasinis.
(iii) Worshipping Suvasinis is treated as an important part of Sree Devee worship, since all women are to be regarded as forms of Sree Devee.
searchText: 971 सुवासिन्यर्चनप्रीता सुवासिनीनाम् अर्चनेन प्रीता। सुवासिन्यर्चनप्रीता। — component of the name (i) one who is pleased by the worship done by suvasinis. (ii) or one who is pleased by doing archana to suvasinis. (iii) worshipping suvasinis is treated as an important part of sree devee worship, since all women are to be regarded as forms of sree devee. in the saubhāgyabhāskara, bhāskararāya anchors her presence directly within living human forms: the living altars: she does not limit her presence to metallic icons or drawings. she sits actively inside living human forms that project auspicious energy (suvāsinīs). when a practitioner honors, clothes, and feeds these living women with absolute reverence, she accepts that energy directly, clearing away obstacles from the path.
esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara, Bhāskararāya anchors Her presence directly within living human forms:

The Living Altars: She does not limit Her presence to metallic icons or drawings. She sits actively inside living human forms that project auspicious energy (suvāsinīs). When a practitioner honors, clothes, and feeds these living women with absolute reverence, She accepts that energy directly, clearing away obstacles from the path.

name: अशोभना/आशोभना
summary: (i) One who is always and everywhere beautiful.
(ii) Her beauty is described in many places in the Sahasranama.
(iii) A large part of Soundaryalahari is devoted to the description of Her beauty.
searchText: 972 अशोभना/आशोभना अविद्यमानं शोभनं सौन्दर्यं यस्याः (पञ्चमी) सा। आसमन्तात् शोभनं सौन्दर्यं यस्याः (षष्ठी) सा। यस्याः — whose अशोभना — component of the name आशोभना। — component of the name (i) one who is always and everywhere beautiful. (ii) her beauty is described in many places in the sahasranama. (iii) a large part of soundaryalahari is devoted to the description of her beauty. bhāskararāya delivers a high non-dual reconciliation of beauty standards: beyond mundane aesthetics: as aśobhanā, she stands completely free from mundane, superficial, and relative standards of physical beauty, which are transient and deceptive. when parsed as āśobhanā, it establishes that she possesses absolute, permanent, and infinite beauty (paramam saundaryam) radiating from every single coordinate of her cosmic form, blinding the intellect with absolute grace.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya delivers a high non-dual reconciliation of beauty standards:

Beyond Mundane Aesthetics: As Aśobhanā, She stands completely free from mundane, superficial, and relative standards of physical beauty, which are transient and deceptive. When parsed as Āśobhanā, it establishes that She possesses absolute, permanent, and infinite beauty (paramam saundaryam) radiating from every single coordinate of Her cosmic form, blinding the intellect with absolute grace.

name: शुद्धमानसा
summary: (i) One whose mind is pure.
(ii) Her internal nature is untouched by impurity.
(iii) She is the embodiment of purity of mind.
searchText: 973 शुद्धमानसा शुद्धं मानसं यस्याः सा। यस्याः — whose शुद्धमानसा। — component of the name (i) one whose mind is pure. (ii) her internal nature is untouched by impurity. (iii) she is the embodiment of purity of mind. in the saubhāgyabhāskara, bhāskararāya defines the pristine clarity of the divine mind: the unblemished screen: human minds gather dust through desires and prejudices. her cosmic mind (mānasa) is śuddha — completely free from any latent impressions, ignorance, or emotional fluctuations. she remains a clear, crystal-like awareness screen that reflects the universe without being stained by it.
esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara, Bhāskararāya defines the pristine clarity of the divine mind:

The Unblemished Screen: Human minds gather dust through desires and prejudices. Her cosmic mind (mānasa) is Śuddha — completely free from any latent impressions, ignorance, or emotional fluctuations. She remains a clear, crystal-like awareness screen that reflects the universe without being stained by it.

name: बिन्दुतर्पणसन्तुष्टा
samasa: बिन्दौ सर्वानन्दमये चक्रे ब्राह्मणादिवर्णचतुष्टयकर्तृकेण तर्पणेन सम्यक्तुष्टा।
summary: (i) One satisfied by tarpana at the Bindu.
(ii) The Bindu is the Sarvanandamaya Chakra, and offerings there please Her.
(iii) Tarpana with milk, ghee, honey, wine or other prescribed offerings is also indicated.
searchText: 974 बिन्दुतर्पणसन्तुष्टा बिन्दौ सर्वानन्दमये चक्रे ब्राह्मणादिवर्णचतुष्टयकर्तृकेण तर्पणेन सम्यक्तुष्टा। क्षीराज्यमध्वासवबिन्दुकरणकेन वा तर्पणेन सम्यक्तुष्टा। बिन्दुतर्पणसन्तुष्टा। — component of the name (i) one satisfied by tarpana at the bindu. (ii) the bindu is the sarvanandamaya chakra, and offerings there please her. (iii) tarpana with milk, ghee, honey, wine or other prescribed offerings is also indicated. bhāskararāya unpacks the secret internal and ritual operations of the lineage: the alchemy of the center point: externally, this denotes offering drops (bindu) of milk, honey, or consecrated liquids into the central hub of the yantra. internally, bindu refers to the absolute central point of the sahasrāra crown. when the practitioner drives the internal energy up to this center, it melts the absolute nectar of immortality. offering this nectar back into the point satisfies her, stabilizing the system.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya unpacks the secret internal and ritual operations of the lineage:

The Alchemy of the Center Point: Externally, this denotes offering drops (bindu) of milk, honey, or consecrated liquids into the central hub of the Yantra. Internally, Bindu refers to the absolute central point of the Sahasrāra crown. When the practitioner drives the internal energy up to this center, it melts the absolute nectar of immortality. Offering this nectar back into the point satisfies Her, stabilizing the system.

name: पूर्वजा
summary: (i) One who is born or manifested first.
(ii) She is prior to all manifestation.
(iii) She is the first and original principle.
searchText: 975 पूर्वजा पूर्वं जा जाता अभिव्यक्ता। जा — born; manifested पूर्वजा। — component of the name (i) one who is born or manifested first. (ii) she is prior to all manifestation. (iii) she is the first and original principle. in the saubhāgyabhāskara, bhāskararāya clarifies her chronological priority over creation: the primordial manifestation: before elements consolidate and before the secondary creators take their stations, she is the very first wave of manifestation (pūrvajā) emerging out of the absolute unmanifest silence. she is the first ray of dawn, setting the template for everything that follows.
esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara, Bhāskararāya clarifies Her chronological priority over creation:

The Primordial Manifestation: Before elements consolidate and before the secondary creators take their stations, She is the very first wave of manifestation (Pūrvajā) emerging out of the absolute unmanifest silence. She is the first ray of dawn, setting the template for everything that follows.

name: त्रिपुराम्बिका
samasa: त्रीणि पुराणि यस्य स जीवः। तेषामम्बिका।
summary: (i) Mother of the three cities.
(ii) The three cities may be the three states of experience.
(iii) She is the mother of the jiva who identifies with those three states.
searchText: 976 त्रिपुराम्बिका त्रयाणां पुराणामवस्थारूपाणामम्बिका जनिका। त्रीणि पुराणि यस्य स जीवः। तेषामम्बिका। त्रिपुराम्बिका। — component of the name (i) mother of the three cities. (ii) the three cities may be the three states of experience. (iii) she is the mother of the jiva who identifies with those three states. bhāskararāya details the structural hierarchy of human experience: the parent of the three states: the "three cities" (tripura) are the three everyday states of human awareness: जाग्रत (waking), स्वप्न (dreaming), and सुषुप्ति (deep sleep). she is ambikā (the mother) to this entire triad. she generates and supports these states, serving as the hidden fourth space (turīya) that anchors them.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya details the structural hierarchy of human experience:

The Parent of the Three States: The "three cities" (tripura) are the three everyday states of human awareness: जाग्रत (Waking), स्वप्न (Dreaming), and सुषुप्ति (Deep Sleep). She is Ambikā (the mother) to this entire triad. She generates and supports these states, serving as the hidden fourth space (Turīya) that anchors them.

name: दशमुद्रासमाराध्या
summary: (i) One properly worshipped through the ten mudras.
(ii) The ten mudras are ritual instruments of worship.
(iii) She is specially adored through them.
searchText: 977 दशमुद्रासमाराध्या दश मुद्राभिः करणभूताभिः सम्यक् आराध्या। दश — ten दशमुद्रासमाराध्या। — component of the name (i) one properly worshipped through the ten mudras. (ii) the ten mudras are ritual instruments of worship. (iii) she is specially adored through them. in the saubhāgyabhāskara, bhāskararāya details the geometric and physical locks of the śrīvidyā tradition: the ten energy seals: mudrās are precise physical gestures and internal seals (running from sarvasaṅkṣobhiṇī up to sarvatrikhaṇḍā) that match the nine enclosures of the śrī yantra. they are not symbolic dances. they act as precise energetic keys that lock the practitioner's nervous system into alignment, opening the channels to receive her grace.
esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara, Bhāskararāya details the geometric and physical locks of the Śrīvidyā tradition:

The Ten Energy Seals: Mudrās are precise physical gestures and internal seals (running from Sarvasaṅkṣobhiṇī up to Sarvatrikhaṇḍā) that match the nine enclosures of the Śrī Yantra. They are not symbolic dances. They act as precise energetic keys that lock the practitioner's nervous system into alignment, opening the channels to receive Her grace.

name: त्रिपुराश्रीवशङ्करी
summary: (i) One who brings Tripura Sri under control.
(ii) Or Tripura Sri herself is the power of bringing under control.
(iii) The name connects Her with the authority of Tripura Sri.
searchText: 978 त्रिपुराश्रीवशङ्करी त्रिपुराश्रीनामिकां देवीं वशं कुरुते इति। त्रिपुराश्रीनामिका देवी वशङ्करी यस्याः सा। यस्याः — whose त्रिपुराश्रीवशङ्करी। — component of the name (i) one who brings tripura sri under control. (ii) or tripura sri herself is the power of bringing under control. (iii) the name connects her with the authority of tripura sri. bhāskararāya details the administrative layout of the śrī cakra: command over the fifth circle: within the anatomy of the śrī yantra, tripurāśrī is the specific administrative deity placed in charge of the fifth enclosure. she is the ultimate source back behind this goddess, directing the distribution of wealth and spiritual merit.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya details the administrative layout of the Śrī Cakra:

Command over the Fifth Circle: Within the anatomy of the Śrī Yantra, Tripurāśrī is the specific administrative deity placed in charge of the fifth enclosure. She is the ultimate source back behind this goddess, directing the distribution of wealth and spiritual merit.

name: ज्ञानमुद्रा
summary: (i) One who is the Jnana Mudra.
(ii) The name also means one who gives delight through knowledge.
(iii) Knowledge and bliss are united in this mudra.
searchText: 979 ज्ञानमुद्रा ज्ञानमुद्ररूपा। ज्ञानेन मुदं रातीति। ज्ञानं चिदंशं मुदमानन्दांशं द्रावयत्यावृणोतीति। ज्ञानमुद्रा। — component of the name (i) one who is the jnana mudra. (ii) the name also means one who gives delight through knowledge. (iii) knowledge and bliss are united in this mudra. in the saubhāgyabhāskara, bhāskararāya decodes the physical and internal lock of wisdom: the seal of awareness: physically, she manifests as the iconic hand gesture where the index finger touches the thumb, forming a complete circuit that symbols individual soul merging with universal spirit. internally, she acts as the force that dispenses intense spiritual joy (mudam) the exact moment pure knowledge (jñāna) flashes open inside the mind.
esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara, Bhāskararāya decodes the physical and internal lock of wisdom:

The Seal of Awareness: Physically, She manifests as the iconic hand gesture where the index finger touches the thumb, forming a complete circuit that symbols individual soul merging with universal spirit. Internally, She acts as the force that dispenses intense spiritual joy (mudam) the exact moment pure knowledge (jñāna) flashes open inside the mind.

name: ज्ञानगम्या
summary: (i) One who is reached by knowledge.
(ii) She is attained through true knowledge.
(iii) Realisation, not mere external action, is the path to Her.
searchText: 980 ज्ञानगम्या ज्ञानेन गम्या प्राप्या। गम्या — attainable; reached ज्ञानगम्या। — component of the name (i) one who is reached by knowledge. (ii) she is attained through true knowledge. (iii) realisation, not mere external action, is the path to her. bhāskararāya delivers the definitive epistemological filter for her presence: the gate of awakening: she cannot be captured by emotional manipulation, mechanical rituals, or dry intellectual logic. she is jñānagamyā because her ultimate reality opens its gate exclusively to pure, non-dual realization (jñāna). every preliminary practice must eventually transform into direct awareness to step inside her sanctuary.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya delivers the definitive epistemological filter for Her presence:

The Gate of Awakening: She cannot be captured by emotional manipulation, mechanical rituals, or dry intellectual logic. She is Jñānagamyā because Her ultimate reality opens its gate exclusively to pure, non-dual realization (Jñāna). Every preliminary practice must eventually transform into direct awareness to step inside Her sanctuary.

name: ज्ञानज्ञेयस्वरूपिणी
summary: (i) One whose form is knowledge and the object known.
(ii) She is both the seer and the seen, knowledge and the knowable.
(iii) The distinction between knower, knowing and known resolves in Her.
searchText: 981 ज्ञानज्ञेयस्वरूपिणी ज्ञानज्ञेये दृग्दृश्ये स्वरूपमस्याः अस्तीति। ज्ञानज्ञेयस्वरूपिणी। — component of the name (i) one whose form is knowledge and the object known. (ii) she is both the seer and the seen, knowledge and the knowable. (iii) the distinction between knower, knowing and known resolves in her. in the saubhāgyabhāskara, bhāskararāya presents a master non-dual equation to collapse epistemological duality: the fusion of subject and object: human tracking splits reality into a subject who looks (dṛg / jñāna) and an object that is looked at (dṛśya / jñeya). she is jñānajñeyasvarūpiṇī because she is the single, non-dual energy field that manifests as both sides of the equation. there is no independent world out there separate from the light that sees it; the knower and the known are one substance.
esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara, Bhāskararāya presents a master non-dual equation to collapse epistemological duality:

The Fusion of Subject and Object: Human tracking splits reality into a subject who looks (Dṛg / Jñāna) and an object that is looked at (Dṛśya / Jñeya). She is Jñānajñeyasvarūpiṇī because She is the single, non-dual energy field that manifests as both sides of the equation. There is no independent world out there separate from the light that sees it; the knower and the known are one substance.

name: योनिमुद्रा
summary: (i) One who is the Yoni Mudra.
(ii) She grants delight in the yoni, the source.
(iii) The yoni itself is the concealing and revealing mudra of the Bindu.
searchText: 982 योनिमुद्रा योनौ मुदं राति। योनिरेव मुद्रा आछादिका यस्य बिन्दोस्तद्रूपा। मुद्रा — mudra योनिमुद्रा। — component of the name (i) one who is the yoni mudra. (ii) she grants delight in the yoni, the source. (iii) the yoni itself is the concealing and revealing mudra of the bindu. bhāskararāya details the ultimate geometric and physical seal of energy conservation: the seal of primordial source: yonimudrā is the highly sacred, non-dual hand gesture that represents the matrix of creation. internally, it refers to the primary inverted triangle enclosure that tightly wraps and protects the central point (bindu). she is this seal, representing the absolute containment and conservation of spiritual power before it translates into manifestation.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya details the ultimate geometric and physical seal of energy conservation:

The Seal of Primordial Source: Yonimudrā is the highly sacred, non-dual hand gesture that represents the matrix of creation. Internally, it refers to the primary inverted triangle enclosure that tightly wraps and protects the central point (Bindu). She is this seal, representing the absolute containment and conservation of spiritual power before it translates into manifestation.

name: त्रिखण्डेशी
summary: (i) Mistress of Trikhanda.
(ii) She is the ruler of the tenth mudra called Trikhanda.
(iii) She is also the ruler of the three mantra divisions known as Soma, Surya and Anala.
searchText: 983 त्रिखण्डेशी त्रिखण्डाख्याया दशम्या मुद्राया ईशी स्वामिनी। त्रयाणां सोमसूर्यानलाख्यमन्त्रखण्डानामीशी। ईशी — mistress त्रिखण्डेशी। — component of the name (i) mistress of trikhanda. (ii) she is the ruler of the tenth mudra called trikhanda. (iii) she is also the ruler of the three mantra divisions known as soma, surya and anala. in the saubhāgyabhāskara, bhāskararāya details the phonetic and physical mastery of this title: the master of the three blocks: trikhaṇḍā is the definitive tenth mudrā that seals the entire śrī yantra ritual work. phonetically, it refers to the three distinct blocks (khaṇḍas) of the supreme mantra—the moon section (soma), the sun section (sūrya), and the fire section (anala). she is the absolute īśvarī (empress) who governs this entire triple structure, binding it into a single power loop.
esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara, Bhāskararāya details the phonetic and physical mastery of this title:

The Master of the Three Blocks: Trikhaṇḍā is the definitive tenth mudrā that seals the entire Śrī Yantra ritual work. Phonetically, it refers to the three distinct blocks (khaṇḍas) of the supreme mantra—the Moon section (Soma), the Sun section (Sūrya), and the Fire section (Anala). She is the absolute Īśvarī (empress) who governs this entire triple structure, binding it into a single power loop.

name: त्रिगुणा
summary: (i) One who has the three gunas.
(ii) Though She transcends the gunas, they also exist in Her as the power of manifestation.
(iii) She is the support of sattva, rajas and tamas.
searchText: 984 त्रिगुणा त्रयो गुणा यस्यां सा। त्रिगुणा। — component of the name (i) one who has the three gunas. (ii) though she transcends the gunas, they also exist in her as the power of manifestation. (iii) she is the support of sattva, rajas and tamas. bhāskararāya details the operational engine of material nature: the weaver of form: while she transcends qualities (guṇātītā), she is simultaneously triguṇā because she coordinates the three qualities (sattva, rajas, tamas) to build the physical world. she acts as the master weaver who spins light, movement, and mass to construct the material universe.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya details the operational engine of material nature:

The Weaver of Form: While She transcends qualities (Guṇātītā), She is simultaneously Triguṇā because She coordinates the three qualities (Sattva, Rajas, Tamas) to build the physical world. She acts as the master weaver who spins light, movement, and mass to construct the material universe.

name: अम्बा
summary: (i) Mother.
(ii) Amba means the mother and the causal source.
(iii) She is the mother of all.
searchText: 985 अम्बा अम्बा माता कारणभूता। अम्बा — mother अम्बा। — component of the name (i) mother. (ii) amba means the mother and the causal source. (iii) she is the mother of all. in the saubhāgyabhāskara, bhāskararāya strips away any remaining technical complexity to reveal the core relationship: the primordial maternal source: the entire cosmic system of mantras, elements, and geometries dissolves into a single word—ambā. she is the direct, unconditional mother of every living soul. she is the ultimate cause (jagat-kāraṇa) that provides love, protection, and the necessary path inputs to guide her children home.
esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara, Bhāskararāya strips away any remaining technical complexity to reveal the core relationship:

The Primordial Maternal Source: The entire cosmic system of mantras, elements, and geometries dissolves into a single word—Ambā. She is the direct, unconditional mother of every living soul. She is the ultimate cause (jagat-kāraṇa) that provides love, protection, and the necessary path inputs to guide Her children home.

name: त्रिकोणगा
summary: (i) One who abides in the triangle.
(ii) The triangle is the yoni-chakra.
(iii) She is present in that sacred triangular centre.
searchText: 986 त्रिकोणगा त्रिकोण योनिचक्रं गच्छतीति। त्रिकोण — triangle त्रिकोणगा। — component of the name (i) one who abides in the triangle. (ii) the triangle is the yoni-chakra. (iii) she is present in that sacred triangular centre. bhāskararāya details the geometric hub of visualization: seated in the core triangle: in the layout of the śrī yantra, the त्रिकोण (trikoṇa) is the primary central triangle that surrounds the absolute center point (bindu). she is trikoṇagā because her consciousness resides directly inside this geometric hub, representing the absolute source out of which the lines of the universe branch out.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya details the geometric hub of visualization:

Seated in the Core Triangle: In the layout of the Śrī Yantra, the त्रिकोण (Trikoṇa) is the primary central triangle that surrounds the absolute center point (Bindu). She is Trikoṇagā because Her consciousness resides directly inside this geometric hub, representing the absolute source out of which the lines of the universe branch out.

name: अनघा
summary: (i) One who is sinless.
(ii) Agha means sin or evil.
(iii) No sin or blemish exists in Her.
searchText: 987 अनघा अंविद्यमानानि अघानि यस्यां सा। अघानि — sins अनघा। — component of the name (i) one who is sinless. (ii) agha means sin or evil. (iii) no sin or blemish exists in her. in the saubhāgyabhāskara, bhāskararāya defines her absolute purity: the blemish-free screen: material entities gather stains (agha) through ignorant actions and historical distortions. she is anaghā because her unconditioned consciousness field is completely free from these defects. she remains a pristine, unblemished template of absolute purity, clearing away the errors of anyone who enters her space.
esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara, Bhāskararāya defines Her absolute purity:

The Blemish-Free Screen: Material entities gather stains (agha) through ignorant actions and historical distortions. She is Anaghā because Her unconditioned consciousness field is completely free from these defects. She remains a pristine, unblemished template of absolute purity, clearing away the errors of anyone who enters Her space.

name: अद्भुतचारित्रा
summary: (i) One whose conduct is wonderful.
(ii) Her deeds are extraordinary and astonishing.
(iii) The name may also indicate protection from evil results connected with portents and disturbances.
searchText: 988 अद्भुतचारित्रा अद्भुतान्याश्चर्यकराणि चारित्राणि यस्याः सा। अद्भुतेषु भूकम्पादिषूत्पातेषु निमित्तेषु चरन्ती। अद्भुतचारीणि दुष्टफलानि तेभ्यस्त्रायते इति। यस्याः — whose अद्भुतचारित्रा। — component of the name (i) one whose conduct is wonderful. (ii) her deeds are extraordinary and astonishing. (iii) the name may also indicate protection from evil results connected with portents and disturbances. bhāskararāya details her extraordinary capacity to manage universal anomalies: the conductor of miracles: her cosmic management features deeds (cāritra) that spark absolute wonder (adbhuta), bypassing linear logical calculation. under the protective tracks, she acts as the stabilizing intelligence that moves through chaotic natural disturbances (like earthquakes), protecting her devotees from destructive outcomes.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya details Her extraordinary capacity to manage universal anomalies:

The Conductor of Miracles: Her cosmic management features deeds (cāritra) that spark absolute wonder (adbhuta), bypassing linear logical calculation. Under the protective tracks, She acts as the stabilizing intelligence that moves through chaotic natural disturbances (like earthquakes), protecting Her devotees from destructive outcomes.

name: वाञ्छितार्थप्रदायिनी
summary: (i) One who grants desired objects.
(ii) She has the nature of bestowing what is longed for.
(iii) Devotees receive their wished-for ends through Her grace.
searchText: 989 वाञ्छितार्थप्रदायिनी वाञ्छितार्थान्प्रदातुं शीलमस्याः अस्तीति। वाञ्छितार्थप्रदायिनी। — component of the name (i) one who grants desired objects. (ii) she has the nature of bestowing what is longed for. (iii) devotees receive their wished-for ends through her grace. in the saubhāgyabhāskara, bhāskararāya details her responsive generosity: the fulfiller of pure intent: she is not indifferent to the practical needs of the seeker. as pradāyinī, she tracks the authentic desires (vāñchita) of the heart, whether material or spiritual, and systematically dispenses the necessary resources to ensure the practitioner does not experience anxiety while pursuing the path.
esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara, Bhāskararāya details Her responsive generosity:

The Fulfiller of Pure Intent: She is not indifferent to the practical needs of the seeker. As Pradāyinī, She tracks the authentic desires (vāñchita) of the heart, whether material or spiritual, and systematically dispenses the necessary resources to ensure the practitioner does not experience anxiety while pursuing the path.

name: अभ्यासातिशयज्ञाता
summary: (i) One known through excess of practice.
(ii) She is realised through repeated practice of the enquiry into the unity of Brahman and the Self.
(iii) Persistent spiritual practice reveals Her.
searchText: 990 अभ्यासातिशयज्ञाता ब्रह्मात्मैक्यानुसन्धानाभ्यास्य आवृत्त्याः अतिशयेन ज्ञाता। अतिशयेन — excessively; through intensity अभ्यासातिशयज्ञाता। — component of the name (i) one known through excess of practice. (ii) she is realised through repeated practice of the enquiry into the unity of brahman and the self. (iii) persistent spiritual practice reveals her. bhāskararāya defines the definitive method of realization as the text approaches its climax: the momentum of realization: she cannot be uncovered by a casual or distracted interest. she is jñātā (realized) exclusively through अभ्यास-अतिशय (abhyāsa-atiśaya) — the continuous, intense, and unbroken habit of non-dual meditation on the identity of the individual soul and the absolute absolute (ब्रह्मात्मैक्य). this continuous momentum clears away the oldest layers of mental habits, revealing the self.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya defines the definitive method of realization as the text approaches its climax:

The Momentum of Realization: She cannot be uncovered by a casual or distracted interest. She is Jñātā (realized) exclusively through अभ्यास-अतिशय (abhyāsa-atiśaya) — the continuous, intense, and unbroken habit of non-dual meditation on the identity of the individual soul and the absolute absolute (ब्रह्मात्मैक्य). This continuous momentum clears away the oldest layers of mental habits, revealing the Self.

name: षडध्वातीतरूपिणी
summary: (i) One whose form transcends the six paths.
(ii) The six adhvas are categories of mantra, word and manifestation.
(iii) Her form is beyond all of them.
searchText: 991 षडध्वातीतरूपिणी षडध्वानान् अतीतं रूपमस्याः अस्तीति। षडध्वातीतरूपिणी। — component of the name (i) one whose form transcends the six paths. (ii) the six adhvas are categories of mantra, word and manifestation. (iii) her form is beyond all of them. in the saubhāgyabhāskara, bhāskararāya delivers a major layout detailing the structural architecture of agamic creation: transcending the six pathways: the entire universe is structured across six major operational pathways (ṣaḍ-adhvā), split into the sound track (śabda-adhvā: varṇa, pada, mantra) and the object track (artha-adhvā: kalā, tattva, bhuvana). she is ṣaḍadhvātītarūpiṇī because her essential unconditioned nature sits completely above and outside this entire structural framework, serving as the raw consciousness that supports the path grid.
esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara, Bhāskararāya delivers a major layout detailing the structural architecture of Agamic creation:

Transcending the Six Pathways: The entire universe is structured across six major operational pathways (ṣaḍ-adhvā), split into the sound track (Śabda-adhvā: Varṇa, Pada, Mantra) and the object track (Artha-adhvā: Kalā, Tattva, Bhuvana). She is Ṣaḍadhvātītarūpiṇī because Her essential unconditioned nature sits completely above and outside this entire structural framework, serving as the raw consciousness that supports the path grid.

name: अव्याजकरुणामूर्तिः
samasa: अव्याजा अनौपधिकी या करुणा सैव मूर्तिः स्वरूपं यस्याः सा।
summary: (i) One whose form is causeless compassion.
(ii) Her mercy is not dependent on any condition or motive.
(iii) Compassion itself is Her form.
searchText: 992 अव्याजकरुणामूर्तिः अव्याजा अनौपधिकी या करुणा सैव मूर्तिः स्वरूपं यस्याः सा। अव्याजा — without pretext; causeless करुणा — compassion मूर्तिः — form यस्याः — whose (i) one whose form is causeless compassion. (ii) her mercy is not dependent on any condition or motive. (iii) compassion itself is her form. bhāskararāya details the absolute purity of divine grace: causeless grace: human compassion is usually savyāja — dependent on external conditions, personal relationships, or expectations of return. she is avyājakaruṇāmūrtiḥ because her grace is completely unconditioned (anaupadhikī). she showers love simply because it is her baseline nature to do so, requiring no complex bargaining from the practitioner.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya details the absolute purity of divine grace:

Causeless Grace: Human compassion is usually savyāja — dependent on external conditions, personal relationships, or expectations of return. She is Avyājakaruṇāmūrtiḥ because Her grace is completely unconditioned (anaupadhikī). She showers love simply because it is Her baseline nature to do so, requiring no complex bargaining from the practitioner.

name: अज्ञानध्वान्तदीपिका
summary: (i) One who is the lamp for the darkness of ignorance.
(ii) Ignorance is like dense darkness.
(iii) She removes it like a lamp giving light.
searchText: 993 अज्ञानध्वान्तदीपिका अज्ञानमेव ध्वान्तमन्धकारस्तस्य दीपिकेव। अज्ञानध्वान्तदीपिका। — component of the name (i) one who is the lamp for the darkness of ignorance. (ii) ignorance is like dense darkness. (iii) she removes it like a lamp giving light. in the saubhāgyabhāskara, bhāskararāya maps her rapid corrective illumination: the solvent of ignorance: ignorance (ajñāna) behaves like a dense, dark room, causing the soul to stumble over illusions. she acts as the blinding, clear lamp (dīpikā) placed inside this room. she does not battle the darkness; her presence illuminates the space, causing the darkness of ignorance to vanish instantly.
esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara, Bhāskararāya maps Her rapid corrective illumination:

The Solvent of Ignorance: Ignorance (ajñāna) behaves like a dense, dark room, causing the soul to stumble over illusions. She acts as the blinding, clear lamp (Dīpikā) placed inside this room. She does not battle the darkness; Her presence illuminates the space, causing the darkness of ignorance to vanish instantly.

name: आबालगोपविदिता
samasa: बालान् ब्रह्मादिकान् गोपयतीति बालगोपः। बालश्चासौ गोपश्च बालगोपः। बालगोपश्च बालगोपश्च बालगोपौ तावभिव्याप्य आबालगोपं तादृशेन विदितं ज्ञानं यस्याः सा।
summary: (i) One known from children up to cowherds, or one known by all.
(ii) The name is explained through Bala and Gopa and by extension all beings.
(iii) Her presence and fame are universally known.
searchText: 994 आबालगोपविदिता बालान् ब्रह्मादिकान् गोपयतीति बालगोपः। बालश्चासौ गोपश्च बालगोपः। बालगोपश्च बालगोपश्च बालगोपौ तावभिव्याप्य आबालगोपं तादृशेन विदितं ज्ञानं यस्याः सा। आबालगोपविदिता। — component of the name (i) one known from children up to cowherds, or one known by all. (ii) the name is explained through bala and gopa and by extension all beings. (iii) her presence and fame are universally known. bhāskararāya clarifies the total accessibility of the absolute: the open secret: lest a seeker think she is accessible only to a small group of elite scholars, this name confirms her presence is universally felt. she is known by the simplest, most humble cowherds (gopas) through pure love, and by the highest creator principles (bālas like brahmā) through deep contemplation. she is an open reality accessible to anyone who approaches with a clean heart.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya clarifies the total accessibility of the absolute:

The Open Secret: Lest a seeker think She is accessible only to a small group of elite scholars, this name confirms Her presence is universally felt. She is known by the simplest, most humble cowherds (gopas) through pure love, and by the highest creator principles (bālas like Brahmā) through deep contemplation. She is an open reality accessible to anyone who approaches with a clean heart.

name: सर्वानुल्लङ्घ्यशासना
summary: (i) One whose command cannot be transgressed by anyone.
(ii) Even Brahma, Vishnu and others cannot cross Her command.
(iii) Her rule is supreme and inviolable.
searchText: 995 सर्वानुल्लङ्घ्यशासना सर्वैर्ब्रह्मविष्ण्वादिभिरप्युल्लङ्घितुमयोग्यमशक्यम् च शासनं यस्याः सा। यस्याः — whose सर्वानुल्लङ्घ्यशासना। — component of the name (i) one whose command cannot be transgressed by anyone. (ii) even brahma, vishnu and others cannot cross her command. (iii) her rule is supreme and inviolable. in the saubhāgyabhāskara, bhāskararāya defines the unyielding certainty of universal law: the absolute rule: the laws of nature—such as gravity, the tracking of karma, planetary orbits, and the steps of dissolution—are not random choices. they are her executive decrees (śāsana). no entity, from a tiny cell up to the highest cosmic administrators like brahmā or viṣṇu, has the capacity to break this law, ensuring absolute order across the multiverse.
esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara, Bhāskararāya defines the unyielding certainty of universal law:

The Absolute Rule: The laws of nature—such as gravity, the tracking of karma, planetary orbits, and the steps of dissolution—are not random choices. They are Her executive decrees (Śāsana). No entity, from a tiny cell up to the highest cosmic administrators like Brahmā or Viṣṇu, has the capacity to break this law, ensuring absolute order across the multiverse.

name: श्रीचक्रराजनिलया
samasa: श्रीचक्रराजं बिन्दुत्रिकोणादिरूपं निलयो वासस्थानं यस्याः सा।
summary: (i) One whose abode is the royal Sree Chakra.
(ii) The Sree Chakra, formed of Bindu, triangle and other parts, is Her dwelling place.
(iii) She resides in the supreme yantra of Sree Vidya.
searchText: 996 श्रीचक्रराजनिलया श्रीचक्रराजं बिन्दुत्रिकोणादिरूपं निलयो वासस्थानं यस्याः सा। यस्याः — whose श्रीचक्रराजनिलया। — component of the name (i) one whose abode is the royal sree chakra. (ii) the sree chakra, formed of bindu, triangle and other parts, is her dwelling place. (iii) she resides in the supreme yantra of sree vidya. bhāskararāya details the geometric anatomy of her house: the dynamic sanctuary: she does not inhabit a distant, imaginary heaven. her definitive architectural palace is the श्रीचक्रराज (śrī cakra rāja) — the supreme arrangement of nine intersecting triangles forming forty-three secondary shapes around the central point. this chart is the exact structural model of her energy field, serving as the gateway where the practitioner communicates with her presence.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya details the geometric anatomy of Her house:

The Dynamic Sanctuary: She does not inhabit a distant, imaginary heaven. Her definitive architectural palace is the श्रीचक्रराज (Śrī Cakra Rāja) — the supreme arrangement of nine intersecting triangles forming forty-three secondary shapes around the central point. This chart is the exact structural model of Her energy field, serving as the gateway where the practitioner communicates with Her presence.

name: श्रीमत्त्रिपुरसुन्दरी
samasa: त्रिपुरस्य परशिवस्य सुन्दरी भार्या। श्रीमती च सा त्रिपुरसुन्दरी चेति।
summary: (i) The auspicious Tripura Sundari.
(ii) She is the beautiful consort of Tripura, the Supreme Shiva.
(iii) She is endowed with Sree and is Tripura Sundari Herself.
searchText: 997 श्रीमत्त्रिपुरसुन्दरी त्रिपुरस्य परशिवस्य सुन्दरी भार्या। श्रीमती च सा त्रिपुरसुन्दरी चेति। सा — she त्रिपुरसुन्दरी — tripura sundari श्रीमत्त्रिपुरसुन्दरी। — component of the name (i) the auspicious tripura sundari. (ii) she is the beautiful consort of tripura, the supreme shiva. (iii) she is endowed with sree and is tripura sundari herself. in the saubhāgyabhāskara, bhāskararāya defines the premier name of the text: the empress of divine beauty: tripurasundarī denotes the sovereign beauty that outshines everything across the three enclosures of time, space, and consciousness. she belongs to tripura (lord paramaśiva). she is śrīmatī because she possesses the absolute, uncorrupted majesty of divine splendor, acting as the radiant queen who directs the lineage.
esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara, Bhāskararāya defines the premier name of the text:

The Empress of Divine Beauty: Tripurasundarī denotes the sovereign beauty that outshines everything across the three enclosures of time, space, and consciousness. She belongs to Tripura (Lord Paramaśiva). She is Śrīmatī because She possesses the absolute, uncorrupted majesty of divine splendor, acting as the radiant queen who directs the lineage.

name: श्रीशिवा
summary: (i) The blessed Sree Shiva.
(ii) She is Shiva endowed with Sree, the sacred consort and integral part of Shiva.
(iii) Whatever meaning applies to Shiva applies to Her also, because She is not separate from Him.
searchText: 998 श्रीशिवा श्रीयुक्ता शिवा। श्रीशिवा। — component of the name (i) the blessed sree shiva. (ii) she is shiva endowed with sree, the sacred consort and integral part of shiva. (iii) whatever meaning applies to shiva applies to her also, because she is not separate from him. bhāskararāya details the total synthesis of material and spiritual wealth: the non-dual alliance: this name fuses the two grand goals of human pursuit: श्री (śrī — material prosperity, beauty, and systemic abundance) and शिवा (śivā — pure, unconditioned consciousness, stillness, and final liberation). she is śrīśivā because she contains both traits simultaneously, ensuring that her path provides earthly support alongside spiritual freedom.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya details the total synthesis of material and spiritual wealth:

The Non-Dual Alliance: This name fuses the two grand goals of human pursuit: श्री (Śrī — material prosperity, beauty, and systemic abundance) and शिवा (Śivā — pure, unconditioned consciousness, stillness, and final liberation). She is Śrīśivā because She contains both traits simultaneously, ensuring that Her path provides earthly support alongside spiritual freedom.

name: शिवशक्त्यैक्यरूपिणी
summary: (i) One who is the union of Shiva and Shakti.
(ii) She embodies the inseparable unity of Shiva and Shakti.
(iii) Like light and illumination, word and meaning, sun and heat, She is the integrated non-dual form of both.
searchText: 999 शिवशक्त्यैक्यरूपिणी शिवशक्त्योरैक्यं सामरस्यमेव रूपमस्याः अस्तीति। शिवशक्त्यैक्यरूपिणी। — component of the name (i) one who is the union of shiva and shakti. (ii) she embodies the inseparable unity of shiva and shakti. (iii) like light and illumination, word and meaning, sun and heat, she is the integrated non-dual form of both. in the saubhāgyabhāskara, bhāskararāya delivers the definitive theological crown of the text: the non-dual oneness: as the sahasranāma reaches its penultimate peak, this name removes any remaining dualistic filters. shiva (pure, still consciousness) and shakti (dynamic, creative energy) are not two separate entities sitting together. they exist in a state of absolute, perfect fusion (sāmarasya) — like heat inside fire. she is this exact non-dual unity. duality is merely her unity displaying its options, anchoring the seeker in absolute oneness.
esoteric: In the Saubhāgyabhāskara, Bhāskararāya delivers the definitive theological crown of the text:

The Non-Dual Oneness: As the Sahasranāma reaches its penultimate peak, this name removes any remaining dualistic filters. Shiva (pure, still consciousness) and Shakti (dynamic, creative energy) are not two separate entities sitting together. They exist in a state of absolute, perfect fusion (Sāmarasya) — like heat inside fire. She is this exact non-dual unity. Duality is merely Her unity displaying its options, anchoring the seeker in absolute oneness.

name: ललिताम्बिका
summary: (i) One who is the beautiful Mother called Lalita.
(ii) This is the concluding name of the thousand names. The hymn begins with Sreemata, Mother, and ends with Ambika, Mother.

(iii) Though She is Brahman, devotees can easily approach Her as Mother. Lalita means She who plays, She who is beautiful, and She who, through divine sport, creates and sustains the universe. At the end of this name, Om is to be pronounced.
searchText: 1000 ललिताम्बिका ललतेऽसौ ललिता च साम्बिका च। ललिता — playful; beautiful ललिताम्बिका। — component of the name (i) one who is the beautiful mother called lalita. (ii) this is the concluding name of the thousand names. the hymn begins with sreemata, mother, and ends with ambika, mother. (iii) though she is brahman, devotees can easily approach her as mother. lalita means she who plays, she who is beautiful, and she who, through divine sport, creates and sustains the universe. at the end of this name, om is to be pronounced. bhāskararāya concludes this thousand-name milestone by anchoring her identity within playful compassion: the ultimate playful mother: the word ललिता (lalitā) defines her core style: she acts with absolute charm, effortless ease, and playful elegance (lalita), managing universes without friction. she links this play directly to her role as अम्बिका (ambikā — the deeply compassionate mother). she runs the grand play of the cosmos to guide, protect, and love her children. this name seals the text, confirming that the ultimate absolute is a mother who welcomes the practitioner home with open arms.
esoteric: Bhāskararāya concludes this thousand-name milestone by anchoring Her identity within playful compassion:

The Ultimate Playful Mother: The word ललिता (Lalitā) defines Her core style: She acts with absolute charm, effortless ease, and playful elegance (Lalita), managing universes without friction. She links this play directly to Her role as अम्बिका (Ambikā — the deeply compassionate mother). She runs the grand play of the cosmos to guide, protect, and love Her children. This name seals the text, confirming that the ultimate absolute is a mother who welcomes the practitioner home with open arms.
